raw_text: AANENSEN, ERNEST FENELON.—B. 1882; ed. Ardingly Coll., Sussex and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., 1900; princ. offr. sect., Jan., 1931; 2nd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1938.

ABBOTT, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. Crown Agents' office after compet. exam., Oct., 1912; astt. head, finance dept., 1929; dep. head, 1933; head, 1938; astt. sec., E. African currency bd., 1922; sec., 1930-38.

ABBOTT, CHARLES, Barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn).—Res. Mag., Kenya, Feb., 1930; crown coun., Oct., 1930; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, June-Sept., 1937; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1938.

ABBOTT, MYLES JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. Kings Schl., Canterbury; admitted solr. (Eng.), Oct., 1929; 2nd lieut., 9th Bn. Middx. Regt. (T.A.), Mar., 1933; lieut., Mar., 1936; astt. crown solr., astt. land offr. and D.R.M., Hong Kong, Mar., 1936; ag. crown solr., Jan., 1937.
ABDUL HADI, RUHI BEY, O.B.E.—B. 1885; held various offices in the Turkish diplomatic service, 1905-20; admve. offr., Palestine, 1920; astt. sec., 1930.

ABRAHAM, ARTHUR ALEXANDER.—B. 1883; agrl. instr., Br. Guiana, 1906; snr. do., 1916; horticultural supt., 1918; sec., B. G. sec., Br. Empire Exhib., Wembley, 1924; astt. supt., botanic gdns., 1928; astt. agrl.supt., 1930; agrl. supt., 1935.

ABRAHAMS, Sir SIDNEY SOLOMON, Kt. Bach. (1936), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil admin., Jan., 1920; advoc. gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just., Uganda, Feb., 1933; do., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; do., Ceylon, 1936; retd. 1939; joint edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1888-1918; sole edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1924.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E. 1918; twice ment. in desps; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov. southern dist., 1922; dist. commr., northern dist., 1925; commr., lands, 1927.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in despatches, Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; ass't prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte. on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte. on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-secret., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin., Jan., 1926; ass't sec., 1st Jan., 1938.

ACHESON, JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1892; ed. the Academy, Ballymena and Dublin Univ.; L.M. Rotunda, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.P.H., M.A., M.D.; war serv., 1915-18; M.O., N. Rhodesia, 1923; ag. D.M.S., 1936-37; S.M.O., 1937.

ACKERMANN, HENDRIK REINHARDT, M.B. Ch.B. (Cape Town).—B. 1908; ed. B.H.S. Stell, S.A.C. Schi. & U.C.T.; M.O., Woodstock Hosp., 1933; snr. M.O., Somerset Hosp. Ct., 1934; M.O., Nelspruit Tuberc. Sanatorium, Aug., 1934; med. supt., do., Nov., 1937.
ADAMS, CYRIL OCTAVIUS.—B. 1907; ed. Sherborne; sub.-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1930; 3rd cls. inspr., 1932; ass't commr., pol., Nigeria, 1935.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; acctnt., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; ass't commr., income tax, 1921; senr. ass't treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas. in 1921, 1925 and 1927; dep. treas., Oct., 1929; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930; dep. treas., Palestine, 1933; mem., rly. bd., road bd., harbr. bd., cent. town planning comm., wages comm., 1935; ag. treas., mem., exec. and advisory comm., currency offr., June-Oct., 1934; finan. sec., Nyasaland, March, 1939; ag. ch. sec., Sept., 1939.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1885; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; ass't dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., K Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st ass't sec., 1919; ag. 1st ass't sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1920; ch. ass't dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regiar. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. Ib, chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; Br. res., Selangor, Dec., 1933; ch. commr., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1936.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning imprv., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Conferees of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offl. repts. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibits, Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt., since 1914; hon. mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cty. Engrs. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914, and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, MAJOR WILFRID CARNE, M.C.—B. 1894; supt., constab., N. Berne, Dec., 1919; adjt., Apl., 1922; ag. offr. commdg., constab. and offl. mem., leg. coun., May, 1927; commdt. and offl. mem., legis. coun., Apr., 1931.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, K.T. BACH. (1924). C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London: Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1906; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925-31; vice-pres., educnl. sec., Br. Assn. (centenary meeting).

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (hons.), 1923; ass't dist. commr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. ass't sec., cls. of couns., edr., offl. gazette, Mar., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. registr., Pemba in 1931 and 1932; dist. offr., Pemba, Apr., 1934; seconded to D.O., June-Oct., 1933; dist. commr., Pemba, Nov., 1934; dist. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1936; admstive offr., Zanzibar, 1938.
ADDO, JAMES ABEE, B.A.—B. 1903; 4th cls. teacher, Gold Coast, 1922; 2nd divn. teacher, 1923; cadet instr., schls., 1927; instr., schls., 1928.

ADKINS, EDWARD CECIL STAPLETON, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1903; ed. Christ's Hosp., Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1927; ag. ass't prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1930; ag. ass't dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Nov., 1932; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1934; do., III, Jan., 1939.

ADSHEAD, PERCY WILLIAMS, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed. Tettenhall Coll.; R.F.A. (T.F.), 1914-19; France, 1915-19; capt. and adjt., 1916-18; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Uganda, Nov., 1926; mem. cotton enquiry comm., 1929; senr. ass't treas., Palestine, 1938.

AGAR, SIR ARTHUR KIRWAN, K.T. BACH. (1939).—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, registrar, of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, regiar. gen., regiar. of titles and legal adviser to govt. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. commr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag.-atty-gen., Dec., 1923 to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1936.
AGNEW, HARVEY LE PAGE.—B. 1896; army, 1915-19; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1920; asst. supt., 1927; supt., 1936.

AHREBECK, JULIUS SEPTIMUS.—B. 1888; ed. High Sch., Heidelberg, Transvaal; mine overseer's certif. and 1st cls. min. man. certif.; (Met reduction wrks., 1910-1914; underground, 1915-27; asst. instr. mines, 1927; instr. mines, 1937.

AINLEY, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. St. Bee Schil. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; barristerat-law, Gray's Inn, 1928; Northern circuit, 1928-35; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Feb., 1936; crown counsel, Feb., 1936; ag-dist. mag., Aug., 1936; ag-solr-gen., Aug., 1937 to Jan., 1938.

AITKEN, ERIC COLQUHOUN.—B. 1884; searcher, gen. regis. office, Jamaica, 1902; astt. treas., 1907; ditto, secretariat, 1911; 2nd cls. clk., 1913; 1st ditto, 1919; conf. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1921; ag. col. sec. on several occasions; audr.-gen., 1932.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., L.L.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Gigglewick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; astt. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. off., Gambia, 1925; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933; ditto, S. Stitts., 1935; judge, Seremban, Nov., 1935; do., Ipoh, July, 1936; puisne judge, Penang, May, 1939.
AITKEN, W. J., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

ALABASTER, CHALONER GRINNELL, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; confirmed in appt., 1931; unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1925; attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug.-Sept., 1934; ag. ch.-just., June-Aug., 1937; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1844-1912."
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsrr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade comsrr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan. 1918; 2nd grade, 1926; 1st grade, Mar., 1931.
ALBURY, WILTON GRANT, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific civ. serv., 1915-20; astt. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1924; seconded as lab. offr., 1929; dist. offr., July, 1931; attd., secretariat, 1933; dist. offr., 1935.

ALLAN, ALBERT, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1893; war serv., 1914-19; station supt. and traffic inspr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1920; astt. sec., G.M.R. office in 1921, 1923, 1925 and 1929; astt. sec., bd. in 1924, 1927 and 1930; divnl. supt. (W.), K. Lumpur, Aug., 1932; do. (Mid.), Genas, Mar., 1934; divnl. supt., K. Lumpur, July, 1936; gen. man. and traffic man., Sierra Leone rly., 1938.
ALLAN, FRANCIS HALLIDAY, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—Jr. 1894; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin., Edin. Univ.; astt. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1924; ar. drainage and irrign. engr., Sept., 1938.

ALLAN, PETER.—B. 1886; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), 1910; D.P.H., 1912; M.D., 1920; R.A.M.C., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; med. inspr., Union of S. Africa health dept., 1920; major, S.A.M.C., 1921; med. supt., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1923; seconded to tuberculosis research comte., 1927-30; reassumed M.S., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1930; astt. health offr., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1935; dep. ch. health offr., Union, 1937.

ALLAN, WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1904; ed. Glasgow Univ., South Eastern Agr. Coll., Wye and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr.; astt. research offr., dept. of agr., N. Rhodesia, 1928; entomologist, 1933; ag. dir., agr., Mar. to Oct., 1935 and Feb. to Oct., 1938; M.L.C., 1938; astt. dir., agr., and entomologist, 1939.

ALLCHURCH, HERBERT PERCY.—B. 1894; G.W.R., 1908-13; Marconi Co., 1913-14; war surv., 1914-19 (R.N.); station mast., K.U.R. & H., 1919; astt. dist. traffic suptd., 1928; dist. traffic suptd., cls. II, 1937.

ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B. 1890 ed.; Christ Hospital; examr., Exchequer and Audit dept., Nov., 1911; astt. audr., Nigeria, June, 1914; seconded, rly. constr., Oct., 1922-July, 1929; senr. astt. audr., Nigeria, Nov., 1930.
ALLEN, CONWAY PHILLIPS, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool)—B. 1901; anaesthetist, F.M.S., July, 1927; med. offr. in ch., offr., in addn., Aug., 1932; surg., J. Bahru, Jan., 1937.
ALLEN, EMILY MAY.—Ed. St. Etheldreda Coll., Norwich; temp. mistress, kindergarten schl., Tamale, Gold Coast, 1927; mistress, 1928; inspr., schl., 1933.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; engnr. astt., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Ilorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engnr., 1924; native admstrn. engnr., 1925; mech. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1926; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1930; ag. D.P.W. and M.L.C., May to Nov., 1934; exec. engnr. i/p pub. wks., 1936; dep. dir., pub. wks., 1938; mem., var. local govt. comtees.; chmn., dischgd. prisoners' aid soc.

ALLEN, GROFFREY CARL.—B. 1896; astt. clk., G.P.O., Lond., Jan., 1914; war serv., 1914-1919; astt. survr., G.P.O., U.K., Dec., 1919; supt., P. & T., S.S., Jan., 1925; permr. astt. to sec. for postal affrs., Malaya, Feb., 1930; ag. contr., Penang & P.W., Nov., 1936; ag. contrlr., posts, Singapore, Apr., 1938.

ALLEN, GEORGE VANCE, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons), D.T.M. and H.—B. 1894; astt. bacteriologist, Kenya, Feb., 1921; senr. bacteriologist, Sept., 1923; bacteriologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. prin., med. coll., Singapore, June, 1929; prin., Mar., 1930; pres., Raffles Coll. in addn., Apr., 1932; mem. dental bd., S. S., Sept., 1933; mem., pharmacy bd., 1934; offl. del., 9th cong. Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

ALLEN, H.—Cashier and acct., E.A.P., July, 1914; on active serv., E. Africa, 1914-16 and 1917-18; tempy. astt. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; astt. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920; astt. treas., Zanzibar, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Uganda, 1935; dep. currency offr., 1936; acct.-gen., 1937.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1856; erld. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high comsrr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., astt. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, Lucien Arthur, O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. res., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jau., 1924; dep. pub. pros., N. Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1931; offr., cls. I B, 1932; do., Kinta, Mar., 1933; comsrr., lands and mines, Johore, Mar., 1934; ag. gen. advr., Johore, 1937; ag. dep. comsrr., lands, F.M.S., Oct., 1938; contrlr., rubber, Malaya, May, 1939.
ALLEN, Mark E.—B. 1917; ed. Charterhouse and Oxford; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., 2nd Oct., 1939.
ALLEN, Percy de Vere.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne, Australia; on mily. serv., E. Africa, Egypt and France, 1914-19; lab. inspr., Kenya, Apr., 1925; lab. offr., Jan., 1929; prin. lab. offr., 1936.

ALLEN, Col. Sir Stephen Shepherd, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1901-04 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in commd., 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in commd., Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; R. of O., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, 1928-31; chmn., transport co-ordination bd., 1934-36; four times ment. in desps.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-genl., N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, William Ruskin, B.A.—B. 1895; war serv., 1914-18; ret. with rank of capt.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, James Douglas, M.B.E. (1935), M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.).—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. asupt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. clk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 3rd Mar., 1937.

ALLISON, Herbert Bruce.—B. 1893; ed. Neilson Schl., Paisley and R. Tech. Coll., Glas.; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade II, Aug., 1924; do., grade I, Aug., 1928; ag. senr. dist. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1936.
ALLISON, J. S., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; ed. Glenalmond and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin.); LL.B. (Cape); Cape law dep., 1903; Union dep. of native affrs., 1918; under sec. for native affrs., 1929.
ALLMAN, George Francis.—B. 1878; under sec. and perm't. head, lands dept., N.S.W., Aug., 1934.

ALLSOP, Herbert Henry.—B. 1885; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade adminstr. offr., Aug., 1922; ag. dep. prov. comsrr. (W. Prov.), Oct., 1934-Dec., 1935.

ALTON, Hubert Faulconer, M.I.W.T.—B. 1893; ed. ptye. Schl. and City and Guilds of London Tech. Coll., Finsbury; mil. serv. 5th (T) Batt. R. Sussex Regt., 1908-13; mercantile marine (troopship), 1914; warrant telegraphist, R.N.R., 1915-19; Indian posts and tels. dept. (W/T), 1922-24; postmr., Seychelles, 1928-34; postal survr. and wireless engnr., Gambia, 1935; dep. P.M.G., Somaliland, 1939.

ALTSON, Ralph Abbey, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.—B. 1894; Bedford Sch. and Royal Coll. of Sci., London; asst. botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1923; botanist and mycologist, Jan., 1928; asst. mycologist, agr. dept., F.M.S., Apr., 1928; del. to 2nd Internat. Congr. for Microbiology, Lond., July, 1936; ag. mycologist, July, 1937; title changed, plant pathologist, Jan., 1938.

ALUWHIRAKE, Richard.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; office ass't. to dir., agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landmg. survr., cust., Dec., 1926; comsrr., requests, Kandy, and addtl. dist. judge, Kegalla, Nov., 1928; asst. sttltm. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Nuwan Eliya, Oct., 1931; contr., finance and supply, Mar., 1934; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1937.

ALVAPILLAI, Kovindapillai, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd. Matara kach., Sept., 1929; attd. office asst., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Mar., 1934; comsrr., requests and pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1936; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1936; do., Kandy, Mar., 1937; sec. to min. for educn., June, 1937.

AMBLER, Capt. Goodman, M.C., V.D., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Steyning Grammar Schl., Brighton Grammar Schl. and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria Inst., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of capt., Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schl., S'pore, Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; capt., Aug., 1930; transf'd. to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; senr. normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931; headmast., Outram schl., Nov., 1932; ag. do., Victoria schl., S'pore, May, 1939.

AMBLED, Lloyd Edward.—B. 1894; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. (comsmd., Oct., 1917); Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-inspr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Western Pacific high comsrr., May, 1921; ag. ch. clk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1927; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high comsrr., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acctnt., Jan., 1932; ag. asst. sec., Western Pacific high comsrr. (conjoint), March-May, 1939.
AMERASINGHE, Hamilton Shirley, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937; office asst., Jaffna, Nov., 1938.

AMERASINGHE, Samson Felix, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office asst. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, June,

ANDERSON, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. Kilmarnock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (Franco) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, 1925; ag. supt., accts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; supt., accts. and stores, 1928; contr., stores, 1938.

ANDREAE, CHARLES EDGAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1902; ed. Charterhouse and Glasgow Univ.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1926; exec. engr., grade I., 1937; senr. exec. engr., 1938; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1938.

ANDREWES, LANCELOT RUGGLES.—Ed. Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. regis. in 1932-38; dep. regis., sup. ct., 1936; ag. regis., sup. ct., offl. admistr., offl. trustee and regis., coys. in 1937; offl. recvr. and regis., trade marks and patents, Oct., 1937.

ANDREWS, HARRY THOMSON.—B. 1897; ed. Pretoria Univ.; P.M.G.'s office, 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; called to bar, Transvaal Prov. divn.; advoc., 1927; pvt. secy., min. of lab. and defence, 1926-30; professional asst., dept. of just., 1930; polit. secy., S. Africa House, London, 1930-35; mem., Imp. shipping comte., 1930-35; accredited rep. of Union of S. Africa to L. of N., 1935; S. African del., internat. lab. confce., 1933, 1934, 1936, 1937; S. African del., L. of N. Assembly, 1933-37.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Oxford; schol., Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgeman Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A.; M.A.; 1928; cadet, adminstr., Uganda, 1925; asst. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; seconded to secretariat, Uganda, 1929; sec., cotton enquiry comsn., 1929; asst. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1935; clk., leg. coun., 1936; attchd. C.O., Dec., 1937.
ANGWIN, JOHN.—B. 1892; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll. Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and asst. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schl. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928; med. inspr. (malaria), 1931; senr. mal. offr., 1938.

ANSDELL, GEORGE ARTHUR REES WETTENHALL.—B. 1894; ed. Liscard High Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1925; ag. dist. offr., 1925; seconded as lab. offr., 1928; A.D.C. and P.S. to gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927 and Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; dist. offr., 1934.

ANSTEY, EDGAR—B. 1917; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol., 1929-35) and King's Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1935-38); maths. tripes pt. I., els. I., pt. II., els. II., moral sciences tripes, pt. II. (psychology), els. I., B.A., 1938; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1938.
ANTEBI, Solomon, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1895; agrl. offr., S. dist., Palestine.

ANTHONY, Philip Arnold, C.M.G. (18), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Sch.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engrnr., F.M.S. Rly., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust.; Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rly., 1925; ag. ch. engrnr. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, Maurice E., B.A., O.B.E. (1938)—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. elk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ast. pvtc. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as pvtc. sec. to gov. Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.O., 23rd July, 1930; seconded as polit. sec. to High Commr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa Jan., 1936; ag. High Commr. for U.K., May-Oct., 1936; ast. sec. C.O., June, 1939; prin. sec. office of the U.K. rep. to Eire, 1939.

ANTROBUS, Sir Reginald L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, Dec., 1882 to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, Dec., 1882 to June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), June, 1885 to Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, Feb. to Aug., 1886; adminstr. the govt. of St. Helena, Nov., 1889 to June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; ast. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; ret., 1918.

APLIN, Frederick George, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Elstow Sch., Bedford; ag. astt. supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, Jan., 1921; astt. supt., do., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1921; ag. sr. astt. supt., do., Perak, Nov., 1923; supt., do., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; supt., cust., Johore, Nov., 1927; supt., preventive br., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1929; ar. supt., F.M.S., Apr., 1933; supt., cust., marine and chandu, Kelantan, Oct., 1935; supt., excise, S.S. (S'pore), 1937; ag. dep. comsurr., do., prov. branch, Oct., 1937; dep. comsurr., excise, S.S., 1938; astt. contrlr., cust., Perak, Dec., 1938.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; astt. dist. comsurr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. astt. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling comsurr., Newam, Oct., 1915, to May, 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ag. dist. comsurr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. comsurr., 1929; prov. comsurr., 1932.

APPLEYARD, Esther, F. Coll. H.—Ed. Thoresby High Sch., Leeds, St. Margaret's Coll., Ripon and Maria Grey Training Coll., London; B. of E. certif.; higher Froebel certif. and trainers dipl.; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1931; inspr. schls., April, 1938.

ARCHER, Clyde Vernon Hargouet, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; Cambridge Univ.; called to bar, Grav's Ed., 1927; clk. to atty.-gen., Barbados, 1930; solvr.-gen., and as pol. mag., dist. "A," various periods, 1930-34; pol. mag., dist. "D," 1933; judge, Bridgetown petty, dbt. etc., 1933.

ARCHER, Sir Geoffrey Francis, K.C.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Ast. collr., East Afr. Prot., Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, Jan., 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. comsurr., Apr., 1903; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa; ast. offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Herbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveying E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinia triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa transit to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admin. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June Oct., 1913; dep. comsurr., Mar., 1913; comsurr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., May, 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; present operations against the Dervishes at Shimberis, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Med. and clasp); member of British Mission occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st clas. gov., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; direct operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prov., Sept., 1922; gov., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, Norman E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman, Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; lieut. (six 1st cls. certifa.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, special course, as torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Royal Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of Stanislas, 3rd cls.).; passed exam. as Russ. interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. admry., H.M. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; ast. under reconstruction scheme ast. prin., med. lab., Sept., 1920; transf'd to C.O., Dec., 1920; pvtc. sec. to perm. under sec. for Dom. affr., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. economic mission to Australia and visited Australia, N. and Canada, 1928-29; pvtc. sec. to partly sec. for Dom. affr., June, 1929; prin. Div. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec., office of the comsurr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., Apr., 1932; ag. high comsurr., Sept., 1934 to 1936.
ARCHER, W. S.—B. 1907; ed. the Law Sch., Barbados, B.W.I.; chief clk., admiralty Dca., 1931; prnl., C.S.O., Antigua and exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1932.

ARCHIBALD, Edgar S., B.A., B.S.A.—1885; recd. pvtv. educn. at Yarmouth pub. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ.; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Sci. in Agric. of the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. expmtlst. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll.; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll., 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Ag. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal hus. man at the Cent. Exptm. Farm, Ottawa; dir. of Exptm. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., 1919.
ARCHIBALD, Sir Robert, Kt., C.V.D.S.O., M.D.—Med. supt., leper sch., Chacachacare, Trinidad, Mar., 1937.
ARDRON, HAROLD GRAHAM.—B. 1892; nily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transfd. polit. serv. as asst. dist. commnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1930; dist. commnr., 1932.

ARGYLE, SIR STANLEY SEYMOUR, K.B.E.—1930; M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond.—B. 1887; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1920; ch. sec. of four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1932-Apr., 1935; leader of opposition since Apr., 1935.

ARMSTRONG, ANDREW CLARENCE FRANCIS.—B. 1907; ed. St Edmunds Sch., Ware and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. commnr., 1931; admistr. ftr., 1932; ag. asst. sec., W. Pao. high commr. on secondment), 1938; ag. sec., Western Pacific high commr., March-May, 1939.

ARMSTRONG, ARTHUR LEOPOLD, O.B.E.—1893.—B. 1888; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1915; asst. sec. for native affrs., Jan., 1920; resigned, Oct., 1920; dist. commnr., and prov. commnr., Dec., 1921; under sec. for native affrs., Oct., 1922; dist. sec., do., Jan., 1927; ag. sec., do., on various occasions, 1927-34; ag. col. sec. (conjoint), June-Aug., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun. and mem. rec. coun. for various periods, 1929-32; last asst. col. sec., Mar., 1932; ag. col. sec., May-June, 1933 and in 1935; gov't. dep., June, 1933 and sept., 1935; ag. sec., native affrs. in 1934; prin. st. col. sec., 1936; offi. M.L.C., 1937; agt. and maul, Tonga, 1937.
ARMSTRONG, ARTHUR SYLVANUS.—B.1903; I. Haileybury Coll.; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; dist. offr., 1938.

ARMSTRONG, GEORGE JAMES, M.H.E. (1938); B. 1901; ed. St Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; K., Basutoland, grade II., 1920; grade I., 1927; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1929; asst. dist. commnr., 1936; seconded to secrt., 1936 and 37; mast., res. commnr's. ct. in addn., 1937; st. commnr., 1938.
ARMSTRONG, HON. HUBERT THOMAS.—B. 1875; ed. Public Sch., Bulls, N.Z.; memb., N.Z. R. of R. since 1922; min. of lab., 1935-39, and in. of immigr., 1935; rep. N.Z., Internat. lab. nce., Geneva, 1937.
ARMSTRONG, CAPTAIN JOHN ANDREW.—B. 1922; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1924; st. commnr., 1932.

ARMSTRONG, J. R., O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1881; clk., defence dept., Union of S. Africa, ov., 1913; clk., High Commr.'s office, S. Africa, Jan., 1915; apptd. to Swaziland estab., n., 1919; ag. conf. clk. to gov-gen. on various occasions from 1916-1919; asst. sec. (in S. Africa), Lord Cave's commr. on S. Rhodesia, 1919; nr. clk., 1925; finan. sec., Swaziland, Dec., 27; govt. sec., Apr., 1933; ag. res. commnr., ar.-Sept., 1935.
ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1906; clk. Swaziland, 1925; asst. dist. commnr., 1931; dist. commnr., 1936.

ARNOLD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1924), RT. HON. TONEY ARNOLD.—B. 1878; was partly priv. o. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the ian. sec. to the treasy., 1914; lib. M.P. for olmfield and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly. under sec. for the coln., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 24.

ARNOLD, LESLIE WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc. (con.) (Lond.).—B. 1891; Free Schl., Penang, June, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1915; headmast., govt., eng. schl., Penang, Jan., 1927; do., Hutchings schl., Penang, Jan., 1928; do., high schl., Malacca, Dec., 1930; senr. educn. offr., grade A, 1931; headmast., Free Schl., Penang, Aug., 1934.

ARNOLD, WILLIAM ERIC.—B. 1882; ed. Napier public schl. and Napier high schl., N.Z.; attd. N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1901; dist. man., 1920; senr. clk., actuarial br., 1925; office examr., 1927; acctnt., 1929; sec. and dep. commnr., 1932; commnr., 1933.

ARTHUR, OSWALD RATNOH, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1905; schol. of Charterhouse Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Nigeria, 1923; asst. dist. offr., 1931; admistr. offr. (asst. commnr.), Cyprus, July, 1937; commnr., Dec., 1937.

ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLIYAH, B.Sc.(Lond.).—B.1896; jnr. demstr., govt. tech. schla., Ceylon, Sept., 1917; ag. senr., demstr., Univ. Coll., Oct., 1921; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prov., May, 1924; pol. mag., Chilaw, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Tangalle, Jan., 1929; do., Badulla, Nov., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Feb., 1935; pol. mag., Colombo, Jan., 1936; adnl. asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Dec., 1937; ag. asst. govt. agt., do., June, 1938.
ARUNDELL, ROBERT DUNCAN HARRIS, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; advr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927; sec. to C.O., 1935-39; asst. oh. sec., Uganda, Aug., 1939.

ASHMORE, JACK HALIBURTON.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., The King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., Apr., 1919; col. pol. offrs. course, R.I.C., May, 1920; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, Oct., 1920; dist. inspr., July, 1923; travelling mag. and warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dista., Aug. 1923 to Apr., 1924; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1926; county inspr., 1927; ag. detective inspr., for various periods in 1926-28 and 1930-32; local commdt., pol. in ch. crim. insign. dept., Cyprus, Dec., 1932; ag. ch. commit. pol. and inspr. prisons in various periods, 1933-37; dep. ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Jan., 1935; title changed to dep. commnr., pol. and inspr., prisons, Jan., 1938; commnr., pol. and inspr., prisons, 1939; Col. Police Medal, 1939.

ASHTON, ALFRED EDGAR.—B. 1888; supt., inspection dept., Woolwich, 1915; war serv., 1917-19; acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., U.K., 1921; asst. acct., P. & T., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; acct., P. & T., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; ag. senr. acct., S.S., & F.M.S., Apr., 1928.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ., civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obuhura.

ASHTON, MAJOR-GEN. E. C., C.B., C.M.G., V.D., M.D., C.M.—B. 1873; ed. Trinity Med. Coll., and graduated in med., Trinity Univ., Toronto, 1898; commnd., Dufferin Rifles of Canada, 1893; gradually promoted to lieut.-col. commig., 1907; at outbreak of war organised and commanded 36th batt., C.E.F., 1915; col., Canadian Militia, May, 1916; after various commands promoted brig.-gen., Apr., 1917; rtnd., Canada to be adjt.-gen., Nov., 1917; major-gen., Jan., 1918; qmr.-gen., Canadian Militia, Aug., 1920; do., defence dept on reorganisation, Jan., 1923; D.O.C., M.D., No. 2, Jan., 1930; do., No. 11, Oct. 1933; oh., gen. staff, dept. of nat. defence, June, 1935; three times ment. in desps. for war services.

ASHWIN, BERNARD CARL, M.Com., A.R.A.N.Z.—B. 1896; ed. Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; joined N.Z. educn. dept., 1912; nat. prov. fund dept., 1920; N.Z. treas., 1922; ch. inspr., 1926; 2nd asst. sec., 1931; ast. sec., 1935; sec., 1939, also rec.-gen., pay-gen., fin. adver. to govt., chmn. local govt. loans bd., dir. reserve bk., mem. bd. of mgmt., state advances corp.

ASHWORTH, HENRY TWEDDLE, M.B.E. (1934), M.M.—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., elk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clks. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept. 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1927; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1934.

ASPINALL, SIR ALGERNON EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1939), KT. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comtese, 1898-38 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; pres. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comte. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comte., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comtees., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comte., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comte., 1923; a vice-pres. of W.I. comte., 1939.

ASSER, LIMUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH JOHN, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1916), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, ARTHUR VINCENT.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schl., Chester, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (open maths. schol.); R.F.A., 1915-18 (M.C.), 1917; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; collr., land rev., July, 1922; sec. to Br. advr., Kedah, Feb., 1930; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Nov., 1930; attd., land office, Penang, Nov., 1931; collr., land rev., Penang, July, 1932; ag. res. coun., Penang, Apr., 1933; offr., cls. II, Oct., 1936.
ASTON, FRANK WILLIAM, M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1903; ed. R. "Dick" Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Kenya, 1928.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUCHLAND.—B. 1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry comsrs., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regisr., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Oct., 1917; mag. dist. B., Antigua, July, 1924; mag. dists. "A" and "B," 1930.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.O.M.G. (1923), G.C.V.O. (1904), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; gov. gen. of Canada, 1940; chan. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George (1934); Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem; Grand Master of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1936.

ATKINSON, HAROLD, B.A. (Oxon.), F.I.C.—B. 1891; ed. Bradford Grammar Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; worked in govt. lab., London, 1914-1920; attd. to inspnr. branch, R.A.S.C. supply reserve depot, Deptford, 1916-20; dep. govt. analyst, Colombo, Ceylon, 1935; govt. chem., Cyprus, Nov., 1937.

ATKINSON, VIVIAN BUCHANAN—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; asst. engr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; ast. ch. engr., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1930.

ATTENBOROUGH, J. P., M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1901; supt. educn., Nigeria, 1924; lect., govt. Arab coll., Palestine, Sept., 1930; senr. inspr., educn., Apr., 1935; ag. senr. educn. offr. in 1936 and 1937; educn. offr., Aden, 1937; dir., educn., 1938.
ATTERBURY, H. W., M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1903; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921; cler. offr., higher gde., 5th March, 1934; staff offr., 1st July, 1939.
ATTERBURY, WILLIAM ALBERT.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; war serv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engr., 1926.

AUBER, EGERTON LANCELOT.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Schol., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supernum. clk., cust. BARRY, CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.—B. 1878; ed. St. Paul's Schil and Trinity Coll., Oxford; admitted Inner Temple, 1905; admitted advoc., Pretoria, 1906; judge, sup. ct., 1926.

BARRY, EDWARD, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expedy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; regiar. and prin. regiar. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comanr., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1906; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920; ret. 1934.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914—Jan., 1920; adminstr. offir. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; astt. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. astt. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; astt. admistr. gen. and astt. regiar. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; res. mag., Oct. 1925; ag. adminstr.-gen., ag. regiar.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. regiar., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. regiar., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926; astt. judge, high ct., Nigeria, 1934; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1937; do., Kenya, 1939.

BARTLEY, WM., C.M.G. (1937), M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; B.A., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; passed cadet, July, 1910; offir. of cls. V., Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offir., Dindings, May, 1912; astt. regiar., impts. and expts., S.S., Dec., 1915; sec., food control comtee., June, 1917; mem., shipping comtee., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr.'s comtee., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by S. of S., Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comanr., 1920; supernmy. offir., cls. III., Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comtee. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; astt. adviser, Kelantan, Apr., 1922; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926; offir. cls. IB comanr., lands, S.S., Dec., 1926; cls. IA; under-secr., S.S., 1930; ag. col. sec., S.S., May to June and Aug. to Sept., 1930; pres., mun. comanrs., Singapore, Mar., 1931; supernmy. offir., cls. IA, S.S., 1932; supernmy. staff offir., S.S., July, 1934.

BARTON, A. E. V., C.B.E. (1936), O.B.E. (1933).—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. astt., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comprtr., cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust. and excise, Trinidad, 1930; collr., cust., Nigeria, 1934; contrlr., cust., 1939.

BARTON, C. J. T., C.M.G. (1937) O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law tripeses; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; astt. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., 1917, 1919, 1921; astt. for various affrs., 1923; ag. senr. astt. sec., 1924-26; ag. astt. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. astt. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. on various occasions, 1928-33; senr. astt. col. sec., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., 1934; nom. offir. M.L.C., 1936; col. sec., Fiji, 1935; ag. gov., Fiji and ag. high comanr., W. Pacific, May-Nov., 1936; chmn., Fiji pub. serv. reorganisation comtee., 1936; gov.'s dep., June-Aug., 1937; ex-officio M.L.C., 1937; offir. admin. govt. and ag. high comanr., W. Pacific, July-Sept., 1938 and Apr., 1939; gov's dep., June-July, 1939; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, GEORGE SAMUEL HOBAC, C.M.G. (1936), B.S.A., D.Sc.A.—B. 1883; ed. Vankleek Hill High Schil., Ontario Agrl. Coll., Toronto Univ. (B.S.A.), Laval Univ. (D.Sc.A.); astt., animal husbandry, 1907; lecturer, 1910; prof., 1911; dean, faculty of agr., Macdonald Coll., McGill Univ., 1925; comdr., Agricole Merite, Quebec govt., 1929; dep. min., agr., Canada, 1932.
BARTON, LANCELOT ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; acct. treasy., Hong Kong, 1921; cashier, 1924.

BARTON, MAJOR PERCIVAL FULLER, O.B.E. (1935), V.D.—Astt. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; attd. col. sec.'s office, Oct. - Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. astt. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. astt. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offir., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920; to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offir., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; treas., Dominica, 1932; treas. and collr., cust., St. Helena, Mar., 1933.
BARTON, THOMAS, M.A.—B. 1904; qualified teacher Scottish educn. dept.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Oct., 1928; senr. do., 1939.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.O.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.). Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. Senator, 1926; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; astt. stlitm. offir., July, 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930; astt. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1933; senr. comanr., for dev. of agrl. marketing, Jan., 1935.

BASTON, LT.-COL. GEORGE ROBSON CAREUTHERS.—B. 1881; ed. Willowbrae Pub. Schil., Edinburgh; S.A. War, 1900; S.A. constab., 1901; supt., O.R.C. pol., 1903-12; sub-inspr., S.A. pol., 1914; inspr., 1923; ch. inspr., 1929; dep. commr., 1931; ch. dep. commr., 1936.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audit office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transf'd, Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; astt. dir., prisons, Apr., 1926; is also comanr. of oaths for Union; sp. grade supt., Pretoria cent. prison and Pretoris gaol and outstations and warden, Baviaanspoort farm col. and inebriate reformatory, Sept., 1930; do., Johannesburg gaol and outstations and warden, Leeuwkop farm col., Jan. 1934.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1919), B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Schil., Faversham and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; lat. cls. clas. moda., 1901; 1st cls. math. moda., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. elk., C.O., May, 1905; ag. 1st cls. elk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. elk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comtees, 1912; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. astt. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adml. commdg., sp. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; astt. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; polit. sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of York during his visit to Australia and N.Z., 1927; astt. under sec. of state for dom. affrs., 25th Jan., 1930; dep. sec., Imp. Conf., 1930; regiar., Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1930-39; high comsnr. for the U.K. in the Dom. of N. Zealand, 1939.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS, C.M.G. (1838).—B. 1896; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; addt. to Colombo cust., May, 1920; addt. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1921; office astt., Badulla kach., Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1923; addnl. astt. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd astt. col. sec., May, 1927; 2nd astt. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., May, 1928; astt. col. sec., Jamaica, 1928; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32; col. sec., Cyprus, 1934; ag. gov., Mar. and June-Oct., 1935 and July-Sept., 1936; ch. sec., Palestine, 1937; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1939.

BAVIN, HON. SIR THOMAS RAINSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called to bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania, 1900; sec. to prime min., C. of A., 1901-4; M.L.A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and col. treasurer, N.S.W., 1927-30; judge, sup. ct., N.S.W., 1936.

BAXTER, HARRY CHURCHILL, B.A. (Cantab.), Lieut. Regular Army R. of O., The Northamptonshire Regt.—B. 1899; ed. St. John's Schl., Leatherhead and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika admstrn., Aug., 1923; admstrv. offr., 2nd grade, Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Nov., 1934.

BAXTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACAULEY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of out. in Dom. govt., 1921; prime min., New Brunswick, 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.B., 1931; ch. just., N.B., 1935.

BAYLES, HERBERT LAURENCE, C.M.G. (1838).—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; clk., C.A.'s office, 1905; clk., cust. serv., Nyasa land, 1909; P.W.D., 1910; Nyasa land Field Force, 1914; astt. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; astt. treas., Zanzibar, 1921; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924; treas., Trinidad and chmn., agrl. bank, May, 1931; treas., and currency offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; treas., and currency offr., Nigeria, 1935; finan., sec., 1937.

BAYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PERCIVAL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; clerical astt., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., post office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. clk. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to admstr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; off. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. astt. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr., Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offi. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica various occasions, 1929-36; comnr., Montserrat, 1932.

BEAN, EDGAR LAYTON, C.M.G., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1893; ed. Scotch Coll., W. Australia, Adelaide University and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined public serv., S. Australia, 1920; crown solr.'s office; assoc. to Mr. Just. Poole, 1921-22; partly, draftsmen since 1924.

BEARBLOCK, ARTHUR, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Certif. L.S.T.M. (with dist.)—B. 1898; ed. Univ. Coll. Schi. and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; war serv., 1917-18; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928.

BEARDMORE, ERNEST.—B. 1882; ed. Manchester and Capetown; B.A. (1907); LL.B. (1911); clk., Cape civ. serv., 1900; prof. astt. and ch. clk., solr.-gen., Cape, 1920; solr.-gen., Cape, 1931; crown pros., Witwatersrand, 1933; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1936; do., Natal, 1937.

BEATON, WILLIAM GAUDENS, M.R.C.V.S., D.T.V.M.—B. 1900; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1926; visited govt. labs. at Ondatepoort, S. Africa, Mpapwa, Tanganyika, Kabete and Kenya, 1927; vety. resch. offr., 1929; ag. vety. pathologist, Nigeria, on various occasions and as dep. dir., vety. serv. in 1937; senr. vety. offr., 1938; dep. dir., vety. serv., 1938; ag. dir., do., 1939.

BEATTIE, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (res. of offr.)—Entd. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Highlands), 1906; transfd. on promotion to the Queen's (now The Queen's Royal) Regt., 1908; served in Aden and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; astt. adjutant, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regt. adjutant, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjutant, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in desa., 1914; M.C., 1918; severely wounded, 1915; astt. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offr. staff offr., K.A.R., 1915; promoted major by brevet, 1918; tempy. lieut. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-depl. comtes., 1915-21; ret. with rank of lieut.-col., Sept., 1922; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; astt. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; res. inspr. of schl., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec., bd. of educn. (ex officio), 1922; local secy., Cambridge Univ. exams. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1925; ag. gov. from May, 1926 to Apr., 1927; ch. astt. col. sec., Cyprus, 8th Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-29; col. sec., Gibraltar, Jan., 1930.

BEATTIE, ANDREW GORDON—B. 1898; ed. Public Schl., Collegiate Inst. and Agrl. Coll., Ontario; ent. army, 1916; served with H.M. Forces, England, France and Germany 1916-19; agrl. offr., Nigeria, 1923; prin. do., 1937.

BEATTIE, JAMES, O.B.E. (1934), M.C. (1920).—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1918-20; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. comnr., 1922; comdt., pol. and dir. prisons, 1930; comnr., pol. and dir., prisons, 1937.

BEATTIE, SIR JOHN CARRUTHERS, K.T. (1920), F.R.S.E. and S.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Ed. and... Wits.).—B. 1866; ed. Moray House, Edinburgh Univ.; Vans Dunlop schl. in Physics, 1851 Exhibn. Schl., Univ. of Munich, Vienna, Berlin and Glasgow; prof. of physics, S. African Coll., 1897; medallist S. African Assocon. for Advancement of Science, 1910; crossed Africa from Cape Town to Cairo with Prof. J. T. Morrison on magnetic survey; chmn., survey coman., 1921; pres., S. African Assocon. for Advancement of Science, 1928; prin., S. African Coll., 1917-18; prin. and vice-chanc., Univ. of Cape Town, 1918 to 1938; mem. of bd. of govrs., S.A. broadcastg. corp., since Aug., 1936; prin. publn.: rept. of a Magnetic Survey of S. Africa, Cambridge, 1909, and (in conjunction with Prof. J. T. Morrison) "On Magnetic Work in Southern and Central Africa," Washington, 1913.

BEATTY, Sir KENNETH JAMES, Kt. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's med. and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. pros., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. asst. res. mag., Mar., 1903; asst. res. mag., Dec., 1905; attd. Natal Carabiners, Natal rebellion, 1906; recd. thanks of Natal govt., med. and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and registr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on mily. serv., 1915-18; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; dep. gov., in 1928; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1931.

BEAUBRUN, Ignatius Cecil, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1889; treasy. clk., St. Lucia, 1906; asst. audit clk., 1907; rev. offr., St. Vincent, 1912; ag. audit clk., 1910 and 1912; rev. offr., Grenada, 1915; 3rd treasy. clk., 1916; ch. clk., treasy. and commr. of income tax, 1922; asst. treas., 1929; col. treas., collr. of cust., supt. of indvl. rev., ch. of excise, Grenada, June, 1930; ag. col. sec. and registr.-gen., June, 1932.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNETH.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls. math. hons., 1910; headmnr., gram. schl. and sub-inspr., schls., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr., schls., Leeward Is., July-Oct., 1916; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; supt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. inspr., educn., 1937.
BEAUMONT, Ernest, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., London; on mily. serv., 1915-18; gen. pract., 1919-28; vety. offr., Kenya, 1927.

BEAUCHESNE, ARTHUR, M.A., K.C., LL.D., Litt.D., F.R.S.C.—B.1876; ed. St. Joseph's (class. course), Memramcook, N.B.; grad. as B.A., M.A. and Litt.D. valedictorian, 1895; studied law, Laval, Montreal; admitted to bar, Quebec, 1904; K.C., 1914; legal adviser, justice dept., 1913-16; clk. asst., House of Commons, Canada, and commr. to admstr. oath of allegiance to M. of P.; clk., House of Commons, 1925; author of "Beauchesne's Parliamentary Rules and Forms," "Ecrivains d'Autrefois," etc.

BECK, David Macdonald Herbert, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; temp. lieut., W.A.F.F., Feb., 1917; admstr. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1919; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., Aug., 1934.

BECK, Ewald Egerton Herbert.—B. 1894; ed. Weymouth Coll., Dorset; pol. probtn., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; 1st asst. commr. pol., Selangor, Feb., 1923; supt., convict establt., Taiping and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1923; offr. i/c, pol. dist., Kuantan, Aug., 1927; commt. pol., depot, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929; supt., pol., S.S., Oct., 1931; ch. pol. offr., N. Semibalan, Feb., 1932; do., Malacca, Feb., 1935; do., Penang, Feb., 1937; do., Spore, July, 1938.

BECKETT, Harold.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitions at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Moda., 1913; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 3rd Nov., 1914; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; priv., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. Indies currency comte., 1923; sent to Mauritius as spec. commr. to enquire into and rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1925; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925; ag. asst. sec., 28th Sept., 1931.

BECKETT, Herbert James Rust, A.C.A.—B. 1880; treasy., N. Borneo, Feb., 1911; dist. treas., Apr., 1911; mag., 1st cls., Apr., 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1916-19; dist. offr., Aug., 1919; asst. commr., cust. and excise, Mar., 1921; asst. commr., excise, Feb., 1926; auditor, Mar., 1929; finance contr., Dec., 1930.
BECKINGSALE, Stanley, F.I.A.—B. 1899; govt. actuary, N.Z. govt., 1937.

BROCKLES, Hilary Albert Maughn.—B. 1898; ed. Combermere Schl., Harrison Coll., Codrington Coll., Barbados and Exeter Coll., Oxford; Ecole Malezieux, Paris; B.A. (Durham), hons. classics, 1920; fellow of Univ. of Durham, 1921; B.A. (Oxon.), hons. juris., 1923; Heath Harrison schol. (Oxon.), 1932; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; rep. Br. Guiana at Brit. social hyg. coun. and world educ. conf., Nice, 1932; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1926.

BECKLEY, Verey Alford, M.C., B.A. (Hons.) (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), A.I.C.—B. 1893; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; R.G.A., 1916-19; research schol., Rothamsted, 1919-20; lect. in chem., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921; senr. agrl. chemist, Kenya, 1924.
BEDDOW, Herbert Howell, B.Sc., hons. (Wales).—B. 1898; flying offr., R.A.F., 1916-19; asst. master, Hong Kong educn. dept., 1927; gunner, H.K.V.D.C., Aug., 1939.

BEDINGTON, Guy Cyril, A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., M.I.M. & Cy. E., E.S.I., M.R.S.A., Chtd. Civ. Engnr. and Chtd. Survr.—B. 1889; ed. Univ. of Bristol; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1913; exec. engnr., Ipoh, 1922; attd. ch. hydr. engnr.'s office, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engnr., waterworks, K. Lumpur, Oct., 1930; sr. exec. engnr., F.M.S. & S.S., Klang, 1932; sr. exec. engnr., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1934; ag. state engnr., N. Semibalan, June, 1935; state engnr., P.W.D., Malaya, 1938.

BEGGIE, Ralph Stockman, M.D., M.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—B. 1901; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; ag. malarialogist, 1931; asst. bacteriologist, 1932; ag. bacteriologist, 1934.

BELCHER, Sir Charles Frederic, Kt. Bach. (1931), O.B.E. (1923), M.R.E. (1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ed. at Geelong Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practise, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodian of enemy property, Uganda, April, 1916; mag., Uganda, June, 1916; asst. judge, Zanzibar, June, 1920; judge of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Aug., 1920 and June, 1925; atty. gen., Nyasaland, Sept., 1920; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, high ct., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922; judge, high ct., May, 1924; ch. just., sup. ct., Cyprus, May, 1927; ch. just., Trinidad, 1930.

BELGRAVE, WILLIAM NORMAN CUMMINS, B.A. (Cantab.), Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agr.—B. 1891; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; asst. mycologist, agril. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; plant physiologist, Jan., 1920; ch., divn. of soils and plant physiology, Feb., 1927; deleg. to internat. soc. of soil science, Konigsberg, 1929; deleg., 2nd imp. mycological conf., London, 1929; asst. to dir., in addn., June, 1930; in ch., divn. of agr. chem., in addn., Sept., 1930; ch. resch. offr., Sept., 1931; dir., agr., S.S., and advsr. on agr., M.S., 1939.

BELL, ALAN MALCOLM.—B. 1905; ed. Waterford, Ireland and Bedford; junr. asst., govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1923; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1924; sub-inspr., pol., and adjt. local forces, 1927; sub-inspr., constab., Trinidad, 1928; A.D.C. to gov., 1930-31; local committ., pol., Cyprus, 1934, title changed to asupt., pol., March, 1938; ag. comsnr., various periods, 1935-38.

BELL, EDWARD PETER STUBBS.—B. 1902; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1920; snr. clk., 1927; clk. to atty. gen., 1927; clk., ex. and leg. couns., Antigua, 1927; pte. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., June, 1929 to Apr., 1930; mag., dist. F and afterwards dist. E, Dominica, Nov., 1931; mag. dist. D and addnl. mag., dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1934; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, July to Sept., 1934; ag. crown atty., St. Kitts, May-June, 1935; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1936; crown coun., Palestine, 1938.

BELL, MAJOR G. H., M.C., Officer Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1885; entered army, 1906; India, 1907-9; Nigeria, W.A.F.F., 1911; asst. dir. of surveys, Nigeria, 1912-14; served in France, 1914-16; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Erpedy Force, 1919; G.S.O., Intelligence, E.E.F., 1920; dir. topographical branch, Gold Coast Survey, 1920.

BELL, SIR HENRY HESKETH JOURDAN, G.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1865; ed. in Paris; 3rd clk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treasy. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. asst. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isl., in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; admnr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; comnr. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Jan., 1906; gov., ditto, Oct., 1907; gov., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1909; gov. Leeward Is., 1912; gov., Mauritius, 1916; retd., 1925; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," etc.

BELL, PHILIP SHAW, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1897; milly. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; S.M.O., Somaliland, 1938.

BELL, THOMAS STEWART, B.A.—B. 1911; ed. Cardiff High Schl. and Univ. Coll., S. Wales and Monmouthshire; 1st cl. hons., Greek; 2nd cl., 1st div., hons., Latin; Gwilym mem. pr.; Univ. of Wales teacher's dipl.; Sacr. student, Br. Schl. at Athens, 1933-34; admvr. offr. (asst. comsnr.), Cyprus, Jan., 1936; comnr., Nov., 1935; asst. sec., Sept., 1937; ag. Br. del. of Evkaf, June-Oct., 1937; clk., exec. coun., June, 1938; ag. ch. asst. sec., May, 1939-July, 1939; comnr., govt. of Cyprus inf. and liaison office, London, Sept., 1939.

BELL, TOM, B.S.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1914; ed. Sevenoaks Secondary Schl., Kent, Farm Inst., and Univ. of Toronto (Ontario Coll. of Agric., Guelph), Cambridge Univ. and I.C.T.A., Trinidad; 1st cls. hons., bacteriology, awarded Col. Agric. Schl.; asst. man., govt. stock farm and agril. station, Acre, Palestine, Nov., 1936; agril. supt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1935.
BELEMORE, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; under-secr. and perm. head, dept. lab. and industry, N.S.W., March, 1932.

BENNETT, ARTHUR GEORGE.—B. 1880; Lond. post. serv., Aug., 1893; cust. offr., Falkland Is., Oct., 1911; spec. serv. as whaling offr. and gen. govt. offr. to Antarctic Dependencies, 1913-27; J.P., Dependencies, 1921-23 and 1925-27; seal fish. offr., 1921; asst. to govt. naturalist, 1922; ag. govt. nat., 1924; author of "A List of Birds of the Falkland Is. and Dependencies", "Whaling in Antarctic", and various scientific papers on birds and whales, some in conjunction with R.R.S. "Discovery".

BENNETT, HON. SIR JOHN ROBERT, K.B.E. (1916).—M.H.A., St. John's West, Newf'dld., 1904; M.E.C., 1913; col. sec., 1913; mem., defence comtee., 1915; deputy chief censor, telegraphs and cables, 1914; press censor, 1915; min. of militia, 1917.

BENNETT, JOHN SLOMAN.—B. 1914; ed. Royal Liberty Schl., Romford and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); 1st cls. hist. tripos. pt. i. 1934 and pt. ii. 1935; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 2nd Nov., 1936.

BENNETT, MAURICE CHRISTMAS, F.S.I.—Served in Great War, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; capt., R. of O.; joined O.E.T.A., Nov., 1918; absorbed in civil adminstr., Palestine, as asst. dir., grade F, dept., lands and surveys, July, 1920.

BENNETT, Rt. Hon. RICHARD BEDFORD, P.C. (1930). P.C. (Can.), LL.B., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. pub. and high schls., N.B., Dalhousie Univ., N.S.; hon. degree L.L.D., Dalhousie Univ., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Alberta; admitted to bar of N.B., 1903; mem., legis. assem., Northwest Territories, 1898-1905; legis. assem. of Alta., 1909; rep. Calgary in H. of C., Canada, 1911-17; min. of just. and atty.-gen., 1921; min. of finance in Meighen adminstr., 1926; leader of conserv. party, Oct., 1927; prime min. of Canada after g.e., June, 1930; leader of opposition, 1936-38.

BENNETTS, ALBERT THOMAS, A.C.S.M., M.A.I.M.E., M.A.I.M.M.—B.1890; ed. Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1908-11; medallist of the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Soc'y., 1910; 1st cls. hons. and silver medallist, City and Guilds of London Inst., 1911; 1st cls. diploma, Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1911; mines dept., F.M.S., 1912-25; asst. ward., mines, Perak, Nov., 1929; senr. inspr., mines, Jan., 1931; asst. ward., mines, Selangor, Mar., 1932; as. ward., mines, Pahang, May, 1932; do., Selangor and N. Sembilan, Sept., 1935; do., Pahang, Jan., 1936 and Oct., 1937; senr. inspr. of mines, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Apr., 1938; ag. do., Perak, Jan., 1939.

BENSON, GEORGE ALLEN RALSTON.—B. 1879; 6th cls. offr., cust., Br. Guiana, 1901; 5th cls., 1904; 4th cls., 1911; 3rd cls., 1915; 2nd cls., 1924; 1st cls., 1925; ast. survr., enst., 1926; survr., cust., B. Guiana, 1927.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, KT. BACH. (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st hon. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp. Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. advsr. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, LILLIE JOSEPHINE, M.B., B.Ch., certif. L.S.T.M.—Lady med. offr., B. Gajah, May, 1921; Ipoh, July, 1921; K. Pahang, July, 1923; Taiping, Apr., 1925; K. Kangsar, June, 1925; lady med. offr., infant welfare centre, Taiping, Aug., 1925; Seremban, July, 1926; K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; Ipoh, Nov., 1927; K. Kangsar, Feb., 1937.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jena Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rifle Brig., 1885; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; (ment. in despatches, Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. comsnr., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkerstroom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary comn., 1902; sec. in office of high comn. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial soc., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; ast. Imperial soc., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water comn., 1909.
BENZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1899; ed. Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919-25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BERENDSEN, CARL AUGUST, C.M.G. (1936), LL.M.—B. 1890; ed. Australian pub. schls., Gore Dist. High Schl. (N.Z.) and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. educ. dept., 1906; lab. dept., 1916; chf. clk., 1917; dep. regist., industrial unions, 1919; Imperial affrs. offr., prime min.'s dept., 1926; perm. head, prime min.'s dept., 1933; also sec. for external affrs., from 1928; mem., N.Z. deleg. to L. of N. assembly, 1936; N.Z. deleg. to Imp. Conf., 1937.

BERNARD, ALBERT VICTOR, O.B.E. (1928), M.D. (Malta), D.P.H. (L'pool), D.T.M. & H. (Engl.), Hon. Assoc., Ord. of St. John, Offr. Ord. Crown of Italy.—B. 1885; ast. res. M.O., Malta gvt. hosp., 1909; M.O.H., 1912; prof. hyg. and prev. med., Malta Univ., 1921; ast. supt., pub. hith., 1929; chf. gvt. med. offr., 1936; temp. offl. mem., coun. of gvt., 1918 & 1921; mem. gen. coun., Malta Univ.; ag. rector, 1935-36.

BERNARD, ANDREW JOSEPH—B. 1892; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad, Ushaw Coll., Durham and St. Edmund's Coll., Herts.; Trinidad civ. serv., 1911; war serv., 1915-19; ast. warden, Caroni, Jan., 1930; do., St. Pat., 1930; senr. do., Victoria, Jan., 1935; warden, Caroni, Aug., 1938.

BERNARD, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR DENIS KIRWAN K.C.B., 1939, C.B., 1935, C.M.G., 1918, D.S.O., 1917.—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lt., Rifle Brig., 1902; capt., 1912; served European War (France, Gallipoli, Salonica and Egypt), 1914-18 (despatches, C.M.G., D.S.O., bt. major, bt. lt.-col., Croix-de-Guerre); G.S.O., Mar., 1915 till end of war; brevet-col., 1923; lt.-col., 1927; col., 1930; major-gen., 1933; commanded 1st Batt. Royal Ulster Rifles, 1927-30; brigadier, gen. staff, Northern comd., India, 1930-34; dir., recruiting and organisation, W.O., 1934-37; comdr. 5th Div. from about May, 1937; lt.-gen., 1939; gov., Bermuda, 8th Oct., 1939.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LEO, O.B.E. (1918)—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. R.W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bndry. comn., 1908 (read. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bndry. comn., 1911; ast. dist. comsnr., Somalil. and Prot., Sept., 1912; dist. comsnr., March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sen. ast. sec. to gov't. of Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; ag. ast. ch. sec., Sept., 1921 to May, 1923, Dec., 1923 to March, 1924; ag. prov. comsnr., Apr., 1930 to May, 1931 and Feb., 1932, Mar., 1934; dep. prov. comsnr., Feb., 1931; mem. leg. comn., 1931; prov. comsnr., Sept., 1934.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland div. ser. as clk. H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfdld., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfdld., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland saving bank, 1911.

BERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH. (1916), K.C. (1913).—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I, 1890; ditta. part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin vers.; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tancred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; electd. to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admstd. gvt., Sept. to Oct., 1903; ag. ch. justice, Sep. Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comn. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; atty.-gen., Caylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to gvt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1925; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BESSBOROUGH, 9TH EARL OF (great 1739), Rt. Hon. VERE BRAZON PONSONBY, P.C. (1911), G.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1919), B.A. (Cantab.), B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks. I.Y., 1905-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1918; served in Great War, 1914-18; L.O.O., 1907-10; M.P., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., Co. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada 1931-35.

BEST, CAPT. CHARLES CAIRN, M.C., L.S. M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1894; ed. Auckland Gram. Sch. and Auckland Univ.; survey dept., N.Z., 1914-15; surv.-on-agr., Selangor, Aug., 1923; ast. supt., trig. surv., Sept., 1927; ast. supt., surv., June, 1928; ag. senr. do., Spore., Dec., 1934; ag. ch. survr., Malacca, Oct., 1938.

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1896; vice-pres. of the exec. comm. Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deleg. govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1908; now retd.

BEST, Sir Thomas Alexander Vans, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medalist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa admn., 1896; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. consmr., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. rep., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Impl. Educn. Conf., 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1916, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; reed. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930; ret., 1933.
BETANCOURT, Nelson, F. Inst. Pet.—B. 1887; land surr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, April, 1909; 1st ast. inspr., mines, Apr., 1921; ag. inspr., mines, in 1927, 1933 and 1934-36.

BETHEL, Charles Preval, L.S.O. (1929).—B. 1891; ag. 4th clk., post office, Bahamas, Apr. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Outislanda, Feb., 1910; 3rd clk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ch. clk., C.S.O., Aug., 1919; enlisted for active serv., 1915, 2nd lieut., Mar., 1919; served in France; twice wounded; discharged from Army, Apr., 1919; resumed duties, May, 1919; ag. clk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, Aug. to Sept., 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1923-34; ag. A.D.C. to admstr., Sept. to Nov., 1925; asst. col. sec., July, 1928; ag. col., sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., various periods, 1934-39.

BETTENCOURT-GOMES, Silvio Casimiro.—B. 1902; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.); govt. med. offr., Br. Guiana, 1926; senr. phys., pub. hosp., Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1938.

BETTINGTON, Digby Rowland Albemarle.—Ed. at United Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; served with Natal Mounted Pol., 1898-1901; with 2nd Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, 1901-1902 (dangerously wounded, ment. in desps., Queen's med. and 5 clasps); inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, 1903; inspr., pol., Sierra Leone, 1906; ag. consmr., pol. and sheriff on several occasions; dep.-comsmr. of pol. and dep.-supt., prisons, Gold Coast, 1910; base comdt., Togoland Field Force, 1914; "1914-15" Star, War and Victory meda.; title changed to dep. inspr-gen. of pol. and prisons, 1915; inspr.-gen. pol. and prisons, Gold Coast, 1917; inspr.-gen. pol., Gold Coast on separation of depts. of pol. and prisons, 1920; King's Police med., 1920; ret., 1924; commdt., pol., Bahamas, 1924; resigned, 1926; chief of pol. Bermuda, 1927.

BEVAN, Charles Edward, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1905; ed. Bromsgrove schl., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge and St. George's hosp., London; M.O., Nigeria, 1933; resigned in 1935 and joined Br. ambulance service in Ethiopia; reapptd. D.M.O., Cyprus, Oct., 1936; seconded as tuberculosis offr., Jan., 1938.
BEVERIDGE, Ian Alfred, B.Sc.—B. 1908; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; post grad. course, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1929; asst. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, 1930.

BEVIR, Anthony, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th Batt.
