King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in desps.; asst. prin., C.O., 9th Feb., 1921; accompanied Mr. Ormsby-Gore on his visit to the West African Colonies, Jan.-May, 1926; pte-sec. to Mr. Ormsby-Gore, Feb., 1926; prin., 1st Apr., 1929; sec., C.O. confere., 1930; seconded to offices of Cabinet, Aug., 1939; ag. asst. sec., 3rd Nov., 1939.
BEVIS, James Henry.—B. 1904; ed. Univ. Coll. and Inst. of Educn., London, B.Sc., hons. botany, dipl. educn.; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1927.
BBWES, George Price Hope.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; asst. dist. consmr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
BEYERS, Brig.-Gen. Leonard.—B. 1894; perm. staff, Union defence forces, 1914-32; dir., prisons, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1932.

BHARUCHA, Navroji Manekji, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1913; addnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, June, 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; offic asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Nov., 1917; dist. judge, 1922; asst. attlm. offr., May, 1925; dist. judge, 1927; offr. cls., I, Oct., 1937.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1905; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1923; staff offr., 4th Sept., 1929.

BIDEN, Claude Eric De Beer.—B. 1887; ed. Radley Coll.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1911; on active serv., attd. Nigerian Overseas Contgts., German E. Africa, and W. African Serv. Brig., 1917-19; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1930; mem., rly. retrenchment comtee., 1931; ag. audr., May, 1932; audr., Palestine and Trans-Jordan, May, 1933.
BIDMEAD, Kenneth Andrew.—B. 1909; pol. probnr., Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; asst. sup., pol., Aug., 1932; attd. Punjab pol., Jan.-Oct., 1935; asst. pol., Nov., 1939.

BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—B. 1889; ed. Dean Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; head of training coll., Oct., 1921; ch. inspr., educn., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1929; ag. asst. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1931, 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. dir., educn., Nigeria, 1934 and 1936; asst. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1936; ag. dir., Apl.-Sept., 1938.

BIGELOW, Henry Veeder, K.C., B.A., LL.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Truro, N.S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S. 1896, practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; K.C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., Feb., 1918.

BIGG, Wilfred Joseph.—B. 1897; boy clk., G.P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1922; asst. prin., 1st Aug., 1925; pte-sec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 3rd Feb., 1930; prin., 22nd June, 1931.

BILLING, ALFRED GEORGE, M.Q.I.S.—B. 1902; ed. Gram. Sch., Queensland, Australia; survey dept., Queensland, 1918-23; surv.-on-agr., F.M.S., Mar., 1924; asst. supt., Mar., 1928; title changed to senr. surv., Sept., 1938; ag. dep. ch. surv., S'pore., Sept., 1938.

BINGHAM, ROBERT PORTER.—B. 1903; ed. Dungannon Royal Schl. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1926; ag. 2nd asst. prot., Chin., S'pore, Oct., 1928; offr., ol. V., Jan., 1929; ag. D.O., X'mas Is., Dec., 1929; ag. 2nd asst. prot., Chin., S'pore, Apr., 1931; ag. asst. prot., Chin., Taiping, Nov., 1931; do., Malacca, Dec., 1932; offr., cl. IV., Jan., 1933; ag. prot. Chin., Kedah, Sept., 1933; ag. asst. prot., Chin., S'pore, Dec., 1935; offr., cl. III., Jan., 1938.

BINNIE, THOMAS REGINALD.—B. 1886; ed. Edin. Acad.; warehousekeeper, K. Lumpur, June 1921; supvr., cust., Oct., 1922; supt., cust., Oct., 1923; sr. supt., trade and cust., Jan., 1931; supvr., ext. duties, F.M.S. and asst. supt., rubber cont., S'pore in addn., Feb., 1938; dep. comnr., Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1933; title changed to asst. comptlr., cust., Dec., 1938.
BIRCH, W. C.—Asst. analyst, E.A.P., July, 1914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRCH, WILLIAM SOMERSET, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; served R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1914-19; sen. asst. med. offr., Jamaica ment. hosp., 1923-24; med. supt., do., 1925-26; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Barbados, 1927; med. supt., do., 1928; ag. oh. med. offr. for various periods, 1933-34.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERIC.—B. 1886; ed. Xaverian Coll.; asst. ch., cust., Zanzibar Prot., Oct., 1914; ag. ch., cust., Jan. to Aug., 1915, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917, and Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zan zibar (in Prize), Jan. to Aug., 1915 and from Dec., 1917; immigr. offr., Sept., 1915; dep. compt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1920; ag. compt., cust., on various occasions, 1921-29; dep. compt., cust., Nigeria, 1930; compt., Nov., 1931.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; war serv., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; asst. dist. judge, S'pore, Aug., 1922; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, July, 1925; asst. advr., B. Pahat, Aug., 1928; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1929; ag. stamp and prob. offr., S'pore, Aug., 1933; ag. dep. treas., S.S., May, 1934; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1934; offr., cls. I., 1938.

BIRKINSHAW, FRANK.—B. 1887; asst. agrl. supt. and sc. mast., gram. schl., St. Vincent, Aug., 1912; agrl. instr., Mauritius, Jan., 1915; inspr., co-op. credit socs. in addn., Oct., 1915; agrl. supt., Sept., 1919; asst. agrl. inspr., Perak, F.M.S., June, 1920; agrl. field offr., Perak North, Dec., 1924; ditto P. Wellesley and Penang in addn., Apr., 1926; sr. agrl. field offr., Perak, Sept., 1931; state agrl. offr., Jan., 1934; ch. field offr., Aug., 1937.

BIRKITT, FRANK.—B. 1885; Imp. postal serv. (tels.) 1898-1902, mails, 1903-07; admtrv. (cler. and acctg.) 1908; staff record offr., 1909; postmtr., Nyasaland Prot. (various offices) 1912; (active serv., 1914-15, N.V.R., Nthn. Rhod. Forces), ag. asst. P.M.G. various occasions; 1st cl. olk., 1920; ob. act. and sktr., 1923; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1927; temp. M.L.C. in 1932-34; ob. act., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory postal serv., 1935; senr. dep. P.M.G., 1936.

BIRSE, ARTHUR LOUIS.—B. 1893; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; collr., income tax, S'pore, Sept., 1921; treas., exec. cmte., Br. Malaya, Br. Emp. Exhibn., in addn., Jan., 1923; recvr., Alsagof Concession, Kubub, Mar., 1926; offr., cl. III., June, 1930; ; offr., cls. II., June, 1935; dist. offr., Larut and regtr., titles, Perak, Feb., 1936; dist. offr., Kinta, Sept., 1938.

BIRTWISTLE, WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Blackburn; asst. naturalist (fisheries), min. of agr. and fisheries; directed research in dept. of oceanography, univ., Liverpool; fisheries economist, S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1924; offr.-in-ch., fisheries dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1928; attended 5th Pacific Science Congress, Vancouver, B.C., S.S. and F.M.S. govt. deleg., June, 1933; dir. fisheries, S.S. and offr.-in-ch., fisheries dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1937.

BISCOE, VINCENT FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., June, 1908; 2nd lieut. London Rifle Brig. (Territorial Force), June, 1909; called to bar Inner Temple, Nov., 1910; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1919; ag. station mag., Dec., 1924.

BISHOP, CECIL WILLIAM EDWIN, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1899; ed. Reigate Grammar Schl. and King's Coll., Lond. Univ.; war serv., 1918-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1922; ag. ex. engr., waterworks constr., 1935-36 and 1939.

BISSET, WILLIAM DAVID, O.B.E. (mil.), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1889; ed. Gordon Schl., Huntly, Aberdeenshire, and Allen Glen's Schl., Glasgow; engnr. lieut., R.N., Jan., 1914; engnr. lieut. comdr., R.N., Apr., 1919; engnr.-in-ch., Misida dockyard, Malta, 1916-19; H.M.S. "Egmont," 1920; asst. engnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1920; prin. engnr., 1933; ag. supt. engnr., for various periods, 1934-37.

BITHREY, WILLIAM BARREY.—B. 1888; ed. Peru's Schl., Alresford; B.S.A. pol., 1913; served with Rhodesia Column, S.W. Afr., 1914; seconded to Rhodesia Native Regt., 1916; supt., Nyasaland, pol., 1920; chmn., native language, exams. bd., comnr., pol., ch. inspr., prin., prin. immigrn. offr. and regtr., motor vehicles, 1938.

BLACK, ARCHIBALD MAULE.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; ent. div. serv., Cape Colony, 1895; addnl. asst. regtr., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1903; asst. regtr., 1904; regtr. and taxing mast., sup. ct., Grahamstown, 1912; regtr. and taxing mast., sup. ct., Cape Town and high sheriff for prov. of Cape of Good Hope, 1928; on active service, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; awarded V.D., 1921; major, Union R. of O. (arty.).

BLACK, JOHN ALAN.—B. 1891; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; offr., cls. V., 1917; offr., cls. IV., 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Dec., 1926; ag. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1929; do., Dec., 1930; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; ag. immigrn. offr., Jan., 1933; offr., cls. I.B., Jan., 1936; pres., munie. comm., Penang, July, 1937.

BLACK, JOHN GRAHAM.—B. 1896; cadet, B.S., May, 1920; pol. mag., asst. dist. judge and dep. regtr., sup. ct., Malacca, 1920; passed exam. June, 1921; coroner, Malacca, Mar., 1923; collr., law rev., May, 1923; offr., cls. V., May, 1923; prov. sec. to O.A.G., S.S., May, 1925; asst. dist. edir., Tapah, June, 1926; ditto, Kroh, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. IV., May, 1927; asst. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1927; res., Labuan, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1932; ag. Br. res., Brunei, Jan., 1937; offr., cls. II, May, 1937.

BLACK, THOMAS.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19, 8th Hussars (France); supt., accts. and corresp., port development dept., Hong Kong, 1926; acctnt., treasry., 1928; sec., exchange fund advy. comttee., in addn., 1936; acct.-gen., 1937, and sec., exchange fund adv. comttee. in addition.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers' Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agr. Coll., 1905-15; sec., economic and development comm., of Canada, 1915-16; comsnt., of agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., soldier sttlmt., bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigr., and colonization, 1921; man., colonization dept., Canadian National Rly., London, 1923; ch. colonization dept., C.N. rly., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKBY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1902; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; house surg., R. Infirmary, Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg., offr., dist. infirmary, Ashton-under-Lyne, 1924; house surg., London Lock Hosp., 1925; asst. med. offr., Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. spec., in 1929 and 1930; med. offr., R.A.M.S., Nyassaland, 1931; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1936; ag. surg. spec., 1939.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, K.C., B.A. (sen. Mod.), LL.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Stonyhurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; do. Roman Law; 1st of 1st cls., hon., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; called to Irish Bar, 1912; active serv., European War; crown counsel, E.A.P., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1923-31; mem. comsnt., on riots, S.E. Provs., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1932; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLACKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—B. 1906; ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; R. Australian Navy, 1924-30 (paym.-lieut.); ret., 1930; corres. clk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931; ag. acct., in 1933, 1935, 1936, 1937 and 1938; ag. asst. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), Aug., 1936; office asst., New Hebrides, Apr., 1938.

BLACKWELL, KENNETH RAY.—B. 1896; ed. Shebbear Coll., N. Devon; war serv., R.F.A., July, 1915-Nov., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; attchd., Pahang sect., Apr., 1921; asst. supt. chandu monop., Ipoh, June, 1922; supvr., cust., Johore, Aug., 1923; offr. cls. V. asst. dist. offr., Gopang and Lampar, Nov., 1923; passed inter. L.A.A., Dec., 1925; asst. dist. offr. Kroh and asst. supvr., cust., Jan., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Nov., 1927; ag. supt., inspect. estabtn., F.M.S., in addn., Feb., 1928; offr. cls. III, Nov., 1932; state treas., Perak, Feb., 1934; chmn., sany., bd., Larut in addn., Oct.-Nov., 1934; ag. sec., res., Perak, May, 1935; state treas., Perak, Aug., 1935; ag. offl. assignee and regiar. coys., F.M.S., Nov., 1937; offr. cls. II, Nov. 1937.

BLAINE, CHARLES HERBERT.—Ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, and Dover Coll. (Eng.); served, S.A. War, 1901-02; Great War, 1914-18; ment. in desps., clk., ct., Christiana, Transvaal, civ. serv., 1902; pub. pros., mag.'s office, Johannesburg, 1906; 1st clk. to A.R.M., Germiston, Jan., 1908; ditto and asst. mag., Krugersdorp, 1909; ditto, Barberton, 1912; senr. clk. and senr. pub. pros., Johannesburg, 1919; addnl. mag., Roodepoort, 1920; ditto, Durban, 1923; ditto, Johannesburg, 1924; 1st gde. mag. and native comsnt., Marico at Zeerust, 1929; senr. gde. mag., Zoutpansberg at Louis Treihardt and native comsnt., 1931; under sec., justice, Pretoria, July, 1934; sec., just., 1936.

BLAIR, HON. ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1876, ed. Wellington Coll. (N.Z.), and Canterbury and Victoria Univ. Colls.; judges associate, 1893-98; admitted barrister and solr., 1899; experience in legal offices, 1898-1910; in practice, 1910-28; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928.
BLAIR, HAROLD ARTHUR.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; gold, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; asst. dist. comsnt., Nov., 1928; dist. comsnt., 1937.

BLAIR, HON. SIR JAMES WILLIAM, K.O.M.G. (1935), KT. BACH. (1930)—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.L.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1902-15; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministries, 1903-7, also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instr., Denham ministry, 1912-15; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1926.

BLAKE, GEORGE STANFIELD, B.Sc. Geol., Lond., A.R.S.M. Lond., M.I.M.M.—B. 1876; ed. R. Schi. of Mines, London; on teaching staff, 1896-97; Imp. Inst. (mineral survey of crown cols.), 1901; Newfoundland and Canada, 1909-15; munitions inspect., Eng., 1916-19; geol. staff, Imp. Coll. of Sci., 1920-22; geol. advr., Palestine govt., 1922.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; elk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. registr.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. elk., registr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. registr.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; comsnt. of sup. ct., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. registr.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAKEMORE, WILLIAM LESLIE, M.B., Ch. B. (Birm.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (L'pool).—B. 1901; ed. Wolverhampton Gram. Schi. and Birm. Univ.; M.O., Malaya, July, 1928; ag. senr. health offr., Penang, May, 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Aug., 1937.
BLANCKENBERG, PIETER BOTHA.—B. 1904; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Prov. Divn.; pvt. sec'y, Rt. Hon. Genl. J. C. Smuts, 1933-39; asst. dir., prisons, Union of S. Africa, Feb., 1939.

BLAND, CHARLES HEBER.—B. 1886; ed. pub. and high schl., Pembroke, Ont., and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.A. (1907); ent. Canadian civ. serv. at first compet. exam., 1909; served with Can. Field Arty., 1917-19; asst. sec., civ. serv. comsnt., 1919; ch. examnr., 1921; civ. ser. comsnt., 1933; chmn., 1935.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Indian Army, R. of O., Apr., 1915; active serv., N.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned Indian pol., Jan., 1919; dist. supt., pol., 1920-24; ch. of international pol., Tangier, July, 1928; asst. to res. comsnt., New Hebrides, Sept., 1929; ag. res. comsnt., New Hebrides, for various periods, 1930-39; attd. C.O., May, 1933; res. comsnt., New Hebrides, 1940.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1896 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high comsnt. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high commnr., 1912; sec. to high commnr., 1919; ag. high commnr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); mily. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comm. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., C.O., 1924; pvt. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

BLEDSLOE, 1ST VISCOUNT (or 1935) RT.HON. SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926), G.C.M.G. (1930); K.B.E., (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agril. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govs.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comm. on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. comm., land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930-36; chmn., N. Rhodesia R. comm., 1938.

BLELOCH, IAN WILLIAM, M.A. (Hon.) Edin.—B. 1901; ed. Dunfermline High Schl. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1926; offr., cl. V, 1929; offr., cl. IV, Jan., 1933; off. cl. III, 1938; sec., res., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1938.

BLOFELD, THOMAS GUEST.—B. 1902; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal and Bedford Schl.; supt. power stations, elec. dept., Ceylon, 1928; asst. engr., elec. dept., F.M.S., 1933; inspr., machinery, mines dept., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLONDIN, LIEUT. COL. THE HON. PIERRE EDOUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; ed. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, C.M.G., (1934), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradahapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office asst., Anuradahapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office asst., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th asst. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regir. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admstd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1934; ag. gov., in 1934, 1936 and 1937; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1935; ag. gov., 1938 and 1939.

BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem.; B.A., 1910, 1st cls. hon., Latin, 2nd cls. hon., math.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl. ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-25; vice-prin., Aobimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BLUNT, DENZIL LATTON, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1891; ed. Shrewsbury and King's Coll., Camb.; served under court of wards, Madras, 1913-14; served H.M. Forces, 1915-19; lieut., R.A.S.C.; senr. agrl. offr., Kenya, 1926; asst. to dir., agr. Kenya, 1930; dir., agr., Cyprus, Aug., 1933; do., Nyasaland Prot., 1937; nom. mem., leg. coun.; dir. agr., Kenya, 1940.

BLYTHE, WILFRED LAWSON.—B. 1896; ed. Birkenhead Inst. and Lpool Univ.; war serv., 1916-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., Malaya, Feb., 1937; on spec. duty in Chinese Prot., Oct., 1937; offr. cl. II, Apr., 1938.
BOAST, HENRY WALTER—B. 1887; clk. Swaziland, 1909; regir. and mast., sup. ct. and sheriff, 1928; dep. asst. commr., 1930.

BODENSTEIN, HELGARD DEWALD JOHANNE, LL.D. (Leyden), barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—Cik. mag.'s offices, Krugersdorp, Lydenburg, Pietersburg, 1909; Piet Retief, 1910; sec., judge pres., J. de Villiers, 1910-12; ch. clk. and professional asst. to atty-gen., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1912; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Amsterdam, Aug., 1912; astd. dr., "Die Burger," 1920; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1922-1927; sec. to prime min. and for external affrs., 1927.

BODILLY, COMMDR. RALPH BURLAND, R.N.—B. 1884; ed. Bradfield Coll. and H.M.S. "Britannia," 1899; midshipman, China station, 1901; sub. lieut. Atlantic Fleet, 1905; lieut., Home Fleet, Jan., 1907; invalided, 1909; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; on war serv., Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; lieut. commdr., Jan., 1915; promoted commdr. for war services, Nov., 1918; rea. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston Ct. on various occasions, 1927-31; acted in appellate ct. on various occasions, 1928-31; pros., marine and water etc.; ch. mag., Palestine, Oct., 1932; author of "Interaction between Vessels."

BODKIN, GILBERT EDWIN, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1886; ed. at Repton Schl., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Cambridge Univ. diploma of agric., 1910; govt. economic biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agrl. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; rep. Br. Guiana at W. Indian Agrl. Conf., 1913; ast. dir. of sci. and agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; joint editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; govt. entomologist, Palestine, June, 1922; offr.-in-ch., Locust campaigns, 1928-30; pres., Internat. Locust Bureau, Damascus, 1930-31; dir., agr., brin., coll. of agr. and regir., co-op. credit socs., Mauritius, 1932; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana and Palestine, scientific rept., to govt., etc.

BOISSIKR, CAPT. JOHN SCOBELL, R.Sc. (hons.), M. Inst. W.E., B. 1894; ed. Exeter Schl. and Birmingham Univ.; ast. engr., F.M.S., Aug., 1920; sec. for ser. in Johore, Jan., 1934-Sept., 1939; exec. engr., P.W.D., Aug., 1929; exec. engr., rural, S'pore., Nov., 1934; senr. exec. engr., June, 1936, and senr. exec. engr., Kinta, Nov., 1937; ag. dep. D.P.W., S.S., Feb., 1938.
BOK, DR. WILLEM EDUARD, JUNR.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. to prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of A...

BOLAND, FREDERICK MALCOLM, LL.B.—B. 1891; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; mag., County of St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1926; mag., eastern dist., Jan., 1927; asst. mag., St. George West, 1933; mag., Victoria, 1934; ag. police judge, May-Dec., 1935; ag. mag., St. George West, Dec., 1936 to Aug., 1937; ag. judge and ag. ch. mag., various occasions, 1938 and 1939.

BOLTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Dipl.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class.exhbr.); B.A. (hons. chem.), 1920; dipl. educ., 1921; lab. ast., H.M. factory, Sandycroft, 1916; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

BONAVIA, WALTER JOSEPH.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-1917; clk., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1917; asst. polit. offr. (cadet), Oct., 1920; adm. offr., 2nd grade, Oct., 1923; ag. ditto, 1st grade, Feb. and Nov., 1923 to Aug., 1925; dist. offr., Oct., 1931; ag. asst. adminstr.-gen., Aug., 1935; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1939; seconded to secr., Mar., 1939.
BOND, MAJOR B. W., M.C.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, May, 1920; dist. offr., 1922.

BOND, RALPH NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office ast., Badulla kach., Jan., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1931; do., Kandy, June, 1933; do., Kurunegala, Dec., 1934; landing surv., H.M. customs, 1936; ag. dep. collr., cant., Mar., 1938.

BONE, MAJOR CHANDOS ERIC, M.C., F.S.I., L.S. (Vict.).—B. 1889; survr., 1st grads., rev. surveys beh., Perak, Oct., 1913; lieut., R.F.A., May, 1916; capt., Apr., 1918; major, Aug., 1918; ast. supt., rev. surveys Selangor, Nov., 1920; supt., surveys, Trengganu, May, 1925; ast. supt., surveys, Spore, Sept., 1927; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., 1933; ag. ast. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., May, 1934; ast. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1935; supt., surveys, Malacca, Jan., 1937; ch. survr., Sel. and N. Sembilan, May, 1938.

BONHAM-CARTER, GEN. SIR CHARLES, K.C.B. (1935), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O., A.D.C. (1917).—B.1876; ed. Clifton Coll., R.M.C., Sandhurst and Staff Coll.; hon. LL.D., Malta, 1937; served S. African war, 1900-01 (Queen's med., 4 clasps); European war, 1914-18; (D.S.O., C.M.G., bvt. col., officer, Legion of Honour, American D.S.M.); maj.-gen., 1926; dir. staff duties, W.O., 1927-31; comdr., 4th divn., 1931-33; lieut.-gen., 1933; dir.-gen., Territorial army, 1933; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1936; col., Queen's Own R. West Kent Regt., 1936.
BONNET, PHILIP HERBERT.—B. 1896; ed. Townsville Gram. Schl., survey pupil, Australia, 1915-21; survr.-on-agr., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ast. supt., Aug., 1927; ag. ch. survr., Trengganu, May, 1938.

BONYUN, E. C. B.—B. 1885; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados and privately; copyist, pol. mag.'s courts, Dist. "A," Barbados, Jan., 1903; 2nd elk., registn. off., Barbados, Sept., 1906; ag. dep. registr., from Jan. to Feb., and Sept. to Nov., 1910; ch. elk., registr. off., Nov., 1913; supervisor of customs, Nigeria, July, 1914; ast. recvr., sup. ct., Nigeria, for the purpose of winding up affairs of German and Austrian firms, Nov., 1914; hon. ast. sec. to West Indian Contingent Comtee. (whilst on vacation leave of absence, and with permission of the S. of S.), Sept. to Dec., 1915; ag. supt. of customs, 1st grade, Feb., 1916; ast. censor, Bonny, Feb., 1916; censor, Bonny, May, 1916; ag. recvr. of enemy estates, Nigeria, June to Dec., 1916, and Jan., 1917; collr., 2nd grade, headqrs., in 1917-18; hon. sec., comtee., Nigerian comforts for overseas forces, Sept., 1917 to June, 1918; in charge of Port Harcourt cust., June-Nov., 1918; ag. supt., 1st grade, Degema, July, 1919 to Jan., 1920; ag. collr., Lagos, Feb.-May, 1920; ag. senr. collr., Lagos, May-July, 1920; collr., Victoria, July, 1921.

BOOKHAM, MAURICE, M.B.E.—B. 1889; imp. prison serv., 1913-27; war serv., 1914-19; dep. ast. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1927-33; ast. supt., pol., Br. Honduras, 1933-38; supt., prisons, 1938; do., Br. Guiana, 1938.

BOON, HERBERT M.B.E. (1935).—Copyist, treasy. dept., St. Kitts, May, 1908 to Mar., 1910; 2nd elk., admstr.'s office, Apr., 1913; ch. elk., post office, St. Kitts, May, 1915; elk. to admstr. and elk. exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1924; has held various ag. appts.; treas., St. Kitts-Nevia, May, 1935; admstr., Antigua and fedl. sec., Leeward Is., 1940.

BORC, ANTHONY J., M.D., Malta.—B. 1889; medical offr., Fiji, 1916; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Ra, 1917; ag. R.M.O., med. supt., pub. lunatic asylum and visiting med. offr., Suva gaol, 1919; D.M.O., Nadroga, 1920; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Nadi, 1921; ditto, Levuka, 1923; ditto, Labasa, 1924; D.M.O. and dist. commnr., Ra (conjoint), 1929; D.M.O., Ba, 1934.

BOSMAN, ANDREW MURRAY.—B. 1888; ed. Endrecht High Schl., Pretoria; Ontario Agrl. Coll., Univ. of Toronto; lectr., animal husbandry, Potchefstroom Schl. of Agric. 1913-15; vice princ., 1915-17; prof. animal husbandry and dean, faculty of agric., Univ. of Pretoria, 1917-33; ch., divn. of agric. educn. and extension, dept. of agr. and forestry, and prof., animal husbandry, Univ. of Pretoria, 1934; dir. of animal and crop prodn., dept. of agr. and forestry, 1939.

BOSMAN, ARNOLDUS JOHANNES, B.A. (Univ. of S.A.); D.H. (Rotterdam); comml. sec., S.A. legn., The Hague, 1929; comml. counsellor, S.A. legn., Berlin, 1934; under sec., dept. of commerce and industries, Pretoria, 1937.

BOSTOCK, H. K.—B. 1892; ed. Repton; L. & N.W. Rly., Crewe, 1909; on milly. serv., 1914-19; divnl. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928; supt., motive power and equipment, 1934; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., July-Dec., 1938.

BOSTON, HUGH MERRICK.—B. 1893; ed. Wallasey H.E. Schl., New Brighton, Liverpool Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.), 1925; served with 2nd W. Lancs. (98th County Palatine) Field Ambulance (T.F.), 1914-19; med. offr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Apr., 1928.

BOSWELL, ALEXANDER BOSWELL SIMPSON.—B. 1901; ed. Edin. Univ.; B.Sc. (For.) (Edin.); ast. conserv., Forests, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; ag. state forest offr., Perak North, June, 1932; ag. conserv., forests, Kedah, Aug., 1933; state forest offr., Perak North, June, 1936.

BOTHA, COLIN GRAHAM, V.D., M.A.—B. 1883; ent. atty., gen.'s dept., Cape Colony, 1901; serv. in offices of civ. commnr., Cape, and mast. of sup. ct.; in charge, archives of Cape Col., 1912; ch. archivist, Union of S. Africa, 1919; accompanied Sir Reginald Blankenburg as pvt. sec. to first assembly of League of Nations, Geneva; hon. or corresponding mem. of several hist., lit. and genealogical socs. of Gt. Britain and Holland; contributed articles to various jnl., mags. and newspapers; author of "Place Names in the Cape District," 1917; "A Brief Guide to the Documents in the Cape Archives, 1652-1906," 1918; "The French Refugees at the Cape," 1919; rept. of tour to various archives in Europe, Canada and U.S.A., 1921; "Social Life in the Cape Colony in the 18th Century," 1927; "Place Names in the Cape Province," 1927; "Public Archives of South Africa, 1652-1910," 1928; "Our South Africa: Past and Present." BOTHÁ, MARTHINUS CHRISTOFFEL.—B. 1886; ed. George and Stellenbosch Univ.; ent. dept. of posts and tels., 1902; teacher, 1906; B.A. (Cape), 1914; M.A. (U.C.T.), 1919; lect. in Afrikaans, Univ. of Cape Town, 1918; prof. of Afrikaans, ditto, 1921; supt.-gen., educn., Prov. of Cape of Good Hope, Sept., 1929; secy. for Educn., July, 1934.

BOTHAMLEY, CHARLES MILDMAY.—B. 1891; ed. Terrace Schl. and Wellington Boys' Coll., N.Z.; comtecl. clk., N.Z. H. of R., 1917; legis. coun., 1919; 2nd clk. asst., legis. coun., 1924; dep. clk. of parltns. and clk. asst., legis. coun., 1931; clk. of parltns., clk., legis. coun., and examnr., petitions for pte. bills, 1934.
BOTTOMLEY, JOHN HUBERT A.M.I.Struct.E., F.R.S.A.—B. 1889; ed. The Salt Schls., Shipley, Bradford Schls. of Art. and Bradford Tech. Coll.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; capt., H.K.V.D.C.

BOTTOMLEY, SIR WILLIAM CECIL K.C.M.G. (1930), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd clk., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comtecl., 1905; sec. to inter-deptl. pensions comtecl., 1907; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 16th Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, 1st Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Lucas, 24th Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 26th Oct., 1911; 1st cls. clk., 1913; principal clk., 8th Dec., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., E. African Currency Bd., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 1927; senr. crown agent for the cols., 1st July, 1938.

BOUCAUD, JOSEPH KAROL.—B. 1904; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad; admitted solr., 1927; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, 1933; dep. regisr. and marshal, Jan., 1935; regisr.-gen., 1936.

BOUCAUD, JOSEPH ERWIN ADOLPHE, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.), M.B., B.S. (Lon.).—B. 1892; asst. regisr., col. hosp., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1919; regisr., col. hosp., San Fernando, 1923; regisr., col. hosp., P.O.S., 1928; sen. med. offr. (surgeon), col. hosp., P.O.S., 1935.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—Asst. dist. comsusr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; 1st grade admstive offr., Jan., 1924; prov. comsusr., 1934.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1937), K.C.M.G. (1934), K.B.E. (1931), C.M.G. (1924).—Ed. Tonbridge and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mag., United Provs., India, Nov., 1908; under-secr. to gov't., Mar., 1913; regisr., high ct. Allahabad, May, 1915; appd. to I.A.R.O., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), Mar., 1918; judi. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; settlement offr., Baghdad, Mar., 1919; polit. offr., Baquba, Oct., 1920; polit. sec. to high comsusr., Jan., 1921; dep. sec. to high comsusr., Dec., 1921; sec. to high comsusr., Jan., 1922; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1929; O.A.G., Apl. to Sept., 1930; ch. sec., July, 1931; gov. and comdtr.-in-ch., Uganda, 1932; do., Nigeria, 1935; gov. gen., designate, Sudan.

BOURDILLON, HENRY TOWNSEND.—B. 1913; ed. Rugby Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schl.); 2nd cls. lit. hum. pt. I, 1933; pt. II, 1935; appd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 6th Oct., 1937; pvt. sec. to perm. under S. of S., D.O., 14th Aug., 1939.

BOURKE, PACET JOHN, LL.B., B.A. (Dublin), Barrister-at-law, King's Inns, 1928.—B. 1906; ed. Mount St. Mary's Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; honis., legal and polit. sci.; practised Dublin and Western circ., 1928 to 1933; legal advr. and crown prov., Seychelles, Mar., 1933; ex-officio mem., exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. just., July, 1933 to Mar., 1934 and May to Dec., 1935; ch. mag., Palestine, Apr., 1936; ag. ch. regisr., sup. ct., Feb. to Apr., 1938; edr., Digest of Cases, Seychelles, 1870-1935.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDONALD.—B. 1886; examiner, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1906; ass't auditor, N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; ass't audr., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1920; audr., Somaliland Prot., July, 1921; audr., B. Guiana, Oct., 1923; senr. ass't audr., Nigeria, July, 1927; audr., Sierra Leone, 1930.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLYNNE, KNT. B.A.C. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Lin. Inn., Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comm. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst., C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902; ret., 1912.

BOVELL-JONES, LIONEL CLIFFORD.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; served in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; demob., 1919; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, July, 1920; dist. inspr., 1924; offr.-in-ch., punitive expdn. to Venezuelan bndry., 1925; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1927; county inspr., 1931.

BOWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912, also depts. of col. sec. and regisr.-gen.; 3rd cls. clk., atty.-gen's off., 1915; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1918; clk. to regisr.-gen., 1919; sec'y, riot inquiry comn., 1919; ag. regisr.-gen. and cust. enemy pty., and contr., L.C.O., 1920; sec'y, yellow fever inquiry comn., 1921; ag. regisr.-gen., 1921, 1923 and 1925; regisr.-gen., regisr., lands titles, supr. vice-adm'y. and bankery, cta., companies, patents, births, marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp commr., dir., pub. offr., guarantee fund; comsusr., income tax, 1927; sec. in arbitrn. between govts. of U.S.A. and Guatemala, 1930; dist. comsrr., Belize, offr., rec. in bankruptcy, offr., admstr., estates, coroner, 1933; ag. asst. col. sec., 1934; postmr. (in addn.), 1935; col. postmr., 1938.

BOWEN, JOHN CAMPBELL.—B. 1872; ed. pub. schls. of Ontario, Brandon Coll., and McMaster Univ.; enlisted 63rd Canadian Overseas Batt., 1915; served in France; mem. for Edmonton in legis. of Alberta, 1921-26; for a time leader of liberal party in legis.; lieut.-gov., Alberta, Mar., 1937.

BOWEN, MAJOR ROBERT LEONARD, M.C.—B. 1881; ed. Wellington Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; K.R.R.E., 1914-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstr. offr., cls. II., 1936; cls. I., 1938.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high comsrr., for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high comsrr., 1884-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; adminstr. govt. on various occasions, 1900-09; ret. 1910.

BOWLEY, WALTER, C.B.E. (1931), B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (clss. triplos, 1899); apptd. clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Nov., 1901; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1902; ag. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local audr. in 1903, 1904, 1905 and of amalgamated territories, Lagos-S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. local audr., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1907, to Mar., 1908; local audr., Gambia, Apr., 1908; local audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1909; title changed to audr., 1910.

BOWMAN, HUMPHREY ERNEST, C.M.G. (1936), C.B.E. (1898).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (2nd clss. mod. hist. 1902); B.A., 1902; M.A., 1906; ent. Egyptian min. of educn., 1903; seconded to Sudan govt. (dept. of educn.), 1911-13; dir., Egyptian students in U.K., 1913-14; serv. with Br. Army, 1914-20; B.E.F., France, 1915-16; staff capt., H.Q., N. Command, 1916-18; India, 1918; Mesopotamia, 1918-20; ret. with rank of major, 1920; seconded from M.E.F. as dir. of educn., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; seconded to gov't. of Palestine, 1920-23; dir. of educn., Palestine, 1920; mem., advisory coun., since 1920; chmn., comsrr. on crafts and industries, 1922; mem., transliteration comtce., 1923; chmn., comtce. on transliteration of geographical names, 1924; chmn., bd. of higher studies.

BOWRING, SIR CHARLES CALVERT, K.C.M.G. (1925), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B. C. Africa, Sept., 1895; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., June, 1899; treas., E. A. P., Oct., 1901; mem., exec. and leg. couns., E. A. P., 1907; ch. sec. to govt., E. A. P., Apr., 1911; ag. govr., Feb. to Oct., 1912, Aug. to Oct., 1913, Apr., 1917 to Jan., 1919, and from Nov., 1919; gov., Nyasaland, 1923-1924.
BOWRING, HON. SIR EDGAR RENNIE, K.C.M.G. (1934), KT.BACH. (1915).—B. 1858; mem. legis. coun., Newfoundland, 1897; high comsrr. for Newfoundland in London, 1919.

BOWYER, JOHN HERBERT, M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), D.P.H. (Trin. Coll., Ireland).—B. 1901; med. and health offr., S.S., Apr., 1925; health offr., S'pore., Oct., 1930; do., Kelantan, Oct., 1931; ag. dy. state med. and health offr., Selangor, Feb., 1935; superscale offr., grade B, Oct., 1935; ag. state med. and health offr., N. Sembilan, Apl., 1937; prin. med. offr., Sarawak, June, 1938.

BOYCE, E. A., B.Sc., A.M.Just C.E., etc.—B. 1898; asst.engnr., P.W.D., Uganda Prot., 1925; asst. engnr. in charge Mbale dist., 1926; ditto, N. Prov., 1927; dep. D.P.W., Bahamas, Aug., 1928; ag. D.P.W. in 1930, 1932 and 1935; D.P.W., Br. Honduras, 1936.

BOYCE, HON. FRANCIS STEWART, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1872; M.L.C., N.S. Wales, 1923-32; min. without portfolio and mem., ex. coun., 1924-25; atty.-gen. and viceregs., ex. coun., 1927-30; judge, sup. ct., Aug., 1932.

BOYD, EDMUND BLAIKIE, C.M.G. (1935), C.V.O., (1938).—B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; apptd. by civ. ser. comsrs. under reconstrn. scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., May to Sept., 1921; sec. inter-deptl. comtce., income tax in cols., 1922; priv. sec. to parly. under-sec. (Mr. Wood, now Viscount Halifax), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormsby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec. Imp. Inst. comtce. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin. 2nd Nov., 1925; sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. development advisory comtce., 1929; pvte. sec. to Lord Pasefield as S. of S. for Doms. affrs. and for theCols., 1st Apr., 1930; pvte. sec. to successive secs. of state for theCols. (Mr. J. H. Thomas, 26th Aug., 1931, Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister (now Lord Swinton), 9th Nov., 1931, Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, 7th June, 1935, Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Nov., 1935, Mr. Ormsby Gore, 29th May, 1936); asst. sec., 19th Aug., 1937.

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1893; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1906 to Dec., 1911; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. asst., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; acctnt., post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. dep. P.M.G., Jan.-Sept., 1927; ag. P.M.G., Sept.-Dec., 1927; col. postmr. and supt., tels., Fiji, Jan., 1928; title changed to P.M.G., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1929; nom. comsrr., currency, Feb., 1930.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A.(1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on war serv., 1915-19; asst. contrlr., labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1923; registr., co-op. societies, May, 1923; asst. offr. in ch., co-op. soca., S.S., Nov., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; asst. dir., co-oper. F.M.S., and S.S., Dec., 1927; on sp. deputation to India, Nov., 1928; ag. dir., co-oper. F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1930 and Apr., 1933; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dir., co-oper. S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1933.

BOYD-SHANNON, GODFREY ECCLESTON.—B. 1907; ed. Wellington and Cambridge; schol., St. John's Coll., 1926; 1st cls. class. triplos, pts. I. 1928, pts. II. 1929; Browne med., 1928; B.A., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., 27th Oct., 1930.

BOYES, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Prince Albert Coll., Auckland; joined N.Z. pensions dept., 1903; regis., pensions, 1910; asst. comsrr., 1919; comanr., 1929; joint pub. serv. comanr., 1936-39; chmn., social security comanr., 1939.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Stitts., 1893; resig., 1895; ast. treas., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comanr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admstr., May, 1901 to Jan., 1902; appt. coll. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; ast. sec., Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comanr., E.Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comanr., Jan., 1905; ag. dep. comanr., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; adminstg. govt., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V.H., O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1883; ed. Bedford; apptd., after compet. exam, olk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; ast. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on ap. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. as finan. sec. to high comanr., Iraq., Mar.-Dec., 1924; acct., 1st Sept., 1930; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932; prin., 1st Apr., 1937.

BRACE, IVOR LLEWELLYN, B.L.—B. 1898; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1930; ditto, Nigeria, 1931; crown coun., 1932; ag. solr.-gen., July-Oct., 1937; ast. judge, prot. ct., 1937.

BRADFORD, SIR JOHN ROSE, Bart. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schlr.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.
BRADLEY, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Malvern and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1925, M.A., 1928; cadet, Uganda, 1930; ast. dist. offr., 1932; transf. to Somaliland, 1937.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.—B. 1901; ed. Wellington Coll.; clk., col. audit dept., June, 1925; ast. audr., Hongkong, Sept., 1925; do., Uganda, 1936; serv. ast. audr., 1936.

BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, O.B.E. (1933), L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.; M.D. (Bruz.).—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late ast. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; ast. med. offr., Seychelles, Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd diva., South Mahé; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr., 1924.

BRADSTREET, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; ent. C.A.'s office, June, 1902; ast. head, checking and insurance dept., May, 1919; do., gen. dept., Jan., 1924; sec., Zambesi Bdg. inter-deptl. comtee., 1928-31; dep. head, finance dept., Mar., 1931; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. clk., 1933; head, appts. and passages dept., 1935.

BRADY, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Sch., Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., Feb., 1904; inspr. of schls., Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-20; commanded 4th and 13th batts. K.R.R.C.; ment. in desps.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; ag. dir. of educn., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.

BRAGG, ALGERNON.—B. 1890; col. audit dept., Apr., 1915; ast. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915; myl. audit, E. African Expedy. Force, 1916-19; ast. audr., Uganda, 9th May, 1919; senr. ast. audr., Uganda, Sept., 1924; ast. audr., Uganda, for various periods, 1926-27; prin. ast. audr., Nov., 1928; ag. audr., May to Nov., 1929 and Mar.-Nov., 1933; dep. audr., Kenya, 1931; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935; do., Kenya, 1938.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Ast. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917; dist. offr., Kenya, 1921.

BRAITHWAITE, ELDERD CUBWEN, O.B.E. (1936), M.B., B.S. (Dunelm), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H., B.Hy. (Dunelm), M.S. (Dunelm), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1885; ed.
