BRANCH, J. R. A.—B. 1894; ed. Antigua Grammar Sch. and McGill Univ.; served in great war (3 meda.); sub-inspr. Leeward Is. pol., 1927-30; inspr., pol. 1931.

BRANCH, STANLEY, M.B.—Ch.B. (hons.), Edin., 1898; D.T.M., Liverpool, 1907; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1899; Uganda, 1900; St. Vincent, 1903; house surg., St. Lucia, 1904; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1912; seconded as dir. under Internat. Health Bd. campaign for eradication of hookworm disease, St. Lucia, 1914-24; ch. med. and health offr., St. Vincent, 1926.
BRANCO, ANTHONY NICHOLAS.—B. 1884; clk., secretariat, Cyprus, 1907; ast. regir. and interp., dist. ct., 1913; examr., acots., 1926; local ast. audr., 1927; ag. audr. for various periods, 1928-39.
BRANDON, KENNETH REVIE.—B. 1888; clk. of cts., Jamaica, 1919; ag. registr., sup. ct., Apr. to Dec., 1921; ag. reg. mag., Oct. and Nov., 1932 and Nov., 1924 to Aug., 1926; res. mag., Sept., 1926.

BRANIGAN, PATRICK FRANCIS, Barrister at law (Gray's Inn and King's Inn, Dublin).—B. 1906; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Downing Coll., Cambridge (T.A.S. Course); B.A. Univ. (Dublin), 1928, 1st cl. hona. (gold med.) legal and polit. science; lat Victoria prizeman, and cert. of hon. at call to bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1927; Eastern circ., 1928-30; admative offr. (cadet), Kenya, July, 1931; ag. crown coun., Kenya, 1931 to 1934; crown coun. Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934; add., legal advisory staff, D.O. and C.O., Dec., 1934 to Jan., 1935; ag. solr.-gen., 1936 and 1937; solr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1938.
BRANSON, VICTOR CECIL, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., B.Sc., F.I.O.—B. 1896; 2nd ast. analyst, Hong Kong, 1924; ag. analyst, 1929 and 1933; analyst, Sept., 1932.

BRASHER, WILLIAM KENNETH, B.A., M.I.E.E.—B. 1897; ed. Clifton Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; R.E. (signals) 1915-19; France; engr., Marconi's W.T.Co., 1920; ast. engr., P.O., Br. Guiana, 1922; ch. engr. and elec. inspr., 1928; exec. engr., P. and T., Iraq, 1929; ch. engr., P. and T., Palestine, 1933.

BRASNETT, NORMAN VINCENT, M.A., Dipl. For. (Cantab.)—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schol. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A., 1914-18 (capt., despa.); asst. cons. forests, Kenya Col., 1920; senr. A.C.F., Kenya, 1928; cons. forests, Uganda, 1929.
BRAZAO, ALFRED CASIMIRO.—B. 1905; called to bar, 1930; crown prosr., B. Guiana, 1934, 1936; ag. stip. mag., 1934, 1935, 1936; stip. mag., Nov., 1937.

BRAZEL, CLAUDE HAMILTON, A.M.I.E.E. A.M.I., Mech. E.—B. 1894; temp. 2nd lieut., R.E., 1914; S.M.R., Chatham, Apr.-June, 1915; lieut., 1915; adjt., R.E., 1916-17; capt., 1917; major, 1917-19; ment. in desps., 1917; Belgian Croix de Guerre, 1917; M.C., 1918; "1914-15" Star, G.S. and Victory med.; France, 1915-19; major, R.E. (T.), 1920; Nat. Defence Force, Apr.-July, 1921; dir., elec. dept., Bahamas, 1926; oh. engrnr. and man., elec. undertakings, Ceylon, 1934.

BREARLEY, L.J., M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1901; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1901; conf. elk., 1st Jan., 1918; staff offr., 7th Apr., 1933.

BREBNER, GEORGE GUSTAV RADLOFF, M.A. (Edin.), LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1883; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; pvt. sec. to Gen. Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Peace Conference, 1919; pvt. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; asst. crown pros. Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. asst. to atty.-gen. Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.
BRETT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseas office, 1919.
BREWIS, HELEN ELIZABETH STUART.—B. 1904; ed. Sherborne Schol. for Girls, Dorset, Froebel Educ. Inst. Training Coll. for Teachers, Roehampton; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1927.

BRIANT, ANDREW KERR.—B. 1903; ed. Dauntsey's Schol., Downing Coll., Cambridge, and Imp. Coll. Trop. Agric., Trinidad; M.A. and Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), A.I.C.T.A.; C.O. agric. schol., 1926; lecturer in mycology and bacteriology, I.C.T.A., Trinidad, 1928-33; asst. to commr. of agric., do., 1933-34; agric. aupt., St. Vincent, 1934-38; agric. offr., Zanzibar, 1938.

BRIDGE, BLACKER FRANK, B.A. (Dublin), Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1930.—B. 1891; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; asst. contr. of lab., June, 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Bentong, Apr., 1922; dep. contr., lab., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, June, 1929; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1930; offr., cls. II., 1931; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Nov., 1932; regist., dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Apr., 1937.

BRIDGES, MAJOR WILLIAM FRANCIS NOEL, D.S.O.—B. 1890; ed. Melbourne C. of E. Gram. Schol. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; suvr.-on-agreement, F.M.S., Sept., 1912; served A.L.F. Galilipoli and France, 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; attd. suvr.-genl's office, Jan., 1928; ag. asst. suvr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1929 and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931; asst. suvr.-gen., Dec., 1931; dep. suvr.- gen., June, 1933; seconded as dep. contr. rubber, May-Aug., 1934; suvr.-gen., Malaya, 1937.

BRIERCLIFFE, SIR RUPERT, KT.B.A.GH. (1930), C.M.G. (1936), O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., B.S., D.P.H. (Manc.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1889; dep. dir., health, Palestine, 1920; dir., med. and sanry. services, Ceylon, July, 1930; dir., med. services, Nigeria, 1936.
BRIERLEY, FRANK NORRIS.—B. 1887; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., June, 1906; warden, county of Caroni, Mar., 1933; do., E. counties, Apr., 1936.
BRIND, WALTER GERARD, D.F.C., M.Sc. (Hon.) (N.U.I.), B.E. (N.U.I.).—B. 1897; transf'd from S. Rhodesian govt. as govt. eugnr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1928; D.P.W., 1938.

BRINK, Lieut.-Gen., ANDRIES JACOB EKSTEEN, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpfu (name) campaign, 1898; Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1920; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st mounted brig., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; temp. lieut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. lieut.-col., whilst so empfd., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., Sept., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs. 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr. No. 6 mil. dist., Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as asst. adjt.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; maj.-gen., Nov., 1929; sec. for defence and G.O.C., Union Defence Forces, May, 1933; dir., civ. aviation, Nov., 1934; transf'd., R. of O., Apr., 1937; commdt.-in-ch., Burger commandos, Apr., 1937; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med.; D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in desps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, FRANCOIS JOHANNES.—B. 1889; ed. Riebeek West Pub. Schol. and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; war serv., 1914-16; Union of S. Africa pub. serv. as asst. inspr., mines, Brakpan, 1925; dep. inspr., mines, Germiston, 1929; inspr., mines and mining commr., Bloemfontein, 1934; inspr., mines, Transvaal, 1935.

BRINK, COLONEL GEORGE EDWIN, D.S.O.—B. 1889; served Great War, German S.W. Africa campaign as staff offr., headqrs., Southern Force, 1914-15; German E. Africa campaign as staff capt. and brig. major, 1st S.A. Mounted Brigade, 1916-17 and on G.H.Q. staff, 1917-18; graduated Staff Coll., Camberley, 1921; comdt., S.A. Milly Coll., Roberts Heights, 1932; O.C., troops, Roberts Heights, 1933; O.C., Cape commd., 1933; dir., army training, Union defence forces, Pretoria, 1937; Croix de Guerre (French); ment. in desp. (3 times).

BRINSLEY, GEORGE GERARD.—B. 1902; temp. asst. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1921; forest survr., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1928; survr., lands dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1932; attd. P.W.D. as supervisor, Falmouth harbr. improvement, Oct., 1934; do., Kingston and Port Antonio harbrs. improvement, Sept., 1936.
BROCK, DENIS GEORGE, B.A.—B. 1916; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1934-38); hist. tripes, B.A., 1937; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1938.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDWARD LEWIS, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; cadet, Straits Stltmte, 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; comnstr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907 to Feb., 1908; ag. res., Perak, Apr., 1908; res., Pahang, Nov., 1908; res., Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1910; col. sec., S. Stltmte, 1910; chief sec., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Sept., 1920; apptd. agt., Malay States Infr. Agency, Nov., 1920.

BROEKSMAN, ABBEOTHEIN HERMAN, K.O., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1895; ed. Boys' High Schl., Stellenbosch; awarded Rhodes scholarship, Univ. of Stellenbosch; called to bar, Cape Town, 1924; senr. asst. to atty. gen., Cape Town, 1931; law advr. dept. of just., Feb., 1933; chmn., disciplinary appl. bd., dept. of S.A. rlys. and harbrs., Apr., 1933; legal advr. to dept. of prime min. and of external affrs., Sept., 1933; mem., S.W. Africa comm., 1938; K.C., 1937; attended impl. Confce. as law advr., 1937; sec. for defence, Sept., 1937.

BROMLEY, Rear-Adml. ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. Farnborough Schl.; H.M.S. Britannia; joined navy, 1892; lieut., 1898; comnmdr., 1908; capt., 1910; served European War, 1914-18; H.M.S. Courageous, 1916-19; flag capt. to Vice-Adml. Napier commdg. light cruiser force; ret. list, 1922; rear admr., ret., 1926; a gentlemen usher to the King since 1927; pvt. sec. (ceremonial and reception) to the S. of S. (Lord Paasfield), 1931.

BROODBANK, ALFRED JOHN BERTIE.—B. 1888; dist. treas., N.Borneo serv., Aug., 1921 to Aug., 1931; asst. audr., in addn., Dec., 1924 to Apr., 1926, and Oct., 1928 to July, 1929; ag. audr., Aug., 1931 to May, 1932; dist. treas., May, 1932; asst. commr. cust., in addn., Mar., 1933; ag. commr. cust. and excise, Apr., 1934; commr. cust. and excise, Apr., 1935.
BROOKE, NEVILLE JOHN.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; seconded to O.C., 1928; census offr., N. Provs., 1930; res., 1930; judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.

BROOKES, VICTOR S.—B. 1888; passed senr. Cambridge local exam., 1906; apptd. copyist, col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1906; cashier, St. John's savings bank, Sept., 1908; acctnt., ditto, June, 1909; ag. acctnt., treasy. dept., June to Sept., 1909; clk. to comnstr. and exec. coun., Virgin Is., Apr., 1911; rev. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1914; acctnt., treasy., St. Kitts; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; ag. treas. and ag. mem. exec. and leg. couns., Aug.-Oct., 1934; ag. treas., Sept.-Oct., 1937 and Apr.-Oct., 1938.

BROOKS, ARTHUR CECIL, M.C., B.Sc.(N.U.I.), A.R.C.S.C.I., A.I.C.—B. 1889; ed. St. And.Coll., Dublin, R. Coll. of Sci. for Ireland and Birm. Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, S.S., July, 1924; dep. govt. analyst, Penang, Oct., 1935; sr. asst. analyst, S'pore, Oct., 1937.

BROOKS, R. L.—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse; Artists Rifles; comnmd., Guards' Machine Gun Regt., 1920; passed inter. exam., Survr.'s Inst.; dipl., forestry, Edinburgh, 1922; asst. conserv., forests, Uganda, 1922; seconded to adminstr., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1925; ag. conserv., forests in 1925, 1928, 1931 and 1933; conserv., forests, May, 1934.

BROWN, CHARLES CUTHBERT, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schlr., 1909; Bell schlr., 1908; 1st cls. class. tripes, 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; asst. dist. offr., Brusa, 1912-13; asst. sec. to res., Perak, 1913-15; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat and priv. sec. to ch. sec., 1916-19; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1919; dist. offr., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1931; Br.advr., Pahang, June, 1933; Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1936.

BROWN, CAPT. ERIC GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1890; ed. Burton-on-Trent Gram. Schl., Malvern Coll., and B'gham Univ.; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly.s., Oct., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade II., Oct., 1924; ditto, grade I., Oct., 1928; dist. engrnr., Jan., 1936.

BROWN, FRANK LESLIE, M.C.—B.1896; ed. Wilson Gram. Sch.; served in France, 1916-19; lieut., M.C. bar (ment. in desps.); prob. N Rhodesia, Oct., 1919; asst. native comnstr., Feb., 1922; native comnstr., Oct., 1926; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as asst. sec., Aug., 1931; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935.

BROWN, GERALD HORACE.—B. 1875; ed. Ottawa pub. schls. and Coll. Inst.; journalist and party correspondent; asst. dep. min. of labour, Canada, 1909-17; sec., reconstruction and development comtee.; re-apptd. asst. dep. min. of labour, 1920; Canadian del., League of Nations, Internat. Lab. Confce., Geneva, 1921.

BROWN, GORDON DIXON, F.R.G.S., commdr. R.D., R.N.R. (retd.).—B. 1894; merc. marine, 1910-13; R.N., 1913-25; 2nd offr., K.U.R. & H., 1925; asst. hydrographic survey offr., 1926; hydrographic offr., 1929; commdr., 1936.
BROWN, SIR HARRY PERCY, K.C.M.G. (1938), M.B.E., M.I.E.E.—Dir.-gen., posts and tels. Australia.

BROWN, HARRY WHIDDEN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1868; ed. Acadia Coll., and Dalhousie Univ.; called to Nova Scotia bar, 1893; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1896, as priv. sec. to min. of militia and defence; dir. of contracts, militia dept., 1904; commr., war purchasing comm., 1919, returning to militia dept., on the dissolution of that comm., 1920; asst. dep. min. of militia, 1920.

BROWN, HON. JAMES T., K.C., B.A.—B. 1871; ed. pub. and high schls., Huntingdon, Quebec, and McGill Univ.; called to bar, 1896; K.C., 1907; elec. to Sask. legislature, 1905; judge, sup. ct. of Sask., 1910; ch. just., ct. of King's Bench, 1918.

BROWN, JOHN BATCHELOR.—B. 1904; ed. Shaftesbury and H.M.S. "Worcester"; R.N.R., 1921-22; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; senr. asst. treas., Mar., 1937; surrenq. offr. and ag. acctnt.-gen., Dec., 1937-Sept., 1938; seconded as finan. offr., Aden, 1939.

BROWN, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1888; ed. Hillmartin Schl. and Coll. de Boulogne S/Mor.; entd. Crown Agents' Office, 1907; asst. sec. Palestine currency bd., 1926; sec., 1930; sec. Iraq currency bd., 1931-33; asst. head, finance dept., Jan., 1934; do., appts. and passages dept., May, 1934; dep. head, appts. and passages dept., 1933.

BROWN, KENNETH VINCENT.—B. 1890; ed. St. George's Coll., Weybridge; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; mag., eastern dist., Trinidad, Dec., 1924; asst. mag., county of St. George West, 1928; mag., county of Victoria, June, 1930; do., St. George West, 1932; ag. judge in 1933, 1935 and 1936; 3rd puisne judge, June, 1936; 2nd do., 1938.

BROWN, MONTAGUE, M.B.E. (1933), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), A.C.I.S., A.L.A.A.—Office supt. and sec., gen. agric. coun. and comte., on agr. economics and marketing; office, commrs. of income tax and super tax, 1915; 38th Bn. R. Fusiliers, 1918-19; Palestine gov't. treas., 1920; dept. of agr. and forests, 1922; sec., Palestine trade delegn. to Egypt, 1935.
BROWN, PHILIP PENTON, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 2nd cls. hona., philos. and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BROWN, RALPH PHELPS.—B. 1877; ed. pub. and high sch., Nova Scotia and Boston; 2nd cls. elk., militia dept., Can., 1904; 1st cls. elk., 1907; asst. acctnt., 1910; asst. acctnt. and paym.-gen., 1917; ch. acctnt., 1918; gen. audr., 1920.

BROWN, ROBERT ALLAN.—B. 1897; ent. P.O. savings bank, Dec., 1912; trans. to gen. bd. of control for Scotland, Feb., 1914; apptd. asst. clk. after compet. exam., Dec., 1914; junr. elk., 26th Apr., 1915; on mil'y serv., July, 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transferred to C.O., 27th Apr., 1922; seconded as acctnt., treas., Cyprus, 1928 to 1930; treas., acctnt., Gambia, Aug., 1930; asst. recr. gen., 1933; ag. recr. gen., June to Oct., 1934; J.P., 1936; senr. acctnt., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Gold Coast, June, 1937; ag. ch. asst. acctnt.-gen., various periods, 1937, 1938, 1939.

BROWN, THOMAS ALGERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A.; 2nd cls. hona., jurisprudence; active serv. Wavistern Field Force, 1920-22, 16th Cav., India Army; called to bar (Inner Temple), Nov., 1926; Midland Circuit, Cent. Crim. Ct., London Sess. (North and South); crown couns., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. atty.-gen., Jan., 1938; ag. solr.-gen. for various periods; attd. legal advisory staff, C.O., July-Aug., 1939.
BROWN, WILLIAM TWERDIE STODART, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1903; ed. St. Bees Sch., Edinburgh Univ. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. cons., forests, Gold Coast, 1926; senr. do., 1939.
BROWNE, GEORGE GILHAM TONK, A.C.A.—B. 1898; acct., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1924; acct., P.W.D., S.S., Apr., 1932; senr. acct., F.M.S., Aug., 1934.

BROWNE, RAYMOND HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; Midland circ.; advoc., Uganda bar, 1925-28; dist. mag. and coroner, Antigua, July, 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., on occasions; offl. mem., legis. coun., Antigua; res. mag., Jamaica, Nov., 1930; ch. registr., sup. ct. and prot. ct., Nigeria, 1936; registr., W. African ct. of appl., 1936.

BROWNE, ROLAND PAUL LAMBERT.—B. 1888; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; served as "clave consulaire" at British Consulate, Danzig, June, 1908 to July, 1909; asst. to res. comsnr., New Hebrides, Feb., 1912; comsnr., Cyprus, Jan., 1924; attd. col. sec.'s office in 1932 and 1934; admstve. offr., Jan., 1935; clk., exec. coun. in 1935 and 1936; ag. ch. asst. sec., various periods, 1936-37; ag. col. sec., July-Aug., 1936; comsnr., 1938.

BROWNE, VERNON DENNISON.—B. 1892; secretariat clk., N.E.R. adminstr., May, 1900; mag. clk., N. Rhodesia (Fort Jameson) Aug., 1911; treasy. clk., Mar., 1913; asst. acct., Mar., 1914; served in R.A.F., 1918-19; senr. asst. treas., N. Rhodesia Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas. on various occasions, 1927-34.

BRUCE, HON. R. RANDOLPH, B.Sc., C.E., F.R.G.S., LL.D. (Glas.)—B. 1863; ed. The Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen and Glasgow Univ.; emigrated to Can., 1887; on eng. staff, Can. Pac. Rly., 1887; silver-lead mining Br. Columbia, 1897-1926; lt.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1926-31; pres., Mine Owners Assn. of Interior, 1923-25; hon. col., 16th Can. Scottish; do., 29th Vancouver Regt.; Canadian minister, Tokio, 1936.

BRUCE, CAPT. THE RT. HON. STANLEY MELBOURNE, C.H. (1927), P.C. (1923), M.C. (1915).—Member of the H. of R. for Flinders since 1918; served in European War, 1915-17; capt., R. Fusiliers, Dec., 1914; twice wounded and invalided from the Army, Apr., 1917; Croix-de-Guerre, 1916; resigned chairmanship of firm of Paterson, Laing and Bruce, on appt. as Federal treas., Hughes ministry, Dec., 1921; prime min., Commonwealth of Australia, 1923-29; hon. min. and res. min. in London, Lyons ministry, 1932; leader Australian delegn. to Ottawa Conf., 1932; resigned from miny. and apptd. high comsnr. in Gt. Britain, 1933; re-apptd. high comsnr., 1938.

BRUCE, THOMAS DUNDAS HOPE, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; final hona., schl. of jurisprudence, 1906; admitted solr., sup. ct. of England, 1911; crown solr., Fiji, Nov., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1915; ag. chief pol. mag., ag. reg. of sup. ct., ag. reg. of titles, ag. curator of intestate and vacant estates, ag. public trustee, May to July, 1916; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1916; tempy. M.L.C., Aug., 1917; on active serv., Dec., 1917 to May, 1919; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1919 to May, 1920; dep. comsnr., Western Pacific, May, 1920; ag. res. comsnr., Gilbert and Ellie Is., May, 1920 to May, 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, May to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Apr., 1922; apptd. a judl. comsnr. for the W. Pacifico, Dec., 1921; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific High Comsnr. to preside over certain murder trials in the Solomon Is., Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1922; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1925; solr.-gen., Kenya, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1932 and 1933; King's proctor, Dec., 1928; puisne judge and mem., W. African ct. of appl., Gold Coast, Jan., 1937.
BRUCE, THOS. ROBERT.—B. 1862; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., St. Helena, Jan., 1898; postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.
BRUCE, SIR WALLACE, Kt. Bach. (1927).—B. 1878; ed. Prince Alfred Coll.; dir., many comnl. and indus. undertakings; lord mayor, Adelaide, 1925-27; chmn., S. Australian harbours hd.

BRUEN, JOHN PATRICK, B.E. (Dub.).—B. 1904; asst. engr., 1st Guiana, 1930; asst. engr., to see defence bd. of comsnrs., 1934; dist. engr., P.W.D., 1937; ag. dep. D.P.W. (sea defence matters), 1939; mem., local govt. bd. and central bd. of health.
BRUMAGE, DOUGLAS O.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1919; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Dec., 1930.
BRUNNEN, JOHN EWART.—B. 1893; war serv., H.M. Navy, 1914; admty., 1915; engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; asst. elec. engr., 1922; elec. engr., 1931.

BRUTON, CHARLES LAMB, O.B.E.—B. 1890; ed. Radley Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 1914; 1st gr. admstrve. offr., 1924; senr. dist. offr., 1936; prov. commr., 1936; res. commr., Swaziland, 1937.

BRYAN, SIR HERBERT, K.B.E. (1925), D.S.O. (1917), C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1866; 2nd lieut., Line regt., June, 1892; lieut., Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., July, 1899; brev. major, Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expeditn. to Bassena (ment. in desp., medal with two clasps); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expeditn., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., May 1902, to Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, Nov., 1903; col. sec., G. Coast, Feb., 1904; ag. gov., on various occasions, 1904-09 and 1911-12; col. sec. and rev. commr., Jamaica, Aug., 1914; served in France, Flanders and Salonika, 1915-18; Constantinople, 1918-19; bvt. lieut.-col., bvt. col., D.S.O.; ag. gov., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1919, 1922, 1924-25; ret.; 1926.

BRYANT, G.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; served with R.N.V.R., Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 24th Aug., 1927.

BRYANT, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (math. scholar); B.A., 1909; M.A., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1912; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., Klang, June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, S.S., Apr., 1915; ast. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1917; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1918; offr., cls. IV, 1st Jan., 1919; sec. to min. min. comrn., in addition, Aug., 1919; attd. for sp. duty, Federal secretariat, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; ag. ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1924 and Apr., 1927; temp. ast. prin., C.O., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1927; ag. chmn., sany., bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr. cls. I.B., June, 1930; pres., mun. comms., Penang, Oct., 1930; ag. res. coun., Penang in addn., July, 1932; offr. cls. I.A., res. coun., Malacca, Oct., 1934.

BRYCE, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1938).—B. 1885; ed. George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., D.Sc.; Abyssinia, 1907-09; Madagascar, 1910-12; ast. botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Ceylon, 1913-23; served with H.M. forces, France, Germany, 1917-19; dir., agr., territory of New Guinea, 1923-26; del. to pan-Pacific conf., Australia, 1923; dir., Rubber Research Inst., Malaya, 1926-29; del. to pan-Pacific sci. congress, Java, 1929; dep. ast. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1930; ast. dir., agr., 1936.

BRYSON, HUGH PATTERSON, M.C.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Sch., Armagh; war serv., 1916-19; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, 1917; France, 1918-19; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attached to dist. office, Kinta, B. Gajah; offr., cls. V, 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; offr., cls. V, attnt. offr., Trengganu, Apr., 1928; ag. sec. to res., Sembilan, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933; ast. advr., Segamat, Feb., 1935; do., Kedah, Apr., 1936; offr. cls. II., Apr., 1938.

BUCHANAN, JOHN CECIL RANKIN, M.D. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (E.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; mily. serv., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1928; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; S.M.O., Somaliland Prot., 1936; S.M.O., and port health offr., Aden, 1937.

BUCHANAN, JOHN KENNETH.—B. 1905; ed. Masonic Schl., Dublin; miny. of finance, N. Ireland, 1922-29; attd. Col. Audit Dept., 1929; ast. audr., Fiji, 1929-34; ag. audr., Fiji and W. Pacific, Apr.-July, 1929; ast. audr., Kenya, 1934-36; audr., Windward Isl., 1936.

BUCHANAN, LAWRENCE ALEXANDER COCKBURN, M.A. (Edin.), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1903; ed. Edinburgh Acad., Edin. Univ. and Oxford Univ.; teacher's dipl., London Univ., 1929; supervr., educn., Zanzibar, 1929; supt., native educn., Nyasaland Prot., 1937.

BUCHANAN-SMITH, SIR ALBERT, KT. B.A.C. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), M.C. (1918)—B. 1879; ed. Repton Schl.; Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; ast. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; ag. commr., lands, 1912, 1914 and 1916; attd., Nigeria Regt. in Cameroons 1914-15 and in E. Africa 1916-18; ag. commr., lands, 1916; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; res., 1921; ag. prin. ast. sec., S. Provs., 1921, 1923 and 1925; ag. sec., S. Provs. 1923 and 1925; staff grade, 1926; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1928-29 and 1930; lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1930; O.A.G., Nigeria, Sept.-Nov., 1930; ret., 1936.

BUCKLAND, LANGLOT LIDDLE ROBER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Oundle Schl., Univ. of Cape Town, S.A.; Artists Rifles, 1918; ast. engr., irrign. dept., S.A., 1919-28; ast. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; exec. engr., 1929; irrign. engr., 1938.

BULKELEY, GEORGE VICARY OWEN, C.B.E. (1932), M.I. Mech. E.—B. 1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Owen's Coll., Manchester; lst cls., rly. operating, Lond. Schl. of Economics; port man., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harb., 1926; gen. man., Nigerian rly., 1933; dir., transport, Nigeria, 1936; author "Mechanical Appliances for handling Railway Traffic" (1922), "Railway and Seaport Freight Movement" (1930).

BULL, HAROLD ROBERT, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open entrance scholar, Queen's Coll., 1907; foundation scholar, 1908; 3rd cls. Maths. Tripos, Part 1, 1910; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1911; attached to Chinese protectorate, Dec., 1911; passed cadet; offr. collr., stamp duties, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Apr., 1924; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1924; sheriff, Singapore, Mar., 1925; 2nd mag., do., Oct., 1925; ast. offl. assignee, do., Jan., 1928; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Apr., 1930; offr. cl. IB, Mar., 1932; dist. judge, Singapore, Sept., 1932.
BULL, OSWYN BOYS, O.B.E. (1939), M.A.—B. 1882; dir., Lerotoli tech. schl., Basutoland, 1931; dir., educn., 1935.

BULMAN, JOSEPH WAUGH, A.M.I.Loco.E.—B. 1893; ed. Petershill Pub. Schl., Glasgow; served in Navy, 1914-19; loco. foreman, Cyprus govt. rly., Nov., 1919; ast. loco. supt., Oct., 1927; supt., rly., July, 1935.

BUNBURY, CECIL MOLESWORTH.—Ed. privately; employed at Crown Bridge Works, W. Bromwich, 1895-1898; Great Central, Great Northern and City Rlyws., 1898-1900; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (medal with 4 clasps); Central S. African Rlyws., 1902-1904; ast. engr., Uganda Rlyw., 15th Jan., 1906; maintenance engr., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. engr., 1925.

BUNNING, ARTHUR JOHN FARRANT.—B. 1895; ed. High Sch., Newport (Mon.); G. W. R., 1912-14; S. Wales Borders, 1914-16 (France, 1916); rly. traffic off., France and Germany, 1917-1920; asst. traffic off., Nigerian Rly., 1921; dist. traffic supt., 1936; traffic man., Gold Coast rly., 1939.

BUNTING, BENJAMIN, N.D.A., F.L.S.—B. 1889; asst. agriculturist, F.M.S., Mar., 1914; ag. do., Dec., 1914; agriculturist, Mar., 1920; on sp. duty in Ceylon, Jan.-Feb., 1930; ag. ch. research off., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1936.
BUNTING, ROBERT HUGH, F.L.S.—Asst. dir. of agriculture, Sierra Leone, 1913-15; asst. dir. of agric. and govt. mycologist, Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions.
BURGE, G. H. K.—B. 1890; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Islands, Jan., 1915.

BURGESS, CLAUDE BRAMALL.—B. 1910; ed. Epworth Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford, B.A. (mod. greats); cadet, Hong Kong Oct., 1932; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Feb., 1935; asst. to supt. of impta. and expts., Aug., 1935; adm. asst. urban coun., Mar., 1936; pol. mag., June, 1936; 3rd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1937; dist. off., North, Dec., 1937; 2nd asst. col. sec., and dep. clk. of couns., June-July, 1938; supt., inland rev., Aug., 1938; sec., taxn. comttee., Dec., 1938; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Apr., 1939; in addn., clk. to compulsory serv. trib., Aug., 1939.

BURKE, LEONARD O'BRIEN (B. Econ.).—Learner, Capetown, 1913; asst. staff br., Pretoria, 1913; 3rd grade clk., do., 1914; 2nd grade clk., do., 1918; 1st grade clk., staff sect. (N. area), 1924; senr. clk., staff br. Pretoria, 1929; prin. clk., postal br., Pretoria, 1932; ch. clk., do., 1934; trans. to office of pub. serv. commn., 1934; pub. serv. inspr., grade II, 1934; asst. sec. (P.S.C.), 1935; under-sec., staff, posts and tels. dept., Pretoria, 1937.

BURKE-GAFFNEY, E.M.O'D.—B. 1887; ed. Belvedere Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; asst. engr., F.M.S., P.W.D., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1929; senr. exec. engr., Feb., 1936; ag. state engr., Kedah, Dec., 1937.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, H. J. O'D., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Duh.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; asst. bacteriologist, 1929; senr. pathologist, 1935.
BURNER, ERIC ANDERSON.—Ed. at Felsted Sch.; command., R.F.A., France, Belgium and Germany, 1915-18; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1920; dist. comsnr., 1924.

BURNETT, BERTRAM DAVIDSON.—B. 1891; ed. Port Elphinston Pub. Schl. and Inverurie Acad.; war serv., 6th Batt. Gordon Highlanders, 1914-19; foreman of wka., 2nd grade, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; headmast., junr. trade schl., Apr., 1924.

BURNETT, CAPT. FREDERICK, M.C., M.A.—B. 1890; ed. Ashwater Schl., Devon, and Oxford Univ.; war serv., R.E., France, 1914-19 (M.C. and bar); divl. agr. offr., Ceylon, 1925; asst. regisr., co-op. socs., 1926; vice-prin., agr. coll., Peradeniya, 1926; dep. dir., agr., Br. Guiana, 1930; rep. col. at confee. of dirs. of agr., London, 1938; land settlem., comsnr., Jamaica, 1938.

BURNS, SIR ALAN CUTHBERT MAXWELL, K.C.M.G. (1836). C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1887; apptd. to treasy. and cust. dept., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905; clk. to mag., Dec., 1909; ag. mag., Anguilla, 1910; dep. coroner and J.P., St. Kitts, 1911; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; supervisor of cust., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; asst. sec., Nigeria, June, 1914; col. sec., Bahamas, Feb., 1924; el. mem., H. of A., 1925-28; del. to W. Indian confee., London, 1926; adm. std. govt. on various occasions, 1924-28; dep. ch. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1929; acting ch. sec. and gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1929-34; gov. and commdr-in-ch. Br. Honduras, 1934; ag. asst. under sec., C.O., 7th Jan., 1940; on active serv. with W. African Regt., 1914-16; adjutant, Nigeria Land Contingent, 1915; served with Egba Exped., 1918; joint compiler, Index to Laws of Leeward Is., 1910; compiler, Nigeria Handbooks, 1917-23; author of "A History of Nigeria" 1929.

BURNS, R. E., LL.B., London (1908).—2nd clk., regisr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town comsnr., Basseterre, June to Dec., 1899; clk., P.O. Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treasy., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treasy., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. gov't. clk., clk., ex. and legis. couns., clk., bd. of health, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Navia, July, 1904; tariff clk., treasy., St. K.N., Oct., 1904; ag. audit clk., May, 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Oct., 1906, to May, 1907; 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Aug., 1907; jun. asst. treasy., July, 1908; asst. treasy., Mar., 1912; asst. censor (temporary) G. Coast, Aug., 1915; ag. senr. asst. auditor, Apr. to May, 1917; ag. treasy., May-July, 1920; dep. treasy., Aug., 1922.

BURNS, ROBERT.—B. 1912; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ. (M.A. hons. classics); apptd. after compet. exam. ast. prin., D.O., 7th Jan., 1936-Dec. 1939; prte. sec. to perm. under sec. temp. ag. prin., Dec., 1939.
BURNS, WILLIAM ALOYSIUS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1900; ed. St. Aloysius Coll. and Vety. Coll., Glasgow; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1923.

BURSIDIJE, ROBERT EDWARD WARRICK, R.A. (Hons.).—B. 1900; ed. Christ's Hosp. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; served R.A.F., 1918; headmast., N. Rhodesia, 1927; inspr. schls., Apr., 1931; ag. dir., European educn., 1932-36 and July, 1939-Jan., 1940.

BURROWES, BERTRAM, LL.B. (Hons.) Univ. Coll., London.—B. 1883; called to bar, Grays' Inn, 1920; treasy., Jamaica, 1903; judl. dept., 1915; clk., cts., 1921; judge, grand ct., Cayman Is., 1924; supernmy. res. mag., Jamaica, 1925; ag. res. mag., 1925; res. mag., 1926.

BURROWES, EDMUND, S.—B. 1906; ed. Queen's Coll.; cler. asst., col. sec's office, Br. Guiana, 1924; 6th cls. offr. cust., 1925; 5th cls. clk., col. secs. office, 1926; 4th cls. offr., 1928; asst. clk., leg. coun., 1929; 3rd cls. clk., C.S.O., 1933; 2nd cls., 1934; 1st cls., 1939.

BURROWES, HUGH.—B. 1909; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1935; dist. offr., Leeward Is., 1937; dist. offr. and mag., Dominica, chmn., Portsmouth town bd., j/c Carib. reserve, J.P., 1937; ag. mag., visiting just. of prisons, comsnr. for income tax, 1938.

BURROWS, WILLIAM.—B. 1883; ent. "Britannia," 1898; ret. from Navy with rank of commdr., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Jan., 1920; seconded Fiji, Feb., 1922; dist. comsnr., Fiji, Jan., 1923; prov. comsnr., 1925; seconded to W.P.H.C. as dep. comsnr., Fanning Is., Sept., 1928; ag. ch. pol. mag., Fiji in 1931, 1934 and 1936; res. comsnr., 1933; res. comsnr., Rotuma, 1935; redesignated adm. offr. gde. II, 1938.

BURT, NORMAN FRANK.—B. 1899; ed. Rugby Schl.; R. Mily. Academy, Woolwich and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1922; cadet, Tanganyika, Sept., 1925; asst. dist. offr., Sept., 1926; dist. offr., Sept., 1936.

BURT, ROBERT HILLHOUSE.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to C.O., 15th Mar., 1916; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Apr., 1923; staff offr., 1st Oct., 1936.

BURTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1893; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., 1908-1912; served in War, 1914-1918, Capt., King's Royal Rifle Corps.; Pembroke Coll., Camb., 1918-1920; plant breeder, agric. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921; senr. plant breeder and experimentalist 1931; in charge coffee team, 1937; chief research offr., 1939; ag. dep. dir. (plant industry), Apr.-Dec., 1933 and Mar.-Dec., 1935; ag. dep. dir. agric., July-Dec., 1937, July, 1938-Feb., 1939 and Aug., 1939.

BURTON, WILLIAM ELLIOT.—M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., 1910; ag. temporary res. surg., Barbados general hosp., 1910; ag. med. offr., St. James' parish, Barbados; ag. visiting physician, leper asylum; ag. surg. to police, dist. II., Barbados, 1911; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Leeward Is., 1912; inspr. public health, St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.

BUSHE, SIR HENRY GRATTAN, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.B. (1932), O.M.G. (1927)—B. 1886; called to the bar, 1909; Western circuit, 1910-11; S. Eastern circuit, 1911; Herts and Essex sessions, 1916; sec. to royal comm. on the contracts between the War Office and Sir John Jackson, Ltd., 1916-17; ag. legal asst., C.O., Nov., 1917; legal asst., 1st Jan., 1919; legal adviser to D.O. and C.O., 1st Apr., 1931.

BUTCHER, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. N.Z. state schls. and Waitaki Boys' High Schl.; ent. registr.-gen.'s dept., 1906; statistical branch, 1912; ch. compiler, census and statistics office, 1915; asst. gov't. statistician, 1929; gov't. statistician, 1932; N.Z. rep. at Br. Empire Conf. of statisticians, Ottawa, 1935; mem., Internat. Statistical Inst.

BUTCHER, PERCY GEORGE, B.A. (hons. Oxon.).—B. 1890; ed. Perse Schl., Cambridge and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Devonshire Regt., 1915-19; France, 1916-19; admstv. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, Aug., 1919; Arabic dipl. Schl. of Oriental Studies, 1927; asx. rev. Boro Prov., various periods since 1928; dep. res., Aug. 1934; admstv. offr., cls. I., 1936.
BUTLER, CHARLES OWEN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1932.

BUTLER, CHARLES RICE.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and at the Sorbonne, Paris; B.A.; supt., educ. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attchd. Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ch. inspr., S. Prov., 1930; ag. asst. dir., educ., S. Prov., 1932 and 1935; prin., Higher Coll., Yaba, 1933.
BUTLER, ERNEST CECIL.—B. 1900; clk., Basutoland, 1920; sub.-insp., B.M. pol., 1927; war serv., Apr., 1917-Mar., 1919; asst. dist. comsnr., 1937; dist. comsnr., 1938.

BUTLER, FREDRICK BRERY LEWIS, F.L.S.—B. 1890; ed. Shaw Schl. (Berks); ent. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1912; served in European War, 1915-19; agrl. instr., E.A.P., 1919; man., Mazetas and Kibos experimental farms, E.A.P., 1921; agrl. supervisor, Kenya, 1923; grader and inspr., Kenya, 1925; ch. grader and inspr. of produce, Cyprus, Jan., 1935.

BUTLER, G. G., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., B.C., Cantab. (hons. nat. sci. tripus), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (with distinction).—Apptd., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1911; seconded for sp. investigation under yellow fever comm., May, 1913 to June, 1914; served Cameroons Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916 to Apr., 1916; temp.-capt. (gen. list) Jan. to Apr., 1916; seconded for serv. with R.A.M.C., 1916-19; dir. of laboratory, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; ag. D.P.M.O., Mar., 1924; dir., med. research inst., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

BUTLER, HON. SIR RICHARD LATTON, K.C.M.G., M.P. (S. Australia)—M.P. for Wooroora, 1915-18 and since the elections of 1921; leader, partly, liberal party and of opposition, Dec., 1925; premier, treas. and min. of rly., 1927-30; leader of opposition, 1930-33; premier, treas. and min. of immigrn., 1933-39.

BUTTERFIELD, HARRY ROBERT FRANZEBICK.—B. 1896; ed. Westminster City Schl.; 2nd lieut., Middx. Regt., Mar., 1915; served Gt. War, 1914-19; 6th K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, April, 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; capt., K.A.R., R. of O., July, 1930; ag. resid. mag. and dist. registr., June, 1931-Sept., 1932; author "Index to Laws of Tanganyika Territory, 1935."

BUTTERS, HENRY ROBERT, M.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow High schl. and Glasgow Univ.; 1st bursar, 1916; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1922; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924; dist. offr., North, 1925; ag. dep. registr. and 2nd pol. mag. in addn., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1926; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1928; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1929; 2nd pol. mag., 1930; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., 1932 and 1933; pol. mag., Kowloon, Sept., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Oct., 1933; extra asst. col. sec., Jan., 1934; dep. clk., couns. in addn., Sept., 1934; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Sept., 1935; P.M.G., Mar., 1936; 1st pol. mag., Mar., 1938; lab. offr., Nov., 1938; cadet offr., cl. I., Nov., 1938; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., and mem., exec. and leg. couns., July-Aug., 1939; ehmn., urban couns. in addn., Sept., 1939.

BUTTERS, SIR JOHN HENRY, KT. BACH., M.I.E.E., M.Am.I.C.E., etc.—B. 1882; ed. Taunton Schl. and Univ. Coll., Southampton; ch. engrnr. and gen. man., govt. hydro-elec. dept., Hobart, Tasmania; chmn., Tasmanian state comte of the C'wealth advisory coun. of science and industry; pres., Tasmanian Inst. of Engrs.; ch. comsnr., fedl. cap. coman., Canberra, Nov., 1924; ret.

BUTTON, FRANCIS JENKINSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Burnley Gram. Schl.; war serv., East Lanes Regt. and R.T.C., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1919; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; exec. engrnr., Apr. 1929; sr. exec. engrnr., May, 1936.

BUTTRESS, ERIC FRANK, B.Sc. (Eng.), Lond., Dip. Eng., King's Coll., A.M.Inat.C.E., Chartd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1905; ed. County Schl., Windsor and King's Coll., Lond. Univ.; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.
BUXTON, C. E. V.—Asst. dist. comsnr. E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919; dist. offr., Kenya, 1922; ag. prov. comsnr., 1937; seconded to dist. admtr., Palestine, Sept., 1938.

BYNOE, ALLAN KENNETH.—B. 1881; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1900; ch. clk., pol. mag. etc., dist. "A," 1922; ag. pol. mag. of various dists. on several occasions, 1922-26; pol. mag. dist. "C," 1927; do., dists. "D" and "F," 1930.

BYRNE, Brig.-Gen. Sir Joseph A., G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1928), K.B.E. (1918), O.B. Military (1916).—B.1874; joined R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1893; served in S. African War, 1899-1902; wounded; served with central judicial (claims) comm., Pretoria, 1903-05; major, 1914; bt. lieutenant-col., 1915; brig.-gen., 1916; asst. adjutant-gen., W.O., 1916; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1916 (C.B. and despatches); imprt.-gen., R.L.C., 1916; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1922; gov., Seychelles, 21st July, 1922; assumed govt., 26th Sept., 1922; gov. and commodr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 1927; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Kenya and high commr. for transport, 1931; ret., 1936.

BYRON, Frederick Augustus William, O.B.E. (1938).—B. 1900; served in R.E. and Royal Corps Signals, 1918-1924; awarded M.S.M. and ment. in despatches, serv. on N.W. Frontier, India; wireless offr., Gold Coast, 1925; electrician in charge, elec. services, Falkland Is., 1930; broadcast offr., Sierra Leone, 1933; aspt., broadcasting, Gold Coast, 1936.
CABABE, Thales.—B. 1887; clk., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1910; ch. clk., secretariat, 1931; asst. postmtr.-gen., 1934; ag. P.M.G., various periods, 1934-39.

CADE, Reginald Courtney.—B. 1891; ed. Academie de Caen and in Germany; joined army, Aug., 1914; served in France, June, 1915 to Sept., 1916 and Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1918; lieutenant, gen. list, H. Qrs., Home Forces, till Mar., 1919; invalided out of army; junr. examr., clearing office, enemy debts), Sept., 1920; senr. examr., June, 1921 to Dec., 1929; temp. adm. offr., C.O., Feb., 1930.

CADMAN, 1st Baron (cr. 1937), Sir John Cadman, K.C.M.G. (1918), O.M.G. (1916), M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Newcastle-under-Lyme, and Durham Univ. Coll. Sc.; Staff, C.C. scholar, 1895; B.Sc. (Dunelm), hon., 1899; M.Sc., 1902; pres., Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1900; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffs., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. insp. of mines (H.O.), East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engnr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; ret., 1922; author of prize papers on "Iron Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

CAHAN, Hon. Charles Hazlitt, B.A., LL.B., LL.D., K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Yarmouth Seminary and Dalhousie Univ.; leader of conserv. party in Nova Scotia legis., 1890-94; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1925; re-el., 1926, 1930 and 1935; sec. of state in Bennett cabinet, 1930.
CAIN, Henry Oliver, M.B.E. (1937)—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; 21st Lancers 1910; Indian Sig. Serv., 1916-23; qmr. sgt., Somaliland pol. force, 1923; pay and qmr., 1928.

CAINE, Sydney, B.Sc. (Econ.)—B. 1902; ed. Harrow County Schl. and London Schl. of Economics; B.Sc. (Econ.), with 1st cls. hons., 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. insp. of taxes (Inland Rev.), 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Nov., 1926; prin., 8th Nov., 1931; visited W. Indies and B. Guiana as sec. W. Indian sugar commn., 1929-30; sec., U.K. Sugar Ind. Inquiry commitee, 1934; finan. sec., Hong Kong, June, 1937; ag. asst. sec., C.O., 1st Jan., 1940.

CALDECOTT, Sir Andrew, K.C.M.G. (1937), KT. Bach. (1935), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1884; ed. Uppingham (schol. and exhb.), and Oxford (schol.), 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1907; B.A., Exeter Coll., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; dist. offr., Jelebu, 1911; dep. contr., labr., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. Sec., 1916; seconded for sp. duty in connec. with Br. Empire Exhib., while on leave in England, Jan., 1923; town planning adminstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Mar., 1926; chmn. housing and P.W.D. enquiry counties, Dec., 1926; dep. contr., lab., Malaya but continues ag. under sec., S.S., July, 1927; offr. cls. Ia. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1928; ag. Br. rea., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1929; ag. Br. rea., Perak, Oct., 1930; ag. Br. rea., Selangor, May, 1931; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., July, 1931; supernumerary staff offr. of rank of ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; col., sec., S.S., Dec., 1933; O.A.G., S.S. and high commr., Malay States, Feb.-Nov., 1934; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Hong Kong, Dec., 1935; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Ceylon, 1937; Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem, 1936.

CALDER, James.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow Univ.; M.A.; war serv., 1916-19; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attd., treasry., S'pore, June, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore and asst. contrlr., rubber in addn., Aug., 1926; ditto, Penang, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1928; asst. dir., co-operat., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., B. Palat, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; mag., Seremban, May, 1935; do., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1935; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Nov., 1936; offr., cl. II, Apl., 1938; leg. advr., Trengganu, Sept., 1938.

CALDER, Hon. James Alexander, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. Ingersoll pub. schls., pub. and high schls., Winnipeg; Manitoba Coll.; hon. grad. in science, Manitoba Univ., 1888 (silver medallist); called to the bar, North-West Territories, 1906; prin. of Moosejaw high schl., 1891-94; insp. of schls., N.W.T., 1894-1900; dep. commr. of educn., N.W.T., 1901-05; elec. to Sask. ass. at first g.e., 1905; prov. treas. and commr. of educn. in Scott min., Sept., 1905; unsucc. cand. for Milestone div. at g.e., 1908; elec. for Saltcoats by-elec., Dec., 1908; min. of immigr. and colonization in federal union govt., Oct., 1917; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for Moosejaw, Dec., 1917; pres., Canadian privy coun., July, 1920; called to senate, Sept., 1921.

CALDER, John A., M.A., C.M.G. (1939).—B. 1889; ed. at Harris Acad., Dundee and Edin. Univ.; Vans Dunlop scholarship in history, 1910; M.A. with 1st cls. hons. in history, 1911; 1st cls. hons. in philosophy, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st cls. olk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th Sept. to 10th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited S. Africa, N. and S. Rhodesia, Nyasaland, Tanganyika, Zanzibar, Uganda and Kenya as sec., E. Africa Parly. Comm., Aug. to Dec., 1924; asst. sec., 27th June, 1933.

CALDWELL, Reginald Percival.—B. 1894; held various appts., Fiji, 1910-17; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R., France, 1917-19; 1st cls. clk., treas. and sec., returned sailors and soldiers land settlmt. scheme and returned sailors and soldiers advances bd. of control, 1922; asst. commr. for Fiji, Br. Emp. Exhib., 1924; col. audit dept., Dec., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1925; asst. audr., Uganda, July, 1930; ag. senr. asst. audr., Aug., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Dec., 1935; ag. dep. audr., May, 1937.
CALDWELL, ROBERT NIXON.—B. 1899; apptd. Fiji, 1906; served in N.Z. Expedy. Force, Samoa, 1914 and Imp. Army, 1915-18; lat ols. clk. and clk. to couns., 1916; dist. commnr., 1922.

CALLAGHAN, MICHAEL AUGUSTINE, D.S.O. (1916)—B. 1888; ed. Christian Brothers' Schl., Cork; temp. commn., 1915; war serv., Flanders, 1915, France, 1916-17 ("1914-15" Star, D.S.O., ment. in desp. 1916); with Brit. war mission, N. America, 1918-19; asst. polit. offr., 2nd gde., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1929-30; dist. offr., Mar., 1932.

CALLAN, HON. JOHN BARTHOLOMEW, K.C. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1882; ed. Christian Bros. schl., Dunedin, and Otago univ., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice many years; served in war as capt., 3rd batt., N.Z.R.B.; lecturer in torts, Otago univ., 1912-33; and dean of faculty of law, 1924-33; mem., coun. of legal educ.; formerly pres. Otago law soc.; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1936.

CALTHROP, LANCLOT HENRY CALTHROP.—B. 1904; ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1924; seconded as junr. dist. offr., Weihaiwei, 1926; asst. supt., pol., 1928; J.P., 1928; passed in Mandarin, 1928; attd., Punjab pol., 1930-31; passed in Urdu, 1931; supt., pol., 1936; passed in Cantonese, 1938.
CALVERT, HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., I.C.S. (ret.), B.Sc.—B. 1875; registr., co-op. socs., Ceylon, Mar., 1935.
CALWELL, H. G., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.) (Belf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.) (Gold Med., Diseases of Children, Belfast).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1931.

CAMACHO, FABIAN JOSEPH.—B. 1898; ed. St. Wilfred's Coll., N. Staffs.; army, France 1917-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mag. and legal advr., Montserrat, Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; mag., E. counties, Trinidad, Nov., 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1935; ag. mag., Victoria, 1936; ag. 2nd mag., May-July, 1937; ag. puisne judge, July, 1938; ag. 2nd mag., 1939.

CAMACHO, MAURICE VIVIAN.—B. 1885; ed. St. Edmund's Coll., Herts.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; prac. at bar, Rangoon (1907), Leeward Is. (1913-20); ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., in 1919 and 1920; atty.-gen., Dec., 1920; K.C., 1924; judge, West Indian ct. of appeal for hearing of certain appeals, 1924; ag. ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1928 to Apr., 1929 and Dec., 1930, to Jan., 1931; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr. to June, 1929 and Jan. to June, 1931; atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1931; ch. just., B. Guiana, 1938.
CAME, FREDERIC HAROLD ROBERTS, M.B.E. (1936), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Impl. Coll. of Sci., S. Kensington; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda, 1925; senr. do. 1939.

CAMERON, HON. ARCHIE GALBRAITH, M.P.—Served A.I.F., Great War, 1916-19; mem., H. of A., S. Australia for Wooroora, 1927-34; el. H. of R. Commonwealth of Australia for Barker, (S.A.), 1934; asst. min., Lyons ministry, Nov., 1937; P.M.G., Nov., 1938 to Apr., 1939; do., Page min., 7th-26th Apr., 1939; leadr., Country Party, from Sept., 1939.

CAMERON, SIR DONALD CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1872; ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov't., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. asst. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov't. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-6-7; temp. transf'd. to S. Nigeria as asst. sec., Jan., 1908; principal asst. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor trade inquiry comte., 1909; ag. prov. commr. in 1910, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb. to 24th Aug., and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912, deputy gov't. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; ch. sec. to gov't., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. gov't. in 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, Nigeria, 1931.

CAMERON, SIR EDWARD J., K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury Schl., Clifton Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxon.; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lea, gov', Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1883; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Stltmt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol, ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec. 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legia. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comsnr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1896, and Apr. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901; admstr., St. Lucia, Apr., 1909; ag. gov. Windward Is., June to Oct., 1909, July to Dec., 1911, and in 1912; representative of St. Lucia, and chmn. of W. Indian delegates at reciprocity conf. at Ottawa, Mar., 1913; gov., Gambia, Feb., 1914; assumed gov't., Apr., 1914; ret., 1920.

CAMERON, GEOFFREY VALENTINE, B.A. (Oxon.), 1st cls. hons. jurisprudence, barrister-at-law and former solr.—B. 1904; advt.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1894; legal advr., Aden, 1937; ag. ch. just., supreme pt., May-Sept., 1938.

CAMERON, MAJOR SIR MAURICE ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1914); C.M.G. (1900), late R.E.—B. 1855; first comsn. in Roy. Engrs., Ang., 1874; dept. col. engrnr., S. Stltmt., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted about two years and a half as col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, 1895-1920.
CAMERON, NORMAN EUSTACE.—B. 1903; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and Fitzwilliam Hall, Camb. (Br. Guiana schol., 1921), M.A. (Cantab); asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1934.
CAMILLERI, LUIGI A., LL.D.—B. 1892; matric., Malta Univ., 1907; LL.D., 1913; al. for legis. assem., 1921; mag., 1924; judge, 1930.

CAMPBELL, VERE BERNARD.—B. 1898; ed. Malvern Coll. and Roval Mily. Acad., Woolwich, 1915; R. Art'y., 1915-21; active serv. (France), 1916-18; asst. dist. comsnr., Nyassaland Prot., 1927-30; asst. treas., 1930-36; seconded, Somaliland Prot., 1936.

CAMP, ISAAC NEWTON, M.A. (Harvard).—B. 1890; attlt. offr., mily. serv., 1917-20; Br. rep. Es-salt, Trans-Jordan, 1920; dep. ch. Br. rep., Trans-Jordan, 1921; pres., demarcation commn., Beisan, 1922; admstr. offr. (seconded to stltmt.) 1927; land attlt. offr., 1936.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD, M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B. Rly. Co. 1906-11; engrnr. design dept., C.A. 1914; war serv., 1916-18; asst. engrnr., 1921; dep. ch. engrnr. (mech.), 1935.
CAMPBELL, COLIN BRUCE, B.A., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Leatherhead Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; aspt., educn. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, May, 1921.

CAMPBELL, DAVID CALLENDER.—B. 1891; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry and Edin. Univ.; asst. sec., Tanganyika, Dec., 1919; asst. ch. sec., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1936; ag. ch. sec., July-Dec., 1937 and Mar.-Nov., 1938.

CAMPBELL, SIR GERALD, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1923), Officer of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; 3rd Cls. Star of Ethiopia.—B. 1879; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (B.A. 1901); apptd. to consular serv., 1906; served Rio de Janeiro, Belgian Congo, Venice and Addis Ababa; consul-gen., Philadelphia, 1920; San Francisco, 1922; New York, 1931; apptd. high commr. in Canada for U.K., Oct., 1938.
CAMPBELL, HAROLD ARTHUR, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1891; war serv. Canadian Engrns., France, 1916-18; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, 1919; do., Nigeria, 1922; do., Kenya, 1926; exec. engrn., 1929.

CAMPBELL, JAMES EDWARD FRANCIS, C.B.E. (1937), M.A.—B. 1879; ed. Christ Church, Oxford; double 1st cls. hons.; called to bar, Middle Temple; Order of the Nile; served throughout great war; major, R. Artillery, 1916-18; ent. Palestine admtn., Mar., 1919; asst. dist. gov., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1920; dist. commr., Jerusalem dist., 1932.
CAMPBELL, JOSEPH GARDEN, A.M.Inst.C.E., Assoc. M. Am. Soc. C.E., Char. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1902; engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1930.

CAMPBELL, J. M., M.B., Ch. B. (Ed.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. (Liv.) (Alan Milne med.)—B. 1897; med. offr., Kenya, Nov., 1923; senr. health offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1930; D.D.M.S., N. Rhodesia, 1938; ag. D.M.S., June-Dec., 1939.

CAMPBELL, LACHLAN BAIN, M.C. (1918), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.)—B. 1882; ed. Auckland Grammar Schl. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; 30 years' serv. in N.Z. pub. wks. dept.; comsnr., borough of Napier (during restoration period following earthquake), 1931-33; sec., mar., 1933.

CAMPBELL, OWEN NEIL.—B. 1884; ed. Auckland gram. schl. and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1901; served overseas as lieut. N.Z. Engrns., 1916-19; ch. drainage engrn., lands dept., 1922; (dist.) comsnr. of crown lands, 1927; dep. under-sec. lands and survey dept., 1934; native trustee and under-sec., native dept., 1935.

CAMPBELL, S. MCNEILL.—B. 1893; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; M.A. (1st cls. hons. Classics) 1915; G.U.O.T.C., Sept., 1914; on military service, Jan., 1916 to Mar., 1919, with Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) 52nd Div. and R.A.F., Egypt and Palestine; lieut., July, 1917; capt., R.A.F., Nov., 1918; temporary olk., in C.O., Apr., 1919; apptd. by civ. serv. comsnrs., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir J. Masterton Smith), Apr., 1922 to Nov., 1923; ag. prin., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin., 1st Oct., 1925; sec., W. Indies Closer Union Comsn., Nov., 1932, Mar., 1933.

CAMPAGLI, LIEUT.-COL. GEORGE MURRAY (R. of O.), M.C., M.Inst.T.—B. 1890; ed. pvtly.; Vict. state rls., 1905-14; served in Australian Forces in Gallipoli and Palestine, 1914-18; ment. in despatches; capt. (staff capt.), May, 1917; dep. asst. dir., rly. traffic, 1918; asst. dir., ditto, 1919; offr.-in-ch., Palestine mily. rly., 1919; transf. to civ. govt. as asst. gen. man. Palestine rly., 1920; lieut.-col., R. of O., July, 1921; ag. gen. man, in 1922, 1924, 1926-1927-28; mem., advisory bd. to Palestine govt. for commerce and industry, Mar., 1928.

CAMPION, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O. (1917), T.D. (1914).—B. 1870; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; lieut.-col., 4th Batt., R. Sussex Regt., 1915; col., 1924; M.P., Lewes divn., Sussex, 1910-24; gov., W. Australia, 1924-31.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES, C.M.G. (1935), B.A., B.Sc., LL.D., F.R.S.C., F.G.S.A.—B. 1876; ed., St. John's Coll., Winnipeg; grad., Man. Univ., 1894; poet grad., geol., of Queen's Univ., Kingston, Harvard, and Massachusetts Inst. of Tech., Boston; geol., Algoma Cent. Rly., 1901; geol., Canadian Northern Rly., 1902-03; geol., geological surv. of Can., 1904; geol., investigations and explns. in Br. Columbia and N. Can., 1904-20; in charge of B.C. and Yukon br. of the geol. surv., 1918; dep. min., federal dept. of mines, 1920; apptd., Dom. fuel bd., 1921.

CANNON, HON. LAWRENCE ARTHUR DUMOLIN, B.A., LL.L., LL.D.—B. 1877; ed. Commercial Coll., Arthabaskaville, Que. (Can.), Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ.; gov.-gen'l.'s med., A.B., 1896; LL.L., 1899; LL.D., 1927; admitted to bar, 1899; K.C., 1910; M.L.A. (Que.), 1916 and 1919; mem. of comen. to revise and consolidate statutes of Quebec, 1923; batonnier, Quebec bar, 1924; hon. sec., Canadian Bar Assn., 1927; judge, King's bench (Que.), 1927; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1930.
OANSDALE, GEORGE SOFER, B.A., B.Sc. (Oxon).—B. 1909; B.A. Forestry (with distinc.), 1923; B.Sc., 1934; asst. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, 1934.

CAPNER, ARCHIBALD NORMAN, A.M.I. Mech. E.—B. 1891; ed. Chester Coll.; on war serv., 1914-19; mech. and elec. foreman, P.W.D., Palestine, Aug., 1922; elec. and mech. engrn., P.W.D., Cyprus, July, 1938.
CARBERRY, JOHN EDWARD DOSTON, LL.B. (McGill).—B. 1893; served in Great War, 1915-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1925; clk. cts., Jamaica, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1927-32; res. mag., Nov., 1932.

CARDIN, HON. PIERRE JOSEPH ARTHUR (Richelieu)—B. 1879; ed. Sorel Comm'l. Coll. and pvtly. teachers; studied law, Laval Univ.; batonnier Richelieu bar sec.; first elec. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1911; re-elec., g.e., 1917 and g.e., 1921; mem., privy coun. and min. of marine and fisheries, 1924; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, 1924; re-elec. at g.e., 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; resigned portfolio with King Cabinet, 1926; re-apptd. min. of marine and fisheries, Sept., 1926; min. of pub. wks. in King cabinet, 1935.

CARDINALL, ALLAN WOLSEY, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1887; ed. Winchester Coll. and Heidelberg; Lond. Matric. 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1921; ag. comsnr., S. Prov., N.T., Apr. to Aug., 1928; ag. comsnr., N. Prov., N.T., Jan. to June, 1929; ret., 1932; comsnr., Cayman Is., 1934.

CAREW, WILLIAM DESMOND.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; 2nd lieut., 13th D.C.O. Lancers, Indian Army, 1918; served on N.W. frontier, 1919; cadet, Fiji, 1921; passed cadet, 1923; dist. comsnr., 1925; ag. ch. pol. mag. on various occasions; dist. mag., 1931; seconded to New Hebrides, as Br. judge, joint ct. 1935.

CAREY, CECIL WILLIAM VICTOR.—B. 1887; B.A., LL.B., Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Dublin, 1910; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Oct., 1915; mag., 1918; admstr.-gen., custodian of enemy property, offl. recor. in bankruptcy, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1921; ag. solr.-gen. in 1923-30; puisne judge, 1930; ag. ch. just., various occasions.

CAREY, HORACE RONALD, M.A. (Cant.)—B. 1903; ed. Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, asst. mast., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; headmtr., Francis Light Sch.; asst. inspr., schls., Penang, Nov., 1930; supt., eduon., Kelantan, Mar., 1931; ag. headmtr., Victoria Inst., Feb., 1934; inspr. of schls., N.S., Apr., 1937; ag. prin., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, Nov., 1938.

CAREY, THOMAS FALKLAND, B.A. (Dub.)—B. 1903; ed. The King's Hosp. Dub. and Trinity Coll., Dub.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1926; offr. cls. V., Jan., 1929; asst. res., Brunei, Jan., 1931; ag. Br. res., Oct., 1931; asst. contr., lab., Klang, July, 1935; ag. prot., labr., Kedah, Apr., 1936; offr., sl. III., Jan., 1938.

CARLISLE, AETHUR EDWARD.—B. 1882; ed. Durban High Schl. and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; advoc., Cape, Transvaal and Natal bars, 1904; practised in Durban; took silk 1927; ag. judge, Natal, 1935-37; judge, Aug., 1937.
CARLTON, ERIC JAMES WATTS.—B. 1908; ed. Bedford Schol. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; passed in sch. of agr., B.A., 1930; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1933.
CARMICHAEL, FRANK BERNARD—B. 1883; loco. supt., Trinidad govt. rly., June, 1928; ag. supt., rly., July, 1934 to Jan., 1935, and July-Dec., 1937.
CARNALL, JAMES ALBERT.—B. 1903; English prison serv., 1928; headmtr., govt. induc. schl., Br. Guiana, 1938.
CARNCRROSS, HON. SIR WALTER CHARLES FREDERICK, KT. BACH. (1922).—B. 1855; ed. privately; mem., N.Z. House of Reps., 1890-1902; M.L.C. since 1903; chmn., comtee. of L.C., 1910-18; speaker, 1918-1939.
CARR, EDWARD THOMAS WHITMORE, B.A., T.C.D.—B. 1883; sub-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, Oct., 1907 inspr., July, 1916; dep. inspr.-gen., 1937.

CARR, FREDERICK BERNARD.—B. 1893; admstr. offr., cls. IV 1919; asst. sec., S. Provs., secretariat, 1925, 1930 and 1931; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1928, 1929 and 1930; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1930; sec., comm. of inquiry in S.E. provs., 1930; ag. prim. asst. sec., S. provs. secretariat, 1930 and 1932; admstr. serv., cls. II, 1933; ag. res., 1928, 1933, 1934, 1935 and 1936; admstr. offr., cls. I, 1936.

CARR, JAMES BENJAMIN.—B. 1889; ed. Bedford Mod. Schol. and Culham Training Coll. (Oxford Diocesean); European asst. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1911; with H.M. Forces, Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1920; Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan. 1921; headmast., T. Kurau govt. eng. schl., June, 1926; asst. inspr., schls., S'pore, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1926; headmast., K. Pilah govt. eng. schl., Dec., 1928; headmast., Rangoon Rd. schl., S'pore, Dec., 1930; group super., S'pore, June, 1935; headmast., high schl., Kajang, Sept., 1936.

CARRIE, WILLIAM JAMES, M.A., B.Sc. (1914).—B. 1891; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. CHISHOLM, Sir Joseph Andrew, Kt., B.A.C. CHITTY, Edward Charles, M.C., M.B., Ch. B. (Aberdeen), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1896; med. offr., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; ag. prof., clin. surgery., S'pore, Apr., 1932; surg., grade B., Sept., 1936.

CHRISTIAN, John Harold Smith, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. CHRISTIANI, Egbert St. Clair.—B. 1886; cler. asst. botanic gardens, Br. Guiana, 1903; clk., dept. sci. and agr. and sec., bd. of agr., 1911; 2nd cls. clk. local govt. bd., 1918; asst. secy., local govt. bd., 1925; secy., do., 1926; secy., dist. admin., 1932.

CHRISTOFFELSZ, Arthur Eric, B.A., LL.B., Cantab., barrister-at-law (Grays Inn).—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1915; extra office asst., Jaffna kach., and pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1917; office asst., Badulla kach., Mar., 1919; pol. mag., Dadagamuwa, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurune gala, Sept., 1921; asst. attltm. offr., Oct., 1926; asst. attltm. offr., Dec., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, June, 1931; do., Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1933; ag. govt. agt., N.W.P., Mar., 1937.

CHRISTOFFELSZ, Basil Morris.—B. 1886; gen. cler. serv., Ceylon, Mar., 1904; Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1931; office asst., Kalutara kach., Feb., 1931; do., Matara kach., Jan., 1934; offr., cl. II, Nov., 1937; office asst., Ratnapura, Apr., 1938.
CHURCH, Basil Hampden, M.C.—B. 1890; asst. govt. marine survr., Hongkong, 1927.

CHURCHILL, William Foster Norton.—B. 1898; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb. (exhib.); B.A. (1921); 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Aug., 1917; served in France, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd. dist. office, Kajang May, 1921; offr., cl. V, Apr., 1924; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangar, Apr., 1924; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Apr., 1926; priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1927; offr., cl. IV, Apr., 1928; chmn. and sec., sany. bd., Larut, Aug., 1932; offr., cl. III, Apr., 1933; dist. offr., Lipis, Aug., 1934; ag. asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Feb., 1936; offr., cl. II, Apr., 1938.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER, P.C. (1907).—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906-8; for Dundee, 1908; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1895; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1896 (lst cl. (Spanish) Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (despa., medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah expd. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile expd. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspdt., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton Homes, Venter's Spruit, Hussar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Kraatz, and Pieters; also operations round De-wetsdorp, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); partly, under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1906; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; pres. of Bd. of T., 1908; Homesea, 1910-11; first lord of the Admiralty, 1911; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, 1915; min. of munitions, 1917; sec. of state, War Office and Air Ministry, Jan., 1919; S. of S. for the Colonies, 14th Feb., 1921 to 26th Oct., 1922; chancellor of the exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; first lord of the Admiralty, 1939; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill."
