Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch (now Univ. of Stellenbosch); clk., defence dept., Union of S.A., 1913; prte. sec. to min. of lands, 1916; ditto, to min. of defence, 1917; ditto, to min. of agr., 1921; under-sec. for pub. wks., 1934; sec. for pub. wks., 1939.

CLAYTON, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD CONRAD.—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Codrington Coll., and Ontario Business Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; ch. clk., pol. mgs. cts., dist. "A," 1927; ag. pol. mag., various dists., on several occasions, 1928-31; consent., income tax, 1936.

CLARENCE, EARL OF (cr. 1776) Rt. Hon. GEORGE HERBERT HYDE VILLIERS, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G., (1930), J.P., D.L.—B. 1877; ed. Eton; prev., lord-in-waiting to H.M.; hon. lieut.-col. (Hertford Reg.) A.D.C. to Lord Lieut. of Ireland (Lord Dudley); chanc., Primrose League, 1919-21; capt., Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1922-23 and 1924-25; dep. speaker, House of Lords; partly, under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., and chmn., oversea settlement ctes., 9th July, 1925; chmn. B.B.C., 1927; gov.gen., Union of S. Africa, 1931-37.

CLARK, ADRIAN JOHN, LL.B. (Lond.), O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1889; King's schol. and Goodenough medallist (Fr. and German) Westminster; led to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; junr. coun. to treasy., 1917; served in Great War, France, 1915-19; capt., 23rd London Regt.; D.A.A.G., Third Army and lieut.-col., R.A.F. (S.O., 1st grade); brevet-major and twice ment. in despa.; mag., Victoria, Trinidad, July, 1924; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Dec., 1924; P.O.S., Trinidad, Jan., 1927; puisne judge, Jamaica, May, 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Nov., 1932; dep. pub. proc., S'pore, Sept., 1934; puisne judge, S.S., 1936; legal advr., F.M.S., 1936.

CLARK, BENJAMIN MAULE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Wits).—B. 1901; ed. King Edward VII Schi., Johannesburg, Pretoria and Witwatersrand Univs. and St. Bartholomew's Hosp., Lond.; ch. med. offr., Mufulira Copper Mine, N. Rhodesia; hon. asst. physician, Pretoria Hosp.; M.O.H., Pietermaritzburg, 1936-37; asst. health offr., Union health dept., Capetown, 1937; senr. asst. health offr., Johannesburg, 1938.

CLARK, SIR ERNEST, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ent. Br. civ. serv., 1884; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1894; Cape Govt., 1904-06; asst. sec. to bd. of inland rev. and of R. commn. on income tax, 1919-20; asst. under-sec. for Ireland, sec. to treasury, and head of civ. serv. N. Ireland, 1920; mem., economic mission to Australia, 1928-29; and of other commns. and comites, including the joint exchequer bd. of G. Br. and N. Ireland; gov', Tasmania, 1933.

CLARK, ERNEST MALCOLM, M.R.O.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1908; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., Lond.; late senr. res. med. offr., West Suffolk Genl. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Nov., 1933; do., Kenya, 1937.

CLARK, JOHN BUCHANAN.—B. 1897; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; S.A. Coll. Schls. and S.A. Coll. (now Univ. of Cape Town); Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.A. (Cape Town); R.A. (Hons.), Oxon.; Rhodes schol., 1917; 2nd int. list, R.A.S.C., France, Aug., 1917 to Feb., 1919; lect., hist., Univ. of Cape Town, 1921; hist., classics and hist., Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1925; inspr., schls., June, 1925; ag. dir., Eur. educn., June, 1926-June, 1927; dir., Eur. educn., N. Rhodesia, June, 1927; mem., European educn. coman., N. Rhodesia, 1928; M.L.C. in 1930, 1931 and 1935; ag. dir., educn., 1935; mem., educn. films bd. and comm., on nutrition, 1936.
CLARK, JOHN ERNEST—B. 1883, orowen, N.S.W., July, 1931.

CLARK, J. MOOK, Ch.B. (Aberdeen), F.R.C.S. (Liverpool), Certif., Liverpool Schol. of Med.—Appld. to W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1914; served in Cameroons Expdy. Force, 1914; temp. lieut. (gen. list), 1915-16; temp. maj. offr., E. Africa, 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Tr. army, 19th Apr., 1918; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1918.

CLARK, RICHARD JOHN BOND, F.F.A. (chart. archt.).—B. 1896; ed. Cornwall; as assy. Schol.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1922; as assy. Schol.; archt., William Clifford, O.M.I., M.A., LL.D.—B. 1889; ed. Williamstown; Schol., Queen's Univ. (Kingston) and Harvard Univ. (Ricardo prize scholarship, Harvard Univ.); Henry Lee memorial fellow and tutor; div. of hist., govt. and economics, Harvard Univ., 1915; successively lecturer, asst. prof., assoc. prof. and prof., economics, Queen's Univ., 1916-33; advr., dept. of lab., Ottawa, 1918-19; prof., commerce, etc., Queen's Univ., 1931-32; finan. advr., Econ. Conf., Ottawa, 1932; do., min., finance and sec., treasy. bd., Ottawa, No.

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1937), K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1930), C.S.I. (1911), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1876; Ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; clk. Bd. of T., 1899; sec. to sp. mission to Shanghai to negotiate comm. treaty with China, 1901; ag. 2nd sec., diplomatic serv., 1902; sec. to royal comm. on supply of food, etc., in time of war, 1903-5; priv. sec. at Bd. of T. to Rt. Hon. D. Lloyd George, 1906, and to Rt. Hon. W. S. Churchill, 1908; and to Rt. Hon. D. Lloyd George as chancellor of the exchequer, 1908-10; mem. for commerce and industry of the coun. of the Viceroy of India, 1910-16; compt.r.-gen., comm. intell. dept. Bd. of T., 1916; compt.r.-gen. dept. of overseas trade, Sept., 1917; high commnr. for Great Britain in Canada, Sept., 1928; high commnr. for Basutoland, the Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland and high commnr. in the Union of S. Africa for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1935-1939.

CLARKE, ARTHUR GREENFELL, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Mountjoy Schl., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1929; passed cadet Jan., 1932; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Feb., 1932; J.P., 1935; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Aug., 1935; 2nd asst. to do., Oct., 1935; 3rd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1936; 2nd asst. do. and dep. clk., couns., Feb., 1937; asst. supt., imports and exports, Nov., 1937; dist. offr., South, 1938; 3rd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1938; prin. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1939; 2nd asst. col. sec., and dep. clk. of couns., Nov., 1939.

CLARKE, CAPT. CHARLES NOBLE ARDEN.—B. 1898; ed. Rossall Schl.; on mil'y. serv., M.G.C., 1916-20; cadet, Nigeria, 1920; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1928; ag. prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secr., 1933; govt. sec. Bechuanaland Prot., 1934; res. commnr., 1937.
CLARKE, F. H.—Asst. game warden, R.A.P., 1919; ag. game warden, Kenya, 1932; senr. asst. do., 1937.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FRANCIS GRENVILLE, K.B.E., M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. Scotch Coll., Melbourne, Melbourne Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.L.O. for N. Prov. (Vic.); mem. for lands and water supply, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1919; min. of pub. wks. and water supply, Oct., 1919 to July, 1923; pres., leg. coun., Victoria, July, 1923.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FREDERICK JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1911), C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. Christ Church Mid. Schl., Barbados, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1879; M.H.A., Barbados, 1887; pres., Barbados agric. soc'y, 1908; mem., cent. poor law board; M.E.C., 1908; J.P.; O.C., Barbados vol. force, 1906; speaker, H. of A., Barbados, 1898-1934.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY T. S.—B. 1879; clk. to P.M.O., Basutoland, 1913; clk., treas., 1914; clk. to asst. commnr., 1919; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Police, 1921; war serv., 1916-19; dep. asst. commnr., 1935; dist. commnr., 1937.
CLARKE, JOHN HUME CHISWELL, M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow), 1936.—B. 1911; med. offr., Somaliland, 1933.
CLARKE, JOSEPH SHERWOOD, B.A. (Harvard).—B. 1903; ed. Rossall Schl. and Harvard Univ.; supt., educn., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; headmnr., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1931.

CLARKE, STEPHEN GRAINGER.—B. 1894; 5th cls. clk., P.W.D., Fiji, 1914; 4th cls. clk., audit dept., 1915; on active ser., 1915; 3rd cls. clk., audit dept., 1923; 2nd cls. clk., 1922; acctnt., Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1924; dep. commnr. (temp. and provnl.), 1926; treas., collr., cust. and ch. postmr., 1928; dep. commnr., 1929; ag. res. commnr., 1935.

CLARKE, THOMAS LISLE EVELYN, M.D., C.M., McGill (1911).—B. 1885; ed. at the Lodge Sohl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and McGill Univ., Montreal; junr. res. surgeon, general hosp., Barbados, Aug., 1911; med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1912; ag. commnr. and mag., May, 1912; mem. of quarantine bd., health offr., mem. of exec. coun., and J.P., Virgin Is., July, 1912; ag. commnr., Virgin Is., Apr., 1913 to Jan., 1914; ag. commnr., mag. and dep. judge, summary jurisdiction ct., Virgin Is., from Dec., 1914 to May, 1917, and from July, 1917 to Oct., 1918; med. offr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1918; provisional mem. fed. leg. and exec. couns., Leeward Islands, Mar., 1915; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.
CLARKE, WILLIAM RICHARD.—B. 1909; ed. Univ. Coll. and Inst. of Educn., Lond., B.Sc. (Lond.), hon. chem., A.I.C., dip.in educn. (Lond.); ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1934.

CLARKE, W. W.—B.1895; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., S. Africa; war serv., Rebellion and German S.W. campaign, 1914-15; S. Africa Overseas Contgt., 1916-18; temp. lieut., 1918; cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. senr. asupt., cust., Feb. May, 1930; senr. supervisor, cust., 1935.

CLARKSON, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1881; ed. Boys' Model Schl. and Boys' High Schl., Durban; admitted atty., Natal prov. divn., Feb., 1905; offr., Durban Light Inf'y., and adjt.; mem., prov. coun., 1915; mem., exec. coun., 1925; senator, 1930; min., posts and tels. and pub. wks., Mar., 1933.

CLAUSON, GERARD LESLIE MAKINS, C.M.G. (1933), O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919.—B. 1891; scholar of Eton, 1903, and C.G.C., Oxford, 1910; Boden Sanskrit Scholar, 1911; 1st cls. Class. Hon. Moda., 1912; Hall-Houghton Syriac prizeman, 1913; 2nd cls. Greats, 1914; B.A., 1919; James Mew Arabic Scholar, 1920; apptd. after compet. exam. 2nd cls. clk., B. of Inland Rev., Sept., 1914, but released for mil. service; 2nd lieut., 7th Som. L.I., 21st Aug., 1914; transf'd. to gen. list, 19th April, 1915; served in War Office, Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; capt. Gen. Staff, 26th Dec., 1915 to 12th Apr., 1916, and 23rd Apr., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; mentioned in despatches Mesopotamia, 1917, Egypt, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, with Palms, 1917; 2nd cls. clk. in C.O., 1st Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; on sp. serv., Lausanne, for Turkish peace treaty, June to July, 1923; accredited rep. to Permanent Mandates Coman. at various sessions from June, 1926 onwards; student at Imp. Defence Coll., Jan.-Dec., 1927; sec., C.O. currency comtte., 1930; visited S.S. and Hong-Kong as mem. and sec. of Hong-Kong currency comtte., Feb. to June, 1931; deptl. adviser to U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, July to Aug., 1932; ag. asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1934; ag. asst. under-sec., 1st Jan., 1940.

CLAXTON, Herbert William, O.B.E. (1937), A.C.A.—Ed. Weymouth Coll., 1909-16; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1917; served Palestine, 1917-18; lieut., 1918; chartered accnt. (Eng.), 1924 (hons. inter.); asst. treas., Somaliland, 1924; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; senr. ast. treas., Somaliland, 1929; treas. and ch. of cust., 1933.

CLAY, GEOFFREY FLETCHER, O.B.E. (1936), M.C., B.Sc.-Agr. (Edin.), N.D.A., N.D.D. (Hons.) Lanc. Dip. in Agric.—B.1896; capt., 5th Batt. Duke of Wellington's Regt., 1914-19; agrl. offr., Uganda, 1924; geneticist, Amani, Tanganyika, 1927-28; senr. agrl. offr., Uganda, 1928; dep. dir., agr., 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1931, 1934 and 1936; mem., sugar comnr., 1934-36; ast. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1936; dir. of agr., Uganda, 1939.

CLEARE, LAURENCE DELANEY, F.R.E.S., F.L.S.—B. 1896; ed. Cornell Univ.; agrl. instr., Br. Guiana, 1919; econ. biologist, dept. sci. and agr., 1922; rep. col. at 2nd Impl. Entom. Conf., London, 1926; curator, B.G. museum, 1924; govt. entom., dept. agr., 1928; rep. col. at 3rd Impl. Entom. Conf., 1930; seconded, moth-borer investigtns., 1931; rep. col. at 4th Impl. Entom. Conf., London, 1936 and 7th Int. Entom. Cong., Berlin, 1938; dep. dir., agr., Br. Guiana, 1939.

CLARKIN, PETER ALPHONSUS.—B. 1887; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast and Lond. Sch. of Hygiene and Trop. Med., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O., Belf., D. P.H., Irel.; path. and ast. M.O., Oxford County ment. hosp., 1913; M.O., Sierra Leone, 1914; M.O. and M.O.H., Kenya, 1916; dir., lab., Dar-es-Salam and dep. dir., lab. serv., Tanganyika, 1929; govt. bacteriologist and pathologist, Br. Guiana, 1938.
CLELAND, EDWARD ESKINE, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1869; ed. Prince Alfred Coll. and Adelaide Univ.; barrister-at-law; judge, sup.ct., Adelaide, 1936.

CLEMENTI, SIR CECIL, G.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1916)—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy, 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; 1st cl. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cl. lit. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. ast. col. sec., and ag. clk. of councils, Dec., 1900, to Oct., 1901; ast. regisr.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of examrs. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; seconded for spec. serv. under govt. of India, May, 1902; J.P., June, 1902; ag. ast. col. sec., and clk. ofcouncils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land ct., New Territories, Dec., 1903; ag. ast. land offr. and pol. mag., New Territories, May, 1905, to Sept., 1906; ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Oct., 1907; attended Internat. Opium Conf. at Shanghai, Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the Admstr., Apr., 1910; ag. col. sec., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., on various occasions, 1911-12; col. sec., B. Guiana, 1913; admstd. govt., 7th June, 1916 to 14th Apr., 1917, Apr. to Oct., 1919 and June to Oct., 1921; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1922; admstd. govt., Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; gov., Hong Kong, 1st Nov., 1925; Knt. of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem, 1926; hon. I.L.D., Hong Kong Univ., 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Straits Sttlmts. and high comnr., Malay States, 1929; resigned, 1934.

CLEMENTI, LOCKHART.—B. 1881; served in Cape Mounted Rifles; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; served in European War; staff offr., 1926; dep. ast. comnr., 1929; ast. comnr., 1930; title changed to dist. comnr., 1936.
CLEMENTS, JOHN BURTON, B.Sc. Forestry (Edin.)—B. 1891; for. offr., Nyasaland, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. for. offr., Oct. to Dec., 1920 and May to Dec., 1923; ch. for. offr., Dec., 1923.
CLEMMY, A. V., M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxf.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.M.S.A.—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

CLIFFORD, CAPT. HON. SIEBED EDMUND HUGH, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1931), C.M.G. (1924), M.V.O. (1920).—B. 1890; served, European War; pvt. sec. Lord Novar, gov.-gen. C'wealth Australia, 1918-20; sec., H.R.H. Prince Arthur of Connaught (gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Union of S. Africa) 1921-23; sec. Earl of Athlone, gov.-gen. and commander-in-ch., Union of S. Africa, 1924; imp. sec., S. African high comnr., Apr., 1924; rep. of H.M. Govt. of Gt. Britain in the Union of S. Africa, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1931; do., Mauritius, Oct., 1937.

CLIFFORD, GEOFFREY MILES, O.B.E. (1939), E.D.—B. 1897; ed. Heath Schl., Horsmonden, Kent; milly. serv., France and Flanders, 1915-18; army of the Rhine, 1919-20; admstd. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1929; dipl., anthropology, London Univ., 1933; ag. res., 1934 and 1935-37; admstd. offr., cls. II, 1937; ag. prin., ast. sec., secr., 1938 and 1939; admstd. offr., cls. I, 1939.

CLIFFORD, SIR HUGH CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1921), G.B.E., (1926), K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1886; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1886; on special service to Pahang, 1887 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); ag. govt. agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1888; ag. Br. res. Pahang, 1890 to 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; visited Cocos-Keeling Is., as special comnr., June, 1894; led armed expd. into native states of Trénggánu and Kélantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1896 (recd. thanks S. of S.); res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, Sept., 1904; sent on deputation to B. Guiana in Nov., 1905, to enquire into municipal institutions; admstd. govt., Mar. to Aug., 1904, and from Apr. to Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1907; admstd. govt., on various occasions, 1907-12; gov., G. Coast, Dec., 1912; gov., Nigeria, July, 1919; gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1925; gov. S. Sttlmts. and high comnr., Malay States, June, 1927; ret., 1929; concluded with M. Nouflard, the lieut.-gov. of Dahomey, in Aug., 1914 an agreement for the temporary partition between the British and French Govts., and for the provisional administration of Togoland; admstd. Br. sphere of occupation Togoland, concurrently with the G. Coast, from Aug., 1914, to Aug., 1919; chmn., comtece. on W. African trade and taxation, Oct.-Nov., 1921 (received thanks of S. of S.); is part author, with Sir Frank Swettenham, of a Malayan dictionary, and author of more than a dozen books, mostly on Malayan subjects, and of a translation of the penal code into Malay; also of a history of the G. Coast regt. in the E. African campaign.

CLIFT, ALBERT HALL.—B. 1896; ed. Boston G.S. & Keble Coll., Oxford; final exam. B.A. (rural econ.) 1923; served with H.M. forces, Italy, 1917-18; supt., educn., Nigeria, 1924; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1936.

CLIPSHAM, PHILIP RICHARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920; seconded as regnr. on staff of high commr., in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Gt. Britain, 1932; rtnd. to C.O. as cler. offr., higher grade, 4th Dec., 1937.
CLIVE, CAPT. J. H.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May., 1920; dist. offr., 1923.

CLOETE, PHILIP ALBERT (B.Sc., Cape).—B. 1882; ed. S.A. Coll., Cape Town, S.A.; Schl. of Mines, Kimberley and Johannesburg; B.E.F., France, S.A. Heavy Arty., 1917-18; asst. inspr., mines, 1927; asst. gen. man., state alluvial mines, Namaqualand, 1929; inspr., mining leases, Union of S.A., 1934.
CLOSE, R. W., B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1867; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; M.P. for Rondebosch, 1920-29; Mowbray, 1929-33; leader, Cape bar; min. plenip., Washington, Jan., 1934.

CLUBB, HON. WILLIAM REID—B. 1884; served on Morris munici. coun., 1916-20; mem., leg. assembly for Morris, Manitoba, 1920-22; re-elec., July, 1922; min. of public works, Manitoba, 8th Aug., 1922; re-elec. for Morris by accl., 26th Aug., 1922.

CLUBER, REGINALD MONTAGU.—B. 1891; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. schl., jurisprudence); barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1915; served Great War, 1914-15; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1932; asst. legal advdr., F.M.S., 1937; puisne judge, Tanganyika, 1939.

CLUTTERBUCK, P.A., M.C.—B. 1897; schol. of Malvern Coll. and of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1915; Coldstream Guards, 1916-19; capt. and adjt., 3rd batt., Oct., 1917; M.C. and ment. in despr., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., G.P.O., 1st Sept., 1919; transf. to C.O., 13th Feb., 1922; sec., managing comtce, Imperial Bureau of Mycology, 1923-25; addnl. pte. sec. to parly. under sec. (Lord Arnold), June-Oct., 1924; sec., Milner-Lovat agrl. comtce., 1924-26; sec., Donoughmore coman. on Ceylon constitution and visited Ceylon, 1927-28; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. for Doma. affrs., 23rd Aug., 1928; prin., Doma. Office, 14th Nov., 1929; U.K. deleg., to L. of N. assembly, 1930 and 1931; negotiations with Egypt, 1930; reparations confces., Lond., 1931 and Lausanne, 1932; sec., Newfoundland Royal Coman., and visited Newfoundland (twice) and Canada, 1933; further visit to Newfoundland, Sept.-Oct., 1933.

CLUVER, COL. EUSTACE HENRY, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., D.P.H.—B. 1894; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S. Africa, Hertford Coll., Oxford and King's Coll., Hosp., London; Rhodes schol. for Stellenbosch; Burrey Yeo. schol., King's Coll.; senr. demy, Magdalen Coll., Oxford; 1st cls., final hona., nat. science (physiology); served with 1st S. African field ambulance in France; lecturer in geology, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1914; prof. of physiology, Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1919-26; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1926; senr. ast. health offr., do., 1934; dep. ch. health offr., Union S. Africa, 1937; sec. for pub. health and ch. health offr., Union, 1938; transf. R. of O., with rank of maj., S.A.M.C., 1929; heut.-col., R. of O., 1932; D.D.M.S. defence headqrs., 1933; D.M.S. with temp. rank of col., 1934; dir.-gen. of medical, hygiene and dental services; comdr., Order of Hosp. of St. John of Jerusalem, 1935; col., 1937; assoc. edr., Journal of Industrial Hygiene (America); published many papers in tech. jnl.s. on subject of physiology and hygiene.

CLUVER, FREDERICK WILLIAM PAUL, B.A. (Cape), M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Cape Town) Fellow of the Royal Institute of Public Health; major, S.A.M.C. (res. of offrs.).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edinburgh Univ. and Univ. of Cape Town; res. med. offr., gen. hosp., Pretoria, and med. offr., to S.A. Air Force (major S.A.M.C.), 1924; O.C., mily. hosp., Wynberg, 1926; O.C., mily. hosp., Roberts Heights, 1927; ast. health offr., Union health dept., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933; dep. ch. health offr., Durban, 1939.

COAKER, HON. SIR WM. F., K.B.E. (1923).—President, fisherman's protective union, New.foundland; M.H.A. for Bonavista, 1913; M.H.A., Twillingate, 1914; mem. of tonnage comtce., 1917; M.E.C., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

COALES, FRANCIS GEORGE, M.I.C.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1889; ed. Elstow Schl., Bedford; ch. draftman, P.W.D., Perak, 1914; ast. engrnr., 1917; ag. exec. engrnr., grade III, 1919; ag. exec. engrnr. and ast. to D.P.W., 1922; exec. engrnr., 1923; ag. ast. D.P.W., 1925; exec. engrnr. in ch., deptl. tech. schl., 1925-27; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 1926; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1929; senr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1930; state engrnr., grade II, Jan., 1936; grade I, Sept., 1936; dep. D.P.W., S.S., Dec., 1938; ag. D.P.W., Apl., 1939.

COATES, RT. HON. JOSEPH GORDON, P.C. (1926), M.C.—B. 1878; ed. privately; mem. of New Zealand parl., since 1911; min. of justice, 1919-20; P.M.G., 1919-25; min. of pub. wks., 1920-26; min., native affrs., 1921-28; min. rly., 1923-28; prime min., 1925-28; min., external affrs., 1928; min., mines, 1928; min., agr., 1928; ret. from office, 1928; joined coalition miny., 1931; min., pub. wks., 1931-33; min., transport, 1931-35; min., finance, 1933-35; min., cust., 1933-35; N.Z. rep. at Imp. Confce., 1926 and at Ottawa confce., 1932; served with N.Z. Exped. Force as capt. and major, 1916-1919; awarded M.C. with bar.

COATS, ROBERT HAMILTON, B.A., F.S.S.(hon.), F.R.C.S.—B. 1874; ed. Toronto Univ.; bankers' scholarship in polit. economy, and Wyld prize, English; on staff of "Toronto Globe," 1897-1900; editor, Labour Gaz., and ch. statistician, dept. of labour, 1901-12; mem., coman. on the cost of living, 1913-14; dom. statistician and contrlr. of census, 1914; author of "The Labour Movement in Canada" (Canada and its Provinces); joint author of life of James Douglas (Makers of Canada series); off. del. to Br. Empire Statistical Confce., 1920; mem. of coman. on statistics under the League of Nations, 1920; mem., International Statistical Inst.

COBB, FREDERIC.—B. 1885; ed. Charterhouse and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); European mast., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; headmast., King George V Sch., Seremban, May, 1934; European mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1935; headmast., high schl., B. Mertajain, Sept., 1937; snr. educn. offr., Oct., 1937.
COBB, MAURICE LAKE, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Tarlidge Schl. and Univ. Coll., Lond., ast. engr., Nigerian rly. capital wks., 1921; do., open lines, 1936; ch. engr., Tanganyika rly., 1938.

COBDEN-RAMSDAY, ARTHUR BAWTREE, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Sherborne Schl., Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1927; ag. sec., rubber restricn., Johore, Feb., 1928; ag. 2nd ast. sec. (B.), S.S., Mar., 1929; passed cadet, July, 1929; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1930; do., IV, Jan., 1934; ag. ast. advr., Kemaman, Sept., 1934; ag. offi. assignee and regisr., companies, F.M.S., Nov., 1936; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Nov., 1937; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1939.

COBON, HUGH HORACE, A.S. (Australasia), M.Q.I.S.—B. 1892; Aus. Imp. Forces, 1915-17; authd. survr., Queensland, 1920; survr., F.M.S. rlya, Nov., 1920; survr., Penang, Sept., 1921; do., Kedah, Feb., 1924; do., F.M.S., July, 1928; asst. supt., Johore, Feb., 1930; do., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. supt., Johore, June, 1934; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1937.

COCHRANE, CAPT. BASIL WILLIAM DUNDONALD.—B. 1891; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1908-16; served in S.W. Africa campaign and Mesopotamia (R. of O.); "Dunsterforce" (Azerbaijan), North Persia, 1918-19; South Persia Rifles, German Brig., aspt. and adjt., 1st Cavalry, 1919-20; "1914-18" star, Br. War, Victory and I.G.S. medal (bar, S. Persia); constable, Kenya pol., 1921; asst. supt., pol., 1925; supt., do., 1935.

COCHRANE, EDGAR, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), (1926), D.P.H. (1931)—B. 1897; on war serv., 1915-19; ag. dist. surg., Jesselton, Br. N. Borneo, 1925-26; asst. gort. med. offr. of health and dep. port health offr., Br. Guiana, 1932; med. offr., Gold Coast, 1936; senr. med. offr., Grenada, 1938.

COCKAYNE, ALFRED HYDE, I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1880; assist. biologist, N.S.W. agrl. dept., 1904; biologist, 1909; dir., fields div., 1923, also dir., plant research st., 1927; asst. dir. gen., agr., 1929; dir.-gen., 1936.

COOKMAN, HERBERT JAMES, D.F.C.—B. 1898; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Merton Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1921; attd., Fed. secret., Jan., 1922; offr. cls. V., Dec., 1924; offr. cls. IV., dist. offr., Pekan, Dec., 1928; offr. cls. III., dist. offr., Lipis, Dec., 1933; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Nov., 1934.

COCKRAM, BEN.—B. 1903; ed. Victoria, Taunton's Schol. and Oxford; hist. schol., Queen's Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls., mod. hist., 1925; Goldsmith's schol., student, 1926; 1st cls., med. and mod. hist., London, 1923; fellow, Michigan, 1925-26; Ph.D., 1926; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., Dominions Offce, 4th Nov., 1926; pte. sec. to the parly. under sec. for dom. affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 1st Oct., 1930; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 22nd June, 1931; prin., 21st Nov., 1934; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1938.

COCKS, HUGH CECIL, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Jan., 1928; attd. secretariat, May, 1928; offr. ass't, Nuwara Eliya kach., Jan., 1929; offr. ass't. to govt. agt., Uva, May, 1930; asst. stimt. offr., June, 1930; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Mar., 1936; do., Kandy, Sept., 1937.

COE, THOMAS PEROWNE, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. King Edward VI Schol., Norwich and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. class. moda., 1908, 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, Dec., 1912; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1913; on war serv. with Norfolk Regt., 1915-18; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd ass't. sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in add., Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; 2nd-lieut. M.S.V.R., Apr., 1921; ag. capt., Nov., 1921; capt., Aug., 1922; supernum. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; major, M.S.V.R., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., July, 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; sec. to res., Perak; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1928; ag. gen. advr., Johore, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. I B: asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Feb., 1930; ag. Br. advr., Kelantan, May, 1931; ag. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1933; ag. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Malaya, Jan., 1934; offr. cls. I A., Jan., 1936.
COGHLAN, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1899; I.F.S., A.M.O., 1922-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

COHEN, ANDREW BENJAMIN.—B. 1909; ed. Malvern and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., 1st pt., class. tripes, 1st cls., 2nd pt., class. tripes; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., inland rev. Oct., 1932; transfd. to C.O., 2nd Oct., 1933; ag. prin., 14th Dec., 1937.

COLE, ARNOLD KIRTLAND.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. res. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. supt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd cls., treasy. dept., 1913; 1st cls., P.O., 1914; ch. cls. and asst. examin. offr., cust. dept., Aug., 1921; ag. contr., cust., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1928; asst. contr., and examining offr., Dec., 1923; survr., shipping, Mar., 1925; ag. contr., cust. and regisr., shipping from Apr., 1926; ag. harbour., Mar. to Apr., 1927; comtr., cust., Sept., 1928; M.L.C., Dec., 1931; del., W. Indies cust. confce., Bermuda, 1934.

COLEBATCH, HON. SIR HAL PATESHALL KT. BACH. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Goolwa, S. Australia; M.L.C. for E. Prov., W. Australia, May, 1912; col. sec., min. for educn., min. of pub. health and leader of legis. coun., 1916-19; premier, W. Australia for short period, 1919; min. of educn. and pub. health, 17th May, 1919; agt.-gen. for W. Australia, 1923-27; mem., Australian constitution royal comm., 1927-29; C'wealth senator for W. Australia, 1929-33; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1933-39.

COLEMAN, EPHEMIA HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1890; ed. Pub. and High Schls., Arnprior and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; sec.-treas., Canadian Bar Assoc., 1919-33; dean, Man. Law Schl., 1929-33; bencher, Law Soc. of Man., 1928-33; under-secv. of state, Canada, 1933.

COLEMAN-DOSCAS, APOLLO ERNEST, Dip. Agr., W.A. Scotch Coll., Claremont, W.A.; Univ. of W.A.—B. 1893; asst. agrl. inspr., F.M.S., July, 1920; inspr. agr., Johore, Aug., 1922; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1925; do., F.M.S. (Perak South), Jan., 1926; ag. sr. agrl. offr., Perak, Sept., 1931; A.F.O., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1933; state agrl. offr., Johore, Feb., 1936; ag. ch. field offr., July, 1938.

COLEY, CAPT. C. F. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1897; ed. Oxford Univ. and Université de Paris; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Devon Regt., and R. Corps of Signals in European War, 1914-18; diplomatic serv., 1918; cadet, Nigerian admstve. serv., Mar., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926.

COLLETT, SENATOR THE HON. HERBERT BRAYLEY, C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Served A.I.F., 1914-1918; col., 1918, C.M.G., 1919; apptd. to senate for W. Australia by Gov.-in-Coun., Apr., 1933; re-el., g.e., 1934; asst. min. in chge. war serv. homes, Menzies miny., Apr., 1939.

COLLIER, C. W. H.—B. 1892; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; cler. asst., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1909; sec. to various col. comtees. and gradually promoted to 1st cls. offr., 1920; immigrn. agt., 1926; sec., colonization (500 family scheme) comtee., 1927; senr. immigrn. agt. and immigrn. agt., Demerara county, 1928; ag. immigrn. agt., gen., 1929; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1930; prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns. 1932; asst. col. sec., 1934.

COLLIER, FRANCIS HOWARD, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Jesus Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., Jan., 1925; Harmsworth schol.; mem. South-Eastern circuit and N. London sessions; priv. practice, Singapore, 1926-30; London, 1930-37; crown coun., Nigeria, 1937; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1939.

COLLIER, HON. PHILIP.—B. 1873; M.L.A. for Boulder, W. Australia, since 1905; min. for mines and rlys. in Scaddan govt., 1911-14; and of mines and water supply, 1914-16; leader, parly. labour party, May, 1917; premier, W. Australia, 1924-1930 and 1933-36; resigned from ministry, 1936.
COLLINGS, WILLIAM RICHARD KINGDON, A.M.I.N.A.—B. 1904; asst. govt. marine survt., Hong Kong, 1933.

COLLINS, CYRIL GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. St. Patrick's Coll., N.Z.; joined N.Z. p. and t. dept., accts., br., 1899; audit dept., 1907; inspr., 1909; supvr., local bodies audit, 1927; dep. contr. and audr.-gen. and ch. inspr., 1937; contr. and audr.-gen., 1939.

COLLINS, F.—B. 1882; clk., Bri. tel. serv., 1899; clk., Cape post and tel. serv., 1900; engr. asst., 1911; inspr., traffic, 1912; traffic supt., 1920; chf. supt., 1923; chf. clk., 1925; contr. tels. and telephones, 1933; under sec., tels., 1936.

COLLINS, FRANCIS RICHARD.—B. 1896; war serv. 1915-20; Inns of Court O.T.C. and 2nd Bn. The Dorsetshire Regt. served in India, Palestine, Syria, Egypt and the Sudan; Col. Audit Dept., 1926; asst. audr., Kenya, 1926; C.A.D. cent. office Lond., 1930; senr. asst. audr., G. Const., 1937.
COLLINS, JOHN VICTOR, M.A., Sc.M. (Dublin), F.I.C.—B. 1897; asst. govt. analyst, Ceylon, Jan., 1925; dep. govt. analyst, Oct., 1927; govt. analyst, May, 1934.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Heidelberg Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Univ. of Wisconsin, 1911; asst. geologist, geol. surv. of Can., 1905; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; fellow, Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir. geol. surv. of Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. Min. Met.; mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; has pub. numerous papers and memoirs on pre-cambrian geol. and petrology.

COLLINS, COL. COMMNDT. THE HON., WILLIAM RICHARD, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B. 1876; ed. Pretoria Staats Gymnasium; joined Repub. civ. serv., 1894; Anglo-Boer War, 1898-1902; practised at side bar, Ernemo, 1902-38; mem., Ernemo, Transvaal parl., 1907-10; Union parl., 1916-38; Wakkerstroom, 1938; serv. during Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; min. of agr. and forestry, Nov., 1938.

COLLINS, WILLIAM OSBORNE HOPE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1909; ed. Tonbridge and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; Br. Guiana pol., 1932; asst. supt., pol., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; asst. dist. commr., Basutoland, 1937.

COLLYMORE, ERNEST ALLAN, M.A. (Oxon.), K.C. (1933)—B. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Merton Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons. final schol. of jurisprudence, 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; serv. in E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-19; capt., Oct., 1915; capt., Barbados Vols. and O.C., cadet corps of the island, 1921-26; sen. mag., Bridgetown and dist. A, June, 1925; recvr. of wrecks, Aug., 1925; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal in 1925 and 1926; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas and coroner for New Providence, July, 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1928; ch. just., July, 1936.

COLTON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1900; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; treasy.clk. N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1927; asst. treas., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., Sept., 1937; dep. acct.-gen., Jan., 1938; ag. acct.-gen., Jan., 1938; acct.-gen., 1938.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLYNEUX, KT. BACH (1920).—Ed. at Halleybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; atty.-gen. E.A.P., 1912; atty.-gen., Nigeria, 1913; ch. just., Nigeria, 1918; ret., Mar., 1929.

COMMISSIONG, TERENCE BERTRAND.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Apr., 1909; 2nd clk., gov't's office, June, 1915; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1919; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s, office clk. of couns., med. regis., and man. govt. printing sub-dept., Feb.-Sept., 1920; seconded for active serv. Sept., 1915 to Oct., 1919; awarded meritorious serv. med. in German E. African campaign, June, 1916; apptd. J.F., Grenada, in recog. of war serv., July, 1919; oh. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. to exec. coun., med.regis. and man., govt. printing dept., May, 1921; asst. col. sec., Grenada, Jan., 1928.
CONLEY, JAMES SHARPE.—B. 1884; G.W.R. 1900-03; Cape govt. rlys., 1903-10; S. African rlys. & harbrs., 1910-27; asupt. (admustve.), K.U.Rlys. and H., 1927; ch. asst. to gen. man., 1929.

CONIBEAR, JAMES HANFORD.—Reg. army, 1897-1919; sub.-inspr., pol. and keeper, prisons, St. Lucia, Nov., 1920; ag. ch. pol. and inspr., prisons, 1922; prison supt., Gold Coast, Jan., 1924; lieut., G.C. Volunteers, Oct., 1924; ag. adjt., 1924-25; capt., G.C. Defence Force, Mar., 1933; supt., grade I, Sept., 1934; senr. prison supt., Jan., 1935; ag. dir., prisons, June-Sept., 1935.
CONN, ALEXANDER ABERDEIN, M.I.Mar.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1895; appren. and marine engr., 1911-24; 2nd engr., K.U.R. & H., 1924; engr.-in-charge, 1932; senr. marine engr., 1933.

CONNELL, W. K., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1893; milv. serv., R.A.M.C., 1916-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1924; junr. surg. specialist, 1934; specialist, 1936.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL, KT. BACH (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick Pub. Schl. and Catholic Schl.; M.L.C., Western Australia, 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and lab., 1906; col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leader of leg. coun. from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. for Perth, 1914; agt. genl. in London for Western Australia, 1917-23; agt. gen. in London for Malta, 1930.

CONRADIE, DAVID GIDEON, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Hamlet and Ceres Schls., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; sen. mod. and gold medal, LL.D., 1906; advoc., Cape Town and Bloemfontein, 1907-09; sec., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1909-15; atty., Reitz and Bloemfontein, 1915-33; mem., prov. and prov. exec., 1920-26; M.P., Bethlehem and Lindley, 1926-33; admstr., S.W. Africa, 1933.

CONWAY, WILLIAM EWAN.—B. 1893; ed., High Schl., Dundee; govt. travelling schl. master, 10th Dec., 1913; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1916; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., June, 1916 to Apr., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., 1920; collr., cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1920; senr. collr., cust., June, 1928; ass't compt., customs, 1938.
COOK, JAMES, L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. Durham)—B. 1904; med. supt., col. hosp., Port of Spain, Trinidad, Oct., 1936.

COOK, RT. HON. SIR JOSEPH, P.C. (1914); G.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1860; mem. of H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia, since 1901; postmrgen., N.S. Wales, 1894-1898; min. for mines and agric., 1898-9; min. of defence, C. of A., June, 1909; prime minister, C. of A., June, 1913-Sept., 1914; min. for the Navy, 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; high commr. in London for C. of A., Nov., 1921; ret., 1927.
COOKE, EDGAR HENRY.—B. 1889; war serv., 1916-17; prob., N. Rhodesia, 1918; asst. nat. commr., 1919; nat. commr., 1924; asst. mag., 1928; dist. commr., 1935; prov. commr., 1939.
COOKE, ELEANOR MARGARET.—B. 1891; matron, Ashmota Coll., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; ast. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W.A., 1896; govt. astronomer, New South Wales, 1912-26; prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, ALEXANDER RAPHAEL.—B. 1899; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Dominica Grammar Sch.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., 1924; practised at bar, 1924-30; ag. crown atty. and mag., dist. COOLS-LARTIGUE, LOUIS.—B. 1905; ed., Dominica Grammar Sch.; ast. mast., Dominica Grammar Sch., Mar., 1923-Aug., 1924; 2nd clk., admrs.'s office, Dec., 1924; junr. clk., do., Aug., 1927; senr. clk., regar's. office, Apr., 1929; prin., admrs.'s office as ch. clk. and clk. of couns., etc., June, 1932.

E, 1930.

COOMARASWAMY, RAJENDRA, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1938.
