COOMBES, H. P.—B. 1894; elk., govt. sec's. office, Nyasaland, May, 1917; senr. clk., Aug., 1923; ag. astt. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1926 to Mar., 1927; ag. senr. astt. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. office supt., secretariat and clk., exec. and leg. couns., in 1929 and 1930; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1931; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1931 to Dec., 1933.

COOPE, ARTHUR EGERTON.—B. 1888; ed., Fonthill Schil., St. Edward's Schil., and Keble Coll., B.A. Oxon.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., May, 1915; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr. astt. advr., lands, Kedah, May, 1924; town planning admir. and state valuer, Ipoh, Oct., 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Apr. to May, 1929; ag. mag., Seremban, Sept., 1929; ag. chmn., sany. bd. in addn., ast. adviser, Muar, Johore, Mar., 1930; ag. mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1931; offr., cls. I B, Nov., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. comsnr., Kelantan, Mar., 1933; comsnr., trade and cust., Johore., Dec., 1934; contr., rubber, Malaya, Mar., 1936; ag. Br. advr., Trengganu, Dec., 1937.

COOPER, EVANS GUSTAVUS.—B. 1875; astt. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; clerk of cts., Hanover, Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag. on various occasions; supernym. res. mag., June, 1923; res. mag., June, 1924.

COOPER, COMDR. GUY EDWARD, R.N. (ret.).—B. 1891; ed. R.N.; Great War, ment. in desps.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1923; legal adviser, Seychelles, Apr., 1928; ag. ch. just., May to Nov., 1928 and Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1932; title changed to dist. mag., 1935; attd. legal advry. staff, C.O., July-Aug., 1936; ag. puisne judge, 1936, 1938-39.

COOPER, WILLIAM GERALD GROVES, Ph.D. (London), B.Sc. (London), A.R.C.S., F.G.S., A.Inst.M.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schol., Crediton, Devon, Battersea Polytechnic Inst. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology, 1911-14; res. engrnr. on location and constrn. of Canadian Northern Ontario Rly., 1911-14; war serv., 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. survey, Nov., 1919; asst. dir. geol. surv., 1937.

COORAY, EDMUND JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B. (London), Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1931; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1931; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1934; do., Mannar and Mullaitivu kach., Mar., 1935; do., Kegalla kach., Nov., 1935; attd. dept. of co-op. socs., Oct., 1936; ag. dep. registr., Mar., 1938.

CORBETT, ROBERT LESLIE, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1895; ed. Ipswich Schol. and Royal Vety. Coll., London; demonstr. of path. and bact., Royal Vety. Coll., 1922; res. asst., Inst. of Animal Path., Lamb., 1924; R.A.V.C., 1915; sergt. dresser, transport corps for serv. in Palestine, 1919; vetry. res. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928; seconded to Siam, 1937.

CORBETT, GEORGE HAMBLIN, B.Sc., F.R.E.S.—B. 1887; ed. King Edwards Schol., Bath, and Medin., Cornell and Harvard Unive.; govt. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1920; deleg. of F.M.S. and S.S. govt., at 2nd Imp. Entomological Conference, London, June, 1925; constg. entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., and Rubber Resch. inst., in adzn., June, 1930-Aug., 1932 and Mar., 1935.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD PILLE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. trip., 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept., 1894; 4th asst. polit. res., Eden, Apr., 1898; 2nd res. asst., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admstr. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in despatches, medal and clasp); dep. commr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admstr. govt., June, 1905; commr., and comdr-in-chief, 15th May, 1906; gov., Uganda, 1st Feb., 1910; gov., St. Helena, 2nd Nov., 1911; assumed govt., 21st Feb., 1912; gov., Bahamas, 1920; ret. 1926.

COREA, CARLTON VICTOR DEMETRIUS DEMETRIEVATNAYE.—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd. Matare kach., Mar., 1927; ag. office asst., Kegalle kach., Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Pedro, Mar., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, June, 1933; settl. settlmt. offr., Dec., 1934.
CORNELL, CHARLES ALBERT.—Called to bar Gray's Inn, 1928; asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; ag. dist. commr., 1926; dist. commr., 1927.

CORNEY, LEONARD GEORGE.—B. 1886; B.A. Cantab., class. exhibtnr., St. John's Coll., Camb.; class. trip., 1908; examn. col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Feb., 1910; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1910; ag. audr., July, 1913, July to Dec., 1914, and Apl. to Oct., 1916; ag. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1917; seconded to G. Coast Regt., Apl., 1917 to Jan., 1918; senr. asst. audr., Jan., 1919; ag. audr., Jan., Oct., 1919; dep. audr., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. cent. office (Col. Audit Dept.), Feb., 1930; dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1931; ag. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932; audr., do., 1936.

CORNISH, HENRY HAVELOCK, K.C., M.A. (double 1st cls. hons.), LL.M.—B. 1887; ed. Kaitangata Schol., Otago Boys' High Schol., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice many years; pres., Wellington dist. law socty., 1926; mem., N.Z. law socty.; coun. of law reporting, rules comte., etc.; prof., English and N.Z. law, Victoria Univ. Coll., 1930-34; solr.-gen., N.Z., 1934.

CORNWALLIS, COL. SIR K., K.B.E. (1929), C.M.G. (1926), C.B.E., Mily. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), Commander of the Order of King George I. of Greece, Order of Nahdhah (2nd cls.), Order of the Nile (3rd cls.), Order of Majidie (4th cls.), three times mentioned in desps.—Sudan civ. serv., 1906-14; Egyptian civ. serv., 1914; dir., Arab Bureau, Cairo, 1916-19; dep. ch. polit., offr., Egyptian Exped. Force, 1918; asst. ch. polit., offr., ditto, 1919; attd. to Amir Faisal in London, 1919; attd. to F.O., 1920-21; accompanied Amir Faisal to 'Iraq and seconded to 'Iraq govt. for two years, June, 1921; adviser to min. of interior, 'Iraq, September, 1921.

CORRIE, SIR OWEN CECIL KIRKPATRICK, Kt. Bach. (1939), M.C.—B. 1882; ed. Monckton Combe Schol. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (maths. exhibr.); on active serv. in France, Belgium and Palestine with N. Somerset Yeomanry and R.F.A. (T.F.), 1915-19; M.C. and ment. in desps.; judl. offr., Nablus, O.E.T.A. (S.), Mar., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, O.E.T.A. (S.), Oct., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, Palestine, July, 1920; senr. Br. judge, sup. ct., Sept., 1924; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1922-26; ch. just., Fiji and ch. judl. commr., W. Pacific, 1936.

CORRY, WILFRED CHARLES STEWART, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1900; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1923; attdch secr., Taiping, Jan., 1924; attdch. land office, J. Bahrn, Apr., 1924; passed cadet, Jan., 1926; offr. cls. V, Dec., 1926; asst. collr., land rev. K. Lumpor, Mar., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Dec., 1930; pr. sec. to govt. S.S. and high commr., Malay States, July, 1931; offr. cls. III, Dec., 1935; ag. 1st asst. sec., S.S., July, 1936; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Nov., 1937; ag. asst. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., July, 1939.

CORSER, LIEUT. E. E.—Ed. Shrewsbury; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt. King's Shropshire Light Infantry (T.), Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1916; served in France, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 3rd Dec., 1919.

COSTAR, NORMAN EDGAR.—B. 1909; ed. Battersea Grammar Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; hist. schol., 1928-32; cls. I divn. 1, hist. trip., pts. I and II; B.A., 1931; Keller prize-man, 1931; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 21st Apr., 1932; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 21st Oct., 1935; seconded as asst. sec., office of high commr. for the U.K.; Canberra, Oct., 1937; ag. prin., 14th Nov., 1938; rtnd. to D.O., Dec., 1939.

COSTLEY-WHITE, C. G.—B. 1913; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod. greats, 1930; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 4th Oct., 1937; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S., 4th Dec., 1939.

COTCHING, HOUGHTON ALDGATE, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1887; serv. in mech. dept., N.E. Ry. Coy., 1903-15; on mil. serv., 1915; seconded for duty with R.E. (inland waterways and docks), 1917; major, D.A.D.R.T. and sent to Chemin de Fer du Hedjaz as loco. supt., 1919; ch. mech. engr., Palestine rly., 1920.

OULSON, NORMAN.—B. 1891; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; (M.A., hons. in cls. and mod. hist.); 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Regt., Cav., Sept., 1914; attd. 8th (K.R.I.) Hussars, Sept., 1915 (France and army of occupation); cadet F.M.S., Apr., 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; attd. to treasy., Penang on sp. duty, Sept., 1922; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1923; dist. offr., Raub, Feb., 1925; seconded for serv. in Kelantan, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1928, supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1928; mag., Seremban, Mar., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Selangor; June, 1931; offr., cls. II., Feb., 1933; ast. treasy., Penang, Apr., 1934; dist. offr., Seremban, June, 1937.

COURT, PHILIP FRANCIS SHELLEY, F.R.C.S. (Ed.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1900; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Oct., 1930; ditto, Victoria Hosp., Mar., 1932; ast. med. offr., Victoria Hosp., May, 1932; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., Mar., 1933; do., Victoria hosp., Feb., 1936; M.O., Queen Mary Hosp., June, 1937; in addn. ag. prof. of obstetrics and gynaecology, Univ. of Hong Kong, Dec., 1937.

COUSIN, D. P.—B. 1889; Brunel medallist, London Schl. of Economics, 1913; war serv. 1914-19; served in Salonica and Palestine (twice ment. in desps.); dist. storekeeper, Tanganyika rly., 1920; dep. ch. storekeeper, 1932; ch. storekeeper, 1934; stores supt., K.U.R. and H., 1934.

COUTTS, JAMES MAGNUS.—B. 1886; ed. Heriot's Schl., Schl. of Medicine and Univ., Edinburgh; L.D.S., R.C.S. (Ed.), 1924; served great war with Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; M.M., 1916; dental surg., Falkland Is., Oct., 1925; ag. loc. audr., Apr., 1926-Apr., 1927; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1927; capt., 1929; ag. O.C., May to Nov., 1928; 2nd in commd., 1927-31; dental offr., Straits Settlements, Aug., 1931; dent. offr., S.S. and dent. surg., S'pore, Jan., 1934; sec., dent. bd., S.S. in addn., Feb., 1934.

OWGILL, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1888; ed. Durham Schl. and All Souls Coll., Oxford (bible-clerk, 1907-11), B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; studied Chinese, 1912-14; on mily. serv., 1915-19; M.C., 1917, ment. in desps., 1919; dist. offr., Dec., 1919; ast. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1923; town planning admstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, June, 1926; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and regiar. titles, Selangor, Nov., 1927; ag. sec. to H. comsnr., June, 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Kinta and dist. sup't., prisons, July, 1930; offr., cls. I.B., 1931; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1934; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Mar., 1937; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1938; ag. dir.-gen., P. and T., Malaya, Sept., 1938; Br. res., N. Sembilan, 1939.
OWVIN, GEORGE STEWART, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1903; ed. Nat. Elementary Schl. and Royal (Dick) Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

OWPER, HERBERT WELLESLEY.—B. 1883; ed. at St. Paul's School, London, Hertford Coll.; Oxford, and in Germany, France and Italy; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; ag. res., 1916, 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1918.
COX, ALFRED JAMES, B.Sc. For. (Edin.)—B. 1902; ast. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; senr. ast. cons. of forests, July, 1937.

COX, ARTHUR HENRY, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1888; ed. Felsted and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; ast. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Nov., 1911; A.D.C. to gov., 1918-19; 1st gr. admstr. offr., Jan., 1922; dep. prov. comsnr., Dec., 1928; ag. prov. comsnr., July, 1929 and June, 1932; ast. ch. sec., Uganda, Mar., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., Mar., 1930 to Oct., 1931; prov. comsnr., Dec., 1932; readst., Buganda, Jan., 1939.

COX, CHARLES EDWARD LANGLETON.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1907; 2nd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office; called to the bar, 1918; stip. mag. for the colony; revising barrister, 1919; served in France as lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.); injured at Loos, Sept., 1916; invalided out of army, Nov., 1917; served under Admiralty as A.T.S.O., mining depot, Grangemouth, Mar. to Dee., 1918; extra A.D.C. to Sir W. Egerton, K.C.M.G., 1913-15; lieut., artillery coy., B. Guiana Militia, 1908; capt., 1919; pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1920-23; ag. atty.-gen. and ch. just., St. Vincent; crown atty. and registrar, sup. ct., Dominica, 1923-25; mem., exec. coun., income tax comsnr., offr. recvr., visiting just., prison, Dominica; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1925; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1928-36.

COX, LIEUT.-COL. SIR C. H. F., K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—Ed. Rugby and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R.H. and R.F.A., 6th Jan., 1900; served S. African War; served in India, 1906-08; seconded Sudan civ. serv., 1913-15; served in Great War, 1915-18; four times ment. in desps., D.S.O., 1917, bar, 1918; brevet. lieut.-col., 1st Jan., 1919; ast. admstr. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1919; mil. gov., Nablus, Mar., 1920; ditto, Galilee, Aug., 1920; dist. gov., Samaria, July, 1922; retd. by Army with rank of lieut.-col., Jan., 1923; ch. Pr. res. Trans-Jordan, Apr., 1924; ret., 1939.

COX, HERBERT CHARLES FAHIE., K.C. (1936)—Br. Guiana pol. 1913-19; called to bar, 1915; on war serv., France, 1917-19; ast. to atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1919; ag. mag. on various occasions; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1925; M.L.C., 1926; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1926, Mar.-Oct., 1927 and from June to Sept., 1929; chmn., bd. of health, 1926-27; chmn. water and sewage bd., 1928-29; dep. gov., Mar., 1929; dep. admstr., June and July, 1929; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Dec., 1929; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1933; ag. atty.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934; atty.-gen., 1935; comsnr. for revision of laws, Nigeria, 1938.
COX, HERBERT WILLIAM, F.I.A.C.—B. 1898; olk., K.U.R. and H., 1922; ast. acctnt., 1925; port acctnt., 1931; senr. ast. acctnt., 1933.

COX, LIONEL, A.S.A.A.—B. 1890; ed. St. Stephens Gram. Schl., Dublin; acctnt., govt. opium shops, S'pore, Feb., 1926; offr. i.c., govt. opium shops, S'pore, July, 1934; acctnt., med. dept., S.S., Sept., 1935; ag. acct.-gen., S.S., Mar., 1938.

CRABB, RICHARD BRUCE—B.1891; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1915; served European War, East Surrey Regt., 1916-18; dist. comsnr., 1922; ast. col. sec., 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1926; ag. clk., couns. in 1927 and 1928; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., Apr. to Sept., 1932; senr. ast. col. sec., Sept., 1932.
CRAGG, WILLIAM GILLIAT, D.S.O. (1918), Major, R. of O. Loyal Regt.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and R.M.C., Sandhurst; ast. dist. offr., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1920.

CRAIG, ERNEST DUNCAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.Z.S.—B. 1888; ed. Aberdeen Univ. and at Exeter Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Jan., 1926; ag. ch. inspr., educn., N. Provs., 1886; ag. prin., Kaduna Coll., Nov., 1887-Sept., 1938; senr. supt., Apr., 1938; ag. ast. dir., educn., Sept., 1938; senr. educn. offr., Oct., 1938.

CRAIG, GEORGE ERNEST, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin).—B. 1886; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1911; apptd. to W.A.M.S. (Gambia), Sept., 1920; transf. to Nigeria, Feb., 1929; S.M.O., Aug., 1929; A.D.M.S., June, 1936; D.D.M.S., Gold Coast, 1937.

CRAIG, JOHN.—B. 1884; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1st cl. hon., Classics; univ. prize in Latin, 1906; and at Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 2nd cl. hon., lit. hum., 1909; after exam., examr., E. and A. dept., Jan., 1909; ast. andtor., S. Nigeria, May, 1909; temporarily transf. to N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; joined col. audit dept., Jan., 1910; auditor, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Sir W. Collett, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1914; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov. R. Walter, C.M.G., gov. W.Hart-Bennett, C.M.G., and gov. Eyre-Hutson, C.M.G., 1915-19; lieut., B. Honduras Territorial Force; audr., Mauritius, Apr., 1920; col. audit dept., 1st Apr., 1924; dep. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926; ag. audr., Mar.-Nov., 1927; treas., Fiji, Apr., 1930; comsrr., inland rev. and stamp duties, Nov., 1930; chmn., economy comttee., 1931; chmn., tariff revision comttee., 1932; mem., Fiji pub. serv. reorganisation comttee., 1937; finan. sec. and treas., 1937; ag. col. sec., various occasions, 1938-39.

CRAIG, RUPERT ERICHMANS, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1897; apprentice, P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1913; served with H.M. Forces, 1915-19; ment. in desps.; ag. ast. engrnr. and ch. draughtsman, Oct., 1921-July, 1922; ast. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jan., 1924; seconded to consultants, Georgetown improvement schemes, June-July, 1923; seconded to harbr. bd. as engrnr.-in-ch., Berbice River training wks., Jan. to Oct., 1925; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., 1926; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., 1929; ag. D.P.W., 1939; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1939; mem., various local govt. comttees.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C., O.B.E. (1934).—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Islds., Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1897; priv. sec. to admnrs., Apr., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; treasy. clk., 1899; J.P., Nov., 1909; postmr., man., savings bank, regnr.-gen., and regnr. of sup. ct., and offl. admnstr., 1911, temp. memb., leg. coun., 1911 and 1914; supt., oensus, 1911 and 1921; local audr., 1916-26; regnr.-gen. and mag., 1926; temp. memb., exec. coun., 1923; treas. and collr., cust., Aug., 1926; has acted as mag. and regnr., sup. ct., and as col. postmr. on various occasions; ag. col. sec., May-July, 1933 and Feb.-Sept., 1934; dep. gov., various periods 1932 to 1937; ag. gov. in 1933, 1934, 1935 and 1937; col. sec., Jan., 1935.

CRAN, CAPT. PETER MCLELLAN, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; 2nd lieut. R.E. (S.R.), 1911; ast. engrnr., govt. rlys., N. Nigeria, 1911; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1912; ast. div. offr., R.E., Forth and Clyde Defences, Scottish Comd., Mar., 1914; div. offr., do., Oct., 1914; ast. to cmdng. royal engrnr., do., Nov., 1916; ment. in desps., Feb., 1917 and Aug., 1919; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1928; temp. ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, Jan., 1931; divnl. engrnr., Mar., 1931; title changed to exec. engrnr., Jan., 1939.

CRANE, ALFRED VICTOR, LL.B. (Lond.), Hons.—B. 1892; solr., sup. ct., judic., Eng. and Br. Guiana; el. mem. Br. Guiana legislature, 1926-33; dep. mayor, Georgetown, 1923; mem. several select and other govt. comttees.; mem. Georgetown sewage and water bd.; mem., bd. of govrs., Queen's Coll.; chmn., "Small Farmers" comttee.; senr. mag., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1933; called to bar (Inner Temple) 1935; chmn., mag.'s oomsn. for making rules of procedure and practice; author of "Law of Unlawful Possession," "Law of Workmen's Compensation," "Workmen's Compensation Practice" (1938), "Law of Compulsory Motor Vehicle Insurance" (1939); chmn., Landlord and Tenant comttee.

CRAREY, WILLIAM RAYMOND DUNSTAN, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Gresham's Sch., Holt and London Univ.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. engrnr., in 1930, 1931, 1933 and 1934; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1935.

CRAWFORD, JOHN GERALD.—B. 1894; ed. King's Sch., Chester and Merton Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1920; lieut., Cheshire regt., T.F., Nov., 1915; capt., June, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; attd. to dist. office, K. Selangor, Apr., 1921; attd. to office of conserv., forests, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, Feb., 1923; superny. offr., cls. V, Feb., 1922; ast. dist. offr., B. Padang, Nov., 1923; ditto, Kroh, May, 1924; offr., cls. IV, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, June, 1927; attd. to co-op. socs. dept., Oct., 1931; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1931; ast. dir., co-opern. (North), Apr., 1932; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1936.
CRAWSHAW, EDWARD DANIEL WESTON.—B. 1903; ast. admnstr.-gen., Tanganyika, 1933; admnstr.-gen., Zanzibar, 1939.

CREAN, SIR BERNARD ARTHUR, Kt. Bach. (1938), Barrister-at-law.—Dep. regar. of titles, Kenya, Oct., 1920; res. mag., Apr., 1921; pulsm judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, Apr., 1930; ag. pres. dist. atf., June to Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just.; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1934; do., Cyprus, 1938.

CREASE, LEONARD GEORGE, M.A., (Oxon), M.R.S.T.—B. 1891; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; educn. appts. in England, 1908-26; war serv., 1915-17; Ceylon, 1926; inspr. schls., Br. Guiana, 1931; dir., educn., 1933; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1939.

CREASY, GERALD HALLEN, O.B.E. (1937)—B. 1897; ed. Rugby; schol., King's Coll., Camb., 1915; served with R.G.A., 1916-19; ast. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to parl. under sec. for Dominion affrs., 1925-27; dep. sec., C.O. confere., 1927; prin., 1927; accomp. parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Ormby-Gore) to Caylon, Malaya and Java, 1928; sec., Col. Agrl. Coun., 1929-31, and of confere. of dirs. of agr., 1931; accomp. parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Lord Plvmouth) to W. Africa, 1935; priv. ptye. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Ormby-Gore), 19th Aug., 1937; ast. sec. 1st Apr., 1939.
CREERY, RANDALL LESLIE, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. R. "Dick" Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; mily. serv., 1914-20; vety. offr., Kenya, 1920; senr. do., 1939.

CRERAR, HON. THOMAS ALEXANDER.—Ed., pub. schls. and Collegiate, Portage la Prairie, Manitoba; teacher, farmer, grain elevator operator; invited to enter Union govt., 1917; P.C., Canada, 1917; min. of agr., 1917-19; M.P. Marquette, Manitoba, 1917-25; leader, progressive party, 1921-22; M.P., Brandon, 1930; min. of rly. and canals, 1929-30; defeated, g.e. 1930; re-el., g.e. 1935 as M.P. for Churchill; min. of mines, min. of immigr. and colonization, min. of interior, and supt. gen., Indian affrs. in Mackenzie King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

CRESSALL, PAUL EWART FRANCIS, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ent. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1911; cler. ast., secretariat and sub-inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1912; ag. dist. inspr., 1914-15; 2nd lieut., B. Guiana Militia Infy., 1914; served in B.W.I. Regt. as capt., E. Africa and Palestine, 1916-19; dist. inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1920; J.P. and coroner, 1920; ag. county inspr., 1920; passed final law exam., 1921; capt., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; ag. staff offr., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; called to the bar, Gray’s Inn, 1923; county inspr., pol., 1923; commnr., for oaths and revising barrister, 1924; stip. mag., Apr., 1924; ag. stip. mag., Georgetown, Aug., 1924 to Dec., 1924 and July to Dec., 1926; chmn., comsn. of enquiry into Onderneeming indu. schl., Sept., 1925; ag. chmn., soldiers’ pensions bd., 1925; chmn., ditto, Nov., 1926; senr. stip. mag., Nov., 1930; senr. Br. mag., Palestine, June, 1931; ag. pres., Nablus dist. ct., May, 1933; do., Jerusalem dist. ct., June, 1933; chmn., legal bd., June, 1933; relieving pres., dist. ct., Aug., 1934; pres., dist. ct., 1936.

CREWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911); ROBERT OFFLEY ASHBURTON CREWE-MILNES, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Great Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl of Crewe (cr. 1895); Earl of Madeley (cr. 1911); ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; ast. priv. sec. to sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), 1883-84; lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-95; lord pres. of the coun., 1905-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1908; Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with Secretaryship of State), 1908; S. of S. for India, 1910-15; accompanied H.M. King George V on the occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; lord pres. of the coun., 1915.

CREWE, EDWIN CHARLES.—B. 1886, 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O. Lond., 1906; ch. clk. and acctnt., P. & T., Gold Coast Dec., 1911; acctnt., 1913; ch. acctnt., Jan., 1920; dep. P.M.G., Apr., 1929; P.M.G., Nigeria, Aug., 1936.
CRICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; med. offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914; ag. senr. med. offr. on several occasions; travelling med. offr., July, 1927.
CRISP, HON. SIR HAROLD, K.B., (1938).—B. 1874; called to bar, 1896; puisne judge, Tasmania, Aug., 1914; admstr. of govt., 1930; ch. jnst., 1937.

CROCKETT, HON. OSWALD SMITH, LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. pub. and high schls., Fredericton, N.B., Canada, and Univ. of New Brunswick; B.A., 1886; atty., sup. ct., N.B., 1891; called to bar, 1892; M.P. (Can.) for York County, N.B., 1904-11; judge, king’s bench div., sup. ct., N.B., 1913; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1932.

CRONIN, BRIAN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll.; served B.S.A. pol. and Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1911; 2nd grade clk., 1921; ag. sub-inspr., 1922; ag. ast. res. mag., 1926; passed 2nd grade Seawana exam., 1923; ag. res. mag., 1927; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1929.

CROOK, WALTER VERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; comsud. Reg. Army, East Surrey Regt., Dec., 1918; served N. Russia, 1919; seconded under C.O., Mar., 1926; cadet, Uganda, 1926; ast. dist. offr., Uganda, June, 1928, and placed on Reg. Army, R. of O.; dist. offr., 1938.

CROSBIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919).—M.H.A., Bay de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comtce., 1915; chmn., tonnage comtce., 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSBIE, ROBERT EDWARD HAROLD, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (class. tripes, hon. cl. II.), 1908; Egyptian civ. serv., 1910-19; seconded for mily. serv. with E.E.F., 1915; apptd. to O.E.T.A. (S.), 1918; dep. mily. gov., Brisban, 1919; dep. mily. gov., Tiberias, 1920; dep. dist. gov., Jenin, 1920; ast. gov. (continuing as ast. dist. coman.), S. dist., 1922; passed advanced Arabic exam., 1924; dist. coman., Southern dist., 1931.

CROSS, GRAHAM.—Cik., native ct., Johannesburg, May, 1902; ast. res. mag. and ast. mag., native ct., Witwatersrand dist., Aug., 1908; mag., Nov., 1915; under sec. for lands, Sept., 1923; sec. for lands, Aug., 1932; chmn., pub. serv. comsn., Jan., 1934.

CROSS, MATTHEW ARNOLD.—B. 1881; clk., deeds, Transvaal, Mar., 1902; examr., deeds, July, 1904; 2nd grade examr., deeds, Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 1st gr. ditto, Nov., 1919; ch. examr., July, 1922; ast. regnr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Nov., 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928; regnr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Apr., 1933; do., Pretoria, Feb., 1934; ch. regnr., deeds, Union and regnr., deeds, Transvaal, July, 1937.

CROSSE-CROSSE, WILLIAM WICKHAM REDER.—B. 1899; ed. Blundells schl.; 2nd lieut., Welsh Guards, 1918; lt., 1920; served with 1st and 2nd K.A.R., in Tanganyika Territory, 1922-24; R. of O., (Welsh Guards), 1924; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1924; ast. dist. offr., 1926; ag. lab. comsnr., 1929; temp. ast. prin., C.O., 1930-32; dist. offr., 1936; ag. ast. ch. sec., Dec., 1938.
CROUCHER, HERBERT HARRY, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Reading Univ.; lect. in chem. and soil sci., Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. chemist, Jamaica, 1931.
CROZIER, DOUGLAS JAMES SMYTH, B.A. (Hons.), Queen’s Univ., Belfast, Dip. Ed., London.—B. 1908; ast. mast. educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1931.

CRUICKSHANK, ARTHUR HOWARD, M.I.Mar.E.—B.1888; jur. mech. engrn. and river surv., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. mech. engrn., 1921; transfd. as engrn., transport offr. to motor transport dept., Aug., 1922; ag. ch. mech. transport offr., 1924; ag. ch. transport offr. for various periods, 1926-34; ch. transport offr., 1937.

CRUIKSHANK, JOHN MERRILL, O.B.E. (1937), M.D., C.M., McGill Univ. Canada 1925, Licentiate Med. Coun. of Canada, 1925—Med. dir., Alexander sanatorium (mental diseases) San Francisco, 1927-28; med. offr., Bahamas, 1928; res. surg., 1929; ag. dir., med. services in 1930; ch. med. offr. and res. surg., 1931.

CRUTCHLEY, ERNEST TRINTRAM, C.B. (1938), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (civ.), 1926, O.B.E. (mily.), 1918.—B. 1878; lieut.-col., R.E., T.F. Res.; ast. surv., G.P.O., 1908; dep. dir., Army Postal serv. (T.F.), 1914-19; staff offr., grade I, miny. of transport, 1919; ag. prin., ch. sec.’s office, Dublin, 1921; Irish office and mem., Irish grants comtce., 1922; C.O., 1923; ag. finance offr., overseas settle-

CRUTTWELL, HUMPHREY JOHN.—B. 1909; ed. Harrow and Gonville & Caius Coll., Camb. (Sayer schol.); Browne Medallist, 1929; B.A., 1931; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1933; 3rd ass't col. sec., Oct., 1933; att. to C.O., Mar.-July, 1933; dist. offr., South, Nov., 1938.

CRUX, RICHARD MILLETT.—B. 1880; ed. in India; dept. of Transvaal prisons, dir., prisons office, Jan., 1904; asst. warden, Houtpoort Reformatory, Apr., 1916; prison supt., 1923; supt., prisons, Pretoria commd., 1934; war serv., Anglo-Boer War, 1900-02; Great War, G.B.E., 1916-17.
CRYAN, ROBERT.—B. 1889; asst. elec. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; elec. engrnr., 1934; ch. electr. engrnr., 1938.

CUBITT, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS ASTLEY, K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1903)—B. 1871; ed. Haileybury and R.M.C. Woolwich; ent. army, 1891; capt., 1900; served W. Africa, 1898 (med. and clasp); staff offr., W. Africa (despa., brevet-major, med. with clasp) 1901; W. Africa, (despa., clasp), 1902; Kanosokoto campaign, 1903 (despa., clasp, D.S.O.); European war in commd., troops, Somaliland, 1914-15; in commd., divn., France, with rank of major-gen., 1918; G.O.C., Presidency and Assam dist., 1920-24; 53rd Welsh Divn., T.A., Shrewsbury, 1927; G.O.C., 2nd Divn., 1928; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Bermuda, 1931.

CUFF, CYRIL CHARLES HERBERT, O.B.E. (1936). M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1888; ed. Guy's and Durham; lieut., R.N.V.R., 1914; passed Bd. of Trade exam. for master, 1916; transf'd to R.A.M.C., 1918; house surg. and bacteriologist, St. Lucia, 1921-23; resigned; re-ent. col. serv. and apptd. to Seychelles as sen. ass't med. offr., res. surg. and visiting mag., outlying islds., 1924; ag. ch. med. offr., June to Nov., 1924; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Apr., 1926; surg. specialist, Dec., 1928; ag. dir., health on various occasions in 1928 and 1932-33; ag. sen. med. offr., 1934; ag. dir., med. services, June, 1937 and May-Nov., 1938.

CULLEN, ARTHUR ARMSTRONG.—B. 1897; ed. Christ Hosp. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1919; 2nd cls. mod., 1914; army and air force, 1914-19; ment. in despa.; admin. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; cls. II, 1939.

CULLEN, JAMES REYNOLDS, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; ed. Tonbridge and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Hertford schol., 1919; 1st hors. mods., 1920; 2nd lit. hum., 1922; dir., educon., Cyprus, Sept., 1930; do., Mauritius, 1939.

JUMARASWAMY, SUBRAMANIAM DAVIDS, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd. Badulla kach, Feb., 1929; attd. Batticaloa kach Jan., 1930; office asst. to asst. gov't agt., Matale, Mar., 1930; attd., Kandy kach., Sept., 1933; office asst., Kandy kach., Feb., 1934; addnl. asst. gov't agt., Matara and Hambantota, Oct., 1936.
CUMMINGS, D. J.—Many years serv., N.Z. pol. asp. comntr., pol., 1936.
CUNDALL, JOSEPH LESLIE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1906; barrister-at-law (Inner Temp.), 1928; dep. clk., cts., Jamaica, 1929; clk., etc., 1933; res. mag., 1937; pol. mag., Sierra Leone, 1939.

CUNLIFFE-LISTER, Rt. Hon. Sir Philip, G.B.E. (1929), K.B.E. (1920), P.C. (1922), M.C., M.P. (Hendon).—B. 1884; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; called to bar, 1908; served in army, 1914-17; joint sec. of Miny. of National Serv., 1917-18; chmn., perm. lab. comtee. of War Cabinet war priorities comtee., 1918; mem., select comtee. on nat. expend. and select comtee. on high prices and profits, 1919; partly, sec. to B. of T., 1920-21; sec., D.O.T., 1921-22; chmn., Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; pres., B. of T., 1922-23, 1924-29 and Aug.-Nov., 1931; S. of S. for the Cols., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for Air, 14th June, 1935.

CUNNINGHAM, JOSEPH MOLONEY, M.M.—B. 1895; ed. Hughes Coll., Belfast; Imp. P.O., Sept. 1912; served in France and Belgium, 1914-19; junr. postmr., Tanganyika, 1920; survr., posts and tele., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. asctnt., 1934 and 1936; divnl. survr., Feb., 1937; ag. contr., posts, 1936 and 1938.

CURLE, ALEXANDER TANGRED, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1900; army, 1918-29; commr., grade III, Somaliland, 1929; ag. vice cons., Jijiga, 1930; ag. cons., Harar, 1932; polt. offr. and asst. commr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bdry. commn., 1933-35; attd., Somaliland Camel Corps as polt. offr., 1935-36.

CURMI, CAPT. HENRY C. O.B.E. (1937.)—B. 1890; ed. Malta Lyceum, Univ. and prte. tuition; matric. (Italian, French and Spanish), 1904; 1st place Malta civ. serv. exam., 1908; served in cust., lieut. gov't, treas., educ. and charitable institutions depts.; 2nd lieut., K.O. Malta Regt., 1910; mobilised 1914 and transf'd. to R. Malta Arty., 1915; with Med. Expedy. Force at Mudros and Anzac from Sept., 1915 to evacuation; ment. in despa., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1916-19; demob., Apr., 1920; served shipping office, cust., sec. charitable institutions comtee., 1920; sec. to min. for posts and agr. and in charge of lab. bureau, 1921; sec., emigrn. comtee., 1922; sec. and offr.-in-charge, tourist comtee., 1923-28; comntr. for lab., Apr., 1928; comntr. for Malta in Australia, 1929 and 1936.
CURRID, PATRICK GRANT, M.B., Ch.B., M.A. (Aberd.), D.P.H. (Univ. Lond.) Certif. L.S.T.M. (with dist.).—B. 1897; war serv., 1916-19; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928.

CURRY, H. D.—B. 1893; ed. Dulwich coll.; served in Fiji, 1911-15; ag. 2nd cls. clk., C.S.O., 1915; cadet, Solomon Islands, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; on mily. serv., 1917-19; 2nd lieut., 1919; priv. sec. to gov. of B. Honduras, 1919; resumed duty Solomon Is., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, 1920; ag. sec., 1920; admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923; seconded as lab. offr., 1926-32; dist. offr., 1936.

CURRY, WILLIAM CLIVE, M.C.—B.1895; ed. City of London Schl.; mily. serv., 1916-18; M.C., 1917; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1922; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927; asst. admstr.-gen., 1928; Br. mag., Palestine, 1932; relieving pres. dist. ct., 1937.

CURTIS, CECIL VIVIAN.—B. 1903; Welsh Guards, 1920-23; Br. Palestine gendarmerie, 1923; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1926; staff offr., July, 1934-Jan., 1935; supt., pol., Zanzibar, May, 1935; asst. supt., pol., Uganda, 1937.

CURTIS, RICHARD JOHN FROUDE.—B. 1897; ed. Blundell's Schl. and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1917; lieut., 1920; R. of O., 1920; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1923; dist. offr., N. Telab, Dec., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; Br. res., Brunei, Dec., 1928; res., Labuan, Aug., 1929; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1931; A.D.C. to gov., June, 1931; offr., cls.
III, Nov., 1932; priv. sec. to gov., Dec., 1932; 2nd mag., S'pore, Aug., 1933; 3rd mag., Sept., 1933; mag., Seremban, Nov., 1934; collr., land rev., Penang, Apr., 1935; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1937.

CUSACK, CAPT. HENRY VERNON (Res. of Offrs.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Aravon Schl. and Royal Coll. of Sci., Ireland; served in European War, Flanders and N. Russia, 1915-19; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, July, 1920; asst. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1922; senr. asst. audr., Nyasaland, May, 1928; ag. audr. in 1928, 1929 and 1932; asst. dir., col. audit dept., July, 1933; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1937; audr., 1938.

CUSACK, JOHN RALPH.—B. 1902; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; apptd. to col. audit dept. as asst. audr., Nigeria, June, 1929; audr., Leeward Is., Mar., 1935; ag. treas., Antigua and Fed. treas., L. I. in addn., July-Dec., 1935; audr., Mauritius, Nov., 1938.
CUSCADEN, CAPT. ROBERT LYONS.—B. 1886; ed. Scots Coll., Melbourne; asst. supt., chandu monopoly, K. Lumpur, July, 1923; supt., cust., July, 1925; supt., Ipoh, 1936.

CUTTERIDGE, JOSEPH OLIVER, M.B.E.(1932), A.C.P., F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; ed. Borough Road Coll., Isleworth, London, 1906-08; teacher's certif. (with 4 distinctions) Bd. of Educn., Lond.; A.C.T. cert., Ed. of Educn., Lond.; staff-capt., H.M. Forces; inspr. Q.M.G. services; maths and drawing master, Junr. Tech. Schl., Liverpool, 1919; prin. govt. training coll., Trinidad, Jan., 1921; mem. Inter-Col. Educn. Confsc., 1921; mem. bd. indust. training; supt., tech. classes, 1922; ag. senr. inspr. schl., Feb., 1923; senr. inspr. schl., Trinidad, 1st July, 1923; ag. dir., educn., July, 1926 to Mar., 1926; asst. dir., educn. and senr. inspr., schl., June, 1926; asst. dir., educn. and ch. inspr., schl., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., educn., May to Nov., 1929; dir., educn., 1934; author "West Indian Readers" etc.

CUZEN, ALLAN LECKIE.—B. 1882; served in R.E., 1901-2; S. African War (medal and five clasps); Cape police, 1902-6; Bech. Prot. police, 1906-14; passed Cape Civ. serv. lower law exam., 1914; 2nd clk. to asst. comsrr., N. Dist., Bech. Prot., 1914; ag. asst. res. mag., ditto, 1915; passed 2nd grade Seawana exam., 1916; 1st grade (B), ditto, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1917; ag. asst. res. mag., 1919; res. mag., 1921; dist. comsrr., 1937.

DABNER, CHARLES J.—B. 1876; on mily. serv., France and Flanders, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; clk., Army pay office, R. Engnr., Mar. to Oct., 1919; clk., Army pay office, command paymr., Overseas Bases, Oct., 1919 to July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 10th July, 1922.

DAKERS, COLIN HUGH, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Manch. Gram. Schl.; war serv., 1915-19; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1923; inspr. liquor shops, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1932; asst. dir., educon., Chinese schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1932; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1933; do., Penang, Aug., 1934; do., Selangor and Pahang, May, 1935; ag. mag., K. Lumpur, Oct., 1936; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1937; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Jan., 1938.
DALE, IVAN ROBERT.—B. 1904; ed. St. Olaves and Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A. sci. (hons.), forestry; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Oct., 1928; asst. conserv., forests, Uganda, 1938.

DALE, WILLIAM LEONARD.—B. 1906; ed. Hymers Coll., Hull; LL.B. Lond.; Holt. schol., Gray's Inn, 1929; called to bar, 1932; N. Eastern circuit, N.R.Y. and E.R.Y. sessions; 3rd asst. legal advr., D.O. and C.O., Feb., 1935; author of "Law of the Parish Church" (1933).

DALLARD, BERKELEY LIONEL.—B. 1889; ed. Rangiora High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. govt. serv., 1907; stamp duties dept., 1907-13; audit office, 1913-18; advisory acctnt., bd. of trade, 1919-23; inspr., pub. serv. consmr.'s office, 1924-26; contr.-gen., prisons from 1926; also asst. pub. serv. comsrr., 1929-33; and under sec. justice and registr.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, 1933.

DALLEY, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1900; pol. probr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920, probry. a.c.p., Jan., 1922; on depm. to Kedah; a.c.p., Nov., 1923; lst a.c.p., Selangor, Dec., 1924; c.p.o., Johore S., Apr., 1926; a.c.p., K. Kangar, June, 1931; a.c.p., S'pore, Sept., 1932; comdt., pol. depot, Nov., 1933; dep. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Aug., 1934; comsrr., pol., Trengganu, Oct., 1936; title changed to supt., July, 1938.

DALTON, ALFRED, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1892; G.S. and W.R., Ireland, 1909; war serv., 1914-19; asst. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., Nov., 1920; traffic supt., 1930; asst. supt., line, Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbors., 1936; supt., 1938.

DALTON, NIELLEWYCH CHISHOLM, KT. BACH. (1938).—B. 1879; ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1900 (Hist. Tripos); M.A., 1905; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1901; legal ass't., land setttn., bd., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; asst. rec. mag. and J.P., June, 1902 to Nov., 1910; advoc.; sup. et., 1904; regier., B. Guiana, Nov., 1910; judge advoc., local forces, 1914; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1912 to Feb., 1913; Nov., 1914 to Jan., 1915; July, 1916, to June, 1917; July to Sept., 1918; Mar. to June, 1919; ag. solr.-gen., May to Aug., 1914; Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915; ag. atty.-gen., Aug.-Sept., 1914; Feb.-Apr., 1918; ag. chief just., July-Sept., 1917; June-Oct., 1919; puisne judge, B. Guiana, June, 1919; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Feb., 1923; puisne judge, Ceylon, Mar., 1925; ag. ch. just., June-Nov., 1933 and Apr.-July, 1936; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; ret., 1939; compiled and edited "Statutory Rules and Orders (B. Guiana)," to 1914, 2 vols.; editor, B. Guiana Law Reports, 9 vols.; an asst. editor "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws," 2nd edit., vol. iv.; compiled Digest of Case Law of British Guiana, 1910-1920; author of "Civil Law of British Guiana."

DALTON, PHILIP NKALE, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1909; ed. Downside and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1933; practised in London and South-Eastern circuit, 1933-37; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Dec., 1937.

D'ANDRADO, WILLIAM ALBERT.—B. 1884; cler. asst., surg. gen'l's office, R. Guiana, 1903; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1903; 5th ditto, 1906; 4th ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, 1921; 2nd ditto, 1928; ch. clk., cust., 1925; compt., cust., 1927; M.L.C., 1930; mem. exec. coun., 1938; del., cust. confce., Trinidad, 1930, Bermuda, 1931, Jamaica, 1937.

DANDURAND, Hon. Senator Raoul, K.C., LL.D., P.C.—B. 1861; ed. Montreal Coll. and Laval Univ.; LL.B., 1882; LL.D., 1909 and hon. LL.D., McGill, Toronto, and Queen's Univ.; pres., Univ. of Montreal, Feb., 1934; called to Quebec bar, 1883; K.C., 1898; called to senate of Canada by Lord Aberdeen, 1898; speaker of senate, 1905-9; P.C., 1909; min. of state and leader of King govt. in senate, 1921, also in 2nd King govt., 1926, and in 3rd King govt., 1935; leader of lib. party, senate, 1930; served as R. comsnr., on educn., Quebec, 1910; el. pres., 6th assem., L. of N., Geneva, 1925; del. of Canada to coun., L. of N., 1927-30; apptd. by pres. of U.S.A., arbitr. under treaty of peace with Brazil.

DANEEL, Josias, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Edin. Univ.; admitted F.R.C.S., Edin., 1922; med. supt., Rietfontein hosp., Union of S. Africa, 1927.

DANGERFIELD, Paul, F.R.I.B.A., M.C., Chev. Leg. d'Honneur.—B. 1894; ed. St. Albans schl.; archit. educn.; articled, 1910-13, and A.A. schl., 1919-20; war serv., B.E.F., 1914-19; gov't. archt., Kenya, 1926.

DANIEL, EDWARD WELLEY, B.A., (Lond.), F.R.S.A., A.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1895; ed. DANIEL, REGINALD YOUNG, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1894; on mil'y. serv., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, civ. serv., Oct., 1920; ag. office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Jan., 1922; office asst. DARBY, Capt. George, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1884; active serv., France, Belgium, N. and S. DAUBNEY, Robert, C.M.G. (1939), O.B.E. (1937), M.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1891; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., and Liverpool Univ.; mil. serv., 1914-19; resch. asst., R. Vety. DAVEY, Peter Christmas.—B. 1914; ed. Eastbourne Coll.; A.D.C. to gov' of Aden, Mar., 1936; polit. offr., Aden, Oct., 1938.

DAVIDSON, ROGER ALASTAIR McLAREN, M.A. (Edin.) (hons.).—B. 1900; ed., Fettes Coll., Edinburgh and Univ. of Edinburgh; supt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, Sept., 1924; ast. dir., educn., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1937.

DAVIDSON, WALKER WHEATLEY, M.C., M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ast. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; exec. engrn., July, 1923; senr. exec. engrn., Nov., 1932; do., Larut, Matang and Krian, Apr., 1934; state engrn., 1938.

DAVIES, D. E.—B. 1889; ed., Llandovery and Worcester Coll., Oxon.; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., May-July, 1915; ag. dist. comsnr., Addah, July, 1915 to May, 1916; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, Sep., 1916 to Sep., 1917.

DAVIES, DAVID, A.R. San. I.—B. 1887; sany. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for mily. serv., 1917-20; storekeeper, sany. dept., 1921; 1st clk. and ast. sec., sany. bd., 1923; sec., sany. bd., 1927; ch. clk., senr. cler. and actg. staff, col. secretariat, 1928; J.P., 1930.

DAVIES, EDWARD HAROLD.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1922; ditto, Panadura, May, 1922, office ast. DAVIES, EVAN WALTER.—Admitted solr., sup. et., England, 1912; lieut., East Surrey Regt., France, 1915-17; adjt., 4th Nigerian Regt., 1918; served, Exha Rebellion, 1918; mem., Nigerian bar, 1920-27; ast. comnr., lands, Gold Coast, Jan., 1928; crown solr., Hong Kong, Aug., 1937.

DAVIES, KENNETH ARTHUR, M.Sc., Ph.D. (Cantab.).—B. 1897; field geologist, Uganda, 1929; senr. ast. geologist, 1936; dir., geol. survey, Uganda, Apr., 1939.

DAVIES, RICHARD MORGAN, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; on mily. serv., Dec., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; office ast., Kegalla kach., Jan., 1922; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1923; dep. rubber contr., May, 1924; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Trinco malee, June, 1926; ast. sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Nov., 1928; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Nov., 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Mar., 1932; ast. sttlmt. offr., July, 1932; ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1936.

DAVIS, HON. HENRY HAGUE.—B. 1885; ed., Brockville, Ont., Can.; Collegiate Inst., Toronto Univ. (B.A. 1907, gold medallist in polit. sci., M.A., LL.B.); Law Schil., Osgoode Hall, Toronto, 1911; called to Ontario bar 1911; K.C., 1928; judge, supreme ot., Ont., 1933.

DAVIS, LIONEL STUART, F.C.S.—B. 1890; lab. ast., Br. DAVIS, REGINALD PERCY BASIL.—B. 1881; ed., Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1908; K.C., 1924; leader of Cape bar, 1933; judge, Cape of Good Hope prov. divn., sup. ot., S. Africa, 1935.

DAVIS, RICHARD PRITCHARD, M.I.M. & C.E., M.T.P.I., M.R.S.I.—B. 1888; town planning supt., Selangor, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; town planner, Selangor, July, 1932; do., Perak, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Oct., 1933.

DAVIS, SIR STEUART SPENCER, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1875; rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 1st clk., treasy. and cust., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; ast. col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., July, 1901; acctnt., treasy., G. DAWE, ARTHUR JAMES, C.M.G. (1938), O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Berkhamsted Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; scholar, 1910-1914; B.A.; war service, 1914-18; staff, 1917; lieut., R.N.V.R. DAWE, MORLEY THOMAS, O.B.E. DAWSON, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class schol.); 2nd lieut., 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; served India (N.W. Frontier), Mesopotamia; demobilised with rank of capt., 1919; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1920; 2nd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1922; dist. advr., P. II, Mar., 1937; Br. advr., Perlis, July, 1937.

DAWSON, PERCY LEE.—B. 1890; ed. Needham House (Ely), Camb.; office ast., Nigerian Army, 1920; ast. traffic supt., 1921; dist. traffic supt., Feb., 1935; senr. traffic ast., open lines, 1938; ag. ch. traffic supt., Apr.-Oct., 1938, Apr., 1939.

DAY, SIR ALBERT CECIL, KT, B.A.C. (1932), M.G. (1925), C.B.E. (1919).—Ast. priv. sec. to gov. of New Zealand, 1910-1912; offr. sec. to gov., 1913-36; apptd. to staff of N.Z. high comsnr., London, 1936.

DAY, EDWARD VICTOR GRACE.—B. 1896; ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch and Timaru High Schl., New Zealand; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1921; attd., col. sec. office, Nov., 1921; ast. sec., Malaya Borneo exhibn., Dec., 1921; A.D.C. to O.A.G., June, 1922; sec., many bd., A. DEAKIN, CHARLES SIDNEY, M.A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Struc.E.—B. 1897; ed. at Orme Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne, Christ's Hosp. and Queen's Coll., Camb., schol., Hughes Eng. essay prize, 1st cl. mech. tripods, engrg., 1923; research grant, bridge oscillations, 1923-24; junr., engrg. dept., science and indust. research, bridge stress coms., 1924-25; John Wimbolt prize, engin. research, 1925; R.A.S.C. DEANE, SIR GEORGE CAMPBELL, KT, B.A.C. (1930).—B. 1873; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; M.A., Oxon. (honours in jurisprudence); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1898; practised at the bar, B. DEANE, LIEUT.-COL. ROBERT, C.B.E. (1937), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; S.A. War, 1900-02; S.A. constab., 1901; Transvaal pol., 1908; S.A. Perm. Force (Staff), 1912; German S.W.A. DEAR, EUGENE CLYDE.—B. 1885; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1902; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; clk. to atty.-genl., 1924; ag. sol. gen. for various periods, 1926-28; regiar., 1930; judge, ast. ct. of appl., and regiar. of friendly socs., 1931.

Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Harvard Univ., U.S.A.; sen. lecturer, economics, Univ. of Cape Town, 1923-24; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Bank, 1932; author of "Finance of the Union of South Africa," "Government Ownership in South Africa," and "Economic History of South Africa."

DE KRETZER, HERBERT KENNETH, M.Inst. C.E., M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., F.R.San.I.—B. 1890; P.W.D., Ceylon, 1904; dist. engrnr., 1908; prov. engrnr., 1926; 1st ass't. D.P.W., 1932; dep. D.P.W., Aug., 1932; D.P.W., Apr., 1936.

DE LA HARPE, CLEMENT HENRI, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1899; temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1924; med. offr., 1927.

DE LA WARR, 9th Earl (cr. 1761), HERBRAND EDWARD DUNDONALD BRASSEY SACKVILLE, J.P.—B. 1900; ed. Eton; served in the Navy during the War; parly. under-sec., W.O., 1929-30; lord-in-waiting to H.M., 1929 and 1929-31; Mayor of Bexhill, 1932-33, 1933-34 and 1934-35; parly. sec., min. of agr. and dep. min. of fisheries, 1930-31 and 1931-35; parl. U.S. of S. for the cols., Sept., 1936-37.

DEL TUFO, MOROBE VINGENZO, B.A.—B. 1901; senr. schol. Trin. Coll. Camb.; wrangler, 1922; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1923; passed cadet, May, 1925; fedl. examr. in Tamil, July, 1926; offr. cls. DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.V.O. (1909).—B. 1874; ed. at Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offrs.; late lieut., Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex I.Y.; served in S. DENNISON, THOMAS ANDREWS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), Barrister-at-Law (King's Inn).—B. 1906; ed. Newcastle High Sch., Staffs., Coleraine Acad. Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin, B.A., 1929; called to bar, 1931; crown coun., Kenya, Feb., 1935; ag. solr.-gen., 1938 and 1939.
DENNY, WILLIAM JOSEPH, M.C.—M.P., S. Australia, since 1900; atty.-gen. and min. for Northern Territory, 1910-12; capt., A.I.F., European War, 1915-19; atty.-gen., 1924-27 and since Apr., 1930.

DE NOBRIGA, John.—B. 1884; 3rd clk., P.W.D., Trinidad, Apr., 1901; astt. clk., water and sewerage dept., Aug., 1903; storekeeper and clk., engnr.'s office, Apr., 1906; clk., mines dept., Sept., 1908; ch. clk., ditto, Apr., 1914; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1921; ch. clk. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1930; 1st astt. col. sec., Mar., 1935; ag. dep. col. sec., various periods, 1934-39; ag. warden, Caroni, Nov., 1937-Nov., 1938; warden, Victoria-St. Patrick, Mar., 1938.

DENTON, Capt. Norman Croft.—B. 1894; ed. Addye and Stanhope Schl., New Cross, S.E.; milly. serv., 1914-20; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstve. offr., cls. II, 1936; do., cls. I, 1937.

DE PINTO, Cyril Ernest.—B. 1892; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; astt., Galle Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; office astt., Batticaloa Kach., Nov., 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1919; office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, June, 1933; do., Matara, May, 1934; dir., govt. tourist and pub. bureau, May, 1937.

DE ROME, F. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.B.E.—B. 1886; B.Sc. Lond. Univ., 1908; Jens Univ. (Germany), 1908-1909; astt. mast., Queen's Coll. Hong Kong, 1910; astt. postal censor, 1914-19; astt. mast., cls. DE VILLIERS, Sir Jean Etienne Renen, M.A., LL.D., Kt., Bach. (1923).—B. 1875; ed. S. Derbyshire, g.e., 1918 and W. Derbyshire 1922; mayor, Buxton, 1919-21; J.P. Derbyshire; parly. under sec. of state for Dom. Affrs., 5th Mar., 1936.

DE WAAL, COL. PIETR.—B. 1899; ed. Zeerust Pub. Schl., and Transvaal Univ. Coll. (now Univ. of Pretoria); lieut., perm. force, Union Defence Forces, 1922; S.A. Permt. Garrison Arty., 1923; attd. Coast Arty. Schl., Shoeburyness, Aug.-Dec., 1923; attd. R.A., S. Area, England, 1924-25; capt., S.A.P.G.A., 1925; seconded to S.A. Staff Corps as staff offr., gunnery, Cape Peninsula, 1925; ag. A.D.C. to genl., 1925-28; grad. Staff Coll., Camberley, 1930; A.D.C. to C.G.S., U.D.F., Impl. Conf., London, 1930; attd., W.O., 1930-31; O.C. S.A.P.G.A. and C.G.A., Cape Peninsula, and promoted major, 1931; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Capetown, with temp. rank of lieut. col., 1932; maj. (temp. lieut.-col.), S.A. DIAS, NEIL QUINTUS, B.A., (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law, (Middle Temple)—B. 1911; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1933; asst. sec. to min. for commun., and wks., Aug., 1937; offr. cl. II, Dec., 1937.

DICKENS, KENNETH JOHN, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Christ's Hosp.; schol. and exhib.; open classical scholarship, Keble Coll., Oxford, 1925; final honour schl., English language and lit., 1929; inspr. schl., Gold Coast, 1929.

DICKENS, WILLIAM SAMUEL, O.B.E. (1938), M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1891; Indian pol., 1906-16; Indian army R., of Offrs., 1916-21; Iraq pol. force, 1921-30; inspr.-gen., pol., comdt., local forces and supt., fire brig., Barbados, 1932; comsnr., police, Mauritius, 1939.

DICKINSON, ARTHUR HAROLD, O.B.E. (1938).—B. 1892; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; pol. probr., S.S., Nov., 1912; asst. supt., pol. Jly., 1916; adjt., S.S. pol., May, 1920; supt., pol., Jan., 1928; King's Pol. Med., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., crim. intell., S.S. and in addn. ag. dir., pol. intell. bureau, Dec., 1928; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., Jan., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Penang, Nov., 1933; senr. supt. and senr. dep. comsnr., Malayan pol. serv., 1936; supt., pol., S'pore, 1937; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., July, 1939.

DICKINSON, JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—Ed. Liverpool Coll.; solr., 1920; 2nd lieut., The King's Own (R. Lancaster Regt.), 1914; lieut., July, 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; France, 1915-18 (M.C. and Bar); asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921; ch. inspr., lab., 1938.

DICKSON, WILLIAM MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. St. Mary's Schl. and Coll. Robertson, and later, Hon. W. A. Gordon, mins. of lab., 1930-33; dep. min. of lab., 1934.

DIEMONT, J. F. W.—B. 1884; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; asst., secretarial branch, posts and tels., O.R.C., 1903; clk., prov. sec., office, O.F.S., 1910; admistr. inspr., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1922-29; prin. elk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1929; ch. do., 1935.

DIMES, CAPT. DOUGLAS HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Kingsbridge Gram. Schl. and Schl. of Agr., S. Devon; R. Vety. Coll., London; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1913; war serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

DINGLE, PERCIVAL ALFRED, C.B.E. (1937), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.S.M., certif. T.M.H., L.S.T.M.—B. 1881; dist. surg., N. DONOVAN, WILLIAM, M.Sc. (N.Z.), F.I.C.—B. 1879; ed. Thames (N.Z.) High Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Otago Univ. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; joined N.Z. govt. serv., 1906; astt. dom. analyst, 1919; dom. analyst, 1930.

DOORLY, A. N., B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister at Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1887; astt. dist. comntr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; res. mag., Kenya, 1919; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1923; ag. judge, high ct., in 1923, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1925; ag. ch. just. in 1926, 1928 and 1929; atty.-gen., 1929; astt., legal advy. staff, C.O., Nov.-Dec., 1932; Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 2nd cls., 1936; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1935.

DOORLY, CANON CHARLES STOKELEY, M.A. Selwyn Coll., Camb.—B. 1882; 4th astt. mast., Queen’s Royal Coll., Trinidad, July, 1911; 3rd astt. mast., July, 1914; 2nd astt. mast., July, 1920; 2nd mast., 1927; ag. prin., 1931 and 1936; prin., 1938.

DOORLY, SIR CHARLES W., KT. BACH. (1935), C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1875; 3rd cls. elk.; imigrn. dept., Jamaica, 1892; 2nd cls. elk., 1894; Hindustani interpreter, 1897; inspr. of imigrn., 1903; protector of imigrn., 1909; gov’t. emigrn. agt., Madras, 1913; astt. col. sec., Jamaica, 1922; admstr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1928; ag. gov’, Windward Is., in 1930, 1931 and 1933; ret., 1935.

DORAN, CHARLES FREDRICK GARFIELD, M.A., B.L.—Ed. Royal Belfast Academical Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1916-19; served in France, Nov., 1916 till wounded in 1917; called to Irish Bar (King’s Inn) 1913; crown counsel, Kenya, Dec., 1925; ag. solr-gen. and M.L.C., Mar.-June, 1930 and Dec., 1931-July, 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Sept., 1933-Jan., 1934, res. mag., 1934; attached legal adv. staff, C.O., Apr.-May, 1938.

DORION, HON. CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Quebec Seminary; Laval Univ. (L.L.L., 1884); admitted to bar of Prov. of Queb., 1884; practised law in Queb. City until 1911; LL.D., Laval Univ., 1894; prof. of laws, Laval Univ., 1898; and since, prof. of civ. code; judge, superior ct., Prov. of Queb., 1911; judge, ct. of king’s bench, 1921.

DORMER, BASIL ANTHONY, M.D., M.B., B.S., (Durham), D.P.H., B.Hy. (Durham).—B. 1903; dem. in pathology, Univ. Durham and clin. pathologist, R. Victory Infirmary, Newcastle-on-Tyne, 1926-27; res. med. offr., King George V sanatorium, 1927-28; house phys. and sen. house surg., Royal Vic. Infir., 1928-29; res. med. offr., 1930; res. and astt. tuberculosis med. offr., city hosp., Newcastle, 1930-33; astt. med. offr. of health, Croydon, 1933-35; med. supt., Nelsoport sanatorium, Union of S.A., 1935; do., King George V hosp. for tuberculosis, Durban, 1937.

D’ORNELLAS, GUSTAVE.—B. 1885; 6th cls. clk., reg. office, Br. Guiana, 1905; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1906; 5th ditto, 1910; 4th ditto, 1915; 3rd ditto, 1923; 2nd ditto, 1925; asst. survr., cust., 1927; survr., cust., 1939.

DOS SANTOS, EBREO LIONEL.—B. 1890; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., Apr., 1912; asst. treas., Jan., 1929; treas. and chmn., agrl. bank, comsnr., income tax and dep. comsnr., estate duties, 1933; offl. M.E.C. and M.L.C.; chmn., C.S. staff bd.; chmn., bd. for admin. cocoa relief and hurricane relief orders; chmn., cocoa subsidy bd.; comsnr. currency; chmn., Trinidad electricity bd.

DOUGHTY, LESLIE ROY, B.SC. (Agric.), N.D.A.—B. 1902; ed. Derby Munic. Secondary Sch., Midland Agril. Coll. and Schl. of Agr., Cambridge; geneticist, East African agril. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
DOUGLAS, CHARLES HOMER, M.M.—B. 1884; ed. Wellingborough Grammar Schl.; war serv., R.E., 1915-19; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; land survr., 1933.

DOUGLAS, CUTHBERT SCHOLTO.—B. 1887; temp. survr., irrig. dept., Union of S.A., May, 1917; temp. asst. engrn., Dec., 1919; 2nd gr. prof. asst., Dec., 1927; prof. asst., Nov., 1929; survr.-gen., Bloemfontein, May, 1934; do., Natal, Dec., 1937.
DOUGLAS, HUGH ALASTAIR, B.A. (Forestry).—B. 1915; ed. Trinity Coll., Glenalmond and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1938.

DOUGLAS-JONES, SIR CRAWFORD DOUGLAS, K.T. BACH, (1934), C.M.G. (1920).—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Aug., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volks., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instr.'s certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. comsnr., Rhodesia, Apr., 1911; ag. res. comsnr., S. and N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1918; res. comsnr., July, 1918 to Sept., 1923; res. comsnr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1923; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; admstd. govt., Sept., 1924 to April, 1925 and from Apr. to July, 1926; col. sec., Jr. Guiana, 1926; O.A.G. on various occasions, 1927-28; retd., 1935.

DOVEY, JOHN EDWARD, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H. (Edin.).—B. 1895; med. offr., Hong Kong; do., Kowloon, 1927-28; med. offr. in ch., subord. staff and families, med. offr. in ch. venereal disease clinic and asst., Victoria Hosp., 1929; M.O. in ch., Victoria Hosp., May-Sept., 1932 and 1934; M.O. in ch., civil and ment. hosp., 1933; do., Victoria hosp., Feb., 1934; visiting M.O., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries and M.O. in-ch., officers' families in admn., June, 1937; M.O., Queen Mary's hosp., June, 1937; S. & T.O. (med. def.), Aug., 1939.

DOBIGHGIN, SIR HERBERT LAYARD Kt. Bach., C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1880; inspr. of pol., Ceylon, Jan., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Apr., 1901; suot. of pol., Feb., 1905; inspr.-gen. of pol., Nov., 1913; ret., 1937; reported on Cyprus pol. force, 1926; Palestine pol., 1930, and N. Rhodesia pol., 1937; Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; Order of Crown of Belgium; King's police medal; Col. police medal.

DOWNES, CAIT. ROGER MEADEN, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. pyte. schl. and Oxford Univ.; admstive. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1921; cls. III., 1929; ag. res., Sept., 1932 and Aug.-Oct., 1936; admstive. offr., cls. II., 1938; ag. res., Oct.-Dec., 1938.

DOWNIE, H. F., C.M.G. (1930), O.B.E. (1934), B.A. (1912), M.A. (1914).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hospital and Oxford; Lodge exhibr. (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class mods., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Dec., 1912; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug. to 19th Oct., 1914, and from 10th June, 1915, to 7th Apr., 1918; ag. lst cls. clk., 3rd Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited Sudan, Uganda, Kenya, Tanganyika, Nyasaland, N. and S. Rhodesia and S. Africa as sec., East Africa comsnr., on closer union, Dec., 1927-May, 1928; asst. sec., 21st Oct., 1935.

DOWSE, WILLIAM ROBERT, A.I.K.E.—B. 1891; Br. P.O. engrn. dept., G. Britain, Jan., 1912; Dub. Univ. ex-graduates, O.T.C., Aug., 1914; comand., R.E., Jan., 1915; Gallipoli and France, May, 1915; asst. teleg. engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1919; demob. reg. army R. of O., June, 1919; engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S. Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., ditto, May, 1928; senr. engrn., P. and T. Selangor and Pahang, Mar., 1932; asst. engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels., Malaya, Dec., 1932; dir., tels. and telephone and engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1939.

DOYLE, BRIAN ANDREW, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1911; called to bar, King's Inn, 1932; mag., St. Geo. West, 1937; mag., Caroni, Oct., 1937; ag. asst. law offr., Jan.-May, 1938; mag., St. Geo. West, May, 1938; mag., Eastern counties, Oct., 1938.

DOYLE, THOMAS FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed. St. Patrick's Acad., Dungannon; offr. in imp. cust. and excise serv., Aug., 1912; inspr., cust., Palestine, July, 1926; ag. dep. dir., cust., excise and trade, periods in 1929 and 1930; dep. collr., cust. and excise comsnr., Mauritius, Feb., 1932; ag. collr., cust. and harbr.-mast., June, 1932; collr., cust. and harbr.-mast., Aug., 1936.

DRAKE, HAROLD WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. City of London Schl.; entd. Col. Audit Dept., July, 1904; seconded to W.A.F.F., Oct., 1914 (Nigeria and Cameroons); paymr., Overseas Contingent, W.A.F.F. (G.E.A.); Col. Audit Dept., Aug., 1918; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Mar., 1919; senr. asst. audr., Dec., 1921; ag. audr., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; audr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1927; asst. dir., col. audit dept., Dec., 1931; audr., Nigeria, 1933.

DRAKE, THOMAS NORMAN.—B. 1896; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast; pilot, R.A.F., Great War, 1917-19; cadet, R.I.C. and dist. inspr., Ulster spl. constab., 1922; Air Force Offrs. Reserve, 1923; 3rd cls. inspr., Jamaica constab., 1924.

DRAYTON, BERNARD MORGAN.—B. 1886; service in post office, pub. wks. and treasy. depts., Grenada, 1902-14; treasy. asst., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; asst. treas., civ. adminstr., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), 1917; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1928.

DRAYTON, HON. SIR HENRY L., K.T. BACH. (1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comtce., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario govt. comsnr. on Toronto Power Comn., 1911; ch. comsnr., bd. of railway comsners., Canada, 1912; resig. to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Govt., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-el. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig. portfolio on defeat of admintr., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comsners., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, ROBERT HARRY, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; miny. of reconstr. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; trea. solr.'s office, 1919-20; astt. legal sec., govt. of Palestine (continuing as astt. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr.-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1925; mem., finan. couns., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. govt. in parly. comm. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftsman, Jan., 1932; called to bar, 1934; commnr. for revision of laws of Palestine, Sept., 1934; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1934; ag. gov., Apr.-July, 1938; legal sec., Ceylon, 1939.

DRENAN, JOHN MATTHEW, D.C.M. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; apptd., after compet. exam., olk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 3rd cls., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, Salonica, Palestine); astt. head, appts. dept., 1919; stores dept., 1922; shipping dept., 1930; dep. head, gen. dept., Jan., 1933; shipping dept., May, 1933.
DREW, DESMOND, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. B.A.O. (T.C.D.).—B. 1886; med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921; prin. med. offr., Swaziland, 1938.

DRIVER, WILLIAM GORDON STANFORD.—B. 1907; ed. St. Andrew's Preparatory Schl. and Coll., Grahamstown, Potchefstroom High Schl. and Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown., grade II olk., Basutoland, 1930; astt. dist. commnr., 1937; seconded to secr., master and regt., High ct., 1938-39.

DRYBURGH, ALEXANDER MITCHELL, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot Schol., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 1919; govt. rice agt., Tapah, Mar., 1920; 1st astt. dist. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1920; attd. Fed. sect., Nov., 1920; supermyn. offr., cls. V., 1922; cls. IV., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1926; dist. offr., Raub, 1928; sec. res., Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III., 1931; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, June, 1933; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1934; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1934-1937; gov't. dep., Sept., 1938; ch. astt. sec., Gibraltar, 1939; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Jly., 1939; gov. rep. on City Coun. as from Aug., 1939.

DU CANE, GEN. SIR JOHN, G.C.B. (1928), K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1910).—B. 1863; heut., R.A., 1884; capt., 1893; major, 1900; lieut.-col., 1902; col., 1908; major-gen., 1915; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen., 1926; D.A.A.G., Staff Coll., 1905-07; G.S.O., 1st grade, hdqrs. of Army, 1908-10; C.R.A., 3rd divn., 1911-12; S.O. to I.G., Home Forces, 1913-14; served S. Africa, 1899-02 (despa., brevet lieut.-col., Queen's med., 5 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European war, brig.-gen., III. Corps, 1914; maj.-gen., R.A., G.H.Q., 1915; sp. appt., min. of munitions, 1916; commanded XV. Corps.; Br. rep. with Marshal Foch, 1918 (7 times ment. in desps.); master-gen., ordnance, 1920-23; G.O.C.-in-ch., Western command, 1923-24; G.O.C.-in-ch., Br. Army of Rhine, 1924-27; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1927; resigned, 1931.

DUCKER, RONALD SIDNEY.—B. 1908; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1929); M.A. (1938); spec. course in educn. at London Univ. for C.O. probationers, 1929; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1930; 2nd inspr., schls., Br. Guiana, 1932; supt., educn., Nigeria, 1936.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD HARLAND.—B.1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll., City and Guilds Engng. Coll., Finabury, and R. Coll. of Science; B.Sc. Lond., A. R. C. Sc.; war serv. with London Elec. Engnr., R.E. (T.), 1915-18; France, 1916-18; inspr., educn., S. Prov., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; author of "The Laboratory Workshop" and various articles in the "School Science Review."

DUCKWORTH, FREDERICK VICTOR.—B.1901; ed. Selwyn Coll., Cam., cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; offr., ol. V., Dec., 1927; offr., ol. IV., Dec., 1931; offr., cl. III., Dec., 1936; 2nd mag., Spore, Aug., 1937; ag. dep. contrir. of lab., F.M.S., June, 1938.

BUFF, DAVID, C.M.G. (1933), M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.).—B. 1863; ed. Portora, Enniskillen and Trin. Coll., Dubl.; sch. in classics; B.A. (nat. sci.) with 1st cls. hons.and gold med.; med. offr., W.A.M.S. and posted to Gold Coast, June, 1910; senr. med. offr., 1925; astt. dir., med. serv., Jan., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; mem. bd. of exams. in native languages, Apr., 1928; dep. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1931; dir., med. and sanry. serv., Feb., 1932.
BUFF, DENNISTOUN CAMPBELL, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1915; astt. conserv. forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1938.

BUFF, SIR H. L., K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1872; apptd. by F.O., astt. res. B.C. Africa, 1897; resig. from serv., 1903; re-apptd. Apr., 1904; res., 1906; seconded, 1909-10; admitted mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; 1st cls. hons. constitutional law, 1909; ag. dep.-gov., July, 1910; and Apr. to July, 1911; 1st grade res., July, 1911; sec. to the admistr., 1912; ch. sec. to govt. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1914; served with Nyasaland Field Force, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1916, mentd. in despatches; served against rebels in Shire Highlands, 1915 (medal and clasp); ch. polit. offr. to Brig.-Gen. Northey, C.B., in operations against G.E. Africa, July, 1916; ag. gov., Nyasaland Prot., 1st Oct., 1918; ret., 1920; author of "Nyasaland under the F.O.," 2nd ed.; and a history of Nyasaland in the native dialect, published by the Prot. govt.

BUFF, Rt. Hon. Sir LYMAN POORH, G.C.M.G. (1934), P.C. (1919), B.A., LL.B. Toronto Univ.—B. 1865; apptd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906; cent. appeal judge, Mil. Serv. Act, 1917; dep. of gov. gen., 1926; ch. just., Canada, 1933.

BUFF, STEUART MURRAY, B.A. (Bombay)—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1928; astt., Kandy kach., Jan., 1929; astt. Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1930; office astt., Nuwara Eliya kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Gampola, Oct., 1931; office astt., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1934; commnr. requests and pol. mag., Puttalam, and office astt., Puttalam kach., Mar., 1935; sec. to finan. sec. and astt. to gen. treas., Mar., 1937.

BUFFERNIN AND AVA, 4th MARQUESS OF, BAHIL SHERIDAN HAMILTON-TEMPLE-BLACKWOOD.—B. 1909; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; mem., Indian ranchers commn., 1932; partly. pvt. sec. to U.S. of S. for India (Marquess of Lothian), 1932; to Pres. Bd. of Educn. (Lord Irwin now Viscount Halifax), 1932-36; to S. of S. for War (Viscount Halifax), June-Nov., 1935; to Lord Privy Seal (Viscount Halifax), 1935-36; Lord-in-waiting, 1936-37; partly. U.S. of S. for the Colts, 1937.

DUGAN, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WINSTON, K.C.M.G., C.B., D.S.O.—B. 1877; 2nd lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1900; served, S. Africa, 1899-1902 (Queen's med., 3 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); adjt., 1901-4; garrison adjt., Irish commd., 1910-14; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., E. Anglian divn., 1914; asst. prov. marshal, 1914; commd. 2nd R. Irish Regt., France, 1916-16; asst. inspr.-gen., training, B.B.F., 1918; brig.-commdr. (temp. brig.-gen.), 1916-18; commdr., 10th Infy. Brig., 1919-23; A.A.G., Southern Commd., 1926-30; A.D.C. to King, 1928-30; commdr., 66th (1st Lond.) divn., T.A., 1931; gov., S. Australia, 1934; gov., Victoria, 1939.

DUGGAN, JOHN WALTER.—B. 1900; ed. Hightown Tech. Boys' Sch., Crewe, Tech. Engng. Coll., Crewe and Manchester Schl. Technology; M.I. Loco. E., 1934; office supt., Nigerian rly. open lines, 1927; asst. wks. man., 1929; supt., workshops, 1937.

DUIGAN, MAJOR-GEN. JOHN EVELYN, C.B. (1937), D.S.O. (1917).—Lieut., N.Z. Staff Corps, Jan., 1903; instr., signalling & engnr. services, Wellington, July, 1908; capt., June, 1909; Staff Coll., Quetta, 1913-14; G.S.O., 3rd grade, hdqrs., N.Z.E.F., Nov., 1914; major, Jan., 1915; G.S.O., Auckland, mily. dist., June, 1915; O.C., N.Z. Tunelling Coy., N.Z.E.F., Dec., 1915; G.S.O., 3rd grade, div. hdqrs., N.Z.E.F., Jan., 1917; G.S.O., 2nd grade, 6th Army Corps, B.E.F., Feb., 1917; G.S.O., 2nd Grade, N.Z. Inf. Res. Group, July, 1917; instr., Senr. Officers' Schl., Aldershot, Dec., 1917; ap. appt., U.S.A. under W.O., Mar., 1918; oh. inf. instr., Featherston mily. camp, Oct., 1918; G.S.O. (temp.) N. mily. dist., Jan., 1919; lieut.-col., Aug., 1919; C.S.O. (G.S.O. 1st grade), N. Comm., Aug., 1919; O.C., N. Comm., Oct., 1930; col., Apr., 1931; major-gen. and apptd. commdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Apr., 1937; ment in desps.

DUKE, CATHERINE HILL, M.A., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Glas. and Edin.).—Ed. Queen Margaret Coll. and Glas. Univ.; lady med. offr., out-door dispensary, women and children, S'pore, and offr., i/c Girls' Schls. in addn., June, 1923; lady health offr., i/c Girls' Schls., S'pore, Jan., 1929; joint S.S. and F.M.S. rep. at 6th Imp. Soc. Hyg. Cong. and English-speaking Conf. on Maternity and Ch. Welfare, July, 1933.

DUKE, EDGAR MORTIMER, LL.B.—B. 1895; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; Br. Guiana open schl. in maths., 1911; Andrews' entrance schl. in science, 1913 and Joseph Hume schl. in jurisprudence, 1914, Univ. Coll., London; Campbell Foster Prizeman of Middle Temple; ap. prizeman in crim. law and procedure, 1915; LL.B. (Lond.), 1916; Barstow law schl., 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; admitted to practise Br. Guiana, 1917 and in Trinidad and Tobago, 1921; ag. regir., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Br. Guiana, in 1929, 1930 and 1932; dep. regir., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Jan., 1930; stip. mag. and mem., dist. licensing bds., Demerara and Essequibo, Sept., 1931; regir., deeds, 1933; ag. crown coun., June-July, 1938, and asst. atty.-gen., July, 1938; author, "Law of Immovable Property in British Guiana."

DUKE, ORIEL ST. A., M.B.E. (1932), M.M.—B. 1896; ent. govt. serv., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1913; serv. European War, 1915-19; inspr., Leeward Is. pol., Sept., 1927; ch. inspr., pol., July, 1933; comsnr., pol., Barbados, 1939.

DUMANOIRK, JOSEPH WENCKSLEN, I.S.O. (1937).—Asst., trigonometrical survey, Trinidad, 1902; 2nd asst., survey office, 1903; 1st asst., ditto, 1913; dep. survr.-gen., 1930; ditto and sub. intendant, 1933.

DUMBRELL, HENRY JAMES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1936), B.A.—B. 1885; asst. mast., Heaton Lodge Schl., nr. Huddersfield; mast., St. Bedes Coll., Estcourt, Natal; headmr., Ompumulo Native Training Coll.; inspr., schls., Natal, 1921; inspr., educn., Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland, 1928; dir., educn., Bech. Prot., 1935.

DUNCAN, GEORGE ANDREW, F.I.A.N.Z., A.R.A.N.Z.—B. 1889; ed. State schl. and Wellington Coll.; engaged acgtg. side, N.Z. dairy industry; war. serv., 1915-18; sec., Hawera co-op. dairy co., 1921; mem., royal comn. on dairy industry, 1934; mem., exec. comn. of agr., 1935; ag. dir., marketing, 1936; dir., dairy produce expt. marketing, 1937; mem., govt. advisory comte., 1936-38.

DUNCAN, HAROLD HANDASYDE.—B. 1885; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; hons. schl. mod. hist.; M.A.; qualified in compet. exam. for appt. as junr. clk., 1st cls., F.O. (placed 4th in 1908 and 6th in 1909); conf. sec. to London comte., Imp. Ottoman Bank for work relating to Turkish govt. finance and indu. enterprise in Near East from Dec., 1909; on sp. mission to Constantinople, 1912; served in army, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; France and Italy; liaison offr. (intelligence A.) between Br. G.H.Q., Italy and Br. Mily. Mission, Comando Supremo from Aug., 1918; capt., Norfolk Yeomanry; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1919; S. Eastern circuit; temp. legal asst., Miny. of Health, 25th Apr., 1927; est., 15th Mar., 1928; 2nd asst. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., Sist Mar., 1930; asst. legal adviser, do., 1st Apr., 1931; ag. legal adviser, Dec., 1931 to Mar., 1932 and Feb., 1933 to July, 1933.

DUNCAN, Rt. Hon. Sir PATRICK, P.C. (1937); G.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1904), K.C., LL.D. (Edinburgh).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, school Ball Coll.; 1stcls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prim. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legia. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906; barrister, Middle Temple and advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; M.L.A., for Yeoville; min. of interior, pub. health and educn., 1921-24; min. of mines, Apr., 1933; gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1937.

DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th clk., P.O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1904; clk., record office, 1909; comsnr., 4th div., 1912; comsnr., grade III., June, 1920; clk., grade III., regir.'s office, July, 1927; ag. asst. regir.-gen., 1928 and from June, 1929.

DUNDAS, Hon. Sir CHARLES CECIL FARQUHARSON, K.C.M.G. (1938), C.M.G. (1934), O.B.E. (1923).—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Sept., 1908; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1914; dist. polit. offr. (with hon. rank of major), German E. Africa, provisional admtn., Jan., 1916; senr. comsnr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. asst. ch. sec., June, 1924; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1926; col. sec. Bahamas, Apr., 1929; admtr., May to Nov., 1929, June to Aug., 1930 and from June, 1931; dep. gov., in 1931 and 1932; O.A.G., Aug. to Sept., 1932; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, July, 1934; ag. gov., July-Oct., 1934; gov. and commdr.-inch., Bahamas, 1937.
DUNN, C. de S.—Served in South Africa, 1901-1902 (medal and five clasps); S.A.C., 1903-1906; camel constab., Somaliland, 1912.

DUNN, J. S., B.Sc.—B. 1902; ed. Secondary Schl., Blyth, Northumberland and Univ. of Reading; B.Sc. (Hons.), London and Reading; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; engnr. chem. P.W.D., G. Coast, 1936.

DUNNING, Hon. CHARLES AVERY.—B. 1885; came to Canada, 1902; ed. pub. schl.; apptd. R., comsnr. by Saskatchewan govt. to investigate question of agrl. credit and grain marketing, Europe, 1913; mem., Canadian coun. of agr., 1911-16; chmn., Saskatchewan comte., victory loan, 1917-18-19; mem., Canada food bd. as dir., food production, Canada, 1918; prov. treas., Sask., 1916; min. of rlys., 1917; do., telephones, 1918, and min., agr., 1919; premier, Sask., 1922; pres., coun., prov., treas. and min. of rlys., 1922; min., rlys. and canals in Dom. govt., 1926; el. for Regina by acclm. after assuming office; resigned, 1926; re-el. to H. of C., g.e., 1926; min., rlys. and canals, 1926; re-el. by acclm., 1926; Canadian del. to L. of N., 1928; min. of finance, 1929; resigned with lib. govt., 1930; min. of finance, 1935-39.
DUNSTAN, HON. ALBERT ARTHUR, M.L.A.—B. 1882; ent. Victorian parlt., 1920; min., lands and forests, 1932-35; premier and treas., Victoria, 1935.

DUNSTAN, SIR WYNNDHAM ROWLAND, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford schl.; lec. and demonstr. of chem. Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lect. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres. section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of advisory comte., on trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. comm. intell. comte. Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Imp. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Imp. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland (reports presented to parlt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil.
