Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Imp. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Imp. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlt.); "Reports on work of Imp. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parlt.); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Imp. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber," in last edition "Ency. Brit."

DUPLESSIS, STEPHANUS HENDRIK.—B. 1885; mast., high ct., Windhoek (S.W.A.), Feb., 1920; asst. mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), July, 1926; ditto, Capetown (C.P.), Dec., 1926; mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), Oct., 1931; do., Pretoria (Tvl.), Oct., 1938.
DUPRE, WALTER HANS EWALD, M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1904; ed. Cambridge and Middle Temple; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1937; agr. puisne judge, Mar., 1938.

DURANLEAU, HON. ALFRED, P.C. (Can.), L.L.L., K.C., M.P.—B. 1871; ed. at Ste. Marie de Monnier; Laval Univ.; mem., Laurier divn., Montreal, 1923-27; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; minister of marine in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930; judge, sup. ct., prov. of Quebec, 1935.

DURUTY, CHARLES ERIC.—B. 1892; war serv. with 28th London Regt., (Artists Rifles) and The Royal Warwickshire Regt.; awarded M.C.; served audit dept. and pol., Trinidad, 1911-21; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1927.

DUTHIE, DOUGLAS WATT, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C.—B. 1904; Aberdeen Univ., 1927; asst. biochem. dept., Rowett research inst., Aberdeen, 1927-28; inst. of animal pathology, Cambridge, 1930-31; lect., chem., I.C.T.A., Trinidad, 1931-37; chem., dept. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1937.

DU TOIT, PETRUS JOHANN, Ph.D. (Zurich), Dr. Med. Vet. (Berlin), B.A. (Cape), D.Sc. Agr. (Stell.), F.R.S. (S. Africa).—Senr. vety. offr., vety. research divn., Vryburg, Dec., 1919; sub-dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1920; dep. dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1921; dir., vety. services, Pretoria, Apr., 1927; dean of faculty and prof., trop. diseases and protozoology, Univ. of S. Africa, 1927; visited Nigeria at request of C.O. to rep. on stock problems, 1927; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Conf., London, 1927; chmn., 6th Pan-African Vety. Conf., Pretoria, 1929; dir., vety. services and animal industry, 1929; del., internat. vety. cong., London and New York, 1934.

DUTTON, ERIC ALDHelm TORLOUGH, C.B.E. (1938), O.B.E. (1930), M.A.—Ed., Hurstpierpoint, and Keble Coll., Oxford; major, W. Yorks Regt.; served in Gallipoli (ment. in desp.); temp. appt., Basutoland adminstr., 1918-19; priv. sec. to gov. of Uganda, 1920-22; priv. sec. to gov., Kenya, 1922-30; sec., high commr., Zanzibar, 1922-25; priv. sec. to high commr., transport, Kenya and Uganda, 1925; prin. asst. ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. ch. sec., various occasions, 1931-37; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1932-37; chmn., finance comm., 1932; col. sec., Bermuda, 1938; ag. gov., 4th Aug.-7th Oct., 1939.

DUTTON, ERNEST GODFREY.—B. 1886; clk., Impl. mil. rlys., Apr., 1902; clk., high commr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1903 to May, 1916; Swaziland service, Apr., 1907; clk. to asst. commr., Maseru, Basutoland, June, 1916; clk. ch. kl., master of ct. and regis., July, 1919; ag. govt. sec., June to Dec., 1923 and Feb. to Oct., 1924; asst. commr., 1924; title changed to dist. commr., 1936.
DUTTON, STEPHEN BERNARD.—B. 1881; served in stores dept., C.S.A.R., 1902-3; served in Zululand rebellion, 1906; comptroller of stores, Basutoland, Oct., 1909.

DYE, W. H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. S.H. and T.M. (Distinc.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., 1914-25; surg. specialist, 1918-20; N. Persian forces memorial med., for research, 1924 and 1927; seconded, Nyasaland Aug., 1921-Apr., 1925; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

DYER-MELVILLE, SYDNEY WHITMORE, M.I.E.E., S.A.—B. 1885; ed. City of London schl.; asst. dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. dir., Apr. to Nov., 1913, Oct., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Dec., 1919 to Feb., 1925; immigr. offr., Sept. to Dec., 1917; ag. director, Jan. to Dec., 1919; ag. dir., rly. elec. and wireless tel. depts. for various periods, 1925-32; ag. D.P.W. and electricity, Apr.-Nov., 1935 and July-Dec., 1937; temp. mem., leg. coun., June-Nov., 1935 and July-Dec., 1937.

DYKE, HAMILTON WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1935), M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1905.—B. 1881; ed. South African Coll., Cape Town and Glasgow Univ.; house phys., Glasgow Royal Infirmary, house surg., Glasgow Western Infirmary, Glasgow Ophthalmic Inst. and Glasgow Royal Hosp. for sick children; med. offr., Basutoland, 1913; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; served in France, 1915-19; S.M.O., S.A. Native Lab. Cont., Rouen area, 1919; surg. spec., govt. hosp., Jerusalem and govt. of Palestine, 1927; prin. med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1929; do., Basutoland, 1935; author of "An Outbreak of Scurvy among South African Natives in Rouen," Lancet, 1917; "A case of Madura Foot in Basutoland," S. African Med. Jnl.
DYKE, KENNETH H., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1892; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

DYSON, EDWARD TREVOR.—B. 1886; ed. Ruthin Gram. Schl., U.C.W., Aberystwyth (B.A.), and Jesus Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1912; pol. mag., Kurunnegala, Aug., 1913; addtnl. asst. col. sec., Mar., 1918; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1918; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1920; asst. govt. agt., N. Eliya, Feb., 1921; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1926; ag. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Mar., 1928; do., Jaffna, Nov., 1930; do., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1934; do., N.P., Jan., 1935; do., C.P., May, 1937; offr., cls. I, gd. I, Jan., 1937; govt. agt., N.P., Nov., 1937.

EAGLESOME, SIR JOHN EGAN, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—M.Inst.C.E.; served in P.W.D., rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of Indian govt., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., 1900; as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Baro-Kano rly. from 1907; dir. of rlys. and works, Nigeria, 1912; ret., 1919.

EALES, SHIRLEY, C.M.G. (1935); C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1883; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; 2nd cls. clk., gov't. 's office, Transvaal and O.R.C., July, 1902; 1st cls. clk., July, 1904; transf'd to Basutoland service and att. to office of high commnr. for S. Africa, July, 1908; prin. clk., Dec., 1911, and asst. Impl. acctnt., Apr., 1915; chief clk., Apr., 1919; mem. of comm. to enquire into establishments, etc., in Bechuanaland Prot., Basutoland and Swaziland, 1921-22; ag. asst. Imp. sec., May-Nov., 1921 and Mar.-Sept., 1922; asst. Imp. sec., 1923; ag. Imp. sec., in 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1931-2; title changed to adm. sec. to high commnr., 1933; mem. for Bech. Prot. on rly. comm., 1935.

EARLE, HUGH J. M.—B. 1896; ed. Cheltenham; on war service, 1914-19; capt., 4th Batt. Essex Regt., 1918; ent. dept. of lands and mines, B. Guiana, 2nd Aug., 1921; 6th cls. clk., secretariat, 1st Mar., 1922; sec. for sp. duty in connexion with Georgetown disturbances, 1924; ag. A.D.C. to O.A.G., May-Nov., 1925; cadet, adm. serv., Nigeria, 1927.

EARLE, SIR LIONEL, K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1911); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., Univ. of Gottingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., Oxford; asst. sec. to royal commn. on Paris exhibtn., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, 1900; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; priv. sec. to lord pres. of the comm. (Earl of Crewe), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), Nov., 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works, 1912; ret.

EASTER, B. H. M., C.B.E. (1936), M.B.E., B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Coll., Finchley; served R.N. Dn., 1917; Scot's Guards (lieut.), 1918; Ampleforth Coll., O.T.C., 1919-23; headmr., St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1923; dir., educn. and headmr., Grenada, 1928; ag. col. sec. and adminr., Grenada, Dec., 1930-Aug., 1931; local adviser, educn. comte., 1931; dir., educn., Jamaica, Apr., 1932.

EASTWOOD, CHRISTOPHER GILBERT.—B. 1905; scholar of Eton, 1917 and of Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1923; apptd. to C.O. as asst. prin. after compet. exam., 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded for serv. as pvt. sec. to high commnr., Palestine, 11th Feb., 1932; prin., 18th Mar., 1935; sec., advy. comte., on nutrition in the Col. Empire, 1939.

EBDEN, WILLIAM SYDENHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; class. tripods, 1905; cadet, S.S., 1911; 2nd lieut., Jan., 1915; lieut., Salonika Front; wounded and p. of w., Bulgaria; dist. offr., F.M.S., 1920; dep. contr., rubber expts., mag., Johore, and recr., Alangoff concession, in addn., July, 1924; registr., sup. ct., Johore and 1st mag., Johore Bharu, Dec., 1926; asst. adviser, Segamat, Jan., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; asst. treas., Penang, Dec., 1928; dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. I.B., Apr., 1931; commr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1932; cls. I.A., 1936.

ECCLES, LAUNCHLOT WILLIAM GREGORY, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Michaelhouse Sch., Natal and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. survr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; asst. examr., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from Dec., 1922; dep. dir., surveys and examr., diagrams and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir., surveys in 1925, 1928 and 1931; commr., lands and mines, 1933; commr., lands and mines, 1935; commr., lands, mines and surveys, 1936; M.L.C., 1933-37.

ECCLES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; clk., educn. office, Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1907; clk. to commrs. of currency, June, 1908; customs, Jan., 1911; temp. sub-instr.ofpol., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1915; on mil'y. serv., lieut., afterwards capt., 1915-19; returned to cust., Trinidad, July, 1919; on sp. mil. duty to B. Guiana and Trinidad, Aug. to Oct.; asst. recvr.-gen. Gambi, Oct., 1919; relinquished comm., with rank of capt., Sept., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Mr. G. Gwyn, Sept., 1921 and to Mr. C. R. M. Workman, Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. clk., exec. coun., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; offr.-in-charge, posts and tels. dept., Feb. to July, 1924; offr.-in-charge, cust. on various occasions between 1923 and 1928; asst. comptr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. comptr., cust., on various occasions, 1929-32; comptr., cust., 1932.

ECKBO, NILS BONNIVIE (M.F. Yale).—Offr. in ch., timber seasoning investigations, Pretoria, 1919; apptd. to perm. estab. as timber investigations offr., 1924; senr. forest offr., Forest Products Inst., 1934; ch. forest offr., do., 1934; ch. prof. offr. (forestry), 1937.

ECKEL, CAPT. PERCY.—B. 1893; audit dept., Trinidad, Jan., 1912; seconded, W. O., 1915-20; wounded, France, 1917; sub-instr., Trinidad constab., 1920; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1927.

EDEN, Rt. Hon. ANTHONY, P.C. (1934), M.C., J.P., D.C.L. (Oxford), 1936, Durham, 1937 and Cambridge, 1938.—B. 1897; ed. Eton and Christ Ch., Oxford, B.A. (1st cls. hons. oriental languages); war serv. with King's Royal Rifle Corps (capt.) and as G.S.O. 3 and as brigade major; contested Spennymoor divn. of Durham, 1922; M.P. Warwick and Leamington, 1923; attended Imp. press confere., Melbourne, 1925; parly. pvt. seccy. to S. of. S. for Foreign affrs. (Sir Austen Chamberlain, K.G.), 1926-29; partly under-secc., F.O., 1931-33; Lord Privy Seal, 1934-35; min. without portfolio for L. of N. affrs., 1935; S. of S. for Foreign affrs., 1935-38; S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 1939.

EDMONDS, B. D.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 7th Aug., 1914 to 5th Dec., 1919; awarded Mily. Med.; temp. clk., C.O., 2nd Feb., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 5th Nov., 1921; clerical offr., higher grade, 3rd Feb., 1928; asst. acctnt., 14th June, 1937.

EDMONDS, EDWARD REGINALD.—B. 1901; B.A. (English hon.), King's Coll., London Univ., 1937; entered C.O., 1917; apptd. after exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; clk., P.W.D., Uganda, 1926; higher grade cler. offr., C.O., 31st July, 1929; asst. prin., 1st July, 1938.

EDMUNDS, HERBERT GLYN.—B.1900; ed. Swansea and Univ. Coll., London; med. offr., Falkland Is., June, 1931; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, July, 1931; ag. prin. med. offr., Mar. to Oct., 1932; M.S.A. (Lond.), 1932; ag. senr. med. offr., Dec., 1934 to Feb., 1935; med. offr., Nigeria, 1936.
EDMUNDSON, K., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.).—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1928.

EDWARD, DOUGLAS SANDMAN, F.R.S.A., A.M.I.Struct.E., A.I.A., M.Inst.R.A., Chart.Struc.Engnr.—B. 1897; ed. Grove Acad., Broughty Ferry, Tech. Coll. Dundee (Armitstead med.), and St. Andrews Univ.; mily. serv., R.E.'s, 1915-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

EDWARDS, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in deep., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; dist. comsrr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in deep., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commit., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. com., 1907-12; comit.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Apr., 1923.

EDWARDS, DAVID, M.A. (1912). LL.B. (Aberdeen), 1914.—Temp. offr., R. Art'y., 1916-1920; served at home, N.W. Frontier of India, German E. Africa and Mesopotamia; Scottish solr., 1921; asst. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1921; ag. regar., sup. ct., Kenya, 1921; registr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; called to Scottish bar, 1924; res. mag., Kenya, 1928; relieving pres., dist. ct., Palestine, 1935; pres., dist. ct., 1936.
EDWARDS, GEOFFREY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1895; asst. engr., P.W.D., Malaya, Feb., 1924; ex. engr., Feb., 1933; ag. attit. engnr., Malacca, June, 1936; ag. senr. exec. engnr., Central, S'pore, May, 1938.

EDWARDS, JOSHUA PRICE.—B. 1898; ed. Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Jan., 1924; ag. conserv., forests, Pahang, Sept., 1930; on sp. dutv in U.K., Sept., 1932; forest offr., S'pore, Nov., 1934; state forest officer, Selangor, Nov., 1936; conserv., forests, Malayan forest service, Sept., 1937; conserv., forests, Johore, Nov., 1937; state forest offr., Pahang, July, 1938.

EDWARDS, RICHARD.—B. 1909; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1932; M.A., 1937; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1933; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Sept., 1936; 1st clk. and mag., Feb., 1936; 3rd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1936; 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1937; dist. offr., North, June, 1938; 2nd pol. mag., July, 1938; 1st do., July-Aug., 1939.

EDWARDS, WILFRED HENRY, D.I.C., F.E.S.—B. 1897; lect., zoology and entomology, Coll. of Agr., Mauritius, 1924; ag. entomologist, botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Mauritius, 1928; govt. entomologist, Jamaica, May, 1929.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM STUART, C.M.G. (1935), K.C.—B. 1890; called to the bar, Ontario, Sept., 1909; legal offr., dept. of justice, Canada, Nov., 1910; sec. of dept. of justice, 1913; asst. dep. min. of justice, 1914; dep. min. of justice, 1924.
EBLES, GEORGE NIXON, B.Litt., M.A. (Oxon.), M.R.S.T.—B. 1897; ed. Lincoln coll., Oxford; asst. educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tombridge Schl.; cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); a J.P. and coroner for S. Stltmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; offr. assignee and regisr. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Stltmts., May, 1897; dep. pres. of mun. comanrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comanr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, July, 1904; gov. and comdr.-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov., B. Guiana, Mar., 1912; ret., Mar., 1917.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1925).—B.1881; ed. Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; F.R.C.V.S., 1914; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland adminstr., May, 1903; prin. vet. offr., 1919; agrl. offr., 1928.

ELLENBERGER, VIVIAN FRANKLIN.—B. 1896; clk. to res. comanr., Bech. Prot., 1915; passed 2nd grade Secwana exam., 1919; sub-inspr., Rech. Prot. pol., 1920; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928; res. mag., May, 1934.
ELLIS, CHARLES WILFRED, M.C.—B. 1898; ed. Sleaford and Edin. Univ. (dipl. forestry); on war serv., 1914-19; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Nov., 1922.
ELLIS, HERBERT JOHN DRUMMOND.—B. 1903; grade II clk., Basutoland, 1925; ag. sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1928; asst. dist. comanr., 1937; Basutoland govt. rep., Johannesburg, 1938.

ELLIOTT, COLIN FRASER (Fraser), K.C., B.A., Sc., C.E.—Solr., income tax br., dept. of finance, Canada, July, 1919; asst. comanr., income tax, dept. of national rev., May, 1926; comanr., income tax, July, 1932.

ELLIOTT, HON. JOHN CAMPBELL, B.C.L., D.C.L., K.C., (Middlesex West).—B. 1872; ed. Ekfrid pub. schl., Strathroy C.I., Glencoe High Schl. and Trinity Univ.; B.C.L., Osgoode Hall, 1898; D.C.L., Toronto Univ., 1905; barrister-at-law; elec. Ontario legis., g.e., 1908; re-elec., 1911 and 1914; first elec., H. of C., Canada, g.e., Oct., 1905; mem.; Privy Coun. and apptd. min. of lab., 1926; re-elec. by-el., Mar., 1926; min. of health and soldiers' civ. re-estab., Apr., 1926; resigned, June, 1926; re-elec., g.e., Sept., 1926; min., pub. wks., King Cabinet, Sept., 1926; re-ele. by accl., Nov., 1926; re-ele., g.e., July, 1930; resigned portfolio after defeat of liberal admnstr. at polls, Aug., 1930; re-ele., g.e., 1933; P.M.G., 1935-39.
ELLIOTT, PHILIP JOHN.—B. 1892; entd. crown agents' office, Dec., 1911; asst. head, dept., July, 1927; dep. head, stores dept., Nov., 1933.

ELLIS, JOHN ALFRED.—B. 1900; ed "Michaelhouse," Natal, tech. coll., Durban; on war serv., E. African campaign, 1917-18; clearance sec., E. African war acts., Kenya and Jubaland, Jan., 1920 to Apr., 1924; examr., accts., audit dept., Kenya, Apr., 1924; ag. asst. audr., Nov., 1924 to June, 1927 and Mar. to Nov., 1928; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., Ap., 1929; ag. dep. ch. acctnt., in 1934 and 1935; acctnt., P.W.D., Cyprus, Apr., 1936.

ELLIS, JOHN MEDICOTT, C.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; ed., Haileybury and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schl.); served, Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914-19; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, March, 1920 and Turkish, July, 1921; ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1923-26; ag. col. sec., Falkland Is., Sept., 1926 to Aug., 1927; col. sec., Aug., 1927; ag. gov., in 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; local audr. from May, 1930 to Feb., 1931; regir. gen. and mag. from July, 1930 to Jan., 1931; col. sec. and regir. gen., Grenada, Oct., 1934; asst. ch. sec., Nyasaland Prot., 1933; ag. ch. sec., May-June, 1936 and Feb.-Nov., 1937; ag. prov. comsnr., July, 1936-Jan., 1937; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; ag. ch. sec., Jan.-Apr., 1939.
ELLIS, SYDNEY EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1908; ed. Calday Grange Gram. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1933; North circuit, 1934-37; crown coun., Gold Coast, May, 1937.
ELLISON, EDWARD WILFRID.—B. 1905; ed. Shrewsbury and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; asst. dist. commr., 1931.

ELLISON, RANDALL ESKEINE, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; ed. Repton and New Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., Nigeria, 1928; seconded for serv. in Somaliland as dir. of educ., 1938; author of "An English-Kanuri Sentence Book" (1937).
ELPHINSTONE, SIR H. G., BART.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1928; ag. prin. asst., native affrs., 1933.

ELPHINSTONE, SIR LANCELOT HENRY, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's inn, 1904; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1919; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; puisne judge, designate S. Stilmlta, 1924; ag. solr. gen., Ceylon, June, 1924; atty.-gen., Oct., 1921; ch. just., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ret., 1932.

EMBERTON, JOHN JAMES, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Newcastle-under-Lyme, Staffs.; mily. serv. 1914-19, England, Egypt, Belgium, France and Germany; admnstr. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1920; do., cls. III., 1929; do., cls. I., 1936; comsnr. of col., Lagos, 1937; prin. asst. sec., secret., and ag. dir., transport, 1939.

EMBURY, BRIG.-GEN. HON. JOHN FLETCHER LEOPOLD, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; served European War, 28th Can. Inf., 13th Inf. Bde., 2nd Inf. Bde., Can. Contingent; Br. G.H.Q., France (Canadian sec.), 1914-19; ment. in desps. three times, C.B., C.M.G.; judge of King's Bench, Saskatchewan, 1918.
EMBURY, W. E.—B. 1898; on mil. serv., Oct., 1916 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Dec., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., 8th Nov., 1921.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1864; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and La Poile, 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909; registr., supreme ct., 1917.
EMERSON, WILLIAM GRAHAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1898; ed. R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; vety. offr., Kenya, 1921; senr., do., 1939.

EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 26th Jan., 1922; promoted asst. prin. under clause 61, 4th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st May, 1936.

EMPSON, CHARLES.—B. 1898; ed. Harrow and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A. (S.K.); joined Iraq civil admin., Aug., 1930; addnl. under sec. to high commr., Sept., 1921; under sec. (consular), July, 1922; H.M.'s consul, Baghdad, Dec., 1924; consular sec., July, 1927.
ENGLAND, RUSSELL, M.B.E.—B. 1894; dairy expert and inspr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1926; ch. agrl. offr., 1934.
ENGLISH, JOSEPH SANDYS, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda Hosp., Dub.), F.C.O.G.—B. 1890; prof. of midwifery and gynaecology, King Edward VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Jan., 1922.

ENGLISH, PATRICK THOMAS.—B. 1902; 5th clk., treas., Montserrat, 1921; clk. to mag. and regr., 1924; ag. offr. in ch., prison, 1924; 3rd clk., treas., 1925; ag. regr., sup. ct., 1926; jun. clk. col., sec.'s office, Antigua, 1927; ag. clk. of couns., 1928 and 1930; ag. clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1930; ag. ast. col. sec., Leeward Is., and clk., fed. couns., 1930; clk. and cust. offr., Gilbert and Elliose Is. Col., 1931; ag. acct., 1931-33; ag. sec. to govt., June-Aug., 1933.
ENTRICAN, ALEXANDER ROBERT, A.A.S.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll., N.Z.; engr. in forest products, N.Z. state forest serv., 1921; dir. forestry, 1939.

EPSTEIN, ALEXANDER, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1886; ed. Hebrew Inst., Liverpool; Jews Hosp., W. Norwood; inspr., dept. of commerce and industry, Palestine, 1920; dist. offr., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1923; admstve. offr., S. dist., 1925.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; office ast. Jaffna kach., July, 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayts., Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Avissawela, June, 1915; ast. comsnr., excise, Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Mar., 1918; office ast. to govt. ast., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1918; pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1924; dep. comsnr., stamps, Feb., 1928; comsnr., do., July, 1931; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1933; ast. govt. ast., Kurunegala, May, 1934; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1934; do., Kalutara, July, 1935; ast. govt. ast., do. Mar., 1936; offr., cls. I., Jan., 1937; ag. govt. ast., E.P., Feb., 1937; govt. ast., N.W.P., Oct., 1938.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VIVIAN ARMIT., M.Inst., C.E.—B.1875; ed. St. Paul's Schl., Lond., and Crystal Palace Schl. of Engng.; served, P.W.D., Jamaica; ast. engnr., Lagos govt. rly., 1900-01; dist. engnr., Gold Coast Govt. rly., 1901; exec. engnr., P.W.D. E. Africa Prot., June, 1905; ag. ast. D.P.W., 1908 and 1909; D.P.W., Uganda Prot., 1911; Uganda Volunteers, 1914-19; hon. lt.-col., 1916; mny of munitions while on leave, 1917; D.P.W., Jamaica, Aug., 1921; mem., leg. coun., D.P.W., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; mem., fed. coun.; chm. electrical bd. and others; mem., rly. bd., immig. comm. and others; ret. July 1928; re-employed as eng. in ch., colonial development fund wks., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

ESPITALIER-NOEL, JOSEPH GEORGES.—B. 1894; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1916; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1919; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Sept., 1921; addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1922; ag. subst. procureur and advoc. gen. on various occasions, 1924-29; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., Mar., 1929; ag. mast. and regisr., supt. et., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1932 and Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct. in 1932 and 1933.

EULER, HON. WILLIAM D. (Waterloo N.)—B. 1875; ed. at pub. schls., Berlin (Kitchener), High Schl., Toronto Normal Schl.; mayor of Berlin (Kitchener), 1913-14; pres., Kitchener bd. of trade, 1917; first elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1917; re-ele., g.e., 1921, 1925 and 1926; mem. privy coun. and apptd. min. of national rev. in King cabinet 1926; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office; re-ele., g.e., 1930; resigned portfolio with Mr. King's cabinet, 1930; re-elec. at g.e., 1935; min. of trade and commerce in King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

EVANS, ARTHUR ANTHONY, O.B.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., Lieut. Col., R. Corps of Signals (Res.)—Ed. Rodribb Acad., Carmarthen and Univ. of Wales, Cardiff; served in S. African War (Queen's med., 4 clasps); engnr. branch, G.P.O. 1898-1913; R.E., signals, Gallipoli, Egypt; asst. dir., Army signals and ch. signal offr., Palestine and Syria, 1914-20; four ment. in desps., Order of White Eagle, 5th cls. with swords, O.B.E., M.C., Br. and Allies war medals, "1914-15" Star; ch. engnr., Palestine posts and tels., 1920.

EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES, F.R.A.S., F.R.Met.Soc.—B.1887; computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1903; junior asst., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1909; 1st asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1912; on military service, 1917 to 1919; ag. ch. asst. in 1915, 1916, 1920, 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1929; asst. dir., 1932; ag. dir. in 1935 and 1939.

EVANS, GEOFFREY WILLIAM.—B. 1882; civ. serv., higher law, 1908; civ. serv., L.L.B., 1922; civ. serv., 1902; mag. various stations, Union; active serv., 1899-1902 and 1914-18; sp. "C" grade mag., Uitenage, Feb., 1937; sp. "B" grade, Oct., 1938.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., KT. BACH. (1926), C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; M.H.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, 1897 to 1908; M.H.A. for Franklin, 1909 to 1937; warden and master warden for marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. for educn., Tasmania, July, 1904 to Oct., 1905; premier and treas., Oct., 1905 to May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker, House of Assembly, for many years; lt.-govrn. since 10th March, 1937.

EVANS, LEONARD WINNE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.Sc., certif. L.S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1891; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1922; med. and health offr., P.W., north and central, June, 1923; ch. med. offr., Kelantan, Jan., 1929; do., S'pore, Sept., 1935; prin. med. offr., Johore, Feb., 1937; ag. prin., coll. of med., S'pore, May, 1938.
EVANS, MICHAEL HENRY LEE.—B. 1910; asst. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1939.

EVANS, RICHARD FRANCIS.—B. 1901; cadet, N. Borneo, Jan., 1921; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., Sept., 1925; mag., 1st cls., Mar., 1931; ag. res., Feb., 1935; addl. sess. judge, Jan., 1936; offr., mem., leg. coun., Jan., 1937; res., July, 1937; judge, high ct., May, 1938.

EVANS, SAMUEL ALBAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; ast. vety. pathologist, May, 1926; ag. vety. pathologist, 1930; senr. vety. offr., 1938.

EVATT, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1883; commr., water conservation and irrign. commn., N.S.W., Apl., 1921; chmn., dried fruits bd., Feb., 1927; mem., canned fruits control bd., Dec., 1930; chmn., dairy products bd., Jan., 1934.

EVELYN, EDWARD ARTHUR,(jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl.; supernym. rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1922; ch. clk., post office, 1927; dep. harbrmr. and cashier, treasy., 1928; govt. offr., Eastern dist. and clk. to mag., dist. F, Dominica, 1930; ag. asst. in treasy. and post office, Tortola, Virgin Isl., 1932; ag. mag., dist. F, also addnl. mag., dist. E, 1933; J.P., 1934; prin., admtrs.' office, clk. of couns. and mem., pub. tenders comtee., St. Kitts—Nevis, 1935.

EVERETT, CYRIL FREDERICK CUNNINGHAM.—B. 1886; supervisor, cust. 2nd grade, Nigeria, 1916; collr., 1924; senr. do., 1936; ag. ast. comptr. and ag. dep. comptr. on various occasions, 1933-37; ast. comptr., 1937.

EVESON, STEPHEN WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. & S.), F.R.S.S., Certif., Schl. of Trop., Med. and Hyg.—B. 1900; clin. ast., ven. dis. clin. (L.C.C.) Lond. Hosp., 1924-25; med. offr., S.S., July, 1925; health offr., Feb., 1926 and Dec., 1927; ag. senr. health offr., Sept. 1935; health offr., superscale grade B, M.M.S., 1937.

EWART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Botelor Schl., Warrington and Rossall Schl., Williams Mem. Exhn.; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; pres., O.U.S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (chem. pt. 1); F.C.S., 1923; 2nd chem., pt. 2., 1924; mem., Inner Temple, 1925; ast. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1926; M.A., 1927.

EYBERS, GEORG VON WELPFING.—B. 1887; ed. Boy's High Schl., Beaufort West, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Univ. of Amsterdam and King's Coll., London; B.A. (Cape); M.A., D. Litt. (Lond.); prin., Graaf Reinet Coll. and Hoët Volksskol, 1919-26; mem., Cape provin. educn. coman., 1926-27; inspr., schl., 1927; under sec. educn., Aug., 1927; prof. ast., Union educn. dept., July, 1934; author, various works on S. African hist. and travel.

EYRE-SMITH, CAPT. ST. JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Edmunds Coll., Ware; served with R.A.S.C., France and Germany, 1915-19; Turkey, 1919-20; ment. in desps.; ast. dist. coman., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. coman., 1925; ast. sec., native affrs., 1931.

EZECIEL, SIR PERCY HUBERT, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1875; B.A. Bombay, 1899 (ct. 14); B.A., London, 1894; major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl., C.O., 11th Oct., 1896; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; sec. to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; mem. of Fair Wages Advisory Comttee, 1909-1920; sec. to Colonial Economic Development Comttee, 1919-20; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., 1919 (resigned secretarieship 1921); crown agt. for the cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, 1921; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire Exhibn., 1921-25; chmn., Palestine currency bd., 1926; mem., Hong Kong currency coman., 1931; ret., 1938.

FAGAN, HON. MARK, M.L.C.—B. 1873; ed. Waratah public schl., Tasmania; appd. mem., N.Z. leg. encl., 1930; represented N.Z. at Empire Parly. Confce., London, 1935; leader, leg. encl. and mem., exec. encl., 1935-39; speaker, leg. encl., 1939.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; arrived S. Africa, 1898; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924; re-apptd., Apr., 1930; chmn., Mar., 1932.
FAIR, HON. ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1885; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown solr., 1921; prin. law offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.Z., 1934.

FAIRBAIRN, SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1855; ed. Geelong Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1924-27.
FAIRBAIRN, H., M.D., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1898; mily. serv. 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; sleeping sickness offr., 1935.

FAIRBAIRN, HON. JAMES VALENTINE, M.P.—Served R.F.C., war, 1916-19; M.L.A., Victoria, Warrnambool, 1932-33; mem. Flinders, H. of R. C. wealth of Aust. from Nov., 1933; vice-pres. exec. encl., min. for civ. aviation and asst. min., defence, Menzies miny., Apr.-Nov., 1939; visited Canada in connection with Empire Air Force training scheme, Oct., 1939; min. for air from 13th Nov., 1939.

FAIRCLOUGH, F.R., M.B.E., (1938)—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv. and assigned to C.O., Aug., 1914; on military service from 13th Dec., 1915 to 9th March, 1919; priv. sec. to Sir James Stevenson, 12th Aug., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; staff offr., 15th July, 1933.

FAIRLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.) B.A., (mech. science tripos) 1913; 6th Bu. E. Surrey Regt., 1914; 1st K.A.R., 1917-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. asst. D.P.W., in 1923 and 1924; exec. engr., P.W.D., 1922; ag. asst., D.P.W., 1923; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1925; ch. engr., P.W.D., N. Rhodesia, 1935; M.L.C., 1936-38.

FAIRWEATHER, EUGENE ERNEST, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Univa. and Dalhousie Law Schl., N.S.; asst. solr., dept. of rlys. and canals, 1912; solr., 1914; counsel, 1919; asst. gen. counsel, Can. Nat. rlys., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, N.D.A., U.D.A.—B. 1896; ed. Montrose Acad., Aberdeen Univ., N. of Scot. Coll. of Agric.; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1921; ag. mun. sec., Malacca in addn., May, 1927-Feb., 1928; agrl. offr., Johore, Dec., 1931; offr., prin. agrl. offr., Apr., 1933; agrl. offr., Johore Cent., Feb., 1935; ag. state agrl. offr., Johore, Oct., 1936.

FAITH, JOHN ALEXANDER AELFRED, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1903; ed. Harrow, New Coll., Oxford; cadet, M.C.S., Jan., 1927; ag. 4th asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1929; passed cadet, Dec., 1929; offr., cl. V, Aug., 1930; ag. 2nd asst. advr., Batu Pahat, Nov., 1931; offr., cl. IV, Aug., 1934; ag. dist. offr., Larut and region., titles, Perak, Mar., 1937; offr., cl. III, Aug., 1939.

FALCONER, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen (1st cls. hon.); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; asst. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. registr., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second asst. advr., Muar, Feb., 1928; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1930; do., Raub, July, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. advr., B. Pahat, June, 1934; commr., lands and mines, Trengganu, Mar., 1938; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1938.

FALK, EDWARD MORRIS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl., on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. commr., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and retd. to England, 1915; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ag. admstr. of col., 1931.

FALLOWS, LIEUT. ALBERT EDWARD, B.Sc. (Civ. Engnr.).—B. 1898; ed. Bolton Ch.Inst.Schl., Merchant Venturer's Coll. and Bristol Univ.; war serv., 1916-19; asst. engr., anti-malaria br., F.M.S., Mar., 1924; ex. engr., Oct., 1933.

FANNIN, C. G., M.C., B.A., Oxon., Assoc. Inst. C. E.—B. 1890; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, Rhodes schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford in 1908-11; hons. engrnr. sci., 1911; G. E. C. London, 1911-14; 1/1st Lond. Heavy Rgt., R.G.A., 1915-16, adjt., 7th H. A. Group, 1917; (ment. in desps., M.C.); asst. inspr., guns, Woolwich, 1918-19; Kenya survey dept., 1920; dist. survr., asst. land offr.; ch. survr., 1937; dir. surveys, 1938; dep. dir., surveys, Gold Coast, 1939; author of paper "Townplanning at Mombasa."
FANSHAWE, DENYS BASIL.—B. 1915; ed. Oundle Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford, B.A. (forestry), 1936; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana Oct., 1937.

FARIS, DESMOND WILLIAM GEORGE, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), Certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1901; ed. Epson Coll., Lond. Hosp. Med. Coll., Lond. Univ.; med. offr., Malaya, Mar., 1926; ag. sr. health offr., Penang, July, 1935; do., S'pore, Feb., 1937; superscale offr., "B", Sept., 1938.

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; asst. clk., Apr., 1917; on mil. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925, staff offr., 20th Apr., 1933; pvt. sec. to parl. under-secr. of state, 4th Dec., 1939.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NIEL, M.C.—B. 1896; on mil. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach., June, 1921; office astt. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec., land commr., Aug., 1928; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1930; clk. to leg. coun., Mar., 1930; clk. to state coun., July, 1931; contr. establishments, July, 1932; col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1934; ag. col. sec., May-June, 1937; gov't. dep. on several occasions.

FARJAKHARSON, JAMES ROBBIE, B.Sc., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1903; ed. Glasgow Univ. and Royal Tech. Coll., 1920-23; L.M.S. Rly., 1923-25; astt. engrnr., K.U.R. and H., 1925; senr. astt. engrnr. and acted frequently as dist. engrnr.; personal astt. to gen. man., Tanganyika Rly., May, 1937.
FARR, FRANK JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928.

FARRELLI, GERALD HILDEBRAND.—B. 1891; ed. abroad and Cranbrook Schl.; Ceylon pol., 1911; N. Nigeria pol., 1914; attd. Nigeria Regiment, 1917-18; Nigeria pol., 1930; ag. astt. inspr. gen., various periods 1931-34 and 1936; ag. dep. do., 1935-36; senr. supt., pol., 1936; ag. astt. commr., various periods 1937-38.

FARRELL, CAPT. WILFRID JEROME, O.B.E. (1936), M.C., M.A., R.O. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Schl. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schl. and Haileybury Coll., 1911-19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educon. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, miny. of educon., Iraq, 1921; senr. educon. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; astt. dir., educon., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir. on various occasions, 1927-33; br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929; dir., educon., 1936.

FAULKNER, ODIN TOM, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. triplos, 1911); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and agrl. expert to Rubber Growers' Assocn., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr., Nigeria, 1921; do., S.S. and advr. on agr., F.M.S., 1936; prin., Imp. School of Trop. Agrio., Trinidad (seconded), 1938.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E.(1918)—Asst. auditor E.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. astt. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.

FAURIE, PHILIP EDUARD.—B. 1879; educated Graaff Reinet Coll., Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; C.S. higher law; clk., atty. gen.'s office, Cape Town, 1898; mag's office, Cape Town, 1900; astt. mag., Simonstown, 1909; pub. pros., Cape Town, 1916; ch. clk. and astt. mag., Cape Town, 1918; mag., Murrayburg, 1919; Bedford, 1923; Nylstroom (Waterberg), 1929; Harrismith, 1932; Paarl, 1937.

FAWCETT, CAPT. ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., B.A.I. (Dublin), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1890; war serv., 1916-19, R.E., France, Persia, Afghanistan, ment. in des.; astt. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Apr., 1920; dist. engrnr., Apr., 1925; do., grade I, Apr., 1929.

FAWDRY, ALAN LOVJOY, M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Cantab.).—B. 1912; ed. Clifton Coll., Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge (Foundation schol. and 1st cls. Nat. Sc. Triplos. Pt. I) and Lond. Hosp.; D.M.O., Cyprus, Feb., 1939.
FAZAN, S. H., C.B.E. (1934), O.B.E. (1930), Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. commr., Kenya, Feb., 1920; senr. dist. commr., 1934; prov. commr., 1936.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A. (litt. hum.), 1902; clk., law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; astt. registr., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; 1st astt. registr. and taxing offr., 1923; registr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.
FEATHERSTONE, ERIC KELLETT.—B. 1896; admstve. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1929; cls. II, 1937; ag. rea., various periods, 1937-39; admstve. offr., cls. I, 1939.

FEETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high commr. of S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A. Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps, 1916-19; mem., Southborough comte., on Indian Reforms and chmn. functions comte., 1918-19; mem., India Office comte., on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHILY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.I., F.A.C.S., D.P.H. Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; med. offr., Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; M.O. in ch. Kowloon and new territories, 1925; second med. health offr., port, 1926; M.O. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., 1927; port health offr. and hosp., immigrts., 1931.
FELL, WILLIAM ARTHUR, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1907; prof. of anatomy, King Edward VII Co'l. of Med., S'pore., Aug., 1936.
FELTHAM, STANLEY CRATHERN, A.R.I.B.A., chtd. architect.—B. 1896; ed. Northern Polytechnic, London; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

FENDALL, FRANCIS ALAN, L.S., N.Z. King's Coll. Auck. Univ.—B. 1891; lic. survr., N. Z., Mar., 1916; survr., F.M.S., Feb., 1920; do., Kedah, May, 1920; astt. supt., surveys, Sept., 1923; do., F.M.S., Mar., 1928; senr. astt. supt., surveys, S'pore, Dec., 1933; ag. senr. supt., do., Aug., 1936; ch. survr., cls. II, Feb., 1939.

FENN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Diocesan and St. Andrew's Colls., S. Africa; clk., staff of gov't. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-28; served, S. A. Arty. and lieut.-R.A.F., 1915-19; sub-inspr., Swaziland pol., 1928; inspr., 1931; ch., pol., 1933.
FENNER, CHARLES ALBERT EDWARD, D.Sc., Dip.Ed., F.R.G.S.—B. 1884; joined South Australian educn. dept., as supt., tech. educon., 1916; dir., educon., 1939.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD.—M.H.R. Commonwealth of Australia since 1910; mem., standing comte., of pub. wke., 1914-17 and of joint comte., pub. accts., 1917-25; opposition whip, 1922-29; min. for trade and cust., 1929; Australian del. to Disarmament Conf., London, 1930; P.M.G., Lyons' Ministry, 1932; resigned, Oct., 1932.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1891; ed. Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia, 1917-19; passed progress exam., in Mendi; asst. DIST. COMMR., SIERRA LEONE, APR., 1915; DIST. COMMR., APR., 1925; SENR. DIST. COMMR., 1936; AG. PROV. COMMR. ON SEVERAL OCCASIONS.
FERGUSON, HON. SIR DAVID GILBERT, KT. BACH. (1934)—B. 1861; CALLED TO THE BAR, 1890; JUDGE OF SUP. CT., NEW SOUTH WALES, MAR., 1912; AG. CH. JUST., 1929; RET., 1932.
FERGUSON, GORDON PATRICK, B.A. (CAMBRIDGE).—B. 1904; ASST. MAST., HONGKONG EDUC. DEPT., 1929.
FERGUSON, JAMES JOHNSTON, M.A. (EDIN.), DIP. ED. (LONDON).—B. 1908; ASST. MAST., EDUC. DEPT., HONG KONG, 1930; GUNNER, H.K.V.D.C., 1939.
FERGUSON, JOHN TAYLOR, A.L.A.A.—B. 1900; OLK., K.U.R. AND H., 1924; CONSTRN. CASHIER AND FIELD PAYMR., 1926; CONSTRN. ACCTNT. AND CH. CLER. ASST., 1928; CONSTRN. ACCTNT., 1929; ASST. ACCTNT., 1936.

FERGUSON, JOSHUA WILLIAM, I.S.O., M.B.E., DIP. ECON. AND COMM. (SYDNEY UNIV.), COMM. OFFR., AGT. GEN. DEPT., LONDON, 1920-24; SEC. AND COMM. OFFR. IN LONDON, 1924-36; CH. OLK., PREMIER'S DEPT., SYD., 1936-38; AG. UNDER SEC. AND PERM. HEAD, PREMIER'S DEPT., MAY, 1938.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES BAETZ, G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1915); K.C.M.G. (1918); O.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1938), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ED. ETON AND SANDHURST; JOINED GRENADIER GUARDS, 1883; ADJUTANT, 1890-94; JOINED EGYPTIAN ARMY, 1896; SUDANESE CAMPAIGNS, 1896-98; SEVERELY WOUNDED, ROSSAIRES (MED. WITH EIGHT CLASPS, MENT. FIVE TIMES IN DESPS.); COMMANDER 15TH SUDANESE, 1899 (2ND CLS. MEDICINE); GARRISON AND DIST., OMDURMAN, 1900; ADJ.-GEN., EGYPTIAN ARMY, 1901-3; COMMANDED 3RD BATT. GRENADIER GUARDS, 1904-7; BRIG.-GEN., GEN. STAFF, IRISH COMMD., 1907-8; INSPR., INFY., 1909-13; SERVED EUROPEAN WAR, 1914-18; IN COMMUL., 5TH DIVN. AND SUBSEQUENTLY OF 2ND AND 17TH ARMY CORPS; MILY. GOV., OCCUPIED GERMAN TERRITORY, 1918-19 (SEVEN TIMES MENT. IN DESPS.); GOV. AND COMMDR.-IN-CH., SOUTH LAND, OCT., 1924; ASSUMED GOV'T., DEC., 1924; RET., 1930; CHMN., W. INDIAN CLOSER UNION COMM., 1932.

FERGUSSON, WILFRED KORTHWRIGHT, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1910; ED. HARRISON COLL., BARBADOS AND CAMBRIDGE UNIV.; CALLED TO BAR, GRAY'S INN, 1933; LEGAL DRAUGHTSMAN AND CLK. TO ATTY.-GEN'L., BARBADOS, 1935; POL. MAG. AND CORONER, 1938.
FERNANDO, JASENTHU LIYANA EDWIN, B.SC. (LOND.).—B. 1914; RES. ASST. IN ENTOMOLOGY, MED. DEPT., CEYLON, OCT., 1938; CADET, CIV. SERV., DEC., 1938.

FERNANDO, PETER OLIVER.—B. 1898; CADET, CEYLON CIV. SERV., DEC., 1921; ATTD. TO KURUGALLA KACH., DEC., 1921; DITTO, BATTICALOA KACH., FEB., 1922; DITTO, JAFFNA KACH., MAR., 1923; POL. MAG., PUTTALAM, APR., 1924; ATTD., RATNAPURA KACH., SEPT., 1925; OFFICE ASST., DITTO, MAR., 1926; POL. MAG., PANADURA, DEC., 1926; OFFICE ASST., MANNAR KACH., OCT., 1927; POL. MAG., MATALE, JAN., 1929; ASST. ATTLM. OFFR., JAN., 1930 AND MAY, 1934; POL. MAG., GAMPOLA, MAY, 1933; ATTD. OFFICE OF MIN. FOR AGR. AND LANDS, APR., 1934; ASST. RUBBER CONTR., IN ADUNI, MAY, 1934; ASST. GOV'T. AGT., MULLAITIVU, NOV., 1936; DEP. RUBBER CONTR., SEPT., 1938.

FERNANDO, SELLAPERUMAGE SHELTON CLARENCE, B.A. (OXON).—B. 1907; CADET, CEYLON CIV. SERV., NOV., 1931; ATTD., CH. SEC. OFFICE, DEC., 1931; ATTD., RATNAPURA KACH., SEPT., 1932; OFFICE ASST., MATALE, MAR., 1933; DO., PUTTALAM, JAN., 1936; ASST. GOV'T. AGT., BADULLA, OCT., 1936.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, KT. BACH. (1930).—ED. BEDFORD SCHL.; CALLED TO IRISH BAR, 1903; CROWN COUN., IRELAND, 1912-21; MEM., COUN. OF BAR OF IRELAND AND OF STANDING COMMITTEE; K.C.I., 1919; COUN. TO MILY. DURING SIAN FEIN REBELLION, 1916; COUN. TO ATTY.-GEN., 1919; COUN. TO CROWN, 1920; CALLED TO BAR, GRAY'S INN, 1922; JUDGE, HIGH CT., PUNJAB, 1922-31; PRES., SPECIAL DIS., SWAZILAND AND BECHUANALAND PROT., JUDICIAL COMMR., BASUTOLAND, AND LEGAL ADV'R. TO HIGH COMMR. FOR S. AFRICA, JULY, 1934; RET., 1937.
FIELD, FRANK EUSTACE.—B. 1911; ED. HARRISON COLL., BARBADOS; CALLED TO BAR, MIDDLE TEMPLE, 1933; AG. POL. MAG., DISTS. "A" AND "C" FROM APR., 1937; POL. MAG. AND CORONER, DIST. "A", JUNE, 1937.

FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1891; ED. ST. JOHN'S SCHL., LEATHERHEAD AND QUEEN'S COLL., CAMBRIDGE (B.A.); ON WAR SERV., 1914-18; DEMOB. (CAPT.) 1919; INSPR. AND SCHOOLMASTER, S.P., NIGERIA, 1922; SUPT., EDUC., 1926; PRIN., GOVT. COLL., IBADAN, 1932.

FIELD, JOHN WILLIAM, M.D. (HONS. B'HAM. UNIV.).—B. 1899; MED. OFFR., F.M.S., JULY, 1926; MALARIA RESEARCH OFFR., II, INST. FOR MED. RESEARCH, F.M.S., MAY, 1931; DO., GRADE I, AUG., 1934; AG. BACTERIOLOGIST, INST. FOR MED. RESEARCH, F.M.S., FEB., 1936; SENR. MALARIA RESEARCH OFFR., AUG., 1937.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMOND, B.A. (OXON).—B. 1889; ED. WORCESTER COLL., OXFORD; ASST. DIST. COMMR., GOLD COAST COLONY, APR., 1915; AG. DIST. COMMR. ON VARIOUS OCCASIONS, 1918-21; DIST. COMMR., AUG., 1922; AG. POL. MAG., MAR. TO JUNE, 1925; PROV. COMMR., 1934.

FIENNES, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR RUSTACE EDWARD TWISLETON-WYKHAM, B.A.T.—B. 1864; LIEUT.-COL., OXFORD IMP. YEO.; SERVED RIOL REBELLION, 1886 (MEDAL WITH CLASP); EGYPT, 1887 AND 1888 (MEDAL WITH CLASP, BRONZE STAR); PIONEER EXPEDN. TO MASHONALAND, 1890; SOUTH AFRICA, 1900 (MEDAL WITH THREE CLASPS; TWICE MENT. IN DESPS.); EUROPEAN WAR, 1914 (FRANCE, BELGIUM, DARDANELLES AND HOME); M.P. (L.) BANBURY DIST. OF OXFORDSHIRE, 1906-1910, AND 1910-1918; GOV'T. AND COMMDR.-IN-CHIEF, SEYCHELLES, 6TH JULY, 1918; ASSUMED GOV'T., 8TH OCT., 1918; GOV'T., LEEWARD ISL., 29TH OCT., 1921; ASSUMED GOV'T., 4TH DEC., 1921; RET., 1929.

FIGGIS, ERNEST CLIVE.—B. 1902; ED. ST. BEES AND CORPUS CHRISTI COLL., OXFORD; CADET, N.P., NIGERIA, APR., 1928; POLIT. OFFR., ADEN PROT., MAR., 1938; AND POSTED ASST. RES. ADVISER, MUKALLA, MAR., 1938; AG. RES. ADVISER, MUKALLA, MAY-SEPT., 1938.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; HOME CUSTOMS AND EXCISE SERV., 1912-24; WAR SERV. IN R.N., 1918; COLL., CUSTOMS, SIERRA LEONE, 1924; DEP. DIR., DEPT. OF CUSTOMS, EXCISE AND TRADE, PALESTINE, 1928.

FINLAYSON, DUNCAN IAIN CAMPBELL.—B. 1906; ED. AT PERTH ACADEMY AND EDINBURGH UNIV., M.B., CH.B. (EDIN.), M.R.C.P. (EDIN.), D.T.M. & H. (ENG.), GOV'T. M.O., BR. GUIANA, 1926; SENR. PHYS., PUB. HOSP., GEORGETOWN, 1938.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1882; ED. PICTON ACADEMY AND DALHOUSIE UNIV.; B.A., 1907; APPTD. TO DEPT. OF INSURANCE, CANADA, 1907; ASSOCIATE OF INST. OF ACTUARIES OF GREAT BRITAIN, 1911; SUPT. OF INSURANCE, CANADA, 1914.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ED. GREY COLL., BLOEMFONTEIN, S.A. COLL., CAPE TOWN, TRINITY HALL, CAMBRIDGE; JUDGE, SUP. CT., SOUTH AFRICA, 1929.
FISSET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; STAFF, ADJUT. MED. CORPS, CANADA.

FITZ-HENRY, WILLIAM COOPER, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dulwich Coll.; asst. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1908; lieut. R.E., 1910; capt., R.E., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and adm'y., 1918; in India on dock and rly. work, 1920-27; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road engrnr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., Aug., 1930; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., 1935; ag. ch. engrnr., Dec., 1938-Jly., 1939.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), 1913; late out-patient offr., astt. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr. Somaliland Prot., 1914.

FITZMAURICE, LAURENCE WYLIE, M.D., C.M., McGill, L.M.C.C., M.C.F. & S., Manitoba—B. 1900; internship, Peking Union Med. Coll. Hosp., Peking, 1925-26; M.O., Canadian Nat. Rly., 1928-36; astt. M.O., Bahamas Gen. Hosp., 1936; M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas Gen. Hosp., 1937; ag. ch. M.O., 1938.

FLAKS, SAMUEL—B. 1888; mily. serv., E. Africa, 1916-18; acct. custodian, enemy property dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. custodian, enemy property, 1929; finan. astt., prov. adminstr., 1930; exec. offr., township authority, 1936; sen. astt. treas., 1937.

FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.)—Asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1913; audr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; audr., Windward Is., June, 1919; audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1925; do., Uganda, 1935; do., Palestine, 1938.

FLECKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoo. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1915; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, ALAN McKINSTY, M.C., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Served with 8th Bedfordshire Regt., B.E.F., 1915-18, ment. in desps.; Middlesex Hosp. Med. Sch.: house physician and house surg., Middlesex Hosp.; house physician, Brompton Hosp. for consumption; res. physician, Ruthin Castle; E. African med. serv., 1929; physician and lect. in medicine, Uganda Med. Sch., 1930-32; invalided, 1933; med. offr., Gibraltar, 1936.

FLEMING, EDWARD DONOVAN.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salojoba, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921, attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; astt. prot., Chinese, Taiping, May, 1930 and supt., convict estabm't, in addn., Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; prot., Chinese, Johore, July, 1934; do., Perak, Apr., 1936; do., Johore, Oct., 1937; ag. imrnigh. offr., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1938; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1938.

FLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown acts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; offr. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd astt. acctnt., 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMMING, MAXWELL HECTOR VANDERMERE.—B. 1904; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; Sudan polit. serv., 1926-32; Grenoble Univ., France, 1933; lector in English, Univ. of Freiburg-im-Breisgau, Germany, 1934; senr. German mast., Merchiston Castle schl., Edinburgh, 1935-38; ch. inspr., schls., Cyprus, Sept., 1938.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd. after compet. exam., S. and T. clks., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1915 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, Sir ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.T. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhbr.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. asst. regt. gen., 1903; J.P., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1905-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; asst. to atty-gen., 1909; registr., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. registr., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treas., 1913; asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comte., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; priv. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high commr., W. Pacific, 1929; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 1936; resigned, 1938.

FLETCHER COOKE, JOHN.—B. 1911; Barham schol., Malvern Coll., 1926; senr. exhibr., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1929; Kitchener schol.; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1934; offr., cls. V, Malayan civ. serv., 1937.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clerk, 1908; ch. clk., 1915; asst. postmtr., 1921; postmtr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLURET, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1899; mesgr., C.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun.clk., Jan., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1919; clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1924; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 1928 to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, Jan., 1931; lieut., Mar., 1934; aide-de-camp to gov., Feb., 1935; ag. offr. commdg. F. I. Defence Force, May-Oct., 1935; adjt., do., May-Dec., 1935; capt., do., May, 1937; ag. col. postmtr., July, 1935 to Feb., 1936; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1937.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; admnstr., offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H. Commr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. clk., exec. coun., Feb., Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. commr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commt., police, Sept., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. societies, June, 1923; registr., co-op. societies, July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. credit societies, June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924 to Feb., 1926; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade commr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927; treas., Cyprus, 1935; col. sec., Barbados, 1938; ag. gov., 1939.

FLIPPANCE, FRDERICK, F.L.S.—B. 1891; ed. Stoke schl., Surrey; edd. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1913; war serv., France 1916-19; asst. curator, Botanic Gardens, S.S. (Singapore), 1919; local head of dept., Waterfall Gardens, Penang, 1921-37; supt., bot. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1937; mem., Nutrition Research couns., 1939; volunteer serv., capt., Malaya, S.S.U.F., 1919-38; capt., H.K.V.D.C., 1938.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933)—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student); apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comte., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prim., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comte., 1924-26; mem. W. Afr. Currency Board, 1928; crown agr. for the cols., Jan., 1938; dir. of colonial scholars, 1st Jan., 1939.

FLOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (1921), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. Cranleigh Schol. and King's Coll., London; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn); ent. bd. of agr., 1894; pvt. sec. to Sir Thomas Elliott (1904-5); Mr. Alwyn Fellowes (Lord Ailwyn) (1905) and Lord Carrington (Marquess of Lincolnshire) (1906-7) as presdts. of bd. of agr.; head, land branch, 1907-14; asst. sec., 1914-19; perm. sec., miny. of agr. and fisheries, 1920; chmn., bd. of cust. and excise, 1927; perm. sec., min. of lab., 1930; high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s gov't. in the U.K., 1934.
FLYNN, JAMES BURNS, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Drogheda Grammar Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1928.
FOENANDER, CLARENCE EUGENE.—B. 1893; gen. cler. serv., Ceylon, 1912; salt advr. to govt., Aug., 1934.

FOGGIE, ALISTAIR, B.Sc. (Forestry).—B. 1908; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., 1932; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, July, 1936.

FOLEY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice men. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); asst. commd., R. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; commd., Police Training Schol., 1926; dist. sub-t. pol., N. dist., 1927; dep. inspr. gen., pol. and prisons, 1938.

FOLL, SENATOR HON. HATTIL SPENCER.—Served, A.I.F., Great War, 1914-15; el., senate, Commonwealth of Australia, 1917; mem., standing comte., on pub. wks., 1920-23; joint counte. pub. acts., 1924-26; mem., Commonwealth delegn. Empire parly. Assn. wh. visited England, 1935; min. for repatriation and in ch., war serv. homes, Lyons miny., Nov., 1937-Apr., 1939; min. for health, Nov., 1938-Apl., 1939; do., Page miny., 7th-26th Apl., 1939; min. for interior, Menzies miny., from Apl., 1939.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1920; attd. C.O., May-Oct., 1925; asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. asst. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. on various occasions; asst. ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1936; ag. pin. asst. ch. sec. and ag. ch. sec., various occasions; gov.'s dep., Dec., 1936; ag. finan. sec., Dec., 1937; admin. sec., 1938; ag. finan. sec., July, 1939-Jan., 1940.

FORBES, ALASTAIR GRANVILLE.—B. 1908; ed. Blundell's Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1932; mag. dist. "E" and govt. offr., E. dist. Dominica, 1936; ag crown atty., in 1936 and 1937; mag. dist. "E" May, 1937; J.P., Dec., 1936 and visiting just. of prison.

FORBES, Rt. Hon. GEORGE WILLIAM, P.C. (1930).—B. 1869; ed. Boys' High Schl., Christchurch; mem. N.Z. parl't since 1908; senr. whip, Liberal party, 1912-22; leader of opposition, 1925; leader of National party, 1925-28; min., lands and agr., 1928-30; prime min., min. of finance, external affs., cust., and stamp duties, 1930; prime min., min. of rly. and min. of external affs., in coalition min'y., 1931; atty. gen., 1933; native min., 1934; min. for Cook Is., 1934, ret. from office, 1935; rep. N.Z., Imp. Conf., 1930 and World Economic Conf., 1933; and at H.M.'s Silver Jubilee celebrations, 1935.
FORBES, JOHN, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chart. Civ. Engr.—B. 1902; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

FORBES, LESLIE.—B. 1889; R.A., Oxon. (lit. hum.), 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; lent to Kedah govt., 1914; sec. to adviser, Kedah, 1916; dist. offr., Dec., 1920-1922; offr. dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; collr., land rev., Seremban, Feb., 1931; dist. offr., Klang, June, 1933; ag. advr., lands, Kedah, Feb., 1935; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1937.
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; asst. ch. acct., Palestine rly., Aug., 1921; ch. acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1926.

FORD, GEORGE JAMES—B. 1896; Harvey Gram. Schl.; P.O., U.K., 1913; war serv. with R.E. in Egypt, Palestine, Turkey and Russia, 1915-19; passed suppl. clerksp. compet. exam. G.P.O. secy.'s office, 1920; sup't., P.O., S'pore, 1921; sup't., P. and T., Perak, Feb., 1930; sup't., i.e.c., N. Sembilan, Nov., 1933; contr. posts and tels., Malaya, 1937.

FORDE, JOHN REID (late Capt., R.A.M.C.), M.B., B.Ch.—On mily. serv., 1915-18; app'td. to W. African med. staff (Gold Coast), 1918; senr. med. offr., 1932; transf'd., Gambia, 1934; mem. exec. and leg. couns.; senr. med. offr., G. Coast, 1935.

FORRER, HENRY AUGUSTUS, M.A. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1886; cadet S. S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet, Aug., 1913; supern. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; att'd. to censor's office, May 1915; services lent to the F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Dec., 1923; dep. registr. and asst. off. assignee, Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1926; registr., sup. cls., Penang, May, 1927; registr., sup. cls., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1930; dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, Jan., 1933; do., Penang, June, 1939.

FORREST, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1892; asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; on active service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; asst. audr. in charge, Somaliland, 1917; ag. senr. audr., E. Africa Prot., in 1917, 1919 and 1920; asst. audr. in charge Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1922; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1925; treas. acct., Jan., 1926; mem. and sec., Kenya cost of living comnr., 1926; title of subst. post changed to prin. asst. treas., 1928; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ag. treas. in 1930, 1932 and 1934; treas., Oct., 1934; currency offr., mem., rly. advs. comn.; finan. sec., July, 1937.

FORREST, ROBERT ANDREW DERMOD.—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1919; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Jan., 1922; att'd. educn. dept., Jan., 1922; asst. head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; asst. treas. and dep. estate duty commr., 1931 and 1935; head, sany. dept., 1934; senr. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1934; supt., impts. and expts., Jan., 1935; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Dec., 1935; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Nov., 1935; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1935 and Aug.-Sept., 1936; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., Apr., 1937; att'd. col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1937; 1st pol. mag., Nov., 1937; ag. chmn., bd. of examiners, in addn., Nov., 1938.
FORREST, STANLEY, M.A. (1920), M.B., Ch.B. (Aberd.) (1924).—B. 1908; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Uganda, 1925; specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1939.

FORSYTH, DOUGLAS DAVID (C.S.H.L.).—B. 1896; clk., comdt. cadets, Pietermaritzburg, 1911; clk., comdt.-gen., Natal Militia, 1912; clk., urge.'s office Windhoek, 1920; Omaruru, 1921; ag. mag., Matjimine, 1923; clk., office sec., S.W.A., 1924; mag., Otjiwarongo, 1925; Sutherland, 1931; pub. serv. inspr., 1934; under-sec. for social welfare, 1937; sec. for S.W.A., 1939.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ag. consmr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island consmr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.

FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LITTLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradford and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); mem. of educn., Egypt, Jan. 1913; asst. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1913; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; ag. prov. consmr., Nov.-Dec., 1933; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan.-Sept., 1934; dep. prov. consmr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1935; prov. consmr., Mar., 1939.

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCE, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., K.S.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-19; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924; Swahili examr.; dep. dir., educn., Uganda, 1930; dir., educn., Zanzibar, 1939.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1935), M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmnr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmnr.-gen. on several occasions.
FOTHERINGHAM, WILLIAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1905; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; Rl. "Dick" Vety, Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Kenya, 1927; vety. resch. offr., 1932; senr. do., 1939.

FOULGER, HARRY CECIL, M.A. (1928).—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; maj'r., R. Fusiliere; asst. res., Nyasaland Prot., Aug. 1914; att'd., K.A.R., ag. pte. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. consmr., Jan.-Sept., 1935.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; gov'r.'s off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-05; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, HAROLD ROBERT LESLIE, B.Sc (Mass.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; Institute of Technology; served in Great War, 1915-19; ment. in desps., demob. with rank of capt., 1919; ch. engnr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1926; ag. dir., June-Dec., 1931.

FOXLEE, RICHARD WILLIAM, M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1885; ed. Westminster Sch.; served in engnr. dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; P.L.A., 1915-21; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1921; dep. ch. engnr. (civ.), 1928.

FOX-STRANGWAYS, VIVIAN.—B. 1898; ed. Winchester; The Guides, 1917-22; despatches; cadet, Nigeria, 1923; asst. sec., Nigerian secret., 1927; ag. asst. prin., C.O., 1929; ag. prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secret., 1930-31; dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr., Nyasaland, 1933; ag. asst. sec. and ag. clk. of couns., Mar. to Sept., 1938 and Jan.-May, 1939.

FRAMPTON, ALGERNON DEKEWEE, C.D.A.—B. 1904; ed. Christ's Hosp., Oxford Sch. of Economics and at Wye Agr. Coll.; Impl. Coll., Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. offr., dept. agr., Nigeria, 1927; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1928; agrl. offr., N.S. and F.M.S., May, 1935.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1909; ag. inspr. cust. preven. serv., Mar.-Oct., 1911; ag. inspr. maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; prov. collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, CLIFFORD CLAUDE, Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; service in Fiji, 1908-12; cadet W. Pacific serv. (Br. Solomon Is. Prot.), June, 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; dep. comsnr., high comsnr.'s off., Dec., 1914; lieut., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Flanders, 1917-18; dist. offr., Gizo, Br. Solomon Is., Oct., 1919; ag. res. comsnr. of Prot., Oct., 1922 to Mar., 1923; rep. natives of Gizo dist. in claims before lands comsnr., 1923; judl. comsnr. for W. Pacific in New Hebrides and Br. judge, joint off., New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; pol. mag., Nigeria, 1930; puisne judge, Uganda, and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, July, 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug.-Oct., 1934 and July, 1937 to Feb., 1938.

FRANCIS, SIR (CYRIL GERARD) BROCK, KT. Bach. (1939), K.C.—Appd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., for various periods, 1908-10; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., May, 1912; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major, Aug., 1914; ment. by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; coy. comdr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsnr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; regir-gen., regir., sup. ct., comsnr., stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsnr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. ch. just. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; ohmn., comsnr. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; ohmn., retrenchment comte., 1922; ohmn., franchise comsnr., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1934; title changed to ch. just., Feb., 1938; ret., 1939.
FRANCIS, JOSEPH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.

FRANCIS, OLIVER MICHAEL, M.D., C.M. (McGill), L.M.S. (Nova Scotia), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1883; med. offr., 5th dist., St. Lucia, Jan., 1919; govt. M.O., Br. Guiana, 1921; supt., G'town prison, 1932; ag. asst. govt. M.O.H., 1936; ag. govt. M.O.H., 1936; 2nd asst. G.M.O.H., 1937.
FRASER, ALASTAIR/GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hone., phil., polit., and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.R.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; Jescon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, D. M.—B. 1890; Br. post office, 1905; post. clk. and telegraphist, Kenya and Uganda, 1913; postmtr., Tanganyika, 1917; ch. acct., posts and tels., 1919; asst. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda, Tanganyika, 1933; ret., 1934; asst. P.M.G., Trinidad, 1936; dep. P.M.G., 1939; ag. P.M.G., 1939.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford(schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. secr., Aug., 1917; priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. asst. advr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regir., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930; offig. mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1933; offr., cls. I.B., 1934; Malayan estab. offr., Mar., 1936; sec., high comsnr. in addn., Aug., 1936; ag. under-secr. to govt., F.M.S. Jan., 1937; under-secr. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; Fed. sec., F.M.S., 1939.

FRASER, HUGH ARTHUR, B.V.Sc.—B. 1901; ed. Scots Schl., Br. Guiana, Ontario Agric. Coll. and Ontario Vet. Coll., Univ. of Toronto, Canada; gold medallist, Ontario Vet. Coll., 1933; govt. vet. surg., Br. Guiana, 1939.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on mil'y. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attl. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1924; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South, Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931; ag. assist. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; ag. crown solr., Aug., 1936 and Feb., 1937; attd., A.-G.'s dept., Sept., 1937 and Aug., 1937; ag. puisne judge, Sept., 1936; temp. addnl. judge, Oct. 1936; cadet offr., els. I, Deo., 1936; ag. lst. mag., Jan., 1937; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1937 and Feb., 1938; editr. of "Ordnances and Regulations of Hong Kong" (1937 edn.).

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; cadet Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.
FRASER, MALCOLM LIONEL.—B. 1893; col. treasy., Trinidad, 1913-15; asst. supt., Trinidad constab., 1915-21; do., Gold Coast pol., 1921-27; supt., 1927.

FRASER, HON. PETER.—B. 1884; ed. Board Schl. and Workers' Educn. Assn.; mem. of, N.Z. H. of R. since 1918; represented N.Z. at Empire Parly. Confco., London, 1936; min. of educn., min. of health and min. of marine, 1935; prime min., Apr., 1940.
FRASER, THOMAS WILLIAM, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1912; ed. Jeppe High Schl., Johannesburg, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr. (cadet), Basutoland, 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAYLING, BRYAN EDWIN, O.B.E.—B. 1893; ed. Denstone Coll. and R. Schl. of Mines; associate, R. Schl. of Mines; dipl., Imp. Coll. (Mining); associate, Instit. Mining and Metallurgy; el. mem., Geolog. Soc. of S. Africa; inspr., mines, Tanganyika, 1926; senr. inspr., mines, 1927; ch. inspr., mines, Nigeria, 1939.

FRAZER, HON. SIR FRANCIS VERNON, KT. BACH. (1935), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; asst. pub. serv. comsnr., 1920; judge, ct. of arbitn., 1921-35; chmn., dairy industry comsnr., 1934; dep. chmn., exec. comsnr. of agr., 1935.

FREEMAN, BENJAMIN JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp., London, and Horsham; br. P.O., Oct., 1903-Dec., 1913; jnr. postal clk. and telegraphist, Kenya, Jan., 1913; 2nd cls. postmr., Apr., 1915; asst. ch. acctnt., Apr., 1918; 1st cls. postmr., Apr., 1920; dep. ch. acctnt., June, 1924; asst. P.M.G., July, 1931; snr. acctnt., S.S., Aug., 1932; contrlr., P. and T., Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1933; do., S'pore, Feb., 1937; dir. posta., Feb., 1937.

FREEMAN, GEOFFREY JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. privately and in Clongowes Wood Coll., Ireland; served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Royal Munster Fusiliers in the Great War, 1914-21; collr., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; senr. do., Fen., 1932.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls. bns. mod., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; appt. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries comsnr., 1921; act. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; prin., 1st April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. Hond. on duty, 1927; seconded for serv. with Col. Development Advisory Comtee., 1930-32; sec., E. African gov't. confco. and to the high comsnr. for transp., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1936; asst. sec., C.O., 1938.

FREETH, ARTHUR CAUSTON.—B. 1890; ed. Lancing Coll. (exhib.), St. Mary's Hosp. and Coll. of Med., Newcastle-on-Tyne, M.B., Dunelm, certif., London Schl. of Trop. Med.; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-20; med. offr., Grenada, 1921; do., Trinidad, 1921; do., Uganda, 1924; ag. senr. health offr., Uganda, 1929; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1930; tuberculosis offr., 1933; M.O.H. and port health offr., 1938.

FRENCH, LEWIS, I.C.S. (Ret.).—Ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; served as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab, afterwards as dep. comsnr. and joint ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, Palestine, 1931.
FRENCH, MARCUS HARRY, B.A., Ph.D.—B. 1909; ed. Cheltenham Gram. Schl. and St. Catherine's Coll., Camb.; dipl., agr. (Cantab.); chem., Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres., dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; ch. just., Apl., 1914; judge, prize ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FREITZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian admidstrn., B.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrols, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; mem., exec. and leg. councils; chmn., agrl. comsnr., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1927; asst. judge, Zanzibar, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., Sept., 1935-Mar., 1936; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa; chmn., riot enquiry comsnr., Feb., 1936; ag. ch. just., May-Nov., 1938; 1st puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1939.
