FRISHY, ALFRED WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); European mast., Penang, Aug., 1922; headmast., Old Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1929; inspr., schls., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., Malacca, Apr., 1933 and Oct., 1936; senr. educn. offr., Malaya, 1939.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND, O.B.E. (1938).—Served in the Great War; just. of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to comsnr., clk. to leg. bd., sec. to bd. of educn., 1920; sub-inspr., pol., 1922; asst. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., gen. man. of savings bank, registr. of shipping, 1923; ag. ass't. comsnr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. comsnr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931; treas., St. Lucia, 1933; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1933-Feb., 1935; admstr., various occasions, 1935-39.

FUAD, MONTAPPA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schl., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; clk., treasy., Cyprus, 1905; inspr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., sheri. ct. of appeal, 1927.

FULLER, Hon. Sir George Warburton, K.C.M.G. (1919).—M.L.A., Kiama, N.S.W. 1889-94; elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Ap., 1910; M.L.A., Wollondilly, N.S.W., 1915-28; col. sec., N.S.W., Nov., 1916-29; premier, 1922-25; agt.gen., N.S.W., 1928-31.
FULTON, Adam, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1915; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and Suez, France and Italy, 1915-18; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1926.

FURNES, Sir Robert Howard, Kt. Bach. (1929).—B. 1880; ed. King William’s Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regar-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to bar, Lincoln’s Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926; do., Jamaica, 1936.

FURNES-SMITH, Cecil, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John’s Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; asst. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. comsrr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1933; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Mar., 1936.

FURSE, Major R. D., C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis now Lord) Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward’s Horse, K.O. D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apr., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O., gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in despatches, asst. priv. sec. (appointments) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec., (appointments) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited on C.O. business—Canada, 1922, 1923, 1928; Australia, New Zealand, Fiji, Java, Malaya, Ceylon, 1928-29; South, Central and East Africa and the Sudan, 1935-36; mem. of Emp. Forestry Conferences in Canada, 1923; Australia and New Zealand, 1928; South Africa, 1935; mem. of comittee for Forestry of Oxford Univ.; asst. sec., C.O., Oct., 1930.

GAHAN, Edward John Beresford, B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—War serv., R.A.S.C. 1914-19; coal comm., 1919-21; engnr. asst., M.G.W.R. (I), 1923; asst. engr., K.U.R. & H., 1924; sr. asst. engr., 1928; dist. engr., 1931; senr. engr., Nigeria rly., 1938; ag. ch. engr., Jan.-June, 1939.

GALSWORTHY, Arthur Norman.—B. 1916; ed. Emanuel Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; senr. schol. of Corpus Christi Coll., 1936-37; let. cls. mod. languages tripods, pt. I, 1936 and pt. II, 1937; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 5th Oct., 1938.

GALWAY, 8th Viscount (or. 1727) George Vere Abundell Monkton-Arundell, P.C. (1937), G.C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O. (1917), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; Sherwood Rangers Yeomanry, 1900-04; 1st Life Guards, 1904; adjt., 1908; capt., 1911; staff capt., 4th Cavalry Brig., 1914; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., 2nd Cavalry Divn., 1915; A.A. and Q.M.G., 1917; brevet lieut.-col., 1919; commd. Life Guards, 1925-29; col. commd., Hon. Arty. Cov., 1933; gov.-gen. and comdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, 1935.

GALWAY, Lt.-Col. The Right Hon. Sir Henry Lionel, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. commr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. commr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. commr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high commr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (desp., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (desp., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn., as chief political officer, 1901-02 (desp., medal with clasp); gov., St. Helena, 1902; gov., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1911; gov., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; assumed govt., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

GALWAY, John Patrick Francis.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier’s Coll., Liverpool; clk., vety. pathological lab., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, mily. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corps, 1st June, 1916; lieut. ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; “1914-15” Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; labour inspn. offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., Dec., 1922.

GAMBLE, Frederick Charles.—B. 1891; ed. Felsted and Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons. jurisprudence) 1914; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Feb., 1915; transp. offr., N. Frontier dist., 1916; ag. dist. commr., 1918; res. mag., July, 1923; offr. attlr., Mombassa town planning scheme, 1929; judge, high ct., Uganda and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1935.

GAMBLES, Robert Moylan, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1910; ed. Westminster Schl. (King’s schl.) and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (cl. II nat. sci. tripods, pt. I in 1931); R. (Dick) Vety. Coll., Elin. (C.O. scholarship) and Univ. of Edin.; vety. offr., Cyprus, Jan., 1936; ag. ch. vety. offr., June-Oct., 1935 and Apr.-Aug., 1937; vety. resch. offr., Palestine, 1938.

GAME, Air Vice-Marshal Sir Philip Woolcott, G.C.V.O. (1937), G.B.E. (1929), K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1935), C.B. (1919), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse; ent. R.A., 1893; capt., 1901; adjt., 1902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., 3rd and 2nd grade, W.O., 1910-14; served, S. Africa, 1901-02 (deeps., Queen's Med. five claps); European war, 1914-18 (deeps. six times); dir., training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air. mem. for personnel on air cun., 1923-28; ret. list, 1929; gov., New South Wales, 1930-35.
GANADO, Prof. Edgar, LL.D.—B. 1884; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; LL.D., 1910; prof. of law, 1921; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., 1928; judge, 1929; ex. in law, 1930.

GANADO, Roberto F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag., judl. pol., 1918; examr. laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., July to Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cts., June to Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., Sept., 1925.

GANDY, Gerard Horace, Lt.-Cmdr., R.N. (retd.).—B. 1896; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1927; re-apptd., harbr. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1931; land survr., 1934; attched. tech. sec.'s off., Apr., 1939; re-called Royal Navy, Sept., 1939.

GANE, Percival Carleton, M.A. (Oxon.), LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1874; ed. Kingswood Schl., Bath and Jesus Coll., Oxford; admitted advoc., S. Africa, 1903; K.C., 1919; ag. judge many occasions, 1920-34; puisne judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1934.

GARDINER, Edmund Austin, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., A.M.I.S.E., Chd. Civ. and Mech. Eng., King's Coll., Lond. Univ.—B. 1898; war survr., 1916-19; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, Apr., 1924; ex. engrnr., Apr., 1933; ag. sr. engrnr., Dec., 1937.

GARDINER, Hon. James Garfield, B.A., LL.D., (Assiniboia, S.).—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls.; el., Sask. legislature at by-elec., 1914; re-elec., 1917, and at g.e., 1921 by acclm.; min., highways and min. in ch., bureau of lab. and industries, 1922; re-elec. without contest after assuming office at by-elec., 1922; re-elec. g.e., 1925; premier, Sask., 1926; prov. treas., 1926-27; min. of educn., 1928-29; re-elec., g.e., 1929; resigned as premier, 1929; re-elec., g.e., 1934; premier and prov. treas., Sask., 1934; resigned as premier when applt. min., agr. in Rt. Hon. W. L. Mackenzie King's cabinet, 1935; first elec., H. of C., Canada at a by-elec., Jan., 1936.

GARDNER, Harold Mence, O.P.E. (1938).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester and Merton Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.) dipl., forestry; asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Mav., 1914; senr. asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928; nom. M.L.C.

GARDNER, John Hyslop, O.B.E. (1939), B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; asst. engrnr., Basutoland, 1909; govt. engrnr., Swariland, 1919; served with R.E. Flanders, 1916-18; D.P.W., 1938.
GARLAND, Neill, A.R.T.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.N.A.—B. 1899; ed. Alloa Acad., R. Tech. Coll., and Glasgow Univ.; flying offr., R.F.C. & R.A.F., France, 1917-19; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1928.
GARLAND, T. V., B.A., B.A.J. (T.C.D.).—B. 1906; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1930; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1934; dist. engrnr., May, 1938.

GARLICK, George Herbert, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1886; med. offr., Johore, 1917; Malayan med. serv., 1923; ag. prin., med. offr., Johore, 1924, 1927 and 1931; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927; prin. med. offr., 1934; Johore rep. at Far Eastern Cong., trop. med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

GARNER, Joseph John Saville.—B. 1908; ed. Highgate Schl. (senr. foundation schol., 1923-26) and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol. 1926-29); mod. and med. languages tripos Pt. I, cls. 2, div. 1; ditto, Pt. II, cls. 1; B.A., 1930; asst. mod. lang. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1929-30; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1930; ag. prin., 1st July, 1937.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907; seconded for service in Oversea Stltnt. Office, Jan., 1919; promoted asst. prin. under clause 51, 1924; asst. to the rep. in the C'wealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1931.

GARRAN, Sir Robert Randolph, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comte. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901, and partly, draftsman; solr-gen., 1916-32; chmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARROOD, Harry St. Clair.—B. 1882; ed. St. Polycarp's Schl., Farnham; articled architect; Sudan civ. serv., 1906; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1917; capt., 1918; served with Hedjaz Expdy. Force; Br. agt., Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; M.C., 3rd els. Order of Nakhla from King Hussein; ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; gen. headqrs., Egypt to 1928; crown survr. and engrnr., Gibraltar, 1929.

GARSON, Alexander Denis.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hons., hist. tripes; M.A.; cadet, Nigeria, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1933.

GARTON, Harry.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th Aug., 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials offr., R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admiralty, 21st Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Comn. of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr.; C.O., 21st Oct., 1922.

GARVEY, Francis Willcocks.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport offr.; served Transvaal town pol., 1904-08; asst. commr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1909; commr., pol., 1918; recd. thanks of govt. for investigation and suppression of human sacrifices to Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; commr., pol., Lagos, 1922; ag. dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Feb., 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Mar., 1925.

GARVEY, Ronald Herbert, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.A.I.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; asst. sec. W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932; ag. sec. in 1932, 1933, 1934 and 1938; ag. readr., commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. (on secondment), May, 1938-April, 1939.

GATER, BOSSLEY ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; asst. entomologist dept. of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria resch. offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist Inst. Med. Resch., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930; del. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Dec., 1930.

GATER, SIR GEORGE HENRY, Kt. Bach. (1936), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916), J.P.—B. 1886; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. hona., mod. hist., dipl. in educ.) ; Oxfordshire educn. comtee., 1911-12; asst. dir., educn., Nottinghamshire, 1912-14; served European War, Gallipoli, Egypt, France (desig. four times, twice wounded, D.S.O. and bar), 1914-18; dir., educn., Lancashire county coun., 1919-24; educn. offr., L.C.C., 1924-33; clk. of the council, 1933-39; joint sec., miny. of home security, Sept., 1939; perm. under-sec. of state for the coll., 1st Feb., 1940.

GATES, CHARLES EDWARD, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1889; ed. Uppingham Schol. and Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambr.; European mast., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; headmast., High Schol., Kajang, June, 1929; ag. headmast., Vict. Inst., June, 1937.
GAULD, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1886; G.N. of Scotland Rly., 1900-13; R.E., 1915-19; traffic inspr., K.U.R. and H., 1919; dist. traffic supt., cls. II., 1928; do., cls. I., 1936.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; apptd. asst. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec., 1928.

GEAR, HARRY SUTHERLAND.—B. 1903; B.Sc. (1st cls. hona.) 1926; M.B., B.Ch. Witwatersrand Univ., S. Afr., 1928; D.P.H., Univ. London, 1931; D.T.M. & H., R.C.P.S. Eng., 1932; med. offr., N. Rhodesia med. serv., Aug., 1929; asst. patho., S. Afr. Inst. Med. Res., Mar., 1930; S. Afr. govt. post grad. schol. in pub. health, London Sch. of Hy. and Trop. Med., Sept., 1930; hd., dept. of prev. med. and med. statistics, Henry Lester Inst., Shanghai, July, 1932; asst. health offr., Union of S. Afr., May, 1935; dep. ch. health offr., Union, 1939; published several articles on anthropology and pub. health in various med. jnl.s.

GEBERT, SYLVESTRE.—B. 1893; ed. Durban High Schol., Royal College, Mauritius and Schol. of Agr., Mauritius; dip. agr., 1st class hons. (Maur.) 1917; scientific asst., div. of biology, dept. of agr., Mauritius, Aug., 1917; lect., zoology and entomology, do., Sept., 1917; extra asst., Bact. Inst. Mauritius, July, 1921; asst. to dir., C.O. anti-malarial survey, Mauritius, 1922-23; scientific asst., Bact. Inst., Mauritius, July, 1923; asst., entomologist, sp., malaria serv., Feb., 1927; asst. entomologist, hookworm and malaria bch., July, 1932; entomologist, med. and health dept., 1934; author of various papers on economic and medical entomology.
GEDDES, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1880; under sec. of just., N.S.W., Jan., 1933; pub. trustee, Jan., 1936.

GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. asst. to Irrgan. engrnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUIJS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Unive. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature study and agric. science, Grey Coll. Schol., 1918; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agr. economics and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under-sec. for agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; comm. coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.

GENEVE, FREDERICK PIERRE, K.C. (1929).—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1922; substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag.-ch. just., Mar., 1931.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, O.B.E. (1935), D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1898; ed. King's Schol., Canterbury and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1914-19; asst. prin., C.O., 1920; sec., rubber restriction comtee., 1922; asst. prin., 1923; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec., 1924; prin., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. confe., 1927; sec., col. services comtee., 1929; seconded to India Office as a sec. of Indian Round Table Confe., 1930; accompd. Permt. Under-Sec. of State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1939.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.R. (1932).—B. 1860; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk., gov.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road survr., Grenada, 1905; ag. asupt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; ast. asupt., pub. wks., 1916, 1918; sup., pub. wks., 1922.

GEORGE, HENRY BERNARD.—B. 1880; clk., Imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; clk., P.W.D., 1903; clk., C. and A.G.'s office, 1905; clk., audit office, Cape admnstr., 1912; served, Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.F.; offr. in ch., local govt. audit, 1920; prov. audr., Natal Prov., 1932; prov. audr., Transvaal Prov., 1933; author of "Standard Electricity Accounts in S.A."
GEORGE, NANCY MARGARET FANNY.—B. 1900; ed. St. Catherine's High Schol., Malta, Devonport Secondary Schol., Froebel Training Coll., Bedford; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

GEORGI, MAJOR CHARLES DENIS VICTOR, O.B.E. (Mil.), B.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., Dulwich Coll.); Univ. of Lond., Univ. Coll.—B. 1890; ast. agrl. chem., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; senr. chem., agrl. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1936.

GERAHTY, SIR CHARLES CYRIL, KT. BACH., K.C. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; maj. (ret.), 3rd (Res.) Batt. E. Lancs. Rgt.; ast. res. and comantr., prov. ents., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; war serv. in France, Belgium and W. Africa, 1914-18; restored to civil estabnt., N. Provs., Nigeria, Nov., 1918; legal asst., lands directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1920-26; ag. atty.-gen. and puisne judge on various occasions; jointcompiler, 2nd vol., stat. law of Cyprus, 1923; atty.-gen., Dec., 1926; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1929; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Apr., 1932; puisne judge, S'pore, July, 1933; legal adviser to gov., Malta, 1935; legal sec., 1936; ch. just., Trinidad, Aug., 1937.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.), B.A. 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1929; supt., marine and cust., chandu, posts and tele., audr. and pub. pros., Kelantan, Jan., 1931; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1933; regar., titles, Selangor in addn., July, 1933; offr., cls. II., Oct., 1934; dist. offr., Seremban, Feb., 1936; offr., cls. IB, 1938.
GERMOND, ROBERT CHARLES, M.D. (Laus.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1897; med. offr., Botswalo leper settlmt., Basutoland, 1933; med. offr., 1939.
GERRAND, JOHN KENNEDY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1899; asst. engr., Basutoland, 1929; ag. D.P.W., 1937; D.P.W., 1938.

GERRARD, FERGUS ST. MAUR, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Belvedere Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Dublin; sub-instr., Br. Guiana, 1927; dist. instr., 1936; cls. I. clg., col. secret., 1937; asst. dist. comnr., 1938.

GETLEY, ALFRED CURBHEY.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schl., Eastbourne; acctnt. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paym.-lieut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit dept., Apr., 1928; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions; asst. audr., Kenya, 1929; asst. audr., col. audit dept., 1933; govt. storekeeper, Oct., 1937.

GIBBON, GEOFFREY MCKAY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1896; ed. Elgin acad., Gordon Coll., Aberdeen and Univ. of Edin.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Oct., 1933; ag. pathologist, Apr.-Sept., 1936; ag. special assistant (surg.) in 1936 and 1937; med. offr., Uganda, 1938.
GIBSON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GIBBS, GEORGE HOWARD.—B. 1889; asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920; dist. comnr., 1925.

GIBSON, LESLIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schl., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S., attd. Grand Fleet; lieut., R.A.F.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; attd., atty-gen.'s office, S'pore, Apr., 1920; attd., food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir. food prodn., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. regar. and asst. offr. assignee, Penang, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1925; asst. regar., sup. ct., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Aug., 1932; attd. M.L.O., Mar., 1934; ag. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, May, 1934; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., Nov., 1936; offr., cls. II., Mar., 1937; crown couns., Jan., 1937; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., May, 1939.

GIBSON, ROY ALEXANDER.—B. 1885; ed. Brandon High Schl. and Brandon Coll.; ent. fed. govt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transf. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; asst. dep. min., June, 1919; comnr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923; dir. lands, parks and forests, dept. mines and resources, 1936.

GIE, S. F. N., D.Phil.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Amsterdam and Berlin Univ.; teacher and instr. schls., Cape Prov.; prof., hist. and chmn., senate, Stellenbosch Univ., 1918; under-sec., educn., Union of S. Africa, 1926; sec., ditto, 1927; min., plenipotentiary, Berlin, Oct., 1934; do., Stockholm, Sept., 1939; publications include a history of S. Africa in Afrikaans in two vols.

GILBERT, HUMPHREY RALEIGH.—B. 1899; ent. R. Navy, 1912; served, Great War 1914-18; ret., Jan., 1923; lieut. commodr. (retd.), Feb., 1928; admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1927; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1933; dist. offr., 1939.

GILBERT, JOSEPH THOMAS, O.B.E. (1883), B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. Bedford and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1914; asst. collr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1912; 2nd asst. sec. and prte. sec. to res., June, 1915; senr. asst. sec., Sept., 1924; asst. ch. sec., Jan., 1928; del. C.O. confee., 1930; ag. ch. sec. for various periods between 1924 and 1932; Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. IV., 1919; cls. III., 1930; ret., 1934; atty.-gen., Bermuda, Jan., 1938.

GILBERT, SAMUEL MOORE, B.Sc. (Lond.), N.D.A. Dip. Agr. (Wye).—B. 1892; ed. City of Norwich Schl., Wye Coll. and London Univ.; command, The 1st King's Regt.; served in War, 1914-18; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1929; ag. dir., 1929-30; M.L.C., 1933; ch. scientific offr., coffee research and experimental station, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1934.
GILBERT, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.R.C.V.S.—Stock dept., N.S.W., 1913; lect., vetv. schl., Sydney Univ., 1914 and 1920; mil. serv., 1914-19; senr. vetv. offr., Palestine, July, 1920; vetv. pathologist, 1934.
GILBERT, THOMAS TRAVERS, M.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Mountjoy Schl., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; exec. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, Aug., 1928; prin., govt. tech. schl., Gold Coast, Sept., 1938.
GILCHRIST, ARTHUR GEORGE.—B. 1870; pres., State superannuation bd., N.S.W., 1933.
GILCHRIST, KENNETH JAMES, M.B., B.S. (Lond.) 1933, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S.—B. 1910; med. offr., Gibraltar, 1936.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunswick House private schl., B. Guiana; entd. H.M. Customs, B. Guiana, Dec., 1899; copyist, regar.'s office, Oct., 1900; 6th cls. clk., ditto, Mar., 1902; 5th cls. clk., cust., 1903; asst. commissary, Sept., 1905; clk. to atty.-gen., May, 1906; stip. mag., J.P., coroner, and comnr. of oaths, 1910; chmn., Rose Hall village partition of land comnr., 1911; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1914; ag. asst. govt. sec., Mar. to Sep., 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1914-20; read. thanks of S. of S.; practised at English bar, 1920-21; comnr. to enquire into disturbance, H.M. penal attlt., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. junr. puisne judge, in 1923 and 1925-6; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1926; ag. puisne judge in 1925, 1926 and 1927; puisne judge, Sept., 1927; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, 1931; ag. ch. just., 1932-3, 1935, 1937 and 1939.

GILKES, HUMPHREY ARTHUR, M.C., M.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Oxon), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Lon.).—B. 1895; med. offr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1925; ag. dir., med. and sanv. services, Oct., Dec., 1930; dep. dir., med. services, Trinidad, June, 1936; ag. D.M.S., June-Nov., 1937.
GILL, EDWIN—B. 1896; on naval serv., 1915-19; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926.

GILL, EDWIN LEONARD, D.Sc.—B. 1877; asst., Manchester Museum, June, 1900; curator, Hancock Museum, Newcastle-on-Tyne, July, 1901; asst. R. Scottish Museum, July, 1922; dir., S. African Museum, Cape Town, Jan., 1925.
GILL, JOHN AUSTIN, M.B., Ch.B. (Belf.).—B. 1898; med. offr., Basutoland, 1928.

GILL, RONALD GODDARD.—B. 1896; ed. Haberdashers' Askes Schol. (Hampstead); on mily. serv. overseas, 1917-19; commissioned, Nov., 1917; temp. cik., Miny. of Lab., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., Mar., 1923.

GILLESPIE, JAMES DAVID, B.Sc.—B. 1905; ed. Musselburgh Gram. Schol., George Watson's Boys' Schol. and Edin. Univ.; three years land reclamation and cotton growing Nile Delta; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1930; agrl. offr., Sierra Leone, 1938.

GILLIAM, WILLIAM.—B. 1896; Imp. G.P.O., June, 1911; mily. serv., 1915-19; supt., post surv., S.S., June, 1921; per. asst. to dir. gen., P. and T., Malaya, Sept., 1934; ag. contr., Selangor and Pahang, Aug., 1936; contr., posts, S.S., 1938.

GILLIMAN, CLMENT, C.B.E. (1937), F.G.S., M.S.A.S.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1882; ed. Swiss Federal tech. high schol., Zurich; on rly. constrn. and surveys in E. Africa, 1905-14; served E.A.P. Force, 1916-19; capt., rly. corps; exec. engrn., E. African mily. rly. (occupied territory), 1916-18; ch. engrn., ditto, 1918-19; dist. engrn., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1919; senr. dist. engrn., Apr., 1925; ch. engrn., Apr., 1928; ag. gen. man., Apr. 1935 to May, 1936.

GILMOUR, ANDREW.—B. 1898; ed. Royal High Schol., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; served with 14th A. & S.H., B.E.F., 1916-17; M.A. hons. (Edin.), 1920; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attch. to res. councillors' office, Penang, May, 1921; offr., cls. V, asst. contrlr., lab., Penang and asst. prot., lab., Kedah, Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Malacca, Feb., 1926; collr., land rev., S'pore, Jan., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; res., Labuan, June, 1928; dist. offr., Jasin, Sept., 1929; ditto, U. Kelantan, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B), S.S., Jan., 1937; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1938.

GILMOUR, GORDON JOHN.—B. 1893; ed. Fremantle schol. (W.A.); supvr. govt. monops., S.S., Feb., 1911; war serv., Jan., 1915-Mar., 1919; supvr., tob. dep., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1919; supvr., grade I, Jan., 1924; contr., Feb., 1930; asst. supt., S'pore, Nov., 1931; do., Penang, June, 1933; in addn., dep. supt., rubber control, Penang Is., June, 1934; dep. comsrr., preventive br., S'pore, dep. supt., rubber control in adln., Oct., 1935; dep. comsrr., Negri Semb., Apr., 1938; title changed to asst. comptlr., cust., Oct., 1938.

GIMSON, FRANKLIN CHARLES, R.A. Oxon.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; on mil. duty, 1918; addnl. asst. col. sec., July, 1919; office asst. Anuradhapura kach., and pol. mag., Sept., 1919; addnl. asst. col. sec., Mar., 1920; 4th asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. collr., cust., Mar., 1928; addnl. asst. dir., educn., Mar., 1929; sec. to min., educn., July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Feb., 1932; do., Kegalle, Dec., 1933; on ap. duty, gen. elec. of mems., state coun., Aug., 1935; chmn., munic. coun., Kandy, June, 1936; ag. contrlr., lab., Jly., 1937; offr. cls. I., Nov., 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1938; contrlr., lab., Oct., 1938.
GITTENS, DARNLEY ERROLL WYNDHAM.—B. 1890; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1913; income tax comsrr., 1931; col. treas., 1935.

GITTINS, JOHN WANSBROUGH, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Blundell's and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1928; resigned (ill-health), 1929; cadet, Fiji, 1930; passed cadet, 1932; dist. comsrr., 1933; elk., to couns., 1933; asst. sec., 1937; re-designated admstve. offr. gde. II, 1938; dist. comsrr., N. dist. 1939.
GLASHAN, H. W.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1907; A.M.O., Pietermaritzburg ment. hosp., 1909; asupt., Fort Beaufort ment. hosp., 1921; asupt., Pietermaritzburg ment. hosp., 1931.

GLEN, JAMES ALEXANDER, M.A.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. dir. of edn., 'Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. adviser, Min. of educn., Apr., 1922.

GLENDAY, VINCENT G., C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1929), M.A. Oxon. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry.—Aast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comsrr., N. Frontier, Jan., 1914 to June, 1919; offr. in charge, Somali disarmament, N. Frontier dist., Mar., 1919 (A.G.S. Med.); seconded as dist. comsrr., Br. Somaliland, Mar.-Sept., 1920; ast. dist. comsrr., Oct., 1922; ag. dist. comsrr., Sept., 1922; dist. comsrr., 1926; Abyssinian mission, 1927-28; ag. prov. comsrr., 1930-31, and from Mar., 1933; senr. dist. comsrr., 1934; prov. comsrr., 1935; gov. and comd-r-in-ch., Somaliland, 1939.

GODBOLT, S. B.—B. 1884; elk., treasy., Cape 1901; Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. elk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; ch. elk., grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; comsrr., pensions, July, 1932.
GODDARD, HARRY FREDERICK.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.
GODDARD, MAJOR THEODORE ALLAN.—B. 1883; inspr., pol., Barbados, 1922; ast. supt., fire brig. (conjoint), 1923; dep. comsrr., pol., 1936.

GODFREY, SIR JOSEPH EP., KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1858; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regisr. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., regisr.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regisr.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of poor law comsrs., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and Bahamas to Internat. Leprosy Confere., Bergen, 1909.

GODLEY, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Dublin Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1895; major, 1897; colonel, 1905; apptd. comdt. New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gazetted major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1916; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and comd-r.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officier (Belgian) 1917; Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Croix de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.

GODSALL, WALTER DOUGLAS, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. N'Eliva kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Mar., 1926; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Dec., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Mar., 1929; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Jan., 1930; do., Galle, May, 1934; temp. attd. C.O., Sept., 1933 to Feb., 1934; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Matara, in addn., Oct., 1934; astt. contr., estabe., Mar., 1935; contr., estabe. in 1935 and 1937.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, ROBERT ANNESLEY, F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; employed on rly. and land surveys, Canada, 1910-14; served with line and field survey units, France, 1916-19; ment. in desps.; rly. survey, Persia, 1920-21; astt. survr., N. Rhodesia, 1922; dir., roads, 1927; dep. dir., surveys, Tanganyika, 1929; dir., land registr. and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1933; Br. del. of Evkaf, Mar., 1935.

GOEPPEL, JOHN, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1906; ed. Chigwell Schl. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji, 1938; clk., couns., 1930; dist. commr., 1932; rep. Fiji at Internat. Anthropological Congress, Lond., 1934; astt. sec., secretariat, 1935-37; seconded as polit. offr., Aden Prot., 1939; astt. polit. sec., 1939; author, "A Text Book of Fijian."

GOLDBURG, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; entd. crown agents' office, 1902; astt. head, gen. dept., 1919; astt. head, finance dept., 1922; astt. head, shipping dept., 1925; astt. head, finance dept., 1930; dep. head, finance dept., May, 1933; accnt., W. African Currency bd., 1912-33.
GOLDSCHMIDT, LT.-COL. G. T., D.L.I.—Offr., cmdg., troops and Mauritius territorial force, Nov., 1937.

GOLDSWAIN, CLEMENT TYSON.—Ed. Pub. Schl., Grahamstown; clk., postal dept., Dec., 1896; clk., Prime Minister's office, Cape town, 1902; clk., mag's. office, Piensa, July, 1906; De Aar, 1908; Dortrecht, 1911; Mossel Bay, 1918; astt. mag., Malmsbury, 1920; mag.; Durbanville, 1920; Bellville, 1926; Robertson, 1929; Lydenburg, 1931; Standerton, 1933, Malmesbury, 1934; Kimberley, 1937.

GOLLAN, SIR HENRY COPPER, KT. BACH. (1921), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, commr. and commt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., Jan.-Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admnstr., June-Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1900-Apr., 1901; ch. just., Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, 1904; compiled pros. of N. Nigeria, 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Cts. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, Dec., 1905; chmn. of produce comm., 1905 and of aliens comm., 1906; chmn. of bd. of educn., Bermuda, July, 1907; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1911; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1918; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1924; chmn., salaries comm., 1928-29; ret., Aug., 1930.

GOMES, STANLEY EUGENE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Jesus Coll., (Camb.); called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; ag. S.M., Br. Guiana, 1927 and 1928; S.M., 1929; ag. astt. atty. gen., 1929-33; astt. atty. gen., 1933; ag. atty. gen., various periods 1934-1939.
GONTHER, GEORGES, C.A., L.I.A.—B. 1869; ed. Commissioner's Schl., Montreal and priv. tuition; audr.-gen., Dominion of Canada, Jan., 1924.

GOOCH, STANLEY JOHN WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1889; astt. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1912; exec. engnr., Johore, May, 1922; sr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., June, 1927; sr. exec. engnr., hydraulics, Selangor, Mar., 1929; res. engnr., civ. aerodrome, S'pore, Jan., 1933; senr. exec. engnr., Kinta, Oct., 1933; state engnr., grade I, Aug., 1935; do., Perak, Nov., 1936; dep., D.P.W., S.S., 1938; D.P.W., Nigeria, 1939.
GOOD, CECIL ALBERT.—B. 1892; called to bar, 1913; served in Army, lieut. and capt., 1915-20; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1921.
GOOD, EDWIN DULLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Auckland gram. schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1904; advisory offr., 1925; astt. comptr., cust., 1930; comptr. cust., 1935.

GOODMAN, ARTHUR MITCHELL.—B. 1886; ed. New Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; supernumerary mag., Ipoh, June, 1915; pres., rent assessment bd. for Penang, July, 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supernum. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. II; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., S.S., Nov., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. IA, mem., exec. coun., S.S., July, 1931; res. coun., Penang, Apr., 1933.

GOODMAN, REGINALD ERNEST.—B. 1886; olk., Basutoland, 1913; accntg. clk., 1916; fin. sec., mast. and registr., deeds, Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1927; audr., Basutoland, Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland, 1935.

GOODMAN, TRAFFORD FLAVIUS.—B. 1900; ed. Lodge Schl., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1926; pol. mag., dist. "A," Barbados, 1927; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., and judge, astt. ct. of appl., various periods, 1928-1930; pol. mag., dist. "C," 1930; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Sept., 1937; ag. ch. registr., sup. ot., 1938; ag. puisne judge, March-July, 1939; ag. ch. registr., sup. ot. on various occasions; ch. registr., sup. ot., 1939.

GOODWIN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR THOMAS HERBERT JOHN CHAPMAN, K.C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1932), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1898), hon. D.Sc. (Oxon), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1871; ed. Newton Coll., Devon and St. Mary's Hosp., London; comand., Army Med. Serv., 1893; staff surg., Mohmand Field Force, N.W. Frontier, 1897 (desps., med. and clasp); European War, 1914-18 (3 times ment. in desps., Mons Star, 1914, Allied and Victory meda.); A.D.M.S. with Mr. Balfour's mission to U.S.A., 1917; dir.-gen., Army Med. Serv., 1918-23; retired, 1923; comdr., Legion of Honour; comdr., Belgian Order of Leopold; comdr., Italian Order of the Crown; Belgian Croix de Guerre; American D.S.M., Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; gov., Queensland, 1927-32.

GOONETILLEKE, OLIVER ERNEST, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1892; astt. audr., rly., Ceylon, Aug., 1921; astt. col. audr., Feb., 1925; col. audr., June, 1931; audr.-gen., July, 1931; Ceylon govt. del. to Internat. rly. cong., Cairo, Jan., 1933.
GOONE WARDENA, HECTOR CLARANCE, M.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1912; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1937.

GOPILL, WALTER LEONARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ed. Alley's Schl., Dulwich, King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; R.F.C. (2nd lieut.), 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918-20; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1926; do., Nyasaland, 1938.
GORDON, HON. SIR ALEXANDER, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B. 1858; judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 1910-28; ret., 1929.

GORDON, CHARLES AUGUSTINE.—B. 1882; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., St. Edmund's Coll., Herts. and Edinburgh Univ. (faculty of law); writer to the signet (W.S.); war serv., France and Flanders, 1915-19 (The Royal Scots); astt. admstr. gen., Zanzibar, 1926; offl. admstr. and pub. trustee, Nigeria, 1929.
GORDON, SIR DAVID JOHN, Kt. Bach. (1926).—B. 1885; M.L.C. of S. Australia since 1913; pres., leg. coun. since July, 1932.

GORDON, GUY MONTGOMERY, B.A., B.A.I., T.C.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1885; mily. serv., R.E. (S.R.), Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-20 (ment. in desps.); asst. dist. wks. offr., Iraq, 1920; dist. wks. offr., 1921; ag. dep. dir., wks., Baghdad, Aug.-Sept., 1921; do., Basrah, Oct., 1921 to Jan., 1922; dir., wks., Bahamas, 1923; asst. D.P.W., Trinidad, May, 1928; D.D.P.W., May, 1937.
GORDON, WILLIAM ANTHONY.—B. 1910; ed. Sedbergh Schl. and Oxford Univ.; B.A. (forestry); asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, May, 1935; do., Cyprus, 1938; seconded as asst. commr., Sept., 1939.

GORDON-HALL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; lieut., M.V.I., Mar., 1923; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, May, 1926; offr., cls. III., May, 1931; dist. offr., Krian, May, 1932; ag. sec., res., Selangor, July, 1933; offr., cls. II, May, 1936; ag. legal advr. and judl. commr., Kelantan, Aug., 1936.
GORDON-SMITH, F.—see Smith, Frederick Gordon.

GORING, GEORGE INGHAM, A.R.T.C. (Glasgow), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Allan Glen Schl.; asst. mech. engrnr., col. transport dept., Br. Guiana; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., transport and harbrs. dept., Nov., 1932-Aug., 1933; ch. mech. engrnr., Sept., 1933

GORMLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1904; ed. Dover County Schl. and Reading Univ.; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; do., Somaliland Prot., May, 1936; dist. offr., May, 1938.

GORSUCH, LESLIE HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Ipswich Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A. (1920); war serv., May, 1916-Jan., 1919; lieut., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1920; attchd. to dist. office, Pekan; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1923; 4th asst. sec. to gvt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1932; asst. sec. to gvt., F.M.S., Feb., 1933; collr., estate duty, S.S., Nov., 1936; ag. 1st asst. estab. offr., July, 1937; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1937; seconded to C.O. as temp. prin., Oct., 1939.

GOSDEN, MINNIE, M.B., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—War serv. as V.A.D. nurse, 1914-17; chem. pathology asst., Hale chem. lab., London hosp., 1925; pathology asst., 1926; asst., bacteriological dept., 1927; asst. med. offr., Lingfield Epileptic Colony, 1928; bacteriologist, Cyprus, Feb., 1929; pathologist, Trinidad, Mar., 1936.

GOSLING, WILLIAM RICHARD, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Sutton Valence and Cambridge Univ.; served in France, the Balkans and Turkey, 1915-1919; demob. with rank of major, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, May, 1920.

GOSS, PHILIP HENRY, N.Z.R.S., M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1895; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ.; survey. dept., N.Z., 1913-20; surv. on agr., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; asst. sept., Sept., 1927; ag. asst. surv. gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; ag. supt., Kelantan, Oct., 1937.

GOSTELOW, CRICIL F.I.A. (Lond.).—B. 1891; ed. Fort St. Model High Schl., Sydney, N.S.W.; actuary, N.Z. nat. provident fund, 1921; asst. actuary, N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1922; actuary, govt. life insurance and friendly societies dept., 1923; also sec., govt. life insurance, 1925; govt. actuary, 1926; regsr., friendly socs., and supt., nat. prov. fund, Feb., 1936; govt. actuary, P.S. bd., N.S.W., and actuary State superann. bd., Oct., 1936; also actuary, regsr., friendly socs., Oct., 1937.
GOTT, JOHN BURY.—B. 1890; ed. Landing Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1922.

GOULD, LIEUT.-COL. HON. SIR ALBERT JOHN, K.T. BACH. (1908).—B. 1847; solr.; M.L.A., N. of Wales, 1881-98; M.L.C., 1899-1901; min. for just. in Reid ministry, 1904-8; returned to the first senate of Commonwealth parlmt., 1901; pres. of the senate, Feb., 1907, to June, 1910; ret. from politics.

GOULD, TAVOR JACK.—B. 1906; ed. Auckland Gram. schl. and N.Z. Univ.; LL.B. (N.Z.), LL.M. (N.Z.); barrister and solr., sup. ct. N.Z., 1923; mem., N.Z. Soc. of Acctnts., 1933; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1934; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong; ag. asst. land offr. and dep. regsr., marriages, 1938; dep. regsr. and marshal, sup. ct., in prize, in addn., 1939.

GOURLAY, WILLIAM NEWLANDS.—B.1894; cadet, S.S., May, 1920; offr., cls. V., Feb., 1922; asst. dist. judge, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Feb., 1926; 3rd mag. Singapore, Jan., 1929; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1931; ag. ast. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Nov., 1931; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1933; offr., cls. II., Feb., 1936; ag. chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1937.

GOWERS, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), M.A., Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1898 (1st cls. class. tripos); entd. service of B.S.A. Co., Jan., 1899; asst. native commr., Matabeleland 1900; resigned, 1902; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1902; 2nd cls. res., 1906; 1st cls. res., 1910; ag. chief sec., Sept., 1912; lieut.-gov., N. Nigeria, June, 1921; gov. of Uganda, 1925-32; senr. crown agt. for the cols., 1st July, 1932; ret., 1938.

GOWING, DUDLEY SAYERS (Capt. R. of O.T.F.).—2nd lt., 6th Bn. Essex Reg. (T.), May, 1915; it., 32nd Sikhs, I.A., 1916, served India, Palestine, Syria and Egypt, 1917-20; 84th Bde., R.A. (T.F.), 1920-24; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Nov., 1924; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast Rlys. & Harb., 1928 and 1932; commr., pol., Jan., 1933; asst. ch. of pol., Gibraltar, Nov., 1933; commr., pol., June, 1937.

GOWRIE, 1st Baron (Cr. 1935), Brig.-Gen. the Rt. Hon. SIR ALEXANDER GORE ARKWRIGHT MORE-RUTHVEN, P.C. (1937), V.C. (1899), G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1928), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Kt.; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Infy., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1898; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent operations. (V.C., 4th cls. Osmanisch, English and Egyptian medls. with clasp, three times ment. in desps.); sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroy of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov. gen., C'wealth of Australia, 1908; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1914-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., ment. in desps. five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brig. (Guards); 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928; gov., N.S.W., 1935; gov.-gen. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1936.

GOWTHORPE, RICHARD COOPER, B.So. (Hons.), A.M.I.C.E., 1925.—B. 1900; war serv., S.A.I., 1917-19; pupil engr., S.A.R., 1922; asst. engr., 1924; K.U.R. & H., asst. engr., 1925; sr. asst. engr., 1933; dist. engr., 1938.
GRAHAM, ALFRED, M.I.Mar.E., A.M.I.N.A.—B. 1896; ed. Falkirk High Sch.; war serv., 1915-19; temp. survr., ships, S.S., Mar., 1924; survr., ships, Mar., 1926; ag. survr.-gen., ships, Mar., 1937.

GRAHAM, Rt. Hon. GEORGE PERRY, P.C. (1925). LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schs., Iroquois and Morrisburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg Herald, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder, elected to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898; 1902 and 1905; provc. sec. of Ontario, 1904; resig. with the govt., 1905; leader of the opposition in the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the privy coun. for Canada and min. of rlyws. and canals, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brockville, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g.e., 1911; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did not seek re-election at gen.-elec., 1917; re-elec., g.e., Dec., 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of naval service in King admn., Dec., 1921; min. of rlyws. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.
GRAHAM, PAUL GEORGE, LL.B. (Edin.). M.A. (St. Andrews).—B. 1887; advoc. (Edinburgh), 1914; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1933.
GRAHAM, REGINALD MORAY.—B. 1898; ed. St. Andrews Coll., S. Africa and Keble Coll., Oxford, dipl. forestry; Indian Army, 1918-22; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Mar., 1925.

GRAHAM, RICHARD, F.L.A.A., F.R.Econ. S.—B. 1896; U.K. post office, 1911-20; sup't., P.O., S'pore, Dec., 1920; acctnt., Penang, 1923; sup't., mails, Penang Apr., 1925; senr. supt., mails, S'pore, June, 1927; acctnt., P. & T., F.M.S., Sept., 1928; ag. senr. acctnt. various occasions 1929-32; acct.-gen., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1935; title changed to ch. acct., Aug., 1937.

GRAHAM, SIR THOMAS LYNEDOCH, KT. B.A.C.H. (1920), K.C.—B. 1860; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Camb. (B.A.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1885; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope; M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min., June to Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Districts local div., 1913-37; chmn., pub. serv. inquiry comsn., June, 1918; title changed to ch. acct., 1937.

GRANNUM, SIR EDWARD ALLAN, KT. B.A.C.H. (1926), C.M.G., 1915.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. regis.'s off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1886, to Nov., 1887; clk. master-in-chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898; asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to Mar., 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor-gen., Mauritius, Apr., 1909; recvr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1919-20 and 1922-23; col. sec., Aug., 1923; admstd. govt. on various occasions, 1924-30; ret., 1931.
GRANNUM, FRANK NEVILLE, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Edin. Univ.; pol. med. offr., dist. "F," Barbados, 1931; sanitation offr., 1936.
GRANT, DONALD KEITH SHAPTEBBURY, O.B.E. (1939).—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Dec., 1912; conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

GRANT, EZEKIEL AUGUSTUS.—Ent. British Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., as copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd ols. clk. (asst. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd ols. clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv. with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st ols. clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. elk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag. asst. inspr., schls. during 1921 to 1923; asst. inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist. comanr., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag. dist. comanr., Belize and offl. admtr. and offl. recr. from Apr., 1931; dist. comanr., N. dist., 1938.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Sch., Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls. V, dist. offr., N. Tobal, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; asst. advr., K. Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1932; asst. advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A), S.S., Feb., 1935; pres. town bd., J. Bahru, Feb., 1937; offr. cls. II, Nov., 1937.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HERDER, M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1929; sec. retrenchment comanr., July, 1930; asst. to P.M.G. in addn., July, 1930; dist. offr., South, in addn., Dec., 1930; extra asst. col. sec., and sec. to harbr. advisory comtee., 1931; 1st pol. mag. in addn., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1933; Imp. Def. Coll., 1934; extra asst. col. sec., Apr., 1936; col. sec., Bermuda, 1935; ag. gov. in 1936 and 1937; col. sec., Jamaica, 1938.

GRANTHAM, WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1898; tempy. asst. engr., P.W.D., Perak, F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst. drnge. and irrign. engr., Jan., 1932; drnge. and irrign. engr., Krian, Feb., 1933; sr. drnge. and irrign. engr., Malay States, May, 1936; ag. asst. advr., drnge. and irrign., Malay States, July, 1936.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Sch.; war serv., 1917-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. exec. engr., May-Dec., 1933.

GRAY, CHAS. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler. asst., offr. recr.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th ols. offr., col. sec.'s office, 1921; 6th cls. offr., 1924; 4th cls. offr., 1926; sec. to prisons comtee., 1928; 2nd cls. clk., 1933; ag. prin. clk. and clk. exec. and leg. couns. in 1932 and 1933; cls. I. clk., 1934; sec. floods investigation comtee., 1934; ag. pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., 1934 and 1935; asst. dist. comanr., 1936.

GRAY, GEOFFREY LEICESTER.—B. 1905; cadet, N. Borneo, Jan., 1925; asst. prot., lab., May, 1925; ag. dep. prot., lab. and asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Aug., 1932; dist. offr., Jan., 1934; under-sec., July,

GRAY, GEORGE BRIAN, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1913; ed. Reading Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr. (cadet) Basuto-land, 1937; asst. dist. commr., 1938; regt., res. commr.'s ct., 1938-39; seconded to high commr.'s office as ast. sec., 1939.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M., Rot. Hosp. (Dub.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr. surgn., class III, Oct., 1913; ch. med. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1919; ch. med. offr., Singapore, Nov., 1920; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S.S., and tempy. M.L.C., for various periods, 1923-31; state surgn., Kedah, Oct., 1931; senr. med. offr., St. Helena, 1936.

GRAY, JOHN MACFARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (I), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (Univ. Dub.).—B. 1898; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1933; med. offr., Uganda, 1925-33; ag. M.O.H., 1934; ag. venereal diseases offr., 1935; H.O. urban coun., 1936; surgeon sub-it., R.N.V.R., 1917-18.

GRAY, JOHN MILNER.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl. and King's Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; admitted solr., sup. ct., 1923; called to bar Gray's Inn, Apr., 1932; war serv. with R.E.F., France (twice wounded); asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 1920; mag., 1924; ag. solr.-gen., 1929; ag. puisne judge, 1933; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1934; O.A.G., May, 1935.

GRAY, R.—B. 1890; clk., treasy., 1902; clk., rev. dept., 1904; dep. recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; clk., rev. dept., Jo'burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin. clk., 1921; survr., 1928; acctnt., 1929; rer., rev. E. London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933; do., Cape Town, June, 1938.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, C.M.G. (1938). M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), M.P.C.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. med. offr., 1911; med. supt., Nelson (N.Z.) mental hosp., 1920; med. supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep. inspr.-gen. mental hosps., N.Z., 1925; dir.-gen., 1927.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ. of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cls. hon., Gladstone Prize and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; some time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech. Coll., Brighton; exammr., Univ. of Edinburgh, Oxford and Cambridge joint bd. and Oxford local exams.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1912; mob., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen staff, Aldershot commd., Dec., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Educ. Corps, Nov., 1920; major, R.A.R.O., Mar., 1923; commr., educn., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1924; (appt. altered to dir., Nov., 1927): mem., educn. comm., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free liby., May, 1926; mem., ct. of policy and combined ct., June, 1927; chmn. diamond and gold industries comm., Sept., 1927; mem. first exec. and leg. comm., under-reformed constitution, July, 1928; ag. col. sec. and gov.'s dep. on various occasions; chmn. primary educn. comm., 1938; admnr. and col. sec., St. Vincent, Windward Islds., 1938.

GREAVES, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham; insp., pol. K. Lumpur, July, 1919; 1st insp., cent. sta., K. Lumpur, June, 1923; asst. super., cent. F.M.S., July, 1923; supt., cust., prev. br. F.M.S., Nov., 1923; supt., trade and cust., Nov., 1931; supt., cust. and excise, Ipoh, Nov., 1935; ag. dep. commr., Perak, Feb., 1936.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (Tor.), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (Lpool).—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Milne med. in trop. med. Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1916-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., Bahamas, 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of small-pox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—B. 1857; B.A., Oxon., honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. inst., Apr., 1902; ret.

GRECH, WYNNDHAM, LL.D. (Malta), 1913.—Called to the bar (Grays Inn), 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; capt., R.A.F., 1918; sp. serv., Malta and Rome, 1917; attd., Italian Sup. Comd., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); transfd., unemployed list (R.A.F.), 1919; asst. legal adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser and crown pros., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922 and in 1924; legal adviser and crown pros., 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to Jan., 1924, Mar., Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.
GREEN, CLAUDE.—B. 1879; entd. Trinidad civ. serv., Mar., 1896; ch. clk. to gen. man. of rlyws., Mar., 1917.

GREEN, FREDERICK RAY HILTON.—B. 1901; ed. Highgate Schl., and R. Schl. of Mines; inspr., mines, Kenya, May, 1933; supt., surveys, Br. Guiana, Dec., 1936; ag. commr., lands and mines, and temp. mem., leg. coun., Mar.-Oct., 1937; do., 1939.

GREEN, FREDERICK WALTER VICTOR.—B. 1891; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 6th cls. clk., cust., 1914; asst. commr., 1917; sec., excise bd., July, 1923; 3rd cls. commr., July, 1925; 2nd gr. offr., dist. admin., Jan., 1932; ag. ch. commissary and dist. commr., Georgetown and E. Bank, Demerara, May, 1934; dist. commr., W. Demerara, Jan., 1935; do., Essequibo, Apr., 1936.

GREEN, GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster City Schl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; temp. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny. of lab., Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., 1920 to June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st June, 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928.
GREEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

GREEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1897; clerical asst., col. sec.'s office, B. Guiana, 1912; 6th cls. clk., 1913; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; sec. to commr. for considering disposal of colony lands, 1917; 2nd cls. clk., 1920; sec. to civ. serv. comm., 1923; sec. R. Guiana section, Wembley, 1924; prin. clk., 1924; sec. rly. comm., 1925; liaison offr., parl. delegation to Br. Guiana, 1926; sec., Br. Guiana delegation to W. Indian confce., London, 1926; sec., civ. serv. comm., 1926-27; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec. and clk. exec. and leg. coun., various occasions, 1925-30; sec., Br. Guiana constitution comm., 1927; prte. sec., O.A.G. in 1928; 2nd astt. col. sec., 1930; astt. col. sec., 1931; prin. astt. col. sec., 1934.

GREEN, JOSEPH.—B. 1897; U.K. post office, Aug., 1914; on mil. serv., lieut., Indian Army Res., 1915-19; supt., mails, Penang, Feb., 1921; acctnt., Sept., 1924 and Nov., 1926; senr. supt., mails, S'pore, Dec., 1925; supt., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1932; ag. contrlr., Penang and P. Wellesley, Dec., 1933; contr., P. and T., Feb., 1937; contr., P. and T., Malaya, 1937.

GREEN, CAPT. MAURICE JOHN, R.N.V.R., V.D.—B. 1878; ed. Sea Point pub. schl. and S.A. Coll.; astt. clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1896; clk. of papers, 1897; comte., clk., Cape leg. coun., 1904, and Union senate, 1910; gent. ush., black rod, 1916; clk. ast., 1926; clk. of senate, 1930; sworn trans. (Eng. Dutch); sec. and shorthand writer various govt. commns.; astt. sec. speaker's confce. on future const. senate; war svrs., Anglo-Boer, in W.P.M.R.; Great war, in Ger. S.W. Afr. as nav. transit. Offr., in Harwich light cruiser. squad. and war cabinet secretariat; O.C., S.A. divn., R.N.V.R., 1929-34.

GREEN, RICHARD THOMAS BONFIELD, M.D., B.S. (Melb.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1897; ed. Melbourne Church of Eng. Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Univ. of Melbourne; med. offr., F.M.S., June, 1926; pathologist II, inst. med. resch., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; malaria reach. offr. I, inst. med. resch., Jan., 1929; Milne Medial Liv. S. of T.M., Mar., 1930; rep. S.S. and F.M.S. at 2nd Inter. Cong. on Malaria, Algiers, May, 1930; mem. of malaria comm., health organizn., L. of N., Dec., 1930; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Resch., Apr., 1932-Feb., 1933; bacteriologist, F.M.S., and malaria reach. offr. I, in addtn., Aug., 1934; title changed to sr. bacteriologist, 1937.

GREENBERG, LEOPOLD.—B. 1885; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., LL.B.; called to Transvaal bar, 1911; practised at Johannesburg, 1911-24; puisne judge, Transvaal Prov. divn., sup. ct., Nov., 1924; judge presid., Transvaal Prov. divn., Mar., 1938.
GREENE, IAN RAWDON, LL.B., B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1909; called to bar (Kings Inns, Dublin), 1931; Dublin and South Western Circuit, 1932-35; crown coun., Tanganyika, 1935; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1937.

GREENE, MAURICE CHERY.—B. 1881; ed. at Rathmines's schl. and St. Stephen's Green schl., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; B.A. 1905; student, King's Inns, Dublin, 1910-11; dist. offr., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; served with W.A.F.F., 1914-15; station mag., 1918; pol. mag., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; ag. puisne judge, Sept.-Dec., 1935; puisne judge, Palestine, 1937.

GREENFIELD, LIEUT.-COL. HERBERT, O.B.E., R. of O.—B. 1889; ed. privately; served in S. African War, 1901-02; joined R.A.V.C.; lieut., May, 1903; served in India, 1903-08; capt., May, 1908; attd. staff coll., 1910-12; sp. serv. and research work, N. and S. China, 1912-14; served in Great war, 1914-18 Tsingtau operns.; major, July, 1915; D.A.D.V.S., 10th divn., Gallipoli, Greater Macedonia, Serbia, Bulgaria, 1915-16; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; D.A.D.V.S., France and Belgium, 1917-18; ment. in desps.; A.D.V.S. (lieut.-col. temp.) Rhine Army, 1919; served in Waziristan operns., 1920 as D.A.D.V.S. and D.D.V.S. (temp. lieut.-col.) Wazir Force, 1920-21; ment. in desps.; D.A.D.V.S., India and in Burma, 1921-24; exec. duty, Aldershot, 1925-28; vety. offr., Barbados, Oct., 1928.

GREENHALGH, N., B.Sc., Hons. (Vict.).—B. 1888; agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1910; lecturer in agric. science to students of training coll., Antigua; astt. inspr., of schls., Barbados, 1912; lecturer in educ. and master of method, training inst., Codrington Coll.; ag. chief inspr. of schls., Trinidad, June to Oct., 1915; inspr., schls., Barbados, 1934.

GREENHILL, MARCUS ARTHUR.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Sep., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil'y. serv., May, 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1923; staff offr., July, 1934.

Barbados, 1923; judge, ct. of appl., Barbados, 1925, mag., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1928 to June, 1929; ag. puisne judge in 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. regis., Oct., Nov., 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Jan., 1931; ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1932; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1936; ag. atty.-gen., Mar.-Oct., 1937 and Sept., 1938.

GREENLAND, STANLEY ARTHUR.—Served with Natal Field Force, Rebellion, 1906; clk., govt., sec. dept., Papua, Mar., 1912; J.P., Sept., 1912; ag. astt. res. mag., Ioma, May, 1913; ag. astt. res. mag., Kairuku, Mar., 1915; inspr., dept. native affairs, Mar., 1917; ag. curator, intestate estates, regisr.-gen., etc., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. inspr., native affairs, 1918; ag. Papua govt. agt., Sydney, N.S.W., Dec., 1919; travelling inspr., native affairs, July, 1922; J.P. for N.S.W., Oct., 1923; ag. mem., land bd., Papua, Nov., 1924; ch. inspr., native affairs, Dec., 1924.
GREENSHIELDS, HON. ROBERT A. E., LL.D., D.C.L.—B. 1861; ed. Danville Acad. and McGill Univ., Canada; ag. ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1929; ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1933.

(1923), K.C., Ireland (1920), K.C., Gibraltar, (1914).—B. 1875; B.A. (hons.), Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st scholar in modern literature, exhibitioner; Chancellor’s gold medallist; called to the Irish Bar, June, 1898; went Connaught circuit; apptd. HALL, ROBERT DE ZOUCHE.—B. 1904; ed. Willaston Schl., Liverpool Univ. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1928.
