Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; 3rd cl. hons. mods.; 2nd cl. hons. final jurisprudence; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1908; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1910; crown counsel, 1914; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1914; senr. crown counsel, 1915; ag. solr.-gen., 1916, 1917 and 1918; circuit judge, Ashanti (temporary appt.), 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1919; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920; puisne judge, 1921; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1925-31; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 1931; ag. gov., Feb.-Mar. and June-Aug., 1934; ch. just., Uganda, 1935; do., F.M.S., 1937, ret., 1938.

HALL, THOMAS DONALD HORN, C.M.G. (1939), LL.B. (N.Z.).—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; N. Z. dept. of agric., 1901; asst. law draftsman, 1921; clk. of house of reps., 1930.

HALLIER, CAPT. FRANK COLLINGS, M.C., Reg. Army, R. of O.—B. 1888; U.D.F. and Cape Corps, 1913-18; S. African Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; G.E.A., 1916-18; seconded as asst. pol. offr., civ. admin., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918; admin. offr., 1st gde., Feb., 1922; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1933; prov. commnr., Nov., 1934.
HALIFAX, JOHN GORDON, C.S.L.L.—B. 1883; clk. law dept. (Cape), 1903; clk. pub. serv. commn., 1913; ch. clk., ditto, 1927; pub. serv. inspnr., 1930; sec., pub. serv. commn., Oct., 1935.

HALLINAN, MAJOR THOMAS JOHN, C.B.E. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Dublin Univ.).—B. 1886; ed. Archbishop Tenison's Schl. and Charing Cross Hosp. (exhbr. and Pereira prize); commd., R.A.M.C., 1910-25; served, B.E.F., Egypt, 1914-16; Mesopotamia, 1916-20; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Amarah, 1918; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Basrah, 1918-20; thirce ment. in desp.; seconded, govt. of Iraq, health offr., 1920; inspnr.-gen., civ. and health services, 1922; ret., R. of O., 1925; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1933; M.L.C., 1934.

HALLOWES, BARAZON HENRY COLLIS, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1884; ed. Corrig Sch., Kingstown and Trinity Coll., Dublin; survr., valuation and landry survey of Ireland, 1910; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; aspt., R.F.A., 1914-19; land survr., 1921; asst. supt., surveys, 1938.
HALSE ROGERS, HON. SIR PERCIVAL, K.B.E. (1939).—B. 1883; judge, sup. ct., N.S. Wales, June, 1928.

HAM, GORDON LUPTON.—B. 1885; ed. King Edward's Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; lent to Kelantan, Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S. S., Jan., 1918; clk. of couns., Sept., 1919; 1st asst. col. sec., S. S. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; asst. offl. assignee, Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high commnr., Feb., 1921; ag. asst. adviser to govt. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. 1st asst. sec. B. S. S., Apr., 1922; ag. dep. treas. (B.), S.S., Dee., 1924; ag. dep. treas. (A.), Mar., 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1926; sec. to res., Perak, Nov., 1926; cls. IB., Jan., 1927; pres., munic. commnr., Penang, May, 1927; Br. advr., Trengganu, Mar., 1931; offr., cls. I.A., dep. pres., munic. comm., S'pore, Nov., 1932; offr. cls. I.A., under sec., S.S., June, 1933; ag. col. sec., S.S., on various occasions, 1933-35; dep. P.M.C., S'pore, Mar., 1936; Br. res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1936.

HAM, LAURENCE PETER VAN ZYL.—Clk., rly. and cent. tel. office, 1928; clk., res. mag., Hoopstad, June, 1902; A.R.M., Hoopstad, Aug., 1902; Jacobdal, Nov., 1903; Thaba 'Nchu, Feb., 1907; Winburg, Feb., 1910; D.A.R.M., Fouriesburg, Aug., 1911; Trompsburg, May, 1915; mag., Phillipolos, Aug., 1919; Winburg, 1920; Wakkerstroom, May, 1920; ag. mag., Harrismith, Apr., 1925; inspnr., pub. serv. comm., Feb., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1928; sec., dept. of mines and industries, Sept., 1928.
HAMBER, HON. ERIC WERGE, B.A.—B.1880; ed. Univ. of Manitoba (B.A.); ent. Dominion Bank, 1899; dir., Dominion Bank, 1912; gov., Univ., Br. Columbia; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, May, 1936.

HAMBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1925.

HAMILTON, ARCHIBALD JOHNSTON.—B. 1892; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; ent. service of govt. of Trinidad, Apr., 1911; mag., county of St. Patrick, Jan., 1927; do., St. George East, Jan., 1933; asst. mag., St. George West, Mar., 1934; mag., Trinidad and Tobago (title changed) April, 1935; ag. ch. mag., Oct.-Dec., 1937, and June-Oct., 1938.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; exhibtnr., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 1911; J.P., 1914; attd. sany. dept., 1915; attd. impts. and expts. office in addn., 1916; attd. to sany. dept. for sp. serv., 1918; dist. offr., 1918; dist. offr., South, 1920 and 1922; 2nd pol. mag., 1922 and 1923; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1924, 1928 and 1931; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1926; dep. clk. of couns. in addn., 1927; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1929; extra assist. col. sec., 1930; 1st pol. mag., 1933 and 1934; supt. impts. and expts., various periods, 1933-39; contrlr. of trade, 1939.

HAMILTON, MAJOR FREDRICK LINDSAY, M.C.—B. 1891; B.E.F., France, 1914-18; N. Russia, 1919-20; dist. inspnr., R.I.C., 1921; asst. commnr., pol., Gold Coast, June, 1921; commnr., 1927; ag. senr. commnr., pol., 1933-1934; title changed to supt., 1937; ag. dep. commr., Apr., 1937; ag. commr., Apr., 1938.
HAMILTON, KYRIE CLAUDE, M.I.N.A., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1894; Messrs. Vickers Ltd., 1911-14; capt., inf., H.M.E.F., 1914-21; asst. govt. marine surv., Hong Kong, 1928.

HAMILTON, PRECIUS JOHN ALEXANDER.—Col. audit dept., Aug., 1915; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Sept., 1916; audr., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1923; audr., Leeward Is., Dec., 1923; audr., Fiji, Br. Solomons Is., Prot., Gilbert & Ellice Is., Col., May, 1928; seconded to audit accounts of condominium govt., New Hebrides, Apr.-July, 1929; asst. audr., Hong Kong, 1933; senr. do., 1939.

HAMILTON, HON. ROBERT ALEXANDER BENJAMIN, (Master of Belhaven)—B. 1903; ed. Eton and R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., R. Scots Fusiliers; seconded (local capt.), Aden Prot. Levies, 1930; seconded, polit. offr., Aden, Sept., 1934; ag. polit. sec., Apr.-June, 1937; offr. in charge, Protectorate Guards, 1938.

HAMILTON, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, K.T. B.A.G. (1918).—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; class. scholar, B.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; student, Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. commr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; regisr., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; asst. judge and admstr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1905; chief justice, 1911; ret., 1920; partly under sec. of state for the cols., 3rd Sept., 1931; reasspt., 9th Nov., 1931; resigned, 1932.
HAMMOND, ROBERT ALSTON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1906; ed. Plumtree Schl., S. Rhodesia, Univ. of Liverpool and R. "Dick" Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; vety. offr., Kenya, 1930.

HAMMOND, STANLEY ALFRED ANDREW, M.A.—B. 1898; ed. Bancroft's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dist. educn., N. Provs., Nigeria, Apr., 1922; dir., educn., Jamaica, 1928; senr. educn. commr., W. Indies, 1936.

HAMP, ARTHUR EDWARD, C.B.E. (1937), A.M.I.C.E., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst. E., B. 1886; asst. engnr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1912; asst. engnr., Uganda rly., 1914; dist. engnr., K.U.R. & H., 1925; asst. C.E., 1928; C.E., 1928.

HAMZAH, BIN Abdullah.—B. 1890; ed. Malay Coll., K. Kangsar; cler. serv., F.M.S., June, 1908; attlit. offr., Selangor, May, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1916; passed cadets' law exam., Dec., 1917; Malay. offr., grade II, Jan., 1918; ditto, grade I, Jan., 1919; ditto, spl. cls., Jan., 1920; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1921; asst. offr. assignee, Perak, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1925; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1929; dist. offr., K. Langat, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, mag., Seremban, Oct., 1930; dist. offr., U. Selangor, May, 1934; offr., cls. II, Oct., 1935.

HANCOCK, CYRIL GEORGE.—B. 1902; ed. Kingsland Grange, Shrewsbury, Bromsgrove Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1923; dist. commr., Jan., 1931; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1938.

HANCOCK, Capt. Otho Lewis, O.B.E. (1925)—B. 1893; ed. King's coll., Taunton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Aug., 1914; lieut., 1914; capt., 1915; wounded, 1915; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1916; staff offr., local forces and adjutant, volunteers, Barbados, 1917; gov., H.M. prison, Barbados, 1920; ag. senr. pol. mag., Aug. to Sept., 1922; commr., Virgin Is., 1923; dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Aug., 1926; inspr., prisons, S. Sttlmts. and supt., Singapore prison, 1930; inspr., prisons, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

HANDS, ANDREW CHARLES.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; war serv., Aug., 1914-Nov., 1919; col. audit dept., Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1922; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Oct., 1932; dep. audr., 1935; ag. audr., May, 1937 to Apr., 1938; dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1939.

HANDS, ARTHUR L. C., M.B.R.—B. 1890; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. see.'s off., Apr., 1898; regisr. of births and deaths, 1904-07; oh. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910; ag. crown prosecutor, clk. of the peace and pol. mag. during 1923.

HANDYSIDE, WILLIAM LUCAS, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), F.R.G.S., F.R. Econ. S.—B. 1891; prizeman, hons. English, Glasgow Univ., 1913; 1st place language, English hons. schl., Glasgow Univ., 1914; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, July, 1915; mast. of method, Hong Kong Univ. (in addn.), 1918-20; ast. mast., cls. II, Jan., 1920; lect. teachers' classes, &c., tech. inst., 1917-20 and 1922-24; external examnr., Hong Kong Univ., geography, history of educn., 1922; head mast., Saiyingpun Schl., 1925; mast. of method (in addn.), Hong Kong Univ.; ast. mast., King's Coll., 1927; lect. on ethics and logic, Hong Kong Univ., 1927; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., 1928; lect. on polit. science, 1928; lect. on econ. hist. and statistics, 1929; headmast., Yaumati schl., July, 1930; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931; lect., H.K. Univ., 1932-1933; headmast., Ellis Kadoorie Schl., 1934; headmast., King's Coll. and mast. of method, in addn., Hong Kong Univ., 1935; J.P., 1935; senr. mast., educn. dept., 1936; headmast., Queen's Coll., 1937; ext. examnr., Hong Kong Univ., statistics, hist. and theory of educn., 1937; headmast., Br. central schl., 1938; senr. inspr., schls., 1938.

HANITSCH, CAPT. KARL VERNON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; war serv., R.F.A., 1914-19; admstr. offr., cls. 4, Nigeria, 1920; ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1928; ag. res., 1932, 1934 and 1935; admstr. serv., cls. 2, 1934; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1936; adminstr. offr., cls. I, 1936.

HANKENY, LIEUT.-COL. SIR M. P. A., G.O.B. (1919); K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1912).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1895; served in H.M.S. "Ramillies," flagship and 2nd flagship, Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major whilst serving on a special comte., 1906; intell. offr., Mediterranean, 1907; ast. sec., comte. of Imp. defence, 1908; ast. sec., 1912; sec. war cabinet, from 1916; British sec., Peace Conf., 1919; comdr., Legion of Honour, 1917; commendatore, Crown of Italy, 1918.

HANKINSON, WALTER CROSSFIELD, O.B.E. (1936), M.C. (1918), M.A.—B. 1894; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, M.A. (1921); private, Royal Fusiliers, Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Yorks and Lancs. Regt., Jan., 1915; lieut., Apl., 1915; capt., May, 1915; served in Egypt and France; demobilised, Feb., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clc., C.O., Feb., 1920; ast. prin., Apr., 1920; ast. priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, Mar., 1923; to Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924 and to Mr. Amery, Nov., 1924; ag. prin., D.O., Nov., 1925; prin., Feb., 1926; acc. U.K. deleg., to naval disarmament CONFEE., Geneva, 1927; ag. rep. in Commonwealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in U.K., 1931-32 and 1935-36; prin. priv. sec. to Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, Feb., 1937; to Lord Stanley, May, 1938; to Mr. MacDonald, Oct., 1938; to Sir Thomas Inskip, Feb., 1939; and to Mr. Eden, Sept., 1939; asst. sec., 1939; seconded as prin. sec. to H.Cr. for U.K. in Canada, Oct., 1939.

HARDING, SIR ALFRED JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Brecon, and St. John's, Camb.; foundin schr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cls. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comtees. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. Ist cls. clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; sec., colonial blue-books comtees., 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. African currency bd., 1926-1928; mem., Palestine currency bd., 1926-1928; dir., col. audit, 1st Oct., 1928.

HARDING, SIR EDWARD JOHN, G.C.M.G. (1939), K.C.B. (1935), K.C.M.G (1928), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1889); 1st cls. clas. moda., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comtees., 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Coman., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 10th Apr., 1918; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1918; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apr., 1916; sec. to comtees. for attlt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1921; asst. under-sec. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925; perm. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 24th Jan., 1930; high consnr. for Basutoland, the Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland, and high consnr. in the Union of S. Africa for H.M. govt. in the U.K., 1939.
HARDING, H. A.—B. 1917; ed. Rugby Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (schol.), wrangler pt. II math. tripos, 1937, distino. pt. III, 1938; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Feb., 1939.
HARDING, WILLIAM, B. LITT., LL.D.—B. 1898; matric., Malta Univ., 1912; B. Litt., 1915; LL.D., 1919; legal adviser to army, navy and air forces, 1929; judge, 1934.
HARDY, EDWARD GERSHAM, N.D.D., N.D.A.—B. 1883; ed. Midland Agrl. and Dairy Coll., Kingston, Eng.; senr. dairy inspr., dept. of agr., Union of S.A., 1913; aspt. of dairying, 1928.

HAREVEN-STONEHILL, DAVID, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1896; ed. privately and Univ. of Nancy (France); mil. serv., B.E.F. (R.E. Signals), 1916-19; served in occupied enemy territory admstrn., 1919; tele. inspr., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. asst. engr., Aug., 1933 to Apr., 1934; ag. engr., in 1934 and 1935; ch. storekeeper, Oct., 1935.

HARFORD, JAMES DUNDAS.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (hon. schol.); M.A.: mil. serv., 1917-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1926; asst. sec., 1930; clk. of couns., 1931-33; attd. C.O., Sept., 1934 to Sept., 1936; admstr, Antigua and Fed. sed., Leeward Is., Sept., 1936; ag. gov., Leeward Is., March-Oct., 1938; admstr., St. Kitts—Nevis, 1940.

HARINGTON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, G.C.B. (1933), G.B.E. (1922), K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—B. 1872; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; ant. 2nd Batt., King's Liverpool Regt., 1892; served on staff, S. Africa, 1899-1900 (despa. twice, D.S.O.); European War, 1914-18 (despa., bt. lient.-col. and col., comdr., Legion of Honour, maj.-gen.) instr., R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, gen. staff, W.O.; bgde. maj., 6th Infantry Brig.; G.S.O.I., B.G.S.G., Canadian Corps.; M.G.G.S. 2nd Army; dep. ch., Imp. gen. staff, 1918-20; G.O.C. Army of Black Sea, 1920-21; G.O.C. allied forces of occupation in Turkey, 1921-23; Northern command, 1923-27; gen., 1927; G.O.C. in C., Western command, India, 1927-31; G.O.C. in C. Aldershot command, 1931; A.D.C. gen. to King since 1930; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1933.
HARKNESS, J., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. & L.R.C.S. & L.R.F.P.S. (Edin. & Glas.), L.D.S. (Ed.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.
HARKNESS, J. W. P., O.B.E. (1935), B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1890; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1920; dep. dir., dept. of health, 1930; title changed to D.D.M.S., 1936; D.M.S., Gold Coast, 1939.

HARLECH, 4th Baron (cr. 1876) ORMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE ARTHUR, P.C. (1927), G.C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Shropshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab bureau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917-18; asst. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British mem., Permanent Mandates Coman., L. of N., 1921-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied C.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; parly. under sec. of state for Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 1924 and since Nov., 1924; chmn. parly. coman. to E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and rept. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926; S. of S. for the Cola., 1936-38.
HARLEY, ROBERT MICHAEL, B.Sc.—B. 1914; ed. George Watson's Coll., St. Andrews Univ., B.Sc. (biology) and Univ. of Edinburgh; B.Sc. (forestry) 1936; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Oct., 1937.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY, M.B.E. (1935).—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902; staff offr., 8th Apr., 1930.

HARNETTY, LIEUT. EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21 passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi and Mandingo; asst. dist. coman., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; passed higher standard exam. in Mende; dist. coman., May, 1928.

HARNEY, EGGBERT EWART.—B. 1898; clk. to regier-gen., Antigua, 1916; 3rd audit clk., 1921; 2nd do., 1922; ch. clk., regier's office, 1927; comanr. for oaths, 1927; ag. regier. and prov. marshal, Dominica, 1931; prin., treasy. dept., Mar., 1933; passed bar final exams. (Inner Temple), Dec., 1934; ag. mag., dist. F., May, 1935 to Feb., 1936; ag. treas., Mar.-Oct., 1936; temp. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Mar.-Oct., 1936; oh. regier., Leeward Is. and registr. and provost marshal, Antigua, 1936; treas. and compt., cust., Dominica, Dec., 1936; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Jan., 1937; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1938.
HARPER, AUSTIN BYMES.—B. 1908; ed. Blundell's; agrl. dipl., Cedara, Natal, S. Africa; agrl. dept., Nigeria, 1930; Jamaica constab., 1933; 3rd cls. inspr., pol., 1938.

HARPER, SIR CHARLES HENRY, K.B.E. (1930), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, Jan., 1900; attd., Ashanti field force, June to Sept., 1900; asst. dist. commr., Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Apl. to June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct., 1908; prov. commr., July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. ch. asst. and clk. of couns., May to June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912 and from May to Aug., 1914; attd., Togoland field force, Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, Sept. to Dec., 1914; ch. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec., in 1915, 1916 and 1917; ch. commr., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov., St. Helena, Dec., 1924; assumed govt., Feb., 1925; ret., 1932.

HARPUR, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1885; P.W.D., Nyassaland, 1917-29; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ag. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1923-29; exec. engrnr., div. pub. wks. office, Dec., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., May, 1936; senr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1937; settlement engrnr., Malacca, Jan., 1938.

HARRAGIN, MAJOR ALFRED ERNEST ALBERT, D.S.O., V.D.—B. 1877; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Feb., 1905; asst. supt., fire brigade, Port-of-Spain, Sept., 1910; sub. inspr., Tobago, divn., May, 1911; inspr., constab., 1912; senr. inspr., constab., Apr., 1934; dep. major-gen., 1936.

HARRAGIN, WALTER, K.C. (1935).—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; clk. of the peace and sub.-registrar, San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary major; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solr. gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from Jan., 1925 to Aug., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Apr., 1927; ag. judge in 1927, 1929, 1930 and 1932; attv.-gen., Kenya, Oct., 1933; ag. ch. sec., May, 1938-Aug., 1939; ag. gov., Sept., 1939-Jan., 1940.

HARRIES, CLIFFORD LOWE REVES.—B. 1879; C.S.L.L.; entd., Transvaal serv., 1900; inspr., native affrs., dept., 1903; native commr., 1904; seconded to Tanganyike admntr., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; returned to Union of S.A. serv., June, 1919; mag., Potgietersrust, Apr., 1923; do., Ladysmith, Aug., 1930; do., Kimberley, Sept., 1935; do., Pietermaritzburg, Aug., 1937.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Poeteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancered student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. elk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounty Conf., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st cls. elk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. elk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. elk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comtee. of comm. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comtee. of Imp. Inst., 1909-16; gov. and o. in c., Newfoundland, Nov., 1917; assumed govt., Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., boy elk., G.P.O., 1912; on mily. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; tempy. employment elk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, PERCY GRAHAM.—B. 1894; ed. St. Bees Schl.; Law Soc.'s studentship, 1911; war serv. with King's (L'pool) Regt. and Nigeria. Regt., 1914-19; France, 1915-16; admntr. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, Jan., 1919; anthropological dipl. (Lond.), 1932; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., Aug., 1934; officiating sec., N. Provs., Aug., 1934; admintr. offr., staff gde., Nov., 1938.

HARRIS, REGINALD HARRISON—B. 1884, ed. Lord Williams Grammar Schl., Thame and King's Coll., Lond.; war serv., France 1915-16, R. Munster Fusiliers; lieut., E. Africa, 4th K.A.R.(capt. M.C. ment. in desps.); tax duties, Tanga, Sept., 1934.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; elk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyassaland Prot., June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; elk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. elk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. senr. asst. treas., in 1930 and 1931; ag. dep. treas., Oct., 1935 to Aug., 1937 and Feb., 1938; asst. acctnt.-gen., 1939; acctnt.-gen., 1939.

HARRISON, AUNTEN ST. BARBE., R.I.B.A. (chtl. archt.), mem. of Town Planning Inst.—B. 1892; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and McGill Univ., Montreal; archt. and town planner, miny. of communications, Greece, 1920-22; archt., P.W.D., Palestine, 1922.

HARRISON, HON. ERIC JOHN, M.P.—Served A.I.F. 1916-19; el. to H. of R. for Wentworth, N.S.W., g.e., 1931, 1934, 1937; min. for int., Oct., Nov., 1934; mem., jt. comtee. on pub. wks., June-Sept., 1937; asst. min., admntr. external territories, Lyons min., Nov., 1938-Apr., 1939; do., Page min., 7th-26th Apr., 1939; P.M.G. and min. for repat., Menzies min., from Apr., 1939.

HARRISON, WILFRID HUGH LANE.—B. 1900; ed. Durham Schl.; served in R.N., 1918-32; ret., 1932 with rank of lieut.-cdr.; housemaster, H.M. Borstal Inst., Portland, 1932-34; supt., cls. III, approved schl., Kabete, Kenya, 1934; supt. prison, Hong Kong, 1939.
HARRY, CHARLES.—B. 1890; ed. Grammar Schl., Hayle, Cornwall; asst. elec. engr., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; ex. elec. engr., Apr., 1933; sr. ex. elec. engr., Jan., 1939.

HART, BRIG.-GEN. SIR HERBERT, K.B.E. (1935), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915), Croix-de-Guerre-French (1919), V.D. (1925).—Served in S. African War, 1902; lieut., N. Z. volunteers 1907; capt. in territorial force, 1911; major, 1912; major in N.Z. exped. force (main body), 1914; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1916; temp. brig.-gen., 1917; in commd. territorial brig., 1920; R. of O., 1920; ret. list, 1930; admstr., Western Samoa, 1931-35; was A.D.C. to gov.-gen., N. Z., 1920-24; five times men in deeps.

HART, HENRY GEORGE.—B. 1894; served S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1913-16; R.F.A., 1916-19; war serv., German S.W. Africa, 1914-16; France, 1916-19; constable, N. Rhodesia pol., 1919; sergt., 1921; lieut., 1927; asst. commr., 1930; dep. commr., 1936; ag. commr., pol. and prisons, on several occasions; commr., pol. and prisons, 1937.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on mily. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of mily. accnts., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. off., C.O., 9th July, 1926.

HART, LAWRENCE HERWARD.—B. 1891; ed., Lancing Coll., pol. probtr., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; asst. commr., pol., June, 1918; ditto, Selangor, Oct., 1920; ch. pol. offr., J. Bahrul, Dec., 1922; 1st asst. commr., pol., Perak, Dec., 1924; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Apr., 1928; 1st asst. commr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1929; asst. commr., pol., F.M.S. Ryks., June, 1930; 1st asst. commr., pol., Perak, June, 1932; ch. pol. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1935.

HART, RUPERT LANCELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; gov't. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. D.I.V.S. for several periods; ag. D.V.S., June, 1932.

HARTER, JEAN DENIS EDGARD.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; asst. mast., R. Coll., 1901; asst. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; asst. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir., educn. and inspr. of schl., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schl., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to hl. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvt. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to coun., from Dec., 1924.
HARTIGAN, THOMAS JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1877; ch. acct., N.S.W. ryks., 1921-27; comptlr., accs. and audit, 1928-31; transport commr. (finance), 1932; commr. for ryks., 1933.
HARTLEY, JOHN WETTERMAN.—B. 1898; ed., St. Paul's Schl.; war serv. with R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1915-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; dist. offr., 1928; ag. res., in 1934 and 1937-38.

HARTLEY, BRIAN JOSEPH, N.D.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1907; ed., Loughborough Grammar Schl. and Midland Agrl. Coll.; C.O. agrl. course, Oxford and Trinidad, 1927-29; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929; agrl. offr., Aden, 1938.

HARTNOLL, AMYAS VICTOR.—B. 1897; ed., Rugby Schl. and R. Milv. Acad., Woolwich; commnd., R.F.A., 1915; war serv., France and Macedonia, 1915-18; M.C., 1918; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; dist. offr., 1931; ag. prov. commr., 1936 and 1937; reverted to dist. offr. 1937.

HARTWELL, CHARLES HERBERT, R.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; attld., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927; attld., secretariat, Oct., 1927; office asst., N. Eliya kach., May, 1928; asst. ch. sec., Aug., 1931; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1932; sec. to min. for lab., industry and commerce, Mar., 1933; addnl. dep. contr., lab., May, 1933; attld. to treas. and sec., select comttee, on salaries and cadres commnr., June, 1933; sec., headmen's commnr. in addn., Aug., 1934; seconded as sec., to Sir Alan Pim in Kenya, Oct., 1935; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Dec., 1936; attld., ch. sec.'s office, Oct., 1937; asst. ch. sec., June, 1938.

HARVEY, CEDRIC, B.Sc. (Agric.), A.I.C.T.A. (Trinidad).—B. 1902; ed., Woodford Coll., Essex and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; agric. offr., Tanganyika, 1927; ag. senr. agric. offr., 1932-1933; senr. agric. offr., Fiji, 1938.

HARVEY, JOHN ALLEN, M.A. (N.Z.), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1924; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1931; dist. offr., Kota Bharu and Treas., Kelantan, July, 1934; offr. cls. III., Dec., 1936.
HARVEY, HON. SIR JOHN MUSGRAVE, KT., B.A. (1933).—B. 1866; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913; ag. ch. just., June, 1933-Jan., 1934; ret., Jan., 1936.

HARVEY, HON. HORACE, B.A., LL.B., LL.D.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. and Toronto Univ.; called to bar (Ont.), 1889; called to bar Northwest Territories, 1893; registr., land titles, Alberta, 1896; dep. atty. genl., Northwest Territories, 1900; judge, supt. ct., N.W.T., 1904; judge, sup. ct., Alberta, 1907; ch. just., Alberta, 1910; chmn., bd. of gov't., Alberta Univ., 1917.
HARVEY, REGINALD JAMES.—B. 1901; ed., Charterhouse and Exeter Coll.; Oxford, 1920-23; 4th cls., honr., mod. hist.; St. George Schl., Jerusalem, 1923-25; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.
HASKINS, HAROLD STANLEY, M.C., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Bristol Univ.; sect. engrnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. ryks., May, 1919; divnl. engrnr., July, 1929; dist. engnr., Aug., 1935.
HASLAM, FRANK CLAUDE, L.R.I.B.A.—B. 1894; architect, P.W.D., Uganda, 1919; senr. architect, P.W.D., Nigeria, 1937.

HASLAM, JOHN, F.C., M.C., M.D., F.R.C.P.E., D.P.H.—B. 1888; ed., Dollar Academy and Edinburgh Univ.; on overseas serv., 1915-19; ment. in depts., 1917; M.C., 1918; asst. to prof. of med. jurisprudence, Edin. Univ., 1919; asst. govt., M.O.H., Br. Guiana, 1921; govt., M.O.H., 1922; asst. dir., Bureau of Hygiene and Trop. Diseases, 1925; physician and bacteriologist, "Shell" group of oil companies, Venezuela, 1930; ch. med. offr., Barbados, 1933; dir., med. services, N. Rhodesia, 1935.

HASTINGS, MILTON PADGHAM, F.S.I.—B. 1881; lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, 1904; govt. survr., 1916; commnr., oaths and affidavits, 1918; warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dist.; dep. navig. offr. for Mazuruni river; warden mag., Mazuruni dist., 1937; ag. supt., surveys, 1936-37; supt., lands, 1938.

HATHORN, ALEXANDER ANTHONY ROT, B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Hilton Coll., Lancing Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1904; admitted advoc., Natal, 1904; K.C., 1922; judge, Natal prov. divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1931.

HAUGHTON, SYDNEY HENRY, B.A. Hons. (nat. sci. tripes, pts. 1 and 2), Cambridge, D.Sc., Cape Town Univ.—B. 1888; palaeontologist, S. African museum, 1911; asst. dir., do., 1914; senr. geologist, 1920; dir., geological survey of Union, 1934; pres., Geol. Soc. of S. Africa, 1925; Murchison grant, Geol. Soc., London, 1927; vice-pres., S. African Geographical Soc.; F.R.S. (S. Africa); mem. nat. and historical monuments commn.; mem. fuel research bd.; mem. Transvaal museum commn.

HAULTAIN, Hon. Sir Frederick William Gordon, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; ed. Montreal High Sch., Collegiate Inst. Peterboro, Ont. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1877; LL.D., 1916; called to bar, Ont., 1882; K.C. (Dom.), 1902; K.O. (Sask.), 1907; mem., northwest coun., 1887-88; M.L.A., northwest, 1888-1905; leader, exec. comttee, Northwest Territories, 1891-97; premier, atty. genl. and commr., of educn., N.W.T., 1897; M.L.A., Sask., 1905-12; ch. just., Sask., 1912; ch. univ., of Sask., 1917; ret., 1938.

HAVENGA, Hon. Nicolaas Christiaan.—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. coun. for Fauresmith, O.F.S. and mem., exec. comttee. for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Fauresmith from 1915; min. of finance, from June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; del., World Economic Conf., London, 1933; del. all Imperial Conf., since 1924.
HAWES, Henry Francis.—B. 1894; ed. Imp. Coll., Windsor; admitted, solr., 1920; legal advr., K.U.R. & H., 1927.
HAWES, Richard Brunel, M.B., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1893; med. offr., Kedah, June, 1923; prof. of medicine, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

HAWKESWORTH, Edward Gerald, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. St. Bees Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mily. serv., 1916-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; dist. offr., 1928; admstve. offr., cls. II, 1938; sec., W. Prov., 1939; admstve. cffr., cl. I, 1939.

HAWKINS, Brian Charles Keith.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hon., mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd asst. collr., treas., June, 1927; asst. aupt., impts. and expts., Nov., 1927; asst. head, sany. dept., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., South, May, 1932; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1933; sec., economic coms., July, 1934; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Oct., 1935; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., Jan.-Feb., 1937; pr. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Apr., 1937; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Nov., 1937.

HAWKINS, Gerald.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ. on mily. serv., 1914-20 (capts.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; sec. res., Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Mar., 1928; cls. III, May, 1928; assessor, wild life coms., Aug., 1930; asst. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933; attd., co-op. socs. dept., Sept., 1934; offr., cls. IB, 1937.

HAY, Alistair William.—B. 1899; ed. Tonbridge Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. V, ag. inspr. Chinese schls., Nov., 1924; passed cadet, June, 1925; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), Sept., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1928; 3rd mag., Spore, Jan., 1932; offr., cls. III, asst. prot. Chinese, Spore, Nov., 1933, do., Malaces, Jan., 1936; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Aug., 1937; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1938.
HAY, Clifford H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1928), M.V.O.—Sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales, Mar., 1916; under sec. and P.H., 1924; agt.-genl. for N.S. Wales, London, May, 1938.

HAY, Mortimer Cecil.—B. 1891; ed. The Sch., Bishop Stortford and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge (exhib.), M.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; dep. contr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930; cls. II, Nov., 1930; Br. advr., Perlis, Dec., 1930; ag. legal advr., Johore, Mar., 1933; cls. B, July, 1934; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, June, 1936; dep. legal advr., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; dep. commr. of lands, cls. IA, 1938.

HAYCRAFT, Sir Thos. Wagstaffe, Kt., Bach. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1886; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chm. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, James J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solic., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; regiar., high ct., Uganda, Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. adminstr.-gen., rev. authority, regiar. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927; offl. recr. and regist., trade marks and patents, Hong Kong, Apr., 1934; puisne judge for appl., Nov., 1934; puisne judge, Dec., 1935; ag. ch. just., May-June, 1936; puisne judge, Kenya, Mar., 1937.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, Hugh.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford Modern Sch.; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928; hon. A.D.C. to gov. Feb., 1929; sec., colonization comttee., Jan., 1929 to Mar., 1930; ag. prin. clk., C.S.O., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; asst. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1931.

HAYES-SADLER, Cecil William, M.A. (Oxon.).—B.1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engnr., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; asst. dist. comsnt., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1926; attached, D.O., 1927; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., 1933; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1934, and Mar., 1935 to Dec., 1936; ag. dep. col. sec., Dec., 1936 to Mar., 1938; ag. asst. ch. sec., Oct., 1938.

HAYWARD, Arthur Edwin.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Feb., 1900; clk., post office stores, Cape Town, 1903; clk., audir. br., 1908; clk., office of contr. and audr.-gen., 1910; 1st cls. clk., do., 1912; senr. clk., do., 1922; prin. clk., do., 1931; ch. clk., do. (Windhoek), 1932; ch. inspr. (rev.), do., 1935; ch. inspr. (rly. and harbours, Johannesburg), do., 1939.

HAYWARD, William Frederick.—B. 1891; clk., G.P.O., 1905-16; postal supt., 1927; acct., 1928; sec. to comms. of currency, 1932; ag. P.M.G., 1932, 1936 and 1937; nom. commr. of currency, 1932; nom. M.L.C., 1936; offl. M.L.C., 1937.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia, 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929; staff offr., 18th Dec., 1934; ag. prin., D.O., 19th June, 1939.

HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; asst. sec., Somaliland Prot., 1919; addt. for duty to C.O., 1926; resumed subst. appt., Somaliland, 1926; asst. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; col. sec. and regt.-gen., Grenada, 1935; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, July-Sept., 1938.
HEARD, WILLIAM PHILLIPS.—B. 1904; engnr. dftsmen, P.W.D., Tanganyika, Mar., 1928; do., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1936; ob. dftsmen, 1939.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, (Lincoln's Inn); asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; senr. mag., Nov., 1924; az. puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929; mem., H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930; chan. of diocese, 1932; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. ch. just., 1935 and 1936; puisne just., Ceylon, 1937.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e., 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his gov't., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint comen. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mily. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; appd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.
HEATH, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1890; asst. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. govt. printer, 1920, 1921, 1923 and 1932.
HEATH, HENRY WYLDE EDWARDS.—B. 1912; ed. Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; sub-inspr., pol., Leeward Is., Sept., 1931; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Jan., 1935.

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT.—B. 1896; ed. privately and Oxford Univ.; lieut., 1/1st Kent Regt. and R.F.C., 1915; H.M. Borstal instns., 1924; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927; commr., prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; commr., prisons, Kenya, 1938.

HEBBEN, GEORGE BRINTNALL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Schl.; tel. constrn. supt., engrnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909; suptg. tel. engrnr., Jan., 1914; asst. engrnr.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; dir. posts and tels. to C.E.F., 1915; engrnr.-in-ch., 1921; P.M.G., 1928; P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika postal serv., 1936.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1879; clk., out., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. clk., East London, 1915; survr., Durban, 1916; oh. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. commr., cust. and excise, 1926; commr., cust. and excise, 1930; del. for Union gov't. at Imp. Cust. Confce., 1921; cust. adviser to deleg. to Australian gov't. (flour shipments), 1921; mem., deleg. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union gov't. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923; advisory mem., Imp. Confce., 1926; del. for Union gov't., Imp. Confce., 1926, 1930, 1932 (Ottaws), 1937.
HEDLEY, RIDDLE, M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; irr. engrnr., Ceylon, 1907; asst. dir. P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1914; dist. engrnr., P.W., Palestine, 1922; asst. D.P.W., 1928.

HERNAN, JOSEPH WILLIAM ALLEN, C.B.E. (1937), LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Wellington Coll., and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. col. secy.'s office, 1906; asst. law draftsman, 1920; first asst. law draftsman, 1930; under-sec. for internal affrs. and clk. of writs, 1935.

HELPS, ERNEST HERBERT.—B.1888; joined Lord Milner's staff in Transvaal, 1905, and served with Inter-Col. coun. of the Transvaal and O.F.S. until July, 1908; elk., gov.'s office, Transvaal, July 1908, and gov-gen.'s office, May, 1910; admstr.'s office, Cape Prov., Mar., 1913; senr. clk., 1920; prin. elk., 1928; munici. sec., Dar-es-Salaam, Dec., 1930; pres., Dar-es-Salaam township authority and admstve. offr. in ch. of town, Aug., 1934.

HEMANS, CAPT. CECIL RUPERT, R.N. (Ret.).—B.1883; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia"; Boxer Rebellion, 1900; med. and clasp; Gen. Serv. Med. with Persian Gulf bar; war serv., 1914-18; ment. in despe.; placed on ret. list, 1922; pvt. sec. to Sir Gordon Guggisberg, 1923; harbmr., Takoradi, Sept., 1927.
HEMMINGS, EDWARD JOHN.—B. 1906; late R.N.R.; cargo supt. and relieving offr., coastal serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1931; asst. harbmr., Jan., 1932.
HENDERSON, ARCHIBALD KEITH JOHNSTON, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1936; offr. sl. II, Oct., 1938.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDERSON, GILBERT THOMAS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Presentation Bros.' Coll., Cork and Skerry's Acad., Cork; Union gov't. vety. serv., 1910-31; seconded to Nyasaland, 1913-14; prin. vety. offr., Basutoland, 1934.
HENDERSON, GORDON, M.C. (1917), M.M. (1915).—B. 1896; war serv., Middlesex Regt., 1914-20; asst. acctnt., Gold Coast rly., Aug., 1920; acctnt., 1926; staff acctnt., Apr., 1935; ch. acctnt., 1939.

HENDERSON, GUY WILMOT MCINTOCK, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. at Blundell's, Wanganui Collegiate Schl., New Zealand and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; served in Great War with R.F.A., for 31 years; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1923; prof. clk., prosecuting staff, G.P.O., London, 1929; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, 1932; ag. atty-gen. and temp. mem., exec. coun., various periods, 1933-37; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1937; ag. solr.-gen., in 1938 and 1939; ag. atty.-gen., Apr.-July, 1938.

HENDERSON, JAMES ARTHUR, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), B.Sc. (Pub. Health), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), Offr., Order St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1889; lieut., R.A.M.C., T.F., 1914; san'y. offr., E.E.F., D.A.D.M.S., Jerusalem, 1919; ret. with rank of major, 1921; senr. med. offr., Jerusalem, 1920; do., Palestine Rlys. and Haifa, 1928; senr. san'y. med. offr., Jamaica, 1930; ag. sup't. med. offr. and mem., leg. coun., on two occasions; surg. gen., Br. Guiana, 1933; mem., leg. coun.; S.M.O., St. Vincent, 1937.

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.R.S.N.Z.—B. 1880; ed., Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geolog. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, MURRAY ROSS, F.L.S.—B. 1899; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; syst. botanist, museum dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1921; bot. gardens, S'pore, Sept. to Dec., 1921; F.M.S. museums Dec., 1921 to July, 1934; curator, herb., bot. gardens, S.S., Sept., 1924; in ch., Waterfall gardens, Penang, Feb., 1937.

HENDERSON, William Walker, C.B.E. (1938) M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Vict.), capt., R.A.V.C. (of O.T.).—Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927; dir., vety. services, 1937.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—On mil. serv., Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; cov. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; M.C. and Bar; foreman of works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., Dec., 1921; seconded to Gambia as prin., new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929; prin., middle boarding schls., G. Coast, June, 1938.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, July, 1888; asst. to conservator, Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, June, 1893; Stutterheim, Aug., 1898; asst. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, Oct., 1905; asst. conserv., W. Conservancy, July, 1907; prin. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturership, Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; asst. conserv., forests, Midland Conservancy, Feb., 1909; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, Apr., 1912; conserv. forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; tranfil., Rhodesian forest serv., Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; offr., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 29th May, 1930.

HENNIKER HEATON, SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1928), B.A. (Oxon.).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regir. gen.'s off., Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. elk., native dept., and ag. asst. native consmr., July, 1906; apptd. asst. native commr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; commr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law commr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comdl., cov. Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925; col. sec., Cyprus, Sept., 1929; ag. gov., for various periods, 1929-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Falkland Is. and Dependencies, Jan., 1935.
HENRI, PAUL, B.Sc.—Ag. supt., schls., Mauritius, July, 1928; supt., schls., July, 1933.

HENRY, GEORGE HAWTT, L.M., L.Ch., L.A.O. (Dub. Univ.).—B. 1905; med. offr., Nigeria, Aug., 1920; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Lai Chi Kok prison, Kowloon mortuary, asst. M.O., Kowloon hosp., M.O. in ch., families, Kowloon, do., pol., Kowloon and do., K.C.R., 1935; med. offr., Nigeria, 1939.

HENRY, ROLAND EDWARD.—B. 1893; ed. Antigua Grammar Sch.; copyist, 1911; jnr. clk., post office, 1912; jnr. audit. clk., 1915; cust. offr., 1916; acct., treas., 1919; prin., treas., 1927; Federal acct., 1930; ag. Federal treas. and treas. of Antigua, Nov., 1930-Mar. 1931.

HENRY, ROBERT NOEL, M.A., higher dipl. educn., T.C.D.—B. 1898; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on staff of St. Columba's Coll., Dublin, 1924-28; war serv., 1916-19; asst. dir., educn., Cyprus, 1930; ag. dir., educn. for various periods, 1929-39.

HENWOOD, SYDNEY WILLIAM.—B. 1898; on war serv., 1917-18 (France); serv., in India, 1919-21; R.M. Schl. of Music, 1921-22; bandmr., The Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1923; served in India, 1923-31; Sudan, 1941-32; bandmr., Br. Guiana Militia Band, May, 1935; lieut., May, 1935; capt., Aug., 1939.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1887; ed. Latymer, Liverpool Inst. High Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schls., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schilmast., 1919; ag. asst. dir., S. Provs., 1927 and 1928; ag. dep. dir., S. Provs., 1929; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1929; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1934 and 1936; asst. dir., educn., 1936.
HERCHENRODER, FRANCIS.—B. 1896; mag., Mauritius, May, 1923; addl. substitute procureur-genl., July, 1934; substitute procureur and advoc. genl., from Dec., 1939.
HERCHENRODER, MARIE PAUL HENRI.—ag. regr.-gen., Mauritius, June, 1934; regr.-gen., Jan., 1937.

HERDMAN, SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, KT. BACH. (1929).—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915-18; judge of N.Z. sup. ct., 1918-35.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1905; ed. Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (chem.) (hons.), R.A. (forestry); asst. conservator, forests, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comdl. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., nat. party govt., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; do., coalition govt., Mar., 1933.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1890; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1914; ag. senr. asst. auditor, Jan. to Mar., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Coll. Audit Dept., July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, Sept., 1926; audr., N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEYMANS, Dr. G. M. A.—B. 1870; M.D. (Brussels) 1895; personal physician to late Pres. Kruger, 1899-1904; consul for Belgium, Transvaal, 1908-29; vice consul for Spain, Transvaal, 1907-29; chmn., med. bd., Pretoria, 1912-29; S. African trade commr., Milan, 1929; 1st sec., S. African legn., Rome, 1929-34; chargé d’affaires, Rome, 1933-34; min. plenip., Rome, Sept., 1934.

HEYWOOD, GRAHAM SCUDAMORE PERCIVAL.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; B.A. and B.Sc., 1925; meteorological research asst., war dept., Leafld, 1928; professional asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1932.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V, Mar., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July 1926; offr. cls IV, Mar., 1928; asst. emigr. consmr. for Malaya; asst. dir., co-op. emigr., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; asst. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1931; offr. cls III, Mar., 1933; coll., estate duty, F.M.S., offr. assignee and ag. regis., companies, Apr., 1934; ag. emigr. consmr., Malaya, Nov., 1935; offr. cl II, Mar., 1938; ag. state treas., Selangor, Aug., 1938.

HIBBERD, ERNEST.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth’s Grammar Schl., Wimborne, Dorset; asst. loco. supt., F.M.S. rly., July, 1919; dist. loco. supt., Grade II, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1926; do. grade I, July, 1928; sr. running asst., Aug., 1932; ag. asst. ch. mech. engrnr., cent. wrkshp., July, 1936.

HIBBERT, JOHN GEOFFREY, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. schl. mod. languages (French), 1912; pvt., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) Divn.; ment. in desps.; attd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr. clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; asst. claims clk., Jan., 1922; attd., Br. govt. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; temp. admstv. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., Feb., 1930; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols., Oct., 1930; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934; pvt. sec. to parl. under sec. of state for cols. (Lord Plymouth), 23rd Sept., 1935; ag. prin., 11th Jan., 1936.

HICKS, ALEXANDER, M.C., M.A.—B. 1884; asst. chf. inspr., schls., N.S.W., Dec., 1929; asst. under sec., dept. educn., Aug., 1930; asst. U.S. & asst. dir., educn., Feb., 1933; also supt. of tech. educn., Feb., 1936; mbr., pub. serv. bd., Sept., 1939.

HICKS, ARTHUR HAXELL.—B. 1894; ed. Cardiff High Schl.; entd. C.A. office after compet. exam., 1913; mil. serv., 1914-19; seconded to G. Coast as sec., harbr. dept., 1925-28; asst. head, pay dept., Feb., 1930; appts. dept., Oct., 1930; finance dept., 1932; sec. to colonial savings banks comttee., 1934; dep. head, stores dept., 1935.

HICKS, ERNEST CRANFIELD, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1898; Eurpn. mast., S.S., Sept., 1921; do., Kedah, Jan., 1923; ag. supt., educn., Nov., 1929; headmast., High Schl., Klang, Apr., 1930; inspr., schls., Johore, Aug., 1931; do., Pahang, June, 1935; senr. educn. offr., Grade B, Malayan educn. serv., 1937; ag. prin., Sultan Idris train. coll., Jan., 1938.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M., O.B.E. (1938); Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1917; 1st grade admstv. offr., Apr., 1921; seconded as dep. lab. comsnr., 1930-31; dep. prov. comsnr., Sept., 1934; ag. prov. comsnr., various periods, 1935-37.

HIDE, EDGAR WALTER.—B. 1893; ed. Muncpl. Secndy. Schl., Eastbourne; P.O., Gr. Britain, 1910; R.E., Sp. Res., France, 1914-19; supt., grade II, P.O., Penang, Nov., 1919; do., S’pore, Nov., 1920; do., Malacca, Dec., 1924; ag. contrlr., P. & T., Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1927; do., N. Sembilan and Malacca, July, 1930; do., Perak and Dindings, Feb., 1932; contrlr., Nov., 1932; P.M.G., Johore, Apr., 1937.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew’s Coll. and R. Vetv. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923; senr. do., 1938.

HILL, ALEXANDER GEORGE GLENDON, B.A., Agr.B., A.R.C.Sc.I.—B. 1906; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; supr., agr., Nigeria, 1923; botanist, Nigeria, 1926; senr. geneticist, sugarcane research stn., Mauritius, 1929; sent to Java to study cane breeding methods, 1930; ag. dir., sgr., Mauritius, Oct., 1930 to Mar., 1932; dir., E. African agrl. research station, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1937.

HILL, HUGH CHARLES NORWOOD, M.C. (1918)—B. 1890; ed. Dulwich Coll., prober., N. Rhodesia, 1914; served with N. Rhodesia Rifles, German E. Africa, 1914; served France, lieut. Welsh Guards, 1916-18; asst. dist. comnur., 1919; dist. comnur. and mag., 1923; ag. prov. comnur. and mag. cls. I., 1934; ret. 1936; comnur., Turks and Caicos Is., 1937.

HILL, HON. LIONEL LAUGHTON, (S. Australia)—B. 1881; comnur. of pub. wks., min. of educn. and min. of industry, S. Australia, 1924-26; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1926-27 and 1930-33; agt.-genl., 1933-34.

HILL, REGINALD DYKERS RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray’s Inn), 1921; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.’s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. clk. and 2nd A.C.S. on various occasions, 1928-29; mag., 1933; res. mag., Jamaica, 1937.
HILL, STANLEY OLIVER, L.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I., A.R.San.I.—B. 1893; ed. Royal Grammar Schl., Guildford and Liverpool Univ.; mily. serv., R.F.A., 1915-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

HIME, CAPT. ERNEST HOLLEY.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Sch. and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; asst. supervr., cust., F.M.S., July, 1923; supt., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1925; sr. supt., F.M.S., Aug., 1933; do., Port Swettenham, Oct., 1935; ag. dep. comsnr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1937.

HIMSORTH, ERIC.—B. 1905; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L. (Oxon.), LL.B. (Lond.); cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Feb., 1929; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; dep. regisr., Feb., 1931; attd. treasy., 1932; asst. head sanry. dept., 1932 and 1935; 2nd pol. mag. and 1st clk., Kowloon, Apr., 1936; pol. mag., Kowloon, Mar., 1937.

HINDS, THOMAS STEPHEN.—B. 1900; ed. Oratory Schl.; cust. asst., Nyasaland, 1927; dep. collr., cust., and commr., excise, Mauritius, 1937; ag. collr., cust., and harb. mast., Dec., 1938-Aug., 1939; dep. contr., cust., Nov., 1939.

HINGLEY, ANTHONY CAPPER MOORE, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1908; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1931; attd., finan. sec's. office, Jan., 1932; do., Kandy kach., Aug., 1932; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro June 1933; pvt. sec. to gov., Apr., 1934; asst. gov't agt., Galle, Apr., 1937.

HIPWELL, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1888; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, July, 1914; lieut., R.E., Oct., 1918; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., July, 1919; Johore, Oct., 1919-Feb., 1924; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1928; sr. exec. engrnr., Aug., 1935.
HIRST, HAROLD RUPERT—B. 1903; ed. Cranleigh; Kenya treasury-ol., Sept., 1925; asst. treas., Mar., 1928; asst. asst. treas., Mar., 1934; seconded to Aden as ag. finan. offr., Feb.-Oct., 1939.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Sttlms., Oct., 1919; attd., treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923; dist. offr., 1936.

HITCHEN, CHARLES STANFIELD, Ph.D., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., D.I.O., F.G.S., A.I.M.M., M.A.M.I.M.M.E.—Ed. Hurstpierpoint Coll. and R. Coll. of Science; scientific resech. under dept. of scientific and industrial resech., 1933-34; ch. geologist, Kenya Development Ltd., 1934-35; govt. geologist, Kenya, 1935; senr. geologist, 1938.

HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; asst. engrnr., water wks., Trinidad, Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, Sept., 1906; divisional engrnr., Apr., 1925; ag. asst. D.P.W., various periods, 1926-36.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1906; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; asst. regnr., prize et., 1914; asst. regnr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. ass't. to atty-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924; atty-gen., O.F.S., 1929; law advr., Pretoria, 1935; atty-gen., Cape Town, 1937.

HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1884; govt. schmr., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educ. and headmr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928; ag. mag. regnr. gen., Mar.—May, 1929 and Aug., 1933—Dec., 1935.

HOARE, HENRY SAMUEL MALORTIE, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929; pvt. sec. to gov., Feb., 1931; landing survr., cust., Nov., 1932; ag. dep. collr., cust., July-Sept., 1933; temp. attd., C.O., Aug., 1936-Apr., 1937; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, June, 1937; sec. to finance sec., Apr., 1939.

HOARE, COL. FRANCIS RICHARD GURNEY, C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1879; ed. Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 9th Middlesex Regt. and 4th Suffolk Regt. (Volunteers), 1894-1900; served S.A. War, 1899-1900; Queen's Med. and two clasps; ent. Transvaal civ. serv., 1903; 2nd lieut., Transvaal Horse Arty., 1912; seconded to Army Ordnance Dept. as temp. ord. offr., 4th cls. with rank of capt., 1914; do., 3rd cls. with rank of maj., A.O.D., 1915; depot commdr., 1916; temp. lt.-col., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., air equipment to R.A.F. training brig., Canada and U.S.A.; maj. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; staff offr., air equipment; in addn. to duties of inspgr. ord. offr., 1920; lt.-col., S.A. Ordnance Corps, 1933; dir. tech. services, U.D.F., May, 1933; col., S.A. Staff Corps, Sept., 1933.
HOBBES, CAPT. CHARLES CHRISTIE ARTHUR, R.E. (R.A.R. of O.); F.R.I.B.A., A.M.I.Struct.Eng., Chtd. Architect.—B. 1891; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1934.
HOBDAY, JOHN HENRY NEWBOLD, B.Sc. (Lond.), M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1907; govt. vety. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1933; ch. vety. offr., 1935.

HOBDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; on mil. duty, 1914; landing survr., cust., May, 1919; 4th asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Matala, Mar., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Kegalle, Feb., 1927; ditto, N'Elia, Sept., 1928; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1932; contr., lab., Dec., 1935; govt. agt., W.P., July, 1937.

HOBSON, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed. Sherborne; admitted solr., 1929; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, 1935; dep. regist. and dep. marshal, Jan., 1936; admitted Middle Temple, Apr., 1938; called to bar, Nov., 1938; crown coun., Uganda, 1939.

HODGE, HENRY PARNELL, CAPT., R.A.M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), L.M.S.S.A. (Loud.), Grad., L.S.T.M.—B. 1887; med. offr., S.S., May, 1923; med. offr., S'pore, June, 1923; do., K. Lumpur, July, 1923; do., Coast, Selangor, Aug., 1923; do., Pahang, May, 1925; do., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1928; med. offr., Coast, Selangor, Aug., 1933; superscale offr., grade B, May, 1935.
HODGE, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1911; ed. Taunton Schl.; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1931; asst. comnr., pol., Nigeria, 1935.

HODGENS, CHARLES JOSEPH, M.C.—B. 1896; on mil'y. serv., 1916-19; asst. treas., Gold Coast, Nov., 1920; asst. do., Sierra Leone, July, 1926; dep. treas., Jan., 1936; acctnt.-gen., Gold Coast, Nov., 1938.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1884; Impl. civ. ser., acctn.-gen'a. dept., G.P.O., London, 1902; ch. acnt.'s office, G.P.O. O.R.C., 1904; Australia, 1908-9; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924; ag. treas., in 1926, 1927, 1928, 1931, 1933; treas. and comnr., currency, Jamaica, 1934.
HODGES, ALFRED WALTER, A.R.I.B.A., Chartered & Reg. Archt.—B. 1893; ed. and archt. training, Exeter; war service, R.E. & R.A.F., 1914; archt. asst., England, 1919; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.

HODGES, WILLIAM CARLETON, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed. King's Schl., Grantham; lieut., 54th (E.A.) Div'l. Cyclist Co., July, 1917; resigned comnr., Dec., 1921; ch. acct., govt. monops., S'pore, Dec., 1921; ch. acct., excise dept., S.S. Apr., 1935; acctn.-gen., S.S., 1937.

HODGINS, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. N.Z. pub. schls. and Otago High Sch.; entd. N.Z. reglar-gen's. dept., 1901; reglar., births, etc., 1907; dep. ch. electoral off., 1913; oh. electoral off., 1928; also dep. reglar-gen., 1932.

HODGSON, ANTHONY GEORGE OWEN.—B. 1890; ed. Rugby; ast. res., Nyasaland, 1913; 2nd lieut., 1st London Bde., R.G.A. (T.F.), 1912; lieut., 1914; capt., 1916; R. of O., 1922; attd., 1st Bn., K.A.R., N.F.F., G.E.A. campaign, 1915-17; ag. A-de-C to gov'r., 1919; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1920-22; 1st gde. admin. offr., Nyasaland, 1923; ag. ast. ch. sec., 1930-31 and in 1933; ag. ch. sec., June to Oct., 1933 and July, 1936-Jan., 1937; prov. commnr., May, 1934; ag. senr. prov. commnr., Feb.—July, 1935; gov'r. dep. various occasions, 1936; senr. prov. commnr., 1939.

HODGSON, DUDLEY HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Forestry.—B. 1899; ast. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; conserv. forests, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. dir., forestry, S.S. and F.M.S., in 1931, 1932 1933 and 1936.

HODGSON, JAMES LESLIE WILBRAMAH.—B. 1902; ed. Haileybury Coll.; ast. commnr., pol., Nigeria, 1928; senr. ast. supt., pol., 1937; A.D.C. and pvt. sec. to gov', 1934-36; ast. dist. offr., Somaliland, 1938.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educn., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., 68th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educn. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educn., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.
HODKINSON, ERIC THOMAS, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (W.W. Rand).—B. 1904; ed. King Edward VII Schl. and Univ. of Witwatersrand, temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1933; med. offr., 1935.

HODSON, SIR ARNOLD WIENHOLT, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1922), Kt. of Just., Order of Hosp. of St. John of Jerusalem.—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave, Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamand mission, 1906; ag. ast. commnr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. commnr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Juluiland, 1917-18; attcl., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); gov. and commdr-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, Dec., 1926; ditto, Sierra Leone, May, 1931; ditto, Gold Coast, 1934; author "Trekking the Great Thirst," "Seven Years in Southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HOEXTER, HON. OSCAR HENDRIK, K.C. (1929), B.A. (S.A.), B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (S.A.)—B. 1893; ed. Rouxville Schl., Grey Univ., Coll., Bloemfontein and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; admitted to bar, 1918; practd. as adv. of supr. ort. of S.A. at Bloemfontein, O.F.S. till 1938; judge, high crk., S.W. Africa, 1938.

HOFFMANN, WALTER HEIKEL.—B. 1901; ed. B.H.S. Roodebosch and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; clk., inland rev. dept., 1918; inspr., co-op. soc's., 1927; pr. clk., treasy., 1934; ast. acct., 1936; secy., pub. debt comms., 1937; prin. acct., agric. dept., 1938.

HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK, M.A., D.Sc., M.P.—B. 1894; ed. S.A. Coll., High Schl., S.A. Coll., Cape Town, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; prof. of classics, Univ. Coll., Johannesburg, 1917; prin., univ. of Witwatersrand, 1919; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1924; M.P., Johannesburg (North), 1929; min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1933; min. of mines, educn., lab. and social welfare, 1936; chancellor, Witwatersrand Univ., 1938; min. of finan. and educn., 1939.

HOGAN, HON. EDMOND JOHN, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian parly. lab. party, Apr., 1926; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and from Dec., 1929 to May, 1932; joined country party, 1936; min., agr. and forests, 1935.

HOGAN, GERALD GEORGE.—B.1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1915; served Australian Field Arty., Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; ment. in desps.; stip. ap. mag., N. Territory, Australia, 1919; dep. judge, sup. ct., N. Territory, 1920; crown law offr., mandated Territory of New Guinea, 1922; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1933.

HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, M.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, Aug., 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1933; ag. dep. dir., various occasions, 1934-37; dep. dir., educn., Jan., 1937.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S.; M.Inst.C.E., Whitworth Schl.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; ast. engrnr., 1919; dep. head, engrnr. insptn. dept., 1920; ch. insp. engrnr. and head, engrnr. insptn. dept., 1934.
HOGGAN, LIEUT.-COMMISS. C. B., R.D., R.N.R.—Pilot, Kenya, 1924; senr. do., K.U.R. & H., 1936.

HOLDER, FRANK WILFRED, B.A. (Durham)—B. 1897; ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; mem., H. of A. and mem., exec. comtee., Barbados; solr.-gen., Oct., 1936.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Peterel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; ast. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbmr., Hong Kong, 1924; comdr., 1927; offl. mem., leg. coun., 1929; dir., air services, 1929; head, Hong Kong delegn. to Shipping Confe., Simla, 1931.

HOLGATE, MAXWELL RUSSELL, M.A.(N.Z.).—B. 1896; asst. mast., Raffles Schl., July, 1920; sr. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Perak, Nov., 1922; ag. prin., do., Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1930; asst. inspr. schls., Singapore and Labuan, Nov., 1930; headmast., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Dec., 1930; inspr., schls., Malacca, Nov., 1934; senr. educn. offr., Malaya, 1937; senr. educn. offr., spec. gde., May, 1939.
HOLLAND, FRANCIS CHAPLIN, B.Sc.(McGill), A.M.I.C.E., Chd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1890; asst. engr., P.W.D., Malaya, May, 1923; ex. engr., May, 1932; senr. ex. engr., Apr., 1938.

HOLLAND, CAPT. FRANCIS GEORGE LEOPOLD, M.B.E., (1931).—B. 1884; N.Z. educn. dept., 1902-11; Germany, 1912-14; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; served in Macedonia, 1915-19; capt., 1917; ment. in desps.; headmast., govt. educn. scheme, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1920; dep. commr. (temp. and provln.), 1929; supt., educn., 1931; ag. dist. offr. at various times, 1928-33.

HOLLAND, JOHN JAMES, B.Sc., B.E., M.Inst.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy.E.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Galway; asst. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, Aug., 1928; divl. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; title changed to ex. engr., Jan., 1939.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misahoe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. commr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, regiar. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-29; admstr.-gen. and offr. recr., Uganda, 1930; admstr.-gen., offr. recr., pub. trustee, regiar-gen., births, deaths and marriages, regiar., companies, trade marks, business names, patents, designs and trade unions, Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, M.B.E. (1935), Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1938; B.A. (Lond.), F.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Schl., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmast., teacher's training schl., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; prin., govt., secondary schl., 1935; author "Milango ya History" (Vols. I, II and III); "Jiografia ya Unguja na Pemba," A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," "Nja za Kufundisha," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. colmr., E.A.P., Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1888; colmr., June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to adminstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstr., lat Apr., 1903; Jubuland expedn., 1900-1, and Nandi expedn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.I.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. gov'r., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admstr., German E. Africa (Tanganyika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov'r., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; 1st cls., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 6th Jan., 1930; assumed govt., 22nd Mar., 1930; ret., 1936; author of works on the Masai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932 and World Econ. Conf., London, 1935; economic advr., treas., Jan., 1934; sec. for finance, Sept., 1937.
HOLMES, LESLIE BENJAMIN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1905; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; lieut., H.K.V.D.C.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Art'y., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; B.A. (lit. hum.) July, 1921; M.A., June, 1930; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926; asst. prte. sec. to S. of S. for dom. affrs. and for cols. (Mr. L. S. Amery), Feb., 1928; accomp. Sir S. Wilson on visit to W. African cols., Aug. to Nov., 1928; prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928; U.K. delegation to monetary and econ. conf., London, 1933; Imp. Defence Coll., 1934; U.K. deleg. to London naval conf., 1935; seconded as sec. to high commr. for the U.K. in Canada, Sept., 1936; ag. high commr. for the U.K., Feb., 1937 and Nov., 1937-Feb., 1938.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. pvt. sec. to gov', Mar., 1927; office asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1929; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Apr. to Sept., 1930; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1931; dist. judge, Tangalle, Sept., 1932; asst. contr., establs., Apr., 1934; dep. registr., co-op. socs., July, 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1934; dep. collr., cust., June, 1935; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Nov., 1935; ag. dep. collr., cust., Oct., 1936; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1937; do., Hambantota, Mar., 1938.

HOLMES, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O., D.P.H. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), D.T.M., D.T.H. (L'pool).—B. 1899; ed. St. Patrick's Cath. Gram. Schl., Mountjoy Schl., Dublin and Dublin Univ.; health offr., Malaya, July, 1928.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A.(Sydney), Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schl. of Mines (London) in Mining, Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schl. of Mines, 1901; mining engr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.
HOLT, HON. HAROLD ERNEST, M.P.—Mem., Fawkner (Vic.), H. of R., C. of A. from Aug., 1935; asst. min. for supply and development, Menzies miny., Apr., 1939.

HOLTTUM, RICHARD ERIC, M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1895; ed. Bootham Sch., York and St. John's Coll., Camb.; asst. dir., gardens, S.S., Mar., 1922; ag. dir., Feb.-Oct., 1923 and in Feb., 1925, dir., May, 1925.

HOME, BRUCE FORDYCE, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), Dip. Psych. (Edin.).—B. 1896; alienist med. offr., Nigeria, Oct., 1927; asst. med. supt., mental hosp., S'pore, Oct., 1931; med. supt., Aug., 1935.

HONE, CAPT. HERBERT RALPH, M.C., K.C., LL.B. (Hons.)—B. 1896; ed. Vardean Sch., Brighton and London Univ.; enlisted, Inns of Court O.T.C., 1910; 2nd lieut., 18th Lond. Regt., July, 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Jan., 1918; attd. H.Q. 60th Divn., Sept., 1915; adjt./1st Lond. Regt., Feb., 1916; served twice B.E.F., France 1916 and 1917-18; capt. R. of O. (T.A.), 1922; asst. treas., Uganda, Sept., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; went S. Eastern Circuit; offr. recr. in bankey, and regiar., H.B.M.'s high ct., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. res. mag., Zanzibar, Jan., 1926 to Feb., 1927; res. mag., Zanzibar, Mar., 1928; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; ag. solr.-gen., Mar. to Sept., 1930; ag. asst. legal advr., D.O. and C.O., Jan.-Aug., 1933; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Aug., 1933; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; K.C. (Gib.), 1934; commr., revised laws, 1935; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1936.

HONEY, SIR DE STYONS MONTAGU GEORGE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedit. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admin., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acctng. offr., Oct., 1904; gov't. sec., Swaziland admin., Mar., 1907; res. gov't., Swaziland, Jan., 1917; gov', Seychelles, 1928; ret., 1933.

HOODLESS, D.W., L.M.S.S.A., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hona., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; asst. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for col., 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. supt., schl., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; asst. dir., educ., 1927; tutor, Cent. Med. Schl., May, 1930; prin., do., med. supt., cent. lunatic asylum and visiting M.O., Suva gaol, 1936; prov'l. offl., M.L.C., 1938.

HOOGENHOUT, P. IMKER.—B. 1884; ed. Stellenbosch and Cape Town, B.A. and prof. teachers' certifa., 1904 and 1906; headmtr., Jepp Educat.
