Inst., Johannesburg, 1908, and Hoogenhout High Schl., Bethal, 1910; inspr., educ., S.W. Transvaal, 1921; ch. examr. to Trans. educn. dept., 1927; sec. for interior, 1929; chmn., nat. road bd., 1937.

HOOK, RUPERT JOHN, D.F.C.—B. 1898; ed. "Wallingbrook", Chulmleigh, N. Devon; mil'y serv., 1915-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; dist. offr., 1929; admstive offr., cls. II, 1937; ag. prin. ast. sec., secr., 1939.

HOOPER, C. A.—Ch. legal draftsmen, min. of just., Baghdad, 1926-28; pres., ct. of first inst., Baarah, 1928-30; pres., land, Jaffa, Palestine, 1931; seconded as judl. advr., Trans.-Jordan, 1931-36; procureur and advoc. gen., Mauritius, May, 1936.

HOPE, WILLIAM MANSFIELD SANDERSON—B. 1881; asst. supt., refugee camps, O.F.S., 1901; supt., do., 1902; sec. and acct., repatriation dept., O.F.S., 1903; inspr. of acct., do. and govt. relief dept., 1903; examr. of acct., audit dept., 1904; 2nd cls. clk., do., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1909; senr. clk., dept. of contr. and audr.-gen., 1912; prin. clk., do., 1921; senr. inspr., do., 1930; ch. inspr. (expend.), do., 1933; asst. contr., and audr.-gen., Nov., 1935.
HOPKIN, OWEN SHIBLEY.—B. 1897; ed. Epsom Coll., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Apr., 1936.

HOPKINS, HERBERT OXLEY, M.A. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), grad. L.S.T.M.—B. 1895; mil'y. serv. with 7th Battn. The King's Liverpool Reg., 1915-18; asst. bactlgst., Middlesex Hosp., 1926; Malaria resech., offr., inst. med. resech., F.M.S., Sept., 1927; bactlgst., S'pore, July, 1929; med. offr., Tan Toek Seng Hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1932; gov't. bactlgst., S'pore, Jan., 1933; pathologist, Penang, June, 1936; do., S.S., 1938.

HOPKINS, ISAAC MORELAND, B.Sc., (Glasgow).—B. 1902; ed. Bellahouston Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, Jordan Hill Coll. for Teachers; prin., govt. training coll. for teachers, Trinidad, 1935; supt., tech. classes, 1937; mem., bd. of induc. training, 1937.
HOPKINS, JOHN GERALD HEMUS, O.B.E. (1939).—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917; dist. offr., 1921; senr. do., 1939.

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler., asst., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 6th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto., 1923; 3rd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. ditto., 1926; sec. comsrs. of currency, 1928; 1st cls. clk., treasy., 1933; senr. clk., do., 1939.

HOPWOOD, TOM, A.M.I.C.E., M.S.A., Soc.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Collegiate Schl., Rhyl, N. Wales; eng. asst., Cape Town, 1903; asst. engr., Port Elizabeth, 1904-11; engr., govt. irrig. dept., 1911; on active serv. (France), R.E., 1917-19; supt., engr., 1936; el. mem., coun., S.A. Soc. Civ. Engrs., 1937.

HORAN, HENRY EDWARD, D.S.C.—B. 1890; ed. Crescent Coll., Limerick, Beaumont Coll., Windsor and Stubbington House, Fareham; entered Navy, 1906; lieut., 1911; comdr., 1924; capt., 1930; served throughout war in destroyers; after war appts. at Admy., Naval Staff Coll., and Imp. Defence Coll.; has commanded H.M. ships "Durban," "Coventry" and "Barham" as capt.; ch. N.Z. naval staff and 1st naval mem., N.Z. naval bd., 1938.

HORN, SIR ARTHUR EDWIN, Kt. Bach. (1937), C.M.G. (1922), M.D., R.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-01; W. African med. staff, 1904 and served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; rec'd thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for rept. on cerebro-spinal fever in N. Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for rent. on sleeping sickness in Volta River dist. of G. Coast, 1910; seconded for sp. serv. at C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. and mem., exec. and leg. coun., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comsn. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and sany. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sany. Comtee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; consulting physician to C.O., 1928; mem., exec. comtee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, ARTHUR JOHN WARD, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. King Edward's High Schl., Birmingham Univ., (B.Sc. 1914) and Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ.; R.F.A., Sept., 1914 to Oct., 1919; agrl. chemist, Nyasaland, Sept., 1921; asst. dir., agrl. and agrl. chemist, Apr., 1931; ag. dir., agrl., June, 1933 to Jan., 1934, and Feb.-Nov., 1937.

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agrl. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in despa.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schl. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services and vety. pathologist, 1930; dir., vety. serv., Jan., 1933; dir., tssetse research Sept., 1938.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; appt. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 14th Apr., 1930.

HORNE, W. K.—B. 1883; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; war serv., 1915-19; ch. just., Tonga, 1925; compiler, revd. edn., "Laws of Tonga," 1928; ag. ch. just., Fiji and ag. ch. judicial comsnr., W. Pacific, Feb.-Sept., 1928; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1930; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933; doc., S.S., Mar., 1937.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schl.; C.A., Jan., 1908; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engnr. contracts, 1920; tech. offr., "Discovery Comtee," 1923.

HORSFIELD, WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Destone and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hons. mech. sci. tripos, 1921; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921; dist. survr., lands and mines dept., 1932.

HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL, O.B.E. (1931), D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., desgrs.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922; polit. offr. with Anglo-Italian (Somaliland) body, comsn., Aug., 1929-Sept., 1930; head, Anglo-Italian claims sub-comsn., Mar.-Sept., 1930; O.A.G., Somaliland for various periods, 1932-33.

HOWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec., 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confederation with Canada; mem. of ex. comsn., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov't., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comsn. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newfild. centre of St. John Ambulance Assocn.; chrmn. of comtee. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfild. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Coman, established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSKING, ETHELBERT BERNARD, O.B.E., (1932).—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Wadham Coll., Oxford (class schl.); asst. dist. comsnr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913; dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1925; ag. prin. asst. to comsnr., local govt., etc., 1932; ag. comsnr., mines, 1933; comsnr., mines, 1934; senr. dist. comsnr., 1935; ag. comsnr., local govt. lands and attlt., 1936; ch. native comsnr., 1938.
HOSKINS, RONALD DOUGLAS.—B. 1893; merino. marine, 1913-20; Kenya pol., 1922; 2nd and ch. offr., K.U.R. & H., 1922; commdr., K.U.R. & H., 1929.

HOSKYNS-ABRAHALL, THEO.—B. 1896; ed. Epsom Coll.; served with H.M. Forces, France, Salonika, Egypt and Palestine; admstr. offr., Nigeria, 1921; senr. dist. offr., 1936; admstr. offr., cls. I, 1937; dep. ch. sec., 1939.

HOUGHTON, H.E., M.B.E. (1937), F.R.A.S.—B.1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high comsnr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transf'd. to Basutoland serv., Nov. 1923.
HOUSTON, THOMAS JACKSON.—B. 1913; ed. Methodist Coll., Belfast and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1935; cadet, Hong Koug, Aug., 1935; 1st clk. and mag., Aug., 1938; 3rd pol. mag., Mar., 1939.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 1st grade Sesuto exam. cls. B.; clerical asst., Basutoland, Oct., 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., July, 1912; chief clk., master of ot. and registr., July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., July, 1919; dep. assr. comsnr., 1923; asst. comsnr., 1927; ag. govt. sec., Bech. Prot., Mar., 1932 to Apr., 1933; title changed to dist. comsnr., Basutoland, 1936; ag. govt. sec.; dep. resit. comsnr. and govt. sec. 1938; ag. result. comsnr., 1938.

HOWARD, ARTHUR ERNEST SAMUEL.—B. 1891; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; sub-inspr., consuls., Fiji, 1914; dist. comsnr., 1924; asst. sec. for Indian affrs., 1932; seconded as agt. and consul, Tonga, 1933; dist. comsnr., Lautoka, 1937; re-designated admstr. offr., gde. II, 1938; dist. comsnr., W. dist., 1938-39.

HOWARD, HON. ERATIS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Schl., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in maths. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist) 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 26th Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof., civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. astt. acctnt., army acctts. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. astt. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt. Nov., 1916; 1st cls. astt. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supermty. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation comm. treas., as astt. contr. of deptl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921; prin., 27th Aug., 1928; attended Imp. Defence Coll. course, 1933; astt. sec., 1st Apr., 1939.

HOWARD, JOHN CURTIS, K.C.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhbr.), hona., nat. sci.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1915; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; prov. dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1926; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1927-30; temp. astt., legal advisory staff, C.O., Nov., 1931; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, Mar., 1933; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1936; legal sec., Oct., 1936; ch. just., 1939.

HOWARD-FLANDERS, WILLIAM EDGAR.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1927; served with H.M. forces, 1914-18; called to bar, M.T., 1923; mem., south-eastern circ. and cent. crim. ct.; mag., St. Vincent, June, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1928; income tax comsrr., 1928; mein., educn. bd., 1928; ag. col. sec., July, 1928; atty.-gen., Sept., 1928; ag. ch. just., May-Dec., 1928; admstrd. govt., Feb., 1930; astt. registr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1930; ag. registr., 1935; admstr.-gen. and offl. recr., 1936; admstr.-gen., Nigeria, 1937; invalided, 1938; ch. registr. and admstr.-gen., sup. ct., Cyprus, May, 1939; offl. recr. in bey., July, 1939.

HOWARTH, GEORGE FREDERICK LEE, A.M.I.A.E., M.I.T.A.—B. 1901; ed. Glasgow High Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; European fitter, govt. transp. dept., Gold Coast, 1928; capt., Defence Force, 1936; mast., govt. tech. schl., 1938.

HOWAT, CLARENCE HUGH, M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1908; ed. Edin. Acad. and Univ. of Edin.; medallist in med. and clinical med.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1932; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., 1933; civ. surg., Aden, 1939; officiating sur. med. offr. and port health offr., July-Oct., 1939.

HOWE, HON. CLARENCE DECATUR, B.Sc.—B. 1886; ed. Public schls. and Massachusetts Inst. of Technology; prof., civ. engng., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S., 1908; ch., engr., hd. of grain comsrs. for Canada, 1913; el. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1935; min. of rly. & canals and min., marine in Mackenzie King cabinet, 1935.

HOWE, GERARD LEWIS.—B. 1899; ed. Armagh Royal Schl., Ireland, Trinity Coll., Dublin and King's Inn, Dublin; barrister-at-law (King's Inn, Dublin, and N. Ireland, 1923); res. mag., Kenya, June, 1930; crown coun., Gold Coast, Aug., 1934; crown coun., S.S., Mar., 1937; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., July to Nov., 1937; ag. dist. judge (civil), S'pore., Apl., 1939.

HOWE, JOHN WALLACE, Dp. Ag. B.S.A., M.Sc.—B. 1901; ed. Westward Ho! and Univ. of Alberta; in ch. grain experimental plots, prov. dept. of agr., Vermilion (Alta.) 1921; cream grader, dairy branch, Alberta dept. of agr., 1922; instr., dairying, schl. of agr., Alberta, 1925; instr., animal husbandry, Univ. of Alberta, 1930; headmr., govt. farm schl., and supt., govt. stock farm, Hope, Jamaica, July, 1931.

HOWELL, CHARLES GOUGH, K.C.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., Cantab.; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-17; served in France and Flanders, May to Dec., 1915 and ret. on account of wounds in Aug., 1917; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1921; mem., S. Wales circuit; crown coun., Kenya, Nov., 1925; ag. solr. gen., June to Oct., 1930; mem., leg. coun., atty.-gen., Fiji, July, 1931; ag. ch. just. and ch. judl. comsrr., W. Pacific, Apr., 1932 to Jan., 1933; legal advr., F.M.S., July, 1933; atty.-gen., S.S., 1936.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offl. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1927 and Sept. to Oct., 1928; puisne judge, Straits Sttlms, and judge, F.M.S., Jan., 1934; ag. ch. just., F.M.S., July, 1937; puisne judge, S'pore., Sept., 1937; do., Penang, Mar., 1938.
HOWES, R.R.R.B., M.A., LL.D., (Trinity Coll., Dublin), LL.B. (Cape), K.C.—B. 1873; ag. judge, C.P.D., Aug., 1937; judge, Nov., 1937.

HOWITT, CHARLES ROBERTS.—B. 1894; ed. Carlisle Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; seconded to Johore, Dec., 1920; 1st cls. mag., 1922-1924; lieut., V.R., Aug., 1924; 3rd mag., S'pore., Oct., 1925; ag. 2nd astt. sec. (B), S'pore, May, 1926; offr., cls. IV, June, 1926; dist. offr., Jasim, May, 1927 and May, 1928; ag. 1st astt. sec. (B), S'pore, July, 1930; offr., cls. III., June, 1931; ag. 1st astt. sec. (B) and clk. of couns., Aug., 1932; astt. Malayan estabt. office, Aug., 1934; ag. Br. advr., Perlis, Apr., 1935; offr. cls. II., June, 1936; ag. Malayan estab. offr., Mar., 1938.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORST.—Clk., control and audit office, Cape, 1895; examr., 1895: ast. acctnt., 1903; ast. acctnt., contrlr. and audr.-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1916; sec., office of high comsrr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1928; sec. for finance, Nov., 1931; pub. debt comsrr., July, 1938.

HUBBARD, PERCIVAL CYRIL.—B. 1902; ed. Mill Hill and Caius Coll., Cantab. (mod. languages scholarship); B.A. hons., 1923; LL.B. hons., 1924; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., May, 1928; ag. astt. dist. offr., Malaita, Oct., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Ysabel, Jan., 1929; ch. mag. and legal advr., Br. Sol. Is. Prot., Feb., 1929; ag. Br. judge, joint ct. and judl. comsrr. for New Hebrides, 1930-32; ch. mag., Palestine, 1934.
HUDD, HON. SIR HERBERT SYDNEY, K.B.E., M.C.—Capt. A.I.F.; Great War; represented dist. of Alexandra in H. of A., S. Australia; comnr. of pub. works, min. of rly., and min. of marine, 1933-38.

HUDSON, HON. ALBERT BLELOCH.—B.1875; ed. Portage la Prairie, Manitoba, LL.B., (Man.); called to Manitoba bar, 1899; bencher, Law Soc., Manitoba, 1913; K.C., 1914; mem., Manitoba legislature, 1914-20; atty.-gen. (Man.), 1915; resigned, 1917; mem., Ho. of Co. Canada, 1921-25; just., sup. ct. of Canada, 1936.
HUDSON, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1883; H.M. postal serv., 1898-1913 and 1919-20; dist. supt., Nigeria, July, 1913; B.E.F., France, 1915-19; postmr., Nyasaland, 1920; asst. P.M.G., 1934; P.M.G., 1937.

HUDSON, JOHN RICHARD, B.Sc. (Vety. Sci.), M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1904; ed., King Edward VI Grammar Schl., East Retford and R. Vety. Coll., London; vety. offr., Kenya, 1926; vety. resch. offr., 1928; dep. dir. (labv. services), 1937.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES, M.O.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, civ. serv., Mar., 1920; addt. to Colombo kach., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Mar., 1921; asst. settlmt. offr., 1922; office asst., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; asst. settlmt. offr., May, 1925; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1929; do., Matare in addn., Sept., Dec., 1932; asst. settlmt. offr., Dec., 1934; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Feb., 1935.

HUDSON, ROWLAND SHEPPINGTON.—B. 1900; ed. St. Edward's Schl.; govt.; probnry. flying offr., R.N.A.S., 1918; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1918-19; clk. and probnry., N. Rhodesia, 1919; asst. native commnr., 1921; native commnr., 1926; dist. offr., 1928; asst. ch. sec., native affrs., 1936; asst. ch. sec., 1939; ag. admstv. sec., Jly., 1939-Jan., 1940.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1932), O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privately and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff, cont. telegraph, London, 1942; dept., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory admstrn., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUETT, FRANCIS JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Anerley Coll.; ag. supvr., cust., P. Swettenham, July, 1921; do., P. Buntar, Aug., 1921; do. T. Anson, Apr., 1923; supt., cust., P. Swettenham, Mar., 1924; do., P. Dickson and harbour, in addn., Jan., 1927; supt., chandu monop., N. Sembilan and supt., cust., in addn., Nov., 1930; sr. supt., trade and cust. dept., F.M.S., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; dep. commnr., do., Apr., 1933; do., Perak, Mar., 1935; title changed to asst. comptlr., cust., Dec., 1938.

HUGGARD, SIR WALTER CLARENCE, Kt. Bach. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honouram and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. puisne judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. puisne judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revid. edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1925; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; az. col. sec. and gov.'s dep., Aug., 1927; atty.-gen., S. Shtltns., June, 1929; ch. just., 1933; ret., 1936; re-apptd. as pres., sp. cts. in Swaziland and Bechuanaland Prot., judl. commnr. in Basuto-land and legal advr. to high commnr. for Basuto-land, the Bech. Prot. and Swasiland, Oct., 1937.

HUGGINS, HASTINGS DUDLEY, M.Sc., Dip. Agr., I.C.T.A.—B. 1905; ed. Antigua Gram. Schil. and Cornell Univ.; asst. mast. Antigua Gram. Schil., 1923; imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1923; asst. field man., sugar expt. stn., Br. Guiana, 1926; asst. agrl. supt., dept. agr., 1928; agrl. supt., 1934; confee., agrl. economists, Scotland, 1936; agrl. economist, Br. Guiana, 1939.

HUGGINS, CAPT. JOHN, C.M.G. (1939), M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schil. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July, 1923; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, May, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, Oct., 1920; 4th asst. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; passed cadet Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; reovr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; pte. sec. to high commnr., May, 1926; offr. cls. III., Aug., 1928; sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. II., Aug., 1933; asst. advr., Kedah, July, 1933; col. sec., Trinidad, 1938; gov't. dep., Sept.-Nov., 1938; ag. gov', Nov., 1938-May, 1939.

HUGHES, FREDERICK EDWARD.—B. 1906; ed. Dean Close Schil. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Forestry), 1927; post grad. course, Imp. For. Inst., 1927-28; asst. consvl., forests, Gold Coast, July, 1928.
HUGHES, GEORGE SPENCER, B.A. (Dub.), LL.B.(Dub.)—B. 1887; Barrister-at-law; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; dist. offr., cls. II., 1919; ag. res., 1923; cls I., admstv. serv., 1930.

HUGHES, HERBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, (schol.), and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A. (1911), M.A. (1914); ordained, 1915-16; appd., educn. dept., Jamaica, 1925; examn., 1925; secy., bd. of educn.; ag. asst. dir., educn., various periods; supervising inspr., secondary schls., sec., Jamaica schls. commn. and Rhodes scholarship selection commn., 1933.

HUGHES, TREVOR DAVIES, Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1901; ed. King's Coll., Univ. of Lond.; cadet M.C.S., Dec., 1925; offr. cls. V., Dec., 1925; offr. cls. IV., Dec., 1932; col. legal serv., crown coun., S.S., Jan., 1937.

HUGHES, RT. HON. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916), K.C., M.P.—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1916; prime min., and atty.-gen., Oct., 1916; Canadian priv. councillor, Feb., 1916; prime min. and atty.-gen., Nov., 1916; ditto. Feb., 1917; ditto., Jan., 1918; resigns! Feb., 1923; min. for health and repatriation, Lyons miny., 1934 and vice-pres. federal exec. coun.; resigned, 1935; min. for external affrs. and territories and vice-pres., exec. coun., Lyons miny., Nov., 1937; min. for ext. affrs., Lyons miny., Nov., 1938-Apr., 1939; min. for ext. affrs., Page miny., 7-26 Apr., 1939; atty.-gen. and min. for industry, Menzies miny., from Apr., 1939.
HUGO, FRANCOIS DANIEL, B.A.—B. 1883; inspr. schls., Natal, Apr., 1916; ch. inspr., schla., Jan., 1931; dir. educn., Aug., 1931.

HUMFREY, ERIC BERTHAM.—B. 1903; ed. Cranleigh; constable, Kenya pol., Jan., 1926; ass't inspr., 1929; ag. ass't supt., 1931-32; local commndt., pol., Cyprus, June, 1933; title changed to supt., pol., Mar., 1938; ag. comsnr., various periods, 1935-38.
HUMPHERSON, Leslie Herbert.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Sch., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; ass't dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1925; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1931.

HUMPHRYS, Lieut.-Coll., Sir Francis Henry, G.C.M.G. (1932), G.C.V.O. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. African War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); ent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; pvt. sec. to ch. comsnr., N.W.F.P., 1904; ass't comsnr., Hazara, 1907; dep. comsnr., Bannu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N. Waziristan, 1915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (despa.); served European War in Europe as pilot R.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khvber, 1919; dep. foreign sec. to govt. of India, 1921; 1st envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary at the court of H.M. the King of Afghanistan, Jan., 1922; high comsnr., Iraq, 1929.

HUMPHRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treas., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treas., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas., from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.
HUNT, John Anthony.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.), B.A., 1928, M.A., 1935; oil exploration geologist, 1928-36; cadet, Malacca Land, Oct., 1936.

HUTCHISON, PATRICK HAMILTON.—B. 1902; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Edin. Univ.; M.A., 1924; LL.B., 1925; admitted solr., Feb., 1927; asst. admstr. gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. admstr. gen., off. recr., pub. trustee, regar., companies, trade marks, patents, designs, trade unions, registr. gen., births, deaths, marriages, house tax and estate duty commrs., 1933-34.

HUTT, ALEXANDER MACDONALD BRUCK.—B. 1904; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, S.A., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (English lit.); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; ag. dist. offr., May, 1932; seconded, secret., Mar., 1935; dist. offr., Jan., 1938.
HUTTON, ARTHUR JAMES SCOTT.—B. 1891; ed. Allen Glen's; sr. asst. archt., M.P.W.S., Feb., 1936; ag., govt. archt. S.S., 1936 and 1939.
HUTTON, THEODORE RALPH, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ag. exec. engnr., various occasions, 1922-27; exec. engnr., 1928-32; sr. exec. engnr., Jan., 1936.
HUXHAM, HAROLD JAMES, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1889; income tax advr., Ceylon govt., Aug., 1929-Apr., 1930; commr., income tax and stamps, Ceylon, Apr., 1932; finan. secy., July, 1934.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, O.B.E. (1931), F.R.Hist.S.—B.1875; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Gram. Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1895-98; supplementary estab., secy.'s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of sec., miny. of infrn., Apr., to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; asst. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contr., lab., Dec., 1921; in charge, dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contr., permits, Apr., 1924; ch. immigrn. offr., Oct., 1926; sec., cent. town planning comm., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comm., chmn. comm., on rates of wages for govt. employees; dep. chmn., lab. standing comm.; dir., immigrn., Jan., 1932; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People," "Palestine, Old and New," "British Projects for the Restoration of the Jews," etc.

HYDE, ANTHONY, M.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1900; ed. Westminster Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford (exhbr.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; attld., dist. office, Alor Gajah, Malacca, 1926; pte. sec. to Gov., 1929; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1931; ag. pte. sec. to partly U.S. of S. for the Cols., Aug.-Sept., 1933; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (B.) S.S., Nov., 1933; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1936; dist., offr., Kuantan, July, 1937.

ILLANGAKOON, JOHN WILLIAM RONALD, K.C., B.A. ("antab."), Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1885; crown coun., Ceylon, Oct., 1924; dep. solr.-gen., June, 1932; solr.-gen., July, 1935; atty.-gen., Oct., 1936.

ILSLEY, THE HON. JAMES LORIMER, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (Digby-Annapolis-Kings).—B. 1894; ed. Somerset pub. schl., Berwick High Schl., Acadia Univ., Wolfville (Arts) and Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax; degrees of B.A. (Acadia) and LL.B. (Dalhousie); barrister-at-law; elec. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of national rev. in King's cabinet, 1936.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. regar., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

INGLEBOW, CEDRIC FRANCIS.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Aug., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office asst., Katnapura kach., Mar., 1924; ditto, Jaffna kach., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, N'Eliya, Dec., 1927; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Oct., 1930; ast. attitl. offr., Mar., 1931.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD, C.M.G. (1939), O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., Sept., 1914 to May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., Dec., 1916; min. of nat. serv., Dec., 1917; min. of lab., demob. and resettlement dept., Nov., 1918; F.Z.S., May, 1919; ast. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Société d'Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif., acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; 2nd ast. sec., Jan., 1926; prte. sec. to ag. high commnr., Apr. to June, 1925; ditto to Br. res., Nov., 1925 to Aug., 1926 and from Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. prte. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, Nov., 1925 to June, 1926; elk., prot. coun., Feb. to June, 1926; edr., Off. Gaz., in 1924, 1925 and 1927; elk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug. to Oct., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; mem. Pemba educn. comtee. and teaching of Koran comm., 1924; mem. museum comtee., 1924 and ag. chmn., Aug., 1926; cls. IV., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1927; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1927; chmn., museum reorganisation comtee., 1928; chmn., comen. of enquiry, med. and health dept., 1928; mem. schla. comtes., 1928-29; vice pres. bd. of dira., Mauritius Inst., 1929; mem., R. Coll., comtee., 1929-31; ag. ast. prin., C.O., 1929-30; ag. recr.-gen., June-July, 1932; ag. col. sec., Jan.-May, and from Aug., 1932; polit. advr., Aden, 1934; ag. polit. secy., Sept., 1934; res. advr. to Quaiti sultan of Shihir. and Mukalla and to Kathiri sultan of Seiyum. (headqrs., Mukalla), 1937; part author, "Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History" (1924), "Schl. History of Zanzibar" (1925), and "Aya Za Kursani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate" (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926), "Guide to Swahili Examinations" (1927), "Zanzibar, Its History and People" (1931), "School History of Mauritius" (1931), "School Geography of Mauritius" (1932), and various pamphlets and articles.

INNES, RT. HON. SR JAMES ROSE, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reptd. Cape div.; atty.-gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comtee. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910-; ch. just. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., Mar., 1927.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, C.M.G. (1929), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Schl. and St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; house physician, Bethlem Royal Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1905; senr. sany. offr., Nigeria, 1914; asst. dir. of med. serv., Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and sany. services, Sierra Leone, 1924; dir., med. and sany. serv., Gold Coast, Mar., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

INNISS, KNOLLYS ULRIC ALEXANDER, M.B., Ch. B. Edin.—B. 1882; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912; ag. surg.-gen., Aug.-Dec., 1934; S.M.O., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1937.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and Bucks. L.I., May, 1910; invalided with rank of capt., Jan., 1918; asst. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to Lord Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, Feb., 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1903; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., July, 1938.
IRISH, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1879; ag. dir., soldiers' attilts., N.S.W., 1920; under-sec. and perm. head, lands dept., 1933; western lands commr., 1934.

IRONSIDE, GEN. SIR (WILLIAM) EDMUND, G.C.B. (1938), K.C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1880; ent. R.A., 1899; capt., 1908; major, 1914; staff capt., S. Africa, 1908-09; brig.-maj., 1909-12; G.S.O., 3rd grade, 1914; 2nd grade, 1915; 1st grade, 1916; brig.-gen. commdg. 99th Infy. Brig., 1918; commdr-in-ch., allied troops, N. Russia, 1918-19; commdr., Ismid force, 1920; N. Persia force, 1921; served S. Africa, 1899-1902 (despa., Queen's med., 3 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European War, 1914-19; commdr., Staff Coll., Camberley, 1922-26; commdr. 2nd divn., Aldershot, 1926-28; commdr., Meerut dist., India, 1928-31; lieut., H.M.'s Tower of London, 1931-33; qmr.-gen., India, 1933-36; G.O.C.-in-ch., Eastern commd., 1936-38; A.D.C. to King since 1937; commdr.-in-ch., forces in Middle East, 1938; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1938-39.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.Sc. Ag., Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; B.Sc. agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1894; ed. Dunfermline High Schl.; bd. of agr., Scotland, 1913; served in France, 1915-18; capt., Lancs. Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V., July, 1921; asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, May, 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., July, 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., May, 1926; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B) S.S. and clk. of couns., Mar., 1928; dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., July, 1930; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A) S.S., Jan., 1934; offr. cls. II., July, 1935; ag. sec. to high commr., June, 1937.

IRVINE, HON. SIR WM. HILL, G.C.M.G. (1936), K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.M.—B. 1858; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. H. of R.C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept., 1914; chief just., Victoria, 1918-35; lieut.-gov., Victoria, May, 1918; ag. gov. from June, 1931 to May, 1934; ret., 1933.

IRVING, FRANCOIS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Sch., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; German S.W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1914-15; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to admtn. of Mafia Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916, to May, 1919; asst., cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. asst. compt., cust. for various periods, 1920-22; asst. compt., cust., Dec., 1922; ag. compt., cust. for various periods, 1924-37; senr. cust. offr., 1938.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD, M.B.E.—Ed. at Charterhouse, and Handelselehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S.A. (medal and four clasps); ch. clk. to cent. repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; prov. sec.'s dept., 1910; lieut., 16th M.R., 1913; seconded for duty as censor, O.F.S., 1914-19; ment. in despatches, 1917; M.B.E., 1918; ch. clk. to prov. sec., 1924; asst. prov. sec., O.F.S. prov. adminstr., June, 1936; prov. sec., O.F.S., Nov., 1935.

IRWIN, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1865; ed. Shelburne Acad.; elec. mem., H. of A. of Nova Scotia for Shelburne County, 1906; re-ele., 1911-1916-20; defeated, 1926; speaker, H. of A., 1917-25; mem. old age pension bd., Nova Scotia, 1933; lieut. gov., Nova Scotia, Apr., 1937.

IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; col. audt. dept., Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Sept., 1937.

IRWIN, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1907, ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Inn of ct. of N. Ireland, 1932; practised at bar, 1932-36; dist. mag. Gold Coast, Oct., 1936; ag. crown couns., Jan.-Mar., 1939; ag. ch. registr., sup. ot., Mar.-Aug., 1939.

ISAACS, Rt. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921), G.C.B. (1937), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1905-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., Mar., 1930; gov. gen. of the Commonwealth of Australia, Jan., 1931-36.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon) (hon.).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; Nigerian Land Contingent, 1914; attd. polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika admtn., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. senr. commr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. and leg. couns., in 1927, 1929, and 1931; Swahili examr.; dir., educn., 1931.
IVES, HON. WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta., Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.

IVESY, COMMIR. A. V. P., R.D., R.N.R.; M.Inst.T.—B.1894; Royal Navy,1915; sub.lieut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; commdr. (ret. list), 1935; Cameroons expedy. force, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; commanded Torpedo boats, 1918; commanded H.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, China, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. mast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 1930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935; dir., marine, 1935; el. younger brother, Trinity House, 1937.

IZARD, H.—Aast. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; war serv., 1915-19, E. Africa, Egypt, Mesopot., Persia; dist. offr., 1921; junr. ast. sec. and clk. of couns., 1923; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1929; senr. dist. comanr., Kenya, July, 1936; offr.-in-ch., Turkana, Feb., 1937; comanr., mines, Dec., 1937.

JACK, HENRY WALTER, M.B.E. (1930), D.Sc., B.A.—B.1891; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork (scholar); B.A. (econ.), 1911, B.Sc. (biol.), 1912; agr. instr., F.M.S., 1914; agr. instr., 1915; ec. botanist, S.S. & F.M.S., 1919; mem., comte., on rubber research, 1923; D.Sc. by research, 1925; rep. of Malaya, Pac. So. Cong., Japan, 1926 and in Java, 1929; economic botanist, Mar., 1929; mem., coconut and oil palm research comte., 1929; hon. sec., rice cult. comte., Malaya, 1930; dir. agr., Fiji, 1934; mem., bd. of trustees, Fiji museum; chmn., Luva schl. comte.; conserv., forests, 1936; offr. M.L.C., 1937; publs. on rice, coconuts, oil palm, cotton, rubber, etc.

JACK, MURRAY M.—B. 1893; ed. Peterhead Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; solr., sup. ct., Scotland, 1920; H.B.M. consul. cts., Egypt, 1922-25; war serv., Gordon Highlands and I.A.R.O., 1914-19 (capt., ment. in desps.); dep. registr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1925; asst. admtr. gen. and offic. recor., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; registr., sup. ct., Kenya and E.A. ct. of appl., July, 1929; rep. and coll., E. A. Law Repts., 1930-32; ch. registr., sup. ct., Palestine, 1935; registr., sup. ct., S. Sttlmts., 1938.

JACKLIN, S., M.C.—Accnt., repat. dept., Christiana, Aug., 1902; rev. olk., law dept., Nov., 1902; clk., civ. comanr. off., Pretoria, Nov., 1903; clk., rev. dept., Mar., 1908; prin. clk., Oct., 1906; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1912; instr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. comanr., Johannesburg, Sept., 1919; ch. clk., estabtms., treas., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1920; sec., pub. ser. comanr., Oct., 1921; ret., Oct., 1926; treas., League of Nations, Oct., 1926.

JACKSON, DONALD EDWARD, LL.B. (Lond.).—B. 1892; called to bar (Middle Temple), 1927; practised at bar, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., 1931; Br. Guiana sp. comanr. to Trinidad to investigate working of workmen's compensation ordnec., 1933; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1933 and 1936; ag. regrr., deeds, sup. ct. and West Indian ct. of appl., various periods, 1934-39; senr. stip. mag., 1936.

JACKSON, SIR EDWARD ST. JOHN, KT. BACH. (1933), O.B.E. (1918). K.C.—Ed. Stonyhurst and Beaumont Colleges and Brazenose College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, Oct., 1912; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Oct., 1918; judge of high ct., July, 1920; pres. of lands comanr., 1920; pres., finance comanr., 1923; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1924; atty.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1929; ret., 1936; comanr. to report on immigrn. of workers to Ceylon, 1936; legal sec., govt. of Malta, 1937; govt.'s dep., July, 1938; ag. lieut.-govr., July, 1939; lieut.-govr., Jan., 1940.
JACKSON, GILBERT.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Schl., Guildford and Westminster; asst. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, JOHN.—Ed. St. Mark's Coll. Schl., Chelsea, and King's Coll.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1934; on serv., 1914-19 (U.K., France and N. Russia); admtr. serv., Nigeria, Dec., 1920; transf. to col. legal serv. as asst. judge of high ct., Nigeria, Apr., 1935.
JACKSON, THOMAS ED., M.I.N.A., M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1895; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1924; senr. surv. of ships, 1939.

JACKSON, SIR WILFRID EDWARD FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G.—B. 1883; ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (classical scholar), B.A. 1905; personal sec. to gov., Trinidad, 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and elk. to exec. coun., Bahamas, Sept., 1906, to Oct., 1907; asst. colir., Uganda, Nov., 1907; ag. dist. comanr., Toro, July, 1911 to Apr., 1912; dist. comanr., Apr., 1912; ag. asst. chief sec., July-Dec., 1912; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. chief sec., Aug., 1913 to Feb., 1914, May to Nov., 1914 and from Apr., 1915; col. sec., Bermuda, 19th Feb., 1916; col. sec., Barbados, 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Feb., 1926; ag. gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Mauritius, Mar., 1930; do., Br. Guiana, 1937.

JACKSON, WILLIAM BRAMLEY.—B. 1881; ed. Wrekin Coll., Shropshire and Manchester Univ.; B.Sc., 1903; M.Sc., 1907; asst. inspr. explos., Cape, 1904; inspr., govt. of Union of S. Africa, 1912; ag. ch. inspr., 1922; ch. inspr., 1926.

JACKSON, WILLIAM HERBERT, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.Struct. E.—B. 1896; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; exec. engnr., Sierra Leone, 1929; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1936; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1937-38; asst. D.P.W., 1938; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1938.

JACOBS, CAPT. CHARLES ERNEST HARVEY (late R.A.), B.Sc. (Chem.), B.Sc. (Bot.) Vict., L.C.P. Diploma in Educn.—B. 1886; ed. Brewood Gram. Schl., Staffordshire and Victoria Univ. (Owen's Coll.), Manch.; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., Cranleigh Schl. and Reading Schl.; sr. sc. master, Raffles Inst., S'pore, Feb., 1921; sr. normal instr., in addn., Apr., 1929; sr. master, Raffles Inst., Nov. 1930; headmast., High schl., B. Mertajam, Aug., 1931; do., Victoria schl., S'pore, Mar., 1935; headmast., English coll. of Bahru, Jan., 1938; do., Victoria schl., S'pore, Apr., 1939.

JACOBS, KENNETH CHARLES, A.C.A.—B. 1904; ed. Bedford Schl.; accnt., forest dept., Cyprus, 1928; offl. recr. (companies), 1930; custodian, deportees' property, 1931; treas., accnt., Gambia, 1934; ag. recr.-gen., May-Oct., 1936; asst. recr.-gen., June, 1937; ag. recr.-gen., various periods, 1936-39.
JACOBSON, RUBEN, M.B., Ch.B. (W.W. Rand).—B. 1907; temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1935; med. offr., 1936.

JACQUES, EVELYN BESSIE, M.B., Ch.B. (Bristol), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—Lady med. offr., Seremban, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; do., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1922; do., Ipoh, Apr., 1924; do., Taiping, June, 1926; do., K. Bahru, Sept., 1934; do., Seremban, Nov., 1937.

JACQUES, FREDERICK VIEL, M.B., Ch.B. (Bristol), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1891; ed. Gresham's Schl., Bristol Univ. and Lond. Hosp.; midshipman and sub.-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1909-15; surg. sub.-lieut., 1916; North Sea, 1914-15; E. Mediterranean, 1916-17; med. offr., Malayan med. serv. (Seremban), 1921; health offr., K. Lumpur, 1922; Kinta, 1924; Perak North, 1926; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1927; ag. senr. health offr., Perak, 1928 and 1931; ag. dep. state med. and health offr., Perak, 1933; senr. health offr., Johore, Sept., 1934; state med. and health offr., Pahang, June, 1936; do. Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1937.

JAMES, MAJOR HENRY POPHAM.—B. 1893; ed. South Lodge, Enfield and Berkhamsted Schls.; mily. serv., 1913-19; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstve. offr., cls. II, 1936; cls. I, 1937; ag. sec., S.P., 1938.

JAMES, IAGO EMETE.—B. 1899; ed. "Meithfrinfa" Prte. Schl., Aberystwyth, Town County Schl., N. Wales and Univ. of Wales; B.Sc. (agri. and econ.); served with H.M. Forces, England, Serbia and Bulgaria, 1918-20; agrl. offr., Nigeria, 1926; prim. agrl. offr., 1936.
JAMES, JOHN MORRICE CAIRNS.—B. 1916; ed. Bradford and Balhol Coll., Oxford (lit. hum., pts. I and II); apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., 2nd Oct., 1939.

JAMES, JOSEPH EDWARD, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls. and Mico Coll., Antigua; ag. educnal-offr., Antigua, Nov., 1901; clk., educn. dept. and educnal. offr., Antigua, Apr., 1904; schlmast., St. John's Training Schl., Antigua, Feb., 1913, to June, 1917; sub-inspr., schl., Antigua, Apr., 1914; sub-inspr., schl., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1919; local inspr., schl., Mar., 1927; ag. fedl. inspr., schl., July, 1929 to Jan., 1929.
JAMES, MARSHALL NICOL.—B. 1875; served under the condominium govt., New Hebrides until Mar., 1922; govt. agt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, S. I.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1919; ent. W.O. (temp.) Nov., 1919; ent. C.O. as clerical offr., July, 1926; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Mar., 1931; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1932; ag. prin., 7th Mar., 1938.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, K.T. BACH. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schls., admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. convcn., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C.; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904-1906; chan. univ. of W. Australia.

JAMES, WILLIAM PARRY, A.C.S.M., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1906; ed. Schl. of Metalliferous Mining, Cornwall; sub-inspr., mines and asst. govt. mining engr., S. Rhodesia, 1934; ag. inspr., mines, 1935; inspr., mines and govt. mining engr., 1936; inspr., mines, Cyprus, Apr., 1939.

JAMESON, CLAUDE FRANCIS SPURRELL, M.C., A.C.S.M., A.I.M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camborne Schl. of Mines; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.E., Jan., 1916; inspr., mines, Selangor, Oct., 1919; asst. warden, mines, F.M.S., Oct., 1928; sr. inspr., mines, Ipoh and S. Siput, Feb., 1931; do. K. Lumpur, Mar., 1933; asst. warden, mines, Selangor, Apr., 1933; do. N. Sembilan, in addn., June, 1933; warden, mines, Johore, 1936; ag. snr. inspr., mines, Selangor, Dec., 1938.

JAMIESON, MORRIS, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., A.I.C.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen Schl., and Glas. and Lond. Univ. (Royal Coll. of Sc.); asst. govt. analyst, S.S., Feb., 1922; snr. asst. govt. analyst, S'pore, Aug., 1929; govt. analyst and dy. comsrr., excise, Oct., 1935.

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; chmn., Saamwerk Unie van Natalse Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; mem., Union H. of A. for Vryheid since 1921; speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924-July, 1929; min. of native affrs., June, 1929 to Mar., 1933; speaker, H. of A., May, 1933.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; ag. office asst., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1915; office asst., Kegalla kach., Jan., 1917; do., Jaffna kach., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1923; asst. stimt. offr., Sept., 1928; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, prr. to June, 1930; ag. stimt. offr., May., 1931; offr., cls. I, Jan., 1937; stimt. offr., Mar., 1938.

JARDINE, SIR DOUGLAS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1938), C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; pvt. sec. to ag. high commr., July to Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. asst. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admtn., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expedy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in des.), senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; gov.'s dep. on several occasions since 1927; ag. gov., in 1929, 1931 and 1933; gov., North Borneo, 1933; accredited rep. to Permit Mandates Comsn., 1929, 1930 and 1931; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Sierra Leone, 1937; joint-editor "Hand-book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FRIER, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917; astl. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admstve. inspr., Mosul prov., 1923; on sp. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admstve. inspr., Sulaimani, 1925; do., Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; ch. inspr., land registn. sec., dept. lands and surveys, Palestine, 1936; author "Grammar of Bahdinan Kurmanji (Kurdish)," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY, K.C.—B. 1896; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; astl. dist. commr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; astl. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr. gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; ag. admstr. and ag. ch. just., St. Vincent in 1930 and 1931; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1933; ag. ch. just. and O.A.G., 1934; dep. gov., 1935 and 1936; col. sec., Aug., 1935; admstve. govt., various periods, 1936-39; ch. just., Windward Is. and Leeward Is., 1940.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Feb., 1916; cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, 1930; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Perak, May, 1931; ag. Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933 and Dec., 1936; offr. cls. I B, 1934; ag. under see. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1934; Br. advr., Trengganu, 1937.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes schol., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926; atty.-gen., Natal, 1933; law advr., 1937.
JAYANETTI, KOTUWE NUHANDIRAM DHARMASENA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1916; clk., gen. cler. serv., Ceylon, Jan., 1936; cadet, civ. serv., Dec., 1938.

JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office astt., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930; office astt., Batticaloa kach., Mar., 1933; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1934; sec. to min. for agr. and lands, Nov., 1936.
JAYASUNDARA, LAYARD, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1935; ag. office astt., Nov., 1936; office astt., Apr., 1938.
JAYASURIYA, CHARLES EDWIN PERERA, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1912; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.

JEFF, CART. JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S.; astd. Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1922; astt. prot., Chinese, Seremban, Dec., 1923; astt. cust. dept., Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Apr., 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1930; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932; ag. astt. sec., Chinese affrs., S.S., Aug., 1933; do., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934; do., Malaya in addn., Oct., 1934; offr., cls. II., Apr., 1937; sec., Mui-Tsai comman., Hong Kong and Malaya, 1936; ag. astt. sec., Chinese affrs., Malaya, Oct., 1937; ag. sec., do., Mar., 1939.

JEFF, RICHARD HEDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. astt. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., June, 1913; dist. engrnr., grade III, head office, July, 1916; supervm. dist. engrnr., July, 1919; on ap. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engrnr., grade I, Jan., 1924; sr. dist. engrnr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931; divnl. engrnr. F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932; ag. maint. and new wks. engrnr., Apr.-Dec., 1936; maint. and new wks. engrnr., Aug., 1937.

JEFFERY, CECIL ALBERT, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1888; ed. Clyde schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; clk., govt. house, 1914; pvt. sec. to various ministers, 1915-26; ch. pvt. sec. to prime min., 1926-35; clk., exec. coun. and sec. to cabinet, 1935; served with N.Z. expdt. force; mem., N.Z. delegtn. to Imp. Conf., 1937.

JEFFREYS, MERVYN DAVID WALDOROV.—B. 1890; ed. St. George's, Bulawayo and Exeter Coll., Oxford; Rhodes schol., B.A. (Eng.) Oxon, 1914; Ph.D. (Sc.) Lond., dip. anth., Lond., 1930; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; lieut. W.A.F.F., 1916; served in E. Africa, 1916-19; dist. offr., 1925; anthropological offr., 1930-31; admative offr., cls. II, 1935; ag. astt. judge, 1936.

JEFFREYS, ROBERT SYDNEY.—B. 1888; ed. Cape Town (S. Africa), Bedford Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B. (1909), Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn, 1910); joined adminstr. of N. Rhodesia, June, 1914; astt. native commnr., and J.P., July, 1916; resigned to proceed on active serv., Feb., 1918; rejoined N. Rhodesia adminstr., May, 1919; native commnr., July 1921; seconded to secretariat, Nov., 1921-Dec., 1922; astt. mag., Apr., 1926; dist. offr. (grade II) with powers of mag., Apr., 1928; seconded as astt. to sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1927-Oct., 1928; ag. crown coun., Nov., 1928-Mar., 1929; ag. pol. mag., May, 1930; res. mag., Apr., 1936; ag. puisne judge, Feb.-Oct., 1939.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wilshire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land sltltm. selection board, 1919; astt. sec., E. Africa currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comttee. on governors' pensions, 1927-28; astt. sec., 1st Oct., 1930; astt. under-sec. of state, 1st Oct., 1939.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. astt., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; 1st astt., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief astt., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929; dir. 1932.

JELF, ST. ARTHUR SELBORNE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhr.); 2nd cls., class. moda., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1909; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admative and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; astt. gen. adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., san. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., gen. staff, S. Sltltm. command; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (militt. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comttee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., June, 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, cls. I.B., Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malaya opium comttee., Dec., 1923; pres., estates health comn., Apr., 1924 (reed. thanks of S. of S.); col. sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., for various periods 1925-32; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927; ret. 1935.
JELLEY, HON. JAMES.—B. 1873; mem. leg. coun. for cent. dist. Adelaide, 1912-15; cent. dist. No. 1, 1915-27; chf. sec., S. Australia, 1924-27; chmn., betting control bd., 1937.

JENKINS, ARTHUR OSWALD.—B. 1892; mil. serv., 1914-18; King's Afr. Rifles, 1918-20; astt. dist. commnr., Uganda, 1920; dist. commnr., 1932; senr. commnr., 1938; dep. res., Jan., 1939; ag. res., Buganda, Aug., 1939-Feb., 1940.

JENKINS, EDWARD ENOCH.—B. 1895; ed. Peterhouse, Cambridge, B.A., LL.B., 1922; M.A., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1924; joined South Wales circuit; war serv. (France) 1914-19; lieut., R.F.A. cadet, Nyassaland, Sept., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Apr.-July, 1927; ag. asst. atty.-gen., July-Oct., 1927; astt. regtr., high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov. 1927; crown coun., Apr., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., various occasions, 1931-37; ag. judge, high ct., 1934; solr.-gen., Jan., 1936; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1938; ag. ch. just., June, 1939.

JENKINS, HON. GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ed. Roseworthy Agril. Coll.; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, 1918-24, 1927-30 and since 1933; min. of agr. and local govt., 1922-23; comsrr., pub. wks., min. of local govt. and marine, 1923-24; comsrr., crown lands, and min. of local govt., 1927-30.
JENKINSON, JACK.—B. 1896; army, 1914-19; apptd. Tanganyika pol., 1920; aast. supt., 1927; supt., 1936.

JENKINSON, THOMAS RICHARD.—B. 1893; Cape Col. postal serv., Mar., 1910; postal aast., N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1914; postmr. various centres, 1915-26; ag. acct., G.P.O., 1925-28; survr., 1928; ag. depy. P.M.G., several occasions, 1927-33; aast. P.M.G., Apr., 1933; ag. P.M.G. and chmn., elec'y control bd., in 1934 and 1936; dep. P.M.G., Jan., 1938; ag. P.M.G., June-Dec., 1939.

JENKS, J. C. A.—Ed. Bristol Gram. Schl. and Angers, France; awarded Croix-de-Guerre (Palm) 1917; Order of Saint Stanislaus with Swords (2nd cls.), and ment. in desps., 1919; colonial audit serv., 1920; aast. audr., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1920; aast. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; senr. aast. audr. in charge, Jan., 1926; mem., retrenchment and rev. comm., Zanzibar, 1927; Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar (5th cls.), 1928; senr. audr. in charge, Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1929; audr., Mauritius, 1933; do., Uganda, 1938.
JENNINGS, F. G.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Kenya, 1923.

JENNINGS, PERCIVAL HENRY.—B. 1903; ed. Christ's Hosp.; col. audt. dept., 1926; aast. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1927; aast. audr., Mauritius, 1931; ag. audr., 1932-34; audr., Br. Hond., 1934; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1938.

JERMYN, LANCELOT AMBROSE SCUDAMORE, B.A.(Oxon.), M.R.S.T.—B. 1886; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; do., Northam rd. Eng. Schl., Penang, Jan., 1923; do., govt. Eng. Schl., B. Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1925 and Mar., 1931; do., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, July, 1930; do., High Schl., Malacca, Sept., 1934.
JERRAM, JAMES HAVELOCK, I.S.O. (1837)—B. 1879; ed. Thames High Schl. and Auckland Gram. Schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance office, 1905; acct., 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. man., 1923.

JESSE, WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served Indian Army R. of O., 1916-19; Afghan campaign, 1919; Calcutta Light Horse, 1921-22; apptd. treasy., Kenya, 1924; aast. treas., Uganda, Jan., 1925; aast. currency offr., Uganda, 1927-28 and 1933; aast. treas., Zanzibar, Oct., 1935; aast. currency offr., Jan., 1936; ag. treas. and currency offr., various periods 1936 and 1937; ch. acct. and currency offr., 1938; ag. finan. sec., 1939.

JESSOP, HARRY GEORGE—B. 1888; ed. Thetford Grammar Schl.; Imp. P.O., 1906-1922; mily. serv., Gallipoli and France, 1914-19; Survr. P. & T., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; ch. acct., Apr., 1936; ag. contr., posts, 1938; col. postmr., Gibraltar, 1939.

JEWELL, MORTON, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; conf. clk., 1911; deptl. aast., 1st Apr., 1923; staff offr., establishments, 1st June, 1928.

JINADASA, HETTIARACHCHIE, B.S. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1934; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1936; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1937; addnl. aast. govt. agt., Kalutara, Sept., 1938.

JOBLING, GEOFFREY LIONEL.—B. 1899; ed. Bowen Schl., Brisbane; called to bar, Jan., 1926 (Gray's Inn); served German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A. Co., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; aast. regisr., high ct., N. Rhodesia; aast. offr. recr. in bankey., aast. admin. gen., aast. regisr., lands and deeds, aast. regisr. births, deaths and marriages, aast. regisr. companies, aast. custodian enemy property, aast. contr. local clearing office, aast. regisr., high ct., etc., Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933 and 1934; legal draftsman, Nigeria, 1937; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1939.
JOBSON, ERIC WILLIAM CHARLES, M.B., Ch.B., Certif. Lond. Schl., Hyg. and Trop. Med.—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Kenya, 1924; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1939.
JOHN, SAMUEL SPEEDING.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Four Courts, Dublin, 1914; war serv., 1914-20; admstve. serv., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; aast. judge, high ct., 1934.

JOHNS, BRIAN MAURICE, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Ed.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1901; med. offr., S.S., Mar., 1928; prof. clin. surgery, King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore, Aug., 1928; ag. senr. surg., Dec., 1935.

JOHNS, ROBERT.—B. 1903; ed. Monmouthshire Agr. Inst., Usk, and Univ. Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; N.D.A., C.D.A., C.D.D., A.I.C.T.A.; stock inspr., Tanganyika, 1926; C.O. agrl. research scholar, Trinidad, and Univ. of Reading, 1929-30; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1931; Carnegie schol., 1934; agrl. offr., Fiji, 1939.

JOHNSON, ARTHUR MERVYN CLIFFORD.—B. 1907; ed. King's Schl., Peterborough and privte. tuition; called to Bahamas bar, Jan., 1931; practised 1931-36; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1936; aast. regisr.-genl., Mar., 1937; ag. stip. and circuit mag. and coroner and atty.-genl., 1937; ag. regisr. gen., 1938 and 1939.

JOHNSON, CHARLES WALTER TREVOR.—B. 1893; Fiji serv., 1910; col. sec's office, 1915; 1st cls. clk. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1920; ag. pvt. sec. to ag. gov. on three occasions; seconded for serv. in W. Pacific high com. as dist. offr., Fanning Island, Feb.-Nov., 1922; ch. clk., secretariat, 1927; ag. prov. and dist. comsrr., June-Sept., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1929; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1931; ag. 1st ast. col. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. prn. ast. col. sec. in 1934 and 1935; mem., Fiji mining bd. and mem., advy. bd., cent. med. schl.; asst. col. sec., 1937; offr., M.L.C., 1939.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M., M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1873; aast. clk., post office, Sept., 1914; ch. clk., Jan., 1924; ag. postmr. in 1925 and 1930; asst. postmr., June, 1926; ag. postmr., Aug. to Sept., 1932.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, I.S.O. (1932), A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspr. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of accts. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties, is also a dep. comsrr. of the high comsrr.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regisr. of high comsrr.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas. and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., Nov., 1919; offi. mem., advisory coun., 1927; ag. resdt., comnrr., Apr., 1939.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT, O.B.E. (1938).—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Sch., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; asst. inspr. W. and M., 1914; asst. sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.

JOHNSON, SIR WALTER BURFORD, Kt. Bach. (1935). C.M.G., L.R.C.P., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schl. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1912; seconded for sp. investig. work under yellow fever coman., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1913 to May, 1914; temp. capt., gen. list, 1914-15; tsetse investigator, 1921; specialist, 1921; seconded for serv. on Tsetse fly c.m.s.; dir., med. and sanry. services, Nigeria, Mar., 1929; ret., 1936; supt. leper sttlmnt., Botshelo, Basutoland, 1937.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1936). O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; mem. in dep., 1917; transf. to depot, 111th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mily. accts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. accts. offr., O.E.T., admin., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admin., July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. and advisory couns. for various periods, 1923-32; mem., comitee. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comitee. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926-32; mem., rly. bd., 1928-32; currency offr., Jan.-June, 1928; treas., mem., exec. and advisory couns., currency offr. and chmn., standing comitee. for commerce and industry, 1932; chmn., income tax comitee, 1934; head, trade deleg., to Egypt, 1935.

JOHNSTON, Hon. Harold Featherston, K.C. (1930). B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1875; ed. Wanganui Coll., N.Z. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn (1897) and N.Z. (1898); many years legal practice; pres., Wellington dist. law soc., 1928; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1934; judge, ct. of review of mortgagees' liabilities, 1935-39; served in France with London Scottish (1916-18).

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOKE, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1898; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., Dec., 1920; admin., offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1923; dist. offr., 1934; K.A.R. res., 1936.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT STEWART, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Belfast), D.T.M. & H. (Edg.), D.P.H. (Lond.), Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1898; ed. R. Acad. Inst., Belfast and Queen's Univ., Belfast; war serv., 1916-19; health offr., Malaya, Mar., 1928.

JOHNSTON, WILLIAM—B. 1890; asst. clk., G.P.O., May, 1910; offr., cust. and excise, Aug., 1911; dep. collr., cust., Mauritius, Dec., 1920; sec., harbr. bd., 1922-25; chmn., ditto, 1923-24; sec., tariff advisory bd., 1927-31; chmn., ditto, on various occasions, 1927-30; comanr. of excise for tobacco in addn., 1927-31 and ag. supt., inland rev., May to July, 1929; ag. collr., cust. and harbr., M.L.C., on various occasions, 1923-30; collr.-gen., Jamaica, Jan., 1932; col. sec., treas. and collr., cust., Br. Honduras, 1935; admst. govt., May-Nov., 1937; col. sec. and finan. sec., 1938; admst. govt., June-Sept., 1939.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNCAN, E.D.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1908; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; asst. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cls. II., polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comanr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922; prov. comanr., Apr., 1923; comanr., S. Prov., Aug., 1929; ag. ch. comanr., N. Territories in addn., Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930, June-Oct., 1932; Gold Coast Local Forces Efficiency Decoration, 1939.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGUE CHOLMELEY.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. asst. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; asst. supt., 1927.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A. 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to asst. advisor's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; asst. state audr., chmn., conservancy bd., Khota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1926; sec. to rivers comanrs. in addn., Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, June, 1930; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; hon. A.D.C. to high comanr., Mar., 1931; offr. assignee and regisr., companies in addn., July, 1936; offr., cls. II., June, 1935; dist. offr., K. Kangsar, July, 1938.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mily. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matare, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment comanr., Jan., 1922; addnl. asst. dir., educn., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Eliya, Apr., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925; asst. col. treas., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1929; dep. collr., cust., Sept., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1935; ag. govt. agt., N.C.P., Nov., 1936; ag. dep. finan. sec., Nov., 1937; attd. to the gen. treasy., July, 1938; mem., retrenchment comanr., Aug., 1938.
JONES, EDWARD LLOYD, M.I. MAR. E.—B. 1891; asst. govt. mar. survr., Hong Kong, 1928.
JONES, HON. FREDERICK—B. 1884; ed. Albany Strt. schl., Dunedin and Normal schl., Christchurch; entd. N.Z. H. of R., 1931; postm. gen., min. of tels. and min. of defence, 1935.

JONES, GEORGE ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; asst. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comtee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1919; promoted, under O.-in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYMN ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1938).—Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1909; asst. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; asst. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comitee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and a director of the Victoria museum, 1915.

JONES, GWILYM Howell Gethin, M.Sc., (Wales).—B. 1898; ed. Llanrwst Grammar Schl. and Univ. Coll. of N. Wales, Bangor; army serv., 1917-19; soil survey asst. and lecturer in soil science, W. Midland Prov., min. of agr. and fisheries, 1925; soil research chemist, S. Eastern Prov., do., 1925; ag. senr. agric. chemist, 1931, 1935 and 1939; soil chemist, Kenya, 1931.

JONES, CAPT. NELSON, M.C. and Bar.—B. 1895; ed. Glasgow Univ.; comand., Cameron Highrs., 1915; dischgd. on account of wounds, Apr., 1919; cadet, F.M.S. Oct., 1919; asst. contr., lab., Penang, Mar., 1920; ditto, K. Lumpur, May, 1921; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; attd. dist. office, Klang to act as 1st cls. mag. 1923; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1924; offr. cls IV Oct., 1926; asst. advr., Kukup, May, 1929; ag. emigrn. comanr., Malaya, Madras, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1931; mag., J. Bahrn, Jan., 1933; offr. admistr., Johore, Nov., 1934; offr., cls. II, Oct., 1936; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, Apr., 1937; ag. chmn., san. bd., K. Lumpur, July, 1939.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYMAN.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cape, 1896; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1924; judge, Aug., 1926; edited "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly with H. O. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S.A."
JONES, REGINALD HUGH, M.Sc., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1898; asst. elec. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., 1928; ch. mech. and elec. engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1937.

JONES, RHYS CARADOC, M.B., Ch.B., (L'pool), M.R.C.S., (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lond.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1901; M.O.H., Nigeria, Aug., 1929; health offr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1935; med. port health offr. and inspr., immigrts., Jan., 1936; H.O. urban coun., 1937.

JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solnr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regnr., 1903; chief judge, native land ct., since 1919; was under-sec., native dept., 1922-33 and native trustee, 1933.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B.Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.).—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Schl.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON, C.M.G. (1939).—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. honours, hist. (1903); barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); cadet F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls.V, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917; do., K. Kangsar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu; Jan., 1920; do., K. Lipis, May, 1920; local examir. in law and Malay, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1923; attd. to fed. sec. on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; localexamir. in Malay, Selangor, Sept., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1928; offr., cls. IB, May, 1931; comanr., lands and mines, Johore, Nov., 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., offr., cls. IA, May, 1935; Br. res., Selangor, Apr., 1937; col. sec., S.S., 1939.

JONES, WILLIAM ANEURIN.—B. 1904; ed. Liverpool Inst. and Liverpool Univ.; admitted solnr., sup. ct., England, 1925; advoc. and solnr., sup. ct., Sta. Sttlments., 1929; asst. crown sol., Hong Kong, Feb., 1937; ag. asst. land offr. and dep. regtr., marriages, May, 1937; asst. crown solnr., treas., Oct., 1938; dep. estate duty commr. and asst. collr. of stamp rev., Nov., 1938; supt., inland rev., and estate duty commr., etc., Feb., 1939.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, C.M.G. (1937), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. 'comanr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. comanr., Dec., 1918; dep. prov. comanr., Jan., 1924; prov. comanr., July, 1927; sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. col. sec., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov't dep., Sept., 1932; ch. comanr., N. Territories, 1933.

JONES, WINNE MARTIN ALBAN, B.A., Dip. Ed.—B. 1902; ed. Llandaff Cathedral Schl., Aldenham Schl., Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge and Univ. of Lond.; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Sept., 1929; dir., educn., Grenada, 1932; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Feb., 1937.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhibr.)—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V, offtq. as asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1916; lieut., M.V.I., Dec., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Raub (Bentong), Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Oct., 1926; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1930; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Dec., 1930; mem. Br. delegn. to L. of N. Opium Confce., Bangkok, Nov., 1931; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1932; sec., Chinese affrs., Malaya; offr., cls. IA, Oct., 1933.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; entr. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.
JORDAN, HON. SIR FREDERICK RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.—B. 1881; oh. just., N.S. Wales, Feb., 1934; lieut.-gov., Sept. 1938.
JORDAN, WILLIAM JOSEPH.—B. 1879; ed. St. Luke's Parochial Schl., London; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1919-36; high comanr. for N.Z. in U.K., 1936; represented N.Z. at Empire Parly. Confce., Canada, 1928.

JOSEPH, ERNEST HENRY AUSTIN, A.R.S.M., M.I.M.M.—B. 1884; ed. Clifton Coll. and R. Schl. of Mines; A.R.S.M., 1907; ch. assaver, R. Siamese mint, 1907; various appts., Witwatersrand gold mines, 1909-20; mily. serv., 1914-19; mines dept., Union of S. Africa; asst. inspr., mines, 1920; d.p. do., 1925; M.I.M.M., 1928; inspr., mines, 1931; asst. govt. mining engnr., 1937; dep. do., 1938.
JOUANNO, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1892; ed. Cliftonville Coll., Margate; ch. loco.draughtsman, K.U.R. and H., 1929.

JOUBERT, M. J. A., B.S.A. (Toronto).—B. 1882; pupil teacher, O.F.S., 1897; asst. to govt. agronomist, O.F.S., 1905; itinerant instr., agr., 1910; offr., u.ch. and prin., Glen schl. of agr., 1913; prin., scl. of agr., Glen., 1917; prin., Grootfontein schl. of agr. (Cape), 1924; asst. ch. dir., plant industry, dept., agr., Pretoria, 1929; ch., divn. of agr. educn. and extension, Pretoria, 1932; under-sec., agr., 1933.

JOWITT, HAROLD.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Southampton, B.A., M.Ed., Dip. Ed. (S.A.); headmr., Edendale Training Coll., Natal, 1913; inspr., schl., Natal, 1918; dir., nat. educn., S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1924; dir., nat. development, Apr., 1929; dir., educn., Uganda, Dec., 1934; author of "Principles of Education for African Teachers"; "Suggested Methods for African Schools".

JOY, EDWARD ARNOLD, A.C.A.—B.1901; ed. Preston Grammar Sch.; acct., med. dept., S'pore, June, 1928; acct., P.W.D., S'pore, in addn., Nov., 1933; attd., treas., Sept., 1935; acct., P.W.D. and excise dept., Penang, Apr., 1936; do., S'pore, Sept., 1937.

JOY, GEORGE ANDREW.—B. 1896; ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges; ent. Army, 1914; served in Belgium and France, 1915 and 1916; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; staff gen. headqrs., Egypt and Palestine, 1919-20; S.W. Arabia, 1921 to 1923; ment. in desps., lieut., R.A.R.O., Feb., 1919; attd. to res. commr., New Hebrides, Aug., 1924; dep. commr., W. Pacific, July, 1926; ag. Br. res. commr., Aug., 1927; res. commr., New Hebrides, 1928; temp. attd., staff of col. development advisory comte., C.O., Jan., 1931; res. advr., Mukalla, Aden Prot., 1940.
JOYCE, G. F. W. C.—B. 1897; ent. E.I.O., June, 1912; asst. olk., Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., O.S.O., 1st Jan., 1920.
