JOYCE, BREVET LIEUT.-COL. PIERRE CHARLES, C.B.E. (1920). D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 1st Batt. The Connaught Rangers, 1900; serv. S. African War, 1900-02 (severely wounded, Queen's Med. with 3 clasp, King's Med. with 2 clasp); attd., Egyptian Army, 1907-16 (Egyptian Med. with clasp, 4th cls. Order of the Medjidieh, 4th cls. Order of the Nile); European War, 1915 (Gallipoli); Arab campaign against the Turks, 1916-18; mily. advr. to Amir Faisal, 1917 (ment.in desps. five times, brevet of lieut.-col., C.B.E., D.S.O., Croix d'Officier, Legion of Honour, Arabian Order of the Naidhah, 2nd cls.); gov., E. and S. Desert Provs., Egypt 1920; mil. adviser to Iraq Govt., Mar., 1921.

JOYNT, HENRY RAYMOND, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Bradfield, and Balliol Coll., Oxon. (math. schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, July, 1914; dist. offr., K. Langat, Nov., 1919; dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Mar., 1921; att. to food contr., N. Sembilan in addn., Apr. to July, 1921; on sp. duty, rubber census office, Kuala Lumpur, June, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; second asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; attd. lab. dept., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; commr., lands and mines, Johore, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. I.B., May, 1931; ag. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., May, 1933 and Mar., 1936; contr., rubber, Malaya, Apr. 1935; acctn.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1937; ag. finan. sec., F.M.S., Dec., 1938; finan. sec., 1940.

JUDD, JAMES.—B. 1890; sub-inspr., constab., Fiji, 1915; dist. commr., 1928; ag. ch. pol. mag. and ag. sec. for Indian affs.on various occasions; M.L.C., 1934 and 1937-38; chmn., publicity bd., 1936; do., Suva town bd., 1936; ag. asst. sec. for native affs., 1936; asst. sec. for Indian affs., 1938.

JULLION, STEPHEN ETHELBERT TREGONZA, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1909; senr. olk., secr., Leeward Is., Jan., 1935; ag. clk., exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, June, 1935; clk., do., Antigua, Jan.-Aug., 1936; headmr., Grammar Sch., Dominica, Aug., 1936; in addn., ag. inspr., schls., Dominica, Jan.-Sept., 1937.

JUNNER, MAJOR NORMAN ROSS, O.B.E. (1936), M.C., D.Sc., D.I.C., M.Inst.M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne Univ. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology; 1851 exhib. schol. for Melbourne Univ., 1913; lect. in geology, schl. of Mines, Ballarat, 1919-20; war serv. in France, Italy and Malta, 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. surv., 1920; dir., Sierra Leone geol. surv., 1927; dir., Gold Coast geol. surv., Oct., 1930.

KANAGASUNDARAM, KATHIRAVALOO, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1912; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; office asst., Hambantota kach., Nov., 1936; pol. mag., Kandy, Mar., 1937; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, June, 1938.

KANNANGARA, EDWARD WILMOT.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; attd. to Kandy kach., Aug., 1919; attd. Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, Avissawella, May, 1921; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, Nov., 1924; ditto, Balapitiya, June, 1926; asst. commr., stamps, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1930; sec. to min., agr. and lands, Nov., 1931; clk., state coun., and sec., bd. of ministers, June, 1933.

KANTAWALA, MOHAN HARGOVINDAS, M.A., Cantab., barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn;—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1915; att. Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1916; addtl. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, in add. July, 1915; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, July, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Oct., 1919; pol. mag., Negombo, Jan., 1921; ditto, Panadura, June, 1923; ditto, Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1927; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; dist. judge, Negombo, Mar., 1930; pol. mag., Galle, June, 1933; dist. judge, Matare, Nov., 1933; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, May, 1934; Ceylon trade commr. in India, June, 1937.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; on. mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; chmn., munic. coun., and addnl. govt. agt., Kandy, Oct., 1931; asst. to distress relief commr., Jan., 1935; chmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1935; ag. commr., local govt., pres., local govt., bd. and sec., min. for local adminstr., Nov., 1935.

KAUFMANN, JOHN ERNEST.—Forester, Umtata, 1900; clk., conservator of forest's office, Umtata, 1901; senr. clk., ditto, 1902; asst. dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1903; transf'd to Kokstad, 1912; dist. forest offr., Kokstad, 1912; transf'd to Knysna, 1925; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, eastern conservancy, 1926; conserv. of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1927; ditto, Midland conservancy, 1929; ditto. Eastern conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., E. inspectorate, 1932.

KAUNTZE, WILLIAM HENRY, C.M.G., (1937), M.B.E., B.A., M.D., Ch.B. (Vist.Univ.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H. and T.M.; (1st place and med.), D.P.H. (Vist. Univ.)—W. African med. staff, Jan., 1914 to Jan., 1919; served with W. African Forces, Aug. 1914 to Mar., 1916; seconded for serv., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1916 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps.; pathologist and asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Jan., 1919; dir. laboratory services, Kenya, Apr., 1919; dir., med. and sanv. services, Uganda, Nov., 1932.
KEATES, CAPT. E. H. O., M.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1896; ed. St. Peter's Sch., York; on mily., serv. France and Belgium, 1915-18; Germany, 1919; admstve. offr., N. Nigeria, 1921; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1926.

KEEGAN, R. J.—B. 1901; ed. R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Indian Army; served, N.W. Frontier and Iraq; asst. to commdt., pol. (Br.-divn.), New Hebrides, Sept., 1927; ag. act., Jan. to June, 1928 and Mar. to Nov., 1930; ag. dist. act., Efate and commdt., constab., Nov., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. commdt., constab., Jan. June, 1933; ag. asst. res. comsrr., Dec., 1934-June, 1935; transf. to B.S.I. Prot., Aug., 1936.

KEEN, KENNETH.—B. 1907; ed. King's Schl., Rochester, Guy's Hosp. and King's Coll. London; cadet, Hong Kong, 1930; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1933; asst. head sany. dept. Nov., 1934; mag., Kowloon, Nov., 1935; dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, May 1936; 2nd mag., Hong Kong, Oct., 1936; dist. offr., South, Aug., 1937; mag., Kowloon, Oct., 1937; 3rd asst. coll. sec., Feb., 1938; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., May, 1938; asst. sec., do., 1939.

KEET, MAJOR HUBERT GORDON, D.S.O., M.C., (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1894; sup't excise, S.S., 1909; served Great War, France and Belgium, 1914-18, (twice ment in desps.); asst. contr., govt. monops., S.S., June 1928; ag. asst. supt., do., 1930; contr., govt. monops., June, 1931; senr. contr., May, 1933; contr., govt. monops., Malacca, May, 1934; dep. contr., rubber, June, 1934; J.P., Malacca, Oct., 1934; supt., excise, S'pore, Mar., 1936; supt., cust. and excise, Taiping, Perak, Dec., 1936.

KEET, JOHAN DIEDEK MOHR.—Asst. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knyana, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knyana, Apr., 1917; conservr. of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1923; ch. forest offr. (ch., divn. of forest management), 1932; dir. forestry, 1935.

KEEVIL, L. G. M., M.I.R.S.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1895; ed. pvtly.; served with R.A.S.C. (TF) and R.E. Sigs. (TF) in France, German East Africa and Turkey, 1914-20; R.A.R. of O. and capt., Gold Coast Defence Force; engnr. asst., posts and tels. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; asst. tel. engr., Gold Coast, 1925; tel. engr., 1929; ag. asst. suptdg. tel. engr. in 1933 and 1934.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1927), Commr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bim-bashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan-Aug., 1916; Darfur Exped. and first inspr., Unkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. comsrr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstr., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; asst. dist. comsrr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. comsrr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernmy. offr., cls. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III., 1925; currency comsrr., Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; European judge, Kedah, July, 1933.

KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Seconded from London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd asst. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1924; postal asst. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. D.P.T., June-Oct., 1927; surve., posts and tels., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; asst. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika postal serv., 1935; regional dir., do., 1937.

KELLY, EDWARD JAMES GLEESON, M.C.—B. 1894; admstve. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1919; ag. Br. consul., Boma, 1929; admstve. serv., cls. II., 1933; ag. res., 1934; admstve. offr., cls. I., 1934; sec., S. Provs., 1936-38; admstve offr., staff gde., 1939.

KELLY, JOHN O'BRIEN; B.A. (Mod.) Ll.B., (Dublin Univ.)—B. 1907; solr. (I.F.S., 1931); pvt. practice, 1931-36; dep. registr., sup. ot., Kenya, 1936; also deputy offl. recr. and ex-officio agt. for pub. trustee, Coast prov.; ag. regt., sup. ct. and ag. registr., H.M. ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1937.

KELLY, ROBERT WILLIAM CREIGHTON, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.)—B. 1888; med. offr. i/c schla. and venereal diseases, S.S., Apr., 1923; med. offr., social hygiene, May, 1926; health offr., May, 1927; chief med. offr., social hygiene, S.S. Feb., 1928.
KELLY, THOMAS DWYER, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1880; under sec. and compt., accts., treasy., N.S.W., July, 1936; mem., pub. serv. bd., May, 1938.

KELVIN-STARK, DAVID.—B. 1905; ed. Taunton; B.A. (Lond.), 1928, F.R.S.S.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1928; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1931; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1934; senr. asst. col. treas., 1935; dep. estate duty comsrr., 1935; senr. asst. col. treas. in addn., Mar.-July, 1937; supt., inland rev., Jan.-Apr., 1938; stats. offr., 1939; asst. finan. offr. (exchange), 1939.
KEMP, ALFRED.—B. 1904; ed. Manchester Cent. High Schl.; matri., 1919; G.P.O. engnr. dept., 1920; asst. traffic supt., G.P.O., 1923; traffic supt., G.P.O., 1932; ch. telephone supt., Palestine, 1935.
KEMP, ERNEST HAROLD.—B. 1898; ed. S.A.; E. Africa, 1916-17; Witwatersrand mines, 1918-25; Barberton mines, 1926; Leeuwoort tin mines, 1927; mines dept., Union of S. Africa, 1937.

KEMP, SIR JOSEPH HORSFORD, Kt. Bach. (1927), C.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1918)—B. 1874; B.A., Cape Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land office work, New Territory, 1899; registr., land ch., 1900; ag. 2nd. pol. mag., 1900-04; ag. 1st. pol. mag., 1901; asst. sec., sany., bd., 1904; dep. registr. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1904; ag. registr., sup. ct., offl. adminstr. offl. trustee, and registr., companies, 1907; ag. offl. recvr. in bank., Apr., 1907-08; ag. 1st. pol. mag., 1908-09; head, sany. dept., 1908; registr. sup., ct., offl. adminstr., offl. trustee, and registr., comp., 1909; crown solr., 1911; ag. puisne judge, 1913; ag. atty.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., 1915; ag. ch. just., Apr. July, 1930; ch. just., Aug. 1930; ret.

KEMP, THOMAS FRENCH HEWITT.—B. 1900; ed. Bedford Schl.; Imperial serv., 1914-19; ag. regisr., criminals, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1921; asst. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1922; 1st asst. comsnr., Selangor, Feb., 1924; adjt., pol., headqrs., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1926; ag. dep. comsnr., pol. and ch. pol. offr., Neger Sembilan, Apr., 1936; supt., pol., S.S., Jan., 1937.

KENNAN, THOMAS BHERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Collet Court, Lond. and Worcester Cathedral Schl.; cler. asst., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War; asst. comsnr., 1929; title changed to dist. comsnr., 1936; ag. govt. sec., 1938; 1st asst. sec., 1938.

KENNEDY, DONALD GILBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Waitaki Boys High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; housemr., Dannevirke High Schl., N.Z., 1920; asst. mast., Grammar Schl., Suva, 1921; headmr., Banaban Schl., Ocean Is., 1922; headmr., Ellice Is. Schl., Vaitupu, 1923; headmr., King George V. Schl., Bairiki, Tarawa, 1931; ag. admstve. offr., Ellice Is. dist., 1932; admstve. offr. and nat. lands commn., Ellice Is. (conjoint), 1934.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1917; London matric., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher gr., 1st June, 1928.
KENNEDY, FRANK ROBERT, M.B.E. (1937), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1895; mily. serv., 1914-18; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 1920; dist. offr., 1932; dep. prov. comsnr., Jan., 1939.

KENNEDY, NORMAN MALACHY, A.C.A.—B. 1900; acctnt., govt., opium shops, Penang, June, 1927; sr. acctnt. and offr. i/c. govt., opium shops, Penang, June, 1932; acctnt., excise dept. and P.W.D., Penang, Jan., 1936; acctnt., med. dept., and excise dept., S.S., Dec., 1936.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., LL.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and solr., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law soc'y., 1924-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st classical schl., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1926; asst. treas., 1927; dep. estate duty comsnr., 1931; senr. asst. treas., 1933; dist. offr., South, 1935; senr. asst. treas., Aug., 1937; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Nov., 1937; asst. to finan. sec., Aug., 1939; appl. comsnr. on possibility of agric. extens. in the col., Oct., 1939.

KENNIF, VICTOR, R.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (AUST.), M.I.M. AND C.Y.E.—B. 1895; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; asst. eng., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. asst. comsnr. of wks., 1927; ag. comsnr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apr. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, May to Dec., 1927; asst. D.P.W., Cyprur, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1930-33; asst. D.P.W., Palestine, 1935.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONEY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stiltns. and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KERR, ALLAN FREER, B.A. (Hons.).—B. 1910; ed. Stowe and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1933; asst. dist. commr., 1934.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWIN POMEROY, M.C., A.C.S.M., M.I.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for mily. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931; warden, mines, Pahang, 1934; do., Selangor, Jan., 1936; title changed to senr. inspr., mines, July, 1938; ch. inspr., F.M.S., 1939.

KERWIN, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1889; ed. Sarnia Separate Schl., Sarnia Collegiate and Osogood Hall; called to bar of Ontario, 1911; K.C., 1928; mem., Ontario parole bd., 1932; just., sup. ct. of Ontario, Sept., 1932; just., sup. et., Canada, July, 1935.
KEUNEMAN, ARTHUR ERIC, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1885; comsnr. of assize, Ceylon, July, 1936; puisne just., Mar., 1938.
KEY, WALTER JOHN SMITH, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Dundee High Schl., Dundee Tech. Coll. and St. Andrews Univ.; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; seconded for mily. serv. 1915 to 1919; asst. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comtee, Kedah, Oct., 1922; attd., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comtee., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. I B, 1934; contr., rubber, Malaya, Apr., 1937; Br. residnt., Selangor, 1939.

KILLICK, ANTHONY BERNARD, B.Sc. Agr. (Lond.).—B. 1901; ed. St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, Herts., Eastbourne Coll., S. Eastern Agrl. Coll., Wye (Dipl. of Agr.), Imp. Coll. of Sc., London, and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad; agrl. offr., Uganda, 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dep. dir., agr., 1931 and 1934; dep. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1936; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika, 1939.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. et., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admstr., Cape exec. comtee, elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."
KIMBER, GERTH.—B. 1906; ed. Perse Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., 1916-1920 (comsn., Aug., 1916); cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; asst. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1928; dep. pres. town bd., in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. pros. Johore, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., Aug., 1933; offr., cls. II., Mar., 1937; ag. legal advr. June, 1937.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attchd. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; J.P., 1908; asst. emigr., offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; mem., bd. of examrs., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1912; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., various occasions, 1921-29; dir., crim. intell., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar., 1932 and 1934; inspr.-gen., pol., Apr., 1935; M.L.C., 1935; comsnr., pol., 1938; King's pol. med., 1938.

KING, WILLIAM CYRIL CAMPBELL.—B. 1891; ed. Cranbrook schl.; probr., Ceylon pol., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1914; on active serv., 1917-19; supt., pol., 1921; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., 1931-32; ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Cyprus, Aug., 1933; King's pol. med., Feb., 1937; dep.-comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1937; ag. comsnr., July, 1937-Deer., 1938.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; apptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; registr. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confec., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930; prime min., 1935.

KINGDOM, FRANCIS WINDSOR PEIRHAM.—B. 1890; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate and Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; col. audit dept., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, Feb., 1916; ag. sen. asst. audr. on various occasions; asst. censor, 1918-19; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Apr., 1933; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.

KINGDON, SIR DONALD, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal asst. and inspr. of schl., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar., to July, 1912; atty. gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty. gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929; pres. W. African ct. of appl., 1936.

KINGDON, HUGH EMERSON, M.A., B.C.L. (Oxon), LL.B. (Lond.)—B. 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1918, in absentia while on mily. service in France; ed. Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1919-21; oom. law bar, western circuit and cent. crim. ct., 1921-36; hon. couns., metropolitan area, Brit. Legion, 1932-36; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1936; ag. cr. coun., Jan., 1938.; ag. judge, Apr.-Nov., 1938; seconded as impt. and expt. contrlr. and custodian, enemy property, Sept., 1939; jt. author of "The Income Tax Act, 1918"; author of "Prosecutions and Police Work in the Courts."

KING-FARLOW, SIR SYDNEY CHARLES NETTLETON KT. BACH (1924).—Ed. Harrow and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A. class hon., 1887; M.A., 1889; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1889; went S.E. circuit and Sussex district; puisne judge, high ct., Uganda, 1912; ag. chief just., 1913; judge of ct. of appeal for E. Africa, 1912-14; puisne judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, Feb., 1915; ag. chief just., S. Leone, May to Aug., 1919, and of G. Coast, June-Oct., 1921; senr. puisne judge, G. Coast, Mar., 1920; ch. just., Bahamas, Nov., 1922; pres., reformatory and indus. schls. coman., 1923; ch. just., Cyprus, Dec., 1924; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1927; ret., 1931; author of "Some Gold Coast Judgments, 1915-17"; "The Massai Case, 1913"; "The Law Affecting a Company Prospectus"; partly candidate, Sheffield (Aftercliffe), 1909-10; S. Hackney, 1910.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1873; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high coman., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. coman., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high coman., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Aast. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. gov't. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for research, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.

KINLOCH, DAVID, B.Sc. (Hons.) For. Edin.—B. 1910; ast. cons. of forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1933; ag. silviculturist, Jan., July, 1934; ag. utilisation offr., July-Nov., 1936; Gold Coast local forces, 1936; 2nd lieut., 1937.
KINLOCH, JOHN BAXTER.—B. 1906; ed. Kirkcaldy High Sch., and Edin. and Oxford Univ.; B.Sc., forestry, Edin., 1927; ast. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1927.

KINNEARD, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.C.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls., certif., Regina Normal Sch., 1912; prin., Earl Grey and Govan, Pub. Schla. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. D., Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932 and 1935-36; S.M.O., Falkland Is., Jan., 1937.
KINSMAN, CYRIL WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1936)—B. 1887; offl. sec. to gov., Victoria and elk., exec. coun., 1930.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darlington Genl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Sheffield Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb. 1929.

KIRBY, ARTHUR CECIL, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. Repton and Balliol; asst. dist. coman., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. coman., 1918; ag. do., Somaliland, 1918-19; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade res., Mar., 1925; ag. prov. coman., in 1933, 1937, 1938 and 1939.

KIRBY, ARTHUR FRANK, A.M. INST.—B. 1899; ed. Sir William Borlase's Sch., Marlow and London Univ. (Schl. of Economics); served European War (France), 1918-19; office asst., Gold Coast rly., 1928; traffic man., 1936; asst. aspt. of the line, K.U.R. & H., 1939.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool., Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.), L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; ast. pol. offr., Hai., 1918-19; civ. surg., Afghanistan, 1919; ast. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., Mauritius, 1922; dir., med. and health dept., 1927; author of "Pub. Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Hints on Equipment and Health for intending Residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.

KIRK, WILLIAM ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1896; ed. Sleaford Gram. Sch., Leeds Modern Sch., Leeds Univ.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, May, 1923; ex. engrnr., May, 1932; ag. sr. ex. engrnr., Apr., 1936; snr. ex. engrnr., Feb., 1938.

KIRKBRIDE, ALAN LOGAN, O.B.E.—B. 1901; admnstr. offr., Palestine, 1921; seconded to Trans.Jordan as 2nd ast. ch. Br. rep., 1921; finan. advr., 1928; ast. sec. on reversion to Palestine, 1934; dist. coman., Palestine, 1938.

KIRKBRIDE, ALEC SEATH, O.B.E. (1932), M.C.—B. 1897; mily. serv., Egypt, Palestine, Hejaz, Trans-Jordan, 1916-21; Br. rep., Es Salt, Apr., 1921; junr. ast. sec., Palestine, Apr., 1922; ast. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. to exec. and advy. couns., Nov., 1924-Nov., 1927; ast. Br. res., Trans-Jordan, Nov., 1927; Br. res. do., 1929.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. KIRKLAND, HUGH MACMILLAN, B.Sc.(Edin.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1899; O.T.C. and war serv., 1916-19; K.U.R. & H., asst. engrnr., 1925; sr. asst. engrnr., 1935.
KIRKLAND, ROBERT IRWIN, A.M.Inst. Mech.E.—Ed. Manchester Grammar Sch.; pilot, R.N.A.S., 1917-19; workshops man. K.U.R. & H. workshops, Nairobi, 1934; works supt., 1935.

KIRKPATRICK, THOMAS WINIFRED, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Colony, 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Research Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
KIRWAN, SIR JOHN WATERS, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B. 1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Australia since 1908; chmn. of comms., 1923-26; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commsr., E.A.P.—Apr., 1914; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. asst. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; asst. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affairs in 1931 and 1933; dep. prov. commsr., Apr., 1932; prov. commsr., 1934.

KITCHING, GEOFFREY CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. Oundle Schol. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. polit. offr., Iraq, 1920; miny. of int., Iraq, 1920-30; dep. dir., development, Palestine, 1931; gov't. sec'y., St. Helena, 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Camb.; survey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1924; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officr. asst. surr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. dep. surr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June and Dec., 1932; supt., surveys, Johore, Mar., 1933; do., Kedah, Mar., 1935; do., Malacca, Nov., 1937; title changed to ch. survr., July, 1938; ch. survr., Singapore, Feb., 1939.
KITSON, GEORGE HERBERT WENTWORTH.—B. 1898; cadet, Nyasaaland pol., 1926; asst. supt., pol., 1928; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, 1937.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1894; ed. Grenada Grammar Sch.; 5th clk., treas., Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1913; served in the Great War, 1914-19; Egypt, 1916; Palestine, 1917; twice ment. in desps.; rev. and cust. offr., Grenada, 1919; 2nd clk., secretariat and clk., leg. coun., 1921; ch. clk., gov't. office, Windward Is. and clk., ex. coun., Grenada, 1925; sec. to gov't. of Windward Is., 1928.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., 1910; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914; commsr., Carriacou, Dec., 1935.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc.(Lond.)—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schol. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schol., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; chmn., agric. experiments comms., 1916; chmn., food supply comms., 1917; ag. gov't. entomologist (conjunct), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.
KOHO BAN WICKREME, ALFRED SILVA, B.A.(Lond.).—Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1938.

KOLLER, Thurstan William, A.S.A.A., C.A. (S.A.), ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; clk., O.F.S. civ. serv., July, 1900; senr. inspr., Union audit dept., Feb., 1929; chf. inspr., rev., do., Sept., 1931; provl. audr., Cape Prov., Dec., 1932.

KOTZE, PETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape), Dip. For. (Oxon.).—Asst. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Concordia, 1914; forest offr., Storms River, 1918; ditto, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forests, Midland conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., Midland inspectorate, 1932.

KOTZE, SIR ROBT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engrnr. and consulting engrnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engrnr., Transvaal, Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn., Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., Nov., 1926.

KREFT, HERMANN HEINRICH GERHARD, (B.A.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal Coll., Capetown and S.A. Coll., Capetown; inspr., schls., Cape Prov., 1916; dir., educn., S.W. Africa, 1923; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1935; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1935; dir., do., 1937.
KROGH, ANDRE LOUIS, M.D., Ch.B.(Edin.).—B. 1887; govt. med. offr., Dec., 1920; dist. med. offr., Tobago, 1922; senr. med. offr., Col. Hosp., San Fernando, Trinidad, 1937.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-19; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., exec. comms., nat. industrial coun. of the printing and newspaper industry of S. Africa.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. commsr. since 1925; pub. debt. commsr. since 1925; a commsr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, GEORG ADOLF CARL, (M.A.).—B. 1892; ed. Normal Coll., Johannesburg, Transvaal Univ. Coll. and Univ. of S. Africa (B.A.), Stellenbosch Univ. (M.A.); teacher, Transvaal, 1912; prin., Transvaal, 1921; inspr., schl., Transvaal, 1927; tech. advtr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1933; dir., educn., Transvaal, 1934; sec. for social welfare, 1937.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stofoberg Gedenk Schl., 1916-24; organising inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regisr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., adminstr.'s office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comms. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admstr. and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admstd., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich, and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripov.; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyasaland Prot., 1930; do., Kenya, 1938.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., O.B.E. (1935); LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903, capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schl., Malta, Aug., 1920; dir., educn., Nov., 1933.

LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., 1910; district commnr., Kenya, Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. commnr., 1931; ag. ch. native commnr., 1933; prov. commnr., grade I, 1934.

LA FLECHE, Lieut.-Col. LEO RICHER, D.S.O.—B. 1888; lieut., 1914; lieut.-col., 1918; examr., hd. of commerce, 1919; mem., purchasing commn., Canada, 1920; mem., pension appl. ct., 1930; dep. min., national defence (Can.), 1932.

LAING, GORDON DACOMB, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (St. And.), D.T.M. & H. (Witwatersrand), D.P.H. (Witwatersrand).—B. 1899; ed. Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; senr. lect. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munic., Nov., 1929; dep. med. offr., health, Johannesburg, 1934; lect. in urban health admin., dept. of public health, Univ. of Witwatersrand; mem. of bd. of examrs., Royal Sanitary Inst. in co-op. with Union govt.; lieut.-col. S.A. Med. Corp., Active Citizen Force; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S. African Assoon. for the Advancement of Science and on preventive med. in S.A. Medical Journal.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN, O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1890; ed. Forest Schl., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immigrn. dept., Apr. 1913; mem., govt. standing bd. of examrs. in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigrn. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1925; clk. to exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928; prte. sec. to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. commnr., Jan., 1932; temp. M.L.C., June, 1935; M.L.C., Oct., 1936; commnr. of lab. and local govt., Jan., 1938.

LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B.1894; 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; K.A.R., German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., G.P.O., 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1921; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932; ag. prov. commnr., Jan.-May, 1938.

LAKE, LIEUT.-COL. MORICE CHALLONER, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1885; ed. Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; commdr., unattd. list (attd. 1st Battn. Somerset Light Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; rectl. duty, 1905-15; Aden, 1913; served, Aden Field Force, 1915-19; offr. in ch., signals, 1915-16; offr. commdg. signal schl., Lucknow, 1916-17; raised and commanded Aden (Arab) Labour Corps, 1917-18; raised and commanded 1st Yemen Infy., 1918-25; sp. duty, W.O. revising mily. rept., Aden Prot., 1926-27; reverted, mily. duty, India, 1927-28; adviser to C.O. and Air Miny. on raising levies, etc., in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, Nov. 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., July to Nov., 1930; polit. secy., May, 1934; ag. ch. commnr., Apr.-Sept., 1935 and May-Oct., 1936; O.A.G., Oct., 1937-Feb., 1938 and Aug.-Dec., 1938.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; appd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transferred to C.O., July, 1923; ag. cler. offr., higher gde., 23rd Nov., 1939.
LAMB, CYRIL HUBERT, M.I.M.S.—B. 1887; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., 1919 to 1920; R.I.C., 1920 to 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to July, 1923; appd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as regt. on staff of high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 1928-32; returned to C.O., 12th Sept., 1932; asst. prin., 1st Feb., 1937.

LAMBERT, EDGAR BUCHANAN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. Dulwich Coll., R. Milly. Coll., Sandhurst and Crystal Palace Engng. Schl.; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt. 1909-11; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st cls. do., 1920; asst. supt., surveys, 1934; do., crown lands, 1938.

LAMBERT, WILLIAM OSBORNE, M.I.N.A., M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1887; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1917; Hong Kong del. to safety of life at sea confce., Simla, 1931; govt. marine survr., 1932; prin. survr. of ships, 1939.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEDWELL, O.B.E. (1930), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Schl., King's Schl., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1905; asst. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; asst. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; asst. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entomist, Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.

LAMBOURNE, NELSON THOMAS, I.S.O. (1937), M.A. (N.Z.)—B. 1877; ed. Mangere Schol. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; many years' teaching ser., N.Z.; inspr. schls.; ch. inspr., primary schls., 1931; dir. educn., 1933.

LAMBRECHTS, PETER SIMON.—B. 1886; Learner, G.P.O., Capetown, 1900; jun. asst., cent. tel. office, Capetown, 1901; asst., do., 1904; asst., G.P.O., savings bank br., Capetown, 1904; clk., deeds, Capetown, 1910; 1st grade olk., 1918; exam., 1919; ag. regis. deeds, Windhoek, 1932; regis., 1932; do., Bloemfontein, 1937; do., Pietermaritzburg, June, 1937.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat., 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895)—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.

LANDOR, JOSEPH VICTOR, M.D. (Lond.), B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Certificate of London S.T.M., with distino.—B. 1897; med. offr., S.S., Aug., 1927; med. offr. i/c. gen. hosp., Jo. Bahru, ag. physn. and radiologist, Johore, in addn., Apr., 1933; ag. prof. of med., Coll. of Med., Spore, June, 1934; physn., gen. hosp., Spore, Apr., 1935; physn. and reger., gen. hosp., Spore, Jan., 1936.

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERT GUSTAVUS.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comnr., 1918; attd., secretariat, 1918; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1919; senr. asst. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; res. mag., Kenya, 1929; ag. judge, supt. ct., 1935, 1937 and 1938; puisne judge, Sierra Leone, 1939; ag. ch. just., 1939.

LANE, HENRY WALKER, Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Christs Coll., Waitaki High Schol. & Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; lieut. R.E., France, Egypt and Palestine, 1915-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1924; senr. asst. engr., 1934; ag. suptg. engr., 1936.

LANG, ERNEST PHILIP HENRY.—B. 1880; Ed. Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1905; pte. prac. Hong Kong, 1905-15; war serv., 1915-19 (capt.); asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928; dep. regis., sup. ct., Oct., 1928; J.P., 1928; ag. offl. recr. and regtr., trade marks and letters patent in addn., 1930; ag. regis., offl. admstr. and regis., companies in 1931, 1932, 1934 and 1935; regis., sup. ct., etc., 1935; regis. in prize, 1939.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales from Dec., 1913; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt., 1920-22; premier, N.S.W., 1926-27 and 1930-32.

LANGE, PIETER OSTWALD.—B. 1893; ed. Alexandria and Old Schol. of Mines, Johannesburg; mily. serv., 1914-15, R.L.I. (S.W.A.); mine offl., crown mines, 1915-28; asst. govt. mine inspr., 1928; dep. inspr., 1933; inspr., 1938.

LANGLEY, CARLETON GEORGE, K.C. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schol.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on milv. serv., Malta, France and Ireland, 1914-21; pte. practice, Lincolns Inn and Bahama Is., 1921-31; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1931; oomsnr., sup. ct., J.P.; ag. puisne judge, 1931-3; dual appt. atty.-gen. and ag. col. sec., 1934-36; admstr., Antigua, 1936-36; 2nd puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1937.
LANGSTONE, HON. FRANK.—B. 1881; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1922-25 and since 1928; min. of lands and comsnr. of state forests, 1935; rep., N.Z., International Lab. Confce., Geneva, 1939.

LANGWORTHY, HENRY BRUCE.—B. 1896; pol. probrr., F.M.S., July, 1920; astt. comsnr., pol., July, 1923; 1st astt. comsnr., Selangor, July, 1925; adjt., fed. hdqrs., Oct., 1925 and Feb., 1930; supt., convict estab., Taiping and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ch. pol. offr., B. Pahat, Oct. 1931; astt. supt., pol. S.S., Dec., 1933; dy. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1935; dy. comsnr., pol. and offetg. commr., pol., Johore, July, 1935; ag. comnr. pol., Johore, Nov., 1936.

LANIER, PAUL EDOUARD MAXIME, M.D., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Middlesex Hosp. London, Paris and Lausanne; astt. med. offr., Seychelles and visiting mag. to outlying islands, 1924; resigned; re-ent. col. serv. as res. surg., Seychelles and visiting mag., outlying islands, 1929; sen. med. offr., 1935.

LANKTREP, CHARLES JOSEPH DANE, Barrister-at-Law, King's Inns, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; office astt. Kandy kach., Mar., 1924; admstr. sec. to dir. of med. and sanry. serv., Oct., 1925; addnl. astt. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; admstr. sec. to dir., elec. undertakings, July, 1928; astt. stlttn. offr., Jan., 1932; ag. man., elec. undertakings, Apr., 1933; addnl. man., do., July, 1934; astt. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1934; do., Trincomalee, Dec., 1936.

LANSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; clk., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Aliwal N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. asst. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal advsr., July, 1907; prof. astt., atty-gen.'s off., Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advsr., dep. of justice, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., Oct., 1931; judge pres. (E.D. local divn.), 1937.

LAPOINTE, RT. HON. ERNEST, P.C., B.A., LL.B., K.C., Lt.-D. (Quebec East).—B. 1876; ed. Rimouski Coll. and Laval Univ., Quebec; called to bar, 1898; K.C., 1908; Canadian del. to L. of N., Geneva, 1922; rep. Canada with Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King at Imp. Confce., 1926; negotiated and signed, with Hon. Mr. Fielding, treaty with France, 1922; negotiated and signed, on behalf of Canada, treaty with U.S.A. regarding Pacific fisheries; signed four other treaties with U.S.; leader of govt. in H. of C. session of 1926; Can. plenipotentiary at Naval confce., Geneva, June-July, 1927; head of Can. delegn. to Imp. Confce., on Imp. and Dominion legis., 1929; barrister-at-law; elec. by accl. to H. of C., Canada, 1904; re-el. at g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911 and 1917 for same constit.; resigned, 1919 to contest Quebec East; elec. 1919; re-el. at g.e., 1921; min. of marine and fisheries, King cabinet, 1921; min. of just., Jan., 1924, and held position in 2nd King adminstr.; re-el. at g.e., 1925, 1926 and 1930; resigned portfolio with liberal admin., Aug., 1930; re-el. at g.e., 1935; min. of just., Oct., 1935.

LARTER, LESLIE N. H., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—Ed. Imp. Coll. (Lond.), schol. and medallist, graduated (1st cls. hons.) 1929; col. agril. schol., Fitzwilliam House, Camb., and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1930-32; geneticist, Jamaica, Nov., 1932.

LASCELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, Kt. BACH. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. etc. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1896; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1888; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, justice divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn. ct., sup. ct., 1923.
LATHAM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926.

LATHAM, Rt. HON. SIR JOHN GREGG, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confec., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confec., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied oomn. on Czechoslovak affrs. at confec.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; a rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confec., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affrs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932; leader, goodwill mission to Japan, 1934; resigned from parl., 1934; ch. just., high ct. in 1935.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Nov., 1936.

LAURENSON, GEORGE LYTTON, A.M. Inst.C.E.; A.M.N.Z.Soc.C.E.—B. 1893; ed. Lyttleton dist. high sch.; Wellington Coll., N.Z.; London Univ.; N.Z.P.W.D., 1911; ass't engnr., 1915; war serv. N.Z. Field Engnr.; res. harbr. engnr., Westport, 1928; civ. engnr., transp. dept., 1930; consnr., transp., 1937.

LAURIE, JOHN INGLIS HALLIBURTON, M.A., M.R., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1912; ed. Uppingham Schl., Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and St. Thomas Hosp., Lond.; D.M.O., Cyprus, Sept., 1939.
LAURIE, WILLIAM, M.Inst.M., M.Cy.E.—B. 1890; ass't engnr., Kedah, Apr., 1920; exec. engnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engnr., Nov., 1936; senr. exec. engnr., Jan., 1938.

LAVILLE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripes, B.A., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S. Stiltns., Dec., 1912; attd. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet; superv. officer, cls. V. Jan., 1916; lent to govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offr. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; sur-v. offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; offr. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, July, 1920; offr. coll. income tax, Singapore, Apr., 1922; mag. and ass't regsr., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; D.P.P., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regsr., sup. ct., S'pore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II, Dec., 1929; offr. regsr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. comrnr., Kelantan, June, 1932; offr., cls. IB, Nov., 1933; dist. judge, S'pore, Feb., 1935; legal advr., Kedah, May, 1936; ag. judge, Johore, Jan., 1937; ag. European judge, Kedah, June, 1938.
LAW, ALFRED NOEL, M.C.—B. 1895; mil. ser., 1915-18; inspr., dist. admstrn., Palestine, 1920; ag. ass't dist. comrnr. on various occasions, 1925-30, and once as dist. comrnr.; ass't sec., 1932.

LAW, SIR CHARLES EWAN, KT. BACH. (1937).—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prize-man, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1905; Burma bar, 1905; staff capt. (tempy. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med.(India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res. of offrs.) comdg. 16th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem., legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili(L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston et al., on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston et al., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions, 1926, and 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comte. of res. mag., June, 1926; mem., comn. of enquiry into admstrn. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just., from May, 1932 to Mar., 1933; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; mem., comn. of enquiry into admstrn. of just. in crim. matters in E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. just., Zanzibar, Mar., 1934; chmn., agrl. indebtedness comrn., Aug., 1934; chmn., Peace Memorial museum, Aug., 1934-Nov., 1939; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, Oct., 1930-Nov., 1939; ch. just., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1939; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1939; mem. Rhodesian (conjoint) ct. of appl.
LAW, DAVID, Grad.Inst.T.—B. 1901; station mast., K.U.R. and H., 1925; accts. clk., 1926; travelling inspr. of accts., 1926; ass't acctnt., 1935.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1888; ass't mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem., bd. of exams., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; reaptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban districts in addn., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.
LAWES, GORDON WILLIAM.—B. 1896; pol. probr., F.M.S., June, 1919; ass't comrnr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1929; offr., superintending pol. circle, Ipoh, June, 1935; adjt., F.M.S. pol., Aug., 1948.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.S.I. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep. adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; adminstr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1896; ag. adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high comrnr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun. pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.
LAWLOR, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—B. 1880; ass't mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Feb., 1908; aest. inspr., schls., Natal, May, 1910; inspr. of schls., Apr., 1912; oh. inspr., schls., Aug., 1931.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR SIR ARTHUR SALISBURY, K.C.M.G. (1937), K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931), D.S.O.—Major, late reserve of officers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desps.)—served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desps.)—ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. comntr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in desps.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. consul., Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; admtd. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-32; sec. to govt., 1926; comntr. and commdr.-in-ch., June, 1932; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., May, 1935; ret., 1939.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES TREVOR, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1879; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Schol.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-05; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. ast. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. ast. prov. comntr., Cent. Prov., 1912; pvt. sec. to gov.-gen., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st ast. sec., S. Prov., 1916; addl., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prim. ast. sec., 1921; comntr., Br. Empire Exhibition, 1923; ag. lieut-govr., S. Prov., 1927; staff grade, 1928; admtd. of col., 1930.

LAWRENCE, HON. HARRY GORDON, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1901; ed. Rondebosch Boys' High Schol. and Univ. of S. Africa; called to bar, May, 1926; M.P. since June, 1929; min. of lab., Union of S. Africa, July, 1933-Sept., 1939; min. of interior and pub. health since Sept., 1939.
LAWRENCE, JOHN DAVIES, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and R. Mily. Coll.; Manchester Regt., Egyptian Army, 1917-20; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; dist. offr., 1933.

LAWRENCE, NATHAN COOJ, B.A. (London), Teachers' Dipl., Educn. (Lond.), Mem., Inner Temple.—B. 1900; ed. Kppong Methodist Schol., King's Coll., London and Univ. of London; clk., gov.'s office, Gold Coast, July, 1924; resigned, 1930; 2nd divn. teacher, 1937; inspr. schls., 1938.
LAWRENCE, EDWIN STEWART, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.).—B. 1899; ed. G'gow Acad. and G'gow Univ.; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928; surg., Penang, Apr., 1938.

LAWSON, HON. SIR HARRY SUTHERLAND WIGHTMAN, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1875; M.L.A for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat continuously in State Parlmt. from 1899; comntr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty-gen., solr. gen. and min. of public instrn., premier and atty-gen. 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth senator for Victoria.
LAWSON, HUBERT JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O., Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1897; ed. Larne Grammar Schol. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; war serv., 1916-17; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1928; ag. C.M.O., Kelantan, July, 1937.

LAWSON, HUGH, A.M.I.M.E., Ch. Mech. Engr.—B. 1893; ed. Queen Victoria Schol. and Tynemouth Munie. High Schol.; inspr. of mach., F.M.S., Oct., 1924; regstr., motor vehicles, Perak, in addn., Apr., 1929.

LAWSON, HON. JOHN NORMAN, M.P.—Mem., Macquarie (N.S.W.), H. of R., C'wealth of Aust., from 1931; mem., C'wealth delegtn., Empire Parly. Assoc., England, 1935; parly. secy. to treasy., July, 1938-Mar., 1939; min., trade and cust., Menzies miny., from Apr., 1939.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schol., Gray's Inn, 1923; clk. acct., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to comntr. for lands, 1908; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; ast. regsr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (red. thanks G.O.C.L. of C.); regsr. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regsr., crown lands, 1920; dep. regsr.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audr., mem., bd. of comntr., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; commr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1914; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; on mily. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; addl., secretariat, June, 1921, office ast. to ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; ast. attitnt. offr., Oct., 1923; office ast. to dir. med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1929; ast. attitnt. offr., Jan., 1930; ast. govt. agt.; Badulla, Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., 1932; ast. ch. sec. and sec., pub. services comntr., Dec., 1935.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902: apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.
LEAKEY, DOUGLAS GRAY HAZZETT.—B. 1907; ed. Weymouth and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. forestry; ast. conserv., forest, Kenya, Aug., 1932.

LEAKEY, RICHARD JOHN MONTAGUE.—B. 1894; ed. Stamford Schol., privte. tuition and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (Cantab.); office ast., Nigerian Rly. open lines, 1923; ast. engrnr., do., capital wks., 1923; ast. engrnr., open lines, 1927; senr. engrnr., do., 1936.

LEATHES, FRANCIS PALLENT, O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1888; S. African M. Pol., 1907-16; E.A. campaign, 1914-18; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1916; ast. supt., 1926; ag. dep. comntr., several occasions; dep. comntr., June, 1936; ag. comntr., 1938.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schol., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.Sc. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as assistant ast., 1903; apptd. forest ast., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, ast. ch., office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and ast. forester; ch. forester, comntr. of conservn., and bd. of rly. comntr., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. comntr., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.
LE CHEMINANT, PRIGY.—B. 1893; ed. Christ Ch. Sch., Clifton; R.A.M.C., 1914-16; R.E. 1916-19; Midland Rly., 1901-22; ast. accntn., K.U.R. and H., 1930.
LECK, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Schol., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, 1914.

LECKIE, CAPT. PETER MARTIN, L.S. (Vict.).—B. 1890; survr., dept. lands and surveys, Dec., 1907; staff surv., May, 1918; war serv., Sept., 1915-18; surv., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1920; do., Kedah, Apr., 1923; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; ag. senr. asst. supt., surveys, Aug., 1936; ch. surv., gde., II, Feb., 1939.

LE CONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser dependencies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regist., sup. ct., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law commr., 1914; war serv. in France and Macedonia, 1916-19; food contr., May 1920; puisne judge, July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1933 and March-Dec., 1938.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripso, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripso, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyasaland admnstr., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the cols., 4th Aug., 1933; seconded to Nyasaland admnstr., 1931-33; visited Bechuanaland Prot. as sec. to finan. commr. (Sir A. Pim), 1933; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the cols., 1933; visited Cyprus as sec. to finan. commr. (Sir R. Oakden), 1934; prin. 1934; seconded to Imp. Defence Coll., 1938.

LEE, HARRIE BERTIE, D.S.O., M.C., M.B., B.S. (hons.) Melbourne, D.T.M. and H., Eng.; certif. London S.T.M. (with distinct.).—B. 1888; ed. Sydney and Melbourne Univ.; four years serv. with A.I.F. in field as R.M.O.; field ambulance offr. and D.A.D.M.S., 1st Australian Divn., 1914-18; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; specialist, Nigeria, Aug., 1933; senr. (do.), 1938.

LEE, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.C.M. (1917).—B. 1891; ed. Albany Street Public Schl., Dunedin; war serv. with N.Z.E.F.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1922-28 and since 1931; partly, under sec. to min. of finance, 1936; do. to min. in ch. of housing, 1938; author of "Children of the Poor," "The Hunted," "Civilian into Soldier" and "Socialism in New Zealand."

LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; asst. P.M.G., 1932; 1st cls. and pol. mag., Kowloon, Feb., 1934; asst. dist. offr., North, Jan., 1935; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., May, 1935; do. to gov., Dec., 1935; extra asst. col. sec., Mar., 1936; dep. clk., comms., Aug.-Sept., 1936; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affs., 1939; pvt. sec. to gov., July-Aug., 1939.

LEE, SYDNEY WILLIAM TIMPSON, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., D.P.H. (Belf.), D.T.M. (Lpool), D.O.M.S. (R.C.P.S. Eng.).—B. 1898; surg. sub. lt., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; M.O.S., Rhodesia, 1921-22; M.O., Uganda, 1922; S.H.O., Uganda, 1930; ag. D.D.S.S., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S., 1934; S.M.O., Zanzibar, 1934; M.O.H., port health offr. and offr., i/c. eye clinic, 1934; ag. D.M.S., 1937 and 1939; S.M.O. i.c., med. and sanv. services, 1939.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), KT. BACH (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, ch. sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.

LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1893; regsy. clk., Western Pacific high commr., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Col., 1920; sec. to res. commr., 1922; sec. to gov't., 1931; ag. admnstr. offr., Fanning Is., June 1936.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Milly. Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.) June, 1924; admnstr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvt. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; olk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admnstr. offr., Palestine, 1929.
LEFEAUX, LESLIE.—B. 1886; ed. privately; service with bank of England, 1904-33; gov't. reserve of N.Z., 1934.

LE GEYT, ASHLEY HUBERT, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1925; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1927; asst. sec., Dec., 1927; dist. offr., Nov., 1937.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg.-gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams., 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922.

LEGGE, ERIC MONTIMER.—B. 1888; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad and Dulwich Coll.; registr. gen.'s office, Trinidad, Jan., 1908; cust., Apr., 1914; supervisor, Sept., 1936; dep. collr., Nov., 1937; compt'r. cust. and excise and reg. shipping, May, 1939.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1891; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; attd., Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; attd. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office asst. to gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1928; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Matara, Dec., 1929; asst. gov't. agt., Matara, Feb., 1930; admnstr. secy. to dir., med. and san. services and sec. to min. of health, Sept., 1932; ag. tea extp. contr., Dec., 1934; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1935; ag. prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port. comm., Dec., 1938.

LEINBERGER, KARL WILHELM.—B. 1886; ed. Technikum, Mittweida and Freiberg R. Schl. of Mines, Germany; asst. inspr., mines, Union of S. Africa, 1920; inspr., mines, 1930; asst. gov't. mining engrnr., 1938.

LE MARE, RALPH.—B. 1893; serv. Gt. War (France, E. Africa), 1914-19; ment. in desps., 1916; treas'y. asst., Nigeria, 1920; ag. senr. asst. treas., 1929-34; senr. asst. treas., Palestine, 1936; acct., acct. gen.'s dept., Nigeria, 1937.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. capt., Schol. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; col. audit dept., Oct., 1921; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1934, ag. dep. audr., May-Sept., 1936, audr., B. Guiana, 1937.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. serv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., oh. clk.'s office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, oh. clk., Apr., 1921;
LEOTAUD, RENE MANDILHON.—B. 1907; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; qualified as solr., 1936 in Trinidad; 2nd asst. crown solr., admstr. gen., offr. recr. and dep. pub. trustee, Trinidad, Jan., 1939.

LE ROUX, JONATHAN JOHAN DU PRÉ, L.R.C.P & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Wits).—B. 1900; ed. B.H.S. Pearl, Univ. Cape Town, Univ. and Royal Colls., Edin., Univ. of Witwatersrand; capt., 8th Field Ambulance, S.A.M.C.; M.O., Pretoria Leper Inst., 1927-32; med. supt., do., 1933-5; M.O.H., Boksburg, Tvl., 1936-38; asst. health offr., Union health dept., Cape Town, 1939.
LESHELIE, COL. R. B., M.C., Lincolnshire Regt.—Commtd., Ceylon defence force, Mar., 1935.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; asst. engnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; divnl. engnr., Apr., 1933; ag. engnr.-in-ch., 1932, 1934 and 1938.

LESTER, A. R., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.)—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-22; mily. serv., India and Persia, 1918-22; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch., soc. sociological investgn., Kahama, 1927-31; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agri.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schl., Capetown; B.Sc. (agri.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHAM, SIR GORDON JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Schl., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Univs.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affrs., Nov., 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933; do., Leeward Is., 1935.

LEVIN, FREDERICK MOSS.—B. 1893; met. office, London, 1912-14; war serv., R.N., R.N.V.R. (signals), London divn., 6th B.S. Grand Fleet mine-sweeper and Northern Patrol, 1915-19; air miny., met. office, London and various R.A.F. stations, 1919-34; air miny. met. office, Malta, 1935-37; R.W.A. met. serv., Gold Coast, 1937.
LEVINKIND, LEOPOLD.—B. 1892; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.Sc. (engnrg.); corp., C.P. Rifles, S.W. Africa campaign; ent. dept., irrig., Union of S. Africa, 1917; hydrographic surv., 1934.

LEWEY, ARTHUR WERNER.—B. 1894; M.A. (Cantab.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; major, R. of O.T.; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; served with Middlesex Regt., Flanders, Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine (severely wounded), 1914-19; pol. mag., Gambia, Jan., 1930; crown coun., Kenya, Oct., 1932; attld. legal advy. staff, C.O., May-June, 1936; solr. gen., Uganda, Aug., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., Sept., 1936-Apr., 1937; commr. for revision of laws; gov.'s dep., Nov., 1936; chmn., Jinja currency enquiry comm., 1937; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Feb., 1939.

LEWIN, CLAUS JOHN, M.C., B.Sc.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ. Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; supt., agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; senr. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir., agr., July, 1933; temp. M.L.C., 1935, 1936 and 1937.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, BERTRAM.—B. 1887; Br. P.O., Sect's office, C. and A.G.'s dept. and London & prov. audit, 1903-17; asst. acctnt., dep. ch. acctnt., Kenya and Uganda, P. and T., 1917-24; ch. acctnt., Palestine, 1924; do., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika post. serv., 1936; regional dir., P. and T. services, 1937.
LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; govt. printer, Feb., 1933.

LEWIS, I. E. G.—B. 1904; ed. Malvern Coll.; solr., sup. ct.; admitted Apr., 1929; advoc. and solr., S. Settlmts., 1934-37; reg'ar, high ct., Zanzibar, Dec., 1937; seconded ag. nest. admtr. genl., July, 1939.
LEWIS, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1907; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man and Keble Coll., Oxford (B.A.); asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnr.; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932; ch. engnr., ways and wks., Aug., 1937.

LEWIS, MALCOLM STUART.—B. 1889; H.A.C., 1914-16; R.G.A., 1917-19; treasy. asst., Nigeria, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1934; senr. acct.-gen. and currency offr. 1937, retaining personal designation of senr. asst. treas.; ag. acct.-gen., 1937 and 1938.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., D.M., B.Ch.(Oxon.), M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.).—B. 1894; resch. student, Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; pathologist II, Jan., 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931; ag. dir., I.M.R., Aug., 1936.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLIAR, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; comen. in R. Engrns., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engrs., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical scen., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATS.—B. 1898; elk. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; 1st elk. treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and collr., cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wrecks and registr., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treas., Cyprus, Sept. 1930; ag. dep. treas. in 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Nov., 1934; ag. treas., various periods, 1934-39.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE, Pay-Cdr., R.D., R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.), B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr.-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admly. war staff (allied naval coun.), 1918-19; staff, comdr.-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; librarian, col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Oct., 1920; office ast., col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Jan., 1922; offr. in-ch., records revision, in addn., Feb., 1924; dep. commr., excise, S.S., Nov., 1929; ag. senr. do., 1933-34; mem. and sec., Malayan tobacco comtce., 1933; seconded dep. commr., cust. and excise, F.M.S., Feb., 1936-Mar., 1936; ag. senr., dep. commr., excise, S.S., June, 1936-Feb., 1937; title changed ast. contr., cust., S.S., Oct., 1938; ag. dep. contr., 1939.

LIDDELL, Lieut.-Gen. Sir CLIVE GERARD K.C.B. (1899), C.B. (1935), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1883; ed. Uppingham and R.M.C., Sandhurst; ent. army, 1902; adjt., 1908-11; capt., 1908; staff capt., 6 dist., N. command, 1912-14; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., 1916; A.A.G., W.O., 1917-18; serv. European War, 1914-18 (desps. 6 times); instr., staff coll., 1919-22; dep. admstr., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1923-26; Imp. Defence Coll., 1927; G.S.O., 1st grade, W.O., 1928-31; comdr., 8th Infy. Brig., 1931-34; maj.-gen., 1933; comdr., 47th (2nd London) Divn., T.A., 1935; comdr., 4th Divn., 1935-37; lieut.-gen., 1938; adjt.-gen. to Forces, 1937-39; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1939.

LIESCHING, PERCIVALE, C.M.G. (1932), M.A.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; pvt., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut. Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1916; ment. in despr., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin. 6th Jan., 1927; prin. 1st Apr., 1929; accompanied U.K. deleg. to L. of N. Assembly, 1927, to signature of Pact of Paris, 1928 and to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; adl. to civ. avia. tion confce., Wellington, N.Z., 1936; seconded as sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1928-32 and as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high commr. for U.K., Aug.-Dec., 1933; offl. sec. to high commr. for U.K. in Australia, 1936, reverted to D.O. and promoted to ast. sec., 13th Feb., 1939; ast. U.S. of S., 9th Oct., 1939.

LIGHTBODY, WILLIAM PATTERSON HAY, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Ed.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), 1916, D.T.M. & H. (Camb.), 1923, D.P.H. (Glas.), 1932.—Ed. Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., Glasgow Royal Infirmary and London Sch. of Trop. Med.; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1920; ast. D.M.S. (H.), Sierra Leone, 1937; D.M.S., 1938.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec. bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec. pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. acts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. commr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., on various occasions, 1929-37; audr., 1939.
LIGHTON, GEORGE.—B. 1913; ed. Manchester Cent. High Schl. and Manchester Univ., B.A., Hons. (geogr.), 1933; M.A., 1935, dipl. ed., 1934; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1937.
LI, KING HONG, B.A. (Hong Kong).—B. 1891; headm.r., vernacular middle schl., Hong Kong, 1926.
LILLEY, RONALD, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Bedford and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audit dept., Apr., 1930; ast. audr., Tanganyika, July, 1930; do., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936; senr. ast. audr., Nigeria, 1938.

LINDOP-ERSKINE, REGINALD ALEXANDER.—B. 1897; war serv., 1915-19; Leeward Is. pol. force, 1919-24; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1924; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and supt., fire brig., various periods, 1925-34; comdt. pol. and inspr., prisons, Bahamas, Oct., 1936; title changed to commr., 1938.

LINDOW, ERIC DELAFIELD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond., certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Christ's Coll. Blackheath, Lond. Univ., King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1923; do., T.T.S. hosp., Dec., 1923, Oct., 1928 and Apr., 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1928 and Apr., 1929; ag. supnmy. ch. med. offr., A, S'pore, June, 1933, sup-scale offr., gr. B., Jan., 1935; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, Apr., 1936; sup-scale offr., gd. A, Sept., 1938.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921; dist. offr., Kenya, 1923; junr. sec., native affrs., 1927.

LINDSAY, WILLIAM HUBBLE, B.Sc. (Engr.), Lond., A.M.I.M. & Cy. E., Certif. in Pub. Wks. of King's Coll., Lond., B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Acad., King's Coll., Univ. of Lond.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, Dec., 1923; ex. engrnr., Dec., 1932; ag. ex. engrnr., Apr., 1937.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripes, part I, cls. I, div. 2; called to bar, 1930; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd ast. regsr.-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1915; ag. 1st pol. mag., on various occasions, 1922-30; ag. puisne judge, 1931 and 1933; ag. ast. atty.-gen., Feb., 1932; puisne judge, 1934; ag. ch. just., Dee., 1935-May, 1936; ag. ch. just., May-Dec., 1938; chmn., comp. serv. tribunal, July, 1939.

LINEHAN, WILLIAM, D.Litt.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. school and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. hons., class., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Jan.-April, 1919; ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1928; ag. ast. Br. advr., supt., lands and regsr., titles, Kelantan, Feb., 1931; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1933; dist. offr., Seremban, Jan., 1934; ag. sec. to res., Perak, May, 1937; offr., cls. IB, May, 1937; dir. educn., S.S., and advr., educn., Malay States, 1939.

LINFORD, GROFFREY BISPE.—B. 1891; pol. prob., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; ast. commr., pol., June, 1918; 1st ast. commr., pol., Ipoh, Jan., 1923; paymaster, Jan., 1925; regsr., criminals, S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1926; adjt., F.M.S. pol., Feb., 1934.
LINGLEY, LORIN GALE, B.A. (Cantab. Law Tripos).—B. 1910; ed. Plymouth Coll. and Cambridge (Downing Coll.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1933; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Dec., 1937.
