LISSAMAN, ARTHUR EVELYN, F.S.I., M. Inst. M. & Cy. E.—B. 1897; ed. Luton Modern Schl. and Coll., Estate Management, Lond.; mil. serv., R.E.'s, 1915-19; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; engrnr., 1923; ag. ex. engnr., Feb.-Nov., 1935, and May, 1939.
LITTLE, Francis Edward.—B. 1884; Natal pol., 1909-13; S.M.R., 1913-19; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1919; asst. supt., 1920; supt., 1928; Col. Police Medal, 1939.
LITTLE, Ronald Scott, M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.—B. 1890; ed. Grosevoren Coll., Carlisle and R. "Dick" Vety Coll., Edinburgh; mily. serv., 1913-19; vety. offr., Kenya, 1920.

LITTLEFAIR, Charles Septimus.—B. 1888; traffic dept., rlye., England, 1902-10; do., Rhodesia, 1910-14; 2nd Rhodesia Regt., Nov., 1914-Dec., 1915; paymr., E.A. Corps, Jan., 1916-Mar., 1919; ocy. sec., 1919-20; mily. accs., 1920-22; asst. treas., Kenya, Nov., 1923; senr. asst. treas., Jan., 1931; offr.-in-ch., pol. stores and accs., Jan., 1935; ag. dep. comsnr. and inland rev. offr., 1938.

LITTLEJOHN, Lewis John Shankes.—B. 1914; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; B.Sc. (hons. botany), 1936; Camb., 1937; Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., 1938; botanist and plant pathologist, Cyprus, Sept., 1938; seconded as asst. comsnr., Sept.-Oct., 1939; seconded to dept. of co-opern., Oct.-Nov., 1939.
LITTLEJOHN, Norman Kemp, A.M.Inst., C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1900; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1926.

LIVERPOOL, Earl of, Rt. Hon. Arthur Foljambe, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912), M.V.O. (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Haslebach.—B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; capt., 1897; staff capt., Dublin district, July, 1900 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906-08; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; governor-general of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LIVERSAGE, Vincent.—B. 1897; ed. Brewood G.S., Harper Adams Coll., Univ. Coll. Wales, Aberystwyth; served, R.F.A., 1916-20 (India, 1917-20); awarded Min. of Agr. fellowship, 1925 at Agr. Econ. Res. Inst., Oxford, and Univ., Wisconsin, U.S.A.; N.D.A., B.Sc. agr., Lond.), M.S. Econ. (Wis.); F.R. Econ. Soc.; lect., agr. econ., Leeds Univ., 1929; agr. economist, Kenya, 1930.

LIVINGSTON, William, A.M.Inst.C.E., P.A.S.Inst.—B. 1890; ed. Schl. of Science & Art, Kirkcaldy and Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; war ser., Gallipoli, Egypt and France, 1914-19; asst. dist. engr., Trinidad, 1919; dist. engr., 1920; dist. engr., Br. Guiana, 1924; seconded for duty on reconnaissance survey to interior, Br. Guiana, Mar.-Aug., 1927; D.P.W., Bermuda, Sept., 1929; asst. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1938; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1938.

LLEWELLIN, John Lionel Bretherton Llywelyn, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915-June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.
LLOYD, Edgar Brinley.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land asst., 1920; ag. regist., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921; registr., titles, 1926.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in desps., 1917; apptd. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., miny. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as asst. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., Jan., 1927; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec., Apr., 1929; prin., Sept., 1929; sec. to comsnr. on Palestine disturbances, 1929-30; sec. to West India Royal Coman., 1938-39; ag. asst. sec., Oct., 1939.

LLOYD, William Butler.—B. 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; puisne judge, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925, and from July, 1928; puisne judge, sup. ct., Nigeria, 1930; ag. ch. just. on occasions.

LLOYD, Capt. William Joseph, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1892; Great War, 1914-18; Ireland, 1921-22; K.A.R., 1922-27; admstv. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1936-37; dist. offr., 1939.

LLOYD-BLOOD, Capt. L. I. N. M.C., K.C. (1938), B.A., Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Dublin Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in desps.; dep. regis., sup. ot., Kenya, Oct., 1920; registr., July, 1921; asst. atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1925, 1926, 1927, 1929 and 1932; solr.-gen., Palestine, 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1933 and 1934; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Sept., 1936.
LOCKHEAD, John.—B. 1888; merc. marine, 1905-15; R.N.R., 1915-19; merc. marine, 1922-23; 2nd and 1st offr., K.U.R. & H., 1923; commdr., K.U.R. & H., 1932.

LOCK, Frederick John.—B. 1888; ed. King's Schl., Peterboro and Quernmore Coll., Upper Norwood; temp. acct., Nyassaland, July, 1915; acct., July, 1918; asst. treas., Feb., 1923; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas., in 1922, 1923, 1924, 1932-33; ag. treas., Somaliland, Apr. to Sept., 1932; dep. treas., Nyassaland, Apr., 1933; chmn., nat. civ. ser. bd., 1933-34; ag. treas., Oct., 1936-Aug., 1937 and Feb., 1938; acctt.-gen., Jan., 1939; comptlr., cust., 1939.
LOCKE, Francis John Thomas, A.M.Inst.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1896; engr., P.W.D., S.S., 1924; do., Hong Kong, 1929.

LOCKHART, Charles Ramsdale, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in desps.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. asst. treas., May, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1923; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; treas., Apr., 1933; finan. sec., 1937; do. Kenya, 1938.
LOCKHART, F. R., M.B., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.T.M. & H. (Lord).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; senr. do., Kenya, 1939.

LOCKHART, Kenneth Spencer.—B. 1890; copyist, admstr.'s office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd clk., regisr.'s office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and harbmr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1917; is also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. bd. of health; govt. trade agt. to enquire into and rep. on conditions re W.L. labourers at Santo Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, May, 1920; ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. dept., July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force; ag. 1st clk., treasy., May-Sept., 1922; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1922; income tax comsnr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.

LOCKHART-SMITH, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham; lst clk., Kowloon Magis., Hong Kong, July, 1924; attd. land office, Nov., 1926; admitted solr., sup. ct., Eng., Nov., 1932; dep. O.R. and reg. tr. marks in addn., Aug., Sept., 1933; O.R. and reg. tr. marks, Sept., 1933-Apr., 1934; asst. crown solr., Jan., 1934; asst. land offr., Aug., 1935; ag. land offr., Apr., 1936; passed Bar final, Jan., 1938; asst. crown solr., Mar., 1938; regiar., high ct., Tanganyika, Territory, Oct., 1938.
LOCKIE, JAMES REACH.—B. 1904; ed. Campbelltown and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (forestry 1st cls. hons.); asst. conservtr., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1926; do., Nigeria, 1936.

LOGAN, SIR EWEN REGINALD, Kt., Bach. (1928), J.P.—B. 1868; ed. Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1899; joined Middx. and N. London sess. and cent. crim. ct., 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 63th squad., I.Y., 1901; Queen's medal and four clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902 to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1905; chief just., Seychelles, 1914; admstr. govt. from July, 1916 to Mar., 1917 and from May to Oct., 1918; drafted in conjunction with Alfred Gellé, Esq., barrister-at-law, code of civil procedure for Seychelles; recd. thanks of sec. of state; puisne judge, G. Coast, June, 1920, with seniority from Mar., 1920; senr. puisne judge, Dec., 1922; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and Feb. to Mar., 1925; oh. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn., bd. of educn., Aug., 1925; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, May, 1927; ret., June, 1931; J.P., County of London, 1935.
LOGAN, ROBERT.—B. 1894; ed. R. Mily. Coll., Australia; war serv., 1914-19; admstr. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; cls. II, Apr., 1939.
LOGAN, WALTER EWART MILLER.—B. 1911; ed. Cheltenham Coll., Queen's Coll., Oxford, and Imp. For. Inst.; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, 1935.

LOGAN, WILLIAM MARSTON, C.M.G. (1939), O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Hulme exhibr., Bridgeman prizeman, B.A.); asst. dist. consmr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., cent. comtece of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., unattached list, 1915-17; ag. dist consmr., 1918 and Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sept. to Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st grade admstr. offr., June, 1924; sen. asst. sec., Dec., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ag. asst. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec., local govt. consmr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1927; prin. consmr. for local govt., lands, sttlmt., and mines, 1933; ch. sec. N. Rhodesia, 1937; ag. gov. Dec., 1937-Jan., 1938 and Mar.-Sept., 1938.
LOLTGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., 18th May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 27th June, 1922.

LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); 1st in nat. sci. tripus, 1910; hist. tripus, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cls. V, 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouce. Regt.; offr., cls. IV, 1919; sec. to res., Pahang, 1920; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts., S.S. and F.M.S. and dep. supervisor, rubber F.M.S., Nov., 1924; cls. II, Nov., 1928; ag. finan. comsrr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1930; sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930; cls. IB, Apr., 1931; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; ag. Br. rea., N. Sembilan, June, 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1934; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1934.
LONGHURST, ALBERT HENRY.—B. 1877; archaeological comsrr., Ceylon, Oct., 1935.
LONG, HOWARD COLLINS.—B. 1903; computor, surv. dept., Kenya, 1928; ch., do., 1936; ag. dir. surveys, 1939.
LONG INNES, HON. REGINALD HEATH, K.C.—B. 1869; judge, sup. ct., N.S. Wales, Feb., 1925; ch. judge in equity, Feb., 1935.

LONGRIDGE, THOMAS WHINNEY, B Sc. (Civil Engrng.), B.S.C. (Mining Engrng.), S.M.I.M.M.—B. 1908; ed. Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, Armstrong Coll. and Durham Univ.; engr., geol. survey, Nigeria, July, 1934; asst. water engrn., Cyprus, Apr., 1938; ag. water engrn., June-Nov., 1939.

LOOTS, JOHANNES HENDRIK, M.D., M.R.C.P. (Edin.).; D.P.H., D.T.M. and H., Cambridge.—B. 1899; ed. Boshoff, O.F.S., Capetown Univ., Univ., Cambridge Univ.; dist. surg., Ovamboland, S.W.A., 1928-29; post grad. study, London, Cambridge, Vienna, 1929-32; ag. med. supt., Nelspoort tuberculosis sanatorium, 1933; do., Somerset hosp., Capetown, 1934; med. supt., Capetown infirmary, 1935; do., Pretoria Leprosy inst., 1936; asst. health offr., 1938; capt., S.A.M.R.

LORD, EDWARD ERNST.—B. 1898 ed. Queen Elizabeth's Schl., Clifton; mily. serv. with H.A.C. and R.A.F., 1916-18; offr. Imp. cust. and excise, Jan., 1920; asst. to comsrr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Oct., 1924; senr. collr. of cust., Jan., 1935; ag. comsrr., cust., May-Aug., 1939; ag. dep. comsrr., cust., on various occasions since 1930.

LORD, GEORGE ERIC LUDWIG, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Felstead Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; col. audit dept., June, 1930; asst. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1930; do., Palestine, 1935; audr., Aden and Br. Somaliland, 1939.

LORD, WELLSELEY TRAVELLYN, F.S.I., J.P.—B. 1894; ed. Queen's Coll., Fr. Guiana, and Royal Schl. of Mines; lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, 1911; govt. survr., 1919; geol. survr., 1925; service with B.G.-Brazil bndy. comsrr., 1930-34; warden and travelling mag., No. 3 mining dist., 1936-38.
LOUW, JAN DANIEL, B.A.—B.1888; ed. Victoria Coll. and Univ., Stellenbosch; educ. serv., Cape Prov., 1912-1930; ch. translator, Pretoria, 1930.
LOWE, CLARENCE VAN RIET, B.Sc., Assoc.M.I.C.E., Cape Town F.R.S., S. Africa, etc.—B. 1894; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein and Univ. of Cape Town; dir., archaeological bureau, Johannesburg.

LOWE, ENOCH WILLIAM.—B. 1886; ed. Durban and Kingswood Schl., Bath; native affrs. dept., Transvaal, Aug., 1903; add. S.N.C., Sihwa, 1910; Great War, capt., R.F.A., 1914-19; S.N.C., Bloemwberg, 1921; add. S.N.C., Pretoria, 1923; S.N.C., Hamanskraal, 1925; N.C. and mag., Tahankulu, 1929; Nqutu, 1931; N.C., Johannesburg, 1935; N.C. and mag., Msinga, 1936; C.N.C., Northern areas, 1936.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vety. offr., May to Dec., 1926; ag. dir., vety. serv., in 1932 and 1933; ag. dep. dir., do., in 1933-34, and May-Dec., 1936; ag. dir., do., Feb.-Nov., 1936; dlr., do., 1938.

LOWSON, JAMES MCKIE ATKINSON, M.A., M.B., Ch.B. (St. Andrew's), M.R.C.P. (Edin.), D.T.M. and H. (Univ. Edin.).—B. 1897; ed. Forfar Acad., St. Andrew's Univ.; M.O., Malaya, May, 1927; physician, Johore, sup-scale offr., gd. "H," Jan., 1939.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH. (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH. (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Term., 1881; ag. solr-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonehurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Stllnts., 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in desps.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; astt. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929; chmn., oil and water bd., 1931; puisne judge, Kenya, 1931; ag. ch. just., 1937; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY, M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief coman., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease coman., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. elk., col. secretariat, 1929 and astt. col. sec., 1937; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders), 1915-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W. O. staff, 1919; edr. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apl., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; astt. coman., excise, July, 1923; astt. attlt. offr., Nov., 1926; addnl. coman., lab., Sept., 1931; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1935; do., Prov. of Uva, Sept., 1937.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEALTHY LUGARD, P.C. (1929), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, Mar., 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880; medal: Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; adminstd. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; apptd. comantr., and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comantr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed gov't., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates coman. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

LUGG, HARRY CAMP.—B. 1882; ed. Greytown; clk. and Zulu interp., mag.'office, Bulwer, Dec., 1899; Msinga, Jan., 1901; Greytown, Feb., 1902; clk., civ. coman., Zululand, Aug., 1903; native affra., Natal, Nov., 1903; Zulu interp. and astt. registr., native high ct., July, 1908; sworn Zulu interp. sup. ct., Aug., 1909; sen. clk. and astt. mag., Durban, Dec., 1916; mag., Ndewedwe, Dec., 1918; Ingwavuma, May, 1920; Mapumulo, Aug., 1921; 2nd grade mag., Richmond, Apr., 1923; 1st grade mag., Verulam July, 1928; senr. mag., Verulam, Aug., 1930; pres., native appl. ct., Natal and Transvaal, Oct., 1932; ch. native comantr., Natal, May, 1933.

LUGG, SIR HARRY CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1939), KT. Bach. (1933), C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem, M.A., D. Litt. (Oxon), hon. LL.D. (Malta).—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; priv. sec. to the gov't. of Sierra Leone, 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the gov't. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comantr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Goold-Adams, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1911; astt. sec. to gov't., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comantr., Paphos, 1917; comantr., Famagusta, 1918; sec., Cyprus defence comtee., Aug., 1914; served in H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; political offr. to Admirals Lord Wetter Wemyss and Christ- ian and gov't. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to June, 1916; (lieut.-comdr. and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.); recl. thanks of adm'y.; Italian med. for mily. valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comantr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; astt. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; mem. of comsrs. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jeru- salem, and on the Jaffa disturbances, 1921, and finan. comantr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; accredited rep. to Perm. Mandates Comantr., 1930; lieut. gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; gov. and commdnr.-in-ch., Fiji, and high comantr. for W. Pac., 1933; a del. for U.K. to Pacific defence confcn., N. Zealand, 1939; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hons.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus under the Turks," 1921; "An Eastern Chequerboard," 1934; "More Moves on an Eastern Chequer- board," 1935; "The Making of Modern Turkey." 1936; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," and "Handbook of Palestine and Transjordan."

LUKE, S. E. V., B.A.—B. 1905; ed. St. George's, Harpenden and Oxford, Bracegirdle exhibr., Wadham Coll., 1924; 2nd cls. mod. hist., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. astt. inspr., taxes (inland rev.), 1927; apptd. after further compet. exam., junr. clk., House of Commons, Feb., 1930; transf. to C.O. as astt. prin., Sept., 1930; joint sec., col. suvrey comtee., 1931-33; visited Br. Hond. as sec. to finan. commr. (Sir A. Pim), 1933; astt. pvt. sec. to Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister (now Viscount Swinton) 7th Apr., 1934, to Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, 7th June, 1936; to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 27th Nov., 1935; seconded to Palestine admnstr., 4th Jan., 1936; ag. prin., 1st Nov., 1937.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.

LUPPRIAN, ERNEST VICTOR, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Dip. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Bishop Currier Gram. Schol. and St. Mary's Coll., Madras; Baldwin High Schol. and Aldham Coll., Bangalore Univ., Madras; med. coll., Madras; St. Mary's hosp. med. schl.; L.S.T.M. and Hygiene; med. offr., Spore, port health, Apr., 1923; do., Mlym. emign. depots, India, July, 1923; med. offr., P. Wellesley, Mar., 1928 and Jan., 1931; sup.-scaled med. and health offr., gr. B., Feb., 1935; ch. med. offr., Malacca, July, 1936.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1938)—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland admnstr., Sept., 1902; astt. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. sey., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927; ag. audr. for high comsn. territories, 1937.

LYALL-GRANT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1934), M.A. (Aberdeen), LL.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; ag. judge, high ct., in 1909, 1910, 1912 and 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising comsn., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prots. defence O. in C., 1916-19; judge advoc. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. councils; puiane just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Jamaica, 1932; ret., 1936.

MABB, CAPT. VICTOR LEOPOLD, M.C., Chevalier de la Légion d'Honneur.—B. 1889; joined "Fort Garry" Regt. 18th Canadian Reserves, Aug., 1914; served in France as comsnr. to Tank Corps, Dec., 1916; left Army, July, 1920; ment. in desps.; admstve. serv., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1920; transf. prisons dept., S. Provs. as suplt., prisons, 1924; ag. inspr., prisons, 1925; dep. dir., prisons, 1926; dir., prisons, S. Provs., 1932; dir., prisons, 1938.

MACASKIE, CAPT. CHARLES FREDERICK CUNNINGHAM, barrister-at-law, F.R.G.S.—B. 1888; cadet, N. Borneo, Aug., 1910; 1st cls. mag., Mar., 1913; on mil. ser., 1916-19 (ment. in desp.); astt. govt. sec., Jan., 1920; legal advr. in addn., July, 1920; prot., lab., Mar., 1922; prot., lab., sec. for Chinese affrs. and legal advr., June, 1925; res., July, 1929; judge, sess. ct., Aug., 1930; judge, high ct., Dec., 1930; O.A.G., Dec., 1930-Jan., 1931; res. and off. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1931; seconded as govt. sec., Sarawak, Sept., 1932; dep. gov. N. Borneo and ag. ch. just. in addn., Dec., 1934; ch. just., Ost., 1935; O.A.G., May-July, 1937.
MOBRIDE, ANDREW HOWIE, B.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Dumbarton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.
MOBRIDE, SENATOR HON. PHILIP ALBERT MARTIN.—Mem., H. of R., Grey, S. Aust., 1931-37; senator, S. Aust. from Oct., 1937; astt. min. for commerce, Menzies miny., Apr., 1939.

MCCALLAN, E.A.—B. 1874; ed. Ontario agrl. coll.; clk., postoffice, Bermuda, 1903; olk., works dept., 1904; rev. offr., 1907; agric. astt., 1913; dir. of agric., Bermudas 1920; del. to Washington 1920 and 1928; mem., immigr. lab. bd.

MCCALLUM, ALEXANDER.—B.1890; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; G.E.A. campaign, 1916-18; trooper, Tanganyika Territory pol., 1918; astt. inspr., 1919; inspr., 1920; astt. supt., 1923; supt., 1928; ag. dep. comsnr., pol. and prisons, 1931.

MCCALLUM, ANGUS, O.B.E. (1939), M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin. and Aberdeen Univ.; capt., B.E.F., France, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1918, M.C. and bar. ment. in desps.; lieut., 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1918 to June, 1922; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1923; comsnr., gr. II, 1929; dist. offr., 1934.

McCARTER, HARRY BERTRAM ADAIR, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1883; entd. civil serv., Cape Col., 1899; served S. African war in Fingeland native levies, 1901-02; law dept., Transvaal civil serv., 1902; clk., Swaziland admnstr., June, 1907; olk. and dep. astt. comsnr., Apr., 1911; passed Cape Univ. civ. serv. lower law exam., 1911; astt. comsnr., 1928; ag. dep. res. comsnr., Mar.-Sept., 1935; dep. res. comsnr., Oct., 1935; ag. res. comsnr. from Apr., 1937.
MACCARTHY, CHARLES THOMAS, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1898; M.O., Malaya, Dec., 1927; M.O. and radiologist, Seremban, Oct., 1937.

MACCARTHY, DENNIS DOUGLAS, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. Schol.; med. offr., rural med. serv., Tokanui, N.Z., 1924-27; med. offr., Zanzibar, Mar., 1927; malaria research offr., 1934.

MACCARTHY, LESLIE ERNEST VIVIAN, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hona. final jurisprudence; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1911; crown counsel, Gold Coast, Sept., 1921; ag. solr.-gen. for various periods, 1922-33; extraordinary mem., leg. coun., Feb., 1930; ag. circ. judge, Ashanti and N.T., Oct., 1931 to Feb., 1932; solr.-gen., 1933; ag. puiane judge, Apr. and Nov., 1933; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods; revising comsnr., Gold Coast Laws, Sept., 1935; puiane judge, Sept., 1939.

MCCONECHY, FERGUS MALCOLM GRAEME, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Sevenoaks Grammar Schol. and Manchester Univ.; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engrnr., 1922; senr. exec. engrnr., 1929; ag. dep. dir. pub. works, S.S., Apr. to Dec., 1932 and July-Nov., 1934; state engrnr., grade I, Nov., 1935.

MCCORMICK, ALEXANDER.—B. 1892; astt. clk., G.P.O., London, 1912; 2nd divn. clk., accountants office, G.P.O., Dublin, 1914; astt. acct., G.P.O., Lagos, 1926; survr. and ag. cn. acct., for various periods until apptd. col. postmr., Gibraltar, July, 1930; ch. acct., posts. and tels., Nigeria, 1939.

McCORMICK, SPENCER ACKLON.—B. 1878; ed. Komgtha Pub. Sch., and pvt. tuition; joined Cape civ. serv., 1896; native affrs. dept., Transvaal, Feb., 1897, and served in Tembuland and Transkei transf. to head office, Cape Town, 1900; ast. mag., Lichtenburg, June, 1901; transf. to various country stations between 1902 and 1906; stationed Johannesburg as 2nd civ. mag., 1907; div. mag., Pretoria, Aug., 1908; ch. mag., Pretoria, May, 1924; under-secr. for just., 1929; judge, native high ct., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1930.

McCRACKEN, ALBERT VICTOR NEILSON.—B. 1886; dep. regir. and solr., Trinidad; serv. with Lond. Regt., 1915-19; marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, Jan., 1926; ast. crown solr., May, 1931; regir. and marshal and regir.-gen., 1932; regir. and marshal, 1936.

MCCRAE, WILLIAM MOIR, M.M.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-18; engnr. survr., S.S., 1920; ast. engr., Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Nov., 1929; exec. engr., June, 1933; ag. ass. exec. engr., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1936; ag. stlmt. engr., Malacca, Feb., 1937; ag. state engr., Kelantan, Aug., 1937; ass. ex. engr., Jan., 1938.
MCURDY, NEVIN RUSSELL.—B. 1900; ast. dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Feb., 1928; dir., July, 1930.

McDAVID, E. F., M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; sec., excess profits tax bd. Br. Guiana, 1920; 1st cl. clk., treasy. and sec., E.P.T. bd., 1921; ch. acct., col. transp. dept., 1923; ag. dep. col. treasy., 1927-29; consnr., income tax, Apr., 1929; dep. col. treasy., June, 1929; ag. col. treasy., for various periods, 1930-34; served on various committees, 1929-34; temp. M.L.C., 1930-34; prov. M.E.C., 1932 and 1934; col. treasy., May, 1935.
McDONALD, ALEXANDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1903; ed. Fraesburgh Acad. and Edin. Univ.; ast. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1925; senr. exec. engr., 1937; ag. ast. D.P.W. and ag. dep. D.P.W., 1938.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER GORDON, F.G.S., M.I.M.M., B.E., N.Z., A.O.S.M.—B. 1885; inspr., mines, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; supernmy., inspr., mines, Jan., 1912; supernmy. ast. warden, mines and ag. warden, mines, Oct., 1920; ast. warden, mines, Dec., 1921 and May, 1925; 1st cls. mag. and collr., land rev., in addn., Nov., 1930; senr. inspr., mines, Selangor, Jan., 1931; warden, mines, Pahang, 1933; do., Selangor, 1934; ag. senr. ward., mines, F.M.S., Sept., 1935; warden, mines, Perak, Jan., 1936; title changed to senr. inspr., mines, Jan., 1938; ag. ch. inspr., do., F.M.S., Apr., 1938.
MACDONALD, SENATOR HON. ALLAN NICOLL.—Served, A.I.F., 1914-19; senator for W. Australia in C'wealth Parlt., from Mar., 1938; ast. min., Lyons miny., Nov., 1937.

MACDONALD, ALLAN RONALD, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attd., secretariat, Jan., 1930; pvt. sec. to gov., Feb., 1932; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1934; addnl. ast. gov't. ast., Colombo, June, 1935; pol. mag., Kandy, Oct., 1935; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Mar., 1937; ast. gov't. ast., Anuradhapura, Apr., 1938.

MACDONALD, HON. DANIEL ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. privately and at gram. schl., comcl. coll. and Prince of Wales coll., P.E.I.; admitted to bar, P.E.I., 1883; Manitoba bar, 1885; practised law in Manitoba; judge, st. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1906; ch. just., st. of King's bench, Manitoba, 1927.

MACDONALD, ERIC MALCOLM.—B. 1909; ed. Margate Coll., prob. inspr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1928; passed do. Mar., 1929; inspr. pol., Feb., 1931; ast. commr., pol., Apr., 1931; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., July, 1936.

MACDONALD, F. H. E.—Ast. supt. pol., B. Honduras, July, 1919; ag. supt., pol., July, 1919 to July, 1920; supt., Belize fire brig., Oct., 1919; on milly. service in Egypt, Mesopotamia, and India, 1915-19 (ment. in desp.); Meritorious Ser. Medal, 1918; dist. commr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

MACDONALD, FARQUHAR VICTOR, A.M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Dingwall Acad.; water and hbr. wks., 1904; G.N.R., 1909 and 1917; Gordon Highlanders, 1914-17; ast. engr., Gold Coast rly., Jan., 1920; dist. engr., 1929; ch. engr., Nov., 1932; gen. man., 1937.

MACDONALD, HON. HEITOR Y.—B. 1876; ed., common schls. of Nova Scotia, and Univ. of St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Antigonish, N.S. (B.A. 1900); studied law in the law schl. of Dalhousie Univ.; called to bar of N.S. 1903; practised in Sydney, N.S., removed to Regina, 1906; called to bar of Sask., May, 1906; law clk. in atty.gen's dept.; city solr., Regina; K.C., 1913; justice, ct. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.

MACDONALD, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1868; ed. coll. inst., Stratford, Ontario, Toronto Univ., Osgood Hall, Toronto; called to Ontario bar, 1889; Brit. Columbia bar, 1897; K.C., 1905; bencher, Brit. Columbia Law Society, 1906-7; elec. to legis. Brit. Columbia, 1903; re-elec. 1907; leader of the opposition, 1903 to 1909; ch. just., ct. of appeal, Brit. Columbia, 1909.
MACDONALD, JAMES FRASER, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1900; ed. Edin. Univ., 1924; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1931; M.O., N. Rhodesia, 1931; surg. specialist, 1939.

MACDONALD, JOHN, D.F.C. (1918), B.Sc. (hons.), F.L.S.—B. 1894; ed. Univ. of Bristol; on war serv., 1914-19; temp. capt., 1918; ast. mycologist, Kenya, 1920; mycologist, 1921; senr. mycologist (title later changed to senr. plant pathologist), 1930; dir., agr., Cyprus, Nov., 1937.

MACDONALD, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1874; min. of pub. wks. and highways, P.E.I., 1923-26; dir., national serv. and hon. fuel and food control admtr. for P.E.I. during war; 1st el. to P.E.I. legis., 1908; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1925; min. without portfolio in Meighen adminstr., 1926; min. without portfolio in Bennett cabinet, 1930; mem., senate, 1935.
MACDONALD, J. H., M.B., Ch.B. (Aberd.)—B. 1902; mil. serv., 1922-25; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926.

MACDONALD, Rt. Hon. MALCOLM, M.P. (Ross and Cromarty)—B. 1901; ed. Bedales Schl., Petersfield and Queen's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; parly. under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 3rd Sept., 1931; re-apptd., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for Col., 7th June, 1935; S. of S. for Dom. affrs., 27th Nov., 1935; S. of S. for the Col., 16th May, 1938; S. of S. for Dom. affrs., in addn., 1938-39.

MACDONALD, Patrick Donald.—B. 1909; ed. Marlborough Coll.and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. hons., 1931; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1932; dep. consnr. (temp. and provnl.), 1934; ag. sec. to govt., 1935; admin. offr., 1936.

MACDONALD, ROBERT ARTHUR SCRTYEOGR.—B. 1898; B.Sc., Edinburgh Univ.; M.R.C.V.S., Royal (Dick) Vety.Coll.; war. serv., Machine Gun Corps, 1917-18; vetr. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1922; vety. research offr., 1927; ag. ch. vety. offr., 1927; ag. dep. dir., animal health, 1932-33; dep. dir., vety. services, July, 1933; publications, "Cattle Management, a Textbook for use in Native Schools in Africa" in collab. with Fell; "Notes on Rabies."

MACDONALD, RONALD, M.C. and 2 Bars.—B. 1895; apptd. to C.A. for Cola. on passing jr. appts. (intermediate) open civ. serv. exam., June, 1914; war service, France and Flanders, 1915-19; seconded from C.A. Office for tour of duty with audit dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; asst. audr., grade A, F.M.S., Aug., 1926; ag. asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S. and asst. audr. (commercial) in addn., Sept., 1926; ag. asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., in addn. May, 1931-Jan., 1932; asst. audr., S'pore, Apr., 1934; ag. senr. asst. audr., Palestine, 1936; audr., Sierra Leone, 1939.

MACDONELL, SIR PHILIP JAMES, K.T.BACH. (1925), B.C.L., M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Clifton Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol.; 1st cls. hist. schls., 1894); of Gray's Inn, utter barrister; Bacon schol.; I. of C. prizeman, constitutional law; called to the bar, 26th Jan., 1900; pub. pros., Transvaal, Apr., 1906; asst. crown pros., Transvaal, Feb., 1907; legal adviser and pub. pros., N.W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1908; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1911; ag. judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Apr. to Oct., 1914, Apr. to July, 1916, June to Nov., 1917; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 13th Sept., 1918; chmn., Native Reserves Comms., 1924-25 (East Luangwa) and 1926-7 (Railway Dist.) ; ch. just., Trinidad, 1927; ch. just., Ceylon, 1930; ret., 1936; comsrr. to N. Rhodesia to examine and report upon inter-tribal relations, Balovale district, 1938-39; priv. counsellor., mem. of the judol. comttee., 1939.

MCDONNELL, SIR MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH, Kt. Bach. (1929), M.A.—B. 1882; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pres., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; asst. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, Apr., 1918; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Apr., 1920; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927 and ex officio pres., full ct. of appl. of Egypt, 1930; compiler, revd. edn. Sierra Leone Laws, 1925; edtr., law rept., of Sierra Leone, 1929; ret., 1930; edtr., law rept., Palestine, 1934.

MCDOUGALL, JOHN CRICHTON, B.A.—B. 1912; ed. Jesus Coll., Cambridge; cadet Hong Kong civ. serv., Aug., 1934; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Aug., 1936; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., Aug., 1936; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., Oct., 1937.

MACDOUGALL, ARCH.—B. 1889; ed. West of Scot. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; civ. engr., P. and W. Anderson, rly. contractors, 1906-11; asst. engr., Caledonian Rly., 1911-12; M.I.E.S., 1914; A.M.I. transport; engr., way and works dept., Demerara Rly. Co., Br. Guiana, 1912-21; ch. engr., ways and wks., col. transport dept., 1922; gen. man., transport and harbors, dept., 1932; J.P., 1936; twice provinl. M.I.C., 1936.

MACDOUGALL, DAVID MERCER, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. St. Andrews Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928; 1st cls. magistracy, 1931; dist. offr., South, 1933-34; J.P., 1934; 3rd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1935; extra asst. col. sec., Oct., 1935; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Aug.-Sept. and Oct.-Nov. 1936; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Feb., 1937 to Mar., 1939; seconded Hong Kong dept. of infrn., and sec., Far Eastern bureau of Brit. min. of infrn., Oct., 1939.

MACE, CLIVE, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. Wellingborough Schol. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; pt. I., maths. tripos; pts. I. and II., geog. tripes; supt., surveys, Cyprus, Mar., 1932; ag. inspr., mines and lab., various periods 1933-38; ag. dir., land registrrn. and surveys, Jan.-Mar., 1939; registr., trade unions, Jan., 1939.

MCDELLERY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cls. hona. maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. registr., sup. ct., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dep. reglar., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; addt. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Cola. on opium question at internat. red cross confere., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Confce., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec. for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gor.'s dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., various periods, 1934-39.
MCELROY, ROBERT SAMUEL, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), D.P.H., D.T.M. (Liverp.).—B. 1900; med. offr. (health), Uganda, 1924; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.

MCLOWANE, SIR PERCY ALEXANDER, Kt. Bach. (1939), B.A. (Hons.), LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to Irish bar (King's Inn), 1908; Alberta bar, 1913; temp. lieut., Royal Irish Rifles, B.E.F., 1916-19; stip. mag. and coroner, 2nd and 3rd dists., St. Lucia, 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Apr., 1923; senr. crown coun., Jan., 1926; ag. solr.-gen., Kenya and M.C., Oct., 1925 to Mar., 1926; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1927; K.C., N. Ireland, Feb., 1929; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1930; atty.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1933; ch. just., S.S., 1936; prepared revd. edn., Laws of Straits Settlements, 1936.
McKENZIE, HON. JOHN, M.P.—El. to H. of R. Commonwealth of Aust. for Echuca, 1934; Indi., 1937; mem., bankruptcy legisln. comttee., 1935-36; min. for interior, Lyons miny., Nov., 1937.

McEWEN, JOHN HENRY, F.S.I.; M.Inst.T.—B. 1886; ed. Kimberley Pub. Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Kimberley; land survey dept., E.A.P., 1911; resigned, 1918; estates offr., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbors., 1926; prin. asst. to gen. man., Nigerian rly., Feb., 1934; gen. man., Nigerian rly., 1936.

McEWEN, JOSEPH REGINALD POWELL, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1890; ed. Sierra Leone Grammar Schol.; Sierra Leone govt., 1906-09; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, S. Nigeria, 1909; office asst. Nigerian secret., 1920; asst. sec. 1922; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1933 and 1937-38; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1933-36; senr. asst. sec., 1937.
McEWIN, Hon. Alexander Lyell.—B. 1897; ed. at Prince Alfred Coll., South Australia; el. to leg. coun. for Northern Dist., 1934; ch. sec., min. of health and min. of mines, 1939.

MACFADYEN, Quentin Allison Ashby, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Berkhamsted sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford cls. (3rd hors. hist.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1927; ast. aupt., impts. and expts., 1930; ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931; J.P., 1931; ch. ast. do., Mar., 1933; dep. regis., sup. ct., May, 1933; 2nd pol. mag., 1934; 1st clk. and mag., Kowloon, Jan., 1935; 2nd pol. mag., 1935; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1936; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), 1938.

MACFLARKLANE, Parlane—B. 1892; ast. engr., Caledonian Rly. 1915; war serv., R.E., 1915-19; Caledonian Rly., 1919; ast. engr., K.U.R. & H. 1921; sr. ast. engr., 1926; dist. engr., 1926; sr. dist. engr., 1926.

McGEGH, William Ryder, M.A.—B. 1901; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; dist. offr., Kenya, 1927; ag. ast. sec., secretariat, Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; admstive offr., Zanzibar, 1934; ast. sec., secret., 1936; pvt. sec. to Sultan, 1937; ast. dist. comsnr., Palestine, Dec., 1937.

McGILL, Ernest Arthur, M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Univ. of Leeds (Brown entrance schl.) lect. in engng., S.A. Schl. of mines (now Witwatersrand Univ.), 1912; hon. sec., S.A. Eng. standards com., 1912; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1915; lieut., 1917; capt., 1918; served in Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ast. engr., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engr., 1935.

McGILL, Major Harold Wigmore, M.D., M.C.V.D.—B. 1879; ed. Manitoba Univ. (M.D.); served as M.O. with 31st Batt. Infv. and 5th Canadian Field Ambulance during war; pres., Alta. Mil. Inst., 1928; first elec. to Alberta legis. assem. at g.e., 1930; resig. on apptntn. as dep. aupt. gen., Indian affrs., 1932; dir. Indian affrs., dept., mines and resources, 1936.

MACKIE, JAMES RICHARD, B.Sc. (Agric.)—B. 1896; ed. Sexey's Sch., Bruton, Somerset and Univ. Coll., Reading; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1921; dep. asst. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1928; asst. dir., agr., 1929; dir. agr., 1936.

MACKIE, JOHN BEVERIDGE, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Liv.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; asst. med. offr., Victoria hosp., Aug., 1931; med. offr. in ch., offrs. families and Lai Chi Kok prisons, Nov., 1932; asst. med. offr., Kowloon hosp., Dec., 1933; med. offr. in ch., New Territories and offrs. families and Kowloon mortuary, Feb., 1934; sg. malariologist, Mar., 1936; ag. health offr., New Territories, Dec., 1936; visiting M.O., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries and M.O. in ch., officers' families, June, 1937; ag. health offr., New Territories, 1938; malariologist, 1939.

MACKILLIGIN, ROBERT SPRINGETT, O.B.E., M.C., M.I.M.M., F.Inst.Pet.—B. 1890; ed. Cranbrook Sch., Kent and Schol. of Mines, Camborne, Cornwall; inspr., mines and petroleum technologies, Trinidad, Mar., 1936.

McKINNEY, E. H., M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1877; ent. Bahamas civ. ser., 1898; 2nd clk., P.O., 1901; res. just., Exuma, 1904; Out Island commr. (2nd div.), 1909; commr. (1st div.), 1912; senr. commr., 1928; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1931, 1932, 1934, 1935 and 1936; ag. sisa inspr., Nov., 1935.
McKINNON, ALEXANDER STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Kintyre Tech. Schol. and West of Scotld. Agril. Coll.; senr. asst. livestock offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925-28; agril. offr., Somaliland, 1938.

McKINNON, ANDREW ALEXANDER, M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1896; Br. postoffice; 1913; milv. serv., 1915-19; postal clk. & telegraphist, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; postmtr., 1926; asst. survr., 1930; postal asst. dir., B. Somaliland, 1931; asst. treas., 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Apr.-Sept., 1937 and Sept.-Dec., 1938.

MACKINNON, WILLIAM LUNAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), 1911.—B. 1888; asst. audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; ag. senr. asst. audr., in 1918 and 1919; ag. asst. treas. (in addition to his own duties), May to Sept., 1919; ag. senr. asst. audr., Oct., 1919; ag. aud. audr. on various occasions, 1926-29; ag. audr. May-Oct., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; prin. do., 1934; acctnt.-gen. and currency offr., 1937.
MACKLEY, GARNET HERCULES, C.M.G. (1938).—B. 1883; ed. pub. schla., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. rlv. dept., 1900; ch. clk., 1928; asst. gen. man., 1931; gen. man., 1933.

MACKY, WALLACE ARMSTRONG.—B. 1903; ed. Auckland Grammar Schol., Auckland Univ. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; M.Sc., Ph.D.; mast., Auckland Grammar Schol., 1924-25; lect. in physics, Auckland Univ., 1926-28; meteorologist, S'pore; 1931-33; lect. in physics, Otago Univ., 1933-35; asst. meteorologist, Wellington, N.Z., 1935-37; meteorologist, Auckland, 1937-39; meteorologist, Bermuda, 1931.

MCLAUCHLAN, HON. ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. Adelaide Univ.; called to bar, 1895; ed. C'wealth senator for S. Australia, Nov., 1925; hon. min., Aug., 1926; ag. atty.-gen., 1926-27; plenipotentiary for Australia for signing of Kellogg Pact, 1928; vice-pres., exec. comm., Lyons' ministry, 1932; resigned, 1934; P.M.G., 1934; do. and leader of senate, Nov., 1937-Nov., 1938.

MCLAUCHLAN, JAMES BOYD, M.C., M.Sc., B.E., A.M.I.C.E. Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway and Royal Univ. of Ireland; on active serv., Canadian F.A., 1915-19; M.C., 1917; mentd. in desp., 1918; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1919; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrn., pub. wks. serv., Malaya, 1934; state engrn., grade I, Dec., 1939.

MACLAREN, COL. THE HON. MURRAY, C.A.M.C., C.M.G., L.D., M.D., C.M., M.R.C.S., F.A.C.S., Commander of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, also of Order of Avia Portugal.—B. 1861; ed. Grainmar Schol., St. John, N. B.; univ. of New Brunawick; Edinburgh univ.; Vienna; served overseas, 1914-19; lat el. to H. of C., Canada, 1921; re-el. at g. e., 1925 and 1926; min. of pensions and national health in Bennett admstrn., 1930; lieut. gov., New Brunswick, 1935.

MCLAUGHLAN, ALLAN HEARTLEY PHILIP, A.M.Inst. C.E., P.A.S.I., chartered survr.—B. 1903; ed. English schl., Cyprus, and prte. tuition; tracer, P.W.D., Cyprus, 1920; temp. foreman, P.W.D., 1925; draughtsman and record keeper, P.W.D., 1926; asst. engrn., P.W.D., 1929; ag. divnl. engrn., P.W.D., on various occasions, 1930-35; divnl. engrn., 1938; title changed to exec. engrn., 1939.

MCLEAN, F. H.—B. 1889; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to bd. of educn., May, 1908; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1911; on military serv., from 25th Apr., 1917, to 23rd Feb., 1919; cler.offr., higher grade, 24th Jan., 1922.

MACLEAN, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1888; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Mar., 1918 to Mar., 1919; temp. capt., Mar., 1919 to June, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; sleeping sickness offr., Apr., 1926; D.D.M.S., 1935.

MCLEAN, SIMON JAMES, C.M.G., M.A., L.D.B., Ph.D.—B. 1871; commr. of the Canadian govt. to report on rly. rate grievances, 1901; advsr. to commr., of govt. to draft Railway Act, 1903; profr. of polit. econ., Toronto Univ., 1906-1908; apptd. a mem. of rly. comm., Canada, 1908; re-apptd., Nov., 1918; asst. chief commr., Aug., 1919.

MCLEAY, SENATOR HON. GEORGE.—El. to senate for S. Australia, g. e., 1934; mem., senate standing comte. on regs. and ordinances, Sept., 1935; chmn., Aug., 1937; vice-pres., exec. comm., Lyons' min., Nov., 1938-Apr., 1939; do., Page min., 7th-26th Apr., 1939; min. for commerce, Menzies, min., Apr., 1939.
MCLENNAN, DAVID, M.A. (Cantab).—B. 1904; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1931.

MACLENNAN, Finlay Malcolm.—B. 1887; ed., Tarbutt Public Sch.; Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., High Sch.; ent. serv. of dept. of rly. and canals, Can., Sept., 1910; terminals work, as jnr. engr., 1912; asst. audr., dept., rly. and canals, Ottawa, 1st Apr., 1915; audr., 1917; ch. audr., 1918; asst. comptlr., 1920; comptr., 1930.

MACLENNAN, Ian Morrison Ross.—B. 1899; ed., Hymers Coll., Hull and Worcester Coll., Oxford; schol., 1928; 2nd cls. hous. mods., 1930; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; Gladstone research student, St. Deiniol's liby., Hawarden, 1932-3; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1933; ptye. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for Dom. affrs. (Marquess of Hartington), 15th Feb., 1937; sec. to high commr., in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., Apr., 1938; ag. prin., 2nd Jan., 1939.

MACLENNAN, Kenneth.—B. 1893; U.K. Post Office, May, 1910—Sept., 1920; supt., P. and T., S.S., July, 1921; per. asst. to sec., postal affrs., S.S., and F.M.S., Dec., 1927, Nov., 1930, Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. P.M.G., Johore, Apr., 1931 and June, 1934; contr. P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1936; contr., posts, S'pore, 1938.

MACLENNAN, Norman Macpherson.—B. 1895; ed., Waitaki College (N.Z.) and Aberdeen Univ.; M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.); war serv., 1915-18; res. med. offr., City Hosp., Aberdeen, 1922; bacteriologist, James Mackenzie Clinical Inst., and M.O.H., St. Andrews, 1923; asst. M.O.H. and tuberculosis offr., Woolwich, 1925; health offr., Kenya, 1927; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1929; senr. health offr., N. Rhodesia, 1931; S.M.O., (endemic diseases), Palestine, 1933; D.M.S., Br. Guiana, 1938.

McNEICE, THOMAS PERCY FERGUS, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; ed. Bradford Grammar Sch., Keble Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1925; offr., cl. V, Jan., 1928; offr., cl. IV, Jan., 1932; ag. 2nd asst. secy., B, S.S., Dec., 1935; offr. cl. III, Jan., 1937; ag. prot., Chinese, Johore, Mar., 1938.

MACNIE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1899; 2nd lieut., Seaforth Highlands, 1918; lieut., 1919-20; served, France, 1918; sub-instr., pol., B. Guiana, 1921; dist. instr., 1925; ag. detective instr., 1925; ag. county instr., 1926 and 1928-31; seconded to col. secretariat as addnl. asst. col. sec., May, 1931; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1932; senr. dist. comsrr., 1936.
MCPHEE, SIR JOHN CAMERON, K.C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1878; premier of Tasmania since 1st June, 1928; ret.

MACPHERSON, BARBARA.—Ed. Durness H.G. Schl. and Skerry's Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after compet. exam. typist, Lond. tel. serv., 1912; transf. to C.O., May, 1918; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler offr., Jan., 1923.

MACPHERSON, CAPT. COLIN, R. OF O., K.A.R.—B. 1898; ed. Alleyn's Coll., Dulwich; lieut., S.L.I., 1917-1920; asst. instr., pol., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; cadet, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929.
MACPHERSON, DONALD FRASER, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1906; ed. Kingussie Secondary Schl. and R. "Dick" Vety, Coll., Edin.; vety. offr., Kenya, 1928.

MACPHERSON, JOHN STUART.—B. 1898; ed. Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ. (M.A., 1921); 2nd lieut., A. and S. Highlanders, Jan., 1918; France, Apr., 1918-Jan., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. 4th asst. sec. to govt. F.M.S., Aug., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Bentong, Mar., 1928; relief asst. sec. to govt. F.M.S., Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1933 attched. C.O., Sept., 1933; ag. asst. advr., supt., lands and regis., titlcs, Kelantan, Oct., 1935; prin. asst. sec., Nigeria, 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1938; ch. sec., Palestine, 1939.

McPHILLIPS, HON. ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1861; ed. St. Boniface and Manitoba Colls.; barrister: K.C., 1900; elected to leg. assem., B.C., 1898; atty.-gen., 1903; subsequently pres. of coun.; judge, ct. of appeal, B.C., 1913.

MCQUADE, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1893; highest marks in U.K. in entrance exam. for cust. and excise, Mar., 1913; Imp. cust. and excise, July, 1913; war serv., Flanders with R.A., 1917-18; asst., cust., Kenya, July, 1920; collr., cust., Aug., 1924; sec., tariff comm., 1930; dep. comptlr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; census offr., 1931; sec., econ. advsy. bd., 1931-33; comptlr., cust., Nov., 1934.
MACQUILLAN, CECIL JAMES, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C. Dublin).—M.O., R.A.F. (res.) served U.K. and Irak, 1924-26; R.M.O., Kent and Canterbury hosp., 1926; medl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1927.

McROBERTS, CAPT. B. A.K.—Natal mounted pol., 1903-08; Natal rebellion, 1906; Usutu operations, 1907; instr., veterinary pol., E.A.P., 1909; asst. instr., pol., Mar., 1910; asst. supt., Sept., 1911; capt., Gordon Highlanders, Aug., 1915; France, 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1917; attd., Highland Light Infy., Mesopotamia, Mar., 1917; seconded, polt. dept., dist. mag. and dep. mily. gov., Basrah, 1917-18; mily. mission to Siberian govt., 1919; supt., pol., Kenya Colony, May, 1920; mag., Zanzibar Prot. Jan., 1921; ag. asst. judge, in 1922 and 1924; ag. atty-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1925; pol. mag., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1937.

McSWAN, DAVID MORRISON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B., Certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1895; ed. Kent Rd. High Schl. and Glas. Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., July, 1925; do., T.T.S., Hosp., S'pore., June, 1931; do., Gen. Hosp., J. Bahru, June, 1932; med. offr. superscale grade B, Sept., 1936; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore., Feb., 1937.

MADELEY, WALTER BAYLEY.—B. 1873; ed. Bombay High Schl.; apprenticed Woolwich Arsenal; came to S. Africa, 1903; rep. Benoni in Union Parlmt. since 1910; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks., Nov., 1925; min. for lab. and social welfare in Smuts cabinet, 1939.

MAFFIE, SIR JOHN LOADER, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.B., (1934), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1921), C.S.I. (1920), C.I.E. (1916).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby and Christ Ch., Oxford; ent. I.C.S., 1899; transf. polit. dept., 1905; served with Mohmand Field Force, 1908 (med. and clasp); polit. agt., Khyber, 1909-12; dep. comsrr., Peshawar, 1914-15; dep. sec., foreign and polit. dept., govt. of India, 1915-16; ch. polit. offr., Afghanistan with N.W. Frontier Field Force, 1919; pvt. sec. to viceroy, 1916-20; ch. sec. to Duke of Connaught, 1921; ch. comsrr., N.W. Frontier prov., India, 1921-24; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1926; perm. undersec. of state for the cols., 1933-37; United Kingdom rep. to Eire, 1939.
MAGLADERY, THOMAS.—B. 1876; M.L.A. for Temiskaming, Ont., 1914-24; promoted in war serv. to capt.; dir., soldier settlmt. bd., 1931; dep. min., immigrn. and colonization, 1934.

MAGRATH, CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1860; ed., priv. tuition; irrigtn. and development work in S. Alberta from 1878; mem. for Lethbridge in N.W.T. assem. for several years, and finally mem. of prov. cabinet; el. to H.C., Can., 1908; for Medicine Hat; def., 1911; fuel contr., Can., 1917-20; chmn., Internatl. Joint Comsln., Can. sect., 1921.

MAGUIRE, ROBERT AUGUSTINE JOSEPH—B. 1888; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1926; cadet, R.H.A., 1916; 2nd lieut. MARSH, SIR EDWARD HOWARD, K.C.V.O. (1936), C.B., C.M.G., M.V.O.—B. 1872; ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st cl. classical tripes, 1893; sen. chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st cl. with distinction classical tripes, Part II, and B.A., 1895; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. elk., C.O., Sept. 29th, 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, July, 1900; to Mr. Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; 1st cls. elk., 1st Apr., 1905; priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, U.S. of S. for the Colonies, Jan., 1907; accompanied Mr. Churchill on his visit to E. Africa and Uganda, 1907-8; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Pres. of B. of T., 1908; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Home Sec., 1910; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as first Lord of the Admiralty, 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, June to Nov., 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Asquith, first lord of treasury, 16th Nov., 1915; returned to C.O. as 1st cls. elk., Dec., 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as min. of munitions, July, 1917; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill, as sec. of state for war, Jan., 1919, as S. of S. for the Colonies, Feb., 1921 and as chancellor of exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; priv. sec. to the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 23rd Jan., 1924; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Exchequer, Nov., 1925; pte. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas as Lord Privy Seal, and afterwards as S. of S. for Dom. affrs., Dec., 1929; pte. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas as S. of S. for Dom. affrs. and for the cols., 26th Aug., 1931; ret., 1937; trustee, Tate Gallery, 1937.

W.—Imp. serv., 1897-98; elk., attv.-gen.'s dept., Cape Town, 1898; examr., accts., 1899; elk., res. mag., King William's Town and distr., stamps, 1900; sec., plague admrstr., 1903; 2nd cls. examr., control and audit dept., Cape, 1905; inland rev. dept., 1910; contr. and audtr.-gen.'s dept., 1912; senr. elk., 1912; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1916; survr., rev., Pretoria, 1921; Cape Town, 1929; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1930; ditto, Johannesburg, 1932.

MARSHALL, CAPT. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry.—B. 1893; exhib. Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1911; hon. mods. (maths.), 2nd cl. hons., 1912; 2nd lieut., Wore. MARTIN, BENJAMIN WILLIAM.—B. 1881; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1899; major, 1st Royal Natal Carbineers (now R. of O.); mily. serv., S. MARTIN, FREDERICK JOHN, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.), F.I.C.—B. 1891; nat. sci. tripods pt. 1 and agril. dipl.; war serv., 1914-19; active serv., France, 1915-17; demob., rank of capt. (Ont.), and bencher of the law soc. of Upper Canada, 1908; commr. to enquire into the Insurance situation in Ont. with a view to improvements in the law; pres., Alumni Assoc. of Toronto Univ.; chmn., exec. comte., of the speaker's patriotic league; author of "Master Company Law in Canada"; justice of the sup. ct. of Ontario, Nov., 1915.

MASTERTON-SMITH, PERCIVAL FRANK, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (hons. mod. hist.); B.A., 1921; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; 60th Rifles, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; capt., res. of Offrs., 60th Rifles; adminstr. offr., Nigeria, 25th Jan., 1922.

MATEER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. Foyle Coll. and Taunton Schl.; astt. treas., Uganda, July 1920; senr. astt. treas., 1921; prin. astt. treas., 1933; prin. astt., acct.-gen., 1937; ag. acct.-gen., Feb.-May, 1938.

MATHER, NORMAN FREDERICK HUGH, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; served in Rifle Brig., Dec., 1915-Sept., 1919; astt. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1919; astt. dist. offr., Mar., 1921; regisar., war serv., land grant scheme, S. Feb., 1922; sec. to res., Pahang, Dec., 1923; mag., Seremban, July, 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, Aug., 1926; cls. II, Nov., 1930; astt. advr., Segamat, May, 1928; dist. offr., Larut and regisar., titles, Perak, Mar., 1932; chmn., govt. rice mill in addn., May, 1933; ag. commr., trade and cust., Johore, Sept., 1935; offr. cls. I.B., Sept., 1935.

MATHERS, FREDERICK FRANCIS, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. schls. in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Dalhousie law schl. and Harvard law schl.; called to the bar, 1892; dep. prov. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., N.S., 1902; also regisar. of joint stock companies, 1909; now deputy astt.-gen.

MATHESON, CAPT. C. L. T., M.C.—Commissioned in Royal Engineers, 1909; astt. dir. of surv., G. Coast, 1914; Togoland campaign, 1914; Cameroons, 1914-15; France with Field Coy. R.E., 1916-17; W.O., 1918; dep. dir. topographical surveys, G. Coast, 1920.

MATTHEW, CHARLES.—B. 1903; ed. Downside schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1927; cadet, admstr., Nigeria, 1929; astt. dist. offr., 1932; ag. pol. mag., crown coun., and offr. admstr. on various occasions, 1930-33; mag., Uganda, 1933; ch. mag., Palestine, but did not proceed; crown coun., Uganda, 1936; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1935-Dec., 1936; judic. advr., Buganda, 1938.

MATTHEWS, ARNOLD BURNETT.—B. 1898; ed. Shrewsbury (schl.) and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (cls. schl.); sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; 2nd cls., Pt. I., classical tripus, 1920; M.A.; 2nd cls., anthropological tripus, 1922; astt. dist. offr., N.P. Nigeria, Jan., 1923; astt. sec., N.P. secretariat, Feb., 1925; astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Aug., 1927; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr.-Sept., 1928; astt., C.O., July, 1929; astt. sec., Nigerian secret., Sept., 1929; astt. dist. offr., N. Prov., Aug., 1930; dist. offr., Jan., 1931; sp. duty reorganization pagan admstr., 1933-35; ch. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, July, 1935; ag. col. sec., various periods, 1936-38; gov.'s dep. on several occasions; dist. commr., headqrs., judic. dist., and presidt., rural areas coun., 1939.

MATHIESON, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C.—B. 1868; ed. Harrington Gram. Schl., Prince of Wales Coll., Taught Schl., Desable Gram. Schl., P.E.I., read law with McLean and Macdonald; called to the P.E.I. bar, 1893; K.C., 1905; el. to legislature for King's county, 1900; re-el., 1904, 1908 and 1912; leader of the opposition, 1903; premier and atty.-gen., 2nd Dec., 1911; ch. just., P.E.I., 13th June, 1917.

MATHIESON, WILLIAM ALLAN CUNNINGHAM.—B. 1916; ed. High Schl. of Dundee, Edinburgh Univ. and Cambridge Univ., M.A. (Edin.); 1st cls., hons., classics, 1938; Univ. scholarship, ancient hist., 1938; senr. foundation scholarship (classics), King's Coll., Cambridge, 1938; 1st cls. classical tripus pt. I., 1939; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1939.

MATHISON, JOSEPH GEORGE, B.L. (Glasgow).—B. 1907; astt. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, 1930; ag. munici. and land offr., Apr., 1934; munici. and land offr., Sept., 1934; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, registr. docs., in addn., Apr., 1935; astt. admstr.-gen. and dep. offr., recr., Uganda, 1936; admstr.-gen., Nigeria, 1939.
MATTHEWS, HON. ALBERT, LL.D.—B. 1873; chmn., bd. of govs., McMaster Univ., Toronto; lieut.-gov., Ontario, Nov., 1937.

MATTHEWS, LESLIE GARD.—Ed. Plymouth, Oxford Univ. O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 1916 and 1917, Lanc. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 1917-21, attd. Indian Army and Iraq Levies; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasps); astt. treas., Somaliland, June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; astt. commr., pol., Nigeria, 1926; supt., pol., Aug., 1937.

MAUDE, HENRY EVANS, M.B.E. (1939).—B. 1906; ed. Highgate Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1933; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. commr., 1931; ag. native lands commr., 1931; admstr. offr., 1932; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-June, 1933; nat. lands commr., Gilbert Is., 1935; rep. Gilbert & Ellice Is. at confere. and seminar on educn. in Pacific countries, Honolulu, July-Aug., 1936; astt. dist. offr., Zanzibar, Sept., 1936; admstr. offr., Gilbert & Ellice Is., 1937.

MAULE, JOHN PATRICK.—B. 1906; ed. Berkhamsted schl. and Sidney Sussex coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1932; dipl., agr., 1929; A.I.C.T.A. (Trinidad), 1930; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1930; man., govt. stock farm, Cyprus, 1934; title changed to livestock offr., 1939.

MAVROGORDATO, COL. ARTHUR STEPHEN, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1886; ed. Oakham Schl., Rutland; clk., pol. headqrs., Cyprus, Nov., 1903; inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, May, 1905; in ch., pol. detachment, Kyrenia, 1907-1909; govt., prison and dep. coroner; in command of Lapho dist. several occasions; detailed for sp. duty in Larnaca dist. in connection with Limassol riots, May-June, 1912; astt. commr., pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1913; J.P. and dep. coroner, 1913; ag. commr., pol., also sheriff, prison visiting just., and govt. nominee on city coun. of Freetown, and performing duties of marshal of the Admy. prize ct., on various occasions, 1915-18; ag. harbour. and detaining offr. under mil. authorities, in 1916, 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. dist. commr. in headqrs. dist., S. Leone, Feb.-Mar., 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, Dec., 1922; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Apr., 1924; commdt., ditto, Apr., 1926; rep. Palestine govt. at internat. pol. confce., Antwerp, Sept., 1930; inspr.-gen., constab., Trinidad, July, 1931; mem., leg. coun.; awarded King's Pol. med., 1934; comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1938.
MAXWELL, HON. ALAN VICTOR, K.C.—B. 1887; judge, sup. ct., N.S. Wales, Aug., 1934.

MAXWELL, SIR WM. GEORGE, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1915); Royal Humane Society's med. (1882).—B. 1871; ed. at Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; jun. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Mar., 1891 asst. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1892; regis. of cts., Kinta, Perak, Feb., 1899; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1905; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1906; sol.-gen., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1906; British adviser to govt. of Kedah, July, 1909; ag. sec. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., 1914; ag. sec. to high comsnr., Malay States and Brunei, 1915; ag. British res., Perak, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; ag. sec. to high comsnr., Malay States and Brunei, June, 1917; vice-chmn., food control comtees, S.S. and F.M.S.; vice-chmn., shipping control comtees, S.S.; pres., rubber coman., 1918; pres., Singapore housing coman.; pres., Singapore centenary comte; general adviser to govt., Johore, June, 1918; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1918; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1919; ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ret., 1926; publications, "Perak Land Laws (past and present)," "The Laws of Perak, 1877-1903;" "The Laws of the S. Sttlmts., 1827-1907;" "In Malay Forests."

MAXWELL-ANDERSON, SIR MAXWELL HENDRY—Lt. Bach. (1934), C.B.E. (mill.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.).—Ent. Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; reed. thanks of Admy. for series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; inst., R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; promoted capt., Apl., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, May, 1919; ag. chief just. and col. sec. on several occasions; ch. just. Fiji and judl. comsnr., W. Pacific, 1929; ag. high comsnr., W. Pacific, June to Aug., 1930; ret., 1936.

MAY, HENRY O'HARA, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.)—B. 1878; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Apr., 1907; med. offr., health, Nov., 1911; junr. sanv. offr., Aug., 1916; dep. dir., sanv. serv., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; dep. dir., health serv., Gold Coast, June, 1926.

MAYBIN, SIR JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1889; M.A. (Edin.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1914; attached to the Badulla kach, Jan., 1914; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., July, 1914, and Jan., 1915; addl. dist. judge and pol. mag., Badulla in addn. June, 1915; pol. mag., Panadura, Mar., 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, July, 1919; 2nd finan. ast. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1920; finan. ast., Colombo Municipality, Nov., 1920; addl. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1922; landing survr., Colombo cust., Mar., 1923; 3rd ast. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 2nd ast. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Mar., 1925; ast. regis., co-op. socs., Apr., 1928; dep. regis., co-op. socs., Sept., 1932; ch. sec., Nigeria, 1934; O.A.G., for various periods, 1934-37; gov't. dep. in 1935, 1936 and 1937; gov' and commdr-in-ch., N. Rhodesia, 1938.

MAYLE, NORMAN LESLIE—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1917; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; on mil serv. from Sept., 1917 to Sept., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; ast. prin., 16th Nov., 1928; ag. pvtc. sec. to parl. U.S. of S. for the Cola., 2nd Feb., 1932; pvtc. sec., do., 18th Apr., 1932; prin., 1st Jan., 1936.
MAYNARD, PRROY.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 15th Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.

MEAD, JOHN PHILLIPS, C.B.E. (1939).—B. 1886; dip., forestry (Oxon); ed. Charterhouse Sch., Royal Indian Engring. Coll. (Coopers Hill) and Exeter Coll., Oxford; ast. cons. forests and ag. dep. cons. forests, Aug., 1907-May, 1916; on mil. serv. 1916-19; impr., forests Kordofan prov., Soudan, Aug., 1918; conserv., forests, Sarawak, 1919; temp. forestry advr. to govt., Fiji, Nov., 1926; ast. cons., forests, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; dep. cons., forests, F.M.S., March, 1928; dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1930.

MEADE, CHARLES RICHARD.—Copyist, treasy. and post office dept., Montserrat, Oct., 1907; 5th clk., Apr., 1912; 4th clk., Nov., 1912; govt. offr., Sandy Point, St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1915; clk., treasy., Virgin Is., May, 1917; clk., exec. council; prin., Apr., 1927; prin., treasy., Dominica, Dec., 1928; ag. treas., mem. exec. and leg. couns. and chmn., comsrs. of income tax in 1931 and 1932; ast. treas. and postmr., Montserrat, Dec., 1933.

MEADE, CAPT. JOSEPH MICHAEL, B.A. (T.C.D.), Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns, Dublin).—B. 1839; European mast., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ast. mast., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Feb., 1921; ast. impr., schls., Perak, Jan., 1923, Sept., 1925 and Apr., 1929; impr., schls., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ast. impr., schls., S'pore, Oct., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B), Nov., 1931; ag. prin., Raffles Instn., Oct., 1935; ag. impr., schls., Penang, Dec., 1935; senr. impr., schls., Selangor, Dec., 1937; senr. educn. offr., gde. A, Malaya, 1939.
MEADEN, HENRY, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1882; ast. warden, Tobago, 1919; ast. warden, St. Patrick, 1922; mag. warden, Tobago, 1st Apr., 1926; warden, St. George and prot., immigr., 1937.

MEDWAY, F.W.—Imp. serv. (acctnt.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O.), 1901-2; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1902; clk., treasy., O.F.S., May, 1908; senr. clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Dec., 1916; prin. clk., Apr., 1917; ch. clk., April, 1923; recr., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1933; do., Durban, June, 1938.

MEGARRY, THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Academical Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (1st cls. hons., classics and mental and moral philosophy); cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1922; passed cadet, Dec., 1924; ast. to sec. Chinese affrs., 1925; ast. head, sanv. dept., Mar., 1928; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1928; sec., currrent comte., in adln., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. district, New Territories, Mar., 1931; head, sanv. dept. and pres., sanv. bd., Jan., 1933; dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, Jan., 1934; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Dec., 1936; supt. impts. and expts., Feb., 1937; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., Nov., 1937.
MEGAW, ARTHUR HUMBERT STANLEY, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1910; ed. Campbell coll., Belfast and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Walston stud., 1931; ast. dir., Br. schl. of archaeology, Athens, Oct.

MEIGHSN, JOHN, M.B.E. (1937)—B. 1895; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv.; temporarily assigned to Board of Agric., July, 1914; Home Office, Aug., 1914; on military service, Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1919; Home Office, Mar.-Apr., 1919; assigned to C.O., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 9th Jan., 1923; staff offr., 13th Oct., 1930; senr. staff offr., 14th Mar., 1940.

MEIGHEN, Rt. Hon. ARTHUR, P.C., K.C.—B. 1874; ed. St. Mary's Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1896; Ist cl. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1898; re-el., 1911, 1913 and 1917; defeated, 1921; el. by-el., 1922; re-el., 1925; solr.-gen., 1913; re-el. accl., 1913; p.o. (Can.), 1915; sec. of state and min. of mines (Can.), 1917; later, same year, min. of interior and sup. gen., Int. affs.; prime min. of Canada, 1920; p.c. (Eng.), 1920; prime min., Can., 1926; def., 1927; mem., senate, 1932; min. without portfolio in Bennett cabinet and govt. leader in senate, 1932; leader of oppos., senate, 1935.
MCKIE, DAVID ERIC CAMERON, M.B., Ch.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1902; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh Univ.; M.O., Malayan med. serv., Aug., 1935; prof. of clinical surgery, Jan., 1937.

MELIZAN, LOUIS FELIX.—B. 1890; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad and Maison de Melle-lez-Gand, Belgium; dip. d'Arp., Gand, 1917; cert. in forestry, Imperial Research Inst. and Coll., Dehra-Dun, India, 1923, with medal for surveying; temp. asst. to forest offr., Trinidad, 1915-18; 2nd dep. conserv., forests, 1923-29; asst. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1930.

MELLISH, Hon. HUMPHREY.—B. 1862; ed. Mount Mellick Schl., Prince of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, 1876-79 (holder of Govt. scholarship for County of Queen's P.E.I., 1876-78; medallist, classics and maths., 1879); Dalhousie Univ. (B.A., 1882, LL.B.); matriculant, Univ. of Lond., hons. div., 1883; called to N.S. bar, 1890; K.C., 1904; practised law, 1901-1918; pres., N.S. Barristers' Soc., 1912-13; agt., Canadian min. of just. in N.S., 1903-04; judge, sup. ct., N.S., 1918, also surrogate, and dep. local judge in adm., for N.S. dist., 1920; local judge in adm., 1921.

MELVILLE, EUGENE.—B. 1911; ed. Queen's Park Schl., Glasgow and United Coll., St. Andrews; Harkness res. schol., 1929-33; Ist cls. hons. classica, 1933; 1st cls. hons. econs. and moral philosophy, 1935; asst. inspr., taxes, 1935; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Jan., 1936; sec. to West African cocoa comm., March-Aug., 1938; pte. sec. to parl. under sec. of state for the colla., Sept., 1938; temp. ag. prin., Jan., 1939; ag. prin., Dec., 1939.

MENZIES, Rt. Hon. ROBERT GORDON, P.C. (1937), K.C., LL.M., M.P., M.L.C., Victoria, 1928-32; min. without portfolio, 1928-29; M.L.A. for Nunawading, Victoria, 1932-34; atty.-gen., min. for rlys. and dep. premier; el. M.H.R. of C'wealth of Australia for Kooyong (Vic.), 1934; atty.-gen. and min. for industry, Lyons miny., 1934-March, 1939; mem., ministerial delgn. to England, 1935; visited England on behalf of C'wealth govt., 1936 and 1938; prime min. and treas., from Apr., 1939; min., defence co-ordination, from Nov., 1939.

MERCIECA, Prof. Sir ARTURO, KT. BACH (1926), M.A., LL.D.—B. 1878; matric., Malta Univ., 1894; M.A., 1897; LL.D., 1901; el. for coun. of govt., 1907-10; asst. crown advoc., 1915; prof. of law, 1915; crown advoc., 1919; judge, 1921; ch. just., 1924; ex. in law, 1921.
MERRITT, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; acct., Sydney metrop. meat industry bd., N.S.W., 1916; sec. and treas., 1931; consnr., metrop. meat industry, 1933.

MERRICK, H. F. C.—B. 1889; ed. Battersea Gram. Schl. and London Univ.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1913; ditto, Sierra Leone, 1914; audr., Malta, 1920-22; clk., col. audit dept., 1922; audr., Gibraltar, May, 1927.

MERRICK, JOHN EDWARD SIEGFRIED, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1911; asst. dist. comnr., Uganda Prot., 1912; 3rd asst. sec., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., 1916; senr. asst. sec., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1919; asst. col. sec. (2nd grade), Kenya, 1926; ag. asst. col. sec. (1st grade) in 1925 and 1926; prin. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1928-32; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, 1932; ag. ch. sec. in 1933, 1934 and 1935; ch. sec., Dec., 1935; ag. gov., July-Dec., 1937.

MESSERVY, GEORGE FORSHAW.—B. 1889; ed. Minto House Schl., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; ag. surv'r's asst., lands and mines, Br. Guiana, 1910; 5th cls. offr., do., 1911; 4th cls. offr., 1915; land offr. and dist. commissary, taxation, Demerara River, 1916; ag. ch. clk., lands and mines, 1926; asupt., lands, 1930; ag. sec. and acctnt., do., on various occasions; seconded to col. secret., 1935; sec., coronation comtee., 1936; sec. and acctnt., land and mines dept., 1937.

METCALFE, CHARLES CAMPBELL, M.B.E. (1931), M.C.—B. 1880; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; elk., W.O., 1901; elk., treasy., Nyassaland, 1911; K.A.R., 1914-22; ag. chief transport offr., Nyassaland, Apr., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ch. transport offr., Feb., 1923.

METIVIER, CAPT. HARRY VINCENT MERCER, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; govt. vety. surg. examr. of animals, Trinidad, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., 1937; author "Tropical Dairy Cattle" and "Paralytic Rabies."

METTAM, RICHARD WILLIAM MORISON.—B. 1893; prof. of bacteriology, Royal Vety. Coll., Dublin, Ireland; M.R.C.V.S., 1917; lieut., R.A.V.C., Aug., 1917; served in France and Flanders, 1917-1918; capt., 1918; Rhine Army, 1919; vety. research offr., dept. of agr., Onderstepoort, Pretoria, Jan., 1920; prof. of anatomy and embryology, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1922-26; prof. of anatomy and embryology, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1926-27; ag. asst., ch. vety. research lab., Kabeta, Kenya, 1927-28; vety. pathologist, Nigeria, 1937.

METZGEN, MONRAD SIGRID, M.B.E.(1932).—B. 1893; temp. clk. to keeper of prisons, B. Hond., Mar., 1910; transfd. to treas. dept., Aug., 1910; temp. asst. keeper of King's warehouses, Sept., 1910; 3rd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1911; 2nd grade ditto, Jan., 1918; 2nd lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, June, 1918; internal rev. offr., Mar., 1920; sec. nded to income tax dept., June, 1921; prin. offr., income tax, Aug., 1922; offr. of cust., Nov., 1922; sec. to select comtee. to enquire into cost of living to civ. servants, Mar., 1923; ag. audr. in 1923 and 1924; ag. dist. comnr., Orange Walk, June-July, 1923; ch. income tax collr., Jan., 1924; dist. comnr., 1935; joint compiler, Br. Hond. Handbook.

MEYER, DAVID DE WAAL.—B. 1897; ed. Grey Coll. Schl. and Grey Univ., Bloemfontein, O.F.S.; M.A., 1919; dipl. commnl. economics, Rotterdam, 1922; pvt. sec., to min. of mines and industries, Union of S. Africa, 1924; in ch., external trade divn., dept. of mines and industries, 1926; mem. govt. econ. advay. and tariff bd., 1930; trade commr., Montreal, 1932; accredited rep. of Union in Canada, 1938.

MICHAUD, HON. JOSEPH E., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Quebec Seminary, Laval Univ. (B.A., 1910), St. Dunstan Univ., Charlottetown, P.E.I., Dalhousie Law Sch. (LL.B., 1913); mayor of Edmundston, N.B., 1919, 1932, 1934; el. to legis. of N.B., 1917, re-el. 1920, 1925, 1930; min. without portfolio in Foster govt., 1921, Veniot admnstr., 1923-25; el. to H. of C., 1933; re-el., 1935; min. of fisheries in govt. of Mackenzie King, 1935.

MIDDEBROOK, STANLEY MUSGRAVE.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll.; Camb. (open exhibnr., mod. history); war serv., 1917-19; cadet, S.S. Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. prot., Chinese, Nov., 1924; attd., govt. monop. dept., S'pore, Nov., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang; do., Muar, Apr., 1931; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., F.M.S., July, 1932; sec., uniform licences and fees comte., (road and rail transport, Malaya), Aug., 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933, asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore; asst. dir., educn., Chinese schls., F.M.S., Aug., 1934; prot., Chinese, Johore, Oct., 1936; do., Perak, Feb., 1937; offr., cls. II, Apr., 1938.
MIDDLETON, J. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1929.

MIDDLETON, HON. JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1924). C.M.G. (1916).—B. 1870; ed. Seiberg and Univ. of Edin.; M.A.; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1901; ag. dist. commr., Warri, 1901; asst. sec., 1902; sen. asst. sec., 1904; officiated as sec. to govt. and divisional commr., 1904-5; dist. commr., 2nd grade, 1906; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and asst. prov. commr., 1906-7; sen. asst. col. sec., 1907; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1908; ag. col. sec., 1908-9, 10-11, 12-13; col. sec., 1913; adminstr. govt., 1914, 1916 and 1919; govt., Falkland Is., 1920; govt., Gambia, 1927; govt., Newfoundland, Sept., 1928; ret., 1932.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1815; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.

MIFSUD, HON. SRU UGO P., KT. BACH (1927), LL.D. (1910), B. Litt. (1906), M.L.A.—B. 1889; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; called to bar, 1910; el. to rep. first dist. in Maltese parlmt., Oct., 1921, in June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. of industry and commerce, 28th Oct., 1921; prime min., Aug., 1924; leader of House, 1921-23 and from 1924-27; leader of opposition and head of national party, Aug., 1927 and June 1932; prime min., 1932-1933; el. to rep. first elect. div. in the coun. of govt., July, 1939.

MILES, ARCHIBALD CLARENCE.—B. 1887; ent. Gold Coast serv., Feb., 1909; left, May, 1919; visited W. Indies to study cocoa cultivation, 1914; rejoined Gold Coast serv., as prov. supt. of agr., July, 1921; dep. imp. agril. confere., Oct., 1927; dep. dir. agr., Nov., 1933; del. 3rd W. Africa agr. confere., Nigeria, June, 1938.
MILLAR, CAPT. REGINALD MORRISON, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1888; divnl. inspr., pol., Bahamas, 1928; supt., prison, 1938.
MILLAR, WILLIAM, R.E. (Irel.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; mily. serv., 1916-19 (ment. in desps.), Gold Coast, 1920; irrign. engr., Iraq, 1922; anti-malarial engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1932.

MILLARD, ALFRED SUTTON, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.); D.P.H.—Med. offr., S.
