Sttlmts., May, 1907; ag. sen. med. offr., Singapore, 23rd Jan. to 17th June, 1909; med. offr., grade II, F.M.S., 6th July, 1909; health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; temp. med. offr., Quthing, Basutoland, Sept., 1916; med. offr. of health, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. med. offr., June to Oct., 1921; ag. ch. med. offr., May to Sept., 1927; title altered to asst. dir. of health, May, 1928; ag. dir. health, June to Sept. and in Oct., 1929.

MILLER, ALBERT EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1892; H.M. Forces, Aug., 1914; serv. in Malta, Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915 and France, 1915-19; 2nd lieut., 1917; wounded, Feb., 1918; inspr., live-stock, Gold Coast, May, 1921.

MILLER, CAPT. EDWARD ALBERT.—B. 1893; ed. Trent Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1919; asst. dist. offr., 1920; dist. offr., 1928; adminstr. offr., cls. II, 1936; do., cls. I, 1937.

MILLER, JAMES INNES, M.A. (Edin.), F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Fettes Coll. (schol.) and Edin. Univ.(exhbr.); James Welsh Bursar, 1911; lst cls. hons., class., 1914; 1st cls. hons. hist., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., June, 1915; on serv. Suvla Bay and Mesopotamia, 1915-18; cadet, Malayan civ. serv. (S.S.), Oct., 1919; dep. collr. and regist. of deeds, Penang, Dec., 1919; mag., May, 1920; agt., food contr., Penang, Oct., 1920; secy. to food contr., Singapore, Feb., 1921; attd. col. secy.'s office in addn., June, 1921; passed cadet, May, 1922; mag. and 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 1923; cls. IV, May, 1924; dist. offr., Bukit Merlajam, 1924; sec., rubber restrm., Johore, 1924; recr., Alsagof concession in addn., Dec., 1924; mag. and asst. regir., Johore in addn., 1924; inspr., prisons, Johore, in addn., 1926; 3rd mag., S'pore, Apr., 1929; cls. III, May, 1929; 2nd mag., S'pore, May, 1929; on sp. duty, N.E.I., Oct.-Nov., 1931; mem. and sec., S.S. trade comm., 1933-34; sup't., rubber control, S'pore and Penang and regir., textile quotas S.S. and F.M.S. in addn.; offr., cls. II, May, 1934; dist. offr., May, 1936; offr., cls. IB, Aug., 1937; legal advr., Kelantan, Mar., 1939.
MILLER, JOHN EASTON.—B. 1896; ed. Berkshireand Sheffeld Univ.; asst. commr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1922; dist. commr., 1925.

MILLER, RALPH W. R., B.A. (Camb.), Dip. Agric. Camb.—B. 1892; ed. Darlington and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd lieut., S. Lancashire Regt., 1915; sp. brig., R.E., 1917-18; asst. analyst, Kenya, 1919; chem. offr., Kenya, 1923; ag. dir. chem. research, 1923; sent. agrl. offr., Tanganyika, 1925; dir. agr., Barbados, 1929; chem., advsy.-comte., B.W.I. cent. sugar cane breeding station 1932; advr., Barbados sugar delegates to S. of S., 1935; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1937.

MILLIN, PHILIP.—B. 1888; ed. S. African Coll. Schi., Cape Town and S. African Coll. (now Univ. of Cape Town), B.A., LL.B., Univ. Coll. of Good Hope; admitted, Transvaal bar 1913; K.C., Union, 1927; acted as ag. judl. commr., Basutoland and treas. spece. of Swaziland and the Bechuanaland Prot., Apr.-Sept., 1937; puline judge sup. ct., S. Africa, T.P.D., Sept. 1937; joint author "Mercantile Law of S.A." 9th edn., 1936.

MILLS, ERIC, C.B.E. (1934), O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919, B.A. (Cantab.), F.S.S.—B. 1892; school of Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1910; Cowell schol., 1913-14; 1st cls. maths, tripes, pt. I., 1912; 2nd cls. maths, tripes, pt. II., 1913; 2nd lieut., Bedford Regt., 1914; gas poisoned, 1915; transferred to Egypt, 1916; staff capt., gen. list, 1918; served in occupied enemy territory admin.; ment. in despa., 1918; major, mily. gov., Gaza, 1919; asst. gov., Samaria dist., Palestine civil admin., July, 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt., and ag. prin., Nov., 1921; 1st asst. sec. to Palestine govt., Oct., 1925; asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1926 and Jan., 1933; seconded as supt. census, May, 1931 to Dec., 1932; ag. ch. sec. on many occasions; comsnr., migr. and statistics, 1934.

MILLS, FREDERICK LEIGHTON VICTOR, A.M.I.C.E., M.C.—B. 1893; asst. engrg., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1922; av. senr. exec. engrg., 1929-34; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1934-1936; senr. exec. engrg., 1934; asst. D.P.W., 1936; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1936; D.P.W., Sierra Leone, 1938.

MILLS, HENRY ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. East London Coll.; 4th cls. clk., C.A.'s office, 1907; on mily. serv., 1914-19; 3rd cls. clk., 1913; exec. offr., 1920; seconded for duty in Hong Kong, Aug., 1924; acct., post office, Hong Kong, Oct., 1926; senr. clerical and acctg. staff, 1927; attd. sup. ct. for sp. duty, 1927; acct., treasy., 1939; ch. acct., post office, 1933; asst. P.M.G. and ch. acct., Hong Kong, 1938.

MILLS, JOHN VIVIAN GOTTLIER, B.A. Oxon., (Merton Coll.), Mods. and Lit. Hum., Barnstaple-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919.—B. 1887; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1911; sec. to Chinese Riots Coms., 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; on sp. duty at Chinese emigr. camp, Port Swettenham, Aug. to Sept., 1914; offr., cl. V., Nov., 1914; asst. prott., Chinese, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Feb., 1915; temp. attd. office, comsnr., trade and cust., Aug., 1918; offr., cl. IV., Jan., 1919; agt. to food contrlr., F.M.S., in addn., Jan., 1919; offt. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1923; registr., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1924; asst. offr. assignee, Penning, in addn., Apr. to July and Oct. to Nov., 1925; cls. I.B., Apr., 1925; senr. genl., S.S., Apr., 1928; temp. puisne judge, Sept. to Nov., 1930 and Jan to Feb., 1931; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1932; puisne judge, S.S., Jan., 1933; judge, Johore, Oct., 1934.

MILNE, GEOFFREY, M.Sc. (Leeds), F.I.C.—B. 1898; ed. Hymers Coll., Hull and Leeds Univ.; mily. serv., 1917-19; asst., later lecturer, Leeds Univ., Sept., 1923; soil chemist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1928.

MILNE, JOHN COUTTS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Aberdeen), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.) Cert. L.S.T.M., Cert. of L.S. of Hygiene.—B. 1901; ed. Roberts Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen and Aberdeen Univ.; M.O., Egypt, 1925-27; health offr., Malaya, Mar., 1928; ag. dy. S.M. & H.O., Selangor, Apr., 1937.
MILNE, T. M.—B. 1890; dep. registr. gen. and dep. registr. and dep. marshal, sup. ct. and registr., friendly societies, Trinidad, Sept., 1931; asst. crown solr., 1932.

MILSTEAD, HERBERT ERNEST GEORGE.—B. 1886; temp. asst. acct., P.W.D., Uganda, May-July, 1921; asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1921; visiting just., Nairobi prison, 1922; sec., cent. tender bd., 1923 and 1927; ag. senr. asst. treas., in 1924 and 1927; seconded to secretariat as estab. offr., June, 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. acct., treasy., May to July, 1927; asst. currency offr., July, 1927; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., Nov., 1929; offr. in ch., cent. rev. office, Jan. 1934; dep. comsnr., income tax, June, 1937.

MILSUM, JOHN NOEL, F.L.S.—B. 1890; asst. supt., govt. planttns, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; asst. agrl., Aug., 1920; on sp. duty in Sumatra, May, 1921; ag. agrl., various occasions, 1924-33; deleg. to 4th Pac. So. Cong., Java, May, 1929; on sp. duty in Java & Sumatra, Apr.-May, 1934; senr. asst. agrl., Jan., 1935; senr. agrl. offr., Aug., 1937.

MILTON, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.V.O. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., 1st Jan., 1879; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., Apr., 1883; sec. to the tender bd., 1881; clk. to exec. coun., 1885; ch. clk. and acctnt., col. sec.'s office, 1891; sec. to prime min., Mar., 1894; detached for serv. in Rhodesia as ch. sec. and sec. serv., Oct., 1897; ag. admr. Rhod., July, 1897, to Nov., 1898; apptd. admnstr. Mashonaland and senr. admnstr. S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1898; admnstr., S. Rhodesia, 20th Dec., 1902; resig., 1914.

MINNITT, ROBERT JOHN.—B. 1913; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Sept., 1934; passed cadet, Aug., 1937; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Aug., 1937; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1938; asst. sec., local defence comtee., Aug., 1939.

MINTER, K. S.—B. 1894; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. clk., and assigned to G.P.O., Sept., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to H.M. stationery office, Aug., 1914; to C.O., Oct., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., Apr., 1939.
MISSO, DOUGLAS GREGORY LEDULX, B.A., (Hons.), Lond.—B. 1911; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1935; office avst., 1936; offr. cls. II., Apr., 1937; office asst., Mar., 1938.

MITCHELL, AUBREY REDVERS, B.Sc.(Engng.).—B. 1900; ed. Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal and Witwatersrand Univ., Transvaal; Witwatersrand mines, 1922-30; mines dept., 1930 as asst. inspr.; mines: inspr., do., 1938.
MITCHELL, EDWARD THOMAS.—B. 1900; on mily. serv., May, 1918 to Jan., 1919; temp. clk., W.O., Feb., 1919 to Aug., 1926; apptd. to C.O. as cler. offr. after compet. exam. (Southborough), Aug., 1926.

MITCHELL, HUBERT MANIFOLD.—B. 1895; joined army, 1914; served in France, Belgium, Egypt, Palestine, 1915-20; wounded; ment. in desps., M.C.; demob., 1920; apptd. miny. of lab. (Irish branch), 1920; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Sept., 1921; ag. staff offr., Apr. to Oct., 1924; commr., pol., Sept., 1927.

MITCHELL, HON. SIR JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1917); M.L.A., W. Australia.—B. 1866; entd. ser. of W. Aust. Bank; 16 years man. of Northam branch; entd. parlmt., 1905; hon. min. and mem. of exec. coun., 1906; min. of agric., 1907; min. for lands in addition to agric., 1909, and again in 1916; premier, 1919-24 and 1930-33; lieut.-gov., W.A. since 1933.

MITCHELL, SIR PHILIP EUGEN, K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1933), M.C.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nyassaland, 1912; heut., K.A.R., 1919; capt., 1917; acjt. 1917-18; ment. in despa.; A.D.C. and pvt. sec. to ag. govt. Nyasaland, 1918-19; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; asst. sec. native affrs., 1926; prov. commnr., 1928; sec. for native affrs., 1928; ch. secy., 1934; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Uganda, 1935.

MOIR, JOHN INNES.—B. 1897; ed. Gordon's Coll., and Univ., Aberdeen; M.B., Ch.B., 1921; D.P.H., 1923; surg.-sub. lieut., R.N.V.R., European War; lieut., R.A.M.C., 1921; house surg., Sick Children's Hosp., Aberdeen, 1921; asst. M.O. City Hosp., Aberdeen, 1921-23; M.O. for Falkland Is. Coy., 1923-27; P.M.O., Falkland Is. govt., Feb., 1929; capt., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1929; major, May, 1930; prin. med. offr., B. Hond., 1934.

MOIR, STEPHEN ESDAILE.—Clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Mar., 1904; rev. offr., Nevis, Apr., 1906; clk. to mag., Dist. "C." St. Kitts, 1913; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; on active service, 28th London Regt., 1916-17; cotton impr., St. Kitts, ag. audit clk., May to Sept., 1922; cashier, treasy. dept., St. Kitts, Oct., 1922; ag. treas., Dominica, 1928; warden, Northern dist. and mag., dist. C., 1928.

MOIR, WILLIAM JOHN, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1893; health offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ditto, Kinta, Nov., 1925; ag. sr. health offr., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1928; sr. health offr., Johore, June, 1929; superscale offr., grade A, June, 1935.

MOLLISON, WILLIAM, A.R.I.B.A., M.I.A.—B. 1888; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, N.B.; architect, asst., P.W.D., Transvaal, 1909; served with S.A.I., Egypt and France, 1915-19; A.R.I.B.A., 1927; asst. dist. engr., P.W.D., 1929-33 in various areas; dist. engr., 1933-36; ch. architect, 1936.

MOLLOY, MICHAEL ALOYSIUS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. Dominican Coll. and Christian Bros. Day Sch., Dublin, Castleknock Coll., Dublin, Salesmen Coll., Farnboro' and Royal Coll. Vety. Surgeons, Dublin; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

MONCKTON, HUGH, CLAUD.—B. 1881; ed. Bradfield Coll., Berks; clk., native dept., Fiji, 1901; cadet, 1504; ch. clk., native dept., 1907; stip. mag., 1912; acct. and asst. stip. mag., 1913; dist. commnr., Ra., 1914; do., Nandronga, and Tholo West, nom. M.L.C., 1930; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1935; M.E.C., 1935; oll., M.L.C., 1937; advr. on native affrs., 1938.

MONRO, JOHN KIRKPATRICK, M.A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.Ch. (Cantab), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), M.D., M.Ch. (Cantab).—B. 1903; prof. of surgery, King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore, Mar., 1935.

MONSON, WILLIAM BONNAR LESLIE, B.A.—B. 1912; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (hist. schol., 1930-34); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1932; 1st cls. hist. schls., 1934; B.A., 1934; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin. D.O., Oct., 1935; ag. prin., C.O., 19th June, 1939.

MONTAGNAC, LOUIS IVAN DE.—B. 1880; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; clk. of ct., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, May, 1921; ag. reg. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. registr., sup. ct., Oct., 1924; registr., sup. ct., July, 1926.
MONTAGUE, CYRIL JOHN.—B. 1893; asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1932; ag. prin. asst. audr. on various occasions, 1934-37; ag. audr., Apr.-June, 1937 and Apr.-May, 1939.
MONTANARO GAUCI, ANTHONY J., LL.D.—B. 1895; matric. Malta Univ., 1915; LL.D., 1922; cl. for leg. assem., 1927; dep. speaker, leg. assem., 1927; speaker, do., 1929; judge, 1930.

MONTGOMERY, H. R., C.M.G. (1936)—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., May, 1908; dist. commnr., Apr. 1914; ag. senr. commnr., Kenya, 1926; senr. commnr., 2nd grade, 1929; 1st prov. commnr., 1932; ch. native commnr., 1934.

MOODY, SYDNEY, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1889; ed. King's Sch., Pontefract, Leeds Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (Hist. Schol.), 2nd cls. hist. finals, 1914; pvt., Pub. Schls. Batt., R. Fusiliers, 1914; 2nd lieut., E. York Regt., France, 1915; lieut., King's Liverpool Regt., Egypt, 1916; lieut., Herefordshire Regt., Palestine, 1917-1918; held various appts. in O.E.T.A. (S.) Palestine, Dec., 1918-June, 1920; dist. offr., Palestine, July, 1920; attd. to Middle East Dept., C.O., Feb., 1923 to Feb., 1924; asst. sec., 1924; ag. asst. ch. sec. and ch. sec. on various occasions, 1926-37; asst. ch. sec., 1934; rep. H.M. Govt. to perm. mandates commn., 1932 and 1935; dep. ch. sec., 1938; col. sec., Mauritius, 1939; offr. admin. govt., 29th Jan., 1940.

MOONESINGHE, NALIN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1924; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1924; do., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1925; do., Badulla kach., June, 1925; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1926; disto Puttalam, May, 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Aug., 1929; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1930; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Jaffna, July, 1933; asst. sttlmt. offr., Mar., 1935.

MOOR, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1891; obtained higher standard certif. in Forestry at Imperial Research Inst. and Coll., Dehra-Dun, India, 1914, with medals for forest engineering and sylviculture; probationer, Indian provincial forest service, 1914; passed exams. in Indian land revenue systems, forest law and accounts, and Hindustani by the higher standard, 1915; permanent extra asst. conservator, 1916; transf'd, to Trinidad as deputy conserv., forests, Jan., 1918; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1923; prin. forest offr., Apr., 1926; div. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, 1939.

MOOR, RICHARD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; ed. Ernysted's Schol., Skipton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. V., July, 1924; cls. IV., July, 1928; sec. to res., Pahang, Nov., 1929; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1931 and Apr., 1932; dist. judge, S'pore., Penang and Malacca, Oct., 1932; ag. regizr., sup. ct., Penang and offl. assignee in addn., Nov., 1932; offr., cls. III., July, 1933; ag. legal advr., Johore, Oct., 1935; asst. legal advr., F.M.S., Jan., 1937; crown coun., Penang, Dec., 1938.
MOORE, CHARLES EDWARD, B. Arch. (L'pool), A.R.I.B.A.—B. 1897; ed. Castletown Grammar Schol., Queen's Coll., Oxford, L'pool Univ.; arch., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

MOORE, ERNEST STANLEY.—B. 1890; Imp. post office, 1909; war serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919; asst. survr., posts and tels., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; survr., do., 1920; divnl. survr., do., 1933; contr., posts, 1937.

MOORE, SIR HENRY MONCK-MASON, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), B.A., Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1910; attd., Colombo kach., May, 1911; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawela, Aug., 1912; ditto, Colombo, and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., Jan., 1913; office asst. to prin. collir., customs, and extra land survr., July, 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Feb., 1914; on mily. duty, May, 1916; addtnl. asst. col. sec., July, 1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919; 4th ditto, Jan., 1920; priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., Mar.-Sept., 1920, in addn.; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1921; col. sec., Bermuda, Jan., 1922; prin. asst. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; ag. dep. ch. sec. and ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. ch. sec. to govt., in 1926 and 1927; dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1927; gov. dep., Sept. to Oct., 1927; col. sec., Kenya, 1929; ag. gov. in 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov. and commdr.in-ch., Sierra Leone, 1934; asst. under sec. of State, C.O., 4th Sept., 1937; gov. and commdr.in-ch., Kenya and high commr. for transport, Jan., 1940.

MOORE, WILFRED HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Kalutara kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Kandy kach., July, 1926; office asst., N’Eliya kach., Jan., 1927; office asst., Ratnapura kach., May, 1928; do., Kandy kach., May, 1931; asst. gov. agt., Batticaloa, Dec., 1931; addnl. asst. gov. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1934; asst. contr., estabs., July, 1934; addnl. do., Feb., 1938.

MOORES-WEDDON, W. C.—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich and Trin. Coll., Dublin; cadet, B.N. Borneo, 1905; asst. dist. offr., 1906; offr. in-charge Tenom-Tawau expdn., 1906; dist. offr., 1908; ag. P.M.G. and supt. of telegraphs, 1917; ag. offr.in-charge, West Coast residency, 1917; examr. in Malay, 1917; on special service in Ceylon and F.M.S., 1918; postmr.-gen. and supt. of telegraph, B.N. Borneo, 1918; ag. res. interior, B.N. Borneo, 1920; ag. food contr., 1920; res., Kudat, 1921; sessions, 1922; ag. res., Sandakan and mem., leg. coun., 1922; res., East Coast, 1923.

MOORMAN, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1887; nine yrs. Met. pol., New Scotland Yard; served in Italy, Corfu, and Salonika, 1914-18; supt. pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1919-21; asst. comnr., pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1921-24; asst. dir., C.I.D., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dir., C.I.D., 1925 and 1930; title changed to comnr., pol., C.I.D., June, 1923.

MOREAU, REGINALD ERNEST, C.M.Z.S., M.B.O.U.—B. 1897; 2nd div. clk., W.O., 1914; exec. offr. gr. II, army audit office, H.Q., B.T.E., Cairo, 1920; sec. & librarian, E. African agril. research station, Amami, Tanganyika Territory, 1928, also in ch. ornithological research.
MORETON, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1905; ed. Northwich Grammar Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Feb., 1928.

MORGAN, HYWEL GLYN.—B. 1899; ed. Univ. Coll. (Wales); B.A.; LL.B. (Wales); called to bar, Lincoln’s Inn, Jan., 1924; practised as barrister in London and on S. Wales circuit, 1924-26; lieut., the Welch Regt., Regular Army R. of O.; war serv. with Machine Gun Corps, India; crown coun., Gold Coast, Oct., 1926; asst. atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., Mar., 1932; ag. atty.-gen., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. judge, Oct.-Nov., 1933; atty.-gen., Aug., 1936; ag. judge, high ct., May-Dec., 1937.
MORGAN, MAJOR KENNETH SAYCE.—B. 1895; ed. City of Lond. Sch.; asst. supt., sp. br., Singapore, Feb., 1936.
MORGAN, LEONARD GEOFFREY, B.Sc. (Lond.), M.A. (Lond.), Dip. Ed.—B. 1905; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1926.
MORGAN, MERVYN JAMES EVERSFIELD, M.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1907; Northern circuit, Preston and Lancaster County sessions; res. mag., Kenya, 1936.

MORICE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1891; ed. Ampleforth Coll., York; Br. P.O., Jan., 1912; war serv., 1914-19; lieut., R.E., Sept., 1919; asst. engnr. engr., P. & T., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; teleg. engr., Johore, June, 1921; engr., P. & T., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; do., Penang, Mar., 1932; sr. engr., P. & T., S.S. & F.M.S., Dec., 1932; sr. engr., Southern div., Aug., 1934; do., Selangor and Pahang, May, 1935; asst. engr.-in-chief, Apr., 1939.

MORLEY, ARTHUR HAROLD, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liverpool), D.P.H., F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1900; ed. Leeds Gen. Infirmary; house surg., Leeds Gen. Infirmary, 1926; ditto, Northampton Gen. Infirmary, 1927; med. offr., Somaliland, 1928; do., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.

MORPHY, HENRY, B.A. (1st cls. hons.), B.E., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I.—B.1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin (senr. moderator), gold med. (experim. sci.) etc.; 2nd grade survr., Nigeria, May, 1914; senr. survr., 1921; passed lower Hausa and lower Arabic; asst. survr.-gen. (supermy.) and seconded to Kano native adminstr., 1928.

MORRIS, EDWARD GILBERT, O.B.E. (1919), B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1884; ed. Bedford Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (exhibnr.); asst. dist. comnr., Uganda, 1908; dist. comnr., 1912; lieut., unattached E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., Uganda T.C., 1916; dist. pol. offr., Tanganyika, 1919; resigned, 1920; re-appt., 2nd grade admin. offr., Uganda, 1924; labour comnr., 1924; insp. schls., 1925; ag. dir., educn., 1927; dep. dir., educn., 1928; dir., educn., 1929; dir., educn., Kenya, 1934; do., Nigeria, 1938.

MORRIS, EMANUEL SYDNEY, M.D., Syd., 1926 (M.B. 1911, Ch.M. 1912), D.P.H. Syd. 1920.—B. 1888; dir. gen., pub. health, pres., bd. of health, chmn., nurses’ registrn., bd., commr., venereal diseases act and mem. aborigines prot. bd. since Oct., 1934; mem. B.M.A., mem., bd. of dirs., Prince Henry hosp., Sydney.

MORRIS, HUBERT MEREDYDD, M. Sc. (Agr.), F.E.S.—B. 1896; ed. Manchester Univ.; research fellow and asst. lect., 1917-18; war serv., 1918-19; research under the Royal Soc. Grain Pests (War) Comte., 1919-20; asst. entomologist, Rothamsted experimental station, 1920-27; entomologist, Cyprus, 1927.

MORRIS, JAMES PATRICK ANDREW, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Ignatius Coll., Galway, Blackrock Coll. and Royal Vety. Coll., Dublin; capt., R.A.V.C.; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, Dec., 1920; dep. dir., animal health, Jan., 1930; ag. dir., do., Dec., 1932; dir., do., 1933; dir., vety. services, 1935.

MORRIS, RICHARD PERCY, A.M.I.E.E., M.Inst.R.E.—B. 1896; ed. Thame Gram. Sch., Oxon; Marconi companies, 1914-30; seconded, R.N.R., 1917-19; wireless engnr., N. Rhodesia Govt., 1930-33; do., Hong Kong, 1933-37; senr. wireless engnr., 1938 to date; ch. elec. engnr., P.W.D. in addn. during 1938.

MORRISON, HON. AULAY MACAULAY.—B. 1863; LL.B., Dalhousie Univ.; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, 1888, and B. Columbia, 1890; mem. of H. of C., Canada, 1896-1904; puisne judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1905; ch. just., sup. ct., 1929.

MORRISON, CHARLES RHODWAT, I.S.O. (1925).—B. 1873; ed. Wesleyan (Boys) High Schl., Freetown; 2nd clk., dist. ct., Karene, Sierra Leone, 1897; elk., Karene Exped. Forces, 1898 (W.A. med. and clasp); 1st clk., 1902; postal agt., 1902-05; ch. clk., 1908; finan. asst., 1911; passed higher stand., Mende and Temme and lower stand., Susu, 1912; office asst., 1921; offr. in charge, Sumbuya dist., 1921; dep. ast. mast., circuit ct. on several occasions, 1905-10.
MORRISON, DR. MALCOLM.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Acad., Inverness and Univ. of Edinburgh, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., London, S.T.M.; M.O., Nigeria, 1921; senr. M.O., 1929; asst. dir., med. serv., 1938.

MORRISS, LOUIS ARNOLD GEORGE.—B. 1896; 15th London Regt., P.W.O. Civ. Serv. Rifles, Feb., 1914; pol. probt., F.M.S., July, 1917; ast. dist. supt., pol., Punjab, May, 1918; ag. 2nd ast. commr., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ast. commr., pol., Dec., 1920; reg., criminals, S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1923; dir., crim. intell. br., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; 1st ast. commr., pol., ch. pol. offr., Selangor, May, 1926; adjt., F.M.S. pol., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929 and Nov., 1934; offr. i/c, traffic br., Aug., 1935; sec., transp. bd., Aug., 1937.

MORT, ERIC LAIDLEY, O.B.E. (1935), B.A.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1914; attchd. Nigeria Regt., 1916, and 1917-18; supt., educn., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., N. Provs., 1935; prin., higher Coll., Katena, 1936; prin., Kaduna coll., 1936; ag. ast. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1937; ag. ast. dir., educn., Apr., Sept., 1938; ag. dir., Sept.-Oct., 1938; ag. ast. dir., N. Provs., Jan.-Mar., 1939.

MORTIMER, CHARLES EDWARD, M.R.E.—B. 1886; ed. Hartley Coll., Manchester; clk., land dep., Kenya, 1917; land ast., 1920; land sec., 1928; commr., lands and settlement, Nov., 1938 and commr., local govt., Oct., 1939.

MORTON, JOHN, M.R.C.V.S. (1927).—B. 1901; ed. Kel Schl. and Glasgow Vety. Coll.; Fraser research schol. at Rowett Research Inst., Aberdeen, 1927-28; Scottish bd. of agric. research schol., 1928-29; vety. offr., Falkland Is., Apr., 1929; lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, 1929; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1933.

MORTON, STANLEY PARK.—B. 1894; ed. Allen's Endowed Schol. and Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., 1914-19; U.K., G.P.O., Feb., 1914; ast. divsn. engr., S.S., Mar., 1921; engr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; engr., S'pore, Nov., 1927; div-sn. engr., Aug., 1935.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Perth, W. Australia and Brasenose Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1904; called to bar, Middle Temp., May, 1905; judge's associate and clk. of arraigns, W. Aust., Oct., 1905; mily. serv., Australia, 1915-16; France, 1916-19; Ireland, 1920-22; M.C., 1919; ast. registr., titles, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. registr., titles, in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. solr.-gen. for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1927-32; dep. land offr., Mar., 1926; crown coun., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyassaland Prot., 1933; ag. judge, Nov., 1933 to Dec., 1934; puisne just., Ceylon, 1936.
MOSS, ALBERT JAMES ROBERT, FT. Lieut. R.A.F.O.—B. 1898; supt., aerodrome, Hong Kong, 1930.

MOSTON, HENRY, ERNEST, C.B.E. (1937). M.S.I., A.I.S.E.—B. 1881; ed. Runcorn and Chester Coll., Cheshire; studied indust. law and factory conditions in Gt. Brit.; joined N.Z. dept. of lab., 1909; dep. ch. inspr., factories, 1928; ast. sec., lab., 1936; sec., lab., 1939; reprntd. N.Z. govt. at L.L.O. conf., Geneva, 1938.
MOTHERSILL, LAWRENCE JOHN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schol. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; regular army, The Royal Scots, 1918-28; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; ast. dist. commr., 1930.

MOTTISTONE, 1st Baron (cr. 1933), MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD SEELY, C.P. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (desps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Aberconwy div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life sav., 1891; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comte. to inquire into organisation of crownagnts'office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; jointly sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.

MOUBRAY, GEORGE ALEXANDER DE CHAZAL DE.—B. 1888; ed. Loretto Schol., Manchester Univ. (civil engnr.), Zurich Polytechnikum and Univ. (pure science); cadet, F.M.S., 1912; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1916; do., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; regir., titles and town planning admintr., Selangor, in addn., July, 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1930; offr., cls. I B, dist. offr., L. Perak, Oct., 1933; do., Kinta, Feb., 1934; do., Lower Perak, Nov., 1934; ag. Br. advr., Kelantan, Mar., 1937; dist. offr., Kinta Nov., 1937; ag. resd. council, Penang, Dec., 1937; commr., lands and mines, Johore, July, 1938; advr., land office, Kedah, July, 1939.

MOUNTAIN, HAROLD GRIFFITHS, M. Inst., M.M.—B. 1887; ed. at King's Schol., Chelsea, and Schol. of Mines, Camborne (1st cls. dip.); served R.E., tunnelling coys. and eng. stores dept., 1916-19; inspr., mines, Cyprus, Oct., 1925; ag. water engnr., July-Oct., 1930; inspr., lab. and regir., trade unions, Jan., 1933; sec. for mines, Gold Coast, 1934.

MUCHMORE, ALFRED, A.L.A.A.—B. 1893; Br. postal serv., 1912-1920; Civ. Serv. Rifles and R.A.F., 1914-19; acct., posts and tels., Tanganyika territory, 1920; ast. treas., 1923; senr. ast. treas., 1931; ag. dep. treas., for various periods, 1933-36; finance offr., Aden, 1936.

MUIR, JOHN COCHRAN.—B. 1902; ed. Kilbarchan, Camphill & Allan Glens schls., Glasgow Univ. and West of Scotland Agrl. Coll.; B.Sc., (Agr.) N.D.A., N.D.D., post-grd. course, Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad; ast. supt., agr. Gold Coast, 1925; senr. agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1935; ag. dir., agr., May-Oct., 1937.

MUIRHEAD, FRANCIS GRAHAM, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1911; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen and Univ. Coll., London Univ.; ast. dist. commr., (cadet) Basutoland, 1937; regir., res. commr.'s ct., 1938; ast. dist. commr., 1939.
MULCAHY, WILFRED, M.A., Dip. Ed. (Dunelm)—B. 1903; ed. Hatfield Coll., Durham Univ.; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, Nov., 1925; ast. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Oct., 1930.
MULLHALL, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1899; on mily. serv., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Kalutara kach., Jan., 1929; ditto, Puttalam kach., Nov., 1922; extra office

MULLER, JAN FREDERIK.—B. 1891; 3rd grade clk., dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1909; 2nd grade clk., 1919; 1st grade clk., 1921; senr. clk., 1926; prin. clk., dept. of mines, 1928; 2nd grade ch. clk., do., 1932; 1st grade ch. clk., do., 1934; under sec., do., 1934.

MULLER, WALTER ANGUS.—B. 1898; ed. University Coll. Schol. and R. Coll. of Sci.; on mily. serv., 1915-19; ass't. sup't. Ceylon pol., 1920; supt., pol., 1927; commr., pol. and commt., local forces, Trinidad and Tobago, 1938.
MULLIGAN, EDWARD JOSEPH, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Summerhill Coll., Sligo and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; mily. serv., 1915-19; vety. offr., Kenya, 1921; senr. do., 1932; dep. dir. (field services), 1937.

MULLINS, CHARLES, M.I.Min.E., M.I. (S.A.) E.E.—B. and ed., Rotherham, England; ass't. inspr., machy., Union Govt., 1913, and was successively ch. inspr., machy. and dir., govt. mech. lab., Johannesburg; mem., elec. control bd.

MULOCK, Rt. Hon. Sir WILLIAM, P.C. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1902), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated at Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; sat in parlmt. 1882 to 1905, representing N. York; postm. gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; ch. just., exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1905; ch. just., Ontario, 1923; ret., 1936.
MUNRO, THOMAS AMBROSE VESPER.—B. 1883; barbmr., Bahamas, 1st Sept., 1913; ag. marshal, admv. side, sup. ct., 1919.
MURDOCH, JAMES WILSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen).—B. 1900; 2nd asst. med. supt., ment. hosp., T. Rambutan, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; med. supt., do., July, 1931.

MURISON, Sir JAMES WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1919), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately and at Trin. Hall, Camb.; senr. class. scholr., 1891; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. triplos, 1894; 3rd cls. law triplos, Part II, 1895; Latham English prize, 1895; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; de-villed and practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; registr., F.A.P.O., Oct., 1902; town mag., Mombasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd asst. judge, H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. asst. judge, Mar., 1905; ch. judge, Zanzibar, 26th Sept., 1914; mem., of ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.: judge, sup. ct. of H.H. Sultan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govts. as to H.H. mainland properties, 1911; reed. thanks of S. of S. for conversion of applied Indian Acts into Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge prize ct. Zanzibar from 1914; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; examr. in Swahili to the Zanzibar Govt.; joint edr., Zanzibar Law Repts., 1868-1918; attv.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1919; ag. col. sec., June, 1920 to May, 1921; ch. just., S. Sttlmts., July, 1925; ret., 1933; commr. to Palestine to enquire into Jewish-Arab riots, 1934; edited the Revised Laws of the S. Sttlmts., 1926; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.

MURPHY, JOHN HOWARD BLACKWOOD.—B. 1895; Schl. of Physic, Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1913; R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; India Office, Apr., 1919; A.D.C. Jubaland, Dec., 1919; dist. commnr., Gosha, 1920; ditto, Choreh, 1922; ditto, Kitui, Ukambani, 1923; resigned, private affairs, 1926; re-apptd., commr., Somaliland, 1927; admin. offr., Kenya, 1930.

MURPHY, JOHN PELLY.—B. 1909; ed. Mount St. Mary's Coll., Chesterfield, B.A., LL.B., (T.C.D.); solr., high ct. of just., Ireland, 1932; jurr. legal offr., Hong Kong, Apr., 1936; ag. dep. regtr., sup. ct., Apr., 1936; ag. asst. land offr., Dec., 1936; ag. dep. regtr., sup. ct., Feb., 1937.
MURPHY, JOSEPH.—B. 1892; ed. Stokane Schol., Sligo; R.I.C., 1913-22; Jamaica constab., Apr., 1922; sub-inspr., Apr., 1926; 3rd cls. inspr., Apr., 1931; King's pol. med., 1926.
MURPHY, PHILIP PAINE, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.T.M. & H. (London).—B. 1910; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal City of Dublin Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, 1935; do., Aden, 1939.

MURPHY, WILLIAM LINDSAY.—B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin; B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd., Kandy kach., Dec., 1910; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1911; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt., Prov. of Uva, May, 1913; asst. land settm. offr., July, 1913; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, Apr., 1915; addnl. dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Sept., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1923; temp. employed, C.O., 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1927; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., May, 1928; compt'r., lab., Sept., 1931; chmn., munic. coun. and mayor, Colombo, Mar., 1932; munic. commr., Colombo, May, 1937, offr. cls. I. gd. I., Nov., 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., Mar., 1938; ag. ch. sec., Aug., 1938.

MURRAY, ALEXANDER ROSS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1923; asst. engr., hydrta., P.W.D. Kinta, Oct., 1928; asst. drnge. and irrign. engr., Jan., 1932; drnge. and irrign. engr., Selangor, Dec., 1932; sr. drnge. and irrign. engr., Malay States, Nov., 1935.

MURRAY, ARTHUR EVELYN FRANCIS, B.A. (Oxon), Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. dis. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admvte. serv., 1928; sec. to commr. of inquiry into disturbances in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1930; staff grade, 1935.
MURRAY, HON. SIR GEORGE JOHN ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1917), B.A., LL.M.—B. 1863; judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1912; chief just., Jan., 1916 and lieut.-gov., Apr., 1916.

MURRAY, JOHN MURRAY.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., LL.B. (Cape of Good Hope), B.A. (Oxon.); advoc., sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1913; K.C., 1932; judge sup. ct. (Transvaal Prov. div.), 1937.

MURRAY, MENZIES.—B. 1890; ed. Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (hons. Stellenbosch), 1909; B.A., LL.B. (T.C.D.), 1914; LL.B. (S.A.), 1920; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.W. Fus., M.C.; advoc., 1921; ch. clk., patent office, Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1930; registr., patents and registr., companies, June 1932.
MURRAY, ROBERT EVERITT KING.—B. 1902; clk., grade II., Basutoland, 1924; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1930; asst. dist. commr., 1937.

MURRELL, ERNEST BARTRAM, M.A., M.B., B.Ch.(Cantab.) D.C.O.G.—B. 1898; anesthetist, S.S., Aug., 1930; ag. prof. of midwifery, Coll. Med., S'pore, Feb., 1934; med. offr. i/o offce., S'pore, in addn., Dec., 1935; med. offr., Batu Gajali, Dec., 1938.

MURTLAND, MAJOR CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1884; ed. Harrogate, York and Dublin; sub.-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, 1908; dist.-inspr., 1912; ag. county inspr., 1910-1911, 1914-1916; capt., B. Guiana militia, 1915; county inspr., 1920; detective inspr., 1927; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., various periods, 1928-33; dep. commr., 1938.

MYCOCK, CHARLES.—B. 1890; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1915; asst. cable censor, 1914-16; on mily. serv., 1917-19; headm. Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1919; do., Yaumati schl., 1928-30 and 1932-36; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1930; headmast., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1937; sen., mast., educn. dept., 1937; J.P., 1938; cable censor, Sept., 1939.

MYERS, Rt. Hon. Sir MICHAEL P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1937), K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1922), LL.B. (1897).—B. 1873; ed. Thorndou Schl., Wellington Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); in legal practice many years; ch. just., N.Z., 1929.

MYLIUS, EDWARD NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Bromsgrove Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mily. serv., 1917-18; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; dist. offr., 1929; admstve. offr., cls. II., 1937; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1938-39; sec., E. Provs., 1939.

NAIRAC, Sir GEORGE EDOUARD, KT. BACH., (1938), K.C.—B. 1876; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, 1898; ag. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc-gen., 1903-06; mem. for Port Louis in coun. of govt., 1911-25; nominated mem., 1926; K.C., 1926; procureur and advoc-gen., July, 1927; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt.; ag. col. sec., July, 1928 to Mar., 1929; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1929 to Oct., 1930; ch. judge, 1935; edr., Mauritius Law Repts. since 1906 and of the Law Repts. Digest, 1902-25.

NAISH, RICHARD BRYANT, B.A. (Oxon.), M.R. San. I.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Galle kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Galle in addn., Apr., 1915; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1918; asst. contr. of estate supplies, May, 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Feb., 1920; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, Sept., 1920; asst. finan. asst. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Mar., 1923; ditto, Karunegala, Sept., 1925; asst. contr., rev., Mar., 1928; sec., univ. comsn. in addn., July, 1928; admin. pol. mag., Colombo, Sept., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Oct., 1931; attd., atty.-gen.'s dept. for sp. duty, May, 1934; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Feb., 1937; offr., cls. I., Jan., 1938; ag. govt. agt., N.P., June, 1938.
NAPIER, HON. JOHN MILLIS, LL.B.—B. 1882; ed. City of London Schl. and Adelaide Univ.; admitted a practitioner of sup. ct., 1903; K.C., 1929; judge sup. ct. of S. Australia since Feb., 1924.
NAPIER, SIR WALTER JOHN, KT. BACH. (1909), D.C.L. (Oxon.).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1907-1909; mem., W. African lands comte., 1912.

NASH, HERBERT FREDERICK WILLIAM.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 26th Nov., 1917; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to C.O., 29th Dec., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

NASH, HON. WALTER.—B. 1882; ed. St. John's Schl.; mem., N.Z. H. H. of R. since 1929; min. of finance, min. of cust., min. of stamp duties, 1935, also min. of marketing, 1936, and min. of social security, 1939; mem. national indus. confce., 1927; has represented N.Z. at various confces. overseas, including 2nd internat. lab. confce., Geneva, 1920, confces. of inste. of Pacific relations, 1927 and 1933, confce. of Br. commonwealth relations, 1933, and Imp. Confce., 1937; conducted trade mission overseas, 1936-7; visited London on spec. mission, 1939.

NASON, WILFRID PAUL.—B. 1894; ed. Christ's Hosp.; served with forces, France and Russia, 1915-19; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory; do., Nigeria, 1928; audr., Leeward Is., 1932; audr., Trinidad, 1925; do., Zanzibar, 1938.

NATTRASS, ROLAND MARSHALL, B.Sc. (Agr.), Lond., Ph.D., Lond., D.I.C., Dip. Agr., Wye.—B.1895; ed. Nottingham High Schl.; asst. to cotton specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; advisory mycologist, Western Prov.; Bristol Univ., Long Ashton Research Statn., 1924; senr. mycologist, mlny. of agr., Egypt, 1928; mycologist, (now plant pathologist), Cyprus, 1931; ag. entomologist, June-Oct., 1937; snr. plant pathologist, Kenya, 1938.

NATTRISS, ERNEST ALFRED, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. C.A. shipping agents, 1898; apptd. to C.A. office, 1912; dep. head, shipping dept., 1922; apptd. shipping offr. to the "Discovery Committee," 1927; head, shipping dept., 1933; do., gen. stores dept., 1935.

NAVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTTU SAMUEL, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1894; demstr., govt. tech. schls., Ceylon, July, 1917; cadet, Dec., 1921; attd., Matale kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1922; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1924; ditto, Chilaw, Feb., 1926; asst. comsr., excise, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1930; asst. atttn. offr., July, 1931; sec. to min. of lab., industry and commerce, Oct., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Aug., 1933; asst. land comsr., Dec., 1933; asst. settltn. offr., June, 1934; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1938.
NEALE, CHARLES NORMAN.—Commr. for road transp. and railways, N.S.W., Aug., 1937.
NEAME, ARTHUR ROBERT LANGLEY.—B.1892; B.S.A. pol., 1912-19; asst. supt., Tanganyika Territory pol., 1920; supt., 1928; ag. dep. comsr., 1933-34.

NEAVE, JOHN RICHARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; attd., res. office, Malacca, Nov., 1919; asst. advr., Kota Tinggi, Feb., 1926; res., Labuan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1930; 2nd ast. sec. (A), S.S., Dec., 1932; offr. cls. II., Jan., 1935; dist. offr., Klang, Sept., 1936; offr. cls. IB, 1938.

NEFF, ERROLL AUBREY, C.M.G. (1939), M.D., L.C.P. & S. (Alberta), D.P.H.—B. 1887; ed. High Schl., Ingersoll, Ont., Toronto Univ. and Western Univ.; on war serv. with rank of major, 1914-19; dist. med. offr., Fiji, and med. supt., Cent. Leper Hosp., Makogai, Fiji, 1921; two Rockefeller foundation fellowships, 1926 and 1931; rep. S. Pacific cols. at Internat. Leprosy Conf., Manila, 1931; senr. med. offr., (now dir., med. services), Cyprus, Nov., 1934.
NEILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920.

NEIL, WILLIAM HARDLEY EDWIN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; ed. Cathedral Schl., Winchester, Taunton's Southampton and Hartley Univ. Coll., Extn., Southampton; war ser., Sept., 1914-Sept., 1919; survey probmr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ast. supt., trig. surveys, Dec., 1923; ditto, topo. br., Dec., 1925; ditto, rev. surveys, Pahang, Jan., 1931; ast. supr., surveys, N. Kedah, Aug., 1932; senr. ast. supt., surveys, Malaya, 1935; ch. survr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1938.

NEILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. schol., 1911; 1st sen. mod. hist. and pol. sci., 1912; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; cadet, Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., Oct., 1915; dist. comnrs., 1916; regtr., sup. et., and offi. recr., 1922; ag. atty-gen. and mem. exec. and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1923; ag. prin. ast. coll. sec. and agt.-gen., imigr., Jan., 1924 and Sept., 1924; mem., bd. examrs. Fijian language and ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; seconded sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. mem., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug.-Sept., 1924; comnrs. of assize, circuit ct., June, 1925; ct. of review, income tax, Nov., 1925; Br. agt. and cons.-gen., Tonga, Jan., 1927; is also a dep. comnrs. for W. Pacific and a judl. comnrs. for matrimonial causes; seconded for sp. duty, Ocean Is., Dec., 1930 to Mar., 1931; special duty in Pitcairn Island, May and June, 1937; admstr., Dominica, 1937.

NEILSON, HARRY ROSS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Med. Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., Uganda, 1912; temp. capt., Uganda med. staff, 1914; health offr., 1921; senr. do., 1924; ag. D.D.S.S. for various periods, 1926-32.

NEILSON, MAJOR JOHN BARRICK, M.C. and 2 Bars, M.A. (Oxon.), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1896; ast. inspr., schl., Perak, July, 1921; ast. mast. govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1921; sr. ast. mast., Raffles Inst., Apr., 1926; inspr., schl., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1928; do., Pahang, Dec., 1930 and Jan., 1933; senr. educn. offr., B. Malaya, Nov., 1936; prin., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Jan., 1938.
NEISH, DONALD GORDON.—B. 1905; ed. Bedford Schl.; 2nd lt., Jamaica local forces, 1928; it., 1930; machine gun offr., 1931-33 sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1933; 3rd cls. inspr., 1936.
NESBITT, ROBERT HENRY, J.P., F.C.I.S. (Eng.)—B. 1883; ed. London, Eng.; Australian trade commr. to N. Z., 1934-37; chmn., milk bd., of N.S.W., 1937.

NESER, FRANCOIS, B.A.—B. 1895; 3rd grade clk., Lands, Pretoria, 1914; 2nd grade clk., 1916; last grade clk., 1919; senr. clk., 1924; prin. clk., 1931; pub. serv. inspr., grade II, 1934; under sec. for lands, 1937.

NESHAM, MAJOR EDWARD WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Torquay High Schl.; articled pupil, Dominion observatory, Ottawa, Canada, 1909-13; Canadian govt. dept. of the Int. Internat. Bdy. survey br. of Dominion observatory, 1909-14; geodetic engr., 1919; R.E. 1915-20, Gallipoli, France and Germany; Croix de Guerre (French); thrice ment. in desps.; survr., survey dept., Gold Coast, 1924-36; dep. commr. lands and survr.-gen., Nigeria, Aug., 1936; ag. commr. do., 1937-38; dep. commr. of land and dir. of surveys, Apr., 1939.

NETTELTON, GERALD ENBAGHT, O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1894; clk., res. commr.'s office, 1914; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot., pol., 1917; sp. J.P., 1917; ag. ast. res. mag., 1920; passed 1st gr. Secwana exam., 1920; passed civ. serv. lowerlaw exam., 1922; ag. res. mag., 1923; res. mag. and hon. inar., Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1924; dist. commr., 1937; 1st., ast. sec., Oct., 1937.

NEVIN, HENRY MILLER, M.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. of Dub.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Lpool), D.P.H. (Univ. of Dub.).—B. 1896; ed. Rathmines Coll., Dublin and Dublin Univ.; Great War, 2nd lt., Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers; M. O. Malaya, Aug., 1927; pathologist II, I.M.R., Aug., 1929; title changed to pathologist, Aug., 1937; do., Penang, Oct., 1937; ag. do., S.S., March, 1939.

NEWBOLD, CHARLES DEMOREK, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1909; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1931; ed. Lodge Schl., Barbados and Keble Coll., Oxford; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, Trinidad, Jan., 1936; relieving mag., 1937.

NEWBOLT, CAPT. A. F.—B. 1893; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; B.A.; on active serv., Oxfordshire Light Infy., Aug., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Flanders, wounded, Apr., 1916; capt., 1915; adjt., 1918-19; ast. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Lord Milner, May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, June, 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

NEWBOULT, ALEXANDER THEODORE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Kingswood and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ast. Perak secret., Dec., 1920; offr., cls V, Aug., 1923; dist. offr., Jejebu, Nov., 1925; offr., cls IV, Aug., 1927; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Aug., 1932; ag. comnrs., lands and mines, Trengganu, Oct., 1933; Br. advr., Trengganu in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1936; offr. cls. II, Aug., 1937; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S. Nov., 1937; ag. undersec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1939.
NEWELL, HUGH HAMILTON, C.B.E. (1936).—B. 1878; mem. main roads bd., N.S.W., Mar., 1925; pres., Jan., 1932; commr., main roads, Dec., 1932.
NEWHOOK, PAUL STUART ALLEN.—B. 1900; ed. Prehendal Schl., Chichester and privately; S. Western rly., 1916-26; ast. loco. supt., K.U.R. and H., 1926; dist. loco. supt., 1930.

NEWMAN, WILLIAM AUGUSTIN, M.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. Sydney High Schl.; ent. New South Wales pub. serv., 1891; served with Australian forces, S. African war (Queen's med., 5 clasps); on staff of first prime min. of C'wealth of Aust., Jan., 1901; senr. clk., dept. external affrs., 1902; ch. clk., C'wealth electoral admin., 1903; C'wealth electoral offr. for Victoria, 1906; ch. clk., home affrs., 1909; offl. sec. to gov. gen., C. of A., and sec. to fedrl. exec. coun., 1913; sec., royal commn. on navy and defence adminstr., 1916; ch. clk., Australian defence dept., 1918; admstr., Island of Nauru, 1927.
NEWSAM, NEVILLE.—B. 1898; ed. Harrison, Coll., Barbados; 2nd. astt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1922; 1st. astt. analyst, 1933; ag. govt. analyst, 1936.

NEWTON, SIR FRANCIS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1892), C.V.O. (1911).—B. 1857; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1880; M.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Hampshire regt., and extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, gov. Cape, 1881; priv. sec. to Sir Thomas Scanlen, prime minister of Cape, 1883; and to Sir H. Robinson, 1884-86; sec. to Mauritius roy. comm., 1886; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., Br. Bechuanaland, 1888; col. sec. and rec-gen., 1889; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., 1892; res. commr., Bechuanaland Protectorate, Dec., 1895-7; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1898; ditto, Barbados, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903; ret., 1919; col. sec., 1st S. Rhodesian ministry, 1st Oct., 1923; high commr. for S. Rhodesia in London, 1930.

NEWTON ISAAC, F.R.C.S., (Edin.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1897; clin. astt., Charing Cross hosp., 1924; 2nd health offr. at port, Hong Kong, 1925; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hosp., 1926; med. offr. in ch. Kowloon hosp., 1927; M.O.-in-ch., govt. civ. hosp., 1930; in ch., Univ. surg. clinic in addn., 1932; med. offr. in ch., Victoria hosp., 1932; med. offr. in ch., govt. civ. mental and Tuen Yip hosp., 1933; do., Univ. surgical clinic, Mar., 1936; M.O.-in-ch., Queen Mary Hosp., May, 1937.

NGATA, HON. SIR APIRANA TURUPA, KT. BACH. (1927), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Waiomatatini Native Schl., Te Aute Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; mem., house of reps., N.Z., since 1905; mem., exec. coun., repg. Maori race, 1909-12; min., native affrs., min., Cook Isl., and mem., exec. coun. representing Maori race, 1928-34.
NICHOLAS, HON. HAROLD SPRENT.—B. 1877; M.L.C., N.S. Wales, 1932; judge, sup. ct., Feb., 1935; ch. judge in equity, 1939.

NICHOLLS, HON. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1868; LL.B., Tasmania; called to the bar, 1892; atty.-gen. and min. of educn., Tasmania, 1903-4; puisne judge, Tasmania, Jan., 1909; chief justice, 1914; also liout.-gov.; resgd., 1937.

NICHOLS, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1882; astt. engnr., Lagos rly., Feb., 1907; astt. engnr., Gold Coast rlys. (open lines), May, 1911; offr. in charge, Togoland Mily. rlys., 1916-17; ch. engnr., G. Coast rlys., Jan., 1920.

NICHOLSON, WILLIAM EWART, R.A.—B. 1890; ed., Leeds Grammar Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jun. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; instr., arts and crafts, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1933; N. Provs., 1934; prin., Higher Coll., Katsina, 1934; dir., educn., Sierra Leone, 1935.
NICOL, ANDREW, M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; exec. engnr., port works office, 1928.

NICOLE, J. - War serv. 1914-19; ag. sub. inspr., pol., Br. Guiana, 1921; sub inspr., 1922; dist. inspr., 1926; county inspr., 1932; adjt., pol., 1925-28 and 1930; ag. staff offr., local forces in 1928, 1929 and 1934; ag. dist. commr. W., Demerara and chmn., canals, polder authority, July to December, 1934.

NICOLL, MAJOR FREDERICK ALAN BENSON, O.B.E., R. of O. (1921).—B. 1883; ed. St. Edward's Schl., Oxford; Imp. Yeo. S. African War, 1900-02; Natal mtd. pol., 1903-09; Natal native rebellion, 1906; dist. supt., pol., Uganda, 1910; R. Sussex Regt., 1914-20 (France, Belgium and Palestine, army of occupation, Germany, and plebiscite troops, Danzig); ment. in desp.; O.B.E. (mil. div.); Uganda pol., 1924; dep. commr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. commr., pol. and prisons, Apr.-Nov., 1930 and Jan.-Oct., 1933; commr., pol., Oct., 1933; King's pol. med., 1934; serv. brother of Ven. Order of St. John of Jerusalem, 1939.

NICOLL, JOHN FARNES.—B. 1899; ed. Carlisle Grammar Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; war serv., France, 1918; cadet, Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., Dec., 1920; astt. dist. offr., Dec., 1922; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925; astt. dist. offr., 1928; seconded, secr., 1930-31, 1933-34, 1935 and 1936; ag. astt. ch. sec. and clk. of couns., 1935; seconded for duty as astt. prin., C.O., 29th Apr., 1937; seconded as ag. dep. col. sec., Trinidad, Oct., 1937; govt. dep., Apr. May, 1938; ag. col. sec., Feb.-June, 1938; dep. col. sec., Sept., 1938; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1938-May, 1939.

NIGHTINGALE, PERCY HERBERT, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Monkton Combe and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji, 1930; clk. to couns., 1932; prvte. sec. to O.A.G., 1933; dist. commr., 1933; re-designated admstr. offr., gd. II, 1938; astt. sec., secr., 1936-39.

NIHILL, JOHN HARRY BARCLAY, K.C., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Pelsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripods, pts. I and II; pres., Union Soc'y., 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. Edge, junr., Lord of Treas., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1921; ag. astt. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. astt. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high commr., Iraq, 1927-31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad in addn., 1928 and 1931; mem., Br. deleg. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; legal advr., Embassy, Baghdad, 1932-33; solr.-gen., Uganda, 1934; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1934; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1934 to Feb., 1935; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1936; puisne just., Ceylon, Nov., 1938.
NISBET, HENRY ALEXANDER, M.C.—R. 1895; mil. serv., France and Rhineland, 1916-20; lab. offr., Kenya, 1928-39; ag. prin. lab. offr., Sept., 1936 to May, 1937; lab. sec., Sierra Leone, 1939.

NISBET, JOHN GRAY.—B. 1895; war serv., R.E.s., 1914-19; astt. engnr., Uganda rly., 1919; resigned, 1920; astt. engnr., K.U.R. & H., 1924; sr. astt. engnr., 1925; dist. engnr., 1928; sr. dist. engnr., 1936.

NIVEN, CECIL REX, M.C., M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; ed. Balliol Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1917-19; admstr. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; ag. senr. astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1936; ag. prin. astt. sec., do., on various occasions, 1935, 1936, and 1939; ag. res., Jan.-July, 1938; offr., cls. II, July, 1939.

NIXON, R., M.B., Ch.B., L.T.M. (Lpool), D.P.H. (Lpool).—Temp. lieut., capt. and major, R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganika Territory, Oct., 1921; senr. sanitation offr., 1928; asst.-dir., med. services, 1936.
NOAD, FREDERICK MITCHELL.—B. 1895; ed. pvtte. (Ascham Coll.) Berkhamsted and St. Lawrence; W.A.F.F., 1915-20; admstv offr., cls. III, Nigeria, 1930; do., cls. II, 1936; do., cls. I, 1937.

NOAD, JOHN ELLIOTT MONCHIEF, O.B.E.—Ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth and Training Cruiser, H.M.S. "Cornwall"; midshipman, R.N., Aug., 1910; H.M.S. "Prince of Wales," Flagship, Atlantic Fleet; resgd., 1911; Edinburgh Univ., 1911-1914; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1914-1918; interned in Holland from fall of Antwerp to armistice; while in Holland passed for A.M.I.C.E., Feb., 1917; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Mar., 1920; asst. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rly., Apr., 1924; senr. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1930; exec. engrnr., 1934.

NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity; Jan., 1911; conf. elk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff elk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comttee., 1st Jan., 1918; sec., "Discovery" comttee., 3rd Mar., 1937.

NOBBS, CAPT. HOWARD.—B. 1891; ed. University Coll., and Inst. of Educn., Lond.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1913 (hons. in chemy.); M.Sc. (Lond.), 1919; A.I.C. (1918); F.I.C. (1923); 2nd lieut., Northamptonshire Regt., Apr., 1914; lieut., 1915, capt., 1916; war serv. in France, 1st battn., Northamptonshire Regt., 1914-15 and with R.E., 1916-18; chemy. mast., R. Coll., Mauritius, 1926; prin., Queen's Coll. Br. Guiana, 1931; ag. dir., educ., Jan.-May, 1938; mem., educ. comttee., advy. comttee. to students proceeding to U.K., 1939; press censor, 1939.
NOBLE, G. S. P., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), Certif. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distinc.).—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganika Territory, 1925.

NOBLE, CAPT. JAMES MORTON, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B., 1890; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1919; do., Kedah, Jan., 1920-Feb., 1924; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1928; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1929-31 and 1934; senr. exec. engrnr., July, 1935; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, Jan., 1938.

NOBLE, PETER, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Elgin and Aberdeen, Scotland; ast. civ. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1906; ag. dist. engrnr. in 1907 and 1909; dist. engrnr., Demerara, B. Guiana, Aug., 1910; ag. in addition as chief draughtsman, 1911, and as ast. col. civ. engrnr. in 1912; col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Aug., 1912; nom. mem., Roseau Town Board, offl. mem. legis. coun. and road board; on war service as mem. commrs. of valuation (min. of munitions); reed. thanks of sec. of state for work in connection with roads of Dominica, 1916; dist. engrnr., N. dist., Palestine, Aug., 1922.

NOOTIT, P. GORDON.—B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasp); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expdn., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; couns. 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Terr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916 served on Cayo expdn., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.

NORMAN, CHARLES BATHURST.—B. 1904; ed. Harrow Schi. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. asst. sec., 1936; dist. comsrr. and chmn., munic. coun., Mombasa, 1936; seconded, Zanzibar, Aug., 1936 to Jan., 1937; Palestine, Jan., 1938.

NORMAN, ROSS JUNNER, M.C., D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., M.I.M.M.—Ed. Melbourne Univ. and R. Coll. of Sci.; war serv., France and Italy, 1914-18; mem., coun. of inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, London, asst. geologist, and geologist Gold Coast geol. survey, 1920; dir., Sierra Leone geol. survey and ch. inspr., mines, 1927; dir., Gold Coast geol. survey, Oct., 1930.
NORRIS, J. A., C.M.G. (1936)—B. 1872; ed. Ballygun and Williamstown, Ireland; acct. to treas., Victoria, Mar., 1914; audr.-gen., Mar., 1919.

NORRIS, JOHN WILSON A.C.G.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Lond. Univ. Imp. Coll. Sc. and Technology; active service B.E.F. (France) 1914-19, major R.E., mentioned in despatches; joined C.A. as engr. inspr., 1919; asst. engr., 1920; visited Gold Coast 1924; services loaned to consult. engr. reporting on Takoradi Harbour; seconded for duty as ast. engr. Gold Coast harbours staff, Takoradi 1924; rejoined C.A. 1926; dep. to ch. insp. engr., 1929.

NORRIS, VICTOR HUGH, L.M.S.—B. 1893; ed. Raffles Inst. & Coll. of Medicine, S'pore; ast. surgn., S'pore, Sept., 1915; ag. med. offr., various occasions, 1917-24; ast. med. offr., Sept., 1925; dep. med. offr., Oct., 1926; med. offr., S.S., Dec., 1930; do., P. Wellesley, June, 1933.

NORTH, ROLAND ARTHUR CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. Blundells and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. with Samoa govt., 1921; studied Japanese in Japan, 1922; 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924 and 1930; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; extra ast. col. sec., Feb., 1931; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., coun., June, 1931 and Jan., 1934; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., coun., in 1933, 1934 and 1935; 1st cls. cadet offr., Apr., 1935; ag. col. sec., Mar., Nov., 1936; O.A.G., Aug.-Sept., 1936; sec., Chinese affrs., Oct., 1936; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Oct., 1937 and in Feb., 1938 and Aug., 1939.

NORTHCOTE, SIR GEOFFREY ALEXANDER STAFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931).—Ed. at Blundells Schi. (Newte and Huish exhibts.) and Balliol Coll., Oxford M.A. (hons.), ast. collr., E.Africa Prot., May, 1904 dist.comsrr., 1909; ast. col. sec., 1921; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1930; gov., and commdr-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1934; do., Hong Kong, 1937.

NORTHCOTE, R. C.—Ed. Blundells and S. African Schi. of Mines and Technology, Johannesburg; Rhodesian Adminstr., B.S.A. pol., 1914-16; Rhodesian Native Regt., 1917-19; German E. Africa campaign (ment. in desp., mily. med.); ast. polit. offr., Tanganika Territory, Feb., 1921; dist. offr., Feb., 1932.

NORTHCROFT, HON. ERIMA HARVEY, D.S.O. (1919), V.D.—B. 1884; ed. Hokitika dist. high schl., Well Coll., and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; joined N.Z. pub. trust offr. as cadet for short period; admitted to bar, 1907; served overseas, 1916-19 with N.Z.F.A., with rank of lt.-col.; dep. judge-advo-gen., 1927-33; judge-advo-gen. with rank of col., 1933-35; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1935.

NORTHCRFOT, LESLIE ARTHUR, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to secretariat, Jan., 1924; ditto, Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; ditto N’Eliya kach., July, 1925; office ast., Jaffna kach., May, 1926; temp. attd., C.O. Sept., 1928 to Mar., 1929; addnl. ast., col. treas., Apr., 1929; ag. ast., gov’t. agt., Matara, Oct., 1929; ast. sttlmt. offr., Nov., 1929; ast. ch. sec., Apr., 1935; adv. offr., Gold Coast, Feb., 1939.

NORTHHEY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—B., 1868; served with Hazara expdn., 1891; Miranzai expdn., 1891; Isazai expdn., 1892; in S.
