Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1929; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V., Apr., 1923; asst. dist. offr., T. Mahin, Feb., 1925; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot., lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932; offr., cls. II., Apr., 1937; on temp. trans. to Mauritius as prot., immigrts., poor law comsrr., and dir. of lab., Mar., 1938.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schl. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd ols., col.-sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; asst. sec., local defence com- ttee, 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. comsrr., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Conf., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S. W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; asst. col. sec., elk. to exec. coun., combined ct. and ct. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1931; ag. gov. in 1932, 1933, 1935 and 1937; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1938.

OWEN, WILFRID HERBERT, B.Sc.Tech., A.R.I.B.A., Chart. Archi., A.M.T.P.I., R.I.B.A. Dip., Town Planning.—B. 1896; ed. St. John's, Cheetham, Manchester, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Manchester Univ.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.
OWEN, HON. WILLIAM FRANCIS LANGER, K.C.,—B. 1890; judge, sup. ct., N. S. Wales, Oct., 1937.
PADDON, CYRIL EVANS, A.C.A.—B. 1899; war serv., 1918-19; asst. audr., S.S., May, 1929; ag. sr. asst. audr., S.S., Oct. to Dec., 1930; asst. audr., Penang, Nov., 1931; do., S.pore, Oct., 1933.

PAGE, RT. HON. SIR EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1938), M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Development League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fedl. parl., 1930 to Sept., 1939; min. for commerce and dep. leader, Lyons min., 1934; also min. for health from Nov., 1937; min. for commerce, Nov., 1938 to Apr., 1939; visited England, 1936, 1938; prime min. and min., commerce, 7th-26th Apr., 1939.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk. mgte. off., July, 1902; asst. mgte., Mar., 1913; agte., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, May, 1928; mag., Pietersburg, Sept., 1928; ditto, Durban, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. mast., St. George's Schl., Windsor Castle, 1913; asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 5th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1926; res. mag., 1929; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1937.

PALLISTER, RICHARD ALAN, M.D., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1903; ed. Rutherford Coll., Univ. of Durham Coll. of Med.; M.O., Malaya, Aug., 1927; asst. prof. of med., Coll. of Med., S'pore, Sept., 1935; ag. assoc., do., June, 1937; ag. prof. of med., July, 1937.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 5th Mar., 1920; second asst. acctnt., 5th Nov., 1928.

PALMER, SIR HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1910; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; comsrr., native rev., N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res., Bornu Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educn. proposals, 1918; ag. lient.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; lient.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1925; gov., Gambia, 1930; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933; ret. 1939.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOHN CLIFFORD, F.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxon.; survr., Can. Pac. Rly., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., 1913; adjt., R.E., ord. surv. office, Southampton, 1918; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1920; ex. offr., land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land offr. and survr., Gambia, 1934.
PALTRIDGE, FREDERICK, A.R.C.S.I., B.Sc. (Eng.) London U., Hon. B.Sc., N.U.I.—Asst. tel. engnr., P. & T., Gold Coast, June, 1928; tel. engr., July, 1932; senr. do., July, 1937.

PANES, JOHN NILSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dean Close Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (M.A. hons.); mast., King Edward VI's Sch., Norwich, 1914-22; asst. mast., King's Coll., Lagos, Nigeria, 1922; supt., educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. inspr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1931; ch. inspr., educn., 1936; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1937.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence comte., 1898; priv. sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims of applicants for the fenian raid volunteer bounty, 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd.; ment. in disp. for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-1918.
PAPAFIO, Mercy Kwale Quartey.—Ed. Accra Grammar Schl. and in England; 6th grade teacher, 1911; 5th grade do., 1912; 2nd cls. do., 1919; 1st cls. do., 1920; headmistress, 1921.

PARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Pub. Schl., Waimate High Schl. and privately; served with N.Z. forces in S. Africa; N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900-18; agr. dent, 1919; inspr., pub. serv. comsrr.'s office, 1920; sec., marine, 1922; asst. pub. serv. comsrr., 1923; asst. sec., treas., 1925; sec., 1930, also finan adv., 1931; joint managing dir., N.Z. mortgage corp., 1935; joint managing dir., N.Z. state advances corp., 1936.

PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, O.B.E. (1933), M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Schl.; European War, 1914-17; lieut. 6th (Serv.) Batt., Border Regt., M.C., desps.; capt., Somaliland Camel Corps, 1917-20; operns. against Mullah, desps.; dist. police offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist., commr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARK, JAMES MILLER.—B. 1883; ed. Glenavy State Schl., Waimate High Schl.; joined N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1902; apptd. section clk., 1919; assessor, 1924; ch. assessor, 1928; dep. comsrr., 1932; comsrr., 1937.

PARKE, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; R.I.C., July, 1908; May, 1913; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. asst. comsrr., pol., Nov., 1923; asst. comsrr., detect. br., Perak, Nov., 1924; dir., crim. intell. br., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1927; do., F.M.S., June, 1932; dep. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1932; dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., Oct., 1934; ag. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1938.

PARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Coman., 7th Batt., Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream Guards, 1911; on war ser., Uganda and India; 1913-18; selected by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Infv., Aden, 1919-20; cadet., Zanzibar pol., 1923-25; asst. finan. advr. and comit., pol., govt. of Bahrain, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr. to govt.); 1st grade inspr., Mauritius pol., 1933.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmtr., Bethesda, 1901; schlmr., govt. bovs' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, PELHAM.—B. 1888; Mediterranean and Egyptian Expedy. Forces, 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Salonika, Palestine and Egypt); 2nd lieut. (Gen. List), 1917; lieut., 1918; miny. of P.W., Egypt (Sudan irrign. serv.), 1921-22; asst. accntnt., P.W.D., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; ag. accntnt., June to Sept., 1930; seconded to treasy., Nov. to Dec., 1930; asst. accntnt., 1st gr., Jan., 1932; ch. accntnt., Nov., 1934; ag. regisr., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., June-Oct., 1936; ag. dep. treas., various periods 1936-39.

PARKES, EDWARD, M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1906; clk. of exec. coun., 1925; sec. to premier, 1925; offl. sec., Tasmanian agt.-gen.'s office, London, 1927-28; under sec. and ch. electoral offr., 1930; state organizer, visit of H.R.H. the Duke of Gloucester, 1934; mem., salmon and freshwater fisheries commn., 1925-35.

PARKHILL, SIR ROBERT ARCHDALE, K.C.M.G. (1936).—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1927; min. for home affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932; P.M.G., 1932-34; min. for defence, 1934; ret. from cabinet, 1937.

PARKINSON, SIR ARTHUR CHARLES COSMO, K.C.B. (1938), K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class. demy, Malvern, 1903-7; 1st cls. mods., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. clk., accntt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comtee. of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comtee. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1925; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929; asst. under-se. of state, 15th Aug., 1931; perm. under sec. of state for the colo., 2nd July, 1937; perm. under-se. of state for dominion affre., 1st Feb., 1940.

PARMELEE, JAMES GRAHAMS, O.B.E.—B. 1875; inspr., iron and steel bounties, Sydney, Nova Scotia; trade and commerce, Canada, 1922; clk., dept. T. and C., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); head clk., Apr., 1919; ch. clk., Jan., 1921; asst. dep. min. T. and C., Apr., 1923; on sp. duty to U.K., 1928; dep. min., trade and commerce, Nov., 1931.

PARR, HON. SIR CHRISTOPHER JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-26; min. of educn., 1920-26; min. of health, 1920-23; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1925-26; high commr. for N.Z. in England, 1926-29 and 1934-36; M.L.C. and leader of leg. coun., 1931-33.
PARRY, REGINALD FRANCIS SCARLETT.—Ed., St. John's Schl., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jur. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Prov., 1934.
PARRY, HON. WILLIAM EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. pub. schls., N.S.W.; mem., N.Z. royal comms. on mines, 1911; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of internal affrs., 1935.

PARSLEY, CLIFFORD HUGH.—B. 1893; apprentice P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1910; 5th cls. offr., lands and mines, 1915; served with H.M. forces, 1915-19 (Egypt and Italy); govt. survr., 1919; ret. 1932; asst. engr., P.W.D., 1939.
PARSONS, HON. SIR HERBERT ANGAS, KT BACH. (1936). LL.B.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARSONS, THOMAS REGINALD.—B. 1898; entd. savings bank dept., G.P.O., June, 1914; apprd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1916; served in R.N., Apr., 1917-Apr., 1919; trans. to C.O. as cler.-offr., Sept., 1922; higher grade cler.-offr., Jan., 1938.

PARTRIDGE, GEORGE JAMES, O.B.E. (1937), B.A. (1915), M.A. (1921). Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhib. (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripods, part I., 1913, B.A. (War), 1915; temp. comsnr., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; asst. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admstr., German E. Africa, Dec., 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; asst. sec., Oct., 1919; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr. asst. sec., Aug., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; asst. ch. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-34; 1st asst. ch. sec., 1934; ag. prov. comsnr., 1937; ag. admistr. sec. Jan.-Apr., 1938; prov. comsnr., 1938.
PASCHALIS, NEOPTOLEMOS, O.B.E. (1937), K.C. (1931).—B. 1880; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1902; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods, 1927-38.

PASKIN, JESS J. B.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed., King Edwards Schl., Stourbridge and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripods, pt. I., 1913; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. clk., miny. of transport, Jan., 1920; asst. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 4th Sept., 1929; pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols., 24th Apr., 1939; ag. asst. sec., 3rd Nov., 1939.

PASSFIELD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1929) RT. HON. SIDNEY JAMES WEBB, P.C., 1924, LL.B. (Lond.), Hon. Doc., Polit. Economy (Munich), etc.—B. 1859; ed. London and abroad; ent. W.O. by open compet., 1878; survr. of taxes, 1879; C.O., cls. I by open compet., 1881; resigned, 1891; sometime hon. lect. on polit. economy at City of London Coll. and Working Men's Coll. and subsequently hon. prof., public adminstr., London School of Economics, 1912-27; M.P. (Seaham Divn.), 1922-29; pres., Bd. of Trade, Jan.-Nov., 1924; sec. of state for dom. affrs. and the cols., June, 1929; has served on numerous royal comms. and deptl. comtes.; author of many works on politics and economics.

PASSINGHAM, FRANK SELWOOD, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hon. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914; supt. of printing, Jamaica, Mar., 1924; supt., govt. printing office, Cyprus, Oct., 1930.

PATENAUME, JOSEPH OSCAR, I.S.O.—Ed. Longueuil Coll., Quebec; clk., Dom. govt. stationery office, 1889; asst. supt., Dom. govt. stationery, Ottawa, 1889; supt., stationery, 1911; contr., purchasing dept., pub. printing and stationery, 1920; King's printer of Canada, 1933.

PATERNERSON, HENRY SIBBIAL.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol.) and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; seconded for war serv., Mar., 1915; active serv. in France and England, 1915-19; ment. in desp.; demob. with rank of major, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1919; seconded as assist. engr., Trengganu, July, 1920; supernumy offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; asst. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; offr. cls. IB, Sept., 1934; P.M.C., Penang, Nov., 1935; comsnr., lands and mines, Johore, July, 1937; ag. Br. advr., Kedah, July, 1938.

PATERSOHN, RALPH STANLEY WATSON, M.C.—B. 1894; ed. Perth Acad. and Tech. Coll., Dundee; val. dept., inland rev., 1913; R.E., 1915; county survr., Zetland, 1919; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; tech. secy., P.W.D., 1932.

PATTERSON, JOHN ROBERT, C.M.G.—B.1892; B.A. (Cantab.); asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. senr. sec., Bornu Prov., Sept., 1925; admstve. offr., cls. I., Aug., 1930; prin. asst. sec., Oct., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1932; sec., N. Provs., 1935; staff grade, 1936; ag. ch. comsnr., N. Provs., Sept., 1938-Feb., 1939.
PATTON, JOHN, M.B., Ch.B., B.A.O. (Belf.), D.P.H.—B. 1896; temp. med. offr., Basutoland, 1936; med. offr., 1937.

PAUL, ALEXANDER JOHN.—Silver medallist, City and Guilds; serv. London Telephone Service, posts and tels., Ceylon, Nigeria and Gold Coast; Ceylon engrnr., 1914; tel. engr., Gold Coast, 1933; senr. engrnr., 1937; ag. ch. engrnr., 1939; ag. engrnr.-in-ch., 1939.

PAUL, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1890; ed. Taonui pub. schl., Palmerston North High Schl.; N.Z. state advances office, 1907; pub. trust office, 1919; acctnt., printing and stationery dept., 1927; govt. printer, 1937.
PAUL, GEORGE GRAHAM, LL.B. (Edin.), M.A. (St. Andrews).—B. 1887; advoc., Scottish bar, 1910; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1933; ch. just., Sierra Leone, 1940.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rly., New Zealand, with city engrnt., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrnr. and engrnr.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrnr.-survr., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrnr., Fiji, 1912; res. engrnr., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAULUSZ, RUTERT DIRK PHILIPPUS, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1938.
PAUW, JACOBUS CORNELIUS, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A. Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., New York, U.S.A.; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1934; sec., Transvaal, educn. dept., 1937.
PAVLIDES, STELIOS.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1916; crown coun., Cyprus Dec., 1927; ag. solr.-gen., for various periods, 1928-38.
PAWAN, JAMES LENOX, M.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., Mar., 1916; bacteriologist and pathological regsr., Nov., 1923.

PAYNE, RALPH LAVINGTON.—B. 1886; ed. privately; col. sec's. office, Grenada, 1903; supervsr., cust., Nigeria, 1911; admstve. offr. (N.P. secretariat), 1915; on active serv., 1917-19; resumed duty, 1919; ag. prin. asst. sec. Nigerian secretariat, Apr., 1930 to Aug., 1931; dep. res., July, 1934; admstve. offr., cls. I, 1935.

PEAKE, HAROLD GORDON, B.Sc. (hons.), Lond., A.C.G.I., M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Eltham Coll. and Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London; admty. engrnr. staff, Rosyth dockyard, 1912; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; mily. wks. services, India, 1919; engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1924; senr. exec. engrnr., 1923; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1933.

PEARCE, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE FOSTER, K.C.V.O. (1927), P.C. (1921).—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlmt.; sec. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comtees; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at, Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence Sept., 1914; rep. Australia at the Washington Disarmament Confce., 1921; min. for home and territories in Bruce ministry, 1923; vice-pres., exec. coun., June, 1926; min. of defence and govt. leader in senate (Lyons' ministry), 1932; resigned min. of defence and apptd. min. for external affrs. and min. in ch., territories, 1934; defeated at g.e., 1937; ret. from cabinet, Nov., 1937.

PEARCE, HENRY JOSEPH, M.C., M.Inst. Struct.E., L.R.I.B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Castle House Schl., Weymouth and Weymouth Tech. and engrg. Schl.; asst. engrnr., Hong Kong, 1919; dep. head, bldg. ord. office, 1925; exec. engrnr., bldgs. ord. office, 1930; J.P., 1937; asst. D.P.W., 1939.

PEARCE, PHILIP GEORGE.—B. 1888; ed. Mt. Albert Schl. and Coronandel dist. High Schl., N.Z.; joined N.Z. valuation dept., 1904; justice dept., 1919; lands and survey dept., 1921; mines dept., 1921; inspr., pub. serv. comsnr.'s office, 1926; under-secr., native dept. and native trustee, 1933-35; comsnr., stamp duties, and sec., lands and deeds, 1935.

PEARSON, COLIN BATEMAN, M.A. Cantab.—B. 1889; capt., R.A.R. of O., Durham L.I.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; European mag., Tonga, Sept., 1928; ag. land ct. judge, 1930; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Oct., 1932; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, May to Sept., 1934; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1935-37; title changed to dist. mag., 1935; asst. judge, prot. cts., Nigeria, 1938.

PEARSON, JAMES RAK, C.I.E.—B. 1871; schol., St. Paul's Schl. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ent. I. C.S., 1892; held various Indian appts., 1892-1926; ret., I. C. S., Aug., 1926; sec. for Indian affrs., Fiji, 1927.

PECK, EDWARD FINCH, M.R.C.V.S., B.V.Sc. (Tor.), D.T.V.M.—B. 1901; ed. Sedbergh, Univs. Toronto, Liverpool and Edinburgh; farming, Canada, 1920; inspr., health of animals br., Canada, 1929; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1931; vety. and agr. offr., Somaliland, 1935; ch. do., 1938.
PECK, MAJOR GEORGE EDGAR, M.C., R.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1875; ed. City of London Engrng. Coll.; engrnr. and mgr., water works dept., Barbados, 1923; dir. P.W., 1934.

PEDLER, FREDERICK JOHNSON, M.A.—B. 1908; ed. Watford Grammar Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; major schlr., 1927-30; Goldsmith's exhibir., 1929; cls. I. divn. 1, hist. tripos, pts. I. and II; B.A., 1930; Schuldham plate prizewinner; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; seconded to prov. admstr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934 to Jan., 1936; pvt. sec. to perm. U.S. of S. for the cols., 11th Jan., 1936; ag. prin., 31st May, 1937; seconded as sec. to Lord Privy Seal, 1937-38.

PRIDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.), Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1912; (Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy, to study Hokkien, Sep., 1913; Chinese Prot., S'pore, Mar., 1916; treasry. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, S'pore, in addition, Mar. to June, 1918; pol. mag., S'pore, Aug., 1919; supermny. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; asst. to atty.-gen., S'pore, Dec., 1920; ag. dep. treas., S'pore, Jan., 1921; offr. astt. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, June, 1921; pol. mag., Penang, Oct., 1921; mem., rent assessment bd., Penang, and munic. comsnr., George Town, Nov., 1921 and Oct., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1924; prot. Chinese, Penang, Dec., 1924; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; dep. pres., munic. comsna., George Town, Penang, (cls. IB.) in addtn., Oct., 1926; regir., sup. ct., in addtn., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, June, 1929; do., S'pore, Sept., 1929; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Dec., 1930; ag. solr. gen., S.S., 1931-34; dist. judge, S'pore, 1933; offr., cls. I.B., Dec., 1933; ag. atty.-gen., S.S.and mem., exec.and leg.couns.; solr.-gen., S.S., Oct., 1934; ag. judge, F.M.S., Dec., 1935; puisne judge, S.S., 1936.
PEDRAZA, R.—Astt. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEL, Sir WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), B.A., 1896, M.A., 1931—B. 1875; ed. Queens' Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripos, 1896; cadet, S. Stltltns., Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ditto, Tampin, F.M.S. June, 1908; seconded as pres., mun. comsnr., Penang, Jan., 1911; offr., Class III, but remained seconded, Jan., 1912; ag. res. councillor, Penang, in addtn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. comsnr., Singapore, July, 1918; offr., Class Ib., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; joint passage contrlr., Malaya, in addtn., Feb., 1919; seconded as food contrlr., Malaya and chmn., shipping ctee., May, 1919; chmn., European unemployment comtee.in addtn., Feb., 1921; contr., lab., F.M.S. and S. Stltltns., Feb., 1922; Br. adviser, Kedah, Oct., 1922; res. coun. Penang, July, 1925; ch. sec., F.M.S., June, 1926; offr. administering govt., S.S. and high comsnr., Malay States, May to June, 1927; on sp. duty, London, Mar., 1928; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Hong Kong, Mar., 1930; ret., 1935; Kt. of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; hon. fellow, Queens Coll., Camb., 1932.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT, O.B.E. (1888)—B. 1882; clk., audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasry. dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and asst. examg. offr., customs, 1914; comprir. of cust., Apr., 1921; ag. recvr.gen., treas. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1925-28; ag. postmr., Sept., 1928; has held numerous other ag. appts.; postmr., Sept., 1928.

PEGG, HUGH HANDLEY.—B. 1889; ed. Ironville Sch., Derbyshire and pvtly.; asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engnr., Jan., 1921; mem., Kowloon communication comte., 1924; dep. head, rds. office, Jan., 1925; exec. engnr., Dec., 1932; A.D.P.W., June, 1939.
PELLISSIER, S. H., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; vice prin. Boshof, 1914; prin. Ficksburg, 1917; dir. educn., O.F.S., Dec., 1926.
PELLEY, RUSSELL STEELE.—B. 1910; ed. Mercer's Sch. and Edin. and Oxford Unis.; B.Sc. (Forestry Edin.), 1931; asst. conservator (forests), Br. Hond., Oct., 1932; do., Sierra Leone, 1937.
PEMBLETON, EDGAR STANLEY, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1911; res., 1928; senr. res., Aug., 1934; sec., N. provs., Oct., 1936-May, 1937; ag. ch. consnr., do., Mar.-Apr., 1937.
PENDER, CAPTAIN JOHN CHARLES FORBES, M.C.—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1914-18; ment in desps.; admstve. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; cls. II, July, 1939.

PENDLEBURY, CAPT. HENRY MAURICE, F.R.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1893; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and R. Coll. of Sc., London; 2nd lieut., Kings Shropshire Lt. Infnty., Sept., 1914; lieut., Feb., 1915; capt., July, 1915; demob., Sept., 1919; asst. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; curator, Selangor museum, Jan., 1932; hon. dy. game warden, Selangor, in addn., Sept., 1934; dir. museums, F.M.S., 1937.

PENGILLEY, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp. (exhbr.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); lieut., 23rd Batt., Royal Fusiliers, 1917-19; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Feb., 1921; temp. attd. for duty in C.O as asst. priv., Nov., 1925-May, 1926; asst. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and dep. regisr. titles, Selangor, June, 1926; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1928; dist. offr., P. Puteh, Kelantan, Aug., 1928; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1929; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1933; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Sept., 1935; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1938.

PENNEFATHER, CHARLES EDWARD DE FONBLANQUE.—B. 1885; apptd., Fiji, 1910; dist. commr., 1914; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1918; ag. registr. gen. on various occasions; seconded as agt. and consul, Tonga, 1921-22; re-designated admstve. offr., gde. II, 1938; ag. advsr., native affrs., and M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1938-39.

PENNEFATHER-EVANS, JOHN PENNEFATHER.—B. 1894; pol. probr., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; A.C.P., Jan., 1918; adjt., fell. hqrs., June, 1921; ag. comdt. pol. depot, Mar., 1924; offr., supt. pol. cir., Taiping, Apr., 1926; ag. consnr., pol. Kodah, Mar., 1931; supt., pol. S.S., Sept., 1932; comdt., pol. depot, S'pore, Dec., 1934; dir., sp. br., Aug., 1935; ag. ch. pol. offr., S'pore, in addn., June, 1938.
PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

PENTZ, HERBERT FREDERICK, M.C., M.M.—B. 1886; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1903; clk. Cape adminstr., 1910; ch. clk., ditto, 1928; pub. serv. insnr., 1930; served in S. African infv. brig. in Great War (overseas); under sec. for pub. wks. Aug., 1931; prov. sec. Transvaal, Apr., 1934.

PEPYS, WALTER EVELYN, C.M.G. (1838).—B. 1885; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1908; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V, Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; do., Pasir Puteh, June, 1915 and Jan., 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1918; offr., class III., Nov., 1920; fed. sect. for sp. duty, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. I.B., comnr., lands and mines, Johore, Jan., 1928; offr., cls. I.A, under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., Mar., 1932, and comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S. in addn., Apr., 1932; comnr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1932; mem., S.S. trade comnr., in addn., Feb., 1933; gen. advr., Johore, 1935.

PERCIVAL, DAVID ATHELSTANEK, B.A. (Oxon.), F.R.S.S.—B. 1906; schol. of Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; trop. African serv. course, Cambridge, 1929-30; admstve. offr., Nigeria, 1930; asst. comnr., Cyprus, Apr., 1939; asst. sec., Oct., 1939.

PERCY, MAJOR GEORGE REGINALD, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., Chtd. Civ. Engnr., Chtd. Survr.—B. 1890; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., May, 1919; exec. engnr., Feb., 1928; sr. exec. engnr., wks. and bldgs., June, 1932; exec. engnr., Malacca, Jan., 1934; muncl. engnr. in addn., Feb., 1934; senr. exec. engnr., Jan., 1935; ag. munic. sec., Malacca, in addn., Dec., 1935; ag. Settlement engnr., S'pore, Jan., 1938; state engnr., grade II, Jan., 1939.

PERCY, H. VANE (formerly Baumgartner).—B. 1885; ed., Bedford Schl.; elk. land settlt. dept., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; elk. and typist audit dept., Dec., 1902; elk., audit dept., July, 1904; amry. of acct., audit dept., Jan., 1908; jnr. asst. treas., G. Coast, Jan., 1914; asst. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. asst. treas., Nov., 1919; ag. dep. treas. on several occasions, 1921-28; dep. treas., 1929; ag. treas., for various periods, 1930-37; acctt. gen., July, 1937.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; pol. prob., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; passed in Chinese, 1915; attd. to Punjab pol., 1915-16; passed in Urdu, 1916; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army, res., 1916-19; J.P., 1920; asst. pol., 1920; asst. census offr., 1921; dir., crim. intell., 1930; divnl. supt., pol., 1930; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Apr., 1935; dep. consnr., 1938.

PERERA, BENJAMIN FRANKLIN, B.A.(Lond.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Colombo kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., Apr., 1926; office asst., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1928; office asst., Kandy kach., Nov., 1928; admnl. pol. mag., Columbo, Aug., 1930; admnl. asst. govt. agr., Matara, Aug., 1930; admnl. asst. govt. agr., Galle, in addn., Mar., 1934; sec., min. for home affrs., Dec., 1935.

PERERA, CONRAD BONIFACE PETER, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Mar., 1927; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1927; office asst., Puttalam kach., Apr., 1928; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1930; admnl. asst. govt. agr., Colombo, June, 1933; admnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1933; asst. sttlmt. offr., May, 1934.
PERERA, LESLIE SIMON BERNARD, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1937; office asst., Feb., 1937; asst. govt. agrt., Kurunegala, June, 1938.

PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN, LL.B. (Lond.).—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; attd. Kurunegala kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Avisawella, July, 1921; asst. consnr. of stamps, July, 1923;
PERSSE, Capt. Edmund Maturin, M.C. (1918), F.R.G.S., R. of O., W. Yorks. Regt.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Mod. Schl.; astt. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Oct., 1919; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1925.
PETERS, Arthur Henry.—B. 1893; ed. St. Peter's Coll.; entd. survey dept., South Australia, 1910; ch. clk., 1923; supt., lands, 1931; ag. dir., lands, 1939.
PETERS, Bernard R., M.B.E., A.M.L.C.E.—Ast. engr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engr., Tanganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 1927; D.P.W., Nyasaland, Aug., 1928.

PETRIDES, Sir Philip Bertie, Kt. Bach. (1936)—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brussels; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, Jan., 1916; ex-officio min., exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., July, 1916 to Mar., 1917, May, 1918 to Oct., 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. emplo., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, June, 1920; admstl. govt., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1924; ag. judge, May to Dec., 1925; puisne judge, Nigeria, Sept., 1926; ch. just., Mauritius, May, 1930; do. Gold Coast, Dec., 1935.
PETTMAN, P. A. H.—B. 1896; acot., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1925; astt. treas., Uganda, 1935; astt. acctnt.-gen., 1937; ag. senr. acctnt., Feb.-Sept., 1938.

PETTY, Charles Roy.—B. 1895; war serv. with 2/5th Batt., Hampshire Regt. (T.), 1914-19; computer, Gold Coast, June, 1920; record keeper, Apr., 1921; record keeper and cartographer, Apr., 1924; survr., Sept., 1925; ag. astt. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1935 to 1937; ag. dep. do., July to Dec., 1938; ag. dep. dir., surveys, Jan. to May, 1939; ag. dir., surveys, May to Oct., 1939.

PHANG, Joseph—B. 1892; lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, ag. govt. survr., 1918; gort. survr., 1923; land offr., Essequibo river and dep. navig. offr., 1929; sub-warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dist., 1933; 1st grade survr., 1938.

PHAR, Harold William, M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Chord Schl.; war serv., 1914-19; prob. inspr., pol., Singapore, Dec., 1919; det. inspr., pol., Aug., 1921; astt. supvr., cust., F.M.S. and ag. supvr., ext. duties, Singapore, Aug., 1923; supr., cust., prev. br., Selangor, Nov., 1923; do. N. Sembilan, Apr., 1924; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; head, prev. ser., Singapore, Feb., 1932; J.P., Singapore, Mar., 1932; dep. supt., rubber control, S'pore, June, 1934; astt. supt., petroleum rev., July, 1934; dep. comsnr., cust. and excise, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1935; ag. senr. dep. comsnt., F.M.S. Dec., 1936; dep. comptlr., cust., F.M.S., Jan., 1939.
PHILIPS, C. H., L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.)—Tempy. lieut., R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915 (General List); med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

PHILLIPS, Arthur, M.A. (Oxon.), B.A. (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1907; ed. Highgate Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford (schol.); dist. offr., Kenya, July, 1931; ag. res. mag., 1933-35; crown coun., Sept., 1936.

PHILLIPS, Harold Ernest.—B. 1877; clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs and P.O. depts., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd S.A. Coll., Cape Town; advoc., sup. ct. of S. Africa; addl. legal advr., Cape Prov. admntr., 1929; war serv., S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-19; partly, draughtsman, partt. of Union of S. Africa, 1936.

PIENAAR, Lieut-Col. Filippus Fourie, D.T.D., O.B.E., R. of O., Union Defence Force—Civ. serv., S.A. Republic; ment. in depts., Great War; admitted sworn translator, sup. ct.; accredited rep. of Union of S.Africa to League of Nations; H.M. cons.-gen. in Mozambique for the Union of S. Africa, 1931; min. plenipotentiary, Lisbon, Jan., 1939.

PIENAAR, General J. J.—B. 1877; farmer; burger, Republican forces, 1899; fieldcornet and later comdt. of Rand Commando, wounded twice; presented with flag by Johannesburg public, 1900; civ. serv., 1904; regir., brands, 1909; contr., fencing and regir., brands, Union; after having qualified at Mily. Coll., apptd. dist. staff offr., commanding E. Transvaal and N. Natal, 1912; resigned, 1914; entered politics, 1920; el. M.P. for Marico, 1924; admtr., prov. of Transvaal.

PIERS, Edward Stanyan Fairbanks, B.Sc.—B. 1903; ed. Dalhousie Univ., 1922-25 and Nova Scotia Tech. Coll., 1925-28; draftsman, Nova Scotia power comm., 1928-30; asst. engnr., Halifax harbours comm., 1930-36; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1937.
PIERS, Major Francis A.—B. 1882; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; inspr., 1923; supt., pol., 1936; commr., pol. and prisons, 1939.

PIGGOTT, Sir Francis Taylor, Kt. Bach. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed asst. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to C.O. as asst. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

PILGRIM, Edward Oliver.—B. 1886; ed. Lodge Schi., Barbados; B.Sc. (Lond.), hons. physics; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1905; ag. prin. various occasions; mem., bd. of land survrs., exams., 1916.

PILLING, Henry Guy, C. M. G. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. King's Schi., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Comm., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high comm., 1926; asst. to high commr., W. Pacific, 1929; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Dec., 1929; ag. gov. in 1930 and 1931; dep. col. sec., Kenya, 1933; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., St. Helena, 1936.
PIMM, Surg. Lieut. Sydney Richard Gerald, R.N.—Radiologist, col. hosp., Port of Spain, Trinidad, Mar., 1937.

PINDER, Richard Francis, (R. of O.).—B. 1898; ed. St. Bees, Cumberland; served, European War, Flanders, Germany, 1916-19; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920; ag. audr., Nov.-Dec., 1920; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1929; ag. audr., Feb.-May, 1930; sen. asst. audr., Uganda, Aug., 1931; ag. audr., July-Dec., 1932; audr., Fiji, Solomon Is. Prot., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Aug., 1934; do., N. Rhodesia, 1938.

PINKERTON, WILLIAM JAMES DUNLOP, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1890; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engrn., Ulu Selangor, July, 1923; sr. exec. engrn., in addn., Jan., 1930; offst. as sr. exec. engrn., Kinta, in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1930; senr. drainage and irrign. engrn. Malay States, Jan., 1932; irrign. engrn. Kelantan, Jan., 1932; asst. advr., drainage and irrign., Malay States, Nov., 1935; az. dir., drnge. and irrign., S.S., and advr., do., Malay States, July, 1936 and 1939.

PINNEY, CAPT. CHAS. ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Schl.; draughtsman, lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transfld. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsman, Papua, 1920; staff survr. in addn., 1924; ag. comsrr., lands, etc., 1927; comsrr., lands, dir., mines, dir., agr., dep. chmn., land bd., mem. exec. and leg. couns., July, 1930; adinstr., Norfolk Is., July, 1932.

PIERIE, D. A.—B. 1886; elk. Burgher camps, Transvaal, 1903; treas., 1903; prin. elk. treas., Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. mily. pensions bd., treas., 1920; ch. elk., grade II, 1928; ch. elk., grade I, 1932; dep. comsrr., pensions, 1936.
PIERIE, JAMES HENRY.—B. 1893; ed. St Paul's Schl., Darjeeling, India; asst. storekeeper, Nigerian Eastern Rly. constrn., 1931; storekeeper, Nigerian rly. open lines, 1931-37; stores supt., do., 1937.

PIROW, OSWALD.—B. 1890; ed. South Africa, Germany and England; admitted barrister, 1913; K.C., 1924; ent. partl., of the Union of S. Africa, 1924; min. of justice, June, 1929; min., rlys. and harbs. and min. defence, Mar., 1933; World Econ. Confce., London, 1933; min., commerce and industry, and defence, 1938.

PITBLADO, DAVID BRUCE, M.A.—B. 1912; ed. Strand Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (schol. 1930-34); hist. triplos part 1 and law triplos part 2; B.A., 1933; hist. triplos part 2, cls. 1, 1934; supervisor in hist., Emm. Coll., 1934-35; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1935; asst. ptyc. sec. to S. of S. for Dom. Affairs (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald) 1st July, 1937; ag. prin., 1st June, 1939.

PITCAIRN, ANDREW.—B. 1894; ed. Edinburgh Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., 1915-19 (France, Mesopotamia and Palestine); lieut., R.F.A.; ment. in desps.; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; asst. dir., agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; ag. dir., agr., on various occasions, 1930-39; ag. dir., animal husbandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June-Sept., 1932; ag. registr., co-op. socs., Sept., 1939.

PITMAN, CHARLES ROBERT SEXHOURSE, D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916)—B. 1890; ed. Royal Naval Schl., Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; att'd. R. Warwickshire Regt.; 27th Punjabis; capt., 1915; war serv. Egypt, 1914-15; France, 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916-18 (wounded, desps. twice, M.C., D.S.O.) Palestine, 1918-21; gen. staff, 1919-21; game warden, Uganda, 1925; seconded to N. Rhodesia for sp. duty, 1931-33.

PITT, WALTER JAMES, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and medieval language triplos) ent. Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; headmr., Cape Coast Govt. Schl., 1910; inspr. of schl., 1912; prov. inspr. of schl., 1914; attd. to polit. serv., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; asst. dist. comsrr., July, 1917; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1921; ag. prov. comsrr., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1920.

PITTMAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1878; ed. S. African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (1904) and Transvaal (1905) bars; dean of faculty of law, Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa eastern dists. local divn., 1925; mem., delimitation coms., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

PLATT, ROBERT PAUS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1905; ed. Shrewsbury and Queens' Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Kenya, 1927; dist. offr., 1929; ag. ast. sec., 1935; seconded to C.O., Jan., 1938 to May, 1939; ast. civ. sec., Aden, June, 1939; ag. civ. sec., July to Sept., 1939.

PLATTS, SHELLEY ARDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; "King's India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th Gurka Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; attd. 2/11th Gurka Rifles and 4/70th Burma Rifles, Burma, 1921; cadet, admstve. service, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926; ast. dist. offr., July, 1928; dist. offr., 1938.

PLAYFORD, HON. THOMAS, M.P.—B. 1896; served in A.I.F. in great war; el. part., 1933; comsrr. crown lands, min. of repatriation and irrign., S. Aust., 1938; premier, treas. and min. of immigrn., 5th Nov., 1938.

PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZHERBERT, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. (class. triplos), ast. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot. Mar., 1912; pol. offr., Turkana Expdn., 1915 (A.G.S. Med. with clasps); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.B.M. Vice-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.B.M. Consul on transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; transfld. Somaliland, 1922; H.B.M. chargé d'affaires, Addis Ababa, Dec., 1925 to Jan., 1926; mem., mission to Abyssinia for coronation of Emperor, 1930; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; ast. comsrr. and pol. offr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bdy. comsrr., 1931-33; sec., govt. of Somaliland, 1933; ag. comsrr., May-Oct., 1934; ag. gov., May-Oct., 1936 and Oct., 1937-Jan., 1938; has passed E. Africa Prot. law exam. and in Swahili, Boran Galla and Ambaric languages.

PLUNKETT, JOHN OLIVER, M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1888; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1914; demob. with rank of capt., Sept., 1919; ast. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; do., S.S., Sept., 1922; exec. engrn., Nov., 1929; exec. engrn., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; senr. exec. engrn., Mar., 1936.

PLYMOUTH, 2nd Earl of (cr. 1905) and 15th Baron Windsor (cr. 1529) IVOR MILES WINDSOR-CULVER.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; D.L., Worcestershire; lord-lieut., Glamorganshire, 1923; capt., hon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1925; M.P., Ludlow divn., Shropshire, 1922-23; partly under-sec. of state for Dominion affrs., Jan., 1929; kt. of grace of St. John of Jerusalem; partly, sec., min. of transport, Sept., 1931; partly, under sec. of state for the cols., 30th Sept., 1932; do., F.O., 1st, Sept., 1936-39.

POLLARD, ARNOLD.—B. 1893; ed. Heles Schl., Exeter; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 1915-19; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; ast. AUDR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, APR., 1920; ASST. AUDR., KENYA, MAY, 1924; AG. SENR. ASST. AUDR. ON SEVERAL OCCASIONS IN TANGANYIKA AND KENYA; AUDR., WINDWARD IS., JAN., 1926; SENR. ASST. AUDR., UGANDA, JAN., 1929; AUDR., TRINIDAD, JUNE, 1931; DO., N. RHODESIA, 1935; DO., HONG KONG, 1938.

POLLOCK, CAPT. JAMES HUBERT HAMIL (R. OF O.), O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1893; ED. ROYAL SCHL., ARMAGH; WAR SERV., 1914-20; GALLIPOLI, MACEDONIA, EGYPT AND PALESTINE; MEN IN DESP.; STAFF CAPT., O.E.T.A.; INSPR., GRADE 3, PALESTINE, JULY, 1920; DEP. GOV., GRADE 3, RAMALLAH, OCT., 1920; ADMSTVE. OFF., NIGERIA, SEPT., 1923; ASST. SEC., COMMR., LAGOS, DEC., 1927; ADMSTVE. OFF., PALESTINE, JAN., 1930; ASST. DIST. COMMR., 1932.

POOLE, EDWARD HUMPHREY LANE.—ED. RUGBY AND BALLIOL COLL.; OXFORD, B.A.; ENT. B.S.A.C.'S ADMSTRN., N. RHODESIA, 1913; ASST. NAT. COMMR., 1915; NAT. COMMR., 1919; NAT. MAG., 1926; TITLE CHANGED TO DIST. OFFR., 1928; AG. DIR. NAT. EDUCN., 1934-35; PROV. COMMR., 1936; MEM. AND SEC., NATIVE RESERVES COMMN., E. PROV., 1924-25; VARIOUS PUBLICATIONS ON NATIVE HISTORY AND CUSTOMS.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; APPTD. AFTER COMPET. EXAM., CLER. OFFR., C.O., 22ND OCT., 1920; CLER. OFFR., HIGHER GRADE, AUG., 1933.

POPE, GEORGE WILLIAM, L.L.M.R.C.P., AND S.I., D.P.H., R.C.P. AND S.I.—B. 1891; MED. OFFR., KENYA, 1920; 2ND HEALTH OFFR., PORT MEDI. DEPT., HONG KONG, 1924; AG. ASST. BACTERIOLOGIST, 1924; ASST. M.O.H., 1925; M.O.H., 1926; HEALTH OFFR., 1936.

POPE, LAWRENCE HENRY.—B. 1896; ED. UPPINGHAM; SERVED EUROPEAN WAR, 1915-19 (FRANCE AND N. RUSSIA); NAT. P.S. TO GOV'T., S. AUSTRALIA, SIR W. E. E. A. WEIGALL, K.C.M.G., 1920; P.S. TO GOV'T., TASMANIA, SIR W. L. ALLARDYCE, G.C.M.G., 1921; COL. AUDIT DEPT., 1923; ASST. AUDR., FIJI, 1923; AG. AUDR., 1924-28; ASST. AUDR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, 1928; DO., NIGERIA, 1935; SENR. ASST. AUDR., 1936; AUDR., FIJI AND W. PACIFIC, 1938.

POPE, STEAD.—BR. POST OFFICE, FEB., 1903; DIST. SURVR., POST AND TEL., GOLD COAST, DEC., 1913; ON ACTIVE SERV., TOGO LAND FIELD FORCE, AUG., 1914; AND LATER IN CHARGE POSTS AND TELS., TOGO LAND TO MAY, 1915; IMP. ARMY SERV., SEPTEMBER, 1915-19; LIEUT. R. E. AND R.A.F.; DIST. SURVR., GOLD COAST, JUNE, 1919; DIST. SURVR., JAN., 1927; P.M.G., NYASALAND, MAR., 1930; REP. NYASALAND AIR MAIL CONFEE., SALISBURY, 1934, AFRICAN POST AND TELECOMMUNICATIONS CONFEE., PRETORIA, 1935, AND CONFEE. RE-INTERLOCKING CENT. AFRICAN SERVICES, SALISBURY, 1935; P.M.G., GOLD COAST, JAN., 1937.

POPHAM, SIR HENRY BRADSHAW, K.C.M.G. (1938), C.M.G. (1935), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ED. TONBRIDGE SCHL.; COMMR., 13TH LIGHT INFY., 1900; SERVED, AFRICAN WAR (2 MEDS.); SECONDED, G. COAST REGT., W.A.F.F., 1906; PRIV. SEC. AND A.D.C. TO AG. GOV'T., G. COAST, MAR. TO AUG., 1909; COL. SEC.'S OFFICE, JAN., 1910; PRIV. SEC. TO AG. GOV', FEB. TO JUNE, 1911; AG. CH. ASST. COL. SEC. AND CLK. OF CONFEE., JULY TO AUG., 1913; AG. SENR. ASST. COL. SEC., OCT., 1913; DIST. COMMR., JAN., 1914; SECONDED TO TOGO LAND POL'IT. ADMSTRN., NOV., 1914; SENR. ASST. COL. SEC. AND CLK. OF CONFEE., JAN., 1920; AG. CH. ASST. COL. SEC., SEPT., 1920 TO FEB., 1921; DEP. PROV. COMMR. AND AG. COMMR., W. PROV., FEB., 1921; PROV. COMMR., APR., 1922; RETD. (INVALIDED) MAY, 1923; COMMR., CYPRUS, OCT., 1925; ADMSTR., DOMINICA, 1933; MEM., EXEC. CONFEE., LEEWARD IS., 1933; GOV. AND COMMR.-IN-CH., WINDWARD IS., 1937.

POPHAM, AIR CHIEF MARSHALL SIR HENRY ROBERT MOORE BROOKE, G.C.V.O. (1935), K.C.B. (1927), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915)—B. 1878; ED. HAILEYBURY, SANDHURST AND STAFF COLL.; ENT. ARMY, 1898; CAPT., 1904; R.F.C., 1912; BT. MAJ., 1913; MAJ., 1915; R.A.F., 1919; SERVED EUROPEAN WAR, 1914-19; COMMDT., R.A.F. STAFF COLL., 1921-26; AIR OFFR., COMMDG. FIGHTING AREA DEFENCES, GT. BRITAIN, 1926-28; AIR OFFR., COMMDG. IRAQ, COMMDT., 1928-30; COMMDT., IMP. DEFENCE COLL., 1931-33; AIR OFFR., COMMDG. IN-CH., AIR DEFENCE, GT. BRITAIN, 1933-35; PRIN. AIR A.D.C. TO THE KING SINCE 1933; I.G., R.A.F., 1935-36; GOV. AND COMMDR.-IN-CH., KENYA AND HIGH COMMR. FOR TRANSPORT, 1936-39.
POPLEWELL, GEOFFREY DOUGLAS, B.A. (OXON).—B. 1904; CADET, PROVINCIAL ADMINSTRN., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, APRIL, 1927; ASST. DIST. OFFR., MAY, 1929; AG. DIST. OFFR., VARIOUS PERIODS, 1931-35.
PORT, FRANCIS GEORGE RICHARD.—B. 1892; ED. TRURO GRAMMAR SCHL.; ASST. ACCTN., NIGERIANLY, OPEN LINES, 1920; ACCTN., 1928; PERSONAL ASST. TO GEN. MAN., 1936; PRIN. ADMSTVE. ASST., APR., 1938.
PORTER, GEORGE EDWARD JOSEPH, M.R.C.S. (ENG.), L.R.C.P.—B. 1909; ED. FLORES COLL., VALLETTA, MALTA, DOUAI SCHL., WOOLHAMPTON AND ST. MARY'S HOSP., PADDDINGTON; D.M.O., CYPRUS, FEB., 1938.
PORTER, REGINALD VERNON HERBERT—B. 1900; ED. CHELTENHAM AND WADHAM COLL., OXFORD, B.A. FORESTRY; INDIAN ARMY, 1918-22; ASST. COMMR., FORESTS, KENYA, OCT., 1929.
PORTER, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A., M.B., B.CH., B.A.O.—B. 1906; ED. FOYLE COLL., LONDONDERRY, TRINITY COLL., DUBLIN, QUEEN'S UNIV., BELFAST, AND R.C.S., EDIN.; D.M.O., CYPRUS, NOV., 1936.

POSTMUS, JOHANNES.—B. 1877; ED AT SNEEK, HOLLAND; GEN. MAN., NETHERLANDS BANK OF S.A., 1920-26; DEP. GOV'T., S. AFRICAN RESERVE BANK, 1927-31; GOV'T., S. AFRICAN RESERVE BANK, JAN., 1932; WORLD ECON. CONFEE., LONDON, 1933.

POTT, GIDEON ELIOT.—B. 1909; ED. ST. ANDREW'S GRAHAMSTOWN, EDINBURGH ACAD. AND TRINITY HALL, CAMBRIDGE; GRADE II CLK., BASUTOLAND, 1933; ASST. DIST. COMMR. (CADET), 1937; REGISTR., RES. COMMR'S OFF., 1937; ASST. DIST. COMMR., 1937.

POTTINGER, JOSEPH HECTOR, M.B., CH.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (LPOOL).—B. 1898; ON WAR SERV., 1915-18; MED. OFFR., HEALTH, W. AFRICAN MED. STAFF, GOLD COAST, 1928; ASST. GOV'T. MED. OFFR. OF HEALTH, BR. GUIANA, 1932; SENR. MED. OFFR., PALESTINE, 1935.

POTTS, JOHN WILFRED, I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1882; SERVED IN BECHUANALAND PROT. POL., 1912-15; CLK., 1915; AG. ASST. RES. MAG., 1920; AG. RES. MAG., 1920 AND 1922-23; PASSED CIV. SER. LOWER LAW EXAM., 1919; PASSED 2ND GRADE SECWANA EXAM., 1922; SUB. INSPR., BECH. PROT. POL., 1924; INSPR., MAR., 1927; RES. MAG., APR., 1929; DIST. COMMR., 1937.

POULIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A., LOND.; CADET, CEYLON CIV. SERV., AUG., 1919; POL. MAG., MATALE, FEB., 1922; ADDNL. DIST. JUDGE, MANNAR AND MULLAITIVU, OCT., 1924; ADDNL. ASST. GOV'T. AGT., COLOMBO, DEC., 1925; COMMR., REQUESTS, KANDY, OCT., 1927; ASST. GOV'T. AGT., MULLAITIVU, NOV., 1928; ASST. GOV'T. AGT., COLOMBO, OCT., 1931; DO., MANNAR, OCT., 1934; DO., KEGALLA, AUG., 1933; AG. GOV'T. AGT., PROV. OF SABARAGAMWA, APR., 1937.

POULTON, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1900; ED. R. MILY. ACAD., WOOLWICH; R.A., 1918; IRAQ DD campaign, 1920; Somaliland Camel Corps (K.A.R.), 1927; comsnr., 3rd grade, Br. Somaliland, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1932.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African war; dist. comdtr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv. dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade comsnr., Durban, S. Africa, 1909; trade comsnr., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for active serv., 1914; returned Can., 1919; inspnr., trade comsnr. serv.; sp. trade comsnr., India, 1919; dir., comm. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade comsnr., Trinidad, 1924.

POWELL, GEORGE MAURICE CALER, L.R.C.P.I. & L.M., L.R.C.S.I. & L.M., D.P.H., R.C.P.S.I., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), F.R.C.S.I.—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1914-18; N. Rhodesia, med. offr., 1920; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.
POWELL, HARRY CRESWELL, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1881; mily. serv., France, 1915-19; traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rly., Jan., 1921; dir., do., Oct., 1930.

POWER, HON. CHARLES GAYAN, K.C., B.A., LL.L. (Quebec South).—B. 1888; ed. in Loyola Coll., Montreal; grad., 1907; B.A., LL.L., Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1910; enlisted Jan., 1916; served overseas, twice wounded, invalided from serv., 1918; capt. and ag. major, M.C.; el. to H. of C., Canada, Dec., 1917; re-el., g.e., 1921, 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of pensions and nat. health, Mackenzie King's cabinet, Oct., 1935.

POWER, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; Indian Army, 1917; seconded, I.C.S. as polit. res., Perim Is., 1922-23; col. audit dep., Apr., 1924; asst. audr., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dep. audr., July, 1928; audr., Windward Is., Feb., 1929; senr. asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1936; ag. audr., Oct., 1937-Sept., 1938.
POWER, GERALD, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1914; asst. dir., educn., Gold Coast, May, 1929; dir., ditto, Feb., 1933.
POWERS, HON. SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1929).—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913; ret.

POYNTON, ARTHUR HILTON.—B. 1905; schol. of Marlborough Coll. (1923-7); 1st cl. hons., mods., 1925; 2nd cl. hons., lit. hum., 1927; M.A., 1933; apptd. asst. prin., dept. of scientific and industrial research after compet. exam., 31st Oct., 1927; transfld. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1929; joint sec., Col. Development Advisory Comtce., 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1930; sec., do., 1st April, 1932; pte. sec. to partly. under sec. for cols. (Sir R. Hamilton), 18th April, 1932 and to (Lord Plymouth), 30th Sept., 1932; seconded to Sierra Leone admnstr., Apr., 1933-35; prin., D.O., 9th Sept., 1935; do., C.O., 4th Nov., 1935.

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON, D.S.O., K.C. (Barbados).—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar. Inner Temple, 1906; served European War, 1914-20, Yorks. L.I., and Loyal N. Lanc. Regt. (desps. thrice, D.S.O., 1917); puisne judge, Leeward Islands, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1925; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1928; puisne just., Ceylon, 1933; ch. just., F.M.S., 1939.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram.schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-84; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1895-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1906; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1906; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class. tripes), B.A., 1905; baccalaureat (B.L. 1st part) Lyons Univ., France, 1908; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911; ag. supt., schla., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1921; promoted cls. I, grade I, admstve serv., Feb., 1928.

PRASAD, MURARI, B.Sc. (Allahabad). Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apr., 1919, office asst. Jaffna kach., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag., Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1927; comsnr. stamps, July, 1932; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Oct., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Oct., 1935; govt. agt., E.P., Mar., 1936.

PREEDY, CAPT. BERNARD, M.C.—B.1894; ed. Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; M.A. (Oxon.); European mast., Taiping, Sept., 1922; headmast., govt. English schl., Kajang, Oct., 1927; do., Clifford sch., K. Kangsar, Feb., 1930.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verandrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910; ch. just., Dec., 1929.

PRENTICE, ROBERT JOHNSTON CALDER, F.S.I., M.R.San.I., M.C.Q.S.—B. 1893; P.W.D., 1909; asst. ch. quantity survr., Union of S. Africa, 1933; ch. quantity survr., 1935; pres.inch., Inst. S.A. Architects and Chapter of Quantity Surveyors, 1938; pres., Chapter S.A.Q.S., 1934-35, 1935-36 and 1939-40.

PRENTIS, JOHN BERNARD.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; admitted solr. sup. ct. of judicature, Eng., 1932; priv. prac. Hong Kong, 1932-35; junr. legal offr., Hong Kong, July, 1935; J.P., 1935; ag. asst. land offr. and dep. regtr., marriages, July, 1935; asst. cr. solr., Dec., 1935; ag. asst. land offr. and dep. regtr., marriages in adnl., Jan., 1936; ag. offr. recr. and regtr. trade marks and letters pat., Mar., 1936; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Aug., 1938.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cl. clk., 1899; asst. regist. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PRETHEROE, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A.; LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; admstve. offr., 1922; astt. commr., lands, 1924; ag. commr., lands, 1924; astt. sec., S. Prov., 1924; pol. mag., 1928; ag. ch. regis., sup. ct. on various occasions; crown coun., 1930; ag. solr.-gen. on various occasions; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1939.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S.S., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and astt. adjt., J.V.R., May, 1918; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; Br. res., Brunei, Mar., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, June, 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; ag. under-sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1937; offr. cls. I.B., Apr., 1937; fin. commr., and aud. gen., Johore, Dec., 1937.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Schl., Somerset and University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, astt. prin., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th July, 1927 (ag. from 6th June, 1927); temp. ag. prin., D.O., 22nd July, 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec. for dom. affrs., Jan., 1928; astt. sec. Imp. wireless and cable confce., Jan., July, 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 15th Sept., 1928; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1928 and 1932; U.K. delegn. to London naval confce., 1930; attd. to sec., Imp. confce., 1930; U.K. delegn. disarmament confce., Geneva, Feb.-Dec., 1932.

PRICE, REGINALD WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Newport High Schl.; war serv., R. Gloucester Hussars Yeomanry and R.F.A., 1914-19; in Egypt, Gallipoli, Mesopotamia and Palestine; audit offr., min. of finance, Iraq, 1920; acctnt., P.W.D. Tanganyika Territory, 1925; dep. ch. acctnt., 1926; ch. acctnt., 1935; ch. acctng. offr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1938.
PRICE, THOMAS JOHN, B.Sc., Dip. Ed. Sheffield—B. 1900; astt. mast., Hong Kong educn. dept., 1928; headmast., E.K. Indian Schl., 1939; mobilised warrant offr., H.K.N.V.R., Sept., 1939.

PRICE-JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; astt. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; dist. commr., Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., 1928; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., Sept., Oct., 1935; ag. ch. regis. and sheriff, various occasions, 1935-37.

PRIESTMAN, HAROLD EDNEY, C.M.G. (1937), B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1888; mil. serv., 1914-19; temp. admin. offr., min. of lab., 1919; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; attached, to C.O., 1930; seconded as ag. col. sec. and senr. astt. col. sec., Gambia, 1931; resumed duties, C.O., 1932; resumed substantive appt., Nigeria, 1933; admin. sec. to high commr., for Basutoland, Bech. Prot. and Swaziland, 1935.

PRING, HARRY.—B.1883; ed. Watford Schl. and King's Coll., London; Imp. serv., 1898-1903; col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1903; dept. of interior, Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., 1925; under sec., 1927; pub. serv. commr., 1931.

PRINGLE, LEONARD DUNCAN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1905; med. offr., F.M.S., 1931; Hong Kong, 1932; ag. radiologist, Jan.-Aug., 1933; M.O. in ch. of officers' families, Victoria Gaol, infec. diseases hosp., Victoria mortuary and remand home, 1934; med. offr. in ch., civ. hosp., 1935; med. offr., Kowloon hosp., Apr., 1936; ag. radiologist, Apr., 1937.

PRITCHARD, NEIL.—B. 1911; ed. Liverpool Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford (hist. schol., 1929-32); hist. schol., 1st ols.; B.A., 1932; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. prin., D.O., Oct., 1933; ag. prin., 2nd Feb., 1939.

PRYER, JOHN HAWKES.—B. 1894; ed. Bedford Harpur Trust; mech. transport, B.E.F., France, 1915-18; cadet pilot, R.A.F., 1918-19; astt. storekeeper, Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1920; astt. ch., do., Aug., 1929; ch., do., Mar., 1932.

PSAILA, JOSEPH STANISLAUS.—B. 1887; ed. Stanislaus Gram. Schl., Br. Guiana and St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges, Belgium; astt. commr., 1917; 3rd grade do., 1926; 3rd cls. offr., dist. commr.'s office, 1932; cls. II offr., do., 1934; inspr., distilleries, 1936.
PUCKERIN, MERVYN ALLEYN.—B. 1891; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; supt., govt. indust. schls., 1924; govt., Glendairy prison, 1932; supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1938.

PUCKRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1895; ed. Exeter Schl.; 2nd lieut., Devon Regt., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 5th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comn. of Control, Germany, 1920; resigned commr., Sept., 1921; astt. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commr., 1925; astt. col. sec., 1928.

PUDNEY, ERIC WILLIAM, A.C.A.—B. 1903; astt. acct., Gold Coast, Dec., 1930; astt. treas., Apr., 1934; ag. dep. treas., June-Sept., 1936; astt. treas., Mauritius, Oct., 1936; ag. treas., Feb., 1938, Jly., 1939; acctnt.-gen., Jly., 1939; acctnt., treasy., Hong Kong, Jan., 1940.

PUDSEY, FAWCOTT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering astt., dock engr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hesle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; astt.
