REELER, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1884; ed. Sea Point High Sch., Cape Town; Cape col. pub. serv., 1899; Cape Prov. admstr., 1910; prov. acct., 1922.
REEFS, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.Sc.(hons. Wales), Dip. Edc. (Wales).—B. 1903; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1930.

REES-DAVIES, SIR WM., Kt. Bach. (1913).—B. 1863; ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1885; called to bar (Inner Temp.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembrokeshire; J.P. for Haverfordwest; M.P. for Pembrokeshire, 1892-8; priv. sec. to the late Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5, and afterwards from 1895-8; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1897; administered the govt., 1901; ag. ch. just., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, Oct., 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1906; K.C., 1908; ag. ch. just., 1909-10; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1912; chmn. of gen. mily. serv. tribunal, 1918; chmn., pub. serv. comsn., Hong Kong, 1919; chmn., exec. comte. on the visit of H.R.H. The Prince of Wales, 1922; ret., 1924.

REEVE, GEORGE WALKER, B.A. hons. (Alta., Canada).—B. 1887; on mil. serv., 1914-19; admstr. branch, A.I.P., Air Mny., 1918-22; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Mar., 1922; lectr., Hong Kong Univ., 1925-33; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931 and 1933; headmnr., Wansai schl., 1934; do., Kadodio schl., 1936; do., Yaumati schl., 1938; senr. mast., 1939.

REEVES, WILLIAM JENSEN OLIFF.—B. 1894; ed. St. Michael's, London, S.W. and Westminster Tech. Inst.; war serv., 1914-18; ch. clk. and draughtsman, Gold Coast Rly., Jan., 1920; asst. engrn. (do.), Apr., 1920; ag. engrn., on various occasions, 1934, 1936, 1937-38; senr. engrn., Mar., 1937; do., Nigerian Rly., May, 1939; ag. ch. engrn., June-July, 1939.

REGNARD, MAJOR LOUIS FERDINAND.—B. 1888; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology and McGill Univ. (B.Sc.); 2nd lieut., R.E., Nov., 1914; war serv., France, Belgium and Palestine, 1914-20; major, R.E., 1918-20; regular army R. O.; asst. D.P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1921; D.P.W., 1926; M.L.C.
REID, C. B. B., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

REID, JAMES STUART WELLESLEY, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1899; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1921; attchd. land office, Kajang, Nov., 1921; do., dist. office, L. Perak, Mar., 1922; do., U. Perak as financ. asst., Sept., 1922; passed cadet, July, 1923; priv. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Jan., 1924; offr. cls. V, Oct., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Apr., 1925; do., Sitiawan, Sept., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Oct., 1928; 2nd asst. advr., Muar, Apr., 1931; ag. dist. offr., K. Bahru and P. Puteli, and treas., Kelantan, May, 1932; offr. cls. III, Oct., 1933; 2nd asst. sec. (A), S.S. Mar., 1935; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A), Dec., 1935; offr. cls. II, Oct., 1938; ag. dist. offr., Kang, May, 1939.

REID, NIEL RICHARD, B.V. Sc. (S. Africa), M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1906; ed. Cordwalles, Natal, Univ. of Witwatersrand, Transvaal Univ. Coll. and Royal Vety., Coll., London; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1929.

REID, V. O.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Johannesburg, 1908-15; mine sampler and survr., Crown Mines, 1916-17; cadet and temp. lieut., R.A.F., 1918; min. survr., Crown Mines, 1919; ch. min. survr., Modderfontein East, 1924; shift boss and mine overseer, Modderfontein East, 1928; mine survr., Crown Mines, 1933; asst. inspr., mines, Johannesburg, 1934; dep. inspr., mines, 1936; inspr., mines, Brakpan, 1938.
REIDY, CAPT. MISCEL JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Veterinary Coll., Dublin; Fiji, 1922; asst. col. vet. surg., Hong Kong, 1924.

REILLY, SIR BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.I.E. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1882; ed. Bedford; joined Indian Army, 1902; ent. Indian polit. dept., 1908; served in India and Aden in various appts.; officiated as polit. res., Aden, 1925 and 1926, and as res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1930 and 1931; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Mar., 1931; ch. consnr., Aden, Apr., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, Apr., 1937.

REILLY, JOSEPH OLIVER.—B. 1891; Imp. postal serv. 1911; war serv., R.E.a., 1914-19; postmr., Tanganyika, 1920; postmr., Nyasaland, 1924; ag. acct. tel. engnr. and survr., P. and T., Nyasaland, 1932; ag. P.M.G., Nyasaland, 1935; P.M.G., Br. Gaiana, July, 1936; dep. P.M.G., Gold Coast, May, 1939.

REINECKE, THEODORE GERALD WELLERBY, M.Sc. Agr.—B. 1888; lect. in chem., Schl. of Agr., Potchefstroom, 1910; prin., Schl. of Agr., Elsenberg, 1918; do., Potchefstroom, 1920; dir., Steitenboch-Elsenberg Coll. of Agr., 1926; prin., Schl. of Agr., Glen, O.F.S., 1929; dir., native agr., Union native affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1934.

REITZ, HON. DENYS.—B. 1882; M.P. for Bloemfontein, 1920; M.P. for Port Elizabeth, 1920-27; M.P. for Barberton, 1929; min. of lands, Smuts cabinet, 1921-24; min. of lands and irrig., coalition cab., 1933; min., agr., irrig., and forestry, 1935; min. of mines and irrig., 1938; min. for native affrs., 1939.

RENDLE, HILARY CAMERON RUSSEL.—B. 1899; ed. Christ's Coll., N. Zealand, Cantby Coll., Univ. of N.Z. and New Coll., Oxford; cadet F.M.S., Nov., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1923; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1924; asst. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1925; priv. sec. to chief sec., July, 1925; asst. dist. offr., B. Pahang, Feb., 1927; offr. cls. IV, 2nd asst. advr., Muar, Nov., 1928; asst. dist. offr., L. Perak, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1933; dist. offr., K. Lipis, Feb., 1934; offr. cls. II, Nov., 1938.
RENISON, PATRICK MUIR, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1911; C.O., Oct., 1932; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1935; priv. sec. to govt., Mar., 1936; office asst., Kandy, Apr., 1938.

RENNIE, ALFRED BAILEY, LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; lieut., B.W.I.R.; war serv., 1916-19; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; elk. of cts., Jamaica, May, 1929; res. mag., Jan., 1933; ag. judge, Kingston ct., Jan.-Dec., 1933.

RENNIE, GILBERT McCall, M.C., M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1896; on mil. serv., July, 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapura kachcheri, June, 1920; attd. to N’Eliya kachcheri, Nov., 1920; office astt. to gort. agt., E. Prov., Nov., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Dec., 1923; ditto, Chilaw, Mar., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1923; office astt. to dir., med. and san. services, Apr., 1926; sec., min. for health, July, 1931; contr., finance and supply, Sept., 1932; sec. to gov., Dec., 1934; finan. sec., Gold Coast, 1937; ch. sec., Kenya, 1939.

RBNWICK, HENRY LANODON.—B. 1898; London Rifle Brig., Apr., 1915; attd. gen. staff, W.O., 1919; ch. clk. to H.M.T.C. in E. Africa, Dec., 1919; astt. imigrn. and embarkation offr., Mombasa, July, 1920; astt. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1920; imigrn., offr., Aug., 1921; ag. astt. compt., cust., for various periods, 1923-34; ag. aviation control offr., Aug., 1932; ag. compt., cust., Feb.-Aug., 1934; senr. supervr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1937.
RETIEF, D. J., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; prin., Boshof, 1913; prin., Winburg, 1921; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1927.

REX, MARCUS.—B. 1886; ed. Highgate and Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhbr.); B.A., 1908; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; astt. sec. to res., Perak, 1912 and 1915; dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1913; supt., chandu monopoly, Sel., N.S. and Pahang, 1917; dist. offr., Raub, 1919; mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1921, sec., retrenchment comm., in addn., Feb., 1922; dep. contr. of rubber expts., in addn., Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. in., Aug., 1930; finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Jan., 1932; offr., cls. IA., June, 1932; contr., rubber, Malaya, May., 1934; ag. ch. sec. to gov., F.M.S., Apr., 1935; ag. Br. res., Perak, Feb., 1936; finan. sec., F.M.S., Aug., 1937; Br. res., Perak, 1939.
REYNOLDS, A. E., M.B.E. (1938).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918.

RHODES, ALICE LEOPOLD, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1884; war serv., 1915-19; gen. foreman, gov. printing and stationery dept., Trinidad, 1921; typography instr., bd. of indus. training, 1922; mem., bd. indus. training, 1923; gov. printer, 1925.

RHODES, HON. E. N., P.C. (Can.), K.C., B.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1877; ed. Amherst Acad., Acadia Univ. and Dalhousie Univ.; apptd. K.C., Nova Scotia, May, 1916; 1st lt. to H. of C. Canada at g.e., 1908; el. dep. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1916; speaker, 1917; re-el. Mar., 1918; el. to legis., N.S., 1925; prov. premier and prov. sec., 1925; re-el. at g.e., 1928; joined Dom. cabinet of Bennett adminstr., as min. of fisheries, Aug., 1930; min. of finance, 1932; mem., senate, 1935.
RHODES, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GODFREY DEAN, Kt., Bach. (1934), C.B.E., D.S.O., R.E.(ret.), M.E.I.C., M.Inst.T., Assoc Inst.C.E., M.S.A.S.C.E.—Ch. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rly., 1920; gen. man., 1928.

RHODES, HON. SIR ROBERT HATTON, K.C.V.O. (1927), K.B.E. (1920).—B. 1861; B.A., Oxford; entd. New Zeal. parl., 1899; served in S. African war (Queen’s medal and 4 clasps); lt.-col., 1st Regt., Canterbury Rifles Yeomanry Cavalry; postmr.-gen., min. of telegraphs, min. of pub. health, 1912-1915; min. of defence, 1920-26; comanr., state forests, 1922-26; leader of leg. coun. and mem., exec. coun. without portfolio, 1926; dep. leader, leg. coun., 1927; ret. from office, 1928 and from leg. coun., 1932; re-apptd., M.L.C., 1934.

RHODES, T. A. F., B.A., M.Econ. —Ed. Transvaal Univ., Pretoria; clk. Cape post. and tel. serv., 1899; sen. asst. acct., 1924; ch. clk., 1926; contr. of stores and transp., 1929; ch. acct., 1930; asst. under-sec., 1932; under-sec. (staff), 1936; prov. sec., Natal, 1937.

RHODES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxon.).—B. 1881; ed. Bromsgrove Schl., Cambridge Univ. and St. Thomas’ Hosp.; sen. govt. pathologist, and offr.-in-charge, govt. vaccine inst., Cape Town.
RIBBINIK, S. J. P.—B. 1889; ed. Holland, Germany and France; studied for the diplomatic and consular serv.; libr., dep. of agr., Pretoria, 1912; libr. to parl. of Union of S. Africa, 1921.

RICE, HARRY PATRICK, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1886; S.A. constabl., 1906-15; K.A.R., 1916-19; astt. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1919; supt., 1926; dep. commdt., pol. (C.I.D.), Palestine, 1932; dep. inspr.-gen., 1936.

RICE, J. A., B.A., LL.B., (T.C.D.)—B. 1904; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll., Ireland; admitted solr., sup. ct., I.F.S.; astt. commr., lands, dept. Gold Coast, 1927; ag. commr., lands, various periods, 1935-1939.
RICH, HON. SIR GEO. EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1932).—Senr. puisne just. of the high court of Australia, Apr., 1913.
RICHARDS, ARTHUR COURTNEY.—B. 1896; 2nd astt. gov’t. printer, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; astt. gov’t. printer, Apr., 1923; gov’t. printer, Apr., 1932.

RICHARDS, SIR ARTHUR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1933), B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1885; ed. Clifton Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; offr., cls. V., May, 1913; mag., cent. ct., Kota Bharu, and state audr., Aug., 1915; adviser, land office, Kedah, and ag. supt. of mines, June, 1916; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1918; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; 1st astt. col. sec., S. Stiltna., and ag. clk. of couns. in addn., Apr., 1921; gen. sec., S.S. retrenchment comte., in addn., Jan., 1922; on sp. duty, London, May, 1923; sec. opium comte., Br. Malaya in addn., Nov., 1923; chmn., exec. comte., Br. Malaya, Br. Empire Exhibn., in addn., 1924; offr., cls. IA., under-sec. to gov’t., F.M.S., Mar., 1927; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Nov., 1929; gov., N. Borneo, Mar., 1930; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Gambia, Nov., 1933; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Fiji, and high commr., W. Pacific, 1936; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1938.

RICHARDS, DAVID MORGAN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1892; ed. Christ Ch. Cathedral Schl., Oxford, St. David’s Coll. Schl., Lampeter, S. Wales and non-collegiate, Oxford; war. serv., 1917-19; mast., educn. dept., F.M.S., Apr., 1921; ditto, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ag. head mast., Central Br. Schl., 1922, 1932 and 1937; astt. mast., Queen’s Coll., 1934; headmast., Wantsai Schl., 1938; senr. mast., 1939.

RICHARDS, EDMUND CHARLES, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1889; agr. dept., E.A.P., 1909; secret., Nyasaland, 1912; astt. polit. offr., G.E.A., provln. admnstr., 1917; dist. polit. off. T.T., 1922; dep. prov. commr., 1928; prov. commr., 1931; dep. ch. sec., 1934; res. comanr., Basutoland, May, 1935.

RICHARDS, FRANCIS OTHIENEL.—B. 1882; vol. 6th cls. clk., postal dept., Br. Guiana, 1905; cler. astt., genl. reg. office, 1907; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1910; 5th cls., 1911; 4th cls., 1916; 3rd cls., 1924; 2nd cls., 1925; 1st cls., 1926; ch. clk., cust., 1927.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.

RICHARDS, GEORGE EDWARD F.G.L.—B. 1891; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Sch., Chicago Univ.; Downing Coll., Cambridge (B.A., L.I.B. Cantab.); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1922; ag. mag., Grenada, 1925-27; St. Lucia, 1927-8; mag., Dominica, 1928; cr. atty., 1931; ch. just. and mag., St. Lucia, Jan., 1936.

RICHARDS, HON. RICHARD STANLEY, M.P.—B. 1885; M.P. since 1918; chmn. of committees, 1924-27; commnr., crown lands, 1930-33; premier treas., commnr., crown lands, min. of mines, min. of marine, repat., and irrgn., Feb. to Apr., 1933; leader, H. of A. since 1938.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER STEWART, B.Sc. (Agr.)—B. 1897; ed. Burgh and County High Schl., Peebles; mily. serv., R. Arty., 1916-19; B.Sc., Edinburgh Univ., 1923; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agril. offr., 1930; dep. dir., agr., Uganda, April, 1937; ag. dir., agr., May, 1937 to Mar., 1938.

RICHARDSON, CECILY.—B. 1889; ed. The Laurels, Rugby and Royal Holloway Coll.; B.A., London, 1912; asst. mistress, govt. girls' schl., Penang, 1914; asst. mistress, Raffles girls' schl., Singapore, 1915; ag. prin., 1920 and 1924; prin., St. George's girls' schl., Penang, Sept., 1928; do., Raffles girls' schl., Apr., 1937.

RICHARDSON, LAWRENCE.—B. 1890; stock verifier, F.M.S. rly., Apr., 1920; stores acct., Oct., 1920; asst. acct., Jan., 1925; finan. offr., stores dept., July, 1928; asst. stores supt., Oct., 1935; ag. stores supt., Jan., 1937.
RICHARDSON, JOHN EDMUND, F.S.I., Chart. Survr.—B. 1904; V. and R.O., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

RICHARDSON, ROBERT ROOTH.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A.; 2nd cls. juris-prudence, 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1927; cadet, Tanganyika Territory adminstr., May, 1924; asst. sec., Dec., 1924; attd. as asst. prin., C.O., Feb. to Aug., 1927; ag. crown coun. in 1929 and 1932; ag. astt. admstr. gen. and registr., companies, 1929; ag. res. mag., 1934; ag. sec., dept., lands and mines, 1935 and 1936; sec., Tanganyika soil erosion and gold mining industry contes.; dist. offr., June, 1936.

RICHERS, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1885; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., London Scottish, Sept., 1917; apptd. to army audit staff, Eastern command, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 17th, July, 1922.

RICHMOND, CHARLES WAKEFIELD, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1880; served constructional engrs., 1888-1907; engng. dept., G.W.R., 1907-10; joined engng. dept., C.A., 1910; ast. engnr., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; dep. head, engng. contracts, 1936.

RICHMOND, ERNEST TATHAM, F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1874; ed. High Croft Schl., Clifton Coll., Bristol, Architectural Schl., Westminster and R. Acad. Architectural Schl.; archt. to pub. wks, miny., Egypt, 1900; subsequently dir. gen. state bldgs. dept.; attd., dept. of fortins and wks., W.O., Dec., 1914-Oct., 1916; in ch. grenade sec., trench warfare dept., miny. of munitions, Mar., 1917; lieut. R.N.V.R., Mar., 1917; attd. attd. to war graves serv., France, 1918-19; astt. civ. sec., Palestine, 1920; dir., antiquities, 1927.

RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., Ph.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario 1919; ch. of agril. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B. 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths., Normal Schl., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench dvn., 1906; judge, appellate dvn. (second divl. ct.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assoc. and of Bar Assoc. of several states; F.R. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univs.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll. (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary juls.

RIDEHALGH, ARTHUR, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Sedbergh and Wadham Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; Northern circuit, 1929-35; crown atty. and mag., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., 1935; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., June to Oct., 1938; ag. judge, W. Indian ct. of appl., Sept., 1938; cr. coun., Gold Coast, May, 1939.

RIDGWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; comsnr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comsn. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., ct. of dirs., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. J.—Ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; supt., pol., 1935.

RIGBY, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Bury Gram. Schl.; served in France, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1918 and June, 1918-Nov., 1919; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attchd., Chinese Prot., Penang, Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1923; astt. supt. govt. monops., S'pore and astt. contrlr., rubber, Mar., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; 3rd mag., S'pore, June, 1930; inspr., prisons, in addn., Sept., 1931; relief astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1932, offr., cls. III; astt. Malayan estabm. offr., Aug., 1933; ag. 1st astt. Malayan estab. offr., May, 1936; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1937; ag. dep. finan. sec., S.S., Mar., 1938.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1886; Scots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT. G.B., M.C.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920; dist. offr., 1923.

RINFRET, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B. 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1877), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-22; prof., munic. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.

RING, JULIUS, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Birm.), F.S.I., Chld. Survr., A.M.I.Struc.E.—B. 1892; ed. Worksop Coll., Notts. and Univ. of B'ham; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; asst. assessor, rates, 1928; 1st astt. do., 1933; assessor, rates, 1938.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1896; lieut., R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonica, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, CAPT. A. T. A., O.B.E. (1936), M.C., B.A. (Oxon)—Game warden, Kenya, 1923; ch. game warden, F.M.S., 1937; game warden, Kenya, 1938.

RITCHIE, HON. SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G., M.L.C.—B. 1864; el. fordist of Alexandra to H. of A., S. Australia, 1902; el. N. dist., leg. coun., 1924; commnr. pub. wks., 1914; has occupied some time or other every portfolio in govt.; chief chief sec., min. of health, and min. of mines; resgd. Aug., 1939; remains M.L.C.

RITCHIE, THOMAS MARSHALL.—B. 1895; war serv., 1914-19 (2nd lieut. flying offr., R.A.F., R. Scots Fusiliers); foreman of wks., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; inspr. of wks., 1928; mast., govt. tech. schl., 1930; prim., middle boarding schls., July, 1938.
ROBBINS, GEORGE JAMES.—B. 1893; ed. Latymer Upper Schl., Hammersmith; war serv., 1914-18; clk., vety. dept., Kenya, 1919; registrar of titles, 1926; prin. do., 1935.

ROBERTS, ALFRED ADRIAN, B.A. (Cape), B.A., LL.B. (Cantab).—B. 1890; called to bar (M.T.), 1915; advce. Pretoria, 1916-19 and 1929-35; regis., Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-29; chmn., govt. commn. on accountancy, 1934; author of "A Guide to Voet"; edr. of "Wessels on Contract"; law advr. to Union Govt., 1935.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR HALLAM, B.A. (Cantab), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1888; ed. Aldenham schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1914; cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1916; priv. sec. to gov., Mar., 1915; az. 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., Jan., 1916; passed cadet, June, 1916; priv. sec., Oct., 1916; 2nd grade dist. commr., Oct., 1916; ag. crown solr., July, 1917; assigned dist. of Savu Savu, Aug., 1917; act. serv., June, 1918; dist. commr., Navua, Dec., 1919; resigned, Oct., 1920; el. mem., leg. coun., 1923-26; registr. sup. ct., taxing master, curator, pub. trustee and off. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1931, 1932, 1935 and 1936; ch. pol. mag., Aug., 1931; mag. and crown coun., Zanzibar, Aug., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., 1937; ag. astt. judge, June, 1939.
ROBERTS, CLIFFORD ELLIS, M.A. B.M. (Oxon.), B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1899; med. offr., Uganda, 1927; senr. specialist offr., Zanzibar, 1939.

ROBERTS, GEORGE, I.S.O. (1939), F.I.H.E., J.P.—B. 1885; ent. Admy., 1902; seconded as exec. engr., Falkland Is., 1925; col. engr., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931; mem., exec. coun., 1933.

ROBERTS, GEORGE WILLIAM PEARSON, M.B.E. (Mil.), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.C.G.I., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl. and London Univ.; mil. serv. (France), 1915-19, capt.; joined C.A. as engrn. inspr., 1920; asst. engr., 1926; dep. to ch. inspr. engrn., 1935.

ROBERTS, JOHN EDWIN THEOPHILUS.—Ed. Mico Coll., Jamaica; head teacher, St. Mary's Schl., St. Kitts; head teacher, Road Town Wes. Schl., Virgin Is.; clk. to commr., Virgin Is., Mar., 1917; ag. educn. dist. offr., Oct., 1919-Feb., 1920; educn. dist. offr., Mar., 1920; clk. to mag., and bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1921; commr. to admtr. oaths, July, 1921.
ROBERTS, JOHN REGINALD, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1893; exec. engrn., Nigeria, 1921; water wks. engrn., 1927; senr. hydraulic engrn., Gold Coast, 1929; dep. D.P.W., 1937.

ROBERTS, JOHN RICHARD, A.M. Inst.T.—B. 1892; ed. Winchcombe County Schl. and Grammar Schl.; G.W. Rly., 1908-21; mily. serv., France, 1915-19; dist. stationmr., Tanganyika Territory rly., 1921; traffic inspr., 1925; asst. traffic supt., 1928; traffic supt., 1935; ag. traffic man., May, 1937 to Feb., 1938.
ROBERTS, M. A. W., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), F.R.C.S. (Ireland).—Med. offr., Kenya, July, 1929.

ROBERTS, PEGGY HAMER.—Prison offr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; served penal settlmt., Massaruni, and at Essequebo and Georgetown county prisons; clerical astt., Georgetown and Essequebo prisons; gaoler, G. Coast, Mar., 1911; keeper, James Fort prison, June, 1911.
ROBERTS, WALTER.—B. 1896; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; Europ. war, 1915-19; asst. commissary, Br. Guiana, 1928; asst. inspr. dista., 1927; asst. dist. commnr., 1935; inspr., lab., 1938.
ROBERTS, W. P.—B. 1893; Out Island commr., grade IV, Bahamas, 1923; grade III, 1926; grade II, 1927.
ROBERTS, CAPT. WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1897; asst. engrn., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engrn., June, 1929; do., P.W., Dec., 1933; do., S'pre, Oct., 1934; ag. senr. exec. engrn., Feb., 1936.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM RODDICK.—B. 1880; war serv., three years, nine months, R.N. and R.N.A.S.; gaol tech. instr., prisons dept., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921; mast., Accra tech. schl., Oct., 1923; seconded to prisons dept., Oct. to Nov., 1923; housemast., Apr., 1926.

ROBERTS-WRAY, KENNETH OWEN.—B.1899; R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich, 1917-18; commd., R. Artv., Jan., 1918; served in France, 1918; ret., 1920; Merton Coll., Oxford.(latcl. jurisprudence, 1922); M.A.; certif. of hon., bar final exam., 1923; called to bar, Jan., 1924; S Eastern circuit; professional legal clk., Miny. of Health, Oct., 1926; ag. asst. ch. clk., Jan., 1929; 2nd astt. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., 1st July, 1931; partauthor of "The Law of Collisions on Land."

ROBERTSON, ALGAR RONALD WARD.—B. 1902; ed. Den Close Schl., Cheltenham; astt. treas., Gold Coast, Feb., 1929; capt., G.C. Defence Force; dep. treas., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1936; ag. col. treas., May-Dec., 1936; capt., R.G. militia, 1937; acctnt. gen. and dep. treas., Fiji, July, 1939; ag. finan. sec. and col. treas., Aug., 1939.
ROBERTSON, COLIN BROWN.—B. 1893; ed. Leven higher grade; war serv., 1914-19; exec. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920.

ROBERTSON, IAN DONALD, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrnr.—B. 1886; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jly., 1914; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., irrigation br., Aug., 1916, Aug., 1919 and Nov., 1921; dir., wks., Kelantan, Jan., 1923; exec. engrnr., Jan., 1923; sr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1933; state engrnr., gd. II., June, 1938.
ROBERTSON, KENNETH STRUAN, B.Eng.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schol. and Univ. of Sheffield; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

ROBERTSON, Sir W. C. F., K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1885 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr., schls. G. Coast, 1898; dir., educn., 1899; ag. comptr., cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ag. sec., native affairs, June to Nov., 1905; prov. consmr., Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, May, 1907; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1908; chief asst. col. sec., Jan., 1909; ag. col. sec., in 1909, 1910, 1911, 1912, 1913; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April to May, 1913; ag. gov., May to Aug., 1914; dep. gov., Aug. to Sept., 1914; col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1915; lieut.-gov. and chief sec. to govt., Malta, May, 1917; offr. admstr. govt., Malta, for various periods, 1920-25; gov.'s dep., in Oct., 1920; lieut. gov. by coman. under new constitution, Apr., 1921; gov.'s dep., for various periods in 1920 and 1922; hon. LLD., Malta Univ., 1925; gov., Barbados, 1925; attended C.O. conferees, 1927 and 1930; ret., 1932.
ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, I.S.O. (1937).—B. 1875; ed. Palmerston Dist. High Schol., N.Z.; N.Z. dept. of lands and survey, 1892; inspr., 1919; asst. under-sec., 1925; under-sec., 1931.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM MACFARLANE, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1897; ed. Edin. Univ.; medallist (equal) and 1st cls. hons. intermediate forestry (including sylviculture) and geology; enlisted Scottish Horse, May, 1916; on active serv., W. Yorks. Regt., 1917-18 and with the army of occupation Germany, until Feb., 1919; asst. conserv., forests, Nigeria, 1922; conserv., forests, 1926; do., Sierra Leone, 1936.

ROBINS, REGINALD EDWIN, C.M.G. (1938), O.B.E. (1932), A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1891; ed. Queen Mary's Schol., Basingstoke; ent. G.W.R. serv., 1908; sp. training under G.W.R. training scheme for offrs., 1920; London Schol. of Economics, 1919-24; Brunel medallist; Kenya and Uganda rly., 1925; gen. man., Tanganyika rly., and ports services, Apr., 1936.

ROBINSON, CAPT. ARTHUR GORDON, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. Asaph gram. schl., N. Wales and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1912; seconded, Kedah, July, 1912; R.E.F., France, Mar., 1917-Jan., 1919; capt. commanding Indian labr., lieut., R.E.; exec. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1921; sen. exec. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., July, 1928; ch. hydraulic engrnr., 1930; asst. advr., drainage and irrign., Malay States, Jan., 1932; dir., drainage and irrign., S.S. and advsr., do., F.M.S., Nov., 1935.

ROBINSON, AUBREY CHARLES.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; commr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compd. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (public, deferred during the war); regisr.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in 1918 and 1919; regisr.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. regisr., supt. ct. and marshal, June to Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, Sept., 1923; regisr., sub. ct. and regisr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1928; 2nd puisne judge, Aug., 1931.

ROBINSON, GEORGE GILMOUR.—B. 1894; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxon., B.A., M.A.; war serv., 1914-19 (France); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1924; practised, 1924-30; res. mag., Kenya, 1930; puisne judge, N. Rhodesia, 1938; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Oct., 1939.
ROBINSON, I. L., B.A.—1904; 2nd cls. hons. classics; admstve. offr. cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1929; dist. offr., 1939.

ROBINSON, KENNETH ERNEST.—B. 1914; ed. Monoux Schol., Walthamstow and Oxford; open schol., Hertford Coll., 1931-36 and state schol., 1931-35; hons. schl., philosophy, politics and economics, cls. I., 1934; B.A., 1934; hons., schl., mod. hist., cls. I., 1936; awarded Beit sen. research schol. in col. history, 1936 but resigned on appl. to C.O., 1936; appt. after compet. exam. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1936.

ROBINSON, LIEUT-COL. SIR THOMAS BULS, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comttee, for setttn. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

ROBISON, LIONEL MCDOWALL, B.A. (Manchester).—B. 1886; last., govt. training coll., Colombo, Ceylon, 1909; vice-prin., June, 1912; inspr., schls., Oct., 1915; army serv., July, 1918; asst. dir., educn., July, 1924; dep. dir., July, 1929; sec. to min. for educn., Feb., 1932; dir., educn., Jan., 1936.

ROCK, ALFRED MILTON.—B. 1888; ag. 3rd outdoor offr. and harbour, treasy., Dominica, 1913-15; clk. and interpreter to mag., dist. "E.", Mar., 1915; clk., mag., regist. and provost marshal "A", commr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Montserrat, Sept., 1915; clk. to mag., dist. "A" and "B", dep. coroner, Antigua, Nov., 1920; senr. clk., regist.'s office, clk., sup. ct. law library, "A" commr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Dominica, May, 1927; prin. for duty as regist. and provost marshal, regist. of titles, admr. of estates and regist.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Apr., 1929.

RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementary clk., col. audit dept., Apr., 1914; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 1919; regisr., 1st Apr., 1928.

RODGER, ROBERT, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Mech.E., A.M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., M.I.Struc.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1893; ed. Wishaw High Schol., Wishaw Tech. Schol. and Glasgow Tech. Coll. (affldtd.); on active serv. with Lanarkshire Yeo., (Lowd. Mtd. Bde., Scot., T.F.), 1914-17; Sappers and Miners, Ind. Army, 1917-20; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1920; road engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, on "Tarmet" scheme, 1924; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, on road development schemes, 1926; road engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, in ch., reconstruction scheme, 1930; ag. asst., D.F.W., various occasions, 1931-36; ag. D.P.W. in 1936; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1937; ast. D.P.W., 1938.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon siv. serv., Dec., 1912; office ast. to govt. agt., W. Prov., May, 1915; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1917; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ag. dist., judge, Matara, May, 1922; ast. comsrr., excise, Sept., 1924; dep. comsrr., excise, Sept., 1927; contr., finance and supplies, July, 1931; ast. govt. agt., Puttalam, July, 1932; govt. ast., N.W.P., Oct., 1935; ag. dir., agr., Mar., 1937; off., cls. I, gd. I, July, 1938.

RODWAY, RUPERT, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge. (math. tripes, pt. I, econ. tripes, pt. II); col. audit dept., Nov., 1931; ast. audr., Sir Seine Leone, Dec., 1931; do., Nigeria, May, 1934; do., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1936; ag. audr., various periods, 1936-39.

RODWELL, SIR CECIL HUNTER, G.C.M.G., (1934), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high comsrr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov., Fiji, and high comsrr., W. Pacific, 1918; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., B. Guiana, 1925; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Southern Rhodesia, Sept., 1928; ret., Dec., 1934.

RODWELL, REGINALD MORTON HASSEL, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Oxford Univ. (Worcester Coll.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; practised at English bar; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Sept., 1938.

KOE, ROBERT JESTIN, M.R.C.V.S. (Williams Memorial Prize), D.V.S.M.—B. 1897; ed. Drogheda and R. Vety. Coll., Ireland; war serv., 1915-16; vety. surg., Cyprus, June, 1921; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Jan., 1923; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Conf., 1927; ag. dir., animal husbandry, July-Oct., 1928; title changed to ch. vety. offr., Jan., 1930; rep. govt. Internat. Dairy Cong., Copenhagen, 1931.
KOE, W.T., B.A., (T.C.D.)—B. 1901; admitted solr., I.F.S. and N. Ireland; ast. commr., lands, Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

ROGERS, HOWARD, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1891; ed. Rhodes Univ. Coll. and S.A. Coll.; apptd. pub. serv., 1911; admitted advoc., sup. ct., 1921; war serv. German W. and E. Africa, 1914-17; nat. commr. Pretoria, 1932; ch. nat. commr. (northern areas), Apr., 1936; contr., native settlments, Aug., 1936; author of "Native Administration in the Union of S. Africa" (1933).

ROGERS, FRANCIS MERVYN.—B. 1890; student, R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1915; mily. serv., 1916-19; sub-foreman, Kew, 1919-20; head gardener, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; supt., plantations, E. African agril. research stn., Amani, 1927.

ROGERS, HON. NORMAN MCLEOD.—B. 1894; ed. Amherst pub. and high schls. and Acadia Univ.; served Great War; B.A., Acadia Univ., 1919; el. to Rhodes scholarship at Oxford; B.A. (hist., polit. sci. and law); prof., hist., Acadia Univ.; admitted to Nova Scotia bar; sec., Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King in his capacity as pres., P.C., 1927; prof., polit. sci., Queen's Univ., 1929; coun. for govt. of Nova Scotia before R. commn. of econ. enquiry, 1934; el. M.P., Canada, g.e., 1935; min. of lab., Mackenzie King cabinet, 1935.

ROGERS, PHILIP.—B. 1914; ed. Manchester Hulme Grammar Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; senr. schol. of Emmanuel, 1935-36; mod. languages tripes pt. I (German cls. II, divn. I, French cls. II, divn. II), hist. tripes, pt. II, (cls. I), economics tripes, pt. II (cls. I, divn. II); apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., Oct., 1936; prte. sec. to perm. under-sec., C.O., Dec., 1939.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon siv. serv., Dec., 1912; adtl. office ast., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; office ast. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office ast. to govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Avisawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., N.W.P., Feb., 1930; ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1931; addnl. govt. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1933; govt. agt., S.P., Oct., 1934; chmn., munic. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1935; govt. agt., N.W.P., Mar., 1936; do., S.P., Nov., 1937.

ROLSTON, CREOL MICHELLE.—M.D., C.M., Univ. of Manitoba, Canada, 1895; L.R.C.P., Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin.; L.R.F.P., and S., Glasgow, 1898; mem. Coll. of Phys. and Surgeons, 1903; F.R.I.; pub. health, London; late civil surg., attached to R.A.M.C. during S.African war, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal, 2 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); apptd. to Leeward Is. med. serv., 1903; med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, and ag. med. offr. of pub. instttns., 1903; dist. med. offr. of health, Vancouver, B. Columbia, 1908; med. offr., dist. 8, Nevis, 1914; ag. mag., Nevis, May to June, 1915; med. offr., dist. 6, Nevis, Sept., 1915.

ROOKE, CHARLES EUSTACE, M.Inst.T., F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Dover Coll.; ast. traffic man., Uganda Rly., Oct., 1914; served with Indian Army Expedy. Force, 1915-17; 2nd lieutenant, Indian Army R. of O.; ast. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., rly., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 1935; ch. traffic supt., Nigerian rlys., 1937; ag. gen. man., Apr.-Oct., 1938.

ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and City and Guilds Engng. Coll., S. Kensington; held various engnr. appts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1908; ast. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag. dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1922.
ROPER, DEVYS, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1895; ed. Keighley Gram. Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; European mast., S.S., Oct., 1924; headmast., King George V. Schl., Seremban, Dec., 1935.

ROPER, MAJOR WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1891; entd. C. A. office after open compet. exam., Oct., 1907; seconded to Falkland Is. Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to govt. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; mil. serv. 1915-19; 1st dist. clk., Nyasaland, Jan., 1920; ag. chf. clk., July to Dec., 1921; edr. Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec. 1922; ag. 2nd ast. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns. in 1922, 1925 and 1926; organising sec., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Sept., 1926; ag. chf. transit. offr., Dec. 1926 to May, 1927; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1927; sec., govt. tender bd., Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. staff offr., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Apr. to Nov., 1929; ag. senr. ast., sec., in 1929, 1930 and 1931; ast. couns., 1930; ast. sec., Apr., 1931; ag. ast. chf. sec., various periods, 1935-39.
ROPNER, GEOFFREY VIVIAN.—B. 1898; mily. serv., 1917-23; ast. count., pol., Aden, 1930; dep. comsrr., pol., 1937.

ROSE, ALAN EDWARD PERCIVAL, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; ed. Aldenham Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; temp. 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., B.E.F., 1918-19; 2nd cls. hons., law tripos, Cambridge, 1921; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, Jan., 1923; mem., S.E. circuit; ch. pol. mag., Fiji, July, 1929; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1930 to June, 1931; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1931; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1931-36; ag. atty.-gen. in 1934 and 1935; solr.-gen., Palestine, Oct., 1935; ag. atty.-gen., July-Oct., 1938, and Feb.-May, 1939; Br. puisne judge, Aug., 1939.

ROSE, FREDERICK GARDINER, M.B.E., B.A., 1907, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond., 1911, M.R. B. Chir., U. Camb., D.M.R. & E. Camb., M.D., Camb., M.R.C.P., Lond.—B. 1885; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; med. sch., B. Guiana, 1915; govt. bac. and path., 1915; ag. G.M.O.H. and port health offr., 1917; ag. G.M.O.H. and port health offr., 1919; govt. radiologist, 1921-22; hon. con. radiologist, 1923; ag. port health offr., 1924; dep. port health offr., 1925; med. supt., lep. hosp., 1926.

ROSE, Lieut.-Col. HENRY BARRON, M.C.—B. 1891; 2nd Lieut., Wiltshire Regt., 1911; lieut., 1914; capt., 1915; war serv., France, Belgium and Russia (1914 Star, B.W.M., V.M., M.C.); major, 1933; lieut.-col., 1938; commdt., H.K.V.D.C.

ROSE, HON. HUGH EDWARD.—B. 1869; ed. at Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto (B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Faaken, Cowan, Chadwick and Rose; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, Dec., 1916; ch. just., high ct., Ont., 1930; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.

ROSEDALE, JOHN LEWIS, Ph.D. (Bonn), D.Sc. (Aberdeen), F.I.C.—B. 1889; prof. of biochemistry, King Edward VII Medical Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927; offl. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Asso. of Trop. Med., Bangkok, Dec., 1930; offl. deleg. to 9th cong., F.E.A.T.M., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

ROSEDALE, WILLIAM ORKEL PRILLY.—B. 1891; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Aug., 1914; dist. offr., 1924; nst. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1927; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1930-31; admstv. offr., class I., Oct., 1930; staff grade, 1937.

ROSENOW, JACOBUS DANIEL MALAN, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1884; ed. Boys' Public Sch., Riversdale and Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ. serv., audit office, Capetown, 1903; atty. gen.'s office, Capetown, 1907, various mag. offices for 12 years; clk., office of dir., prisons, 1922; jun. prof. asst. atty. gen., Natal, 1923; registr., high ct., S.W. Africa, 1926; sen. prof. asst. atty. gen., Natal, 1927; solr. gen., Grahamstown, Mar., 1933.

ROSENOW, RODERICH EDUARD GERHARD.—B. 1883; ed. Somerset West Pub. Sch., Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Univ. of Cape Town; joined bar, Cape Town, Jan., 1923; transf., Windhoek bar, Jan., 1925; professional ass't, atty.-gen., Windhoek, Apr., 1932; atty.-gen., S. W. Africa, Mar., 1933.

ROSS, ARTHUR FREDRICK PALMER.—B. 1899; junr. draughtsmen, P.W.D., Jamaica, 1917-20; asst. draughtsman, 1925; supt., hydrographical survey, Rio Minho irrig. scheme, Jamaica, 1928; asst. engr., 1930; supt., pub. wks., Ist cls., 1937; asst. dir., pub. wks., Somaliland, 1938.

ROSS, CHARLES CLAYE, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1901; ed. Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1927; pte. practice, 1927-34; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug., 1934-Feb., 1935; admstv. govt., Aug., 1935; atty.-gen., Grenada, Nov., 1935; K.C.
