SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; registr. and marshal and regist.-gen. and prim. registr., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925 and 1926; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1928; ag. ch. just. in 1932, 1934 and 1936; puisne judge, Gold Coast, June, 1937; judge, high ct., Jamaica, 1939.

SAWARD, Capt. Sidney Carmon, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; comsmd., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in despa., Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1925-32; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1929; ag. survr.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934, and Mar.-Aug., 1936; dir. of surveys, 1938.

SAXTON, Sidney Waring.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1905; paymr.-lieut. comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter, lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, and ag. trav. comsnr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; promoted pay comdr., ret. list, 1927; d.c.p. prov. comsnr., 1930; prov. comsnr., 1938.

SAYERS, Eldred Frank.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, Gerald Fleming.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; asst. sec., Tanganyika territory, July, 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923; attd. to C.O., Aug., 1924 to Sept., 1925 and from July to Sept., 1930; clk., leg. coun., 1926-32; ag. asst. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1927-1931; asst. ch. sec., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Nyaaland Prot., 1933; ag. ch. sec., Feb., 1934; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1935; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. sec., in 1935 and 1937; adminstr. sec., 1937; edtr., Handbook of Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

SCAMMELL, Ernest Henry.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; asst. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. comsnr., Canada, 1915; asst. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-establishment, Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; sec., pensions divn., dept. of pensions and national health, 1931; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHARFF, John William, B.A., M.D., B.A.O., B.Ch., D.P.H. (T.C.D.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1895; surgm. sub.-lieut., R.N.V.R., July, 1915; health offr., S.S., Nov., 1920; lect. in biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine in addn., May, 1921; hon. sec. for Malaya of the Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Aug., 1921; health offr., Malacca, June, 1929; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1928; offr. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Bangkok, Dec., 1930; superscale offr., grade A, Jan., 1935; ch. health offr., Spore, July, 1935.
SCHEMBRI, Judge Salvatore, LL.D.—B. 1881; matric., Malta Univ., 1899; LL.D., 1907; ag. judge, Malta, 1936; judge, 1937.

SCHMIDT, Carl Friedrich, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; asst. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examnr., May, 1898; ch. examnr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec., educ. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, Sept., 1926; contr. and audr.-gen., 1929; rector, Pretoria univ., 1935.

SCHMITT, Louis John, A.I.C.A., F.C.A.A.—B. 1885; ed. Victoria Parade coll., Australia; acct., for some years; sec., N.Z. bd. of trade prior investig. tribunal, 1919; investig. acct., bd. of trade, 1921; sec. to advisey. coun., N.Z. sect., Wembley exhibn., 1924; dep. gov't. comsnr., Dunedin internat. exhibn., 1925; advy. acct., industries and commerce dept., 1927; mem. (rep. employers), N.Z. ct. of arbitn., 1928; N.Z. govt. tourist and trade comsnr. in Australia, 1930-35; sec. and gen.-man. N.Z. dept. of industries and commerce, tourist and publicity, 1935.

SCHNEIDER, George Albert Wilhelm.—B. 1884; ed. Pretoria High Schl. and S.African Coll., Cape Town; solr., sup. ct., Transvaal; civ. serv., Transvaal, 1907; ch. clk., dept. of lands, 1931; under-sec. for agr., 1935.

SCHOCHE, August Arnold.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional asst. to attr.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dist., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, dept. of just., Pretoria, Sept., 1925; senr. law adviser, Oct., 1931.
SCHOKMAN, Clive Conrade.—B. 1897; 4th sqn., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1924; 1st do., Apr., 1928; inspr.-gen., prisons, Mar., 1932.

SCHOLEFIELD, Guy Hardy, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly liby. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, William.—Dep. clk., res. mag.s., etc., Jamaica, Mar., 1913; clk., res. mag.s., etc., Feb., 1917; asst. comsnr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, July, 1919; ag. comsnr., ditto, Sept. to Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCHOLES, Alwyn Denton, M.A. (Hons. Cantab.).—B. 1910; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1934; practised at English bar, Central Crim. Ct., Midland circuit and Birmingham, Lincoln, Leicester, Northampton, Warwick, Nottingham, Bedford and Derby City and County sessions; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Apr., 1938.

SCHREINER, OLIVER DENEYS.—B. 1890; ed. Rondebosch H. Schl., S.A. Coll. Schl., S.A. Coll. (Cape Town), Trin. Coll., Camb. (fellow 1916); barrister, Inner Temple; M.C., 1916; advoc., Johannesburg, 1920-37; K.C., 1935; judge, supr. ct., S.A., T.P.D., 1937.

SCHUMANN, THOROD E. WERNER, B.A., M.Sc., Ph.D.—B. 1896; ed. Stellenbosch, S.A.; Gottingen, Germany and Yale, U.S.A.; lect. in physics and maths., Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1918-19; and at Stellenbosch, 1920-21; dir., fuel research inst. of S. Africa, June, 1931, resgd., Feb., 1933; ch. meteorologist, Union of S.A., Sept., 1933.

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., M.B.E. (1935), Hon. M.A. (Oxon.) (1938). L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regiar., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of gov't. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; librn., Malta public library, July, 1923; ag. dir. of museum, 1938.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELEANOR.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, MAJOR CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1893; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edinburgh); European mast., S.S., Apr., 1923; headmast., Rangoon Rd., schl., S'pore, Aug., 1932; inspr., schls., N. Semban, June, 1934; headmast., King George V schl., Seremban, Feb., 1935; ag. headmast., English Coll., J. Bahru, Oct., 1936; ag. supt., educn., Johore, Apr., 1937.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M. & H. (Camb.); Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls., London and Copenhagen; prosecutor, 1894; asst. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; asst. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willeiden Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII, mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge hosp., Aldershot, 1916; govt. pathologist dir., serum inst. Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect. trop. disease, Westminster hosp., med. schl., 1926; examnr., D.M.T. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research countee, 1928.

SCOTT, Sir JOHN, K.B.E. (1932), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; admstd. govt. for various periods, 1924-29; col. sec., S.S., 1929; admstd. govt., various occasions, 1929-31; ret., Aug., 1933.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, R. R., C.M.G. (1939), M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924; dep. dir., sanv. serv., 1932; D.M.S., 1935.

SCOTT, WALTER RICHARDSON.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; asst. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1925; attd. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu, 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C., to gov., 1929; ag. dir., crim. intell., 1932-36; supt., pol., 1933; divnl. supt., pol., 1936.

SCRIMGEOUR, COLIN GRAHAM.—B. 1903; ed. Gisborne pol. schl., N.Z.; home-missioner, Methodist Ch., 1923; city missioner, Auckland, 1926-32; dir., Friendly Road broadcasting statn., 1932-36; contrlr., N.Z. comm. broadcasting serv., 1936.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERICK.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; comand. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; asst. ch. engnr., Palestine rlys., 1920; engnr., ways and wks., Palestine rlys. 1924.

SCRIVENER, T. V.—B. 1908; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1930; apptd. temp. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ass't. dist. offr., Palestine, 1937.

SCUPHAM, MAJOR WILLIAM ERIC HALSTEAD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; army, 1914-19; dipl., anthropology, Cambridge, 1926; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1931; dep. prov. comar., Jan., 1934; prov. comar., Oct., 1936; senr. prov. comar., Dec., 1937; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1936 and 1937; adminis. sec., 1939.

SCULLIN, RT. HON. JAMES HENRY, P.C.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1929-31.

SCULLY, HUGH DAY.—B. 1883; ed. pvte. and pub. schls. and Parkdale Collegiate Inst., Toronto, Univ., of Toronto; 1st cls. hons. and gold med. in polit. sc. and Alexander MacKenzie fellowship, 1906; commr., excise, Canada, 1932; commr., cust., 1933.

SEAGER, BASIL WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1898; ed. abroad; mil'y. serv., 1916-22; Br. pro-consul, Jedda, Saudi Arabia, (Br. agency and consulate, later Br. Legn.), 1926-33; lent to Aden govt. as temp. polit. offr., during Anglo-Yemeni treaty negotiations, 1933-34; reverted to Jedda as Br. pro-consul, Mar.-Aug., 1934; polit. offr., Aden, Sept., 1934; ag. polit. sec. for various periods in 1935-39.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDPINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. comsnr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. ast. to res. comsnr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. nat. advocate, Oct., 1915, to Dec., 1922; ag. ast. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. ast. to res. comsnr., on various occasions, 1923-39; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt. dist. dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. div.), Apr., 1925.
SEARLE, Lancelot Alhan.—B. 1915; pol. probnr., Hong Kong, 1936; ast. supt., 1938.

SEDGWICK, P. C. M.—B. 1911; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate, Brasenose Coll., Oxford and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Sept., 1934; passed Cantonese, 1937; 3rd ast. col. sec., Aug., 1937; admstve. ast., urban coun., Sept., 1937.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripods, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; appd. under reconstruction scheme ast. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; ast. prin., C.O., 1922; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929; prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930; offl. sec. to high comsnr. for U.K. in Australia, Sept., 1938.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; ed. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wonesh, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; ast. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ast. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. ast. audr., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also pvt. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933; senr. ast. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936; ag. dep. audr., Nov., 1936.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macclesfield Grammar Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv., Cheshire Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; appd. to air minv., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as ast. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; pvt. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the cols. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June 1929; prin., 17th Sept., 1929; sec., Rhodesia-Nyasaland R. Coms., 1938-39; ag. ast. sec., C.O., 3rd Nov., 1939.

SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1881; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Trinid. Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; ast. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man.-sec., agric. bank, Jan., 1928; sec., cocoa industry relief comtee., 1930; sec., agric. industry relief comtee., 1933; sec., cocoa subsidy bd., 1936.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, A.C.G.I., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1886; ast. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. ast. to ast. D.P.W., Jan., 1925; exec. engnr., July, 1930; divnl. engnr., Jan., 1936.

SEBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1839; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comtee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high comsnr. for S. Africa, and gov. and commdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELBY, JOHN ALFRED ROBERTSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1900; health offr., (social hygiene), med. dep., Hong Kong, 1930.

SELKE, E. A.—B. 1890; ed. St. Peters, York, St. John's Coll., Johannesburg, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; called to bar, 1914; Transvaal and Natal bars, 1917; K.C. (S.A.), 1929; just., sup. ct. of S.A., Natal Prov. divn., 1938.

SELWYN-CLARKE, PERCY SELWYN, M.C., M.D. B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Eng.) with distine., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—B.1893; ed.St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health, W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. sany-offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., sany. serv., May to Aug., 1927; del. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever confce., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; ast. dir., sany. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Médaille d'Argent des Épidémies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930; ast. dir., med. serv., Gold Coast, Jan., 1932; dep.-dir., health serv., 1933; ag. dir., med. serv. in 1934 and 1936; G.C. del. to Pan-African Health Confce., Johannesburg, 1935; dep. dir., health serv., Nigeria, 1936; dir., med. services, Hong Kong, 1937; M.L.C., 1938; H. K. del. to 10th congress of the Far East Assoc. of Trop. Med., Hanoi, 1938; mem., exec. cl., 1939.

SIMPLE, JOHN MERVYN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O.T.C., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934; ast. D.M.S., do., 1938.
SIMPLE, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Sofala Schl., N.S.W.; mem. N.Z. H. of R., 1918-19 and since 1928; min. of pub. wks. and min. of transp., 1935.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd., Matare kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927; ast. attnt. offr., Apr., 1929; dep. tea contrlr., Mar., 1938.

SENNETT, CEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S.S., 1914; ast. regist., impta. and expts., 1917; ast. supt., govt. monopolies 1920; ast. dist. offr., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; ast. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Nov., 1923; dep. regist., sup. ct. and ast. off. assignee, Penang, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1926 (offr., cls. III.); 2nd mag., ast. dist. judge and coroner, Penang, Nov., 1926; collr., land rev., Penang, Apr., 1927 and Mar., 1930; off., assignee and ass't. regisr., coys., Penang, July, 1929; off., cls. II., Nov., 1931; dist. offr., L. Perak Apr., 1933; 1st mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1933; legal advr., Trengganu, Dec., 1936; oomsnr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1938; dist. offr., Prov., Wellesley, Apr., 1939.

SERTSIOS, B. D., O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; ass't. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to ass't. atty-gen., May, 1925; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; ag. oh. just., July-Sept., 1934.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT, M.C. (1918).—B. 1888; ed., Framlingham Coll., Lealeham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; wounded, 1918; twice ment. in desp.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926; Order of Istighal, 2nd cls.; pres., dist. ct., Haifa, 1931; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1935; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1935; publication "Legislation of Trans-Jordan, 1918-1930."

SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed., Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, ROTHESAY EGERTON, M.C.—B. 1892; ed., Diocesean Coll., Rondebosch, S.A.; pte., 1st Rhodesia Regt.; tpr., Belfield's Scouts; lieut., intell. dept., 1914-18; ass't. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; admin. offr., Mar., 1921; dist. offr., Mar., 1930.

SEYMON-HALL, CHARLES F.R.S.A., F.R.E.S., M.R.A.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1918; capt., H.M.A.Z., 31, 1918; demob., 1919; ass't. dist. oomsnr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd ass't. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. ass't. admstr.-gen., 1926-27; ag. ass't. sec., priv. sec. to Br. res., clk. of couns. and edr., offl. gaz., 1928; ag. prov. oomsnr. (with seat on leg. coun.), 1929, 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1934; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; assisted in revision of Madan's Swahili Dictionary, 1931.

SHACKLES, DERK HOLMES.—B. 1900; ed., R.N.C., Osborne and Greshams Schl., Holt; solr.; ass't. oomsnr., lands, Gold Coast, 1929; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. regisr., 1933; regisr., high ct., Uganda, 1934.
SHAFTAIN, FRANK WALTER.—B. 1891; joined Hong Kong pol., 1912; sub. inspr., H.K.; inspr., 1929; ch. inspr., 1935; col. pol. long serv. med., 1937; ass't. supt., pol., 1938.

SHAND, KEITH RAE.—B. 1881; ed., S. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, S. Africa; Assoc. M.Inst.C.E., 1913; irreg. dept., Union S. Africa, Apr., 1917; circle engnr., Dec., 1924; ch. constr. engnr., July, 1933; ass't. dir., Dec., 1937.

SHAPCOIT, LOUIS EDWARD, C.M.G. (1935). M.V.O. (1920), I.S.O. (1927), Officier d'Academie Francaise, 1921.—B. 1877; ed., pub. schl., Kingston and Allendale, Victoria; under sec., premier's dept., W. Australia, since 1914; clk., exec. oom.

SHARP, COMM. GEORGE ARTHUR COXALL, D.S.C., R.N. (ret.)—B. 1891; ed., R. Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; war serv., 1914-18; ret., 1920; marine offr., Tanganyika, 1927; marine supt., Takoradi, Gold Coast, 1935.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed., Univ. Coll. Schl. and King's Coll., London; compt'r. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rtna Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contgt., 1914-18; ch. acct., P. and T., Nigeria, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G. in 1928-30; ret., Oct., 1930; reapt'd. col. postmtr., Barbados, July, 1931 and in 1934; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1939.

SHARP, WILLIAM JOSEPH CURRAN.—B. 1888; ed. Gram. Schl. Maldon and Regent St. Polytechnic; war serv., 1915-19, R.E. Signal Sect.; U.K.O.P.O., 1904-19; supt., P.O., S.S., May, 1921; supt., teleg., Penang, Apr., 1924; acct., Penang, Nov., 1930; supt., Malacca, Apr., 1932; do., Selangor and Pahang, Feb., 1934; do., Malacca, Sept., 1934.
SHARPE, H. B.—Ass't dist. oomsnr., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1919; dist. oomsnr., 1932.

SHAW, FREDERIC LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; ag. headmast., English Coll., Johore Bahru, Feb., 1921; headmast., govt. English Schls., Muar, June, 1922; 1st ass't. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Nov., 1925; ass't. mast., Raffles Inst., Jan., 1926; inspr., schls., Selangor, Apr., 1926; ass't. inspr., schls., Singapore and Labuan, Mar., 1927 and Dec., 1928; headmast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1929; supt., educn., Johore, 1936.

SHAW, GEORGE INGRAM, M.M., M.R., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; M.O.H. Malaya, 1928; M.O., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1934; M.O. in ch., Vict. hosp., Jan., 1935; do., Vict. gaol, inf. dis. hosp., officers' families (H.K.), remand home and pol. urg., H.K., 1935; M.O. H.K. prison, 1938.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed., Clifton Schl., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schl., Kingstown; ass't. dist. oomsnr., Sierra Leone, May, 1921; dist. oomsnr., May, 1928.

SHAW, JOHN VALENTINE WISTAR.—B. 1894; ed., Repton Schl.; service with R.E., 1914-19; ass't. dist. oomsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. oomsnr., 1925; ass't. col. sec., 1928; seconded for duty at C.O., Mar.-Sept., 1930; ass't. sec., grade F. Palestine, 1935; senr. ass't. sec., 1938; dep. ch. sec., 1939.
SHAW, ROBERT PHILLIP, A.R.I.B.A. (Chartered Architect).—B. 1888; ed., Bathgate Acad. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edinburgh; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; exec. engnr., 1937.
SHEARER, HAROLD MAXWELL LEFROY, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1915; ed., Edinburgh Acad. and Edinburgh Univ.; ass't. dist. oomsnr. (cadet), Basutoland, 1937; ass't. dist. oomsnr., 1939.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed., Christian Schls., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Sept., 1917; demob., May, 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; sec., rubber restrn., Oct., 1922; supernoy. offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contrlr., rubber expts., Johore, in addn., Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1927; dist. offr., Temerloh, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1932; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Nov., 1933; offr., cls. II, Mar., 1937; pub. trust. and offl. admstr., F.M.S., Nov., 1937; contr., rubber, Malaya, July, 1938; ass't. adver., Muai, Mar., 1939.

SHERIDAN, Sir Joseph, Kt. Bach. (1932), LL.D. (Dublin).—B. 1882; ed. Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Ireland, 1907; went Connaught circuit; judicial clk. (title subsequently changed to ass't to att'y-gen.), Nyasaland Prot., 1908-13; ag. att'y-gen. on different occasions, 1909-13; ag. judge, high ct., Dec., 1909 to Jan., 1910; res. mag., Kenya, 1913-20; ag. puisne judge, Kenya, and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1919-20; puisne judge, Kenya and mem. ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1920; ag. ch. just., Kenya, 1928-29; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ch. just., Kenya, 1934.

SHERINGHAM, Anthony Ilex.—B. 1890; ed. Brighton Coll.; asst. supt., Ceylon pol., 1909-19; on active serv., France 1917-18 (lieut., 18th Hussars); supt., pol., 1919; comsnnr., pol. and comsbr., prisons, Zanzibar, Apr., 1931; King's Police Medal, 1934; Order of St. John of Jerusalem, 1935.

SHERLOCK, David Thomas Joseph, K.C. (1922), B.A.T.C.D., M.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; judl. comnrr. and offl. mem., leg. coun., N. Borneo, Dec., 1920; ch. just., Feb., 1926; ret., Aug., 1935; judge, ct. of appl., Jamaica, 1935.
SHERRARD, Peter James Corry, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1936; office asst., July, 1938.

SHERWELL, Arnold Guy.—B. 1891; ed. St. Edward's Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A. (schl. of jurisprudence, hons. cls. III); called to bar (Inner Temple), 1914; ass't res., Nyasaland, 1914; mily. serv. in Africa, ment. in desps., 1915; practised at bar, 1920-25; dist. judge, Sudan, 1925; ass't advoc. gen. and legal advr. to Sudan govt., rly. and steamers, 1928; gov't advoc., Palestine, Apr., 1929; relieving pres. of dist. cts., 1930; pres., dist. ct., 1935.
SHIELD, Charles Geoffrey.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov', and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.

SHIELS, Dr. T. Drummond, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch. B.); served European War as offr. R. Scots.; in command with rank as capt., one of trench mortar batteries of 9th (Scottish) Divn. (severely wounded); was a mem., Edinburgh town coun.; M.P., East Edinburgh since 1924; mem., sp. comsnr., Ceylon constitution, 1927; partly, under sec., India Office, June, 1924; partly, under-sec. for cols., 1929-31.

SHILL, A. T.—B. 1895; on active serv. with Rifle Brig. and Dorset. Regt., 1915-17; wounded; Imp. cust. and excise dept., 1919; also dep. supt., mercantile marine, recr. of wreck, regist., shipping, lascar transfer offr., etc. asst. recr. gen., Gambia, Aug., 1928; ag. ass't col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1929 and from July, 1931; contr., food and fuel, May, 1931; supervisor, cust. and excise dept., Mauritius, Apr., 1932.

SHIPWAY, Walter Robert, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911; registr. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 14th Nov., 1928.

SHIRLEY, Evelyn Philip Skewallis, O.B.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll., Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1923; 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade comsnr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1932.

SHORLAND, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1902; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S.; 1924; offr., cl. V, Dec., 1927; offr., cl. IV, Dec., 1931; offr., cl. III, Dec., 1936; dist. offr., Kuantan, July, 1936; dep. contr., labour, Kedah, Apr., 1938.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; astt. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. astt. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I.O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judi. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; astt. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. astt. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; astt. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924; dep. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

SHUTE, GEOFFREY GAY, C.M.G. (1939), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Schl., Eastbourne, Uppingham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; cls. I, admstve. serv., 1930; prin. astt. sec., S. Provs., 1931; sec., S. Provs., 1933; staff grade, 1936; ag. ch. cmanr., S.P., 1937-39; ch. cmanr., E.P., 1939.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BINDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripes, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1916; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as astt. prim. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comte., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comte., 1927; pvte. sec. to partly. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 15th Sept., 1928; pvte. sec., perm. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929, prin., 24th Jan., 1930; accomp. perm. under S. of S. for the Cols. (Sir J. Maffey) to W. Indies, 1936.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., June to Oct., 1925.

SIM, DAVID.—B. 1899; ed. at Maghill Pub. Schl., Glasgow; came to Canada, 1911; pvte. sec., to Hon. W. D. Euler, 1927; do., to Hon. E. B. Ryckman and Hon. R. C. Matthews, successive Dominion mins. of nat. rev.; commr., excise, Canada, 1934.
SIMMONS, ROBERT JOSEPH.—B. 1894; dir., vetv. services, Uganda Prot., May, 1939.
SIMMS, HENRY, O.B.E. (1939), M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; surv. engnr., Gold Coast, 1908; surv. engnr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1925.

SIMPKINS, ALICK LOUIS, A.C.I.E., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.M.E.—B. 1901; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and City and Guilds Engng. Coll.; astt. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1926; exec. engnr., 1933; superintending engnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, June, 1937; D.P.W., 1937.
SIMPSON, ALFRED EDWARD, I.S.O.—B. 1868; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., and Adelaide Univ.; fellow Royal Aus. Inst. of Architects; ent., pub. serv., S. Australia, 1890; architect-in-ch., since 1920.

SIMPSON, THOMAS, L.R.C.P.I. & L.M.: L.R.C.S.I. & L.M., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1903; naval serv., 1918-19; M.O., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; do., Nigeria, 1929; awarded North Persian memorial med., 1939.

SIMPSON-GRAY, LESTER CARTWRIGHT, B.A. (Oxon.).—L. 1904; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; Imp. serv., 1924-26; cadet, M.C.S., Dec., 1926; ag. astt. emgr. commr., Negapatam, Feb., 1928; ag. astt. contr. lab., May, 1928; passed cadet, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1933; ag. mag., Ipoh, July, 1936; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1938.

SIMS, GEORGE, M.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Edon.—B. 1908; ed. Chesterfield Grammar Schl.; non-collegiate soc., Oxford; astt. mast. educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; lect., tech. inst., 1934-35; govt. exam., colloquial Cantonese, 1928; headmast., English schl., Cyprus, 1936 (title changed to principal 1938).

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mens. of swine comsn. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.I., 1908; el to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admstn., 29th Dec., 1921; senator, June, 1930.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and 1918-32; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comsn., 1912.

SINCLAIR-BURGESS, MAJOR-GEN. SIR WILLIAM LIVINGSTONE HATCHWELL, K.B.E. (1935), Kt. Bach (1934), C.B., C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916)—Lieut., N.Z. Staff Corps, Mar., 1911; adjt., 16th Waikato Regt. and O.C. No. 4 group, Mar., 1911; capt., June, 1913; proceeded as exchange offr. with Australian mil. forces, 1913; brig.-major, 6th mil. dist., 1913-14; left Australia with A.I.F.; O.C., 9th Batty., 1914; major (A.I.F.), 1914; commdr., 3rd Australian F.A. Bde., with rank lieut.-col., Feb., 1916; commdr., 4th Australian Div. Arty. with rank brig.-gen., Aug., 1917-19; major, N.Z. staff corps, 1919; lieut.-col., Aug., 1919; arty. staff offr., cent. commd., Nov., 1919; G.S.O. (1) at G.H.Q., Apr., 1922; dir., mily. training and intell., Jan., 1924; ch. of gen. staff, Apr., 1924 with temp. rank col. on staff; ag. commdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Sep.-Dec., 1925; temp-brig., June, 1928; major-gen. and apptd. commdt. N.Z. mily. forces, Apr., 1931 to Apr., 1937; Croix d'officier of Legion of Honour; awarded American D.S.M.; six times ment. in deaps.

SINEL, ROR, A.R.A.N.Z.—Ed. Auckland Gram. Schl.; joined N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1904; health dept., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; pub. trust office, 1919; acctnt. wheat control bd., 1920; ch. acctnt., native trust, 1921; ch. inspr., employment div., lab. dept., 1934; sup't, nat. provident fund and regis. friendly socs., 1936.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT DONNELL, KT. BACH (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897; to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; ag. res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902; and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier the ag. gov't., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apl., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, do., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, do., Manchester, Apl. to June, 1904, do., St. James and Trelawny in 1904, 1905 and 1906; regisr.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. comsnr. of Belize, Apl., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.-gen., 1919-20; seconded, puisne judge, Jamaica, 1920; 1st puisne judge, 1921; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. rules, 1926; apptd. arbitrator by govt.s of U.S.A. and Guatemala in case of P.W. Shufeldt v. the govt. of Guatemala.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIRAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.). Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; att.l., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; ditto, Puttalam, Oct., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Nov., 1929; dist. judge, Kegalle, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1932; do., Batticaloa, May, 1934; do., Mannar, May, 1937.
SKAN, D. A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., B.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1918-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926; pathologist, Nyasaland, 1939.

SKEETE, CECIL CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hons. nat. sci. tripos pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schl. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Barbados, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1924 and 1925; asst. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir., sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 15th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., Dec., 1922; dep. prov. comsnr., July, 1927; prov. comsnr., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., native affrs. Apr.-June, 1930 and Apr. to Oct., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., June-Sept., 1930.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-instr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o.c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. instr. of schl., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb. 1920; clk. to comsnr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns., Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927; ag. prin., Prince of Wales Coll., July to Sept., 1929; asst. col. sec., Sept., 1931; ag. clk., couns., May to June, 1932.

SKINNER, HUGH WELBY.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; served in France, India, Mesopotamia; capt., T.A. res., gen. list, R.A.; app'tl. corp. audit dept., 1923; asst. audr., Nyasaland, 1923; asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; col. audit, 1930; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1935; joint audr., Somaliland and Aden, 1937; audr., Cyprus, 1939.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL, M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsnr., income tax, May, 1927; prte. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; off. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and off. mem., exec. and leg. couns., May to Sept., 1930; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Mar., 1931; off. mem., fedl. exec. coun. and gen. leg. coun.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Antigua; dep. treas. and comsnr., income tax, Trinidad, Mar., 1934; ag. treas., Aug.-Dec., 1936, and June, 1939.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyalatala, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional asst. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deputn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on sp. duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. gov'r., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1932; resigned, 1934.

SLEEP, CAPT. ARTHUR B.Sc. (Manchester).—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; attd. land office, Kelantan, Feb., 1920; passed cadet Nov., 1921; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1931; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1931; offr. cls. II, Mar., 1936; ag. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S. in addn., Jan., 1937; ag. dep. fin. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1938; ag. finan. couns. and audr.-gen., Johore, May, 1939.
SLEIGH, JOHN MANSON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; ed. Aberdeen Grain Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1926; senr. exec. engrnr., 1937.

SMITH, CLIVE ALEXANDER.—B. 1903; ed. Malton Grammar Schl.; R. Navy, 1919; sub. inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1924; 3rd cls. inspr., May, 1930.

SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926; sup't., pol., 1936.

Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reapp't., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agrt., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir. animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Eben scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripods, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. SOLLIS, Clifford George, M.A.—B. 1895; ed. Bristol Grammar Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, asst. mast., Bukit Zahrah Schol., Johore Bahru, Dec., 1920; ag. inspr., schls., Pahang, Oct., 1925; do., Penang, Mar., 1928; supt., educn., Kedah, Dec., 1930; inspr., schls., Selangor, Aug., 1931; senr. inspr., Eng. schls., Hong Kong, 1937; J.P., 1937; dir. of educn., 1939; censor, 1939.
SOLOMON, Saul.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, Kandiah.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo kach., Dec., 1922; office asst. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office asst., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th asst. col. sec., May, 1928; admnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1929; admnl. asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1931; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1932; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, June, 1933; dep. contr., lab., May, 1934; contr., finance and supply, Jan., 1936.

SOMERS, Cecil George.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; govt. pilot, Kenya, 1924; asst. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr. on various occasions, 1929-37; asst. port and cust. offr., Jan., 1938; ag. port offr., Mar., 1938; port and cust. offr., Sept. 1938; ag. senr. cust. offr., June, 1939; lt.-comdr., Zanzibar Volunteer Force, Aug., 1939.

SOPER, Major Edward Joseph, (R. of O.), V.D.—B. 1889; engng. acct., F.M.S. Rly., Sept., 1923; asst. acct., Jan., 1925 and June, 1931; fincl. offr., engng. dept., July, 1928; do., gr. I, Sept., 1928; rev. acctnt., Aug., 1935.

SOPER, John Robert Philpott, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., 1926; agrl. offr., 1928; do., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.
SOUTHALL, Colin Lambert.—B. 1898; ed. Sideot Schol.; R.F.C. 1915-19; B.S.C. (1st cls.) Birmingham; govt. chem., Fiji, 1921-28; asst. analyst, Straits Sttlmts., 1928; govt. analyst, Nigeria, 1936.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1st Baron (creat. 1917), Rt. Hon. Sir Francis John Stephens Hopwood, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W.W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comte., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comte., 1906; mem. of comte. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comte. on electoral reform, 1909; registr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comte., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, Sir Wilfrid Thomas, K.C.M.G. (1938), K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (M.A.); hon. LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.) 1936; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prim. asst. col. sec., July, 1920; prim. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port comte., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1923 and exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., for various periods, 1927-35; gov. and commodr-in-ch., Gambia, Oct., 1936.
SPAFFORD, Harold Hamilton, B.A.—B. 1899; mily-serv., France, 1917-18; demob., 1921; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928; senr. inspr., do., 1938.

SPARROW, Herbert Robert, A.M.I.E.E.,—B. 1889; asst. elec. engrnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; elec. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr. in addn., various periods, 1928-31; senr. elec. engrnr., Selangor, Sept., 1930; ag. advr., elec. Malay States, Mar. 1937; advr., do., 1938.

SPARROW, William Labaute.—B. 1904; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; pol. probationer, Oct., 1924; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, 1927; seconded to Weihaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; passed in Mandarin and Cantonese; atttl. Punjab pol., 1931-2; passed in Urdu, 1932; supt., pol., 1936.

SPEED, Sir Edwin Arney, Kt. Bach.(1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coli., Camb. 1st cls. class. tripes, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripes, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.
SPEERS, R. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1925.

SPENCE, John, C.M.G. (1936), B.A., LL.B., (Syd. Univ.)—B. 1878; mem. pub. serv. bd., 1924-28; chmn., N.S.W. budget comtee., 1928; chmn. N.S.W. taxation investgn. comtee., 1933-35; audr.-gen., N.S.W. and trustee, Australian museum since, 1928.

SPENCE, Louis Percival.—B. 1895; clk., treas., St. Vincent, 1914; 1st cls. rev. and port offr., 1919; ch. rev., excise and port offr., 1927; ch. inland rev. offr., 1936; ag. col. treas., collr., cust., etc. on several occasions, 1934-37; col. treas., collr. cust., etc., Jan., 1938; ag. admr. in 1938.

SPENDER, Hon. Percival Claude, K.C., M.P.—Enlisted A.I.F., Sept., 1918; mem., Warringah (N.S.W.), H. of R., C. of A., from Oct., 1937; assist. treas., Menzies miny., Apr., 1939; ag. treas., Nov., 1939.

SPICER, Rot Godfrey Bullen, C.M.G. (1934), M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary asst. supt. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; asst. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; (lieut., The Carabiniers): wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I., 1923; commr. pol., Kenya, June, 1925; inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, 1930.

SPIFFER, John Wyatt, C.M.G. (1938), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schil. (Som.); served in engnr. dept., G.W.R., as pupil, survr., and draughtsman, new wks. asst. and divnl-engnr., 1896 to 1910; joined engnr. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stiltns., 1920; ch. engrnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., and ch. engrnr. rly., F.M.S., 1926.
SPOONER, Arthur Casswell.—B. 1906; ed. Epsom Coll.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; asst. dist. comsrr., 1930.

SPOWERS, James Howaud.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; do., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; do., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. supt., surveys, Johohe, Jan., 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1933; do., rev. surveys, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Nov., 1935; title changed to ch. survr., gde. II, May, 1938.
SPRAGUE, William, A.M.I.N.A.—B. 1901; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1929.
SPURLING, Antony Cuthbert, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1906; asst. legal advr., Miny. of Health, 1934; res. mag., Kenya, 1936; crown couns., 1939.

SQUIBBES, Francis Leopold.—B. 1895; ed. Towcester Schil.; served Manchester Rgt., 1914-19; Imp. war graves comm., 1920-22; entd. R. botanic gardens, Kew, 1922; asst. supt., botanical and forest dept., Hong Kong, 1924; asst. curator and agrl. offr., Dominica, 1926; ag. agrl. supt., Apr. to Nov., 1931; asst. dir., agr., Seychelles, 1932; dir., Mar., 1934; mem., ex. and lex. couns., Apr., 1934; agric. offr., Gold Coast, 1939.
SRIKHANTA, Mayilvakanam, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937; office asst., Putalam, Aug., 1938.

STACPOOLLE, George Wentworth.—Asst. comsrr., lands survey dept., Gold Coast, 1925; transf'd. to lands dept., 1927; ag. dep. comsrr., July, 1928-Apr., 1929; ag. comsrr., lands, in 1932 and 1933; comsrr., lands, Oct., 1933.
STACEY, Herbert Edward, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1902; cler. serv., secre., Kenya, 1926; legal dept., 1928; ch. elk., 1936; legal asst., 1938; called to bar, 1939; ag. crown couns., 1939.

STACY-MARKS, Lieut.-Commdr. N.J., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1893; ed. H.M.S. "Worcester"; served R.N., 1914-20; demob., 1920; 2nd offr., lake steamers, K.U.R. & H., 1920; asst. to port man., Mombasa, 1928; port man., 1933.

STAFFORD, Frank Edmund O.B.E. (1934).—Active serv. with Queen's Regt., 1914-19; Ira. civ. admtrm., Feb., 1919; asst. finan. advr., 1921; dep. finan. secy., Jan., 1923; finan. sec., 1923; ass't. treas., Nigeria, 1936; senr. asst. sec., 1937; ag. pr n. asst. sec., 1937; prin. asst. sec. (finance br.), 1938; ag. dep. finan. sec., 1939.

STALLARD, Charles Frampton.—B. 1871; ed. St. Edward's Schil. and Merton Coll., Oxford; Grays Inn (Holt schil.); called to bar, 1895; S. African War, 1900-02; advoc., Union of S. Africa; K.C., 1910; prov. coun., Transvaal, 1910-13; Great War, S.W. Africa, 1914-16, Flanders and Italy, 1916-18 (D.S.O., M.C., ment. in desps. three times); lieut.-col.: M.P. for Roodepoort, 1929-38; Pietermaritzburg, 1939; min. of mines, 1939.

STANHOPE, Reginald Alfred Browning, B.V. Sc. (Melb.), M.R.C.V.S., Melb. Univ. and Royal Vet. Coll., Lond.—B. 1891; vet. surg., F.M.S., May, 1920; ag. dir., vety. reach. and vety. advr., Malaya, July, 1936.

STANLEY, Sir Herbert James, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B.1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll. Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1871-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Com., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov-. gen., 1913; resident commr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high commr. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, Aug., 1927; H. M. high commr. for S. Africa, and first high commr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1931; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., S. Rhodesia, 8th Jan., 1935.

STANLEY, ROBERT CHRISTOPHER STAFFORD.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster Schol. and R.M.A., Woolwich; R.G.A., 1918-21; on war serv., Palestine, 1918; admnstr. offr., Nigeria, May, 1925; do.; Cyprus: asst. sec., Dec., 1935; commr., Mar., 1936; ch. asst. sec., Mar., 1938; ag. col. sec., various periods, 1938-39; public informn. offr., Sept., 1939.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st bdr regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901. (med. and clasp); ag. trav. commr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. commr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. commr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. commr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. commr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd to C.O., Mar., 1923; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 23rd Nov., 1939.

STAPLES, RAYMOND RHODES.—B. 1897; M.A. dip. agr. (Cantab.); botanist, Union of S.A., 1922; agr. economist, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. dep. dir., agr., 1930; res. offr. (pasture, vety. dept.), 1931; botanist, 1935; ecological survy., Basutoland, 1937.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1915-35; speaker, 1923-35; M.L.C., 1936.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbrmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; regir. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan., 1919 to Mar., 1920; refugee commr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; regir. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Française, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. commr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; regir. of companies, July, 1922; ch. sup't., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; regir. of companies, July, 1922; regir. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; regir. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. regir.-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; regir., trade marks and patents, Apr., 1928; ag. regir., companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immgr. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., clerk., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.

STEED, ROGER HERBERT, M.I.C.E., Chtd.civ. engrn.—B. 1894; ed Dulwich Coll.; war serv., 1915-19; asst. engtn., P.W.D., Malaya, Feb., 1924; supt., stores & furniture, S'pore, Oct., 1926; ag. ex. engrn., S'pore, Nov., 1929; ex., engrn., S'pore, Feb., 1933; ag. senr. ex. engrn., hdgtrs., Jan., 1933.

STEELE, Lieut.-Commr. ARTHUR DAVENY, R.N.R. (Retd.).—B. 1893; ed. Vale Coll., Rams-gate; cadet H.M.S. "Worcester," 1908-10; H.M. ships, 1914-18 (Dover Patrol and Destroyers based on Devonport and Portsmouth); harbmr. and marine supt., Gambia, 1930; hydrographic surveying course, 1930; el. younger brother, Trinity House, 1933; capt. of port, Gibraltar, regir. of shipping and Trinity agt., 1934.

STEELE, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schol. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; admitted Grenadabar, July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regir.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920; pol. mag., Grenada, Oct., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1931.

STEELE-PERKINS, WING COMMANDER, ALFRED HORACE STEELER (R.A.F. retd.), O.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. Eastmans R. Naval Acad.; R.N., 1905-16; R. Naval Air Serv., 1916-18; R.A.F., 1918-28; Fleet Air Arm, 1928-31; comte. of Imp. Defence, 1931-33; H.O., 1933-38; C.O., 1938; air raid precautions dir., Hong Kong, 1938.

STEPHENSON, ERNEST FORTUNATUS, A.M.I. E.E., M.A.M.I.E.E., Chtd. Elec. Engrn.—B. 1891; asst. elec. engrn., F.M.S., 1924; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrn., 1930; elec. engrn., 1933; sr. elec. engrn., Sel., Oct. 1937.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914 to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923; attld. secretariat, Imp. Economic Confce., 1923; prin., 12th Dec., 1924; U. K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1925; attld. secretariat (economic sec.), Imp. Confce., 1926; int. sec., confce. on opern. of Dom legislation, 1929; admnstr. asst. sec., Imp. Confce., 1930; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1936; asst. under sec., 1938.
STEPHENSON, WILLIAM RALPH EWING, M.A. (N.Z.).—Col. audit dept., Mar., 1936; asst. audr., Hong Kong, Apr., 1936.

STEVENS, Hon. Bertram Sydney Barnsdale.—B. 1889; M.L.A., Croydon, N.S.W., from Oct., 1927; asst. treas., 1927-29; treas., N.S.W., 1929-30; premier and treas., May, 1932 to Oct., 1938; premier, Oct., 1938 to Aug., 1939.

STEVENS, George Alexander B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Sch., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation school in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cl., hons., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, William Oswald, M.A., Oxon.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna in addn. May, 1915; att., Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avisawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1932; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1933; offr. cls. I, May, 1936; govt. agt., N.C.P., Mar., 1938.

STEVENSON, Cecil Young, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; capt., R.E., 1915; adjt., 1916; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mona Star," ment in desps.; exec. engnr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. D.P.W., Apl. to Dec., 1920; ag. dep. D.P.W., on various occasions, 1921-30; D.D.P.W., 1931; ag. D.P.W., 1931-33; D.P.W., Jan., 1934.

STEVENSON, Duncan.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Schl., Edin. Univ., B.So. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1918; lieut., 1919; France, 1918-19; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conserv., Apr.-Oct., 1927; senr. asst. conserv., forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Apr., 1934; sr. do., July, 1937.

STEVENSON, Major Hubert Craddock, C.M.G. (1938), O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—B. 1888; ed. Glengorse, Meada, Eastbourne and Harrow; war serv., 1915-19 (commanded batty., R.F.A.); admst. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1920; asst. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; ag. prin. asst. sec., do., 1927; asst. sec., do., 1927; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; ag. res., 1933-34; admst. serv., cls. I, 1934; ch. commr., Ashanti, 1936.
STEVENSON, Neil Stuart.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924; conservator, Jan., 1930.

STEVENTON, L. E., A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1897; ed. County Schl., Wolverton and Elstow Schl., Bedford; on mil. serv. in E. Africa, Egypt, Salonika and Asia Minor; asst. divnl. aspt., Nigerian rly., 1928; ag. aspt., line, open lines, Nov., 1936; Mar., 1937; ag. ch. traffic aspt., Apr.-Sept., 1937; traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 1938.
STEWART, Augustus George, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922.

STEWART, Capt. John Livingstone, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory medls.; ment. in desps.; vev. offr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1923; prin. vev. offr., 1930.

STEWART, Douglas Roy, C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1886; native dept., Fiji, 1905; cadet, 1906; passed cadet, 1908; stip. mag., 1910; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1912, asst. native commr., 1914; asst. sec. for native affrs., 1916; asst. col. sec., 1917; prin. do., 1921; dep. commr., Western Pacific, 1925; col. sec., Barbados, 1926; O.A.G., in 1927, 1929, 1930; admtr., St. Kitts-Nevia, 1931, O.A.G., Leeward Is. in 1935, 1936, 1937.

STEWART, Hon. Sir Frederick Harold, M.P.—Mem., Parramatta (N.S.W.) H. of R., C. of Aust. from 1931; min. for commerce, Lyons miny., 1932-34; visited N.Z. to conduct trade negotiations, 1934; partly under-sec. for employment, Nov., 1934-Feb., 1936; rep. Australia at Internat. Labor Conf., Geneva, 1935; KT., 1935; min., health and min., social servs., Menzies miny., Apr., 1939; min. for navy from 13th Nov., 1939.

STEWART, Major Malcolm John.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Lovat Scouts Yeomanry, 1914; Indian Army, 1916-22; capt., 1919; Impl. Schl. of Instrn., Zeitoun, Cairo, 1919-21; coy. offr., coy. commdr., 53rd Sikhs F.F.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; ag. senr. asst. treas., 1930, 1931, 1932, 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, Sept., 1933-Mar., 1934; treas. and collr., cust., mag., regis. gen. and harbr., Falkland Is., 1935; maj. and O.C., F.I.D.F., 1935; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1936 and June-Dec., 1937; dep. for gov., May and July, 1936; dep. treas., Sierra Leone, 1938.

STEWART, Neil, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.) (men. in desps.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; supt., 1927; King's pol. med., 1935; dep. commr. (C.I.D.), pol. Tanganyika, 1939.

STEWART, William.—B. 1881; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1899; commr., crown lands and ch. survr., Christchurch, 1926; valuer-gen., 1934.

STEWART, William Gordon, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1893; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; do. Johore, Sept., 1925; exec. engnr., Nov., 1929; do., F.M.S., May, 1930; do., S.S., Aug., 1934; senr. exec. engnr., Sept., 1936.

STEYN, Colin Fraser.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein; LL.D.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1911; admitted Transvaal and O.F.S. sup. ct., 1912; K.C., 1924; parlmt., 1915-28, re-entered 1938; min. of just., 1939.

STEYN, Daniel Hendrik, Dr. Econ. (Zurich, Switzerland).—B. 1901; ed. Gymnasium Potchefstroom, Stellenbosch Univ., Com. Coll., Vienna, Zurich Univ.; tech. asst., dept. agr., Jan., 1928; economist, Board of Trade and Industries, Union of S. Africa, June, 1929; mem., Board of Trade and Industries, July, 1934; econ. advr., do., Jan., 1937; prof. asst., dept. of finance and econ. advr., dept. of commerce and industries, May, 1939.

STEYLER, Johannes Cornraad van Wyk.—B. 1891; ed. Boys' High Schl., Paarl, Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein; dept. of int., Sept., 1913; dept. of just., Feb., 1915; mag., Phillips-town, Feb., 1930-35; mem., farmers' assistance bd., May, 1935; sec. state advances recoveries office, Sept., 1935.
STIFF, Thomas Charles, Comdr. R.D., R.N.R. (retd.), F.R.S.A., A.I.N.A.—B. 1891; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1929; asst. hbr. mast., Feb., 1939.
STIRLING, John Wightman.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912; prin. med. offr., Bech. Prot., 1935.

STOCKDALE, Sir Frank Arthur, K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1925), M.A. (Cam- tab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magda- Hene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibnr., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Imp. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to W.I. agric. conferees, 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. coman., 1909; mem., banana coman., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comitee., 1911; comanr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regiar., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irri- gation comitee., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regiar., co-op. societies and vice-pres., agric. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comitee., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to Imp. entomological confere., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to Imp. botanical confere., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management, tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dists., N. India with Ceylon delega- tion, 1925; asst. agr. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several repts., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schi., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comanr., Aug., 1922; senr. dist. comanr., Jan., 1931; prov. comanr., 1932.
STONE, THOMAS HENRY, M.B.E., B.Sc., M.I.R.Sig.E., F.P.W.I. (London).—B. 1888; Messrs. Belliss & Morcom Ltd. 1903-12; S.E. & C.R., 1912-13; draughtsman, K.U.R. & H., 1913; ch. draughtsman, 1926.

STONEHAVEN, 1st VISCOUNT (cr. 1938), Rt. Hon. Sir JOHN LAWRENCE BAIRD, Bart., P.C. (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvte. sec. to Sir U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinian frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med and clasp, desps., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Avr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. mem., Air Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of trans- port, 1922-24; 1st comanr. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Aus- tralia, 1925-31.

STOODLEY, EDWIN ALAN.—B. 1890; ed. Cranleigh Schi.; coll. audlt. dept., Mar., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915 (seconded to E. Africa, mily. audlt., 1916-19, asst. audr., Somaliland, 1919); dep. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. audr., May, 1934.

STOOKE, GEORGE BERESFORD, F.R.G.S., F.R.Econ.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1897; ed. Bath; paymr. cadet, R.N., Jan., 1914; asst. naval sec., Malta Dockyard, 1915; asst. sec., rear admir. Sir D. L. Nicholson, 1917; do. to Adml. Sir L. Bayley, 1918; transf., emergency list of offrs., R.N., 1919; cadet, Sarawak civ. serv., 1920; asst. sec., 1924; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. elk. of leg. coun., 1931; elk., exec. coun. in addn., 1932; asst. treas., Mauritius, 1933; dep. treas., Kenya, 1936; dep. ch. sec., 1936; author of various papers on anthropological subjects.

STOREY, HAROLD HAYDON, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.).—B. 1894; ed. Cambridge Univ.; milly. serv., 1914-19; mycologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1922; plant pathologist, E. African agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928.

STORRS, SIR RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripods, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo- French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopo- tamia (desps.); secretariat o war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comitee, Palestine exploration fund; milly. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917- 20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc., Jerusalem schi. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local govt. comanr., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, Sept., 1926; do., Northern Rhodesia, Nov., 1932; ret., 1934; rep. Cyprus at 17th cong. of orientalists, Oxford, 1928; rep. Cyprus at C.O. confere., 1930; joint edtr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

STOURTON, I. H. E. J., O.B.E. (1939).—B. 1901; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., Lancs.; prob. inspr., pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1921; A.D.C. and pvte. sec. to O.A.G., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923; inspr., Jan., 1924; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July-Dec., 1932; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1933; supt., police, Zanzi- bar, 1938.

STOUTE, CYRIL EUGENE.—B. 1882; elk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade elk., and ch. elk., C.S.O., and elk., leg. coun., Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec., for various periods, 1926-32; man., govt. savings bank, 1932.

STOWELL, EDGER DE LA MOTHZ, B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Sedbergh Schi.; schlr., Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; asst. mast., K.M.S., Mar., 1921; asst. mast., Penang Free Schi., Nov., 1924; instr., Penang Normal Cls. in addn., July, 1925 and Oct., 1931; headmtr., High Schi., B. Mertajam, Jan., 1927 and Dec., 1930; sr. normal instr., Penang in addn. Apr., 1927; asst. instr., schls., Sept., 1931; supt., educn., Kedah, Oct., 1936.

STOYLE, HERBERT BLIN, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schi., Cre- diton, Battersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. comanr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rlys., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923; loco.supt., K.U.R.H., 1932.

STRACHAN, ANDREW ANDERSON, A.M.I.E.E., Chtd. Elec. Engr.—B. 1896; ed. St. Andrew's Univ., B.Sc. (St. Andrews); asst. tel. engr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Dec., 1924; engr., Selangor dist., Sept., 1932; sr. engr., Penang & Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1936; div. engr., P. & T., March, 1938.
STRACHAN, JAMES DONALD, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1905; ed. Bell Baxter Acad., Cupar, Scotland and Univ. of Cape Town; med. offr., Basutoland, 1939.

STRACHEY, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtee., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtees. for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.

STRADBROKE, (3rd EARL OF) (cr. 1821). GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, BARON ROUS (cr. 1796), VISCOUNT DUNWICH (cr. 1831), K.C.M.G. (1920), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.

STRAHAN, FRANK, C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B.—Sec., prime min's. dept., C'wealth of Australia; sec., Australian delegn. to the Imp. conf., 1923; govt. dir., Amalgamated Wireless (A/sia) Ltd.; sec., Br. Australian and N.Z. Antarctic research expdn.

STREET, BRIG. HON. GEOFFREY AUSTIN, M.C., M.P.—Served A.I.F., 1914-19; brig.-major, 1917; lt.-col. commdg. 4th Light Horse, 1932; in commd. 3rd Cav. Bde., 1935; temp. brig., 1938; el. to H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, g.e., 1934, 1937; parly. sec. for defence, July, 1938; min. for defence, Lyons' Miny., Nov., 1938-Apr., 1939; do. Page miny., 7th-26th Apr., 1939; do. Menzies miny., 26th Apr., 1939-13th Nov., 1939; min. for army, from 13th Nov., 1939.
STREET, HENRY EASTWOOD, D.E.C., A.F.I.A.—B. 1882; under sec. and perm. head, local govt. dept., N.S.W., May, 1931; under sec. and perm. head, wks. and local govt. dept., Feb., 1936.
STREET, HON. KENNETH WHISTLER.—B. 1890; judge, sup. ct., N.S. Wales, Oct., 1931.

STRICKLAND, 1st BARON (cr. 1928), SIR GERALD STRICKLAND, COUNT DELLA CATENA, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripos; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera comn., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres. bd. of health, pres. comtce. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtce. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrs.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 36th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrs., N.S. Wales; held dormant comn. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1927; el. to rep. first elect. div. in the couns. of govt., July, 1939.
STRINGER, HON. SIR THOMAS WALTER, KT. BACH. (1928).—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONACH, JOHN CLARK, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Dublin, Dublin Univ. and Eng. Sch. T.C.D.; asst. engrn., Indian pub. wks., 1911; exec. engrn., 1918; New Delhi constrn., 1913-26; seconded to Kenya as supdg.-engnr. (bdgs.), 1926-29; transf. Kenya, 1930; ag. dir., P.W., 1930 and 1934; D.P.W., 1936.
