STRONG, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1899; ed. Edin. Univ.; B.Sc. (For.) Edinburgh; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; do., Johore, May, 1926; dep. conserv., forests, June, 1931; conserv., forests, Mar., 1936; do., Ceylon, Sept., 1939.

STRONGE, SIR HERBERT CECIL, KT. BACH. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—B. 1876; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., July to Aug., 1914, Sept. to Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; reed. thanks of Tongan govt. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comn., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; ch. just., Cyprus, 1931; ret., 1938.

STROTHER STEWART, ROBERT, M.A., B.C.L., B.Litt., Durham Univ., Gladstone prize-man.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynemouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr. commdg. Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1915-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser, C.O., July to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, Jan., 1930; mem., privy and nominated couns. and coun. of health; examr. in English lit. and hist., Univ. of Malta; chmn., comtce. to enquire into motor transport, 1930; dep. gov., July-Aug., 1932; chmn., traffic control bd., 1930-32; chmn., trade union council claims bd., 1932; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933; ag. ch. just., various occasions, 1935-38.

STRUTHERS, ERNEST ANDREW, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (St. Andrew's), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Cert. L.S.T.M.—B. 1901; ed. Harris Acad., Univ. Coll., Dundee, St. Andrew's Univ., and Lond. Univ.; M.O., Malaya, Mar., 1926; ag. sr. health offr., Johore, June, 1936; sup. scale offr., gd. B., M.M.S., July, 1938.

STUART, ALEXANDER, L.L.—B. 1881; came to S. Africa 1902; apptd. clk., Transvaal serv., 1903; priv. sec., min. of interior, 1915-19; sec., Influenza Com., 1918; prim. clk., Union health dept., 1919; ch. clk., 1933; under-sec. for pub. health, 1939.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mily. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19; deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hemdy Rise, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD CARLETON.—B. 1881; ent. R.N., 1895; lieut. (navigating offr.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron navigating offr., First Battle Squadron, 1920-23; asst., King's harbour., Portsmouth, 1923-25; in command, H.M.S. "Agamemnon," 1925-27; ret. with rank of capt., Mar., 1927; master attendant, Colombo, Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); Ist cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cls. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Stltnts, F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comtee., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; O.A.G., Jan. to Oct., 1913, Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed gov't., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. LL.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt.-gen. and gov. in ch. Jamaica, 1926; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Cyprus, Dec., 1932, do., Ceylon, 1933; ret., 1937; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT, C.M.G. (1932), A.I.A.—Pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas. since 1923; chmn., state bank bd., chmn., pub. serv. superannuation fund bd.; pub. debt commnr.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl., Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr., Toronto observv.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straite, 1884-85; supt. and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observv., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comtee., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20; ret., 1929.

STURGES, ROBERT SIMON MANFIELD, O.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. Radley and Christ Ch.; ast. pol. offr., 'Iraq civ. admin., Apr., 1920; external oriental sec. to high commr., 'Iraq, Feb., 1921; ast. sec., Apr., 1921; polit. sec., Oct., 1924.

STURROCK, HON. FREDERICK CLAUD, M.P., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1882; min. of rly. and harbours, Union of S. Africa; min. without portfolio, 1936-38; pres., assn. of chambers of commerce of S. Africa, 1924; mem., educn. admnstrn. comms., 1924; mem., Port Elizabeth harbour enquiry commn., 1925; chmn., main reef road commn., 1928; M.P., Turffontein div. of Johannesburg, 1929; mem. prov'l. finance commn., 1933.

STUTTAFORD, RICHARD.—B. 1870; M.P. for Newlands, and Claremont, Union of S. Africa from 1924; min. without portfolio, 1933; min. of interior and pub. health, 1936; min. for commerce and industries, 1939.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office ast. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pvte. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; office ast. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Nov., 1928; on sp. duties in connexion with new constitution, Jan., 1930; admvte. sec., income tax dep., June, 1932, clk., bd. of review in addn.; admvte. sec. amalgamated depts. of income tax, estate duty and stamps, and clk., bd. of review, Oct., 1933; sec., elec. bd. in addn., July, 1936; sec. to gov., Jan., 1937.

SUKUNA, JOSEVA LALABALAVU VANAALIALI, C.B.E. (1939), B.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—ed. Wanganui, N.Z. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; held various appts., col. secretariat and native offices, Fiji 1907-12; served in France with the Foreign Legion and awarded the Mily. Med., 1914-16; ag. ast. sec. for native affrs. and aide-de-camp, 1916; cadet, Fiji, 1917; served in France with Fiji Labour Corps, 1917-18; called to bar, 1921; passed cadet, 1921; native lands commr., 1922; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1922; ast. commr. for Fiji, Br. Empire Exhib., 1924; ag. chmn. native lands commr., 1927; dist. commr., Lau, 1932; nom. native mem., leg. coun., 1932; govt. rep., coronation, 1937; native M.L.C., 1937; re-designated admvte. offr., gde. II, 1938.
SULLIVAN, HON. DANIEL GILES, J.P.—B. 1882; ed. Marist Bros. schl., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of rlys. and min. of industries and commerce, 1935; min. of supply, 1939.

SULLIVAN, JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C., B.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; capt., R. of O., barrister-solr. and atty., former vice-pres., Montreal bar; mem., gen. coun., Quebec bar; mem., bd. of examrr., do.; el. to H. of C., Canada, July, 1930; dep. P.M.G., Aug., 1935.

SUMNER, JAMES A.W.—B. 1903; ent. L.P.S., May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transf., C.O., Oct., 1923; seconded for serv. with Rhodesia-Nyassaland R. Comsn., Apr., 1938; cler. offr., higher gde., May, 1939.
SUMNER, LIONEL RANDOLPH COLBERIDGE, Major, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Lancing Coll.; army serv., 1914-20; ment. in desps.; admvte. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1921; cls. III, 1924; cls. II, Mar., 1939.

SURIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; ast. sec., ch. secs. office, Cyprus, Mar., 1918; ag. ch. ast. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local comdt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. ast. sec., Oct., 1920; ag. commr., Aug., 1922; ag. ast. sec., Sept., 1922; commr., Jan., 1924; admvte. offr.; commr., and regiar., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Jan., 1936; registr., co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Mar., 1936.

SURRIDGE, ERNEST REX EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. Felsted and St. John's Coll., Oxford; mod. hist. (hons.); B.A., 1922; lieut., 7th Batt., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1917-20; France and Germany; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; seconded to secretariat, 1925-28; aide-de-camp and prte. sec. to ag. gov., 1928; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Sept., 1930-Feb., 1933; seconded to secretariat, 1933; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1935; asst. ch. sec., 1936; seconded to C.O. as prin., July-Oct., 1938.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr. law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice rept., Queb., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, HON. DONALD MATHESON, P.C. (Can.), M.B., D.S.O.—B. 1879; 1st el. to rep. S. Oxford in Ont. legis., g. e. May, 1902; re-el. g. e., Jan., 1905; dir., colonization and immigrn., Ontario, Mar., 1909; el. at g. e., Dec., 1921; re-elec., g.e., 1925 and 1930; min. of national defence in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug. 1930.
SUTTON, GEORGE, A.M.C.T.—B. 1902; ed. Accrington Grammar Schl. and Tech. Schl., Manchester Coll. of Technology; mast., govt. tech. schl., Gold Coast, June, 1927; ag. prin., do., 1938.

SUTTON, GEORGE GERHARD, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Dale Coll., King William's Town, Union of S. Africa, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1903; admitted to bar, sup. ct., Cape Town, Oct., 1903; K.C. for Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1921; ag. judge, Cape prov. divn. on various occasions, 1922-28; judge, Cape prov. divn., Feb., 1929.

SWABEY, CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1906; ed. Winchester Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; Younger silver med. for forestry, 1927; B.Sc. (forestry), 1928; asst. conservator, forests, Trinidad, 1928; forestry offr., Jamaica, 1937.
SWAIN, EDWARD HAROLD FULCHER.—B. 1883; dipl. forestry (N.S.W.), M.I.A.W.A.; forestry commnr., N.S.W., Aug., 1935.

SWAINE, CAPT. DAVID RICHARD, M.A. (Wales), M.C.—B. 1889; asst. mast., Penang Free Schl., Sept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1915-19; twice men. in despa., M.C., asst. mast., Penang Free Schl., Feb., 1920; sr. normal instr., Penang, in addn., Mar., 1920; headmast., Northam Rd. Sch., Dec., 1924; European mast., cls. IB; prin., King Edward VII Sch., Taiping, Sept., 1929; ag. ch. inspr., Eng. schls., S.S. & F.M.S., Sept., 1934; prin., Raffles Inst., S'pore, June, 1935; supt., educn., Johore, Oct., 1935; ag.ch. inspr., English schl., S.S., and F.M.S., July, 1937; prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Tanjong Malim, Mar., 1938.
SWAN, GEORGE.—B. 1897; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1924; Hong Kong del. to safety of life at sea confee., Simla, 1931; senr. survr., ships, 1939.

SWANZY, H. V. L.—B. 1915; ed. Wellington and New Coll., Oxford (open exhib., 1933); Gibbs schol. in mod. hist., 1935-36; lstcls. hon., schol. of mod. hist., 1936; appt. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1937; asst. prin., D.O., 17th May, 1939.
SWAYNE, ARTHUR CHRISTOPHER CARLENS, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1900; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1923; dist. offr., 1931; dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, 1938.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon.; exhibitioner, Worces. Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2nd class classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admtd. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admtd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admtr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct., 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., Jan., 1906; recd. coronation medal, 1911; gov., Fiji, and high commnr., W. Pacifico, March, 1912; assumed govt., July, 1912; ret., 1918; rep. of Fiji at Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Stlttns., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and commnr. ct. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. commnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; ment. in desps.; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; commnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. commnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res. gen., Malay States, Jan., 1896; admtd. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909.
SWIFT, EDMUND HAROLD, M.A., A.I.C.A.—B. 1893; under sec., dept. of pub. wks., N.S.W., 1934; under sec. and comptr., accts., treasy., N.S.W., 1938.

SWINBOURNE, MAJOR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1894; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt.rlys., May, 1900; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; clk., pay and qrtmr., with rank of sub-inspr., Fiji constab., Oct., 1909; capt., Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; comdg. offr., Fiji cadet corps., Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., Apr., 1911; attd. 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fiji 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, Sept., 1915; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., Oct., 1916; ment. by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active serv., July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., Jan., 1918; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Feb., 1920; dep. commnr., W. Pacific, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Ellice Is., Aug., 1922; 1st dist. offr., Jan., 1928; senr. admstve. offr., July, 1931; ag. res. commnr. in 1928 and 1932.

SWORDER, GERALD HOPE.—B. 1887; survr., topo. br., F.M.S., June, 1921 and Jan., 1925; asst. supt., trig. br., July, 1924; offr. i/c, topo. br., Feb.-Dec., 1932; asst. supt., rev. br., Perak, Nov., 1933; ag. asst. supt., rev. surveys, Kinta, 1935; dep. ch. survr., Jan., 1938.

SYDENHAM-CLARKE, FREDWAY SEYMOUR SYDENHAM, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1936; attd. ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1937; office asst., Matara, May, 1938; offr., cls. II, Oct., 1938.

SYKES, EDWIN LEONARD.—B. 1914; ed. Leys Schl. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (senr. schol., 1936); 1st cls. history trip., pt. I, 1936 and pt. II, 1936; B.A., 1936; app't after compet. exam.; asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1937.

SYKES, GEORGE ROGNVALD, M.A. (Liv.)—B. 1889; cadet, S. Stlnta., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attd. censor's office, S'pore; passed cadet, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Apr., 1917; asst. regist. impts. and expts. (war trade dept.) S'pore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., S'pore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Jan., 1920; supernumy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; asst. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1925; prot. Chinese, Kedah, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. II, prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1930; ag. immigrn. offr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1936; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Nov., 1936; commnr. trade and customs, Johore, April, 1938.
SYKES, JOHN.—B. 1892; dep. dir., educn., Uganda Prot., Apr., 1939.
SYME, JAMES KERR GODFREY.—B. 1903; ed. Durham Schl.; cadet and asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1928.

SYMES, Lieut.-Col. Sir GEORGE STEWART, G.C.M.G. (1939), K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps); Aden, 1903-04 (despr., D.S.O.); attd. Egyptian Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expdn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov. gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times ment. in despr., med., brevet Lt. col.); attd. staff of high commnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd. miny. int. dept., Egyt., 1919-20; dist. gov., Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admst. gov., Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept., 1927; accredited rep. to mandates commnr., 1926 and 1928; res. and commdr-in-ch., Aden, 1928; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1933.

SYMONS, CLAUDE TREVINE.—B. 1879; B.A. Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, June, 1912; ag. gov't analyst, in addn., Dec., 1916; gov't analyst, June, 1919; ag. registr., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TADMAN, JOHN LABROOKE, B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Norwich Schl. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford (Goldsmiths' Exhibn., 1929); col. aud. dept., Feb., 1931; asst. audr., Uganda, Apr., 1931; asst. audr., Mauritius, Feb., 1935; ag. audr., June, 1936-Mar., 1937, and Aug.-Dec., 1938.
TAGOE, EMANUEL, 1st Cls. Teacher's Certif.—B. 1893; ed. Wesleyan High Grade Schl. and Govt. Training Coll., Accra; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, July, 1939.

TAIT, HUGH NIMMO, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripes, Parts I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripes, Part I.; appt'd after compet. exam., olk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st class clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian Comm., 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. sec., 24th Jan., 1930.

TAIT, HORACE VIBART.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd clr. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offr., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd ast. instr. of schools in 1914, 1915 and 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. off., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. class. mast., Queen's Coll., Apr.-July, 1917; ag. asst. mast., do., Mar., 1930; asst. mast., 1934.

TALBOT, CHARLES HARRY VINCENT.—B. 1902; ed. Dulwich Coll. & St. John's Coll., Oxford; ent. ch. sec.'s office, Bermuda, July, 1925; asst. col. sec., Feb., 1937; ag. col. sec., Mar.-Oct., 1937, July-Oct., 1938, Aug.-Oct., 1939.
TAMBAYAH, GODFREY PRASAD, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1937.
TAMWORTH, IAN PHILIP, B.A. (Forestry).—B. 1913; ed. Wellington Coll. and Magd. Coll., Oxford; asst. supt., botan. and for. dep., Hong Kong, 1937.

TATHAM, BERNARD MEABURN.—B. 1891; ed. Rossall Schl.; asst. acct., P.W.D., Uganda, May, 1921; ch. acct., Apr., 1922; asst. treas., Uganda, June, 1926, (with seniority from May, 1921); senr. ast. treas., Sept., 1934; senr. ast. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1936; ag. dep. treas., Sept., 1936-Apr., 1937; ag. treas., May-July, 1937; ch. ast. acctnt.-gen., July, 1937; ag. acctnt.-gen., various periods 1937, 1938, 1939.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR JOHN COLLINS.—B. 1892; ed. Caterham Schl.; pol. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; acct., 1920, 1921-31 and 1932-35; instr., stores, P.W.D., Nov., 1920; ag. cashier, treasy., 1931-32; ch. acct., Kowloon Canton rly., 1935.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR PERCY, M.C., 1914 (Mons Star).—B. 1892; ed. Colchester Royal Grammar Schl.; called to bar, Grav's Inn, 1926; served with R.N.V.R., R.N.D., R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1914-19; severely wounded, 1917; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; asst. commdt., Northern Territories constab., Sept., 1920; transf'd. to Gold Coast pol. as ast. commnr., pol., Nov., 1923; commnr. pol. and in charge, C.I.D., 1926; seconded to Ashanti judiciary as ag. pol. mag., May, 1927; pol. mag., sup. ct., Gold Coast, June, 1928; crown coun., Sept., 1932; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933.

TAYLOR, CHARLES JAMES, B.Sc.—B. 1912; ed. Shanghai Pub. Schl. for Boys and Univ. of Edinburgh (B.Sc. forestry, 1934, post-grad. botany course, 1934-35, med. in Indian and col. forest trees); Imp. Forestry Inst., Univ. of Oxford, 1935-36; ast. conserv. of forests, Gold Coast, Sept., 1936; Gold Coast local forces, 1936.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL, Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918; France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; attch. dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V, May, 1924; asst. dist. offr., Remban, Dec., 1925; offr., cls. IV, May, 1928; asst. dist. offr., L. Perak, Dec., 1928; ag. asst. offr. assignee and regiar. companies, S'pore, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1933; ag. D.P.P., Penang, Mar., 1934; offr. assignee and regiar. companies in addn., May, 1934; D.P.P., Mar., 1936; offr. assignee, 1939.
TAYLOR, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS.—B. 1909; ed. Queens Coll., Bahamas; clk., P.W.D., Bahamas, 1925; senr. clk., 1934; treas. supt., Br. Hond., 1938; mem., Belize loan bd., 1938.

TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practised in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc., 1917; just., ot. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.
TAYLOR, GREVILLE LAUGHTON, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1902; ed. Lodge Schl., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1927; clk., H. of A., Barbados, 1930; pol. mag., 1936.
TAYLOR, GRAVILE POWER.—B. 1899; ed. Elstow Schl., Bedford, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, March, 1925; dist. comsrr., Mar., 1931.
TAYLOR, H. A., F.I.C.—B. 1888; asst. analyst, Hong Kong, 1913; asst. supt., impts. and expts. dept. and monopoly analyst, 1914; ag. supt. in addn., in 1930.

TAYLOR, PHILIP PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy.E., M.I.W.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Catherine's Schl., Littlehampton, Xaverian Coll., Mayfield and Rutherford Coll., Newcastle; asst. engr., P.W.D., Palestine, Sept., 1926; suptg. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, May, 1938; ag. D.P.W., June-Sept., 1939.

TAYLOR, REGINALD WILLIAM, B.Sc.—B. 1895; ed. St. Lawrence Coll. and Univ. Coll., Lond.; war serv. 1914-18 (infy. and Tank Corps); asst. engr., P.W.D., Uganda, 1920; ag. dep. dir., 1937; senr. exec. engr., Nigeria, 1938.

TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, O.B.E. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; exhib., Charing Cross Hosp., London; D.M.O., Cue, West Australia; M.O.H., Cue and M.O.H., Day Dawn, West Australia; M.O., Uganda, Sept., 1914; active serv., E.A. campaign and temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1916; S.M.O., Somaliland, May, 1922; P.M.O., Somaliland, July, 1925.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schl. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown agt's. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1920; ag. treas., Dec., 1921 to May, 1922; treas., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1922; ag. ch. sec., Apr. to June, 1927; gov't. dep. 10th to 27th May, 1927; ret., Mar., 1933; recr.-gen. and treas., Bahamas, 1936.
TAYLOR, ROYDEN ERNOLLIFFE, M.B.E. (1937)—B. 1889; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, Oct., 1905; seconded for war serv., Sept., 1915 to May, 1919; J.P., 1919; regiar., sup. ct., Grenada, May, 1931.

TAYLOR, WILLIAM CECIL, LL.B., B.A. (Manchester).—B. 1902; ed. Bolton Sch. and Manchester Univ.; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; offr., cl. V, Dec., 1927; offr., cl. IV, Dec., 1931; ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1932; offr., cl. III, Dec., 1933.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, C.B.E. (1933), D.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B.1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. gov. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909, govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; offr. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibitn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dir., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised govt. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agr. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agril. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agril. and indus. exhibitn., 1926; chmn., various comtees.; dir., agr., S.S. and f.m.s., Jan., 1929; pres., bd., R.R.I., Malaya, Apr., 1929; visited Java as del., 4th Pacific Sci. Cong., June, 1929; prin., schl. of agr., Malaya, in addn., May, 1930; chmn., comtee., rice cult., Malaya, July, 1930; del., coffee, of col. dir., of agr., Lond., July-Dec., 1931; dir. agr., S.S., and advr. agr., M.S., Jan., 1933; asst. agrl. advr. to S. of S.; author of numerous papers and repts. on agrl. chem., cane sugar production, agrl. educn. and trop. agr.
TEMPLE, ALFRED JOHN BROOMHALL.—B.1912; ed. Denstone Coll.; 16/5th Lancers, 1931-32; Palestine pol., 1933-36; sub-insp., pol. Ceylon, 1936-38; supdt., pol., Somaliland, 1938.
TEMPLE-PERKINS, ERIC ARNOLD.—B.1890; dep. prov. comsrr., Uganda, Sept., 1899.
TEMPLER, JAMES TREVOR.—B. 1900; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, 1938.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RYAN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B.1898; on mily. serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Matale, Dec., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1932; addnl. asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1935; comsrr., development of agrl. marketing, Nov., 1936; dep. comsrr. for dev. of agrl. marketing, Sept., 1937; attd. office of food contr., Apr., 1938; asst. food contr., Nov., 1938.
TENNEKOON, HERBERT ERNEST, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1911; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1935; office asst., Matale kach., June, 1937; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1937.

TERRELL, ARTHUR KOBERWEIN A BECKETT, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1908; war serv., 1914-19; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Jan., 1931; judge, ct. of appl., Kedah, May, 1931; ag. ch. just., F.M.S., Feb., 1933 and Apr., 1936; ag. ch. just., S.S., June, 1937; judge of appeal, Malaya, 1938.

TERRINGTON, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1892; N.E.R., England, 1906-12; B.A.M.C., 1912-14; war serv., 1914-19; station mast., K.U.R. & H.; 1919; ast. dist. traffic supdt., 1928; dist. traffic supdt., 1930, ast. aupt., cl. L., 1938.

TESTER, L. M.C.—B. 1891; war serv., 1915-18; treas. ast., Nigeria, 1919; ch. auct., P.W.D., 1930; treas., Mauritius, July, 1932; ag. col. sec., 1934; treas. and compt., cust., Zanzibar, Nov., 1934; currency offr., Jan., 1936; ag. ch. sec., July, 1936-Mar., 1937 and Nov., 1937-July, 1938; dep. for res., June and Aug., 1937 and Jan., Mar., and June, 1938; finan. sec., Jan., 1938.

TETLEY, ALBERT EDWARD MARRIOTT, B.Sc. (Civil Eng.), A.S.A.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. S. African Coll. High Schl. and Witwatersrand Univ.; served in G.E.A. campaign; ast. engrnr., irrig. dept., Union of S. Africa, 1923; hydrographic survr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1929; hydraulic engrnr. & hydrographic survr., 1937.

TETLEY, JOHN LEWIS, M.A., A.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Bootham Schl., York and St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1927; A.I.C., 1928; M.A., 1936; ast. analyst, Hong Kong, 1931; ast. supt., impts. & expts. dept., 1937; ast. chem., Inst. Med. Res., F.M.S., 1937.

TETTKY, VICTOR ADOLPHUS, M.B.E. (1939), B.A. (Durham).—B. 1891; ed. govt. primary schl., Accra Grammar Schl., Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone (Durham Univ.); 2nd cls. teacher, Accra Training Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; 1st cls. teacher, Apr., 1921; ast. headmast., Apr., 1923; inspr., schls., July, 1926; senr. inspr., schls., July, 1937; dep. dir., educon., July, 1939.

TE WATER, CHARLES THEODORE.—B. 1887; ed. Normal Coll., Bedford Grammar Schl. and Cambridge, B.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, and mem. of Pretoria bar; repd. Pretoria (Cent.) in Union parl., 1924-29; high commr. for the Union of S. Africa in London, 1929.

THACKER, RANSLEY SAMUEL.—B. 1891; ed. Choir Schl. of H.M. Chapels Royal, St. James', Dulwich Coll. and Univ. of London; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; ch. just. and pol. mag., St. Vincent, Nov., 1930; rep. St. Vincent at conf. of W. Indian sea island cotton growers, Barbados, Mar. to Apr., 1932; temp. attd. legal advisory staff, C.O., Sept.-Oct., 1933; atty-gen., Fiji, Nov., 1933; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., 1935 and 1936; K.C. (Fiji), Aug. 1937; puise judge, Kenya, Mar., 1938; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, May, 1938; chmn., farmers' conciliation bd., Kenya, July, 1938.

THACKRAY, CHARLES GOWER.—B. 1887; ed. govt. schls., Middelburg, Cape Prov. and Potchefstroom, Transvaal; atty., sup. ct., Transvaal, conveyancer and notary pub., 1909; ent. law dept., Johannesburg, 1909; pros. and clk. of ct., Piet Retief and Wolmaransstad, 1909; ast. to law and costs clk., 1913; war serv., Tanganyika, 1916; prof. ast. to govt. atty., 1925; enrolled, atty., sup. ct., Natal, O.F.S. and Cape Prov., 1930; mag., senr. grade, Rustenburg, 1933; mag., Bloemfontein sp. "B," 1938.
THATCHER, GEORGE SIENSON, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.W.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1897; ed. Ven. Bede Colleg. Schl.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Malaya, June, 1924; ex. engrnr., June, 1933.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. Schl.; examr. of loan accs., audit dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1904; examr. of accs., ditto, July, 1904; prov. aud. O.F.S. Prov., Apr., 1912.

THEEMAN, FREDRICK WALTER, LL.B.—B. 1901; ed. Wilson's Grammar Schl. and Univ. Coll. London; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1923; res. mag., St. Lucia, 1927; ag. atty-gen., 1931; res. mag., Jamaica, 1932; res. mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1937; ag. puise judge, various periods 1938 and 1939.
THEIS, S. E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.

THERON, COL., FRANCOIS HENRI.—B. 1891; ed. St. George's Cathedral Grammar Schl., Cape town; ent. civ. serv., O.F.S., 1909; lieut., Ensln's Horse, 1915; ast. staff offr., 4th Mounted Brig., G.S.W.A. campaign, 1915; A.D.C. to Gen. Smuts and ast. Permt. Force (Staff), 1915; A.D.C. to Gen. Botha and granted temp. rank of capt., 1916; served, G.E. Africa, July to Sept., 1916; ast. war cabinet staff as ast. staff offr. to Gen. Smuts, 1917; ast., 2nd S.A. Infy., France, 1917; served in France, 1918; comy. commdr., Nov., 1918; ment. in desps. twice; 1914-15 Star, Br. War and Victory med.; Peace conf., Paris, as A.D.C. to Gen. Botha, Mar. June, 1918; lieut. (temp. capt.), Permt. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; graduated Staff Coll., Camberley, 1921; staff offr., G.S. sec., defence headqrs., Pretoria, 1922; posted to S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; A.D.C., O.A.G. of Union, 1923-24 and in 1928; ag. adjt., S.A. Mily. Coll., Roberts' Heights in 1924; offr. instr., S.A. Mily. Coll., Roberts' Heights in 1931; temp. maj., 1932; temp. lt. col., 1932; staff offr. (G.I.), gen. staff sec., defence headquarters., Pretoria, 1933, major, Apr., 1933; lt. col., Apr., 1933 with seniority from 1st Jan., 1933; dir., mily. operns. and training, Oct., 1933; O.C., Transvaal commd., 1934; O.C., Roberts Heights and Transvaal commd., 1934; temp. col., 1934; offr., Order of Hosp. of St. John of Jerusalem, 1936; col., 1936; qmr.-gen., U.D.F., Apr., 1937; mem., war supplies bd., May, 1937.
THIBOU, CLEMENT GARRY.—Foreman of wks., P.W.D., Antigua, Mar., 1915; ast. town clk., St. Kitts, Apl., 1916; ag. supt. of pub. wks., Aug., 1916.

THOMAS, A. MUSGRAVE, Barrister-at-Law.—Crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Nov., 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1928; 2nd puise judge, Trinidad, May, 1929, re-transf. to Cyprus as puise judge, Dec., 1929; ag. ch. just. for various periods, 1930-37; judge, high ct., Nyasa land, 1938.

THOMAS, AMBLER REGINALD.—B. 1913; ed. Gresham's Schl., Holt (schol.) and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge(foundation schol.); B.A., 1934; 1st cls. pts. I and II economics trips; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., min. of agr. and fisheries, 1935; transf. to C.O., 1936; ag. prin., 1939.

THOMAS, EDMUND VINCENT STEWART.—B. 1893; ed. Munro Coll., Malvern, Jamaica and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; mily. serv., France, 1913-19; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; dist. offr., 1929; admstve. offr., cls. II, 1936.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY RIVERS, F.S.I., A.M.T. P.I. (Spec. Dip. Town Planning).—B. 1904; ed. City of London Schl. & Coll. of Estate Management, Lond.; quantity surveying ast., P.W.D., Kenya, 1925; senr. ast. do., 1926; quantity survr., 1934; quantity survr. & contracts offr., 1939.
THOMAS, GEORGE ROSS, C.M.G. (1938), B.A. Syd. Univ.—B. 1876; inspr., schls., N.S.W., June, 1918; ch. inspr., Dec., 1929; under sec. and dir., educon., Aug., 1930.

THOMAS, HAROLD BEKEN, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll.; C.P.R., engrnr. dept., 1907; Uganda Prot. survey dept., 1911; senr. staff survr., 1915; capt., E. African Prot. Forces, German East Africa, 1917-18; dist. survr., Uganda, 1921; dep. dir., surveys, 1924; dir., surveys, land offr. and comsrs. of mines, 1938. Pubns. (jointly) offl. handbook "Uganda," 1935; "History of Uganda Land and Surveys," 1938.

THOMAS, HUGH WHITELEGGE.—B. 1887; ed. Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham and Jesus Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; ast. dist. comsrs., Gold Coast, Aug., 1913; dist. comsrs., June, 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. comsrs., cent. prov., May to Aug., 1924; dep. prov. comsrs., May, 1926; dep. sec., for native affrs., Apr., 1928; sec., native affrs., 1933; ag. col. sec. in 1932 and 1934; gov's. dep. in July and Aug., 1932; ag. ch. comsrs., Ashanti, Oct.-Dec., 1932; accredited rep. to L. of N., 1931 and 1934.

THOMAS, Rt. Hon. JAMES HENRY, P.C. (1917), M.P., J.P., LL.D., Cambridge.—B. 1875; served as cleaner, fireman and driver, G.W.R.; has served in turn as organiser, ast. sec., gen. sec. and pres., Nat. Union of Rlymen; gov. of Dulwich College; mem., Balfour mission to America, 1917; M.P. for Derby since 1910; J.P., Surrey, 1920; S. of S. for Cols., 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924; Lord Privy Seal, 15th June, 1929; S. of S. for Dom. Affrs., 13th June, 1930; S. of S. for Doms. affrs. and for the Cols., 28th Aug., 1931; S. of S. for Doms. affrs., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for the Cols., 27th Nov., 1935; author of "When Labour Rules" and other works.

THOMAS, MAJOR KENNETH ROBERT.—B. 1880; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; atty.-gen.'s office, Cape Town, 1897; relieving offr., dept. of just., 1899; ag. mag., Taungs, Barkly West, Mateking, Somerset East, Knysna, Pieterberg, Victoria West, Jansenville, Somerset West, Wynberg, Vryburg, Carnarvon, Aliwal North, Naauwpoort, Griquatown, George, Cape Town, Indwe, Queenstown, Sterkstroom, Glen Grey, Worcester, Tulbagh, Uitenhage, Britstown, Victoria East, Okahandja, Windhoek, Walvis Bay, Swakopmund, Karibib, Germiston, Eikosburg, Rustenburg, Pretoria, Johannesburg, East London; mily. serv., Boer War, 1899-1902 (siege, Kimberley), Great War, 1914-18; mily. duty, S.W. Africa, 1915-25; mily. mag., Okahandja; mag., Durban, 1938.

THOMAS, LESLIE ARDING.—B. 1891; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl.; pol. probt., S.S., Nov., 1910; ast. supt., pol., Sept., 1914; ch. pol. offr., P.W., Mar., 1921; dep. consent., pol., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ast. supt. pol., S.S., Oct., 1926; adjutant, S.S. pol., Nov., 1926; commdt., pol., depot, S.S., and ag. supt. pol., Jan., 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, July, 1930; do., Selangor, Feb., 1932; dep. comsrs., pol., Perak, 1935; ch. pol. offr., Mar., 1935; ag. comsrs., pol., F.M.S., Apr., 1936; senr. supt., pol., S'pore, July, 1939.

THOMAS, SIR SAMUEL JOYCE, K.T. BACH. (1935).—B. 1875; ed. King's Coll., London; Inglsch schl. and early English Text Soc'y, prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) King's Coll., 1897; B.A., Lond., 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1898; enlisted, 1915; commis- sioned, R.A.S.C. (M.T.), and attd. to R.G.A. (145th Siege Battery), France, Flanders, Italy; invalided, 1919; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1919; ag. col sec. and admstr., St. Vincent, Feb. to Dec., 1920, and on three subsequent occasions; thanked by S. of S. for index of laws St. Vincent, 1921; mem., W. Indian ct. of appl., 1921-23; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923; chmn., oil and water bld., 1923; ag. 1st puisne judge on various occasions, 1923-26 and in 1928; puisne judge, Kenya, 1929; ch. just., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ret., Oct., 1937.

THOMAS, THOMAS JOHN, M.C., M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1883; ed. Univ. Coll. of Wales and Jesus Coll., Oxford; European mast., F.M.S., July, 1923; ag. headmast., High Schl., Malacca, Apr., 1930; do., Penang Free Sch., Aug., 1933; headmast., Sultan Abdul Hamid Coll., Alor Star, Dec. 1936.

THOMAS, SIR THOMAS SHENTON WHITELEGGE, G.C.M.G., (1937), K.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1929), O.B.E. (1919).—ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons., 1901; ast. dist. comsrs., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd ast. sec., Apr., 1911; senr. ast. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. ast., ch. sec., Aug.–Oct., 1913, Aug., 1916–Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917–Jan., 1919; clk. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917–19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. commsn., 1918; ast. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development commsn., 1919; gov's. dep., Jan.–Feb., 1920; prin. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.–Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., in 1925 and 1926; gov.'s dep., on various occasions, 1925-26; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1927; gov., Nyasaland, 1929; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Gold Coast, 1932; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Straits Settlms. and high comsrs., Malay States, 1934.
THOMAS, W. E., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1884; rly. engnr., Sudan, Brazil, F.M.S., New South Wales, and India; dep. res. engnr., Benue bridge constrn., Nigeria, Aug., 1930.

THOMPSON, ALDWORTH, B.Sc.—B. 1900; ed. Cork Gram. Schl., Univ. Coll., Cork and Royal Coll. of Sc. Lond.; ast. mycologist, agrcl. dept., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; mycologist, Oct., 1933; del. 3rd Imp. Mycological Conf., London, Sept., 1934; senr. plant pathologist, Jan., 1938.

THOMPSON, AUBREY DENZIL FORBITH.—B. 1897; ed. Weenen County Coll., Natal and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); lieut., R.F.A., 1917-19; cadet, Uganda Prot., June, 1921; A.D.C., July, 1923; dist. offr., 1933; ast. res. comsrs. and gov't. sec., Bechuanaland Prot., 1937.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. comsrs., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; 1st grade admstive offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. comsrs., 1933; senr. dist. comsrs. 1934.
THOMPSON, CYRIL SYKES, B.A. (Cantab.), Bd. of Educn. Certif. in Physical Educn.—B. 1911; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; inspr. schis., Gold Coast, July, 1939.
THOMPSON, DOWNING CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1892; mil. serv., 1914-19; ast. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1921-29; ast. sec., Palestine, 1929.

THOMPSON, EDWIN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1898; cadet, gov't. lab., Leeward Is., Dec., 1922; 2nd jun. ast., gov't. lab., Apr., 1915; 1st do., Aug., 1917; senr. clk., cust., Antigua, Apr., 1927; transf., gov't. lab., Leeward Is., 1928; milk analyst, Antigua, June, 1930; transf. treasym. and cust. dept., June, 1933; tariff clk., 1934.

THOMPSON, HENRY BURFORD.—B. 1880; cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Apr., 1906; acctnt., ditto, Apr., 1906; 3rd outdoor offr., treasy., Jan., 1908; mem., Middle Temple, 1912; cashier, treasy., May, 1914; comsrs. to admstr. oathes, Antigua, July, 1916; senr. clk., 1926; postmr., St. Kitts, Nevis, 1936.
THOMPSON, HERBERT W., M.R.E. (1937).—B. 1899; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919;

THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1925.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1892; diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; appt. after compet. exam., asst. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library asst., C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; asst. librarian, 10th Oct., 1925; register, col. laws and asst. librarian, 19th Nov., 1928.
THOMPSON, RIVERS FENDALL.—B. 1911; grade II clk., Basutoland, 1929; asst. dist. commr., 1937.

THOMPSON, HON. VICTOR CHARLES, M.P.—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Aust. for New England (N.S.W.), 1922; mem., joint select comtee. of C'wealth elect. law and procedure, 1926-27; chmn., select c'tee on tobacco industry, 1929-30; asst. min., Lyons miny., Nov., 1937-Apl., 1939; do., Page miny., 7th-26th Apl., 1939.
THOMPSON, WALTER PHILIP.—B. 1909; ed. St. Lawrence, Ramsgate; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Oct., 1929; asst. supt., pol., Dec., 1931; supt., pol., Oct., 1939.

THOMPSTONE, CAPT. ERIC WESTBURY, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury Schl.; 2nd lieut., King's Shropshire Light Infy., 1915; served in France and Italy, 1916-19; wounded; silver med. for valour (Italy); adminis., offr., N.P., Nigeria, July, 1919; do., cls. II, 1936; ag. res., Dec., 1934-July, 1935; admative, offr., cls. I, 1937.

THOMSON, JAMES BEVERIDGE.—B. 1902; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edinburgh Univ. (Landine bursar) M.A.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1929; probt., dist. adminstr., N. Rhodesia, 1926; dist. offr., 1928; dist. commr., 1929; asst. to sec., native affrs., 1932; res. mag., 1936.
THOMSON, ROBERT.—B. 1892; ed. High Schl., Greenock and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; serv. with H.M. forces, 1914-19; asst. engrnr., Zanzibar, June, 1925; ag. dist. engrnr. on various occasions, 1927-31.

THOMSON, ROBERT LESLIE.—B. 1880; ed. Perth Acad.; asst. commissary, Br. Guiana, 1907-20; served with H.M. Forces in France, 1914-18; asst. inspr., dists., 1925; 2nd gde. offr., dist. adminstr., Jan., 1932; asst. dist. commr., 1936; dist. commr., 1938.

THOMSON, WALTER MORRIS, M.A.—B. 1905; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1933; 1st clk., mag., Feb., 1934; J.P., 1934; senr. asst. col. treas., Feb., 1935; 1st clk. and mag., Mar., 1935; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, May., 1935; asst. supt., impts. and expts., Dec., 1935; ag. supt., do., May, 1936; extra asst. col. sec., Oct., 1936; asst. supt., impts. and expts., May, 1936 and Aug., 1938; Board of Examiners, Jan., 1938.

THORBY, HON. HAROLD VICTOR CAMPBELL, M.P.—M.L.A., N.S.W. for Wammera, 1922-27; Castleleigh 1927-30; min. for agr., 1927-30; M.H.R. of C'wealth of Australia for Calare, N.S.W., since Dec., 1931; asst. min. in Lyons miny., 1934; visited Gr. Britain on occasion of Silver Jubilee; min. for defence, Lyons's ministry, Nov., 1937-Nov., 1938; min. for works and min. for civil aviation, Nov., 1938-Apl., 1939; do., Page miny., 7th-26th Apl., 1939.

THORNTON, BENJAMIN BECKETT, F.I.A.C.—B. 1890; war serv., 1914-19; clk., K.U.R. & H., 1922; ag. asst. ch. acctnt., Jan.-Nov., 1924; constrn. acctnt., 1925; workshops acctnt., 1930; senr. asst. do., 1932.

THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (TEMP. COL.), SIR EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond., D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., commdr. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosp. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br. sec. I, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C.S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtee. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Rochampton, and mem., comtee. management, Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtee. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problema arising out of the war, 1916-20; senr. asst. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for Union, 1920; sec. for pub. health and ch. health offr. for Union, 1932.

THORNTON, GEORGE EDWIN, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 1899; war serv., 1916-17 (E. Africa); clk., N. Rhodesia, 1917; ag. asst. commr., taxes for periods, 1926, 1927 and 1928; asst. commr., taxes, Apr., 1928; commr., income tax, Apr., 1932; ag. treas., 1933; ch. acctnt. and commr., income tax, 1937, and acct.-gen., Aug., 1939.

THORNTON, HUGH CHOLMONDELEY, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1920).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Viscount Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agr. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, RUSSEL WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1936).—B.1881; ed. Graaff Reinet and Elenburg Schl. of Agr., Cape Prov. (dipl. with hons.); served in Anglo-Boer War as Scout and intelligence offr.; joined dept. of agr., Cape Prov. field crops and experiment and research section, 1905; offr.-in-ch., agrl. research branch, agrl. dept., Cape prov., 1908; prin., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr. and experiment station, 1910; sent by govt. to England, Europe and Australia to study agrl. educn., experiment station organisation, etc., took charge of expedn. to W. Central Africa, in connection with ostrich industry of Union, 1912; active serv. in France, 1917; resumed charge of Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., 1919; dir., field and animal husbandry, Oct., 1923; dir., native agr., dept. of native affrs., Apr., 1929; dir., agr., Basutoland, and agrl. advr. to H. Cr., 1934.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb.; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Ipoh; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kamper, Feb., 1926; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; asst. contrlr., lab., Klang; asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, May, 1931; ag. emigrn. comsrr. for Malaya, May, 1932; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933; mag., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1937; mag., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1938; ag. dist. judge, Malacca, Feb., 1939.
THORPE, CYRIL EDWARD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1899; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1924; senr. exec. engnr., 1935.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.)—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. coun., Br. Hond. Nov., 1922; asst. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Hond., Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Hond. Defence Force, Apr., 1928; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Oct., 1934; audr., Cyprus, 1937; prin. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1939.
TILLMAN, JOHN, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1901; temp. med. offr., Somaliland, 1936; med. offr., 1937.

TILNEY, CHARLES EDWARD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1909; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1932; attd., office of dir., med. and san. services, Feb., 1932; attd., Galle kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Ratnapura kach., June, 1933; admstr. sec., dept. of med. and san. services and sec. to min. for health, Mar., 1934; asst. comsrr., lands, Mar., 1937; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, July, 1938.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Dec., 1922; judge pres.; Transvaal prov. divn., 1937; judge of appeal, 1938.

TINWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; mil. serv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; assr. acctnt., F.M.S. rlyas., 1921; ag. dep. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rlyas., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933; ch. acctnt., 1936; ag. gen. man., 1938.

TISDALL, EDWARD GORDON ST. CLAIR, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; served in France 1st Monmouth Regt., 1915-18; asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; ag. comsrr., mines, Kenya, 1935 and 1936.

TISDALL, HERBERT GEORGE, B.SC., A.R.C.S., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schl.); eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; asst. engrnr., engng. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engng. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, HENRY IAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; asst. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.

TODD, CECIL LINDLEY.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schl.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1929; senr. asst. audr., June, 1933; dep. audr., Apr., 1937; ag. audr., Jan.-June, 1939; dep. audr., Kenya, July, 1939.

TODD, CHARLES DOUGLAS.—B. 1901; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923-25; resigned; re-apptd., 1929; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, June-Oct., 1935; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Leeward Is., 1936; asst. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1938.

TODD, DUDLEY CLARKE.—B. 1906; ed. St. Kitts-Nevis Grammar Schl. and Lodge Schl., Barbados; clk. and cotton inspr., agrl. dept., St. Kitts, 1928; ch. sany. offr., country dists., 1930; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., 1933; 2nd lt., defence force, 1935; ag. ch. rev. offr. and harbr. mast., 1937.

TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1930; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1933; chmn., urban coun., 1936; asst. finan. sec., 1939.

TOLFREE, WILLIAM NORMAN.—B. 1892; ed. Dudley Coll., N. Stafford Regt. and R.A.F., 1915-20; inspr., schls., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1921, ag. ch. inspr., Dec., 1930-May, 1931; senr. supt., 1938; prin., May, 1938; ag. ch. inspr., June-Oct., 1938.
TOLMIE, GEORGE MACKENZIE, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1904; ed. Inverness Royal Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forest Inst., Oxford; asst. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1926; senr. asst. do., 1939.

TOMLINSON, SIR GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903-04; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. Nigeria, 1911; 3rd cls. res., 1911; ag. res., 1912; 2nd cls. offr. (change of title), 1914; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; ag. sec., N. Provs., 1919; asst. sec., nativ. affrs., 1921; ag. sec., do, 1921 and 1925; staff grade (supern.), 1925; ret., 1928; asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Oct., 1930-30th Sept., 1939.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engrnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rlyas., 1911; served with R. Engrns., France, 1915-19; res. engrnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rlyas., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1925; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engrnr., govt. rlyas., Trinidad, 1928; supt., Trinidad govt. rlyas., 1933.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, KT. BACH (1925).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.1871; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admin. of the occupied terr., of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TONGUE, EDWIN, M.M.—B. 1898; pol. probr., S.S., Jan., 1925; asst. supt., pol., June, 1929; asst. supt., pol., detec. br., S'pore, June, 1930; dep. commr., estate duties, in addn., July, 1934; coll., estate duty, S.S., Jan., 1936; offr.-in-ch., stamp office, in addn., Apr., 1936; regar. of companies, S.S., in addn., Aug., 1937; title changed to commr., estate duties, May, 1938.

TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCEY, O.B.E. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhibr.); asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1923; lab. comsnr., May, 1930; reverted dist. offr. on abolition of post, Oct., 1931; senr. dist. offr., Dec., 1935; prov. comsnr., 1937.

TONKIN, SYDNEY, B. Eng. (Liverpool), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E. Chtd. Civ. Engrnr., Chtd. Mech. Engrnr.—B. 1886; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1925; do., F.M.S., govt. factory, June, 1929; senr. exec. engrnr., June, 1936.
TONKING, HAROLD DENNIS.—B. 1902; asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, June, 1927; pathologist, Mauritius, Dec., 1936; senr. pathologist, July, 1939.

TORY, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1912; ed. King Edward VII Schl., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol., 1931-35); med. and mod. languages tripos, pt. 1 cls. 1, pt. 2 cls. 1; B.A., 1934; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin. D.O., Oct., 1935.

TOUGHER, ROBERT ALEXANDER HUNTER.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; war serv., Dardanelles, Egypt and Syria, ment. in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; solr., Ireland, 1920; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; asst. comsnr., lands, Gold Coast, 1937.

TOVEY, DUNCAN LORIMER PATTISON.—B. 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; asst. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S. Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dec., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; prin., 4th Nov., 1937.

TOWNLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.) in Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Belgium and Germany 1914-19; asst. gov't. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. gov't. printer, May, 1927 and Nov., 1929; gov't. printer, Gold Coast, 1931; do., Nigeria, 1939.

TRATMAN, EDGAR KINGSLEY, L.D.S., M.D.S.—B. 1899; ed. Clifton Coll. and Bristol Univ.; prof., dental surgery, coll. of med., S'pore, Oct., 1929; mem., dental bd., F.M.S., Apr. 1931; do., S.S., July, 1931.

TREMLETT, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL SCHWEDER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Harrow Schl. and R. Vety Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.

TREVOR, IVAN BERNARD.—B. 1900; ed. Tyttenhanger Lodge, Seaford; Rossall exhib., 1915 (not taken up); cadet, Midland rly., 1917; traffic asst., Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; ag. traffic supt. and ch. storekeeper in addn., 1925 and 1927; ag. ch. acct., 1928; passed Cantonese, 1928; traffic man. and ch. storekeeper, 1932; capt., H.K. Vol. Def. Corps., 1935.
TRING, RICHARD FRANK.—B. 1909; ed. Westminster Choir Schl. and Hurstpierpoint Coll.; The Life Guards, 1926-34; Palestine pol., 1934; polit. offr., Aden, 1939.

TRINICK, COMDR. GEORGE WILLIAM, R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1889; merchant serv., 1904-13; extra master's B.O.T. certif.; R.N., 1914-19 (H.M.S. "Diana," Home waters, near and far East, H.M.S. "Temeraire," North Sea and Russia); marine offr., Nigeria, 1922; lieut. comdr., 1924; comdr., on retired list, 1934; ag. dir., marine, 1935 and April, 1937; prin. marine offr., 1935.
TRIPE, WILLIAM BORROWDALE.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch, N.Z., Victoria Univ. Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929.

TROUGHTON, EDWARD PERCEVAL—B. 1899; ed. Forest Schl.; R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-18; asst. to dist. comsnr., Basutoland, 1920; asst. to finan. sec., 1923; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; senr. asst. treas., Sept., 1935; dep. treas., Mar., 1937; acct-gen. and currency offr., Aug., 1937.

TROUGHTON, JOHN F. G., M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; foundation schol. in maths.; B.A. (seur. moderatorship and gold med. in ment. and moral philosophy), 1924; LL.B. (1929); admstve. cadet, Kenya, 1926; dist. offr., 1928; on sp. duty, Kenya land comsnr., 1933; as clk., leg. coun. and clk., exc. coun., 1933; seconded to C.O., 1936; seconded to B.B.C. as Empire news adr., 1936; on sp. duty, C.O., 1938; dep., finan. sec., Kenya, 1939.

TROWELL, JAMES HENRY ATKIN, A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1891; inspr., machinery, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Nov., 1929; do., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; ag. licensing offr., automobiles, Perak South, in addn., Mar., 1924; ag. ch. inspr., machinery, F.M.S., in addn. June, 1932; inspr., machinery, Ipoh, and ag. ch. inspr., machinery, F.M.S., in addn., Apr., 1933.

TROY, HON. MICHAEL FRANCIS.—B. 1877; ed. Pub. Schl., Wardell, N.S.W.; rep., Mt. Magnet, leg. assem., W. Australia, 1904-39; speaker, 1911-17; min. for mines and agr., 1924-27; lands and immigrn., 1927-30 and 1933-39; agt.-gen., W.A., 1939.

TRUPHET, JOHN SYDNEY, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; asst. head, stores dept., 1919; do., insurance and checking dept., 1920; do., shipping dept., 1922; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1931; head, do., 1933; head, shipping dept., 1935.

TRUSTED, SIR HARRY HERBERT, KT, B.ACH. (1938), K.C.—B. 1888; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Trim. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1913; served overseas (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, East Indies), 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1925; atty.-gen., 1927; comsnr. to revise the federal acts, Leeward Is., 1928; K.C., Leeward Is., 1929; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug., 1931; atty.-gen., Palestine, 1932; ch. just., 1936.

TRUTER, SIR THORODUS GUSTAFT, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ. ser., Dec., 1892; chief examr., accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (med. and four clasps); asst. mag., Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermelo, Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, Apr., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, July, 1910; comsnr., S. African pol., Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa; ret., 1923.

TUCKER, D.E.A., M.C.—B. 1896; ed. at St. Francois de Sales Fathers, Givisiez (Fribourg) and Florimont Coll., Lanoy (Geneva), B.-es-Lettres (Ire partie) Grenoble Univ., July, 1914; Wilts. Regt. and M.G.C., 1914-20; 2nd lieut., June, 1917; H.M. cust., U.K., 1923; inspr.-in-ch., preventive serv., Tanganyika cust., 1929.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, C.B.E. (1932), F.L.A.A.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-08; entd. engrg. dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audt. dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asst. audr., G. Coast, Sept., 1913; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (mentd. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volks, Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; capt., Nyassaland Field Force, 1919; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; dep. treas., Nyasaland, Dec., 1921; treas., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., and dep. to gov. various occasions; ag. gov., Feb., 1934; finan. sec., N. Rhodesia, Feb., 1938; ag. ch. sec., June-Sept., 1938 and July, 1939-Jan., 1940; chmn., agric. advisory and maize control bds.

TUCKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab), B.Ed., (CapeTown).—B. 1892; ed. Wilsons Grammar Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., nat. sci. triplos pt. I (hons. chem. and zoology) 1913; 1st cls. nat. sci. triplos, pt. II (zoology) 1914; asst., S. African museum, CapeTown, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921-28; entomologist, Barbados, 1928; publs. include various papers on Araneae and Acari, and economic papers on control of Acarid and insect pests.

TUDHOPE, CUTHBERT DRYDEN.—B. 1892; ed. S. Andrew's Coll., Rhodes Univ. Coll. and S.A. Coll.; active serv., 1914-19; B.Sc. (mining engrg.), Cape, 1911; asst. inspr. mines, 1929; dep. inspr., 1933; inspr., mines, 1937.
TUDOR, DUDLEY HEMING.—B. 1904; ed. Cranleigh schl.; acct., bankruptcy dept., S.S., May, 1930; acct. pub. wks. and excise depts., Penang, Jan., 1937; asst. treas., Penang, in admn., Apr., 1937.

TUPPER, HENRY DE BEAUVIOIR, A.M., R.N., Capt., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1883; naval cadet, 1898; commdr., 1917; capt., 1928; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Otranto barrage and admv.; Legion of Honour (France), Order of Aves (Portugal) and Al Valore (Italy); barhm., Br. Guiana, 1925; do., Jamaica, Oct., 1931.

TURBET, CHARLES RUPERT, B.V.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; served, A.I.F., 1915-19, Egypt and France; vety. offr., Fiji, 1923; senr. vety. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, 1937 and provisional offr. M.L.C.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TURNELL, K.C.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-22 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 as atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., 1933; puian judge, Gold Coast, Aug., 1939.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNER, MAJ. CLARENCE ROY, A.M.I.Mech.E. Chartered Mech. Eng.—B. 1891; ed. Brompton Publ. Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Adelaide, S. Australia; Australian Imp. Forces, 1914-19; Gallipoli and France (ment. in desps.); maj., Apr., 1917; now maj., Reg. Army R. of O.; asst. loco. supt., Gt. Southern of Spain Rly., Nov., 1919 to Aug., 1921; asst. loco. supt., rly. constr., Nigeria, Jan., 1923; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., Jan., 1925; dist. loco. supt., Jan., 1927; run. supt., Jan., 1932; ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1937.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot srgt-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, May, 1923; ch. of pol., Grenada, Jan., 1927.
TURNER, HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Oamaru South Publ. Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. pub. trust office, 1899; asst. pub. trustee, 1923; under-sec. of defence, 1930.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regir.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; comsnr. to administer oaths, St. Kitt., 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1906; 1st clk., regir.'s off., Antigua, May, 1906; comsnr. to admin. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. regir. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regir. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regir., Sept., 1917; comsnr. income tax, July, 1923; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1926; addnl. mag., dist. A., Antigua, Jan., 1928; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1929; J.P., May, 1930; comsnr. sup. ct., Oct., 1931.

TUTE, SIR RICHARD CLIFFORD, KT. BACH. (1937).—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Schl., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet exam. for Indian civ. serv., 1898; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly, Meerut and other dists., from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major, R.G.A.; dist. judge, Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1920; pres., land ct., Samaria, 1921; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; admtd. govt. in Aug., 1932; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code," and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam; retd., 1939.
TWNING, EDWARD FRANCIS, M.B.E.—B. 1899; dep. prot., immigrts. and poor law comsnr., Mauritius, Feb., 1939; dep. dir., lab., Nov., 1939; ag. dir., do., Nov., 1939.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls., hons., civil engrg., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.E. (sp. res.) 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engnr., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambia, Dec., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1930; dir., wks. and transport, Trinidad, May, 1934; mem. leg. coun.

TYNDALE-BISCOE, CKCEL JULIAN, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Glenalmond, Trent and Jesus Coll., Camb.; war ser., R.A. 1914-18 (France and Palestine); M.C., 1916; instr. Imp. Sch. of Gunnery, 1918; polit. offr., Nazareth; passed Indian civ. serv. exam., 1919; supt., educn., Tanganyika, 1924; ag. dep. dir., eduon., 1932; ag. dir., do., May-Nov., 1932 and Feb.-Sept., 1935; dir., native educn., N. Rhodesia, 1936.

TYNDALL, ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1899), A.M.Inst.C.E., M.Am.Soc.C.E., M.N.Z.Soc.C.E., solr., sup. ct., N.Z.—B. 1891; ed. Blue Spur Schi., Lawrence dist. high schl., Otago univ. and Massachusetts inst. of technology; joined N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1909; asst. engnr., 1913; engrnr.-in-ch., pub. wks. dept., W. Samoa, 1920; highways engnr., N.Z., 1924; under-sea., mines, 1934, also dir., housing constrn., 1936.

TYRRELL, SIR FRANCIS GRAEME, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the off. admstr. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. asst. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1905, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1921; prin. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; contr., rev., June, 1927; dep. ch. sec., July, 1931; ch. sec., May, 1932; O.A.G., Sept.-Dec., 1933 and June-Nov., 1935; ret., 1937.

TYRWHITT-DRAKE, ROBERT HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, S. Africa, Magdalen Coll., Oxford and Rhodes Univ. Coll. Grahamstown; computer, survey dept., Kenya, 1927; dist. survr., Kenya, 1930; dir. land survey and regisrn., Zanzibar, 1936; dir. of survs., Kenya, 1939.

TYSOE, MAJOR WILLIAM, D.S.O. (1918), O.B.E. (1936), M.C. (1918), French Croix de Guerre (1918), (ment. in desps., 1918), War serv., 1914-18, France; North Russia, 1919; B.S.A. pol., 1920; N. Rhodesia pol., and N. Rhodesia Regt., Dec., 1920; lab. offr., July, 1938.

UPSDELL, GEORGE EDGAR SKYNNER, M.A. (Cantab.), H.C.F., C.I.E.—B. 1886; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1919; 1st cdmr., Indian Schi., 1931; Yaumati Schi., 1932; Cent. Br. Schi., 1935; az. headmast., King's Coll., 1937; senr. mast., 1938; do., cent. Br. schi., 1938; prin., do., 1939.
UPTON, THOMAS HAYNES, O.B.E., M.Sc. M.C.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1889; pres. Metropolitan water, sewage and drainage bd., N.S.W., Apr., 1935.
URICH, LEO GOTTFRIED WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.C.M. & H.—B. 1899; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlmt., Trinidad, Jan., 1933; M.O.H., 1937.
URQUHART, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Edin.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1901; asst. engnr. K.U.R. & H., 1926; senr. asst. engnr., 1935.
USHER, CAPT. C. G., M.C. — Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927 and 1931.

USHER, WALTER WHITBREAD.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; served Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. Africa, 1914-15, Flanders, 1916-18; clk., Swaziland, 1910; registr. and master, sp. ct. of Swaziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. asst. comsnr., 1919.
UTTLEY, DENTS PLORAT, B.A.—B.1906; ed. St. John's, Leatherhead and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; col. audit dept., May, 1930; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1930; audr., Br. Honduras, 1938.

UTTLEY, KENNETH HARRISON, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.D., B.Ch. (Camb.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Hong Kong; asst. med. offr., Victoria Hosp., 1929; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Lai Chi Kok Prison, Kowloon mort.; asst. med. offr., Kowloon Hosp., in ch. offrs.' families, Kowloon, and pol. surg., Kowloon, 1930; med. offr., ditto, 1933; M.O. in ch., govt. civ., mental and Tsan Yuk hosps., 1936; M.O. in ch., Kowloon hosps., June, 1937.

VAITHIANATHAN, KANTHIAN, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; attd., Colombo kach., July, 1923; ditto Ratnapura kach., May, 1924; ag. office asst., ditto, Sept., 1924; extra office asst., ditto, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Avissawella, February, 1926; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1926; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Dec., 1928; office asst., Colombo kach., Mar., 1929; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1929; asst. col. sec., Mar., 1930; sec. to min. for home affrs., July, 1931; sec. to min. for commun. and wks., Oct., 1936; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Aug., 1937; sec. to retrenchment comm., Aug., 1938.

VALENTINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1892; ed. Palmer's Schi. and Lond. Hosp.; temp. comen., K.O.S.B., 1914-16; R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1919; in ch., civ. hosp., 1925-28; in ch., Kowloon hosp., 1929; in ch., Victoria hosp., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S., 1931, 1932, 1935 and 1936; M.O. in ch., civ. and ment. hosp., Jan., 1934; ag. D.D.M.S., Feb., 1936; D.D.M.S., Oct., 1936; ag. D.M.S. and M.L.C., June, 1937-Feb., 1938.

VALLADARES, ERIC.—B. 1908; ed. Dan. Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; clk., transp. dept., Br. Guiana, 1924; cler. asst., med. dept., 1926; prob. offr. of cust., 1927; 6th cls. clerk., col. sec.'s office, 1932; 5th do., 1933; clk., leg. coun., May, 1936.

VAN COPPENHAGEN, FREDERICK PASCHOUDE.—B.1885; 3rd grade clk., deeds, Capetown, 1904; 2nd grade clk., 1912; 1st grade clk., 1918; 2nd grade examr., 1919; prin. clk., deeds, Pretoria, 1933; regist., deeds, Kingwilliamstown, 1937; asst. regist., deeds, Capetown, 1937; regist., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1938.

VAN BROEKHUIZEN, DR. H. D.—B. 1972; ed. Ficksburg, Senekal, Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edinburgh, Utrecht and Leyden; D.D. Leyden; min., Dutch reformed ch., Pretoria, before and after Anglo-Boer War; M.P., Pretoria, 1924-33; mem., S.A. acad.; historical soc., Leyden and Utrecht; min. plenip., for Union S. Africa, The Hague, Aug., 1933.

VANDER BYL, MAJOR THE HON. PIETER VOLTELYN GRAHAM, M.C., M.A. (Cantab.), M.P.—Gen. Botha's G.H.Q. staff, G.S.W.A. campaign, 1914-15; Gen. Smuts G.H.Q. staff, G.F.A. campaign, 1916-17; G.H.Q., R.A.F., France, 1918-19; A.D.C., Field Marshal Earl Haig, 1921 (twice ment. in desps., M.C., Chevalier Legion d'honneur (France) and Brevet Major); leader, S.A. deleg., Internat. affrs. confce. on C'wealth relationships, Sydney, 1938; el. M.P. for Bredasdorp, 1929; Union Cabinet, 1939.

VAN DER HORST, A. C.—B. 1887; joined Impl. Mily. Rlys., Johannesburg, 1900; inspr., white lab., 1912; inspr., lab., dept. mines and industries, 1917; indus. inspr., dept. of lab., 1926; sp. mem., wage bd., 1930; ch. clk., dept. of lab., 1934; asst. supt., welfare and probation serv., dept., social welfare, 1937; pub. serv. inspr., 1938; under-sec., social welfare, 1939.

VAN DER LINGEN, Pieter Moll, B.A.—B. 1879; ed. Kroonstad and Stellenbosch; headm.r., Jeppie educn. instit., Johannesburg, 1903; do., Middelburg secondary schl., 1908; inspr., educn., Transvaal, 1913; pub. serv. oomnr., 1934.
VAN DER MERWE, Hermanus Zulch, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. at Ceres High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; inspr., schls., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Cape Prov., 1934.

VAN DER SPUY, Lt.-Col., Adrian Johannes, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Capetown).—B. 1889; ed. Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Glasgow Univ. and Capetown Univ.; H.S. and H.P., Western Infmy., Glasgow, 1914-16; capt., S.A.M.C. (Overseas), 1916-20; served in France and Belgium, 1918; Br. War and Victory medals; O.C. mil. hosp., Wynberg, as capt. (temp. maj.), S.A.M.C. (A.C.F.), 1920; maj., S.A.M.C., (Permit Force), 1923; defence headqrs. (med. services sect.), 1926; O.C. mil. hosp., Roberts' Heights and senr. med. offr., Roberts' Heights, 1926; transf. to dept. of public health as ast. health offr. on Union, 1927; senr. ast. health offr., Union, 1937; lt.-col., R. of O. (med. br.), U.D.F., 1927; dep. dir., med. services, U.D.F., attd. to Staff Corps from R. of O. (in addn. to civ. duties as ast. health offr. for Union), 1927; dir., med. servs., U.D.F. col. (whole time), 1938.

VAN DER SPUY, Michiel Louw.—2nd gr. compr., Natal, Feb., 1913; ch. draughtsman and compr., Apr., 1914; prof. astt., Apr., 1917; survr.-gen., Natal, Sept., 1931; do., Pretoria, May, 1934; do., Capetown, Dec., 1937.

VAN LANGENBERG, William James Albert, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.C.G.I.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attd., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1930; attd., Anuradhapura kach., May, 1930; ag. office astt., Nuwara Eliya kach., Oct., 1931; admstive. sec. to dir., med. and san. services, and sec. to min. for health, Feb., 1936; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, June, 1937.

VAN NIEKERK, Christiaan Andries.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commdt. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del., peace confce, Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation comsn. till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S., 1907; leg. assem., Union, 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances comsn., chmn., sp. pensions bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; pres., 1930.

VAN RYNEVELD, Major-Gen., Sir Pierre Helperus Andrias, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—B. 1891; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment, C.O., Scnussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in des., six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), the Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., Feb., 1920; col., S.A. staff corps, Nov., 1929; O.C., troops, Roberts Heights and commdt., S.A. Milv. Coll. in addn. to duties as dir., air services, Dec., 1929; brig.-gen. and apptd., ch., gen. staff, May, 1933; maj.-gen., Apr., 1937.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, Johannes Zacharias, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; astt. Bethlehem; vice-prin., Ficksburg, 1906; do., Normal Practising Schl., Bloemfontein, 1910; prin., model schl., Bloemfontein and vice-rect., Normal Coll., 1913; inspr. of schl., O.F.S., Nov., 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland, Germany and Austria inspr. of instituta., Union edn. dept., 1921; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn. since 1922; organiser, child welfare and specl. ed., 1925; supt., welfare and probation servs., 1937.
VAN STON, James John, M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1894; ed. Diocesan Boys' Schl., Hong Kong; survr., ships, S.S., May, 1923; do., S'pore, May, 1927; do., Penang, Feb., 1932; do., S'pore, Oct., 1936.
VANSTON, James John, M.R. Mar. E.—B. 1896; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; war surv., 1914-19; survr., ships, S.S., Jan., 1929; ch. inspr. of machinery, S.S., Sept., 1938.
VAN ZELEN, Frederik Cornelius.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engr., draughtsman, harbr. engr.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; astt. civ. engnr., 1925; dep. civ. engnr., 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, Hendrik Stephanus, LL.B. (Camb.), B.A., L.L.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1905-10; parl. draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comsn., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932; judge-pres., Cape prov. divn., 1935; chmn., S.W. Africa constitution comsn., 1935-36.

VAN ZYL, Gideon Brand.—B. 1873; ed. Normal and South African Coll., Capetown; atty.-at-law and notary pub., 1898; conveyancer, 1897; astt. dir., war recruiting, 1918; dep. chmn., comtees., 1921-24; dep. speaker and chmn., comtees., Union of S. A. H. of A., Jan., 1934.

VAN ZYL, Jacob Ernest Siebert.—B. 1885; prob. clk., R.M., Hanover, 1904; clk., deeds, Vryburg, 1904; clk., R.M., Calvinia, 1905; clk., master's off., C.T., 1906; 2nd gr. clk., Union educn. dept., 1910; 1st gr. clk., do., 1914; senr. clk., do., 1917; 2nd gr. prin. clk., do., 1920; 1st gr. prin. clk., 1921; 2nd gr. ch. clk., 1925; 1st gr. ch. clk., 1928; pub. serv. inspr., 1931; under sec., educn., July, 1934.

VAN ZYL, Jacques Petrus, D.Phil., M.A.—B. 1890; teachers' dipl., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; vice-prin., secondary schl., 1911-12; resch. astt. to 1918; agrl. chem., Univ. Gottingen; lect. in chem., Glen schl. of agr., 1919; resch. offr., Understeopoort, 1920; resch. chem., div. of chem., Pretoria, 1927; astt. ch., div. chem.services, dept., agr. and forestry, 1934; ch., 1936.

VAN ZYL, L. J., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Boys' High Schl., Wellington, and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; head, High Schls., Vredfort, Smithfield, Ladybrand, 1910-21; inspr., educn., 1921-35; sec., educn., O.F.S., 1935.
