VASKESS, Henry Harrison, O.B.E. (1938).—B. 1891; clk., Western Pacific high coman., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., June, 1915; clk. in ch., acts., W. Pacific, Sept., 1919; ch. clk. and acctnt., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high commn. for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1925 and 1927-29; sec., Western Pacific high commn., Dec., 1929.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L.S. & A.—B. 1891; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; ed. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripus), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; ast. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the roun., July, 1924; ag. registr., high ct., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; res. mag., July, 1929; ag. judge in 1931, 1932 and 1933; ast. atty.-gen., 1933 and 1934; ag. atty.-gen. in 1934 and 1935; comsnr. for revision of laws, 1934; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1936 to Oct., 1937, and in 1939; author of "The Dual Jurisdiction in Zanzibar" (1935).

VEAL, CYRIL JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1893; ed. S.A. Coll. Schl. and S.A. Coll., Cape Town, war serv. with Union forces in W. Africa (1914) and Imp. forces in E. Africa (1915-18); Union Defence Force, 1912-26, and on R. of O. with rank of capt.; joined mines dept., 1925, as ast. inspr.; dep. inspr., 1929; inspr., 1935.
VEERASAWMY, JOHN ALPHUS.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple), 1913; ag. stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1922; stip. mag., 1928.

VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1891; ed. Campbell Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1917; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1921; 2nd mag., S'pore, 1923; 2nd ast. sec. (A), col. sect., S'pore, 1924; Br. res., Brunei, 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; mag., Seremban, 1927; dist. offr., Upper Perak, 1929; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; Br. advr., Perlis, July 1932; dist. offr., K. Kangsar, May, 1936; offr. cls. I.B., Jan., 1937; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1938.

VENNING, GUR, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; 2nd offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENOUR, LOUIS STEPHEN DOUGLAS HAMILTON.—B. 1885; ed. pvly.; capt., 5th batt., Middx. Regt.; ast. comsnr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; comsnr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1922; ag. comsnr., pol. C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; senr. comsnr., pol., 1929; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., on various occasions, 1925-33.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, essay. and cust., Br. Guiana, 1936; ag. ch. just., Nov.-Dec., 1936; 1st puisne judge, 1937; ag. atty.-gen., July 1937; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1938; ch. just., Zanzibar, 1940.
VERNALL, RICHARD JOHN, A.R.I.B.A., M.I. Struct. E.—B. 1901; ed. Marling Schl., Stroud, archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; ag. tech. sec., P.W.D., 1934; Lieut. Cmdr., H.K.N.V.F.
VERRALL, ROBERT JOHN WYNDHAM.—B. 1896; ed. Warninglid and Cologne Coll.; B.S.A. pol., 1920; N.R. pol., 1928; ast. supt., 1929; supt., 1931; dep. commr., 1937.

VIADER, RENE, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; registr., col. sec.'s office, May, 1919; finan. ast., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1925; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. ast. col. sec., 1925-30 and Apr., 1932.

VICKERS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. & H. (Camb.), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Gram. Schl. and Birmingham Med. Schl.; Gt. War, Br. Expdy. Foe., 1916-19; res. staff, gen. hosp., B'mgham, 1923-25; med. and health offr., Malay States, 1925-38; snr. med. offr., dept. of health, Palestine, 1938.

VICKERS-HAVILAND, L. A. W.—B. 1895; lieut., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; seconded, 2nd K.A.R., 1918; ast. polit. offr., 1920; admstve. offr., 2nd grade, 1922; ast. dist. offr., 1926; addl., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 1930; dist. offr., 1931; seconded as ast. to dir., agr., 1932-33; ag. dep. prov. comsnr., 1939.

VIGNE, CHIDLOW.—B. 1900; M.Sc. (Yale), B.A. (Oxon.), dip. for. (Oxon.) Worcester Coll.; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ast. consvr., forests, Gold Coast, Mar., 1922; commonwealth fund fellow, 1930-32, Yale Univ.; sylviculturist, Gold Coast, Oct., 1933; sr. ast. consvr., forests, July, 1937.

VILJOEN, PHILIPPUS RUDOLPH, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., Dr. Med. Vet. (Berne).—B. 1889; ent. dept. of agr. as ast. vety. bacteriologist, Onderstepoort, Pretoria, 1913; vety. research offr., Onderstepoort and Vryburg, 1914; seconded to dept. of defence, Oct., 1914; retd. to dept. of agr., June, 1915; resigned, Jan., 1919; reapptd. dept. of agr. as senr. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. research and prof. in vety. medicine, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Apr., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services, 1927; under-sec., agr., Jan., 1931; sec. agr., May, 1933; chmn., national marketing coun., 1937.
VINCENT, STEWART LEE.—B. 1902; ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1926; supt., Feb., 1936.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., (Lond.).—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's Schl., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISSEER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; ast. mag. and ag. civil comsnr., 1902-05; clk.-ast., Cape House of Assembly, 8th Apr., 1907; clk.-ast., House of Assem., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assembly, 1st Oct., 1920.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, 1916; 3rd ast. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919; ast. dist. offr., 1920-21; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, 1922; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; addl., col. seca. office, S'pore, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1926; ag. dist. offr., B. Padang, July, 1927 to Sept., 1928; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1929; supt., 1931 census, Malaya, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. II, Dec., 1931; economy offr., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1932; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., 1934; offr. cls. I.B., Oct., 1934; finan. comsrr. and aud. gen., Johore, July, 1936; food contr., Malaya, July, 1938; sec. for defence, Malaya, Nov., 1938.
VOLLET, DENTS HENRI RENE.—B. 1898; served in Great War; med. offr., Basutoland, 1927.

VON BONDE, CECIL, Ph.D., M.A., F.R.S.S.Af.—Ed. Univ. of Cape Town; senr. lect. in Zoology, Univ. of Cape Town, 1918; awarded "Purcell Memorial Prize" for Zoology in 1924; ag. prof., Zoology, Univ. of Cape Town, 1926; dir. fisheries survey and govt. marine biologist, Union of S. Africa, 1928; seconded for marine biolog. survey to conferees of E. African govs. in Kenya and Zanzibar, 1928.

VOSS, GEORGE DU TOIT, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Advocate of the Sup. Ct., of S. Africa.—B. 1884; ast. reg. offr., sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal prov. divn.), 1918; reg. high ct. of S.W. Africa, 1919; atty.-gen., S.W. Africa, 1925; solr. gen., Eastern dists., Cape Prov., 1931; law adviser, dept. of just., Nov., 1931.

WACHER, LAWRENCE FORD, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1884; ed. Waldon House Schl., Herne Bay, Kent Coast Coll. and Agrl. Coll., Wye; mem. staff, Wye Coll., 1910; agrl. offr., Basutoland, 1911; war serv., 1915-19; 2nd lieut., H.C.H.B., 1915; lieut., 1916; ag. capt. and adjt. 2nd D.A.C., 1918; returned to Basutoland, 1920.
WADDELL, CECIL JAMES, B.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen's Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; war serv., 1915-18; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; seconded, Kowloon-Canton rly., 1931-32.

WADDINGTON, SIR E. J., K.C.M.G. (1939), C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1919).—Ast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. comsrr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. ast. sec. secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; ast. sec., govt.'s conferees, 1926; res. comsrr., Mombasa, 1928; col. sec., Bermuda, 1932; do., Br. Guiana, 1935; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Barbados, 1938; ag. gov. Br. Guiana, 1939.
WADDINGTON, HARRY, B.Sc.—B. 1893; mily. serv., 1915-18; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); dist. offr., 1928; ast. judge, prot. ct., 1934.

WADE, SIR A. DE V., Kt. Bach (1937), C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtn. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; ast. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native comsrr., Kenya, 1926; prin. ast., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928; ch. native comsrr., 1932; ag. col. sec., 1933; col. sec., 1934; ag. gov., 1935 and 1936.

WADE, CAPT. T. G. ROCHEPORT, M.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1901; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Columba's Coll., Dublin; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1926; M.A., 1930; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, May, 1931; C.O., St. Lucia Volunteer Force; headmast., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1938.
WADIA, DARASHAW NOSHPAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S.B.—B. 1883; govt. mineralogist, Ceylon, Nov., 1938.

WADIA, SIYAXH HIRJI, B.A. (Bombay), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attd., Jaffna Kach., June, 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1915; finan. ast. to chrmn., munici comsrr., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; ast. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addnl. ast. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; ast. comsrr., stamps, Jan., 1926; ast. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1931; ast. govt. agt. Matara, Dec., 1932; offr. cls. I, Mar., 1936; excise comsrr., Mar., 1938.
WADLEY, WALTER JOSEPH DURHAM, M.A.—B. 1903; ed. City of London Schl.; class schol., Lincoln Coll., Oxford; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; senr. inspr., schls., July, 1937.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Makran, S. Persia; transfd. to Bengal pol., 1901 on sp.; famine operns., 1906-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egyt. and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. commt., of pol. Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAKEFIELD, ARTHUR JOHN, B.Sc., N.D.A., N.D.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Schl., Harper Adams Agrl. Coll., Reading and Edinburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Infy., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., in 1933 and 1935-6; dep. dir., agr., 1935; dir., agr., 1938.

WAKEFIELD, BRIG.-GEN. HUBERT STEPHEN, O.B.E. (Mil.) 1919—B. 1883; ed. privte. schl. and Ellesmere Coll.; joined Cape Mtd. Riflemen, 1901; S.A. war, 1899-02 (Queen's med., 3 clasps); lieut., C.M.R., 1912; lieut., 1st regt., S.A.M.R., 1913; operns. in G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15; adjs., 1st Regt., S.A.M.R., 1915-16; capt., 1st S.A. Infy. (Overseas), 1917; operns. France and Belgium, 1917-18; capt., 1st Res. Batt., S.A. Infy. and seconded for sp. employment, 1918; D.A.Q.M.G. and temp. major, 1918; operns. Persia, 1918-19; ment. in desps., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory med.; on staff of O.C., Union garrison troops, Cape Peninsula, with temp. rank of capt., 1921; D.A.A.G. (later staff offr.) defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1921; capt., 1922; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; maj., do., 1929; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Cape Town, with temp. rank of lt.-col., 1931; lt.-col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1931; O.C. No. 4 mil. dist., Johannesburg, 1932; adjt.-gen., U.D.F., 1934; temp. col., 1934; col., 1935; brig.-gen., Sept., 1937; dep. ch., gen. staff, retaining appt. of adjt.-gen., Sept., 1937.

WAKEFIELD, THOMAS CLEAVE, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), L.M., Rotunda Hosp., Dublin, grad., London S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1893; med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; med. offr., European hosps., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1929; med. offr. in ch., Tan Toek Seng Hosp., S'pore, Oct., 1935; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Feb., 1936.

WAKEHURST, 2nd Baron (Cr. 1934), CAPT. JOHN DE VERE LODER, K.C.M.G.—B. 1895; ed. Eton; 4th R. Sussex Regt. and Intell. Corps, 1914-19; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (desps.); clk., F.O., 1919-21; M.P., E. Leicestershire, 1924-29; do., Lewes, E. Sussex, 1931-36; gov. and commr.-in-ch., New South Wales, 1937.

WALCOTT, EDWARD KEITH, K.C. (1937)—B. 1892; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; war serv., 1915-18; solr., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1921; solr.-gen., Barbados, 1930; ag. att.-gen., various periods, 1930-34; also M.H.A.; atty.-gen., July, 1936.
WALCOTT, JAMES ETRE, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1894; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Glasgow Univ.; govt. bacteriologist and pathologist, Barbados, 1934.

WALKER, ALAN ERNEST, A.M.I.E., Aust.; Chartered Engrnr., Aust.—B. 1906; ed. Bendigo High schl. and Bendigo schl. of Mines, Victoria, Australia; tempy. asst. engrnr. P.W.D., Cyprus, May, 1936; ag. divnl. engrnr., various periods, 1936-38; asst. engrnr., Oct., 1938; ex. engrnr., Jan., 1939.

WALKER, GEOFFREY, F.C.R.A. (1913)—B. 1889; ed. Market Harborough Gram. Schol. and Lond. Univ.; apptd. after competitive exam., 2nd divn.clk. Jan., 1908; apptd. after competitive exam., examr., Exchequer & Audit dept., 1911; on mily. serv., 2nd City of Lond. Yeomanry, 1914-19; seconded, K.A.R., 1917-19; ment. in desps.: audr., E. & A.D., 1920; senr. do., 1924; dep. dir. audit, 1933; regional offr., unemployment assist. bd., 1934; offr., coll. acts., C.O., 1936; hon. treas., civ. serv. sports coun., since 1931.
WALKER, GEORGE BLYTH, M.B., Ch.B.(Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.), certif. of London S.T.M.—B. 1899; med.offr., Nigeria, 1922; M.O.H., 1924; senr. health offr., 1927; dep. dir., health service, 1938.
WALKER, HENRY JAMES, M.B.E.—B. 1891; home civ. serv., 1907-14; Uganda, 1914; office asst. to ch. engrnr., 1928.

WALKER, HUBERT EDMUND, O.B.E. (1937), M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1919; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., 1926; supr. native admintr. wks., 1929; dep. D.P.W., 1935; ag. D.P.W., 1937 and 1938.
WALKER, IVAN LAWRENCE.—B. 1883; ch. inspr., dept. of lab., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1924; under sec. for lab., Sept., 1929; sec. for lab., May, 1932.
WALKER, J. W., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1929.

WALKER, CAPT. REGINALD DAVID, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.T., Chtd. civ. engrnr.—B. 1892; ed. R. Coll. Science, and City and Guilds Engrng. Coll., Whitworth Exhibr., 1913; field cov., R.E., France, Salonika, Egypt, 1915-18; capt., 1917; R.O.D., R.E., Egypt, 1918-20; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1920; ag. dist. engrnr., 1923; dist. engrnr., grade II, 1925; mntrc. asst. engrnr., wks. and wks., 1926 and 1932; dist. engrnr., grade I, 1929; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., 1932; ag. estate and survey offr., F.M.S.R., 1933; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton Rly., Hong Kong, 1934; maj., H.K. Vol. Defence Corps, 1935.

WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S., Rlys., 1914-18; asst. acct., do., 1918; dep. acct., Jan., 1926; ag. chief acct., various periods, 1926-34; ch. acct., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbrs., 1935.
WALKER, RICHARD, A.R.C.A. (Lond.)—Ed. Royal Coll. of Art; art supt., S.S. Dec., 1923.

WALKER, ROBERT PHILIP SEBASTIAN, M.A., (Oxon.).—B. 1898; attd. Eng. Coll., Johore, Nov., 1920; attd., govt. Eng. schl., Muar, Feb., 1921, Eng. coll., Johore Bahru, June, 1922; Raffles Inst., Spore, Apr., 1924; Malay coll., K. Kangsar, May, 1924; govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1925; King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Sept., 1926; headmast., Eng. schl., Johore, Dec., 1930; prin., King Ed. VII schl., Taiping, Oct., 1931; senr. eduon. offr., grade A, Malayan eduon. serv., 1937.
WALKER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER RIDDLER.—B. 1906; ed. St. Alban's Schol. and Glasgow Univ.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; asst. dist. comanr., 1931.

WALKINGSHAW, RICHARD, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.T.M., D.T.H. (L'pool), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1898; ed. Airdrie Acad.; M.O., Malaya, July, 1927; ag. surg., Penang, Nov., 1930; ag. surg., Perak, Dec., 1937; surg., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1938.

WALL, ERNEST, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Worcester Royal Grammar Schol.; R.E., 1915-19; capt.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nyasaland, 1921; exec. engrnr., 1929; ag. D.P.W., May, 1931 to Feb., 1932 and 1934-35; senr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1937.
WALL, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1900; supt., prisons, Trinidad, Nov., 1932.

WALLACE, GEORGE BRUNTON, B.Sc. (agr.), Ph.D.—B. 1899; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh; mily. serv. R.G.A., 1918-19; B.Sc., 1922; Ph. D., 1925; senr. demonstr. in agr. and forest entomology, univ. of Edinburgh, 1922-23; asst. in plant pathology, bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1924-25; micrscopist, game pres. dept., Tanganyika territory, 1925; plant pathologist, dept. of agr., 1927.

WALLACE, HORACE BLAKE.—Natal civ. serv., 1896; mily. serv., native rebellion, 1906; German S.W. and E. Africa, 1914-16; mag., Mobisa, 1918; add. mag., Durban, 1923; mag., Pinetown, 1928; Greytown, 1929; Ikopo, 1930; add. mag., Johannesburg, 1937.

WALLACE, JOHN ANDREW LESLIE, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), 1903, M.D., 1909, F.R.A.C.P., 1938.—B. 1876; asst. phys., Crichton-Dumfries, 1906-10; N.S.W. mental hosps. dept., 1910; med. supt., Callan Park, 1922; inspr.-gen., mental hosp., 1935.
WALLACE, SIR LAWRENCE AUBREY, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1910).—Acting admtr., N.E. Rhodesia, May, 1907; ag. admtr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admtr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.

WALLACE, THEODORE DAVID, B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law (King's Inn).—B. 1906; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; practised, S.-Western circuit, 1930-34; crown coun., Kenya, 1934; ag. solr.-gen. and King's proctor and nom. mem., leg. coun., Sept., 1936-Mar., 1937 and from Mar., 1937; ag. atty.-gen., 1938.
WALLER, GEORGE RONALD, M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.) D.P.H. (Eng.), Cert. of L.S.T. Med.—Med. offr., Nigeria, 1924; senr. health offr., 1928; A.D.M.S. (health), Sierra Leone, 1938.

WALLINGTON, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1917-1919; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1924; headmast., Yaumati schl., 1936-37; ag. prin., King's Coll., 1939.
WALLIS, KENNETH.—B. 1901; ed. Epworth Coll., Ryland Univ. Coll., Nottingham; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1923; A.L.C., 1924; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad and Tobago, 1929; govt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1933.

WALMSLEY, CUTHBERT HOPPER, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.M. and Cy.K., M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1882; ed. Eccles Grammar Schol.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906; Kenya, Magadi water wks., 1913; war serv. in B.E.A., G.F.A. and P.E.A.; ment. in desps., 1916, 1917, 1918; ent. col. serv., 1922; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1922; ag. contracts offr., 1924; exec. engrnr., 1925; ag. asst. dir. in 1926 and 1930-31; ag. hydraulic engrnr., 1931-32; ag. suptdg. engr. in 1933 and 1934; senr. exec. engrnr., 1937; ag. D.P.W., 1937.

WALSH, DUDLEY J.C.—B. 1895; ed. Merchant Taylors Schol.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Jan., 1933.

WALSH, CAPT. (Hony.) EDGAR STANLEY.—B. 1893; ed. Bradford Grammar schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1914-19., mentd. in desps.; admstr. offr. cls. IV, Nigeria, 1919; cls. III, 1927; ag. res., Apr.-June, 1936; admstr. offr. cls. II, 1937; ag. res., Oct., 1938-Apr., 1939.

WALSH, PATRICK JOSEPH STANISLAS, Ph.D., K.C.—B.1872; ed. Clermont and Paris (France), Innsbruck (Tyrol), Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, King's Inns, June, 1898; K.C., Feb., 1910; Ph.D. (metaphysics and ethics, ancient languages and comparative philology, maths.); junr. and senr. crown pros., N.W. circuit and winter assizes, 1906-20; war serv., 1915-1918; ag. county ct. judge for two circuits, midland counties; dep. recorder, Galway; dep. county ct. judge for counties Kerry, Tipperary and West Riding of Cork; pres., Br. pensions appl. tribunal (Ireland), Mar., 1920-May, 1929; pres., dist. ots. Cyprus, 1929-31; ch. just., Seychelles, 1931; O.A.G., Seychelles, Dec., 1931-May, 1932.

WALTER, A., O.B.E. (1935), F.R.A.S.—Join magnet and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comm., 1909; census comm., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir. Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imigrts. and poor law comm., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs., confere., 1926; dir. Br. E. African meteorological serv., 1928; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, ARTHUR WILLoughby.—B. 1900; ed. Cordwallis Prep. Schl., Michaelhouse; secr. clk., Swaziland, 1919; dist. admstr., Swaziland, 1920; treasy. clk., Swaziland, 1922; transf. as treasy. clk., grade I, to Basutoland, 1928; ag. finan. sec. in 1933, 1936 and 1937; acct., 1934.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., Dec., 1911; att. Galle kach., May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929; ag. govt. agt., N.W.P., Apr., 1931; do. E.P., Nov., 1932; addnl. P.M.G., July, 1933; P.M.G., Sept., 1933; offr., cls. I, gde. I, 1938.

WALTON, BYRON STAGG.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1913; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Spore, Mar., 1914; ag. censor, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., Spore V.R., Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. govt. monops., Spore, Nov., 1916; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Dec., 1917; ag. asst. supt. govt. monops., Penang, Aug., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., K. Selangor, May, 1924; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; supt., lands, Kelantan, Aug., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1928; ag. collr., land rev. and ast. treas., Malacca, May, 1929; cls. II, Nov., 1930; 1st ast. sec. (A), S.S.—Aug., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Jan., 1934; ag. advr., land office, Kedah, May, 1936.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt., 1910-20; treas. gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, KT. BACH. (1925).—B.1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926; ret., 1931.
WALWYN, ALGERNON EDWARD VERE.—B. 1888; ed. Bath Coll. and Peterhouse, Camb.; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, Apr., 1915; res., Sept., 1932; sec., N. Provs., 1933; staff grade, 1937.

WALWYN, VICE ADMIRAL SIR HUMPHREY THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1939), K.C.S.I. (1923), C.B. (1928), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1879; ed. H.M.S. Britannia, Dartmouth; went to sea, H.M.S. Camperdown, 1895; gunnery lieut., 1904; commodr., 1912; H.M.S. Warspite, 1915-17; capt., 1916; in commd., destroyer flotillas and senr. offr., Mediterranean destroyers, 1923; dir. gunnery divn., naval staff, admry., 1924-26; commanded H.M.S. Valiant, 1926; flag offr. commodr. R. Indian Navy, 1928-34; rear-adm., 1928; vice-adm., 1932; ret. list, 1934; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1936.

WAMSLEY, R. W., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; clk., civ. commr.'s office, Kimberley, 1903; inland rev. dept., 1911; prin. clk., 1921; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1921; recr., rev., Bloemfontein, 1931; ch. rev. offr., 1932; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1933; asst. commr. for inland rev., June, 1938; commr. do., Apr., 1939.
WAN, IU-SHING, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1892; inspr., vernacular schla., Hong Kong, 1928.

WANN, MAJOR JAMES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B.1890; ed. Dulwich Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; mily. serv., 1915-1919; ment. in desps.; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1920; dist. offr., 1928; admstr. offr., cls. II, 1936; do., cls. I, 1938.

WARD, ERNEST RUKUL LA TOURETTE, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; pol. mag., dist. "A," Barbados, 1931; ag. registr., and judge, ast. ct. of appl., various periods, 1932-33; judge, petty debt ct., Bridgetown, 1936; regiar., 1938.

WARD, ESPIRE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1916 to Feb., 1916; temp. lient. gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri: Etiology, Symptoms, and Treatment," "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.)

WARD, E.R.—B. 1902; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Coll. of Estate Management, Lincoln's Inn Fields; cadet, Nigeria, 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1929; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., S. Provs., Nov. 1930-May, 1931; ditto to gov., July, 1931-Mar., 1934.
WARD, FRANK DORRINGTON, F.R.I.B.A., M.R.San.I.—B. 1885; ed. Kent Coll., Canterbury; P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1920; govt. architect, S.S., Nov., 1928; registr., bd. of archts., S.S., Jan., 1932.

WARD, JOSEPH CORBET, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.) I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Sotik expdn. (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. registr., docs., land dept., K.A.P., 1905; attd. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1915-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. lient. col. (desps. 3 times, 3 war medals); staff offr. and intell.offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencies, London, 1926.

WARD, LENNOX, K.C., B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope).—Called to bar, 1909; prof. ass't., atty.-gen., Cape, 1910; prof. ass't., solr.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926; judge-pres., native high ct., 1933.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Grammar Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl., entd. Univ. of Sydney, graduated in arts, 1900, and in engnr., 1903; lect. in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; asst. geologist and inspr., mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served, France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lances, Fusiliers May, 1915; seconded, M.G.C., France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; asst. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. ast. treas. F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Dec., 1930; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1933; pub. trustee and offi. admstr., F.M.S., Mar., 1934; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1936; offr., cls. III., Aug., 1937.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. hon., B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDLE, JAMES MONTE, B.Sc., C.E., M.E.I.C.—B. 1888; ed. Br. Columbia Pub. Schls. and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.S., 1912; engnr. on locn. and constrn., Canadian N. Pac. Rly., Br. Columbia; highway engnr., nat. parks, Canada, dept. of intr., 1914-18; supr., Banff nat. park, Alta., 1918-21; ch. engnr., nat. parks, Canada, 1921-35; western superv., nat. parks, 1932-35; dep. min., dept. of intr., Ottawa, 1935; dir., surveys and engnr. br., dept., mines and resources, 1936.

WARE, THOMAS WALTER, M.B., Ch.B. Bristol, D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1890; med. offr., Bristol; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, in ch., subord. staff families, Kennedy Town infec. dis. hosp. and asst., Victoria hosp., 1927; in ch., civ. hosp., 1928; visiting med. offr., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries, 1930; 2nd port health offr. and inspr., immigrts., Sept., 1932 and Nov. 1933; ag. port health offr., etc., 1933; visiting health offr., Nov., 1934; health offr., Kowloon and New Territories, June, 1937; ag. port health offr. and inspr., immigrts., Aug., 1937 to Aug., 1938; health offr., urb. cl., 1939; ag. D.D.H.S., Aug., 1939.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1914; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19; supt., pol., 1936.

WARREN, JOHN JOSEPH, O.B.E.(1939)—B. 1880; R.I.C., 1910-13; inspr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst., comsnt., pol., Mar., 1921; dir., crim. intel., May, 1926; head prev. serv., S'pore, Nov., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., Sept., 1929; senr. asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1932; senr. dep. comsnt., excise, 1934; ag. comsnt., cust. and excise, S.S., 1935.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ. Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice mentd. in desps.; French Croix de Guerre; asst. dist. comsnt., G. Coast, 1919; dist. comsnt., Apr., 1924.

WASE-BALLEY, BASIL NOEL VICTOR, M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1926, D.P.H. (Eng.), 1929.—B. 1899; med. serv., B. Guiana, 1926; G.M.O., pub. hosp.; ag. govt. bact., 1926; asst. G.M.O.H., personal asst. to surg.-gen. and dep. port health offr., 1927; govt. med. offr. of health, surg. gen.'s dep. and port health offr., 1931; immigrtn. agt.-gen., regtr.-gen., 1935; ag. chmn., poor law comsnt., 1935; dep. ch., local govt. bd., 1937; ag. surg.-gen., 1937; ch. med. offr., Barbados, 1938.

WATERER, RALPH RONALD, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1902; passed B.A. in estate management and dipl. forestry, Cambridge Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1928; conserv., forests, 1937.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; math. schol., Caius Coll., 1899; maths. tripos, 1902; law tripos, 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922; judge of appl., 1937.

WATERMEYER, THEODORE HEINRICH.—B. 1879; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Old Gymnasium, Stellenbosch, Bath Coll., Bath and Mason's Univ. Coll. (now Birmingham Univ.), M.Inst.C.E., appt., Cape govt. rlys., 1902; asst. ch. civ. engnr., 1920; rep. S. African Rlys. at Internat. Rly. Congress, Rome, 1922; asst. gen. man., Bloemfontein, 1925; asst. gen. man. for Cape suburban electrification, Capetown, and consulting engnr., 1927; asst. gen. man., Johannesburg, 1928; gen. man., 1933.

WATERS, Harold Bertram B.A., Oxon.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Guildford and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; served in The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regt. and Signal Serv., R.E., 1914-19; supt., agric., Nigeria, Mar., 1921; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to report on oil-palm industry, 1926; dep. asst. dir., agric., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; comsrr., Canadian national exhib., 1928; dep. dir., agr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; dir., agr., Kenya, 1933; do., Gold Coast, 1939.

WATERSON, Sidney Frank.—B. 1896; ed. St. Clare, Walmer and Westminster (King's schol.); served European War, 1915-19; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Spec. Res.) R. Sussex Regt. attd. Machine Gun Corps, Salonika, 1916, France, 1917-18; M.P. (Union of S. Africa) for S. Peninsula divn., 1929-33; envoy extraordinary for Union of S. Africa to France, 1939; high comsrr. for Union of S. Africa in London since 1939.

WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Schl.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925; med. offr., Kenya, 1934.

WATSON, Andrew D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; contrib. to jnl. of Inst. of Actuaries and transactions of Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, Donald Francis, O.B.E., (1926),—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comittee, 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. asupt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bld., and chmn., proffitering comittee, July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., comsrr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, registr. of shipping, Apr., 1923.
WATSON, Ernest Sibbles, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, H.C., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., R.U., Ireland.—B. 1882; ed. Campbell Coll. and Queen's Coll., Belfast; Transvaal civ. serv., July, 1909; physn. supt., Fort Beaumont hosp., 1919; Alexandra instn., Cape Town, 1921; Bloemfontein ment. hosp., 1923.

WATSON, Hon. Herbert Gordon, I.S.O. (1922)—C.I.C., gov. of Zululand's office, 1895; ag. sec. for Zululand at different times, 1896; clk., gov't. office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf'd. Cape civ. serv. and apptd. clk., gov't. office, Mar., 1901; ch. clk., July, 1904; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., major-gen. E. S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, K.C.V.O., Dec., 1909; asst. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, James Gilbert.—B. 1889; ed. Kgl. Preuss. Forstakademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; ag. dir. forestry, S.S. and advr., forestry, M.S., June, 1937.

WATSON, Noel Duncan, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1915; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl. and schol. of New Coll., Oxford; admstr. offr. (asst. sec., col. sec.'s off.), Cyprus, Oct., 1938; elk., exec. coun., Sept., 1939.

WATSON, Oswald, L.S.—B. 1891; survey cadet, N.S.W. and N. Guinea, Sept., 1910-14; milt. serv., survey sec., R.E. and topo. corp., A.L.F., 1915-18; loc. land survr., Australia and N.Z., Sept., 1919; survr., S.S., Apr., 1920; do. F.M.S., Jan., 1921; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; ag. supt., Aug., 1936; dep. ch. survr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1938; dep. ch. survr., Kinta, Oct., 1938.

WATSON, P. O. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; mil. serv., 1917-18; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. asst. treas., 1930; acct.-gen. and currency offr., Sept., 1938.

WATSON, William Law, A.M.I.C.E., M. Coun. Inst. Loco. Engrs.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engrg. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engrg. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrg. (mech.), 1928; ch. engrg. (contracts), 1935.
WATT, Cecil James.—B. 1899; under secy., dept. of pub. health, N.S.W., Nov., 1938.

WATT, Michael Herbert, C.B.E. (1935), M.D. (N.Z.), D.P.H. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ. (N.Z.); med. offr. of health, 1917; asst. ch. health offr., 1920; dep. dir.-gen. of health, 1925; dir.-gen., 1931; also comsrr., social security dept., 1939.

WATT, Sir Thomas, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish ct.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WAITS, Chiv.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; ag. asst. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan., 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., for various periods, 1928-33; offr. rec. in bankruptcy, Mar. to June, 1931; Br. del. of Evcaf. Nov., 1933; treas., Mauritius, 1934; finan. sec., Nov., 1939; ag. col. sec., Jan. to Dec., 1939.

WATTS, Guy Tracky.—B. 1892; ed. Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Apr., 1914; war serv., 1915-18; crown counsel, Nigeria, Mar., 1925; pol. mag., Calabar, 1923; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1930; chmn., bd. of educn. mem., leg. coun.; ag. ch. just., in 1930, 1931 and 1932; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1933.
WAUCHOPE, Major-Gen. Sir Arthur Grenfell, G.C.B. (1936); G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.B.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. pte. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29 and 1932; ag. ch. asst. sec. in 1930 and 1933; ag. consmr., in 1931, 1934 and 1935; admst. offr.; asst. sec., Jan., 1935; consmr., Mar., 1935.

WEATHERHEAD, HENRY DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1899; served in Great War, 1916-19; senr. res. surg., B'dos gen. hosp., 1926; M.O., Leeward Is., Dec., 1926; asst. surg., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1931; med. supt., Holberton hosp., Antigua, 1933; senr. M.O., St. Lucia, Nov., 1933.
WEAVING, L. A.—Asst. dist. consmr., Kenya, June, 1921; ag. sec., 1930.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prize man and John Brooke scholarship, King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres. dist. ct., Samaria, 1921; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933; ch. just., Sierra Leone, 1937; ch. just., Tanganyika, 1939.

WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, June, 1923; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.
WEBB, HON. PATRICK CHARLES.—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1913-18 and since 1933; min. of mines, 1935; min. of labour, 1938.

WEBBER, FERNLEY DOUGLAS, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1918; ed. Cotham Sch., Bristol and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; major open hist. schol. and Goldsmiths' exhibnr. (2nd cls. hon. hist. tripods); apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1939.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admntr., Oct., 1916; administr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. consmr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. consmr., Jan., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. consmr., Feb., 1928; prov. consmr., junr. grade, May, 1929; senr. prov. consmr., Nov., 1934; offl. mem., leg. coun., 1935 and 1936.

WEBSTER, GEORGE HENRY, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1887; Br. post office, 1903-14; war serv., 1915-20; twice ment. in desp.; asst. dir., posts and tels., Palestine, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G., numerous occasions; dep. P.M.G., 1935.

WEBSTER, HAROLD JOHN.—B. 1892; served S. Africa Rebellion, S.W. Africa and German E. Africa campaigns (ment. in desps.); asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Nov., 1929.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mg., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settlmt. offr. Jan., 1911; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Orde," Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Orde," Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settlmt. offr., July, 1920; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; govt. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929; els. I, grade I, 1931; govt., agt., W. Prov., Mar., 1931; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; offr. apptd. to report on poor relief, Aug., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1934; ag. ch. sec., Oct. 1934-Mar., 1935 and June-Nov., 1935; ch. sec., Jan., 1936; O.A.G., June-Nov., 1937.

WEGENER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Crossley Schl., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rlys., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S. rly., 1922; worksman, loco. dept., F.M.S.rlys., July, 1928; asst. ch. mech., engrnr., Oct., 1932; ch. mech. engrnr., F.M.S.rlys., Oct., 1937.
WEIGALL, CECIL EDWARD, K.C., B.A. (Oxon.), LL.B. (Syd.)—B. 1870; solr. gen. and asst. law offr., N.S.W., Dec., 1922.

WEIGALL, Lieut.-Col. Sir William Ernest George Archibald, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Horncastle Div., Lines., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem. select comm. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., fedn. of county agric. comtees; J.P., Kent and Lines.; gov., S. Australia, 1920-22.

WEIR, ANDREW HERON WILSON, B.Sc.Edin.—B. 1893; ed. Viewpark Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (R.F.A., U.K., India and Mesopotamia); men. in desps.; asst. conserv., forests, Nigeria, Aug., 1920; conserv., forests, col. forest serv., May, 1935; ag. ch. conserv., foresta, Nigeria, July, 1935-July, 1936; Mar.-June, 1938; Nov., 1938-Sept., 1939; author of "Gum Arabic in Bornu Province," and "Report on Gum Arabic in Anglo-Egyptian Sudan."

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripods.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., May, 1920; mag., July, 1922; ag. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929; asst. pub. trustee's office, Dec., 1930; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., offr. cls II, Nov., 1931; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1933; offr. cls. Ib, July, 1935; treas., S.S. Dec., 1935; finan. sec., S.S., 1937.
WELCH, T. B.—B. 1898; ed. Brighton Coll.; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; asst. divn. supt. (loco.), 1927.

WELDON, LEO FRANCOIS, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Stonyhurst and Univ. Coll., London Univ.; lieut. R.F.A., 1915-19; asst. engr., Min. of Transport, 1924-26; temp. asst. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1931; asst. engr., 1936; ag. divn. engr., P.W.D., June to Sept., 1932; exec. engr., Jan., 1939.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; prof. assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1921; law advr., 1926; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1929.

WELSH, GORDON JAMES.—B. 1894; ed. Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schl., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pvt., 1/4th Batt. The Buffs and 2nd lieut. The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; clk. to gov., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. clk., Apr., 1923; asst. sec. to gov., Somaliland, Mar., 1929; ag. dist. comsnr., Burao dist., Aug., 1929; asst. sec. in ch., secretariat on various occasions, 1929-1935; ret., 1936; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1938 (western circ. and C.C.C.); temp. admin. offr., C.O., Feb., 1940.
WEMYSS AND MARCH, EARL OF, FRANCIS DAVID CHARTERIS, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1912; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr. (cadet), Basutoland, 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939.
WENTWORTH, MAURICE FRANK GERARD, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., London (teacher's dipl., Lond.); inspr., schls., Aug., 1930.
WENTZEL, LOEWYK MARTINUS.—B. 1887; M.P.C., Wolmaransstad, 1923; M.P., Delarey, 1929; chmn., West bd., 1935; chmn., cent. land bd., Mar., 1939.

WEST, HAROLD.—B. 1882; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1910; lieut., R.G.A., 1917-19; lat. cls. land survr., 1920; prin. land survr., Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; 1st asst. aspt., crown lands, 1926; asst. aspt., crown lands, 1932; aspt., crown lands and surveys, 1938.

WESTON, HERBERT JOHN, M.Sc. (Agr.) S. Africa, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.H.S.—B. 1907; ed. Ashford Grammar Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; horticulturist (now supt., agr.), Cyprus, 1931; sec. as asst. comsnr., Dec., 1937; asst. comsnr., May, 1939.

WESTON, ERNEST ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1880; B.A. Cantab., 1902; served with R.E. during Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1914; lieut.-col., 1916; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1907 and served in various posts under Nigerian govt. until 1927 when retd. on med. grounds; on staff of Imp. Economic Comtee., 1927-31; comsnr., Cayman Is., Dec., 1931; comsnr., Cyprus, 1933.

WESTON, FREDERICK THOMAS.—B. 1908; ed. Ebbo Vale (Mon.) and Port Talbot (Glam.) secondary schls. and Univ. Coll., Cardiff, B.A. (hon.), Wales, 1929; dipl. ed., 1930; asst. mst., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1935.

WESTON, HARRY CHARLES, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond., Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war surv., 1914-19; ch. asst. engr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engr., Dar-es-Salam, 1925, prin., Accra Tech. Schl., May, 1929.

WHARELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; on war surv., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in despa., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W. M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; asst. comdt., pol., Bahamas, Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol., impr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., Aug., 1926.

WHINDUS, ERNEST JAMES.—Warserv., S.W. and E. African campaigns, 1914-17; clk., treas., N. Rhodesia, 1917; asst. treas., 1922; senr. asst. treas., 1935; ag. dep. treas., 1935 and 1936; contr., stores and transp., 1937; contr., stores and transp., 1938.

WHISKARD, SIR GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; asst. pvt. sec. and subsequently prin. pvt. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; asst. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., overseas settlement c'tee., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. under sec. of State, D.O. in addn., 1st Jan., 1931; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; high comsnr. in the C'wealth of Australia for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1936.

WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B.1899; ent. C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 1917; on mil. serv., Jan., 1918 to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; clor. offr., higher grade, Apr., 1927.
WHITE, ALBERT FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1930; passed cadet, 1931; dist. commr., Fiji, 1934; re-designated dist. offr., 1938.

WHITE, ALBERT GEORGE.—B. 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., H.M.S.O. Mar., 1920; transf. to C.O., Mar., 1928; accompanied U.K. delegn. to Imp. economic confce., Ottawa, 1932; seconded as regis. on staff of high comsnr. in S. Africa for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1934-37.

WHITE, ARTHUR REGINALD, M.C.—B. 1886; ch. draftsmen, Kelantan, May, 1912; on milly. serv. 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, Kelantan, Nov., 1918; supt., surveys, Jan., 1923; supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1929; supt., surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1931; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1933; senr. supt., survey, Perak, Aug., 1935; dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Nov., 1937.
WHITE, GEORGE, B.So. (Eng.), A.M.I.Mech.E., Chart. Mech. Engr.—B. 1898; capt., T.A.R.O.; prin., junr. tech. schl., Hong Kong, 1932; do., trade and tech. schls., 1935.

WHITE, HUGH FORSTERCE MOREBY.—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1916; admnve. offr., cls. I, Sept., 1932; prin. asst. sec., 1936; ag. dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1936-Feb. 1937 and July-Dec., 1937; admnve. offr., staff gde., 1937; ag. ch. comsnr., S.P., 1938; do., W.P., 1939.

WHITE, JAMES BOWIE, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M. Inst.W.E.—B. 1903; ed. R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow and Glasgow Univ.; engr., African Lakes Corpor., Nyasaland, 1924-28; asst. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1930; do., P.W.D., Uganda, 1937.
WHITE, LT.-COL. THOMAS WALTER, D.F.C., M.P., V.D.—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia for Balaklava (Vic.), 1929; min. for trade and cust. Lyons ministry, 1933 and Jan., 1933-Nov., 1938.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Thomas, P.C. (1920); G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls, Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law schl.; apptd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.

WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mil'y serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Min'y. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; apptl. cler. offr., C.O., 24th Apr., 1922; higher grade cler. offr., July, 1938.

WHITELEY, Gerald Charles, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1891; ed. Bradford and Jesus Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); 2nd cls. hons. mods., 3rd cls. lit. hum. asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. res., Muri prov., 1925; admstve. offr., cls. 1, Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1931; ag. ch. sec., June, 1931; dep. ch. sec., 1934; ag. ch. sec. various occasions, 1934-37; gov'ts. dep. in 1936, 1937 and 1938; ag. ch. sec., 1938; ch. commnr., W.P., 1939.

WHITFIELD, Lionel Digby.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl., Dublin; M.A. (T.C.D.), M.R.S.T.; European mastr., S.S., Aug., 1922; headmast., govt. English schl., K. Pilah, normal cls. instr. in addn., Jan., 1928; ag. inspr., schl., 1928; ag. headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, May, 1936.

WHITTINGDALE, William.—B. 1885; ch. draughtsman and compr., surveys, Cape, Mar., 1917; prof. asst., Sept., 1920; 1st grade prof. asst., trig. survey, Nov., 1928; dir., trig. survey, May, 1933.

WILLIAMS, CYRIL HENRY, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Cert. of L.S.T.M. (with dist.), Cert. of Lond. Schl. of Hygiene, F.R.S.S.—B. 1898; ed. Otago Coll., univ. of N.Z.; health offr., Malaya, Mar., 1928.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; asst. to sec., for Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. dist. offr., New Territories, Aug., 1928; 2nd pol. mag., Sept. 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, 1932; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Apr., 1935; ag. sec., Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Aug.-Oct., 1935 and Sept., 1937; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Nov., 1935; ag. puisne judge, May-June, 1936; ag. crown solr., June-July, 1937; attd. atty.-gen's dept., Aug., 1937; ag. puisne judge, May-Dec., 1938; crown couns., Dec., 1938.

Africa, Dec., 1920; elect. inspr., Kenya, and ch. teleg. engnr., Kenya and Uganda, Apr., 1926; engnr.-in-ch., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1933; ag. P.M.G., 1936 and 1938.

WILLS, DANIEL.—B. 1898; ed. King's, Rochester; R.M.C., Sandhurst; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1921; offr. cls. V, Oct., 1924; ag. sec. rubber restrn., Johore, Apr., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Oct., 1928; asst. advr., K. Tinggi; do., Kukup, Apr., 1930; offr. assignee, Penang, Apr., 1931; asst. dist. offr., B. Mertajam, Aug., 1931; offr. cls. III, 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1933; asst. advr., Sengamat, Feb., 1936; ag. asst. advr. supt., lands and regisr., titles, Kelantan, Feb., 1937.
WILLS, JOSEPH ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1894; on war serv., R.F.A., France and Italy; ass't. conser., forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1921; provl. forest off., Oct., 1931.

WILMOT, AUBREY CHARLTON.—Cape civ. serv., 1900; transf., Transvaal, 1902; ch. clk., mast. sup. ct., 1910; ch. clk., land bank, 1912; ass't. gen. man., 1921; gen. man., 1924; man. dir., 1930; chmn., farmers' spec. relief bd., 1931; chmn., farmers' assistance bd., 1935; chmn., immigrts. selection bd., 1935; chmn., cent. bd., land and agrl. bank of S. Africa, 1937.

WILSON, CECIL SAMUEL, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. B.A.O. (Dub. Univ.)—B. 1897; med. offr., F.M.S., Jly., 1925; surg., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; surg., N. Sumbilan, Apr., 1931; do., Perak, July, 1932 and Nov., 1933; do., Selangor, Aug., 1933.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES, Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns).—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (siz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; att'd Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V, May, 1916; lieut., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in dep., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1920; gov't. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., M.V.I., Perak, Apr., 1921; emigrn. agt. in India, Aug., 1922; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1925; accomp. contr. lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1930; offr., cls. IB, 1934; emigrn. comsnr., Malaya, Madras, Oct., 1934; contr., lab., Malaya, Feb., 1936.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and clk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. gov't. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, DONALD BAGSTER, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1929.
WILSON, D. E., M.B., B.Ch. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928; pathologist, 1936.
WILSON, DOUGLAS MITCHELL.—B. 1901; vety. serv., Basutoland, 1923; clk., grade II, 1926; asst. dist. commr., 1937.

WILSON, ELIZABETH, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1886; ed. privately in Aberdeen, and at St. Mary's Abbey, Neill Hill, Lond.; war serv. 1st Eastern Gen. Hosp., Cambridge, with T.F. nursing serv., 1916-18; staff nurse, Basutoland med. serv., 1920; matron, Mohale's Hoek hosp., 1930; matron, Maseru hosp., 1933.

Sch., S'ban, May, 1929; do., Sultan Abdul Hamid Coll., Alor Star, Feb., 1934; do., High Schl., Malacca, Aug., 1937; ag. prin., Vict. Schl., S'pore, Apr., 1938.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Amersham Gram. Schl.; clk. acctnt.-gen.'s; dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, FERGUS BRUNSWICK.—B. 1908; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., Hertfordshire Inst. of Agr. and Cambridge Univ.; M.A. (1936); dip., agr. (Cantab.) (1931); Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad; A.I.C.T.A. (1932); recorder, Cambridge dept. of agr. (econ. br.) on eastern counties survey, 1932; agr. offr., Zanzibar, 1933.

WILSON, FRANK KERSHAW, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1891; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; attd. col. sect., Dec., 1914; attd., censor's office, S'pore, Jly., 1915; supt., posts and tels. and ast. supt. lands, K. Bahru, Aug., 1917; sheriff and dep. regisr., sup. ct., S'pore, Oct., 1925; chmn., rubber assessment comte., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1929; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; mag., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1933; ag. ast. advr., Muar, Oct., 1936.
WILSON, G. A., M.B., Ch.B. (Aberd.) D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1906; med. offr. tuberculosis investgn., Tanganyika Territory, 1931-32; med. offr., 1933.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, O.B.E. (1935), B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec's. off., 1904; ag. astt. sec., ch. sec's. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. commr., on various occasions, 1923-35; ch. collr., cust., and excise, July, 1924 (now styled compt., cust. and inland rev.); regisr., Br. ships, regisr., Cyprus ships, inspr., fisheries; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31; regisr., partnerships, Apr., 1929; treas., Cyprus, Apr., 1939.
WILSON, GEOFFREY STUDHOLME.—B. 1913; ed. Radley Coll.; pol. probr., Hong Kong, Oct., 1932; astt. supt., pol., Mar. 1935.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; astt. dist. commr., G. Coast, Aug., 1914; dist. commr., Jan., 1921.

WILSON, HENRY WILCOX, LL.B., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1896; ed. Uppingham and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); S. E. circuit; Kent sessions; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; legal sec., Somaliland, July, 1935; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1937.
WILSON, JOHN, M.A.—B. 1903; mast., educn. dept., N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1929; inspr., schls., Apr., 1931.
WILSON, JOHN MAURICE, B.Sc. (London), Dip. Ed.—B. 1907; astt. mast., Hongkong educn. dept., 1929.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. RT. HON. STR LESLIE ORME, G.C.S.I. (1929), G.C.I.E., (1923), P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1900), etc.—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1895; lieut., 1896; capt., 1901; served S. Africa, 1899-01; (severely wounded), desps., Queen's med. 5 clasps, D.S.O.; A.D.C. to Gov., N.S.W., 1903-09; temp. lieut.-col., R.M. and served through Galipoli operns., 1914-15 (desp.); served in France (severely wounded), 1915-16; M.P. (Reading), 1913-22; S. Portsmouth, 1922-23; parly. astt. sec. to war cabinet, 1918; chmn., national maritime bd. 1919; parly. sec. to miny. of shipping, 1919; parly. sec. to treasy. and ch. unionist whip, 1921, 23; Gov., Bombay, 1923-28; Gov., Queensland-1932.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schl., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; astt. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, July, 1919; ag. astt. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. commr., 1921; dist. commr., Aug., 1926; ag. astt. ch. col. sec., Apr.-July, 1935; ag. prov. commr., N. Prov., June, 1936; presdt., rural areas coun., 1938.

WILSON, MARK.—B. 1896; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; first schol. (hist. and pol. sci.), 1921; B.A. (senr. moltr. and gold medallist), 1921; LL.B., 1922; served with R.A.F., 1918-9; called to bar, King's Inns, Dublin (1st cls. hons. at final examn.), 1924; cadet, admin. serv., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; mag., Uganda, 1926; ag. admstr.-genl. and offr.-recr. on various occasions, 1926-9; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1933-35; chan., Uganda diocese, 1934-36; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1934; senr. mag., 1935-36; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1936; ag. ch. jus., March, 1938.

WILSON, REGINALD CHARLES, D.Sc., F.G.S., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers and R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese E. Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1919-27; dir., 1927; publns. on E. Africa and Nigerian Geology.
WILSON, ROBERT, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1883; ed. Manchester Tech. Coll.; ch. engrnr., Hollins Mines, Rhodesia, 1910; inapr., machy., Union of S. Africa, 1919; ch. inspr., machy., 1937.

WILSON, RONALD ERIC.—B. 1898; ed. Faversham and Sandhurst; cadet, F.M.S., Sept. 1921; attchd. dist. off., K. Selangor, Jan., 1922; do., cust. dept., Aug., 1922; passed cadet, Dec. 1923; offr. cls. V, Sept., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1925; 4th astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr. cls. IV, sec. to res., Pahang, Sept., 1928; astt. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Oct., 1930; do., Kuantan, Oct., 1932; offr., cls. III, Sept., 1933; sec. to res. N. Sembilan, May, 1934; ag. astt. advr., B. Pahat, July, 1937, offr. cl. II, Sept., 1938.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B. (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Légion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Légion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff offr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; gov., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the colts., 6th Aug., 1925; ret., 1933.

WILSON, WILLIAM GLASSFORD WALKER, M.I.M.E.—B. 1894; ed. Kilmarock Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; astt. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., 1929; supt. workshops, 1937; ch. mech. engrnr., 1937.

WILTSHIRE, Eric.—B.1893; ed. Monmouth Grammar Sch.; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; dist. engnr., Nov., 1929; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1931; mun. and land offr., 1939.

WILTSHIRE, H. G., M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; exhibr. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (coll. prize); L.S.T.M. certif. with distinc. (2nd place); war serv., 1914-19; France, Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (1914 Star with clasp); bacteriological specialist, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1924; do., Zanzibar, 1930; pathologist, 1932; senr. do., Uganda, 1937.
WIMBUSH, SAMUEL HUMPHREY.—B. 1903; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; dip., forestry; asst. conserv., forests, Kenya, Oct., 1925.

WINCHESTER, JOHN WISHART, M.D., Ch.B. (St. Andrews), D.M.R.E. (Camb.), Cert., L.S.T.M. (with dist.).—B. 1901; ed. Harris Acad., Dundee and St. Andrews Univ.; M.O., Malaya, Sept., 1927; port health offr., S'pore, Apr., 1929; med. & health offr., Labuan, June, 1929; M.O. and asst. radiological dept., S'pore, May, 1933; radiologist, Sel., Jan., 1935; do., S'pore, May, 1939.

WINDHAM, RALPH, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1905; ed. Wellington Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st class hons., law tripod; called to bar, 1930, (Lincoln's Inn, Buchanan prizeman); legal draftsman, Palestine, May, 1935.
WINDRUM, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1895; cadet, Fiji, 1921; passed cadet, 1924; dist. comm., 1925; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1929.
WINSER, CYRIL LEGH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govs. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, HERBERT WILLIAM, F.I.S.A.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Sch., Kingston-on-Thames, Surrey and Schol. of Accountancy, Glasgow; head acctnt., Usin Gishu Rly., Kenya, 1921-25; ch. acctnt., 1925-26; acctnt. and audr., rly. dept., Mauritius, 1927-29; ch. acctnt., 1929; ag. gen. man., Dec., 1934 to Sept., 1935; prin. asst. to ch. acctnt., Nigerian Rly., open lines, Apr., 1938; ag. ch. acctnt., Nov., 1938-May, 1939.

WINSTEEDT, SIR RICHARD OLAF, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comtee, for Malay studies in addn., Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., asst. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Sttlmts. and F.M.S., June, 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., S'pore, June, 1921; offg. as sec. to high comsrr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir. educn. S.S. and F.M.S., cls. Ia, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comtee. in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech. educn. comtee. in addn., Nov., 1925; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confce., Apr. to July, 1927; chmn., comtee., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malay an delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. confce., June, 1930; gen. advr., Johore, Oct., 1931; ret., 1935; B. 1883; ed. pub. schls. and Toronto Univ.; on author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Saws and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.

WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan.-Apr., 1927; senr. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934.
WIRASINHA, VICTOR LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; offr. cls. II, 1937.

WISDOM, RALPH HENRY WALTER, B.A.—B. 1892; ed. March Gram. Schl.; B.A. (Lond.) (1st cls. hons.), 1923; Dip. Ed. (Lond.), 1930; asst. mast., Bedford Mod. Schl., 1919-26; inspr. of schls., Kenya, 1926; ag. dir., educn. in 1931; ch. inspr., schls., 1935; ag. dir., Jan., 1938-Mar., 1939; M.E.C. and M.L.C.; dir., educn., Nyasaland, 1939.

WISE, WILFRID, M.Inst.C.E., O.B.E. (1936), B.Sc. (Hons. Lond.), M.I.Mun.Cy.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ed. ptyly. and City and Guilds of London Cent. Tech.
