M.L.C., 1937.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to oversea prize disposal comtee., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comtee., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comtee., May, 1917; priv. sec. to partly U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Amery), 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin. 1st Apr., 1920; accompanied partly U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Hon. E. F. L. Wood) on visit to W. Indies, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; rep. U.K. govt. at W. Indian confce., Ottawa, June-July, 1925; ast. sec., 18th Sept., 1927; D.O., 1928.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Josceline Henage, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsrr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; comld. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1888-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, R. E., O.B.E., M.D., D.P.H.— mil. serv., Canada, France and England, 1914-19; prov. dist. med. offr., Fort William and Port Arthur in 1912 and again in 1919; exec. sec., Canadian Tuberculosis Assoc., 1921; dep. min., dept. of pensions and national health, Dom. of Canada, 1933.

WODERMAN, GUY STANLEY, C.M.G. (1939).—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. ast., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. comsnr. of excise attd., the office, excise commn., Apr., 1913; ast. comsnr., excise, Cant. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ast., ch. censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comsnr., excise, Nov., 1926; commr., excise, Sept., 1927; govt. agt., S.P., Nov., 1930; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port commn., Nov., 1931; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1934-Mar., 1935 and June-Nov., 1935; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1937; ag. ch. sec., May-Oct., 1937, Apr.-Aug., 1938; O.A.G., Aug.-Oct., 1938.

WOLFE, HENRY, O.B.E. (1935), M.Sc.(Agric.), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. ast., dept. of agr., S.Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Kenya, 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1933, 1937 and 1939.

WOLLASTON, ALFRED CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1893; ed. Elmhurst Schl.; ast. supt., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; supt., Penang, July, 1928; do., S'pore., Sept., 1934; personal ast. to dir. gen., Feb., 1936; ag. contrlr., Sel. and Pahang, Feb., 1937.

WOMACK, OSWALD CEDRIC, P.A.S.I.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Leeds Univ.; temp. capt., R.A.S.C. and Rifle Brig., 1915-20; ast. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1921-24; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; land survr., do., 1932.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; ast. conserv. Indian forest surv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1918; conserv. of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; reverted to Indian forest surv., Oct., 1927; conserv., foresta, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., bd. of exams., Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ag. off. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928; chmn., local govt. bd., 1929-30; offl. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1930; do., leg. coun., Jan., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1932.
WOOD, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Schl., Wakefield; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; land survr., 1921.

WOOD, HARRY.—B. 1895; ed. Bearnes Schl. and Tech. Schl., Newton Abbot; G.W.R., 1910-14; R.E., 1914-20; S. American Rlys., 1920-27; Crown Agts., 1927-32; ast. loco. supt., K.U.R. and H., 1933; dist. loco. supt., c.l. I., 1938.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902. M.Sc., 1905); ast. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. ast., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. ast., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, JOHN, C.M.G. (1938), M.I.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. State schl. Timaru, and Timaru Boys High schl.; antd. N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900; ast. engr., 1904; ag. res. engr., Nelson, 1905; engr.-in-ch., Arthur's Pass tunnel constrn., 1907; engr.-in-ch., Westport dist., 1909; dist. engr., N. Auckland, 1912; inspgr. engr. for N.Z., 1920; ast. engr.-in-ch., 1934; under-sec. and engr.-in-ch., pub. wks. dept. and chmn., main highways db., 1936.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. triplos, pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. triplos., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1914-20; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of couns. for various periods, 1922 and 1929-32; senr. ast. col. sec., 1930; ag. prin. ast. col. sec. in 1931 and 1932.

WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; ast. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.
WOOD, RONALD MACKAY, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Manchester), A.M. Inst. C.E., Chtd. Civil Engr.—B. 1905; ed. Bradfield Coll. and Manchester Univ.; engr., P.W.D., Hongkong, 1928.

WOOD, S. T.—B. 1889; ed. Upper Canada Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Kingston, Ontario; inspr., R.N.W.M. pol., Nov., 1912; supt., Oct., 1931; ast. commr., Oct., 1933; ag. dep. commr., Feb., 1938; commr., Mar., 1938.

WOODHOUSE, LIONEL GILBERT OLLEYTT.—B. 1888; supernum. survr., Ceylon, Aug., 1905; 3rd gde. survr., May, 1908; ast. supt. survrs. Jan., 1910; mily. surv., Oct., 1918-Mar., 1919; supt., block survrs., Feb., 1928; ast. survr.-gen., Feb., 1932; dep. survr.-gen., May, 1936; survr.-gen., June, 1937.

WOODING, JOHN CONRAD—B. 1901; ed. Harrison Coll., and Codrington Coll. Barbados; B.A., Durham; classl. hon., 1922; M.A., 1927; senr. ast. mast., Gram. Sch., St. Kitts, Aug., 1929; ag. headmr., Montserrat, and St. Kitts, various periods, 1927-33, stem., Middle Temple, Nov., 1933; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, Dec., 1935; regsr. and provost marshal St. Kitts and addl. mag., dists. C.D.H.K., Dec., 1935; mem., pub. tenders comte. and income tax commr., May, 1936; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1938; ag. mag., dist. D., St. Kitts, June, 1938; mag., Dominica, 1939.
WOODMAN, RAYMOND, HENRY, B.Sc., Eng. (Lond.), Dipl. Civ. and Munic. Engng., Univ. Coll., Lond.—B. 1903; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. pvtc. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. at Port Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schl. of Trop. Med. exam., 1909; med. offr. and radiologist and dist. commr., Br. Honduras, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.
WOODROFFE, LESLIE ARTHUR, A.B.I.C.C.—B. 1904; ed. Mun. Tech. Coll., Somerset; mast., Govt. Tech. Schl., Gold Coast, July, 1939.

WOODWARD, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Nuneaton Gram. Schl. and Birmingham Univ.; served Gt. War, 1915-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; ag. exec. engr., waterwks. constrn., 1930, 1932-5 and 1937-38; ag. exec. engr., waterwks. maint., 1936; exec. engr., 1939; waterwks. engr., 1939.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL, C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—B. 1893; on mil. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., June, 1921; 4th asst. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1926; asst. gov't. agt., N'Elia, May, 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Nov., 1930; sec. to gov', July, 1931; col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935; ch. sec., Nigeria, 1938.

WORKMAN, JOHN EDWARD—B. 1897; served Gloucestershire Regt., King's Liverpool Regt., R.F.C., R.A.F., 1915-1918 (Mesopotamia, India and Egypt); dist. inspr. and intell. offr., R.I.C., 1920, platoon commdr., R. Ulster spl. constab., 1921; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Jan., 1925; commr., Jan., 1933; commr., pol., Fiji, 1937; comdt., Fiji defence force, offl. M.L.C., 1937.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of educon. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov. 1926; asst. dir., educon. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., in 1927 and 1930; 1st mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1931, offr. cls. II; regiar., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, May, 1932; D.P.P., S'pore, Mar., 1934; offr. cls. IB, Jan., 1935; solr.-gen., S'pore, Mar., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., in 1936 and 1937.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches, Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925; seconded to Seychelles, Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Seychelles, Oct.-Nov., 1929; audr., Br. Guiana, May, 1932; audr.-gen., Jamaica, 1936.
WORRELL, CECIL THEOPHILUS WILFRED EVERARD.—B. 1894; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Jan., 1932; do., St. George East, Jan., 1934; asst. law offr., 1936.

WORSLEY, RICHARD ROBERT LE GEYT, Ph.D. (Lond.), D.L.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc.—B.1899; ed. St. Paul's and R. Coll. of Sci.; R.E. O.C.B., 1918-1919; sub. dir., chem. sect., miny. of agr., Egypt, 1926; biochemist, E. African agril. research tn., Anani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

WORTH, HELEN ISOBEL (née Robertson), M.A., M.D., D.C.O.G.—Lady med. offr., K. Kangsar, Mar., 1927; do., Teluk Anson, May, 1927; do., K. Lumpur, May, 1928; do., K. Pilah, Dec., 1930; do., Penang, Aug., 1935.

WORTHINGTON, GRAHAM.—B. 1902; ed. St. Michael's, Crewe; asst. gov't. printer, Sierra Leone, May, 1928; ag. gov't. printer in 1928-33; supt., printing dept., Gold Coast, May, 1933; gov't. printer, Sierra Leone, 1939.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1920), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. soc'y, 1906; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmst., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; commr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food commr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comtece. on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; dir. agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."
WRENCH, CHARLES GILFILLAN.—B. 1893; admstr.-gen. and offl. recr., Uganda, Mar., 1938.

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, C.B.E. (1932), M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918), R. of O.—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high commr., and ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1927-37; ch. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1930; ag. commr., Mar.-June, 1932; ag. treas., Aug.-Nov., 1934; ag. Br. del. of Evkaf, 1934; admstr. offr.: ch. asst. sec., Jan., 1935; col. sec., Apr., 1937; ag. gov't., July-Aug., 1938 and May-Aug., 1939.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALBAN, C.M.G., (1937), B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schl., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut., 3rd Bn, Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. honors, schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; passed cadet, 1914; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1915; dist. commr., Nadi, 1915; do., Colo East, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. commr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. commr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929; 1st asst. col. sec., Jan., 1931; sec. for native affre., Mar., 1932; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. col. sec. in 1932, 1933, 1934 and 1935; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1932-35; ag. gov't., Mar.-Aug., 1935; admstr. and col. sec., St. Vincent, June, 1936; admstr., St. Lucia, July, 1938; ag. gov't., Windward Is. in 1938 and 1939; gov't. dep., 17th-19th Sept. and 2nd-4th Oct., 1938.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.: B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A., 1916; asst. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt.analyst and prof.of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, LIEUT.-COMMDR. LEONARD WILLIAM SIDNEY, R.N. (ret.).—B. 1900; ed. R.N. Colls. Osborne and Dartmouth, 1914-16; ret. from R.N., 1923; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1930; dist. offr., 1932; seconded Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col. as admstr. offr., 1938.

WRIGHT, OWEN FRANKLIN.—B. 1887; R.I.C. depot, 1908; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1908; 3rd cls. inspr., June, 1911; 2nd cls. inspr., Aug., 1919; 1st cls. inspr., June, 1924; inspr.-gen., pol., Nov., 1932.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Sch., stud., engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engnr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; aspt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; asst., D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W. in 1927, 1929 and 1931; dep. D.P.W., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; ag. D.P.W. in 1933, 1934 and 1935; res. engnr. in ch., constrn. of Lusaka, Sept., 1933-Aug., 1935; col. engnr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1936.
WURTH, WALLACE CHARLES, LL.B. (Syd.).—B. 1896; asst. under-seo. and industrial regtr., dept. of lab. and industry, N.S.W., Aug., 1932; mem., pub. service bd., Jan., 1936; chmn., May, 1939.

WYATT, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut. Hampshire Regt., 1919-26; 2nd K.A.R., 1923-26; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., various periods, 1930-38.

WYATT, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1892; ed. Lond. City & Guilds Inst.; war serv., 1914-19; H.M.S.O., Gr. Br., Aug., 1922; aspt. printing, Palestine, Mar., 1923; dep. aspt. gov't. ptg. office, S.S., July, 1933; aspt. ptg., dept., F.M.S., July, 1935.

WYNNE, MERVYN LLEWELYN.—B. 1891; ed. Corrig Schl., Ireland, Ellesmere Coll., Shropshire, and in Hanover, Munich and Edinburgh; Malayan pol. serv., 1911; asst. consnr., pol., Selangor, 1915; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1916; served in France 1917-18; comdt., pol. depot, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; consnr., pol., Trengganu, 1923; dep. consnr., pol., and ag. dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., 1928; consnr., pol., Johore, 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., June, 1931; dir., ap. br., S.S., June, 1931 and Nov., 1933; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1933; dir., sp. branch, S.S., Nov., 1933; senr., aspt., and senr. dep. consnr., Malayan pol. serv., 1936; ag. ch. pol. offr., S'pore, June, 1936.
WYNNE, COMDR. WALTER ROBERT MORANT, R.N. (Retd.).—B. 1885; King's harbr. mast., Bermuda, 1924-26; harbr. and shipping mast., Barbados, 1928.

WYNNE-JONES, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1895; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; attd., col. secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra asst. col. sec., Nov., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., in 1929; dist. offr., North, 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Feb., 1932 and Nov., 1934; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1934 and Mar., 1935; P.M.G. and chmn., Hong Kong broadcasting comte., Dec., 1936; chmn., overcrowding of prisons comte., June, 1937 (in addn.).

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins. from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; asst. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YEARSLEY, VINCENT CLAUDE RUNKIN.—B. 1891; ed. High Schl., Newcastle-u-Lyme; ent. Crown Agents' Office after compet. exam., Feb., 1912; mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. head, stores dept., Sept., 1928; shipping dept., July, 1931; stores dept., Dec., 1934; dep. head, stores dept., July, 1938.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KARNER, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1899; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. registr. gen., 1895-7; dist. consnr., Orange Walk, 1898; registr. gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and in Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1915 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief ust., Fiji, 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, G.C.M.G. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1918), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local commt., mil. pol., Kyrena, Aug., 1878; asst. consnr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; consnr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prim. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admstd. the govt. July, to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec. S. Sttlmts., June, 1906; admtd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Sttlmts., Aug. 1911; assumed govt., Sept., 1911; also high consnr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; consnr. of crown lands and immigrn., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comte., for stint. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 1917; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR SIR HUBERT WINTHROP, K.C.M.G. (1934), KT. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Arty., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb., Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahda); pres. local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as ast. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo confce., Apr., 1931; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high comsnr., Iraq, Sept., 1929; ag. high comsnr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary, Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Nyassaland Prot., 1932; do., Northern Rhodesia, Mar., 1934; do., Trinidad and Tobago, 1938.

YOUNG, HON. SIR JAMES ALEXANDER, K.C.V.O. (1935).—Mem. N.Z. parl., 1911-35; min. of health, 1926-28; min. of industries and commerce, 1928; min. of health, min. of immigrn. in coalition miny., 1931-35; min., internal affrs., 1933-35.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univ.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919, ast. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, JOHN GEORGE.—B. 1887; ed. Winton Dist. High Schl.; joined N.Z., P. & T. dept., 1902; inspr., 1921; dep. ch. inspr., 1922; 2nd dep. dir-gen., 1937; dep. dir-gen., 1938; dir-gen., 1939; served with N.Z.E.F., 1915-19; attained rank capt.; ment. in despa., 1917; col., 1939.

YOUNG, SIR MARK AITCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th ast. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd ast. sec., col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd ast. col. sec., July, 1920; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. gov. in 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930; admstd. govt. for various periods, 1930-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Barbados, June, 1933; admstd. govt., Trinidad and Tobago, Nov., 1937-Feb., 1938; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1938.

YOUNG, ROBERT MARCUS, B.A., B.A.I. (Elect.) A.R.C.Sc.I.—B. 1900; ed. Mountjoy Schl., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; European mast., S.S., Aug., 1924; headmast., govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Nov., 1929; ag. supt., edcn., Kedah, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1933; headmast., govt. Eng. schl., K. Pilah, Mar., 1936; do., High schl., B. Mertajam, May, 1938.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibr., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. ast., children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927, 1930 and 1933; M.O.H. and ag. senr. med. offr., 1935; S.M.O., Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1892; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; med. offr., K. Lipis, Nov., 1922; ditto, Taiping, Oct., 1924; ditto, Larut, May, 1925; ditto, B. Gajah, Mar., 1927; ditto, B. Pahang, May, 1928; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1928; med. offr., Larut, Aug., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., soc. hyg., F.M.S., May, 1930; ag. state, med. and health offr., Pahang, Jan., 1932; ch. med. offr.; Malacca, Oct., 1935; superscale offr., grade A., Sept., 1936.
YOUNG, WILLIAM ARTHUR, B.S., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1890; prof. of bacteriology, Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Mar., 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ast. ast. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admstd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govt., Leeward Is., 1909; admstr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govt., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govt., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1915; ret., 1920.
YOUNG, WILLIAM MARTIN.—B. 1890; N. British Rlwy., 1905-20; dep. ch. acct., Ceylon govt. rlwy., 1921; ch. acct., Palestine rlwy., 1927.
YU WAN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; inspr., vernacular schls., Hong Kong, 1922; mem., bd. of examrs., 1923.

ZIAI, MUSTAFA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., Feb. Mar. and May-Sept., 1933; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1939.
